From the report view, you can:

- Apply additional filters to the report
- View the agreements that comprise a segment in the pie or bar chart
- Customize the data that is displayed in the agreements list
- Download the report

Tip: Select the party name from the breadcrumb navigation located at the top of the view to return to the dashboard.

title: IAM Party Hub
text:
The Agreements tab shows a list of all the agreements that are tied to a specific party or alias group:

![A screenshot of the party hub Agreements tab that shows a list of agreements that are tied to the selected party or group.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Party-hub_Agreements-tab.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc3OTksInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.JUb8tVkw23wmBFqgED13fn4RMrN_KeVASrAsrV7Ku80&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the party hub Agreements tab that shows a list of agreements that are tied to the selected party or group.")

From the Agreements tab, you can:

- Search for specific agreements.
- Apply filters to the agreements list.
- Sort the agreements list.
- Customize the data that is displayed in the agreements list.
- Download and share individual agreements.
- View the Activity Log for an agreement.
- Add selected agreements to an agreement set.

  Note: The Organize
  and manage all agreements permission is required to
  perform agreement set management tasks. This includes adding selected
  agreements to an agreement set.
- Download selected agreements.
- Export the table data for selected agreements to a CSV file.
- Go to the Agreement Preview page for an agreement.

  Note: Use the Back
  arrow located in the top left to return to the
  Agreements tab.

Tip: Agreement statuses provide a quick look at where the agreement is within its life cycle. These statuses are calculated based on an agreement's extracted Effective Date and Expiration Date: ![A snippet of the party hub Agreements tab that shows the Status filter and options.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Party-hub_Agreements_Status-filter_expanded.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc3OTksInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.JUb8tVkw23wmBFqgED13fn4RMrN_KeVASrAsrV7Ku80&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the party hub Agreements tab that shows the Status filter and options.")See the [Agreement Statuses](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=con6a3bd080-da17-4d1c-a92a-b9b9340c44fd.html&_LANG=enus "Read about agreement statuses, including how they are calculated, visibility within Agreement Manager, and which types of statuses are being tracked.") topic to learn more about this feature.

title: IAM Party Hub
text:
The Hierarchies tab shows how your agreements are related to each other.
Whether one depends on another or they contain supporting documents, you can create
hierarchies to reflect their relationship to each other.

![A screenshot of the party hub Hierarchies tab that shows a list of hierarchies that are tied to the selected party or group.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-Preview-page_Hierarchies-view.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc3OTksInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.JUb8tVkw23wmBFqgED13fn4RMrN_KeVASrAsrV7Ku80&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the party hub Hierarchies tab that shows a list of hierarchies that are tied to the selected party or group.")

Hierarchies show how agreements are related. If there are no existing hierarchies for the
selected party or group, use the Create Hierarchy option to
build one. You are prompted to:

- Define hierarchy details
- Add agreements to the hierarchy
- Arrange the agreements to provide a relationship structure for the
  hierarchy

See the [Agreement Hierarchies](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=con1b654754-bd6d-4fbd-ac0c-8c44a9b1d666.html&_LANG=enus "Read about agreement hierarchies, including their purpose, use cases, entry points, and common tasks.") topic to learn more about creating and managing agreement hierarchies.

title: IAM Party Hub
text:
The Obligations tab shows a unified summary of all the obligations that are tied to a specific party or alias group:

![A screenshot of the party hub Obligations tab that shows a list of obligations that are tied to the selected party or group.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Party-hub_Obligations-tab.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc3OTksInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.JUb8tVkw23wmBFqgED13fn4RMrN_KeVASrAsrV7Ku80&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the party hub Obligations tab that shows a list of obligations that are tied to the selected party or group.")

From the Obligations tab, you can:

- Search for specific obligations.
- Apply filters to the obligations list.
- Sort the obligations list.
- Customize the data that is displayed in the obligations list.
- View the details for a selected obligation from the Obligations pane on the
  Agreement preview page.

  Note: Use the Back arrow located in the top-left
  corner to return to the Obligations tab.

Restriction: The Obligations tab is only
available to IAM plans that have purchased Obligation Management Plan items, such as
obligations tracking.

Obligations are surfaced in English (U.S.) agreements only. The AI model that processes obligations is run from a U.S. server and can only process English agreements in this region.

If your language and region are not supported, manually create the obligations to include this information in dashboards, reports, and other insights.

See the [Obligation Management](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=csm1727128259486.html&_LANG=enus "Read about obligation management, including how to use this functionality to capture, track, and fulfill your agreement obligations. This topic answers the question: How can I find and track my agreement obligations?") topic to learn more about managing your obligations.

title: IAM Party Hub
text:
The Details tab shows the display name, party type, and party side details for the selected party or group.

![A screenshot of the Details tab that shows the display name, party type, and party side details for the selected party or group.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Party-hub_Details-view.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc3OTksInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.JUb8tVkw23wmBFqgED13fn4RMrN_KeVASrAsrV7Ku80&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Details tab that shows the display name, party type, and party side details for the selected party or group.")

Use the Edit Details option to update the party's or group's details as needed. For example:

- Add an alias for the party or group
- Update the party type (organization or individual)
- Update the party side (external or internal)

Be sure to Save your updates to the Details tab.

title: View Obligations at the Party Level
heading_path: View Obligations at the Party Level
text:
Learn how to view obligations that are associated with a selected party or alias group.

The "party hub" displays a unified summary of all agreements and obligations that are tied to a party or alias group.

Note: The Obligations tab lists the payment, renewal, and termination obligations that were surfaced in your agreements. The list also includes manually created obligations. When creating an obligation, you can choose from a number of different types of obligations to track.

Access the party hub from various places within Agreement Manager, including:

- The PARTIES column on the Completed Documents view.
- The Details pane on the Agreement preview page.
- The NAME column in the Parties table.

This topic reviews the steps to view party obligations by selecting a name from the PARTIES column.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed.

   ![A screenshot of the Completed Documents view that highlights the search field and filters.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Navigator_Completed-documents_Search-and-filters-highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcxNjgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.IelfHx_1pND7VZ69bvtHDmNQx4LUXveYn91w4FHdq6g&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Completed Documents view that highlights the search field and filters.")
4. Perform a keyword search or use filters to locate the party that you want to review:
   - Enter the party name in the search field and select Enter.
   - Expand the Filters option and select the Parties filter. Search for and select the appropriate party, and select the Apply option.

   The table is updated to reflect the entered criteria or applied filter. The names in the Parties column are clickable and link directly to the party hub.

   ![A snippet of the Completed Documents page that highlights the Parties column as an entry point for the party hub.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Party-hub_Entry-point_Completed-example.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcxNjgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.IelfHx_1pND7VZ69bvtHDmNQx4LUXveYn91w4FHdq6g&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Completed Documents page that highlights the Parties column as an entry point for the party hub.")
5. Select a name from the Parties column.

   The party hub opens for the selected party or alias group. The Overview tab is displayed by default.
6. Select the Obligations tab:

   ![A snippet of the party hub that highlights the Overview, Agreements, Obligations, and Details tabs.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Party-hub_Overview-dashboard_highlighted-tabs.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcxNjgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.IelfHx_1pND7VZ69bvtHDmNQx4LUXveYn91w4FHdq6g&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the party hub that highlights the Overview, Agreements, Obligations, and Details tabs.")

   The Obligations tab opens. All the obligations tied to the selected party or alias group are displayed in the table.

title: View Obligations at the Party Level
heading_path: View Obligations at the Party Level
text:
hub that highlights the Overview, Agreements, Obligations, and Details tabs.")

   The Obligations tab opens. All the obligations tied to the selected party or alias group are displayed in the table.

   ![A screenshot of the party hub Obligations tab that shows a list of obligations that are tied to the selected party or group.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Party-hub_Obligations-tab.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcxNjgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.IelfHx_1pND7VZ69bvtHDmNQx4LUXveYn91w4FHdq6g&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the party hub Obligations tab that shows a list of obligations that are tied to the selected party or group.")
7. Apply additional filters to refine the obligations list as needed.
8. Select an obligation.

   The Agreement preview page opens and the obligation details are displayed for the selected obligation.

   ![A snippet of the Agreement preview page that highlights the details of the selected obligation.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Party-hub_Obligations_Agreement-preview.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcxNjgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.IelfHx_1pND7VZ69bvtHDmNQx4LUXveYn91w4FHdq6g&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Agreement preview page that highlights the details of the selected obligation.")
9. Review the obligation details as needed.
10. Return to the party hub. Select the Back arrow from the top-left corner of the page.

    You are returned to the party hub.
11. Return to the Agreement Manager homepage. Select the Completed option from the navigation pane on the left.

    The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed. Your previously entered search criteria and applied filters are still in effect.

title: View Obligations for Selected Parties and Groups
heading_path: View Obligations for Selected Parties and Groups
text:
Learn how to view all the obligations that are tied to a specific party or alias group.

The "party hub" displays a unified summary of all agreements and obligations that are tied to a party or alias group. Access the party hub from various places within Agreement Manager, including:

- The Parties column (Completed Documents view)
- The Details pane (Agreement preview page)
- The Name column (Parties view)

Note: This topic reviews the steps to access the party hub from
the Completed Documents view. You can also search for and
select the relevant party from the Parties page.

![A snippet of the Parties page that highlights the Search parties field.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Party-hub_Parties-page_Search-parties-highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc4MzAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.F6f9YkxGNJ6CgpocP2BogKCoHL4MmJyY1GN0VGUBBQI&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Parties page that highlights the Search parties field.")

- Go to Home  >  Agreements and select All from the PARTIES section in the navigation pane.
- Enter search criteria in the Search parties
  field.
- Select a party from the search results.

The party hub opens for the selected party or group.

**Access the party hub from the Completed Documents view:**

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens. The Inbox view is displayed by default.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed. Filter this view for a specific party.
4. Filter the agreement list table for a specific party:

   ![A screenshot of the Parties filter that shows the Search field, Party Role dropdown, and the party list.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-documents-table_Parties-dialog-box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc4MzAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.F6f9YkxGNJ6CgpocP2BogKCoHL4MmJyY1GN0VGUBBQI&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Parties filter that shows the Search field, Party Role dropdown, and the party list.")

   1. Expand the Filters option and select the Parties filter.
   2. Enter a keyword or search string in the Search field.

      As you enter search criteria, the list of parties updates to match the keyword.
   3. Select the relevant party.

      Important: When using the Parties filter, you must also select a
      Party Role. If you do not know
      which role to select, accept the default value, All.
   4. Apply the Party Role filter to view agreements where the selected party plays a specific role. If you do not know which role to select, accept the default value, All.

      Select a role from the Party Role dropdown. Choose from: Buyer, Seller, or Other.
   5. Select the Apply option.

   The table updates and agreements that are tied to the selected party or group are displayed. The names in the Parties column are clickable and link directly to the party hub.

   ![A snippet of the Completed Documents view that highlights the Parties column as an entry point for the party hub.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Party-hub_Entry-point_Completed-example.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc4MzAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.F6f9YkxGNJ6CgpocP2BogKCoHL4MmJyY1GN0VGUBBQI&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Completed Documents view that highlights the Parties column as an entry point for the party hub.")
5. Select a name from the Parties column.

   The party hub opens for the selected party or group. The Overview tab is displayed by default.

title: View Obligations for Selected Parties and Groups
heading_path: View Obligations for Selected Parties and Groups
text:
s the Parties column as an entry point for the party hub.")
5. Select a name from the Parties column.

   The party hub opens for the selected party or group. The Overview tab is displayed by default.

**View the Obligations list:**

6. Select the Obligations tab:

   ![A snippet of the party hub that highlights the Overview, Agreements, Obligations, and Details tabs.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Party-hub_Overview-dashboard_highlighted-tabs.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc4MzAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.F6f9YkxGNJ6CgpocP2BogKCoHL4MmJyY1GN0VGUBBQI&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the party hub that highlights the Overview, Agreements, Obligations, and Details tabs.")

   The Obligations tab opens. All the obligations tied to the selected party or group are displayed in the table.

   Restriction:

   Obligations are surfaced in English (U.S.) agreements only. The AI model that processes obligations is run from a U.S. server and can only process English agreements in this region.

   If your language and region are not supported, manually create the obligations to include this information in dashboards, reports, and other insights.

   See these support articles to learn more about obligation management:
   - [Obligation Management](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=csm1727128259486.html&_LANG=enus "Read about obligation management, including how to use this functionality to capture, track, and fulfill your agreement obligations. This topic answers the question: How can I find and track my agreement obligations?")
   - [Manually Create an Obligation](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=rrf1727128588540.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to create an obligation from the Agreement preview page for a selected agreement.")

   ![A screenshot of the party hub Obligations tab that shows a list of obligations that are tied to the selected party or group.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Party-hub_Obligations-tab.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc4MzAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.F6f9YkxGNJ6CgpocP2BogKCoHL4MmJyY1GN0VGUBBQI&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the party hub Obligations tab that shows a list of obligations that are tied to the selected party or group.")

   Tip: Use the Favorites (star) icon to "star" your most active, time-sensitive, or high-value parties and groups. Each user can add up to 50 parties and groups to their Starred list.

   Your "favorite" parties and groups are listed under the Starred tab on the Parties page.

**Filter and customize the Obligations list:**

7. Apply additional filters to locate relevant obligations.
8. Customize the table data as needed:

   ![A screenshot of the Show or Hide Fields dialog box that shows the columns you can choose to display in the agreements table.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Party-hub_Overview-dashboard_Customize-columns.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc4MzAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.F6f9YkxGNJ6CgpocP2BogKCoHL4MmJyY1GN0VGUBBQI&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Show or Hide Fields dialog box that shows the columns you can choose to display in the agreements table.")

   1. Select the Show or Hide Fields (toggle) icon located on the right side of the page.

title: View Obligations for Selected Parties and Groups
heading_path: View Obligations for Selected Parties and Groups
text:
he Show or Hide Fields dialog box that shows the columns you can choose to display in the agreements table.")

   1. Select the Show or Hide Fields (toggle) icon located on the right side of the page.

      The Show or Hide Fields dialog box opens. These fields represent the columns in the table.
   2. Select the toggles to turn the associated field (column) on (blue) or off (white).
   3. Re-order the columns as needed. Hover the cursor over the Reorder (dots) icon and drag the column (field) to the appropriate location.
   4. Select the Save option.

   The dialog box closes and the columns are updated according to your configuration.

**View obligation details from the Agreement preview page:**

9. Select an obligation from the table.

   The Agreement preview page opens and the associated obligation is displayed in the Obligations pane.

   ![A snippet of the Agreement preview page that highlights the details of the selected obligation.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Party-hub_Obligations_Agreement-preview.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc4MzAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.F6f9YkxGNJ6CgpocP2BogKCoHL4MmJyY1GN0VGUBBQI&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Agreement preview page that highlights the details of the selected obligation.")
10. Review the obligation details and place the pane in *Edit* mode to make updates as needed. Be sure to save your changes before leaving the page.

**Return to the party hub:**

11. Select the Back arrow from the top-left corner.

    You are returned to the party hub.
12. Select Parties from the breadcrumb navigation at the top of the page.

    You are returned to the Parties view.

    Tip: Select Completed from the navigation pane on the left to view your agreement list. The Completed Documents view opens. The agreements to which the user has access are displayed in the table.

title: Display the "Sets" Column in the Agreement List Table
heading_path: Display the "Sets" Column in the Agreement List Table
text:
Learn how to display the Sets column in the agreement list table on the Completed Documents page.

Use the Show or Hide Fields feature to display the Sets column in the agreement list table. The Sets column displays the sets to which the corresponding document was added.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed.
4. Select the Show or Hide Fields (toggle) icon located to the right of the filters:

   ![A snippet of the Completed Documents page that highlights the Show or Hide Fields (toggle) icon on the right-side of the page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-documents-table_Customize-columns-gear-icon.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTczNzcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.jJT8KX43Q7qm0cKvVJnorfV-oZZuV1aZG3k9_NiYrIo&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Completed Documents page that highlights the Show or Hide Fields (toggle) icon on the right-side of the page.")

   The Show or Hide Fields dialog box opens. A number of fields are turned on (blue) by default. These fields represent the columns in the agreement list table. Columns display in the order that the fields are represented in the dialog box.

   Important: The Show or Hide Fields function only applies to the data that is displayed in the agreement list table. When downloading or exporting the report, all fields are included in the output file. This includes both custom and standard fields.
5. Perform a search query to locate the Sets column and select the toggle to turn it on (blue position):

   ![A screenshot of the Show or Hide Fields dialog box that shows the Sets toggle.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-set-management_Customize-columns_Sets-toggle.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTczNzcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.jJT8KX43Q7qm0cKvVJnorfV-oZZuV1aZG3k9_NiYrIo&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Show or Hide Fields dialog box that shows the Sets toggle.")
6. Select the Save option.

   The dialog box closes and the Sets column is displayed in the agreement list table.

   Tip: If necessary, re-order the columns from the Show or Hide Fields dialog box. Hover your cursor over the Reorder (dots) icon for the filter you want to move and drag it to the appropriate location. For example, drag the Sets column to the appropriate location and select the Save option.

You have successfully displayed the Sets column in the agreement list table on the Completed Documents page.

title: Renewal Notifications and Subscriptions
text: This topic answers the question: What are my reminder options for renewals in Agreement Manager? Read about the renewals and document-level notifications, including the content and frequency of the emails.

title: Renewal Notifications and Subscriptions
text:
Use the *Subscriptions* feature to sign up to receive email report notifications. You can subscribe to receive email report notifications for any of the charts found on the Agreements, Obligations, and Renewals dashboards.

Note: The *Subscription* feature is also available for your custom reports and dashboard charts.

This feature is accessed from the detailed report view for a Renewals dashboard chart. Use the Manage Notification (bell) icon to create or manage your email reports.

![A snippet of the detailed report view that highlights the subscription feature.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Subscriptions_Subscribe-highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcyNjMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.vXS6lli1s0FcZJABhRKq8V5bz3rkZQybny0sxsEH_1E&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the detailed report view that highlights the subscription feature.")

Complete the Create subscription dialog box to set up one-time or recurring email reports for yourself and others:

![A screenshot of the Create subscription dialog box that shows the subscription configuration fields.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Renewals-dashboard_Report-view_Create-subscriptions.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcyNjMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.vXS6lli1s0FcZJABhRKq8V5bz3rkZQybny0sxsEH_1E&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Create subscription dialog box that shows the subscription configuration fields.")

Note: When you create a subscription, you have the option to add individual users and groups. Once created, the subscription is displayed in the Manage report subscriptions dialog box for any individual users who were added. Currently, the subscription is not visible to users if they were added as part of a group.

See the [Subscribe to Renewal Reports](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=nis1709931821078.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to set up one-time or recurring email reports for yourself or others to receive notifications about your renewals.") article to learn more about subscribing to receive renewal-related email reports.

title: Renewal Notifications and Subscriptions
text:
Document-level email notifications help you stay informed about upcoming milestones for individual documents. This function has two entry points:

- **Agreement list table:** Hover the cursor over an agreement to display the Set a notification (bell) icon. ![A snippet of the agreement list table that shows the Set a notification (bell) icon.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-snippet_Document-level-notification_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcyNjMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.vXS6lli1s0FcZJABhRKq8V5bz3rkZQybny0sxsEH_1E&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the agreement list table that shows the Set a notification (bell) icon.")
- **Agreement preview page:** Select the Set a notification (bell) icon to set a document-level notification.![A snippet of the Agreement preview page that shows the Set a notification (bell) icon.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-preview_Manage-Set-a-notification_snippet.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcyNjMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.vXS6lli1s0FcZJABhRKq8V5bz3rkZQybny0sxsEH_1E&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Agreement preview page that shows the Set a notification (bell) icon.")

When you select the bell icon, the Renewal Notice Date and Expiration Date notifications are enabled by default. Other notification types can be configured by using the + Add option.

Note: When you enable the notification, you are designated as the recipient and all frequency options are selected. Use the Edit  function to update the frequency and recipients for the notification as needed.

The Manage notifications (bell) icon is displayed once the notification is set. Select this icon to add new notifications or to edit existing ones:![A snippet of the agreement list table that shows the Manage notifications (bell) icon.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-list-table_Manage-notifications_snippet.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcyNjMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.vXS6lli1s0FcZJABhRKq8V5bz3rkZQybny0sxsEH_1E&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the agreement list table that shows the Manage notifications (bell) icon.")See the [Set Document-Level Notifications](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tsk62370c10-0d3b-4218-a325-4383b9d5f7cd.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to set and manage notifications to track upcoming milestones for an individual document.") article to learn more about setting document-level notifications.

title: Renewal Notifications and Subscriptions
text:
Docusign provides email notifications for all account-related updates, including updates related to your agreements in Agreement Manager. Notification settings give you control over the email notifications that you receive.

The Notification Preferences page is accessed from the My Preferences option in the user profile menu. By default, all the notification types in the Completed Agreements section are enabled:![A screenshot of the Notification Preferences page that highlights the selected notification types for completed agreements.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/My-preferences_Notification-Preferences_For-Completed-Agreements_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcyNjMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.vXS6lli1s0FcZJABhRKq8V5bz3rkZQybny0sxsEH_1E&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Notification Preferences page that highlights the selected notification types for completed agreements.")

Deselect a checkbox if you no longer want to receive email notifications for the corresponding notification type.

Tip: Periodically review your Notification Preferences to ensure that you are receiving the relevant email notifications. Additional notification types will be added in the future.

See the [Customize Notification Settings for Your Completed Agreements](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tsk155b1ed7-7b45-43cd-be92-2a5279630c0f.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to customize your email notification settings from the Notification Preferences page.") topic for steps to update your notification preferences.

title: File Ingestion from Third-Party Cloud Storage Providers
heading_path: File Ingestion from Third-Party Cloud Storage Providers
text:
This topic answers the question: How can I upload selected files to Agreement Manager from
third-party cloud storage providers?

The Agreement Manager upload experience includes the ability to upload files from third-party
cloud storage providers. This experience also lets you choose which files
you want to upload. Benefits of this enhanced ingestion process include:

- Simplified agreement storage - you no longer have to manage agreements in multiple
  storage locations
- Improved internal collaboration
- Single source of truth for signed agreements
- Ability to surface agreement data that would otherwise be sitting unused in cloud
  storage

The upload process supports the following third-party cloud storage providers:

- Box
- Dropbox
- Google Drive
- OneDrive
- SharePoint

Cloud storage providers must first be installed from the Docusign App Center.
This task must be completed by an account administrator.

Important: If these apps were previously installed from
the Docusign App Center, you must uninstall and reinstall them. Due to
versioning issues, backward compatibility is not currently possible.

Note: See the [Organization Administrators](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=rrf1583359212854&topicId=god1583359152535.html) article to learn more about the
required permissions.

**For account administrators:**

The [Install Cloud Storage Apps for Account Administrators](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=twl1726764423921.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to install various cloud storage providers from the Docusign App Center. This topic answers the question: How do I install cloud storage providers so users can upload documents to Agreement Manager?") topic walks you through the steps to install
and enable cloud storage providers. Once enabled, you can choose files from your cloud
storage locations to upload to Agreement Manager for AI processing.

title: Agreement Type Definitions
text:
Look up definitions for Docusign-provided agreement types.

Docusign provides a number of standard agreement types. These agreement types can be customized to meet your organization's unique use cases. Review the agreement type definitions to recognize the purpose and usage of each type.

title: Agreement Type Definitions
text:
| **Agreement Type** | **Definition** |
| --- | --- |
| **Addendum** | A legal agreement that is added to an existing contract. Addendums are additions that are made to an agreement after it has been completed or executed.  Addendums can provide additional information or clarification, or make changes to certain terms or provisions in the original agreement.  An addendum modifies the original agreement. It is not a standalone document. Once signed by all involved parties, the addendum becomes part of the original document. |
| **Amendment** | A legal agreement that details an official modification to an already existing agreement or legal document.  Amendments are used to modify the original document without completely rewriting it. Once signed by all involved parties, the amendment becomes part of the original document. |
| **Appendix** | A supplementary document that is appended to a legal agreement or larger document. Appendices provide additional support to the main content of an agreement or document. |
| **Application** | A formal request for approval, service, or consideration. Typically applications are submitted to an authority, institution, or organization.  Examples of applications include: Employment applications, loan applications, bids, and claims. |
| **Attachment** | A document or group of documents that are physically added to a contract post-signature. Attachments can also be included by reference in the body of the agreement.  Attachments can include plans, drawings, specifications, or other information that is necessary for understanding or fulfilling the agreement. |

title: Agreement Type Definitions
text:
| **Agreement Type** | **Definition** |
| --- | --- |
| **Certificate** | A formal document that serves as official proof of a fact, qualification, status, or legal right. Certificates are issued by authorized entities, such as a government agency, educational institution, financial institution, or professional organization.  Important: All the custom and standard fields that are associated with an agreement type are surfaced in the relevant documents. However, not all non-agreement types are surfaced in your documents. This includes the custom and standard fields that are associated with the type. These non-agreement types are not surfaced during agreement processing: Certificate, Handbook, Notice, and Policy. |
| **Certificate of Insurance (COI)** | A document issued by insurance companies or brokers that provides proof of an active insurance policy.  COIs typically include:   - Proof of insurance coverage - Policy information - Certificate holder - Exclusions and limitations - Notification requirements. |
| **Change Order** | A legal agreement that describes any modifications or changes to the agreed-upon scope of work, schedule, or budget for an existing contract. |
| **Consulting Agreement** | A legal contract between a consultant or consulting firm and a client. Consulting contracts outline the terms and conditions of their working relationship. |
| **Contractor Agreement** | A legal contract between a business or individual and an independent contractor. Contractor agreements outline the terms and conditions of their working relationship. |
| **Credit Card Agreement** | A legal contract between a cardholder and a credit card issuer. Credit card agreements set forth the terms and conditions governing the use of the credit card. |

title: Agreement Type Definitions
text:
| **Agreement Type** | **Definition** |
| --- | --- |
| **Employment Separation Agreement** | A legal contract between employers and employees that outlines the terms and conditions for the termination of employment.  This agreement typically addresses various aspects of the separation, such as:   - Severance pay - Continuation of benefits - Confidentiality obligations - Non-disparagement obligations - Other post-employment obligations |
| **Engagement Letter** | A legal agreement between clients and service providers that outlines the terms and conditions of a professional services agreement.  Examples of service providers include accountants, lawyers, or consultants. |
| **Event** | A non-legally binding document in any written, printed, or digital form that provides information about a specific occurrence.  Events can include details such as the date, time, location, participants, and outcomes of the event. |
| **Exhibit** | A document that is attached to the end of a contract to provide additional information or evidence.  Exhibits support the claims made in the main documents or in ancillary documents and may have an evidentiary purpose. |

title: Agreement Type Definitions
text:
| **Agreement Type** | **Definition** |
| --- | --- |
| **Fee** | A contractual agreement between service providers and clients. Contractual agreements outline the terms and conditions related to the fees or compensation for provided services.  These agreements establish the financial arrangement between the parties and ensure clarity and mutual understanding about the payment structure. |
| **Form** | A structured document with predefined fields, sections, or spaces that is designed to collect, organize, or present information systematically. |
| **Franchise Agreement** | A legal contract between franchisors and franchisees that outlines the terms and conditions under which the franchise is operated.  Franchise agreements detail how the franchisee operates a business using the franchisor's brand, trademarks, and business model. |

title: Agreement Type Definitions
text:
| **Agreement Type** | **Definition** |
| --- | --- |
| **Handbook** | A comprehensive reference guide that provides essential information, instructions, or guidelines on a specific topic. Handbooks are typically designed for easy access and practical use.  Important: All the custom and standard fields that are associated with an agreement type are surfaced in the relevant documents. However, not all non-agreement types are surfaced in your documents. This includes the custom and standard fields that are associated with the type. These non-agreement types are not surfaced during agreement processing: Certificate, Handbook, Notice, and Policy. |

title: Agreement Type Definitions
text:
| **Agreement Type** | **Definition** |
| --- | --- |
| **Intellectual Property Assignment Agreement** | A legal contract that is used to transfer ownership of intellectual property (IP) rights from one party to another. |
| **Investment Account Agreement** | A legal contract between individuals or entities (account holders) and financial institutions or brokerage firms.  These agreements outline the terms and conditions for opening and maintaining investment accounts with financial institutions or brokerage firms. |
| **Invoice** | A formal, itemized document issued by sellers to buyers that serves as a demand for payment of goods or provided services.  Invoices outline the details of the transaction, including the amount due, payment terms, and other relevant information. |

title: Agreement Type Definitions
text:
| **Agreement Type** | **Definition** |
| --- | --- |
| **Joint Venture Agreement** | A legal contract between two or more parties to collaborate on a specific project or business activity. These agreements outline the terms and conditions under which the collaborating parties will undertake the business project or activity.  Joint venture agreements are typically for a limited time and establish the rights, responsibilities, and obligations of each party. |

title: Agreement Type Definitions
text:
| **Agreement Type** | **Definition** |
| --- | --- |
| **Lease** | A legally binding agreement where one party conveys land, property, services, or privileges in exchange for the other party making regular payments. Payments can also include an exchange for something else of value.  The most commonly occurring type of lease is between landlords (owners) and tenants. Landlords grant their tenants the right to use and occupy a piece of real property. In return, the tenants make regular payments (rents) for the duration of the lease.  In lease agreements for real property, the tenant is often referred to as the lessee and the landlord as the lessor. |
| **Letter of Intent (LOI)** | A document that outlines the preliminary agreement between two parties for a future or proposed contract.  An LOI is not legally binding. It is a written statement of the key terms and conditions of the proposed agreement.  LOIs typically precede the execution of a formal contract. |
| **License** | A legal agreement that grants permission and sets terms and conditions for individuals or organizations to use, reproduce, or distribute a product, service, or intellectual property. For example, software, music, or patents.  A license can be exclusive or non-exclusive and can be granted for a specific period of time or indefinitely.  In license agreements, the individual or entity who owns or controls the rights to the product, service, or intellectual property is the licensor. The individual or entity who is granted permission to use, reproduce, or distribute the product, service, or intellectual property is the licensee . |
| **Loan** | A legal contract between lenders (the party providing the loan) and borrowers (the party receiving the loan). These agreements outline the terms and conditions under which the loan is made. |

title: Agreement Type Definitions
text:
| **Agreement Type** | **Definition** |
| --- | --- |
| **Marketing** | A legally binding contract between two or more parties that outlines the terms and conditions for promoting, advertising, or selling a product, service, or brand.  Marketing agreements typically define the roles, responsibilities, compensation, and expectations of each involved party. |
| **Master Services Agreement (MSA)** | A legal contract between two parties, typically clients and service providers, that establishes the terms and conditions under which services are provided to the client.  MSAs are a type of framework agreement that establishes a long-term business relationship between the parties. These agreements are used for ongoing, repetitive services or for a series of related projects. |
| **Memorandum of Understanding (MOI)** | A legally non-binding document that outlines the terms and understanding of an agreement between two or more parties.  Although not legally binding, an MOI signals the willingness of the parties to move forward. The parties can act and negotiate in good faith to reach a contractual agreement.  MOIs are used to establish a framework for a formal agreement and often serves as a starting point for negotiations. |
| **Miscellaneous (Other)** | Legal agreements or non-legal documents that fall outside of the active agreement types, including custom agreement types. |

title: Agreement Type Definitions
text:
| **Agreement Type** | **Definition** |
| --- | --- |
| **Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA)** | A legally binding contract between two parties where one party (the disclosing party) agrees to share confidential information with the other party (the receiving party).  The receiving party agrees not to disclose or use confidential information without the disclosing party's consent. |
| **Notice** | A formal written communication that provides information, warnings, or legal notifications to an individual, business, or organization.  Notices serve as official documentation of an event, obligation, or change in legal status.  Important: All the custom and standard fields that are associated with an agreement type are surfaced in the relevant documents. However, not all non-agreement types are surfaced in your documents. This includes the custom and standard fields that are associated with the type. These non-agreement types are not surfaced during agreement processing: Certificate, Handbook, Notice, and Policy. |

title: Agreement Type Definitions
text:
| **Agreement Type** | **Definition** |
| --- | --- |
| **Offer Letter** | A formal document that is issued by employers to prospective employees to extend a job offer.  Offer letters outline the terms and conditions of employment, including job title, salary, benefits, start date, and other pertinent details. |
| **Order Form** | A document that sellers use to collect purchase details from buyers for goods or services.  Sellers can be referred to as vendors or suppliers. Buyers can be referred to as purchasers.  Order forms typically include: contact information, a description of goods or services being ordered, quantity ordered, price, shipping details, and terms of payment. |

title: Agreement Type Definitions
text:
| **Agreement Type** | **Definition** |
| --- | --- |
| **Partnership Agreement** | A broad legal contract between two or more parties who agree to run a business together continuously for profit.  The agreement outlines the rights, responsibilities, and obligations of each party and governs the operation and management of the partnership. |
| **Plan** | A structured outline, strategy, or set of actions designed to achieve a specific goal. Plans typically include:   - Objectives - Steps - Timeline for completion - Resource requirements |
| **Policy** | A formal set of principles, rules, or guidelines established by an organization, government, or institution.  Policies regulate behavior, guide decision-making, and ensure consistency in actions.  Important: All the custom and standard fields that are associated with an agreement type are surfaced in the relevant documents. However, not all non-agreement types are surfaced in your documents. This includes the custom and standard fields that are associated with the type. These non-agreement types are not surfaced during agreement processing: Certificate, Handbook, Notice, and Policy. |
| **Privacy and Security** | A legally binding contract that outlines how an individual's or organization’s sensitive or confidential data is collected, used, stored, shared, and protected. |
| **Proposal** | A written document that outlines a specific plan or suggestion for a project, product, or service for consideration.  Proposals are intended to persuade the reader to agree to the proposal. |
| **Publishing Agreement** | A legal contract that outlines the terms and conditions between authors (or creators) and publishers for acquisition of the rights to publish and distribute the author's work. |
| **Purchase Agreement** | A legal contract that outlines the terms and conditions of a transaction between buyers and sellers for the purchase and sale of goods or services. |
| **Purchase Order (PO)** | A formal request from buyers to purchase goods or services from sellers.  POs act as legal offers to buy goods or services. They are issued by buyers to sellers to provide key transaction details, such as terms, types, quantities, and pricing.  Buyers can be referred to as purchasers. Sellers can be referred to as vendors or suppliers. |

title: Agreement Type Definitions
text:
| **Agreement Type** | **Definition** |
| --- | --- |
| **Quote** | A document that sets out the estimated cost for goods or services that are offered by sellers or service providers.  Sellers or service providers provide quotes to potential customers. These quotes typically include the price or estimated cost for the goods or services that they plan to provide. |

title: Agreement Type Definitions
text:
| **Agreement Type** | **Definition** |
| --- | --- |
| **Release/Waiver** | A legally binding contract where one party (the releasor) agrees to waive or relinquish legal claims, rights, or liabilities against another party (the releasee).  Releases or waivers often include compensation, a settlement, or other considerations in exchange for relinquishing rights to further action. |
| **Renewal (Agreement/Notice)** | A legally binding contract that extends the duration of an existing agreement, often under the same or modified terms.  Renewal agreements or notices ensure the continuation of the rights, obligations, and benefits between the involved parties. They extend the existing agreement without requiring a new contract. |
| **Report** | A formal document that presents information, findings, analysis, or recommendations about a specific topic.  Reports are often structured, factual, and designed for a particular audience, such as businesses, government agencies, or academic institutions. |
| **Retainer** | A legal document that outlines the terms of an engagement between clients and professional service providers, such as attorneys, consultants, or agents.  In the retainer, clients agree to pay a certain amount of money upfront to retain professional services.  Retainers are used to secure the time and services of a professional in advance, with a deposit before any work is started. |

title: Agreement Type Definitions
text:
| **Agreement Type** | **Definition** |
| --- | --- |
| **Service Level Agreement (SLA)** | A contract between service providers and customers that defines the level of service that the provider will deliver. SLAs typically include:   - Responsibilities - Measurable performance standards - Penalties for service failures - Credits for services that exceed expectations - Service hours - Resolution times for service requests. |
| **Services Agreement** | A legal contract between service providers and clients that outlines the terms and conditions of the services being provided.  Services agreements help ensure that both parties understand their responsibilities and obligations, and can provide a framework for resolving any disputes that may arise. |
| **Statement of Work (SOW)** | A contract that provides a detailed description of the specific tasks, activities, and deliverables for a project or a piece of work.  SOWs typically include:   - Scope of work to complete - Objectives and outcomes to achieve - Timeframe for completion - Project requirements |
| **Stock Purchase Agreement** | A legally binding contract that outlines the terms and conditions under which sellers transfer the ownership of stock (shares) in a company to buyers. |
| **Subscription (Agreement for Software or Services)** | A *software subscription agreement* (SSA) is a legally binding contract between software providers (licensors) and customer (subscribers). SSAs grant customers access to use the software on a subscription basis.  A *service subscription agreement* (SSA) is a legally binding contract between service providers and customers. SSAs outline the terms under which the customer subscribes to and receives ongoing, recurring services. |
| **Summary** | A concise and structured overview of the key points, main ideas, or essential details of a larger text, discussion, or event.  Summaries present the most important information in a clear, brief, and easily understandable format without unnecessary details or personal opinions. |
| **Supplemental Document** | A document that provides additional information or materials that support or elaborate on the main document.  These documents or materials are used to supplement the main document and to provide more detailed information. |
| **Supply/Distribution** | The key difference between a supply agreement and a distribution agreement is that in a supply agreement, the buyer receives goods or services for their own use, consumption, or manufacturing. In a distribution agreement, a distributor purchases the goods with the intention of reselling them.  - A *supply agreement* is a legal contract between a supplier and a buyer that sets out the terms and conditions under which the supplier will provide certain goods, materials, or services to the buyer over a specified period. - A *distribution agreement* is a legal contract between a manufacturer or supplier and a distributor that typically specifies the products or services that the distributor is authorized to sell. The contract also specifies the territory in which they can sell the product or service and any exclusivity provisions that may apply. |

title: Agreement Type Definitions
text:
| **Agreement Type** | **Definition** |
| --- | --- |
| **Termination (Agreement/Notice)** | *Termination agreements* are legally binding contracts that formally end an existing agreement between two or more parties.  These agreements outline the terms, conditions, and obligations related to ending a contractual relationship. They ensure that all understand their rights and responsibilities post-termination.  *Termination notices* are formal, written documents that notify a party of the intent to end a contract, agreement, or relationship.  These notices specify the termination's effective date, the reason (if applicable), and any final obligations or required actions before the agreement officially ends. |
| **Terms & Conditions** | A document that outlines the rights, responsibilities, and obligations of both the customer and the company providing the product or service.  Terms and conditions also set out the rules regarding any limitations or restrictions on the use of a product or service.  Companies establish these rules and guidelines to govern the use of their products or services. |

title: Agreement Type Definitions
text:
| **Agreement Type** | **Definition** |
| --- | --- |
| **Wealth Management Agreement** | A legal contract between clients and financial institutions or wealth management firms.  These agreements outline the terms and conditions of the wealth management services being provided. |

title: Activate Your Custom Agreement Types
text: Learn how to activate the AI detection for a custom agreement type and surface it in selected historical agreements.

title: Activate Your Custom Agreement Types
text:
This topic reviews the last step in the AI detection set-up process, *Activate AI detection*. These steps are completed by administrators and contract managers.

Remember: Administrators automatically have access to configure agreement data. For non-administrator users, agreement type management tasks require Organize and manage all agreements access to Agreement Manager agreements and functionality.

Complete these topics before attempting this task:

- [Configure New Custom Agreement Types](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=gke1727282238086.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to create custom agreement types to support organizational use cases.")
- [Define Your Custom Agreement Types](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tske5fe1ad5-2832-4e6c-9177-f33a9ffade15.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to initiate the AI detection set-up process and complete the first step to define the custom type.")
- [Add Examples of Your Custom Agreement Types](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tskd38465c6-c8c0-4588-bfb3-fdb6916a1926.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to complete the second step of the AI detection set-up process, Add examples of this agreement type.")
- [Test the Extraction Accuracy for Your Custom Agreement Types](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=ogw1740503179389.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to test the accuracy of the AI detection for your custom agreement types.")

In the previous step, you tested the accuracy of the AI detection against a group of selected documents. After verifying the identified agreement types, you selected the Done option and the Results dialog box opened.

![A screenshot of the Results dialog box that shows the results of the accuracy test.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Manage-Agreement-Type_Test-for-accuracy_Results.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2MDUsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.j0q4HjFlgQY-6eNypwrka_ZPRAvMb7JSFB93HlMarB4&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Results dialog box that shows the results of the accuracy test.")

You are prompted to activate the AI detection for the custom agreement type. The
Activate AI detection option is selected by default.

1. Accept the default selection, Activate AI detection, and select the Confirm option.

   The Activate AI detection dialog box opens. Use this dialog box to choose when the system should look for the agreement type. The All new agreements option is selected by default. This option cannot be deselected.

   ![A screenshot of the Activate AI detection dialog box that prompts you to choose when to look for the field.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Manage-Agreement-Type_Enable-AI-detection-for-custom-agreement-type--Activate-AI-detection.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2MDUsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.j0q4HjFlgQY-6eNypwrka_ZPRAvMb7JSFB93HlMarB4&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Activate AI detection dialog box that prompts you to choose when to look for the field.")
2. Look for the custom agreement type on your selected historical agreements. Select the Find on specific agreements option.
3. Select the Activate option.

   The AI detection is published and the custom agreement type is active for new agreements.

   The Select documents to process page opens and a list of agreements is displayed. You are prompted to select at least one document to process for the new agreement type.

title: Activate Your Custom Agreement Types
text:
active for new agreements.

   The Select documents to process page opens and a list of agreements is displayed. You are prompted to select at least one document to process for the new agreement type.

   Note: The Find on specific agreements option can be used at any time to process specific documents for custom agreement types. See the [Find Custom Agreement Types in Specific Documents](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tskb991328c-dc5f-4d45-9617-dada91a5672a.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to use the Find On Specific Documents function to surface custom agreement types in selected agreements.") topic to learn more about this function.
4. Locate the documents that you want to process for the new agreement type:

   ![A snippet of the Select documents to process page that shows the search field and filter options to locate documents to process.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-Types-Manager_AI-detect-activated_Find-historical-docs.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2MDUsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.j0q4HjFlgQY-6eNypwrka_ZPRAvMb7JSFB93HlMarB4&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Select documents to process page that shows the search field and filter options to locate documents to process.")

   1. Search for the relevant agreements. Enter a keyword or search string in the search field. The dropdown opens and search suggestions are displayed. Select a search suggestion or press the Enter key.
   2. Filter the agreement list table to find the relevant agreements. For example, filter by Sets, Status, Parties, Agreement Type, or AI Suggestions.

      Tip: Use the All Filters option to apply additional filters to the results table.

      Expand the filter, choose the relevant filter value, and select the Apply option.

   The results table updates and the documents that match the search criteria and applied filters are displayed.
5. Choose the documents that you want to process.
6. Select the Start Processing option in the bottom right.

   The Agreement Types view opens. A message confirms that the selected documents are being checked for the new type.

   ![A screenshot of the message that states the selected documents are being checked for the new type.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-type-management_Enable-AI_check-for-type-in-selected-docs.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2MDUsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.j0q4HjFlgQY-6eNypwrka_ZPRAvMb7JSFB93HlMarB4&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the message that states the selected documents are being checked for the new type.")

   Tip: Select the View Progress option to view the processing status for the job. The job monitoring hub opens, and the Processing status and Job details are displayed. Review the processing results, including any error messages. Use the Close (x) icon to return to the Agreement Types view.
7. Return to the  homepage. Select the Close (x) icon located in the top right of the Manage Agreement Types page.

   You are returned to the Completed Documents view.

You have successfully activated the AI detection for a custom agreement type, including surfacing it in selected documents.

title: Use Case: Give Users Permission to View All Agreements in an Account
heading_path: Use Case: Give Users Permission to View All Agreements in an Account
text:
Learn how to assign permissions so users can view all agreements in Agreement Manager for their account. This answers the question: How do I give users access to view agreements in Agreement Manager?

In Agreement Manager, users can only view their own shared, received, sent, and uploaded agreements by default. An administrator can give users access to view all agreements in the account.

Note: User permissions are managed at the account-level. Make sure that you are updating records from Accounts administration instead of Organization administration.

1. Log in to your Docusign account using administrator credentials.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Admin from the global header.

   The Admin page opens.

   Important: User and group permissions are managed at the *account*-level. Make sure that you are updating records from Accounts administration instead of Organization administration.If your organization has multiple accounts, verify that you are updating the correct account. If necessary, select Accounts from the pane on the left and choose the appropriate account.
3. Select Users from the USERS AND GROUPS section in the navigation pane on the left.

   The Users view opens.
4. Locate the user whose Agreement Manager permissions you want to update:

   1. Expand the dropdown at the top of the page and select Name to search for users by name. You can also search by email address.
   2. Enter the user's name in the Enter Keyword field.
   3. Select Search.

   Users that match the entered search criteria are displayed in the table.
5. Expand the Actions option for the user that you want to update and select the Manage Agreement Manager Access option.

   The User Profile view opens for the selected user. The Agreement Manager Access tab is displayed by default.
6. Turn on the View all agreements in this account toggle (blue position) from the Agreement Manager access section.

   By turning on this toggle, you are giving the user access to view all agreements in the account.

   Note: Turn the toggle off (gray position) to remove the user's access to view all agreements in the account.
7. Select Save Changes.

   Next, go to the audit log to verify the changes.
8. Select Audit Logs from the AUDITING section located at the bottom of the navigation pane.

   The Audit Logs view opens. The audit log table displays records of changes to your account.
9. Locate the change in the table to verify the user's settings were successfully updated.

title: View the Activity Log for an Agreement
heading_path: View the Activity Log for an Agreement
text:
Learn how to access the Activity Log to view agreement-level events for a selected agreement.

The agreement-level Activity Log records and displays user-performed events for individual agreements. Recorded events include access changes, attribute updates, and agreement uploads, downloads, and views. For each event the date, user, action, item, and details are recorded and displayed. Events can take up to 15 minutes to display in the log.

Note: Your access to the agreement-level Activity Log is dependent on your access to the associated document. You need Can Manage agreement access to view the corresponding Activity Log:![A screenshot of the document-level access permissions.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Share-an-agreement_available-access-permissions.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc5MzAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.XCjHvogBh6WH9W7YkUE-QXpHkLC9SuHlxT1-HQgHSI0&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the document-level access permissions.")

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed.
4. Enter search criteria and apply filters to locate the agreement that you want to review.

   ![A snippet of the Completed Documents view that highlights the search field and available filters.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Navigator_Completed-documents_search-field-and-filters-highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc5MzAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.XCjHvogBh6WH9W7YkUE-QXpHkLC9SuHlxT1-HQgHSI0&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Completed Documents view that highlights the search field and available filters.")

   1. Enter a keyword or search string in the search field and select Enter.
   2. Filter the agreement list table. Expand the Filters option, select the relevant filter, choose a value, and select Apply.

   The table displays agreements matching the search criteria and filters that you applied.
5. Expand the More actions (three dots) menu for the agreement that you want to review and select Activity Log::

   ![A snippet of the Completed page that highlights the Activity Log option in the Actions menu for an agreement.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Activity-Log_access-from-Actions-menuCompleted-documents.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc5MzAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.XCjHvogBh6WH9W7YkUE-QXpHkLC9SuHlxT1-HQgHSI0&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Completed page that highlights the Activity Log option in the Actions menu for an agreement.")

   The Activity Log opens for the selected agreement and a list of agreement-level events are displayed. The events are displayed in reverse chronological order with the most recent at the top.

   The table displays 200 rows of recorded events. If additional events exist, you are prompted to download the table data to a CSV to view the full list of events.

title: View the Activity Log for an Agreement
heading_path: View the Activity Log for an Agreement
text:
r with the most recent at the top.

   The table displays 200 rows of recorded events. If additional events exist, you are prompted to download the table data to a CSV to view the full list of events.

   ![A screenshot of the Activity Log that shows recorded events and data fields for the selected agreement.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Activity-Log-example.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc5MzAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.XCjHvogBh6WH9W7YkUE-QXpHkLC9SuHlxT1-HQgHSI0&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Activity Log that shows recorded events and data fields for the selected agreement.")
6. Select the sorting arrows in the column headers to sort the table by DATE, USER, ACTION, ITEM, and DETAILS.
7. Review the table data to locate the relevant agreement-level event information. Details related to these user-performed events are recorded in the log:

   - Access changes
   - Agreement downloads
   - Attribute updates for select standard fields
   - Agreement views
   - Document uploads

   Note: The DATE column displays the date and time that the event was performed. The time zone is localized based on the account's time zone preference.
8. Select the Download CSV option.

   The CSV file is downloaded to your local Downloads folder.
9. Return to the Agreement Manager homepage. Select the Close (x) icon from the top-right corner.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed.

**View the Activity Log from the Agreement preview page:**

The agreement-level Activity Log can also be viewed from the Agreement preview page. Continue from the Completed page.

10. Select the agreement that you want to review from the agreement list.

    The Agreement preview page opens for the selected agreement.
11. Expand the dropdown in the top-right and select the Activity Log option:

    ![A snippet of the Agreement preview page that shows the Activity Log option in the dropdown.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Activity-Log_access-from-Agreement-Preview_Downloads-dropdown.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc5MzAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.XCjHvogBh6WH9W7YkUE-QXpHkLC9SuHlxT1-HQgHSI0&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Agreement preview page that shows the Activity Log option in the dropdown.")

    The Activity Log opens for the selected agreement and a list of agreement-level events are displayed.
12. Review the table data to locate the relevant agreement-level event information as needed. You can also download the audit events to a CSV.
13. Select the Close (X) icon from the top-right corner.

    The Activity Log closes and you are returned to the Agreement preview page.
14. Return to the Agreement Manager homepage. Select the Close (x) icon from the top-left corner.

    You are returned to the Agreement Manager homepage. The Completed Documents view is displayed.

title: Use Case: View Agreements Associated with a Specific Party
heading_path: Use Case: View Agreements Associated with a Specific Party
text:
This topic answers the question: How can I find all the agreements for a specific party? Learn how to filter the agreement list table to view the agreements that are tied to a specific party.

As a contract manager or analyst, you might want to view all existing agreements for a specific customer or vendor before preparing a new agreement. For example, apply filters to locate all master service agreements (MSAs) for a specific party and view data from previous agreements.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed Documents from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Completed Documents view opens. Use the available filters to locate master service agreements for a specific party.
4. Expand the Filters option and select the Parties filter.
5. Enter the name of the party in the Search field.
6. Choose a party from the list and select Apply.

   Agreements for the selected party are displayed in the agreement list table.
7. Select the party name from the Parties column.

   The party hub opens for the selected party or alias group. The Overview dashboard is displayed.
8. Select the Agreements tab.

   The Agreements tab opens and all the agreements that are tied to the selected party or alias group are listed.

   Further refine the list to locate specific types of agreements.
9. Expand the Agreement Type filter and enter Master Service Agreement in the Search field.
10. Choose Master Service Agreement and select Apply.

    The master service agreements for the selected party are displayed in the table.
11. Customize the table to show key data points for an upcoming renewal:
    1. Select the Show or Hide Fields (toggle) icon located on the right-side of the table.

       The Show or Hide Fields dialog box opens. These fields represent the columns in the table.
    2. Select the toggles to turn the associated field on (blue) or off (white).
    3. Re-order the columns as needed. Hover the cursor over the Reorder (dots) icon for the field that you want to move and drag it to the appropriate location.
    4. Select Save.

    The dialog box closes and the columns are updated according to your configuration.
12. Select one or more agreements to download or export to a CSV file.
    1. Select the agreements that you want to export.
    2. Select Download Documents.

       A zipped folder containing the selected agreements is downloaded to your local Downloads folder.
    3. Select Export to CSV.

       The CSV file is downloaded to your local Downloads folder.
13. Return to the Agreement Manager homepage. Select the Completed option from the navigation pane on the left.

    The Completed Documents view opens. Your previously entered search criteria and applied filters are still in effect.

title: Agreement Types Across the IAM Platform
heading_path: Agreement Types Across the IAM Platform
text:
Read about how agreement types are used across the Intelligent Agreement Management (IAM) platform.

In the Docusign IAM platform, an agreement type identifies what kind of document an agreement is, including applicable fields. For example, purchase orders (POs), non-disclosure agreements (NDAs), or master service agreements (MSAs). Each type has its own set of key fields, like parties, term dates, and contract value.

Agreement types and fields are defined once in a central data model and reused across IAM
experiences. For example, they are used in Agreement Preparation, eSignature, CLM,
Agreement Manager, and workflows. This shared model helps teams to:

- Capture consistent agreement data
- Reduce duplicate configuration
- Align downstream reporting

This topic reviews how agreement types are used in the IAM workflow.

title: Agreement Types Across the IAM Platform
heading_path: Agreement Types Across the IAM Platform
How IAM products use agreement types
text:
Agreement types are used to classify documents and drive which fields are relevant throughout the agreement lifecycle. These fields are centrally stored and can be reused across IAM surfaces. Here's how agreement types are used across the IAM platform:

- **Agreement Preparation:** Agreement types are auto-detected at upload. Based on the identified type, suggested sender fields are surfaced to tag and merge into templates. The types and fields are stored and later used in Agreement Manager. Administrators and contract managers can manage the types and field centrally from Agreement Manager.
- **eSignature:** Templates require users to select an agreement type so that relevant fields are recommended. Envelope- or document-level types and fields provide consistent data points for downstream processes and reporting.
- **Contract Lifecycle Management (CLM):** Defined agreement types and fields are adopted and used in negotiation workflows. CLM-specific attributes that may not be exposed outside of CLM are also supported.
- **Agreement Manager:** Completed agreements with extracted attributes, including the agreement type, are stored in Agreement Manager. Users can filter, sort, and report on agreement data by type or field value. Administrators and contract managers can centrally manage standard and custom agreement types and associated fields. In addition to predefined agreement types, users can add custom types to meet the specific needs of their organization.
- **AI-Assisted Review:** Playbooks are organized by agreement types to ensure that reviews are aligned to type-specific standards. This feature is available to CLM and select IAM plans.
