title: Rules for Updating the Spreadsheet File
heading_path: Rules for Updating the Spreadsheet File
text:
Read about important rules for updating the pre-formatted spreadsheet file.

The *Bulk Metadata Editing* feature uses a pre-formatted spreadsheet to update the agreement data for a group of documents. This process relies on strict adherence to file structure and field-level formatting rules.

Tip: Bookmark the [Formatting Requirements for Metadata Editing](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=refa47e5582-efd8-403b-b39c-45a0c05beb1f.html&_LANG=enus "Look up spreadsheet rules and formatting requirements for using the Bulk Metadata Edit.") article for future reference. For example, to verify the spreadsheet rules and field format requirements.

**File structure implications**

File structure rules relate to the column and row structure of the pre-formatted spreadsheet. Retaining the structure of the Docusign-generated, pre-formatted spreadsheet is key to successfully using the *Bulk Metadata Editing* feature.

If the file structure does not match the pre-formatted spreadsheet (template), the upload is rejected. For example, if you upload the wrong spreadsheet file, such as a CSV file generated from another Agreement Manager feature.

**FIeld-level rule implications:**

The field-level rules apply to the cell values that you enter in the spreadsheet. The columns in the spreadsheet represent the custom and standard fields in your account. Formatting rules are in place to ensure consistency and data normalization.

When you import the updated spreadsheet, if a field value (cell) fails validation, Agreement Manager:

- Applies all the valid field values in the spreadsheet.
- Skips the invalid field values (cells). The files that fail to update and are logged in the Error details table for the upload job. The error log includes field-specific error messages for each of the failed files.

**View your error details**

After importing the data file, you will receive an email notification when the upload job is completed. The email shows the number of files that failed to update, including an option to view details.

Go to the Job status page for the upload job to review the Error details for the failed files:

![A snippet of the Job status page for an upload job that shows the Error details table.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Bulk-metadata-editing_Rules_Invalid-fields_Error-details_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY3ODMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.CBnZlzAf_V1a1RPnJv5ELBfpXT2CBWvefKFPcEIfP28&_LANG=enus)

Review the error message and correct the value in the metadata spreadsheet. After correcting all the errors, import the data file again. Continue this process until all the errors are addressed. See the [Review Job Results from the Notification Email](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tske7ac3afe-c04e-4acc-95b1-9f83b1b66ed5.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to view the results of an upload job from the email notification. A system-generated email is sent when the upload process is completed.") article for steps to complete this task.

title: Rules for Updating the Spreadsheet File
heading_path: Rules for Updating the Spreadsheet File
text:
When you edit the pre-formatted spreadsheet file, follow the file structure rules to ensure a successful data import. If the file structure of your spreadsheet does not match the original template, the upload is rejected.

| Rule | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Start from the Docusign-generated, pre-formatted spreadsheet. | Always download a fresh spreadsheet for the agreements that you want to update. Edit only the data cells. Do not edit the header row or mapping values. |
| Do not edit the header row. | Keep the column names exactly as they are displayed in the downloaded file. |
| Do not edit the field-mapping values. | The second row of the spreadsheet contains mapping information for the fields. Do not edit these values. |
| Do not edit, reorder, or remove the first three columns, Agreement ID, File Name, and Agreement Type. | The first three columns in the spreadsheet are used to map the agreement metadata to the correct agreements. Do not change these columns to avoid errors during the upload process. |
| Do not add columns. | Keep the original column layout to ensure a successful upload. Tip: Instead of removing or reordering the columns in the spreadsheet, use the Hide function to hide the unnecessary columns. While you can remove and reorder the columns, it is not recommended. Retain the original file structure as much as possible to avoid errors. |
| One row per agreement. | The pre-formatted spreadsheet is populated with identifying and mapping data for the selected documents. Each row represents an agreement.  - Do not add extra rows. - Do not delete the agreement ID for any row. - Do not update the agreement type for any row. |
| Do not change the Agreement ID column. | Agreement Manager relies on the agreement ID to apply the metadata changes to the correct agreement.  - Do not edit the agreement ID values. - Do not add agreement ID values. - Do not remove agreement ID values. |

title: Rules for Updating the Spreadsheet File
heading_path: Rules for Updating the Spreadsheet File
text:
The field-level rules dictate the appropriate formats for editing your agreement data. When fields fail validation due to formatting errors, the details are listed on the Job status page.

| Field type | Formatting rule |
| --- | --- |
| Text fields | There is a 2,000 character limit for custom text fields and a 400-character limit for standard fields.  Important: Depending on how the standard fields are used across the platform, the maximum character limit can vary.  When the value exceeds the maximum, it fails validation and an error is logged in the job monitoring hub. |
| Date fields | Date fields use the standard, U.S. format, MM-DD-YYYY. |
| Number fields | Numbers are entered without commas. For example, 10000 (not 10,000). Note: Decimals are approved where the field supports them. For example, 2500.75. |
| Currency fields | Enter an amount followed by the appropriate [ISO 4217 currency code](https://www.iso.org/iso-4217-currency-codes.html). Use the format, [Number][Space][ISO currency code]. For example, 25 USD. |
| Duration fields | Duration fields use the format, [Number][Space][Time unit]. For example, 1 Year, 6 Months, 3 Weeks, 10 Days. |
| Multiple-value fields | When fields contain multiple values, complete the field as a comma-separated list of values. Note: This option is available for custom fields that were configured for multiple-values. If you enter multiple values for a field that was not defined for this type of input, the field fails to update. |
| Party and Role fields | The Parties value is entered with both the party name and role for each party:  - Separate the party name and role with a vertical line (pipe) divider. - Separate party values with a semi-colon. - Use the format: [Name] | [Role]; [Name] | [Role];   For example, PartyA (Buyer) and PartyB (Seller) is entered as: PartyA | Buyer; PartyB | Seller;  Note: When updating the Parties column, the Role is required. If you are unsure of the party role or do not want to include it, use the format: [Name] | [None]. |
| Dropdown fields | Dropdown fields must contain data that matches at least one of the expected dropdown values. For example, if you enter a value that is not a valid option for that field, the field fails validation. |

title: Rules for Updating the Spreadsheet File
heading_path: Rules for Updating the Spreadsheet File
text:
When you import the updated spreadsheet file, your edited values replace the current agreement data. This action includes the extracted values. Additionally, your user-provided metadata is considered "confirmed" and the fields are excluded from future updates. For example, when the document is reprocessed for additional data points.

Important: Review your spreadsheet carefully before uploading it to Agreement Manager. Once the job succeeds, the imported values cannot be undone. You cannot revert to the previous agreement data.

If you cleared values from the pre-formatted spreadsheet, the upload process removes the existing value. When you clear a value, any user-provided data is deleted and the field is empty. A new value is generated the next time the agreement is processed.

title: Party Management for CLM Accounts with IAM Plans
heading_path: Party Management for CLM Accounts with IAM Plans
text:
Read about party management for CLM accounts with IAM plans. Stakeholders use the IAM platform to manage parties, access party reports, and use Docusign AI to surface party information.

Parties are the people or organizations that you do business with. Docusign CLM accounts with an IAM plan integrate with the Docusign Intelligent Agreement Management (IAM) platform. The platform provides one central place to manage all of your parties.

Important: This party management experience is available only for CLM accounts with an IAM plan. For more information about IAM or IAM plans, see the [IntelligentAgreement Management website](https://www.docusign.com/intelligent-agreement-management) or contact your account representative.

This party management experience also includes the following capabilities:

- Access reports about party agreements
- Use Docusign AI to surface party information during agreement ingestion
- See obligations and insights for each party

Note:

- Party attributes updated in Agreement Manager on a completed agreement will not be updated in CLM.
- For CLM parties to appear and to be in sync in IAM, each party must be mapped to a standard party attribute in CLM. If the party is already mapped to a different custom attribute, contact Professional Services to update your attributes by mapping them to the IAM party field.

For complete information about the party management experience in IAM, see the [Agreement Manager documentation](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=bpv1742428468463.html&_LANG=enus).

title: Agreement Manager Reprocessing Experience
text:
Learn about the Agreement Manager reprocessing experience, including its purpose, trigger points,
and job size considerations.

When organizations are first onboarded to Agreement Manager, their historical documents are ingested and processed. During processing, attributes and field values are surfaced based on the current AI model. When new or updated models are available, the previously ingested and uploaded documents are not reprocessed for the new insights.

The new or updated AI models include a number of updates that require reprocessing:

- **New extraction sets:** sets of related activities such as Human Resources (HR) and Obligations
- **New extractions:** single attributes, such as Line of Business and Party Role
- **New languages:** expanded support for new languages, such as Brazilian-Portuguese, French, German, Japanese, and Spanish (Mexico, Spain)
- **Accuracy improvements**
- **Bug fixes**

Tip: Reprocess your historical documents when new custom agreement types and fields are available. For example, select specific agreements to reprocess for a new agreement type.

Users can only benefit from these updates by reprocessing their agreements for the latest AI models.

title: Agreement Manager Reprocessing Experience
text:
During processing, key data points are extracted based on the original AI model that was used during onboarding. These extractions populate the agreement insights that you use to manage your agreements, renewals, and obligations.

When new or updated AI models are released, your agreement insights become outdated and unreliable - possibly missing key data points.

Reprocessing is necessary to ensure that your searches, filters, and reports provide reliable and accurate agreement insights.

title: Agreement Manager Reprocessing Experience
text:
Depending on the type of insights and enhancements that are available with the new AI model, users can choose to:

- Opt in for reprocessing from the Track obligations badge (obligation-related attributes and insights)
- Reprocess all the outdated agreements for the account (new or updated AI model)
- Reprocess selected agreements (custom and standard data agreement types and fields)

title: Agreement Manager Reprocessing Experience
text:
Each type of reprocessing experience has its own triggers or entry points. For example, the Track obligations badge is displayed when new obligation-related attributes are available: ![A screenshot of the expanded Track obligations badge that prompts you to run the latest AI model on your agreements.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reprocessing_New-Insights-pill_expanded.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc4NzksInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.g0Tr-SDRU3ZbDYUpph0lfN-gmr8tbFnnFO5DS9idvlU&_LANG=enus)

Likewise, you can manually reprocess groups of selected agreements to surface new standard and custom data points:![A snippet of the Completed Documents page that highlights the Update Details dropdown and data options.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reprocessing_Completed-page_Update-Details_expanded.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc4NzksInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.g0Tr-SDRU3ZbDYUpph0lfN-gmr8tbFnnFO5DS9idvlU&_LANG=enus)

When users interact with insights that were released since their initial onboarding, they are prompted to update their AI model:

![A snippet of the Completed Documents view that highlights the banner prompt to Update Now to the latest AI version.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reprocessing_banner-messaging_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc4NzksInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.g0Tr-SDRU3ZbDYUpph0lfN-gmr8tbFnnFO5DS9idvlU&_LANG=enus)

Note: The banner message is triggered when users apply relevant filters from the agreement list table located on the Completed Documents page. Using these filters from other locations, such as in the party hub or under the Insights menu, does *not* trigger the banner message.

The banner message and prompt are triggered when the certain criteria are met:

- The user applies a filter from the Completed Documents page that is not
  available with their current AI model. For example, the banner is
  triggered if you apply the Line of Business,
  Party Role, or Status
  filters.
- The account contains agreements with outdated extractions.

Every user has access to initiate the reprocessing job for all the agreements in their account -
regardless of the account size.

title: Agreement Manager Reprocessing Experience
text:
The reprocessing job happens in the background. While it does not impact your current experience, processing times vary depending on the size of the job. Other items to consider:

| Job size | Consideration |
| --- | --- |
| Less than 1,000 documents | Reprocessing jobs with less than 1,000 documents are sent to the "live" queue. The "live" queue is used for manually initiated processing jobs, such as agreement uploads and imports. |
| More than 1,000 documents | Reprocessing jobs with more than 1,000 documents are sent to the "historical" queue. The "historical" queue is used for larger jobs, such as batch processing and onboarding new organizations. |
| More than 25,000 documents | Reprocessing jobs with more than 25,000 documents are batch processed. The job is separated into batches of 25,000 each and sent to the "historical" queue. Note: Batch processing jobs have a built-in time delay of 2 days. |

Once started, processing cannot be canceled.

title: Agreement Manager Reprocessing Experience
text:
Review these frequently asked questions to learn more about agreement reprocessing:

| Question | Answer |
| --- | --- |
| Can I reprocess agreements? | Yes. Any user on the account can trigger reprocessing jobs. |
| What if some of my agreements are up to date? | The reprocessing job automatically skips any documents that are currently up to date. |
| Will my existing agreement data be overwritten? | The reprocessing job may result in the surfaced data points being recalculated and overwritten:  - For values that were manually entered or marked as   "accepted", the reprocessing job does *not*   overwrite or alter these values. - For fields that contain multiple values, the   reprocessing job may surface additional values -   even if the fields were "accepted". |
| Are my agreement sets impacted by reprocessing? | If you use smart filters with your agreement sets, the reprocessing *could* change which documents are stored in a set. For example, an agreement was classified as a non-disclosure agreement (NDA) and added to a set based on a smart filter. If reprocessing results in the agreement's type being changed to a master service agreement (MSA), the agreement will be removed from its current set.  Important: If an agreement was manually added to an agreement set, the agreement remains in the set even if the surfaced values are changed during reprocessing. |

title: Agreement Manager Reprocessing Experience
text:
When new obligation-related attributes are available, the Track obligations badge is displayed on the Completed Documents page. For example, when an account is enabled for obligations. This badge indicates that you are not using the latest AI model.

Select the Track obligations badge to get started:

![A screenshot of the expanded Track obligations badge that shows information related to processing agreements for added insights.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reprocessing_New-Insights-pill_expanded.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc4NzksInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.g0Tr-SDRU3ZbDYUpph0lfN-gmr8tbFnnFO5DS9idvlU&_LANG=enus)

Select Start Now to opt in for additional processing. Another dialog box opens:

![A screenshot of the dialog box that contains the opt-in toggle for processing your agreements for new insights.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reprocessing_Turn-on-processing-toggle.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc4NzksInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.g0Tr-SDRU3ZbDYUpph0lfN-gmr8tbFnnFO5DS9idvlU&_LANG=enus)

Turn on the Process for additional insights toggle (blue position) and select the Save Changes option. The dialog box closes and a message states that your agreements are being processed for additional AI insights.

Note: Once the toggle is activated, it cannot be turned off. Also, you can no longer deactivate processing. Agreements will be processed based on the user's account access. For example, if the user has obligation management as part of their plan, opting in for additional processing includes obligation-related insights.

Review the [Extracted Agreement Insights and Definitions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=uwk1702945852405.html&_LANG=enus "Look up the names and definitions for the attributes and values that are surfaced in Agreement Manager agreements with AI. This topic answers the question: What data is being extracted from my Agreement Manager agreements?") article to learn more about the insights being surfaced in your agreements with AI.

title: English (Global) Language Support
text:
Read about the Agreement Manager AI models and features that are supported globally for English agreements.

Agreement Manager is available to customers globally, however, the features and insights vary by region and language. Read about the supported agreement types, attributes, and data points that provide insights into your English (global) agreements.

CAUTION: The accuracy and appropriateness of AI-assisted insights may vary depending on the agreement language and customer use case. Customers should review all suggested data points for accuracy and to determine if a value is appropriate for its particular circumstances.

See the [Language and Regional Support](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=xvp1729795957126.html&_LANG=enus "Read about language support and regional availability for Agreement Manager AI models and features.") article to view supported agreement types, attributes, and data points for other languages and regions.

title: English (Global) Language Support
text:
During agreement processing, attributes and field values are surfaced in your agreements. Review the table to see which attributes are surfaced globally in English agreements.

Important: English (global) is applied to all customers deployed across Docusign datacenters located in the United States (NA), Europe (EU), Canada (CA), Australia (AU), and Japan (JP).

title: English (Global) Language Support
text:
h agreements.

Important: English (global) is applied to all customers deployed across Docusign datacenters located in the United States (NA), Europe (EU), Canada (CA), Australia (AU), and Japan (JP).

| Attribute | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Assignment and Change of Control Insights** | |
| Assignment (Change of Control) | Indicates whether the agreement allows for the assignment of rights and obligations to another party in case of M&A or other change of control activity. |
| Assignment (General) | Indicates whether the agreement allows one of the parties to assign its rights and obligations to another party. |
| Assignment (Termination Rights) | Indicates whether the agreement allows for termination if one of the parties assigns its rights and obligations to another party. |
| **Compensation and Payment Insights** | |
| Annual Contract Value | The average yearly revenue generated from a contract or customer subscription. It is commonly used in subscription-based businesses to measure the annualized value of a contract, helping organizations track recurring revenue over time. |
| Total Contract Value | The total monetary value of the contract or agreement between two or more parties. In other words, it is the lifetime value of a contract. |
| Late Fees Apply | Indicates whether the agreement explicitly states that if payment is late fees, charges, or interest will be added. |
| Late Fee % | The percentage of the contract value that is charged when late fees are incurred. |
| Payment Terms | The conditions set by a seller regarding when and how payment for goods or services is expected from the buyer. These terms typically specify the time frame for payment, for example, *Net 30* means payment is due 30 days after the invoice date. |
| Price Cap Increase % | The maximum allowable price increase under the agreement. |
| **Confidentiality Insights** | |
| Duration of Confidentiality | Indicates the length of time that the obligations of an NDA remain in effect. |
| **Governing Law, Jurisdiction, and Dispute Resolution Insights** | |
| Governing Law | The rules and laws that will govern the agreement if legal issues or disputes arise. The clause typically identifies whose laws will govern the interpretation and enforcement of the agreement. |
| Jurisdiction | The legal jurisdiction of an agreement in case of a future dispute. This value is typically listed as a specific county or city. |
| **Limitation of Liability Insights** | |
| Liability Cap Amount | The maximum amount or limit that a party will pay for damages or any liability in a specific dollar amount. |
| Liability Cap Duration | Indicates the length of time during which claims can be made under the liability cap within the agreement. |
| Liability Cap Multiplier | The limit a party will pay for damages or any liability, including a multiplier that can increase the cap amount under certain conditions. |
| **Objective Information Insights** | |
| Execution Date | The official date that the agreement was signed by all parties. |
| Languages | Indicates which languages are present in the agreement. |
| NDA Type | Indicates the type of NDA that is in place for the agreement. For example, mutual or one-way. |
| Parties | Contracting parties. Refers to a provision naming the individuals, organizations, or entities that enter into a legally binding agreement (contract). |
| Title | The official title of the agreement. |
| Type (Agreement Type) | Classification of documents based on their title and certain commonalities in their subject matter. For example, Master Services Agreement (MSA), Non-Disclosure Agreements (NDA), and Statement of Work (SOW). |
| **Renewal Insights** | |
| Automatic Renewal (Renewal Type) | Renewal type. Refers to provisions stating that an agreement automatically renews at the end of its term unless one or both parties take action to terminate, opt out, or modify it. |
| Evergreen (Renewal Type) | Renewal type. Refers to clauses that contemplate a perpetual continuation of the contract term unless a party takes action to terminate it. |
| Option to Renew (Renewal Type) | Renewal type. Refers to provisions that set out a party's option to extend the term of the contract. |
| NonRenewal Notice Period | Refers to the period of time required for a party to give notice of their intent not to renew an agreement. |
| Renewal - Extension Period | Refers to provisions setting out the additional period of time (duration) that a contract is to be extended beyond its initial term. |
| Renewal Notice Period | Comprised of two data extractions: Renewal Notice Period and NonRenewal Notice Period. Refers to the period of time required for a party to give notice of their intent to renew an agreement. |
| Term - Renewal Automatic | Refers to provisions that specify the duration of the auto-renewal period. |
| Term - Renewal Option | Refers to provisions setting out a party's option to extend the contract for a specific term. |
| **Term Insights** | |
| Effective Date | Refers to the date on which the contract became legally binding and enforceable. It is the date agreed upon by the parties for beginning the period of performance under the contract. |
| Expiration Date | Refers to the date on which the contract comes to an end and is no longer in force. |
| Term Length | Indicates the length of the term of the agreement (how long the agreement is in effect). Signing parties are obligated to adhere to the terms and conditions within the agreement until the expiration or end date. |
| **Termination Insights** | |
| Termination for Cause - Notice Period | Refers to provisions setting out the notice period required for a party to terminate a contract for cause. For example, for material breach or other events of default. |
| Termination for Convenience - Notice Period | Refers to provisions setting out the notice period required for a party to terminate a contract for convenience. For example, without a specific reason for doing so and without having to show any breach or default by the other party. |

title: English (Global) Language Support
heading_path: Limitation: "Dropdown" attributes (fields) with pre-populated value sets
text:
For attributes (fields) that use dropdowns with pre-populated value sets, only those values are surfaced in your agreements. Agreement processing extracts an attribute value only if it matches one of the values in the dropdown.

When processing cannot locate the value within the agreement, the extracted value is *Other*. For example, if an agreement's payment term is 50 days, the surfaced Payment Term value is *Other*:

![A screenshot of the Party Term filter that shows the pre-populated value set for the attribute (field).](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Payment-term-filter_All-options.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTYxMDcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.kFNfhB8bXn1heY0YExiZgD8azBS7WCmozE419YSWX6g&_LANG=enus)

This limitation applies to these attributes (fields) and values:

| Attribute (Field) | Value Set |
| --- | --- |
| Assignment (Change of Control) | No, or consent required; Yes, notice required; Yes, restrictions apply; Yes, with conditions |
| Assignment (General) | No, or consent required; Yes; Yes, with conditions |
| Assignment (Termination Rights) | Yes |
| Employment Type | Fixed Term; Full Time; Intern; Part Time |
| Late Fees Apply | Yes; No |
| NDA Type | Mutual; One Way |
| Party Role | Buyer, Seller |
| Payment Term | 15 days; 30 days; 45 days; 60 days; 7 days; 90 days; >90 days; On Receipt; Other; Under 7 days |
| Renewal Type | Auto-Renew; Evergreen; Fixed Term; Renewal Option; Unspecified |

title: Agreement Statuses
text:
Read about agreement statuses, including how they are calculated, visibility within Agreement Manager, and which types of statuses are being tracked.

Agreement statuses provide a quick look at where the agreement is within its life cycle. Users gain clarity on which agreements are active or inactive without manual tracking:

![A screenshot of the Completed Documents view that highlights the Status column in the agreement list table.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-statuses_Completed-view_Status-column_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY1NTUsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.-7zu4GnWbqwoYo98Shf7cWEeh7sAibIQKBEH51Ardc0&_LANG=enus)

These statuses are calculated based on an agreement's extracted Effective Date, Expiration Date, and Renewal Type. The statuses are re-calculated and updated daily, based on the current date.

Note: A Renewal Type value is not required for calculating an agreement's status. However, if an extracted value exists, it is taken into consideration when calculating the status. For example, if an agreement has an Expiration Date and the Renewal Type is Evergreen, the agreement is always Active.

There are two types of agreement statuses:

- **Primary status:** indicates whether an agreement is *active* or *inactive*.
- **Secondary status:** defines the agreement's current state, such as *expiring soon*, *renewing soon*, and *effective soon*.

title: Agreement Statuses
text:
The primary statuses are calculated based on an agreement's extracted Effective Date and Expiration Date values. If the agreement has an extracted Renewal Type, this value is also taken into consideration when calculating the status.

These statuses indicate whether the agreement is Active or Inactive:

![A snippet of the agreement list table that highlights examples of the primary status in the Status column.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-statuses_Primary-statuses_highlighed.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY1NTUsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.-7zu4GnWbqwoYo98Shf7cWEeh7sAibIQKBEH51Ardc0&_LANG=enus)

| Status | Calculation Explanation |
| --- | --- |
| Active | The agreement is in-effect, or active. Active agreements are calculated based on the effective and expiration dates:  - Effective date is equal to or before the current date (the agreement is "active") - Expiration date is equal to or after the current date (the agreement has not "expired")  For agreements that are missing either the effective date or the expiration date:  - No  Expiration date, but the Effective date is equal to or before the current date (the agreement is Active) - No Effective date, but the  Expiration date is equal to or after the current date (the agreement is Active) |
| Inactive | The agreement is not in-effect, or inactive. Inactive agreements are calculated based on the effective and expiration dates:  - Effective date is after the current date (the agreement is "not yet effective") - Expiration date is before the current date (the agreement has "expired")  Important: If both the Effective date and the Expiration date are missing or null, the agreement is Inactive. |

Note: If the agreement has an extracted Renewal Type, this value is also taken into consideration when calculating the status.

When available, AI-generated sub-text is displayed under the primary status:

![A snippet of the agreement list table that highlights examples of the Status sub-text.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-statuses_Status-column_subtext_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY1NTUsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.-7zu4GnWbqwoYo98Shf7cWEeh7sAibIQKBEH51Ardc0&_LANG=enus)

This sub-text is generated based on the agreement's extracted Effective
Date, Expiration Date, and Renewal Type. For example, the
sub-text indicates when an agreement is expiring or renewing and displays the
relevant date.

title: Agreement Statuses
text:
While the primary statuses define whether an agreement is Active or
Inactive, the secondary statuses define the agreement's
current state. For example: Renewing Soon,
Expiring Soon, and becoming Effective
Soon:

![A snippet of the agreement list table that highlights examples of secondary statuses.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-statuses_Secondary-statuses_highlighed.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY1NTUsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.-7zu4GnWbqwoYo98Shf7cWEeh7sAibIQKBEH51Ardc0&_LANG=enus)

As with primary statuses, if an agreement has an extracted Renewal Type, this value is taken into consideration when calculating the secondary status.

| Primary Status | Secondary Status | Calculation Explanation |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Active | Expiring Soon | These agreements are Active but will expire soon:  - Expiration date is within 30 days of the current date - Renewal type is not Auto-Renew or Evergreen |
| Active | Renewing Soon | These agreements are Active and will renew soon:  - Expiration date is within 30 days of the current date - Renewal type is Auto-Renew or Evergreen |
| Inactive | Effective Soon | These agreements are Inactive and are not yet effective:  - Effective date is within 30 days of the current date |

The associated renewal, expiration, or effective date is displayed in the sub-text for the secondary status.

Note: The secondary statuses are only displayed in the agreement list table in Agreement Manager. These statuses do *not* display for the agreement lists found under the Insights menu.

title: Agreement Statuses
text:
Once identified, use the Status filter to filter the agreement list table by status:

![A screenshot of the Status filter that shows selected filter values.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-statuses_Status-filter.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY1NTUsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.-7zu4GnWbqwoYo98Shf7cWEeh7sAibIQKBEH51Ardc0&_LANG=enus)Select the Filters option and locate the Status filter. Expand the filter, choose the relevant agreement statuses, and select Apply. The agreement list updates and the documents that match the applied filters are displayed in the table.

title: Agreement Statuses
heading_path: Status column and filter in Agreement Manager
text:
The Status column and filter are associated with the agreement list table and can be found on the:

- Completed page
- Agreements page in the party hub
- Detailed report view for a selected dashboard or report

Important: If you previously customized the columns in the agreement list table, you must manually display the Status column in the table. See the [Display the Status Column in the Agreement List Table](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tsk57b92226-d6e4-4725-99c4-79ebf42e9f8b.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to customize the agreement list table to display agreement status information.") topic to learn about this task.

title: Docusign Agreement Manager for CLM
text:
Read about Agreement Manager for CLM customers with IAM Plans.

Docusign Agreement Manager for CLM enables organizations to store, manage, and analyze agreements. The Agreement Manager for CLM integration automatically organizes CLM agreements data for easier discovery and analysis. Agreement Manager for CLM is a unified repository of both CLM and eSignature documents.

With Agreement Manager, you can:

- Centralize agreements and empower your most valuable teams to find what they need fast.
- Use AI to uncover cost-saving opportunities by harnessing agreement data.
- Use AI to organize agreements into parties for better portfolio analysis and visibility.

To learn more, see the documentation about [Docusign Agreement Manager](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=adf1702945446135.html&_LANG=enus).

Note:

- Docusign Agreement Manager integration is available only for CLM customers who have purchased an Intelligent Agreement Management (IAM) Enterprise or Sales plan. For more information, contact your account representative.
- Agreement Manager leverages artificial intelligence (AI) to provide insight to your agreements. For complete information, see the documentation about [AI denotation in Agreement Manager](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=rsz1722973735168.html&_LANG=enus) and [AI Data Controls Setting FAQs](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fzd1707173174972&topicId=dcs1708531444159.html&_LANG=enus) .

title: German Language Support
text:
Read about the Agreement Manager AI models and features that are supported for German agreements.

Agreement Manager is available to customers globally, however, the features and insights vary by region and language. Read about the supported agreement types, attributes, and data points that provide insights into your German agreements.

CAUTION: The accuracy and appropriateness of AI-assisted insights may vary depending on the agreement language and customer use case. Customers should review all suggested data points for accuracy and to determine if a value is appropriate for its particular circumstances.

Important:

As new or updated AI models are released, your historical agreements can be processed again to surface these data points. Depending on the type of new insights that are available, users can choose to:

- Opt in for reprocessing from the New Insights Available badge (obligation-related attributes and insights)
- Reprocess all the outdated agreements for the account (new or updated AI model)
- Reprocess selected agreements (standard or custom fields)

See the [Agreement Manager Reprocessing Experience](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=con44590246-0f0e-43b4-9692-caadb57335ac.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about the Agreement Manager reprocessing experience, including its purpose, trigger points, and job size considerations.") article to learn more about this experience.

See the [Language and Regional Support](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=xvp1729795957126.html&_LANG=enus "Read about language support and regional availability for Agreement Manager AI models and features.") article to view supported agreement types, attributes, and data points for other languages and regions.

title: German Language Support
text:
During agreement processing, attributes and field values are surfaced in your agreements. Review the table to see which attributes are surfaced in German agreements.

Important: English (global) is applied to all customers deployed across Docusign datacenters located in the United States (NA), Europe (EU), Canada (CA), Australia (AU), and Japan (JP).

title: German Language Support
text:
n agreements.

Important: English (global) is applied to all customers deployed across Docusign datacenters located in the United States (NA), Europe (EU), Canada (CA), Australia (AU), and Japan (JP).

| Attribute | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Assignment and Change of Control Insights** | |
| Assignment (Change of Control) | Indicates whether the agreement allows for the assignment of rights and obligations to another party. For example, in case of a merger and acquisition (M&A) or other change of control activity. |
| Assignment (General) | Indicates whether the agreement allows one of the parties to assign its rights and obligations to another party. |
| Assignment (Termination Rights) | Indicates whether the agreement allows for termination if one of the parties assigns its rights and obligations to another party. |
| **Compensation and Payment Insights** | |
| Annual Contract Value | The average yearly revenue generated from a contract or customer subscription. It is commonly used in subscription-based businesses to measure the annualized value of a contract, helping organizations track recurring revenue over time. |
| Total Contract Value | The total monetary value of the contract or agreement between two or more parties. In other words, it is the lifetime value of a contract. |
| Late Fees Apply | Indicates whether the agreement explicitly states that if payment is late fees, charges, or interest will be added. |
| Late Fee % | The percentage of the contract value that is charged when late fees are incurred. |
| Payment Terms | The conditions set by a seller regarding when and how payment for goods or services is expected from the buyer. These terms typically specify the time frame for payment, for example, *Net 30* means payment is due 30 days after the invoice date. |
| Price Cap Increase % | The maximum allowable price increase under the agreement. |
| **Confidentiality Insights** | |
| Duration of Confidentiality | Indicates the length of time that the obligations of a non-disclosure agreement (NDA) remain in effect. |
| **Governing Law, Jurisdiction, and Dispute Resolution Insights** | |
| Governing Law | The rules and laws that will govern the agreement if legal issues or disputes arise. The clause typically identifies whose laws will govern the interpretation and enforcement of the agreement. |
| Jurisdiction | The legal jurisdiction of an agreement in case of a future dispute. This value is typically listed as a specific county or city. |
| **Limitation of Liability Insights** | |
| Liability Cap Amount | The maximum amount or limit that a party will pay for damages or any liability in a specific dollar amount. |
| Liability Cap Duration | Indicates the length of time during which claims can be made under the liability cap within the agreement. |
| Liability Cap Multiplier | The limit a party will pay for damages or any liability, including a multiplier that can increase the cap amount under certain conditions. |
| **Objective Information Insights** | |
| Execution Date | The official date that the agreement was signed by all parties. |
| Languages | Indicates which languages are present in the agreement. |
| NDA Type | Indicates the type of NDA that is in place for the agreement. For example, mutual or one-way. |
| Parties | Contracting parties. Refers to a provision naming the individuals, organizations, or entities that enter into a legally binding agreement (contract). |
| Title | The official title of the agreement. |
| Type (Agreement Type) | Classification of documents based on their title and certain commonalities in their subject matter. For example, Master Services Agreement (MSA), Non-Disclosure Agreements (NDA), and Statement of Work (SOW). |
| **Renewal Insights** | |
| Automatic Renewal (Renewal Type) | Renewal type. Refers to provisions that state an agreement automatically renews at the end of its term unless one or both parties take action to terminate, opt out, or modify it. |
| Evergreen (Renewal Type) | Renewal type. Refers to clauses that contemplate a perpetual continuation of the contract term unless a party takes action to terminate it. |
| Option to Renew (Renewal Type) | Renewal type. Refers to provisions that set out a party's option to extend the term of the contract. |
| NonRenewal Notice Period | Refers to the period of time required for a party to give notice of their intent not to renew an agreement. |
| Renewal - Extension Period | Refers to provisions setting out the additional period of time (duration) that a contract is to be extended beyond its initial term. |
| Renewal Notice Period | Comprised of two data extractions: Renewal Notice Period and NonRenewal Notice Period. Refers to the period of time required for a party to give notice of their intent to renew an agreement. |
| Term - Renewal Automatic | Refers to provisions that specify the duration of the auto-renewal period. |
| Term - Renewal Option | Refers to provisions setting out a party's option to extend the contract for a specific term. |
| **Term Insights** | |
| Effective Date | Refers to the date on which the contract became legally binding and enforceable. It is the date agreed upon by the parties for beginning the period of performance under the contract. |
| Expiration Date | Refers to the date on which the contract comes to an end and is no longer in force. |
| Term Length | Indicates the length of the term of the agreement (how long the agreement is in effect). Signing parties are obligated to adhere to the terms and conditions within the agreement until the expiration or end date. |
| **Termination Insights** | |
| Termination for Cause - Notice Period | Refers to provisions setting out the notice period required for a party to terminate a contract for cause. For example, for material breach or other events of default. |
| Termination for Convenience - Notice Period | Refers to provisions setting out the notice period required for a party to terminate a contract for convenience. For example, without a specific reason for doing so and without having to show any breach or default by the other party. |

See the [Extracted Agreement Insights and Definitions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=uwk1702945852405.html&_LANG=enus "Look up the names and definitions for the attributes and values that are surfaced in Agreement Manager agreements with AI. title: German Language Support
heading_path: Limitation: "Dropdown" attributes (fields) with pre-populated value sets
text:
For attributes (fields) that use dropdowns with pre-populated value sets, only those values are surfaced in your agreements. Agreement processing extracts an attribute value only if it matches one of the values in the dropdown.

When processing cannot locate the value within the agreement, the extracted value is *Other*. For example, if an agreement's payment term is 50 days, the surfaced Payment Term value is *Other*:

![A screenshot of the Party Term filter that shows the pre-populated value set for the attribute (field).](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Payment-term-filter_All-options.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTYxNzAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.fDeLI-bgxwdm53fccy_zY-u9tMBqVL-HHutysYPqH5s&_LANG=enus)

This limitation applies to these attributes (fields) and values:

| Attribute (Field) | Value Set |
| --- | --- |
| Assignment (Change of Control) | No, or consent required; Yes, notice required; Yes, restrictions apply; Yes, with conditions |
| Assignment (General) | No, or consent required; Yes; Yes, with conditions |
| Assignment (Termination Rights) | Yes |
| Employment Type | Fixed Term; Full Time; Intern; Part Time |
| Late Fees Apply | Yes; No |
| NDA Type | Mutual; One Way |
| Party Role | Buyer, Seller |
| Payment Term | 15 days; 30 days; 45 days; 60 days; 7 days; 90 days; >90 days; On Receipt; Other; Under 7 days |
| Renewal Type | Auto-Renew; Evergreen; Fixed Term; Renewal Option; Unspecified |

title: German Language Support
text:
Obligations are surfaced in English (U.S.) agreements only. The AI model that processes obligations is run from a U.S. server and can only process English agreements in this region.

If your language and region are not supported, manually create the obligations to include this information in dashboards, reports, and other insights.

See these support articles to learn more about obligation management:

- [Obligation Management](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=csm1727128259486.html&_LANG=enus "Read about obligation management, including how to use this functionality to capture, track, and fulfill your agreement obligations. This topic answers the question: How can I find and track my agreement obligations?")
- [Manually Create an Obligation](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=rrf1727128588540.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to create an obligation from the Agreement preview page for a selected agreement.")

Look up the standard fields that are associated with each agreement and non-agreement type.")

title: Custom Agreement Type Extractions
text:
Read about custom agreement type extractions, including their use, purpose, and entry points in Agreement Manager.

 

Custom agreement types capture specific, unique information that isn't covered by the standard, Docusign-provided types. For example, when the standard agreement types don't match the organization's taxonomy or vertical.

Other reasons for creating custom agreement type extractions:

- To classify agreements consistently across the Intelligent Agreement Management (IAM) platform
  using relevant type names.
- To include custom data points in search results, filters, dashboards, and reports to reflect
  expanded business-specific types.
- To track the critical, business-specific data in your agreements.
- To provide more robust and tailored data in your agreement insights and reporting.

title: Custom Agreement Type Extractions
text:
Custom agreement types are created and managed from the *Agreement Types Manager*. These tasks are completed by users with Organize and manage all agreements access to Agreement Manager.

This feature is accessed from the Agreement Manager homepage under the Settings (gear) menu:

![A screenshot of the Agreement Manager homepage that highlights the Agreement Types option in the Settings (gear) menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-types-manager_Settings-menu_Agreement-Types_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc1ODQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.n9rs-NRMst_5GS3JtC0SDVxVOmmEFGBw4WYYcLfiXmY&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Agreement Manager homepage that highlights the Agreement Types option in the Settings (gear) menu.")

The Manage Agreement Types page opens. A list of standard and custom agreement types for the account is displayed in the Agreement Types table. From this page, administrators and contract managers can add new agreement types and enable them for AI detection.

title: Custom Agreement Type Extractions
text:
Use the + Agreement Type option to create new custom agreement types:

![A snippet of the Manage Agreement Types page that highlights the + Agreement Type option.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-Types-Manager_Add-Agreement-Type-option_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc1ODQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.n9rs-NRMst_5GS3JtC0SDVxVOmmEFGBw4WYYcLfiXmY&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Manage Agreement Types page that highlights the + Agreement Type option.")

The New agreement type dialog box opens. The first part of the configuration process is to provide agreement type details:

![A screenshot of the New agreement type dialog box that is used to provide agreement type details, such as Name and Category.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Custom-agreement-type-extractions_New-agreement-type_dialog-box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc1ODQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.n9rs-NRMst_5GS3JtC0SDVxVOmmEFGBw4WYYcLfiXmY&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the New agreement type dialog box that is used to provide agreement type details, such as Name and Category.")

The custom agreement type is pre-populated with standard, Docusign-provided fields to provide structure for the type. These fields are added automatically depending on the selected category from the previous step.

Select additional fields for the custom agreement type to ensure that it meets your business-specific needs:

![A screenshot of the Select fields for type dialog box that prompts you to add custom and standard fields to the new type.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Custom-agreement-type-extractions_Select-fields-for-type_dialog-box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc1ODQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.n9rs-NRMst_5GS3JtC0SDVxVOmmEFGBw4WYYcLfiXmY&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Select fields for type dialog box that prompts you to add custom and standard fields to the new type.")

After saving the new type, the agreement type details page opens. The custom and standard fields that are associated with the type are displayed in the Fields table.

The custom agreement type was created, however, the data points are not surfaced yet in your
agreements. Complete the AI detection setup process to surface the new custom
extraction in your new and historical agreements. See the [Configure New Custom Agreement Types](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=gke1727282238086.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to create custom agreement types to support organizational use cases.") topic to learn how to complete this task.

title: Custom Agreement Type Extractions
text:
After configuring the custom agreement type, use the Set Up option to enable the AI detection for the custom extraction. This option has two access points: the Agreement Types table and the agreement type details page.

![A snippet of the agreement type details page that highlights the Set Up option in the top right.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Custom-agreement-type-extractions_agreement-type-details_Set-Up-option_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc1ODQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.n9rs-NRMst_5GS3JtC0SDVxVOmmEFGBw4WYYcLfiXmY&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the agreement type details page that highlights the Set Up option in the top right.")

The AI detection setup wizard opens. Complete three steps to activate your custom agreement type extractions:

![A screenshot of the setup wizard that introduces the three easy steps to enable the custom agreement type extraction.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Custom-agreement-type-extractions_Setup-wizard_Get-Started.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc1ODQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.n9rs-NRMst_5GS3JtC0SDVxVOmmEFGBw4WYYcLfiXmY&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the setup wizard that introduces the three easy steps to enable the custom agreement type extraction.")

1. **Define your agreement type.** This step describes what the AI model should be looking for and what the data means. See these topics to learn more about defining your custom agreement type:

   - [Define Your Custom Agreement Types](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tske5fe1ad5-2832-4e6c-9177-f33a9ffade15.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to initiate the AI detection set-up process and complete the first step to define the custom type.")
   - [Best Practice: Writing Definitions for AI Detection](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tmk1741317734856.html&_LANG=enus "Read about tips for writing good definitions for the AI detection setup process. These definitions are an important part of the process and help to \"teach\" the AI detection.")
2. **Choose good examples of this type of agreement.** This step provides examples to show the AI model how the data displays in your agreements. See the [Add Examples of Your Custom Agreement Types](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tskd38465c6-c8c0-4588-bfb3-fdb6916a1926.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to complete the second step of the AI detection set-up process, Add examples of this agreement type.") topic for steps to complete this task.
3. **Test for accuracy, or activate detection immediately.** This step helps you confirm that the AI model is finding the right data in your agreements. See these topics to learn more about testing for accuracy and activating the extraction:

   - [Test the Extraction Accuracy for Your Custom Agreement Types](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=ogw1740503179389.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to test the accuracy of the AI detection for your custom agreement types.")
   - [Activate Your Custom Agreement Types](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=cgn1740452596841.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to activate the AI detection for a custom agreement type and surface it in selected historical agreements.")

Once activated, the custom extraction is surfaced in your new agreements by default. The data points are included in your agreement insights, such as filters, searches, dashboards, and reports.

text:

Once activated, the custom extraction is surfaced in your new agreements by default. The data points are included in your agreement insights, such as filters, searches, dashboards, and reports.

You can select specific historical agreements to process for the new agreement type. See the [Find Custom Agreement Types in Specific Documents](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tskb991328c-dc5f-4d45-9617-dada91a5672a.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to use the Find On Specific Documents function to surface custom agreement types in selected agreements.") topic to learn about processing your historical agreements for custom types.

title: Custom Agreement Type Extractions
text:
When uploading or ingesting agreements to Agreement Manager, if an agreement does not match an existing standard or custom type, it is identified as Miscellaneous. If 10 or more similar agreements are identified as Miscellaneous, a custom agreement type is suggested.

For example, 10 charter agreements were uploaded and identified as *Miscellaneous*. The agreements are recognized as being similar and a custom agreement type, *Charter Agreement*, is suggested:

![A snippet of the agreement list table that shows a suggested custom agreement type for a Miscellaneous document.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Autosuggest_New-suggestion-available_dialog-box_charter-agreement-example.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc1ODQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.n9rs-NRMst_5GS3JtC0SDVxVOmmEFGBw4WYYcLfiXmY&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the agreement list table that shows a suggested custom agreement type for a Miscellaneous document.")

Use the Create option and follow the steps to create a new custom agreement type. See the [Use Auto-Suggestion to Create Custom Agreement Types](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tsk2093dc24-1da8-4393-b4cd-526990bc7118.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to create a custom agreement type from the auto-suggestion feature.") article to learn more about using this feature.

Once created, the custom type is applied to all the relevant documents that were previously identified as Miscellaneous. Depending on the processing queue, it may take an hour or longer for the agreements to reflect the new type.

You can filter the agreement list table using the new custom type:

![A snippet of the agreement list table that shows the new custom type in the Agreement Type filter.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Autosuggest_Agreement-Type-filter_Charter-Agreement-example.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc1ODQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.n9rs-NRMst_5GS3JtC0SDVxVOmmEFGBw4WYYcLfiXmY&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the agreement list table that shows the new custom type in the Agreement Type filter.")

Important: The agreements contain attributes and field values that were surfaced with AI. Complete a data review to validate these values, including the Agreement Type. See the [Validate Extracted Field Values (Complete a Data Review)](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=yqq1702945693043.html&_LANG=enus "This topic answers the question: How can I make sure that the suggested attribute (field) values are accurate? Learn how to complete a data review to verify suggested field values.") article to learn more about validating AI-surfaced values.

title: IAM Fields Manager
text:
Read about the Fields manager, including its purpose, use, and role within the Intelligent Agreement Management (IAM) platform.

The *Fields Manager* is the single source of truth for the account's custom and standard
fields. It is the central place for administrators and contract managers to define,
organize, and manage the fields in their account. These custom and standard fields are
reused across the Docusign Intelligent Agreement Management (IAM)
platform.

This feature is accessed from the Agreement Manager homepage. Expand the Settings (gear) menu located on the Completed Documents page and select the Fields option:

![A screenshot of the Agreement Manager homepage that highlights the Fields option in the Settings (gear) menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-types-manager_Settings-menu_Fields_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc1NTksInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.lL_HWjQWWVzh5Mdz0I50wfXhcXd3lL6aS3mS2OrCuNo&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Agreement Manager homepage that highlights the Fields option in the Settings (gear) menu.")

The Manage Fields page opens and the Fields view is displayed. All the custom and standard fields in the account are displayed in the table:

![A screenshot of the Manage Fields page that shows the custom and standard fields for the account.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Fields-Manager_Manage-Fields_Fields-view_generic.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc1NTksInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.lL_HWjQWWVzh5Mdz0I50wfXhcXd3lL6aS3mS2OrCuNo&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Manage Fields page that shows the custom and standard fields for the account.")

title: IAM Fields Manager
text:
The Fields table lists all the standard (Docusign-provided) and custom (user-defined) fields that are associated with the account:

![A snippet of the Manage Fields page that shows custom and standard fields in the Fields table.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Fields-Manager_Manage-Fields_Fields-table.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc1NTksInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.lL_HWjQWWVzh5Mdz0I50wfXhcXd3lL6aS3mS2OrCuNo&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Manage Fields page that shows custom and standard fields in the Fields table.")

- The Field column displays the name of the field and includes a field type indicator. For example, currency, date, dropdown, number, and text. Hover the cursor over the indicator to view the field type:![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/FIelds-Manager_Fields-table_Field-column_type-indicator-displayed.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc1NTksInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.lL_HWjQWWVzh5Mdz0I50wfXhcXd3lL6aS3mS2OrCuNo&_LANG=enus)

  Tip: Use the Sort items (arrows) icon, located in the column heading, to sort the table by the Field column.
- The Category column indicates the classification for the field. For example, General, Payment, Legal and Compliance, Renewal, Termination, Other, and Human Resources.
- The Created By column indicates whether the field is a custom (User) field or a standard (Docusign) field.
- The AI detection column displays the AI status for the field. For example, whether the field is Active or it must be Set Up. You can also Activate the field from this column.

For user-defined custom fields, the More actions (three dots) menu is available. The menu options are dependent on the AI detection status for the custom field. Once the field is active, the menu includes actions related to the AI detection:

![A snippet of the Fields table that highlights the More actions (three dots) menu, including AI detection options.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Fields-Manager_Fields-table_More-actions-menu.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc1NTksInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.lL_HWjQWWVzh5Mdz0I50wfXhcXd3lL6aS3mS2OrCuNo&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Fields table that highlights the More actions (three dots) menu, including AI detection options.")

Important: Use the Edit Details option to update the Name and Category details for custom fields. Once the changes are saved, they are reflected everywhere that the field is used. See the [Update Custom Field Details](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tsk6f53c09e-3ef6-4bb5-8a5b-1121742d4155.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to update the details for your custom fields, such as the name and category.") topic to learn how to update the details for custom fields.

title: IAM Fields Manager
text:
Use the search field and table filters to locate the relevant fields:

![A snippet of the Manage Fields page that shows the search field and filter options for the Fields table.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Fields-Manager_Fields-table_search-and-filters.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc1NTksInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.lL_HWjQWWVzh5Mdz0I50wfXhcXd3lL6aS3mS2OrCuNo&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Manage Fields page that shows the search field and filter options for the Fields table.")

- Enter a search term in the Search fields... field. As you type the keyword, the table updates and the fields that match the search term are displayed.
- Use the Created By filter to view standard (Docusign) or custom (User) fields.
- Use the Category filter to view fields by classification. For example, General, Payment, Legal and Compliance, Renewal, Termination, Other, and Human Resources.
- Use the AI Status filter to view fields based on their AI detection status, Active or Needs Setup.

title: IAM Fields Manager
text:
The *Fields Manager* helps administrators and contract managers to:

- **Standardize agreement data:** Define and manage shared custom and standard fields for use across Agreement Manager and other IAM products.
- **Manage field definitions in one place:** Create and manage custom fields without editing
  each agreement type or template individually.
- **Control where fields are used:** Add or remove fields from one or more agreement types at the same time. The same field can be reused across multiple agreement types.
- **Manage custom field extractions:** Review and refine the AI detection for custom fields, including refining definitions, updating examples, and testing the accuracy.

Tip: See the [Custom Field Extractions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=condece8d7e-2da8-4e5f-8ba4-3f0175c2a625.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the custom field extractions, including entry points for creating new fields and a review of the AI detection setup wizard.") topic to learn more about creating custom field extractions.

title: IAM Fields Manager
heading_path: Fields Manager across the IAM platform
text:
The *Fields Manager* is a shared IAM workspace for the unified fields that are used across several IAM products and workflows.

- **Agreement Manager:** The *Fields Manager* is the central place for administrators and contract
  managers to create and manage custom fields. The workspace also hosts
  the custom extraction wizard that is used to create custom fields. This
  feature is accessed from the Agreement Manager homepage.
- **Agreement Preparation:** The *Fields Manager* provides a unified fields library to the
  agreement preparation surfaces. For example, agreement templates and
  send experiences. Template builders and senders can pick from the same
  set of fields that are used in Agreement Manager. This ensures that the data
  captured during preparation flows into Agreement Manager as structured fields,
  reducing duplicate fields.
- **Agreement Desk:** The *Fields Manager* lets you define shared fields once and reuse them across request forms and approvals. This keeps intake consistent and improves processing speed and accuracy. For example, you can standardize fields like Customer Type or Renewal Date.
- **Web Forms and Integrations:** When external forms and connected systems write into IAM, they
  map directly into the structured *Fields Manager* fields. This
  connection ensures that the data is collected into the same unified
  fields that are used across the IAM platform.
- **Docusign CLM:** The *Fields Manager* provides a unified fields
  library into which CLM maps its field data. This ensures that CLM custom
  attributes are mapped appropriately and the fields are not
  recreated.

title: Spanish Language Support (Mexico, Spain)
text:
Read about the Agreement Manager AI models and features that are supported for Spanish agreements in Mexico and Spain.

Agreement Manager is available to customers globally, however, the features and insights vary by region and language. Read about the supported agreement types and attributes that provide insights into agreements for customers in Mexico and Spain.

CAUTION: The accuracy and appropriateness of AI-assisted insights may vary depending on the agreement language and customer use case. Customers should review all suggested data points for accuracy and to determine if a value is appropriate for its particular circumstances.

Important:

As new or updated AI models are released, your historical agreements can be processed again to surface these data points. Depending on the type of new insights that are available, users can choose to:

- Opt in for reprocessing from the New Insights Available badge (obligation-related attributes and insights)
- Reprocess all the outdated agreements for the account (new or updated AI model)
- Reprocess selected agreements (standard or custom fields)

See the [Agreement Manager Reprocessing Experience](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=con44590246-0f0e-43b4-9692-caadb57335ac.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about the Agreement Manager reprocessing experience, including its purpose, trigger points, and job size considerations.") article to learn more about this experience.

See the [Language and Regional Support](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=xvp1729795957126.html&_LANG=enus "Read about language support and regional availability for Agreement Manager AI models and features.") article to view supported agreement types, attributes, and data points for other languages and regions.
