title: Advanced Search Queries
text:
Read about the advanced search query feature that gives you control over how your results are matched and ranked.

Advanced search queries extend your searches beyond basic keywords and filters to control how Agreement Manager finds and ranks your agreements. Use a combination of Boolean operators, exact and proximity searches, boosting, and wildcards in a single query:

![A snippet of the Completed Documents page that highlights an example query string that combines terms, operators, and modifiers.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Advanced-search-queries_example-querystring_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY0MjQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.EF5ob63yt2updb74q_-UDMTEaBXUiAsQB3n2mBLX98M&_LANG=enus)

When you perform a basic keyword search, your query is treated as a loose set of words. Advanced queries give you explicit control to:

- Combine multiple conditions
- Control how closely words appear together
- Emphasize some terms over others

title: Advanced Search Queries
heading_path: When to use advanced search queries
text:
Use advanced search queries when you want to:

![An infographic that depicts the reasons for performing advanced search queries.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Advanced-search-queries_When-do-use-advanced-searches_infographic.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY0MjQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.EF5ob63yt2updb74q_-UDMTEaBXUiAsQB3n2mBLX98M&_LANG=enus)

- Isolate specific clause language within a large document repository. For example, locate
  agreements that mention both a product and a specific risk.
- Exclude noise that cannot be removed with filters. For example, terms that overlap multiple lines of business or teams.
- Find agreements where terms appear near each other, even if the exact wording varies. For example, locate agreements where "landlord" and "indemnify" appear within a few words of each other.
- Search for name or spelling variations without listing every option. For example, you are searching for agreements by party names with varying spellings.
- Combine multiple conditions in a single, reusable query string that can be shared with others or reused later.

title: Advanced Search Queries
text:
Advanced search queries are entered from the same place in Agreement Manager as basic keyword searches. The difference is how your input is treated. Agreement Manager treats your input as a structured query, not just a list of words. The search looks for special pieces, like operators, quoted phrases, proximity markers, wildcards, and boosts.

![An infographic that depicts how advanced searches work in Agreement Manager.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Advanced-search-queries_How-advanced-search-works_infographic.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY0MjQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.EF5ob63yt2updb74q_-UDMTEaBXUiAsQB3n2mBLX98M&_LANG=enus)

The filters that you apply decide *which* agreements are eligible for inclusion. Your advanced query decides:

- *How* the agreement text matches the criteria.
- *Which* matches should rank higher in the results.

When you enter a valid query string, the search returns a focused, predictable result set. The
search engine parses the string into terms, operators, and modifiers, and runs it
against your agreement content.

If the query string is constructed poorly, the search applies filters based on the entered
string. If no relevant filters are detected, a basic keyword search is performed.
You still get search results, but without advanced logic.

title: Advanced Search Queries
text:
Use Boolean operators to express must-have, optional, and excluded terms in a single query. Supported operators include: AND, OR, NOT, + (plus), and - (minus).

Review the table to learn more about these operators:

| Operator | Description | Example |
| --- | --- | --- |
| AND | Both terms must be present | msa AND "data center" |
| OR | Either term can be present | "data center" OR "co-location" |
| NOT | The term after NOT must be absent | "data center" NOT "Azure" |
| + (plus) | Term is required (must appear) | +renewal "master agreement" |
| - (minus) | Term must *not* appear | renewal -"evergreen" |

Operators can be combined and grouped with parentheses for more complex logic. For example:

```
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | ("data center" OR "hosting") AND (Azure OR AWS) NOT test |
```

Important: **Rules and restrictions**

- **All caps:** When using Boolean operators, the operators are case-sensitive. Enter the operators in all uppercase (AND, OR, NOT) so they are interpreted as operators, not words.
- **Inside quotes:** Operators that are inside quotes are treated as text, not logic. For example, "AWS and Azure" searches for the phrase literally, not as "AWS" AND "Azure".

title: Advanced Search Queries
text:
Boosting is used to favor some terms over others when ranking results, without excluding anything outright. When you enter a term in the search field, add a caret (^) and a weight to indicate its relative importance. For example:

- quick^2 fox (documents matching 'quick' are prioritized or weighted)
- "master services agreement"^3 msa (the exact phrase is prioritized over the abbreviation)

Boosting is helpful when:

- Several terms are important but you want to emphasize one over the others.
- You prefer one term variant over another. For example:

  ```
  |  |  |
  | --- | --- |
  |  | "limitation of liability"^3 "limitation of damages" |
  ```
- Combining broad context with strong signals. For example:

  ```
  |  |  |
  | --- | --- |
  |  | renewal^3 "auto-renew"^2 termination |
  ```

Important: Large boosts can produce unintuitive relevance. Use small integers to start.
The suggested range is 2–5. Boosts work within the advanced query, however,
filters still control which agreements are included in the results. If a boosted
term never appears in the results, it has no effect.

title: Advanced Search Queries
text:
Exact phrase matching is used to search for words in a specific order, together, rather than anywhere in the agreement. When you know the exact language for which you are searching, enter the phrase in double quotes. For example:

- "Master services agreements"
- "Governing law"
- "Force majeure"

Important: **Rules and restrictions**

- **Order-sensitivity:** Phrases are order-sensitive. For example, "data center" is not the same as "center data".
- **Single search term:** Multi-word quoted phrases are interpreted as a single term in Boolean logic. For example, enter:

  ```
  |  |  |
  | --- | --- |
  |  | msa AND "data center" |
  ```

  to locate master services agreements that contain the quoted phrase, "data center".
- **Multiple quotes, no operators:** If you enter multiple quotes without any operators, the search interprets the string as an AND expression. Both words must be present. For example, enter:

  ```
  |  |  |
  | --- | --- |
  |  | "data" "center" |
  ```

  to locate agreements that contain both words, in any order.

title: Advanced Search Queries
text:
Proximity searches are used to find phrases where words appear near each other, but not necessarily next to each other. Enter a quoted phrase followed by ~ (tilde) N, where N is the maximum distance (number of words) allowed between terms.

For example, enter:

```
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | "limitation liability"~5 |
```

to search for agreements that
contain both words, within 5 words of each other. The words must appear in the same
agreement, in roughly the same order, within N words of each other.

Proximity searches are helpful when you want to account for:

- **Clause variations:** Find clauses where the wording varies slightly, but the core concept
  stays together.
- **"Near miss" phrases:** Capture phrases while excluding extra words or formatting differences.

Combine your proximity search with Boolean operators to expand the query. For example: enter

```
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | "data center"~5 AND ("AWS" OR "Azure" OR "GCP") |
```

to find agreements that contain "data center" and either AWS, Azure, or GCP. "Data" and "center" can appear within 5 words of each other.

Important: **Rules and restrictions**

- **Large proximity values:** The higher the maximum distance between words, the broader and slower the results. For example, ~50 does not reflect a realistic clause structure. Smaller numbers are recommended.
- **Phrase order:** Proximity searches respect the phrase order. For example, "license lawful"~10 is interpreted differently than "lawful license"~10.
- **Multiple proximity searches:** When you combine multiple proximity searches in one query,
  the complexity is increased. If the performance degrades,
  simplify the query or split it into separate searches.

title: Advanced Search Queries
text:
Wildcard searches are used to match word variations without typing every form. Advanced search queries support the use of the \* (asterisk) and ? (question mark) wildcard characters:

| Character | Description | Example |
| --- | --- | --- |
| \* (asterisk) | Matches zero or more characters | - indemnif\* (returns indemnify, indemnification, and indemnified) - bio\* (returns biotech, biological, and biography) |
| ? (question mark) | Matches exactly one character | - organi?ation (returns organization and organisation) - preten?e (returns pretense and pretence) |

Wildcard searches are used to:

- Find name variants
- Handle spelling differences
- Capture families of related terms

Important: **Rules and restrictions**

- **Leading wildcards:** If you enter a wildcard at the beginning of a term, the query scans through a multitude of possibilities. If a query fails or feels slow, move the wildcard to the end or anchor more of the word.
- **Spaces and punctuation:** Wildcards do not match across spaces or punctuation. They typically operate within a single term, as indexed.
- **Combine wildcards for the best results:** Combine wildcards with at least one regular
  keyword or operator instead of using a wildcard-only query.

title: Advanced Search Queries
text:
A query string search is any query that combines multiple operators and features into a single expression. The advanced search uses a query string parser to interpret the full expression that you enter.

The query string parser supports:

- Boolean operators (AND, OR, NOT, +, - )
- Exact phrases and proximity ("...", "..."~N)
- Wildcards (\*, ?)
- Boosting (^N)
- Parentheses for grouping

Full query strings can look like:

```
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | (title:"master services agreement"^3 OR "MSA") AND indemnif* |
```

```
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | ("data center"~5 AND (AWS OR Amazon OR Azure)) NOT test |
```

```
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | +(renewal OR "auto-renew") "notice period"~5 -template |
```

Important: If a query is malformed, the search can return an error and not run the query. Typically, the system reverts to a basic keyword search. You still get results, but without advanced logic. Build your queries incrementally and verify that each piece works on its own. Once verified, combine all the pieces into a single, well-structured query string.

title: Advanced Search Queries
text:
Follow these tips to create your advanced search queries:

- **Start simple.** Test a short keyword or phrase first, then layer on
  advanced query operators one at a time.
- **Check operator syntax for Boolean operators.** Use uppercase AND,
  OR, and NOT and keep them outside of any quotes.
- **Watch your quotes and parentheses.** Unbalanced quotes or
  parentheses can cause errors or unexpected results. If your query fails,
  remove the quotes or simplify the groupings and try again.
- **Be careful with wildcard characters.** Avoid leading wildcards and
  broad patterns. They can slow searches or press internal limits. Use
  anchored forms like `cloud*` or
  `indemnif*`.
- **Limit complexity.** Long queries with many query operators are more
  likely to fail or revert to a basic keyword search. If this happens,
  split your logic into smaller queries.
- **Test pieces independently.** If a query stops working, run each part
  on its own to see which component is causing the issue. After isolating
  the issue, fix the component or remove it.

title: Custom Field Extractions
text:
Read about the custom field extractions, including entry points for creating new fields and a review of the AI detection setup wizard.

 

Custom field extractions are used to capture business-specific data points that standard extractions might not track. For example, use custom fields to capture terms, dates, amounts, and other details that are important to your organization.

Note: Administrators automatically have access to configure agreement data. For non-administrator users, this feature requires Organize and manage all agreements access to Agreement Manager. These tasks are primarily completed by administrators and contract managers.

You can configure each field once with a name, definition, and example agreements that show the target data. The AI model learns from those examples to detect and surface the values in your agreements, including selected historical agreements.

Custom field extractions expand your agreement insights to include unique, business-specific data points. Other benefits include:

- Reduced manual data entry.
- Improved data quality and consistency.
- Enhanced agreement insights and tailored reporting.

See the [Custom Extractions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=yrx1747083411531.html&_LANG=enus "Read about custom extractions, including how these data points bring value to our organization.") topic to learn more about the custom extraction process.

title: Custom Field Extractions
text:
Custom fields capture specific, unique information that isn't covered by standard agreement fields. Agreement Manager supports these custom field types:![A snippet of the Add a field dialog box that shows the available field types for your custom fields.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Add-custom-field_Field-type-options.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2NDUsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.WKDB4jc10g6HdauwcKGvb91Xo7oSHCkBRgz7SgKikVg&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Add a field dialog box that shows the available field types for your custom fields.")

- Text
- Number
- Date
- Dropdown

When using the Dropdown field type, you must provide a list of options for the dropdown:

![A snippet of the Add a field dialog box that shows the Options field for adding dropdown options.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Add-custom-field_Options-for-dropdown-type.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2NDUsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.WKDB4jc10g6HdauwcKGvb91Xo7oSHCkBRgz7SgKikVg&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Add a field dialog box that shows the Options field for adding dropdown options.")

See the [Update Custom Dropdown Fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tskf056e8d3-baa4-41f2-8f71-12d0bb5c735b.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to update the dropdown options for custom fields with the Dropdown field type.") topic to learn more about configuring custom dropdown fields.

Restriction: When integrating Docusign CLM with IAM, 'dropdown' field types cannot be mapped. For example, CLM attributes that correspond to custom dropdown fields in Agreement Manager cannot be mapped.

title: Custom Field Extractions
text:
Use the Add a field function to configure new custom fields. For example, provide a name and specify the field type, agreement types, and category for the new field. Once created, enable the AI detection and activate the custom extraction.

You can launch the Add a field function from a number of entry points in Agreement Manager:

**Data management entry points**

The data management options are located in the  (gear) menu on the Agreement Manager homepage:

- Manage Fields page (Settings > Fields): Select the + Add Fields option from the top right.

  ![A snippet of the Manage Fields page that highlights the + Add Fields option in the top right.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Custom-field-extractions_Entry-point_Manage-Fields-page.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2NDUsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.WKDB4jc10g6HdauwcKGvb91Xo7oSHCkBRgz7SgKikVg&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Manage Fields page that highlights the + Add Fields option in the top right.")
- Manage Agreement Types page (Settings > Agreement Types): Select the + Add Fields option from the agreement type detail page for a selected type.

  ![A snippet of the Manage Agreement Types page that highlights the + Add Fields option in the agreement type detail view.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Custom-field-extractions_Entry-point_Manage-Agreement-Types.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2NDUsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.WKDB4jc10g6HdauwcKGvb91Xo7oSHCkBRgz7SgKikVg&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Manage Agreement Types page that highlights the + Add Fields option in the agreement type detail view.")

  See the topic to learn more about using this option.

**Agreement preview entry points**

There are two entry points for creating new fields from the Agreement preview page:

- Add (+) menu (Details pane): Expand the menu from the top of the Details pane and select the Add a Field option.

  Tip: Use this option to add metadata to an agreement without tying it to a specific sentence or clause. For example, you want to create a field to capture data about the agreement, like 'internal owner' or 'risk rating'. This information is not tied to a specific sentence or clause in the document.

  ![A snippet of the Agreement preview page that highlights the Add a Field option in the top right of the Details pane.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Custom-field-extractions_Entry-point_Agreement-Preview_Details.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2NDUsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.WKDB4jc10g6HdauwcKGvb91Xo7oSHCkBRgz7SgKikVg&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Agreement preview page that highlights the Add a Field option in the top right of the Details pane.")
- Text highlighting (Preview pane): Highlight the relevant value from the preview pane. After selecting the text that you want to track, a menu opens. Select the Track with AI option.

  Tip: Use this option when you want to track content that is directly associated with specific text in the agreement. For example, you want to track a specific clause, sentence, or value in the agreement.

title: Custom Field Extractions
text:
: Use this option when you want to track content that is directly associated with specific text in the agreement. For example, you want to track a specific clause, sentence, or value in the agreement.

  ![A snippet of the Agreement preview page that shows a highlighted field value and the Track with AI option.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Custom-field-extractions_Entry-point_Agreement-preview_Track-with-AI.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2NDUsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.WKDB4jc10g6HdauwcKGvb91Xo7oSHCkBRgz7SgKikVg&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Agreement preview page that shows a highlighted field value and the Track with AI option.")

See the [Create Custom Fields from the Agreement Preview](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tsk0830ea60-9b29-4efa-b73f-9eb15b2d6561.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to create custom fields from the Agreement preview page for a selected agreement.") topic to learn more about using the Agreement preview options.

title: Custom Field Extractions
text:
All of these entry points launch the Add a Field dialog box. However, the experience differs based on where you launched the feature.

**From data management pages:**

Complete the Add a Field dialog box to configure the custom field details. Enter a unique name and specify the field type, agreement types, and category:

![A screenshot of the Add a field dialog box that is used to configure new custom fields.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Custom-field-extractions_Add-a-field-dialog-box_basic-version.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2NDUsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.WKDB4jc10g6HdauwcKGvb91Xo7oSHCkBRgz7SgKikVg&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Add a field dialog box that is used to configure new custom fields.")

See the [Add Custom Fields to Agreement Types](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=hcn1736879099815.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to create and add custom fields to the custom and standard agreement types in your account.") topic to learn about adding new fields from the data management pages.

**From the Agreement preview page:**

When accessing this function from the Agreement preview page, you have the option to initiate the AI setup. The dialog box has two parts: field configuration and AI setup initiation.

![A snippet of the Add a Field dialog box that shows the additional AI setup options.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Custom-field-extraction_Add-a-Field-dialog-box_new-experience.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2NDUsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.WKDB4jc10g6HdauwcKGvb91Xo7oSHCkBRgz7SgKikVg&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Add a Field dialog box that shows the additional AI setup options.")

**Configure the field:**

Complete the top part of the Add a Field dialog box to define the new custom field:

![A snippet of the Add a Field dialog box that shows the required fields for defining a new field.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Custom-field-extractions_Add-a-field_top-portion.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2NDUsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.WKDB4jc10g6HdauwcKGvb91Xo7oSHCkBRgz7SgKikVg&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Add a Field dialog box that shows the required fields for defining a new field.")

Enter a unique Field name and specify the Field type and Category for the field. The Agreement types field defaults to the agreement type of the current document. Select additional agreement types as needed.

**Initiate AI setup:**

Complete the bottom part of the Add a Field dialog box to initiate the AI detection setup process:

![A snippet of the Add a Field dialog box that shows the options for initiating the AI detection setup for the field.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Custom-field-extractions_Add-a-field_bottom-portion.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2NDUsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.WKDB4jc10g6HdauwcKGvb91Xo7oSHCkBRgz7SgKikVg&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Add a Field dialog box that shows the options for initiating the AI detection setup for the field.")

The Use AI to find this field in future agreements option is selected by default. Leave this option selected if you want to surface the custom field in other agreements. For example, in newly completed agreements and in selected historical documents.

Tip: Deselect this option to opt out of AI detection for the field. It will not be surfaced in your other documents. You can Save the field configuration and add the AI detection later from the data management pages.

title: Custom Field Extractions
text:
is option to opt out of AI detection for the field. It will not be surfaced in your other documents. You can Save the field configuration and add the AI detection later from the data management pages.

Provide a detailed definition for the custom field. This description informs the AI model of the field's purpose and use. The Refine with AI option displays after you enter at least 50 characters. Use this option to standardize and refine your definition.

Once you save your configuration, the Track this field with AI setup wizard opens.

Important: The Track this field with AI wizard is only launched when you add fields from the Agreement preview page. If you access the Add a Field function from the other entry points, the legacy AI detection setup wizard opens. Both follow the same high-level process steps.

See the [Create Custom Fields from the Agreement Preview](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tsk0830ea60-9b29-4efa-b73f-9eb15b2d6561.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to create custom fields from the Agreement preview page for a selected agreement.") topic to learn about adding new fields from the Agreement preview page.

title: Custom Field Extractions
heading_path: 'Track this field with AI' setup wizard
text:
When you create new custom fields from the Agreement preview page, the Track this field with AI setup wizard launches. Complete three steps to start tracking the custom field extraction with AI:

![A screenshot of the Track this field with AI setup wizard that displays when you create custom fields from the Agreement preview page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Custom-field-extractions_Track-this-field-with-AI_setup-wizard.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2NDUsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.WKDB4jc10g6HdauwcKGvb91Xo7oSHCkBRgz7SgKikVg&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Track this field with AI setup wizard that displays when you create custom fields from the Agreement preview page.")

1. **Define the field.** This step describes what the AI model should be looking for and what the data means. See these topics to learn more about defining the custom field for AI detection:

   - [Add Custom Fields to Agreement Types](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=hcn1736879099815.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to create and add custom fields to the custom and standard agreement types in your account.")
   - [Create Custom Fields from the Agreement Preview](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tsk0830ea60-9b29-4efa-b73f-9eb15b2d6561.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to create custom fields from the Agreement preview page for a selected agreement.")
2. **Select examples.** This step provides examples to show the AI model how the field value is displayed in your agreements. See the [Add Examples of Your Custom Fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tskcb56face-b8de-4cf9-85ce-6eac3669dd82.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to complete the second step of the AI detection set-up process, Select examples.") topic for steps to complete this task.
3. **Test for accuracy.** This step helps you confirm that the AI model is finding the right data in your agreements. See these topics to learn more about testing for accuracy and activating the extraction:

   - [Test the Extraction Accuracy for Your Custom Fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=wjr1740452628309.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to test the AI detection accuracy for your custom fields and refining the results to improve accuracy.")
   - [Activate Your Custom Fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tsk443eb55b-5980-4fa6-b145-e00b3415cdbe.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to activate the AI detection for a custom field and surface it in selected historical agreements.")

Important: Use the Finish Later option to save your progress and finish the AI detection set-up later. Using this option ends the Track this field with AI experience. See the [Enable AI Detection for Your Custom Fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tsk02bd31a2-b125-4d53-b632-20d9972163a8.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to enable the AI detection for your custom fields. Once activated, the field is surfaced in your new agreements automatically.") topic for steps to finish the process.

Once activated, the custom extraction is surfaced in your new agreements by default. The new data points are included in your agreement insights, such as filters, searches, dashboards, and reports.

You can select specific historical agreements to process for the new field. See the [Find Custom Fields in Specific Documents](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tsk79824e36-d51d-4477-a5ca-7228cde30fb2.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to use the Find On Specific Documents function to surface custom fields in selected agreements.") topic to learn about processing your historical agreements for custom fields.

title: French Language Support
text:
Read about the Agreement Manager AI models and features that are supported for French agreements.

Agreement Manager is available to customers globally, however, the features and insights vary by region and language. Read about the supported agreement types, attributes, and data points that provide insights into your French agreements.

CAUTION: The accuracy and appropriateness of AI-assisted insights may vary depending on the agreement language and customer use case. Customers should review all suggested data points for accuracy and to determine if a value is appropriate for its particular circumstances.

See the [Language and Regional Support](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=xvp1729795957126.html&_LANG=enus "Read about language support and regional availability for Agreement Manager AI models and features.") article to view supported agreement types, attributes, and data points for other languages and regions.

title: French Language Support
text:
During agreement processing, attributes and field values are surfaced in your agreements. Review the table to see which attributes are surfaced in French agreements.

Important: English (global) is applied to all customers deployed across Docusign datacenters located in the United States (NA), Europe (EU), Canada (CA), Australia (AU), and Japan (JP).

title: French Language Support
text:
h agreements.

Important: English (global) is applied to all customers deployed across Docusign datacenters located in the United States (NA), Europe (EU), Canada (CA), Australia (AU), and Japan (JP).

| Attribute | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Assignment and Change of Control Insights** | |
| Assignment (Change of Control) | Indicates whether the agreement allows for the assignment of rights and obligations to another party. For example, in case of a merger and acquisition (M&A) or other change of control activity. |
| Assignment (General) | Indicates whether the agreement allows one of the parties to assign its rights and obligations to another party. |
| Assignment (Termination Rights) | Indicates whether the agreement allows for termination if one of the parties assigns its rights and obligations to another party. |
| **Compensation and Payment Insights** | |
| Annual Contract Value | The average yearly revenue generated from a contract or customer subscription. It is commonly used in subscription-based businesses to measure the annualized value of a contract, helping organizations track recurring revenue over time. |
| Total Contract Value | The total monetary value of the contract or agreement between two or more parties. In other words, it is the lifetime value of a contract. |
| Late Fees Apply | Indicates whether the agreement explicitly states that if payment is late fees, charges, or interest will be added. |
| Late Fee % | The percentage of the contract value that is charged when late fees are incurred. |
| Payment Terms | The conditions set by a seller regarding when and how payment for goods or services is expected from the buyer. These terms typically specify the time frame for payment, for example, *Net 30* means payment is due 30 days after the invoice date. |
| Price Cap Increase % | The maximum allowable price increase under the agreement. |
| **Confidentiality Insights** | |
| Duration of Confidentiality | Indicates the length of time that the obligations of a non-disclosure agreement (NDA) remain in effect. |
| **Governing Law, Jurisdiction, and Dispute Resolution Insights** | |
| Governing Law | The rules and laws that will govern the agreement if legal issues or disputes arise. The clause typically identifies whose laws will govern the interpretation and enforcement of the agreement. |
| Jurisdiction | The legal jurisdiction of an agreement in case of a future dispute. This value is typically listed as a specific county or city. |
| **Limitation of Liability Insights** | |
| Liability Cap Amount | The maximum amount or limit that a party will pay for damages or any liability in a specific dollar amount. |
| Liability Cap Duration | Indicates the length of time during which claims can be made under the liability cap within the agreement. |
| Liability Cap Multiplier | The limit a party will pay for damages or any liability, including a multiplier that can increase the cap amount under certain conditions. |
| **Objective Information Insights** | |
| Execution Date | The official date that the agreement was signed by all parties. |
| Languages | Indicates which languages are present in the agreement. |
| NDA Type | Indicates the type of NDA that is in place for the agreement. For example, mutual or one-way. |
| Parties | Contracting parties. Refers to a provision naming the individuals, organizations, or entities that enter into a legally binding agreement (contract). |
| Title | The official title of the agreement. |
| Type (Agreement Type) | Classification of documents based on their title and certain commonalities in their subject matter. For example, Master Services Agreement (MSA), Non-Disclosure Agreements (NDA), and Statement of Work (SOW). |
| **Renewal Insights** | |
| Automatic Renewal (Renewal Type) | Renewal type. Refers to provisions that state an agreement automatically renews at the end of its term unless one or both parties take action to terminate, opt out, or modify it. |
| Evergreen (Renewal Type) | Renewal type. Refers to clauses that contemplate a perpetual continuation of the contract term unless a party takes action to terminate it. |
| Option to Renew (Renewal Type) | Renewal type. Refers to provisions that set out a party's option to extend the term of the contract. |
| NonRenewal Notice Period | Refers to the period of time required for a party to give notice of their intent not to renew an agreement. |
| Renewal - Extension Period | Refers to provisions setting out the additional period of time (duration) that a contract is to be extended beyond its initial term. |
| Renewal Notice Period | Comprised of two data extractions: Renewal Notice Period and NonRenewal Notice Period. Refers to the period of time required for a party to give notice of their intent to renew an agreement. |
| Term - Renewal Automatic | Refers to provisions that specify the duration of the auto-renewal period. |
| Term - Renewal Option | Refers to provisions setting out a party's option to extend the contract for a specific term. |
| **Term Insights** | |
| Effective Date | Refers to the date on which the contract became legally binding and enforceable. It is the date agreed upon by the parties for beginning the period of performance under the contract. |
| Expiration Date | Refers to the date on which the contract comes to an end and is no longer in force. |
| Term Length | Indicates the length of the term of the agreement (how long the agreement is in effect). Signing parties are obligated to adhere to the terms and conditions within the agreement until the expiration or end date. |
| **Termination Insights** | |
| Termination for Cause - Notice Period | Refers to provisions setting out the notice period required for a party to terminate a contract for cause. For example, for material breach or other events of default. |
| Termination for Convenience - Notice Period | Refers to provisions setting out the notice period required for a party to terminate a contract for convenience. For example, without a specific reason for doing so and without having to show any breach or default by the other party. |

title: French Language Support
text:
e notice period required for a party to terminate a contract for convenience. For example, without a specific reason for doing so and without having to show any breach or default by the other party. |

See the [Extracted Agreement Insights and Definitions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=uwk1702945852405.html&_LANG=enus "Look up the names and definitions for the attributes and values that are surfaced in Agreement Manager agreements with AI. This topic answers the question: What data is being extracted from my Agreement Manager agreements?") to learn more about the attributes and field values that are surfaced in your agreements.

title: French Language Support
heading_path: Limitation: "Dropdown" attributes (fields) with pre-populated value sets
text:
For attributes (fields) that use dropdowns with pre-populated value sets, only those values are surfaced in your agreements. Agreement processing extracts an attribute value only if it matches one of the values in the dropdown.

When processing cannot locate the value within the agreement, the extracted value is *Other*. For example, if an agreement's payment term is 50 days, the surfaced Payment Term value is *Other*:

![A screenshot of the Party Term filter that shows the pre-populated value set for the attribute (field).](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Payment-term-filter_All-options.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTYxNDksInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.0sR82INgrl_yMhmDDu9nW5fYgSI2m99jAil2Z4hlnbA&_LANG=enus)

This limitation applies to these attributes (fields) and values:

| Attribute (Field) | Value Set |
| --- | --- |
| Assignment (Change of Control) | No, or consent required; Yes, notice required; Yes, restrictions apply; Yes, with conditions |
| Assignment (General) | No, or consent required; Yes; Yes, with conditions |
| Assignment (Termination Rights) | Yes |
| Employment Type | Fixed Term; Full Time; Intern; Part Time |
| Late Fees Apply | Yes; No |
| NDA Type | Mutual; One Way |
| Party Role | Buyer, Seller |
| Payment Term | 15 days; 30 days; 45 days; 60 days; 7 days; 90 days; >90 days; On Receipt; Other; Under 7 days |
| Renewal Type | Auto-Renew; Evergreen; Fixed Term; Renewal Option; Unspecified |

title: French Language Support
text:
Obligations are surfaced in English (U.S.) agreements only. The AI model that processes obligations is run from a U.S. server and can only process English agreements in this region.

If your language and region are not supported, manually create the obligations to include this information in dashboards, reports, and other insights.

See these support articles to learn more about obligation management:

- [Obligation Management](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=csm1727128259486.html&_LANG=enus "Read about obligation management, including how to use this functionality to capture, track, and fulfill your agreement obligations. This topic answers the question: How can I find and track my agreement obligations?")
- [Manually Create an Obligation](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=rrf1727128588540.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to create an obligation from the Agreement preview page for a selected agreement.")

title: French Language Support
text:
During agreement processing, the supported agreement types are identified in your agreements. Once identified, the fields that are associated with the agreement type are surfaced in the agreement.

Review the table to see which agreement types are supported in French agreements. The types are grouped under three categories: *Business Services*, *Human Resources*, and *Miscellaneous*.

| Business Services |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Consulting Agreement | Credit Card Agreement | Engagement Letter |
| Franchise Agreement | Investment Account Agreement | Joint Venture Agreement |
| Letter of Intent | Loan | Marketing |
| Master Service Agreement | Memorandum of Understanding | Non-Disclosure Agreement |
| Order Form | Partnership Agreement | Proposal |
| Purchase Agreement | Purchase Order | Quote |
| Service Level Agreement | Services Agreement | Statement of Work |
| Stock Purchase Agreement | Subscription | Supply/Distribution |
| Wealth Management Agreement |  |  |

| Human Resources |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Contractor Agreement | Employment Separation Agreement | Offer Letter |

| Miscellaneous |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Addendum | Amendment | Appendix |
| Attachment | Certificate of Insurance | Change Order |
| Event | Exhibit | Fee |
| Intellectual Property Assignment Agreement | Lease | License |
| Miscellaneous | Privacy and Security | Publishing Agreement |
| Release/Waiver | Renewal | Retainer |
| Supplemental Document | Termination | Terms and Conditions |

See these topics to learn more about agreement type management and agreement types:

This topic answers the question: What is agreement type management and how does it benefit me?")
- [Agreement Type Definitions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=cfy1747077418341.html&_LANG=enus "Look up definitions for Docusign-provided agreement types.")
- [Standard Fields for Agreement and Non-Agreement Types](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=smm1727282189861.html&_LANG=enus "This topic answers the question: What fields are associated with Docusign-provided agreement types? Look up the standard fields that are associated with each agreement and non-agreement type.")

title: Use Docusign FedRAMP-Certified Apps
text:
Learn how to identify FedRAMP-certified apps in the Docusign App Center.

When FedRAMP customers access the Docusign App Center, they must know which
apps are FedRAMP compliant. Using this information, customers can make informed decisions
about app installation and creating connections. Administrators and non-admin users can now
install and use FedRAMP compliant apps in Workflow Builder and Agreement Manager.

Currently, these FedRAMP-certified apps are available in the Docusign App Center:

- Coupa
- Email Verification
- Microsoft Dynamics 365
- SharePoint
- Salesforce
- Smarty
- SSN and FEIN Verification
- Twillio
- Vonage

The FedRAMP-certified badge identifies FedRAMP-certified apps in the Docusign App Center. The badge displays on the app’s tile in the App Center page or the app's
detail page as shown in this example.

![A screenshot of the FedRAMP-certified badge in the Docusign App Center.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/ac-fedramp-tile-ex.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTgyODEsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.Uez-WVtYZN4Z8zqOzB86zfkjop5THMmNs-jKErQGbx4&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the FedRAMP-certified badge in the Docusign App Center.")

title: Use Docusign FedRAMP-Certified Apps
text:

From the App Center page

From the Extension Apps page

title: View Assigned Obligations
text:
Learn how to view your assigned obligations.

Assigned obligation owners are not notified when an obligation is assigned to them. However, you can find assigned obligations from the Obligations dashboard by applying filters.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Insights from the global header.

   The Insights hub opens and the Overview dashboard is displayed.
3. Select Obligations from the navigation pane on the left. It is located in the Dashboards section.

   The Obligations dashboard view opens.
4. Filter the dashboard to locate your assigned obligations:

   ![A screenshot of the Obligation Owner filter that is expanded to show the search field and user names.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Obligation-Management_View-assigned_Select-Obligation-Owner.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcxNjcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.Dolht3X7mtyo7kL8zu26gSkB-wq60XG-ZvMRCFqPp64&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Obligation Owner filter that is expanded to show the search field and user names.")

   1. Expand (select) the Obligation Owner filter.

      The Obligation Owner filter opens.
   2. Enter the relevant name in the Search field.

      Users that match the entered search word are displayed in a type-ahead dropdown list.

      Choose the relevant name in the list and select the Apply option.

   The Obligations dashboard updates and obligations assigned to the selected owner are reflected in the charts.
5. Access the detailed report view for the Obligations by type chart.

   ![A snippet of the Obligations dashboard that highlights the View Report option for the Obligations by type chart.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Obligation-Management_View-assigned_View-obligations-by-type-report.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcxNjcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.Dolht3X7mtyo7kL8zu26gSkB-wq60XG-ZvMRCFqPp64&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Obligations dashboard that highlights the View Report option for the Obligations by type chart.")

   - Select the Obligations by type chart heading.
   - Expand the More actions (three dots) menu and select the View Report option.

   The detailed report view opens for the selected chart. Your assigned obligations are represented in the chart and listed in the table.
6. Apply filters and customize the columns in the table as needed.
7. Choose to download or save the report.

   ![A snippet of the report view that highlights the More actions (three dots) menu used to download or save the report.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Report-view_Dropdown-expanded.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcxNjcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.Dolht3X7mtyo7kL8zu26gSkB-wq60XG-ZvMRCFqPp64&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the report view that highlights the More actions (three dots) menu used to download or save the report.")

   - Expand (select) the More actions (three dots) menu from the top right and select the Download option.

     The report is downloaded to your local Downloads folder.
   - Expand (select) the More actions (three dots) menu from the top right and select the Save As option. The Save Report dialog box opens.

     Enter a Report name and select the Confirm option. The report is saved to the Custom tab on the Reports view.
8. Set up one-time or recurring email report notifications. Select the Manage notifications (bell) icon and complete the Create subscription dialog box.

title: View Assigned Obligations
text:
is saved to the Custom tab on the Reports view.
8. Set up one-time or recurring email report notifications. Select the Manage notifications (bell) icon and complete the Create subscription dialog box.

   ![A screenshot of the Create subscription dialog box that is used to subscribe to report notifications.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Create-Subscription.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcxNjcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.Dolht3X7mtyo7kL8zu26gSkB-wq60XG-ZvMRCFqPp64&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Create subscription dialog box that is used to subscribe to report notifications.")

   See the [Subscribe to Receive Report Notifications](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=wfw1734545937270.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to set up one-time or recurring email reports for yourself or others to receive notifications about your obligations, renewals, or agreements.") topic to learn more about this feature.
9. Return to the Obligations dashboard. Select the Obligations option from the breadcrumb navigation at the top of the page:

   ![A snippet of the Obligations by type report that highlights the breadcrumb navigation.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Report-dashboard_Review-view_breadcrumb-nav-example.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcxNjcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.Dolht3X7mtyo7kL8zu26gSkB-wq60XG-ZvMRCFqPp64&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Obligations by type report that highlights the breadcrumb navigation.")

   You are returned to the Obligations dashboard.

You have successfully reviewed your assigned obligations.

title: Download an Agreement
text:
Learn how to download an agreement from Agreement Manager. This topic answers the question: How
can I download an agreement to share with others?

Use the Download feature to download and share agreements with key
stakeholders, for example during negotiations. This feature is available from the
agreement list table (Completed Documents view) and the
Agreement preview page.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign
   Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the
   AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed.
4. Perform a keyword search and use the filters to locate the agreement that you
   want to download.

   The agreement list table updates and agreements that match the entered search
   criteria and applied fields are displayed.
5. Expand the More actions (three dots) menu
   for the agreement that you want to download and select
   Download:

   ![A snippet of the Completed Documents view that shows the Download option in the More actions (three dots) menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed_documents_table_vertical_ellipses_Download.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY2NjUsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.NyrVUS3LCp65g3rJgyBC42IDJpywI9nZw61i0HtCCCY&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Completed Documents view that shows the Download option in the More actions (three dots) menu.")

   The agreement downloads to your local Downloads
   folder.
6. You may want to view the agreement before downloading the file. Select the
   agreement that you want to review.

   The Agreement preview page opens.

   ![A screenshot of the Agreement preview page that highlights the Download button in the upper right corner.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement_Preview_page_Select_Download.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY2NjUsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.NyrVUS3LCp65g3rJgyBC42IDJpywI9nZw61i0HtCCCY&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Agreement preview page that highlights the Download button in the upper right corner.")
7. Review the agreement as needed.
8. Select Download from the top-right corner.

   The agreement is downloaded to your local Downloads folder.

   You can share the downloaded file with key stakeholders.
9. Select the Close (x) icon from the top-left
   corner.

   You are returned to the Completed Documents view. Your
   entered search criteria and applied filters are still in effect.
10. Download multiple agreements from the agreement list table:

    ![A snippet of the Completed Documents view that highlights the Download Documents option for a group of selected agreements.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-documents-table_Download-Documents_Highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY2NjUsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.NyrVUS3LCp65g3rJgyBC42IDJpywI9nZw61i0HtCCCY&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Completed Documents view that highlights the Download Documents option for a group of selected agreements.")

    1. Select the agreements that you want to download.

       - Select individual agreements.
       - Select the Choose all checkbox from the
         header row to select all the agreements on that page of
         results.
       - Select the Select all that match your
         filters link to select all the agreements in the
         table that match your applied filters.

       - Select the Select all that match your
