title: Manage Agreement Sets
text:
Read about the agreement set management functions, such as editing smart filters and updating share settings.

Contract managers and users who are assigned the Organize and manage all agreements permission can create and update agreement sets.

Set management tasks are completed at the set-level. Locate the set that you want to update from the table on the All agreement sets view:

![A snippet of the All agreement sets view that highlights the set management capabilities in the More actions (three dots) menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-set-management_Actions_expanded.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTczOTIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.0spuCcmPWNKhSMuoIg08gQzWJ8REYtmkymrOQ5CPIPo&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the All agreement sets view that highlights the set management capabilities in the More actions (three dots) menu.")

Every set has a More actions (three dots) menu. Use this menu to perform set management tasks, such as:

- **Edit details:** used to update the set's name and description.
- **Edit smart filters:** used to add or update the set's smart filters.
- **Share:** used to share the set with additional users or groups, and update or remove set access.
- **Delete:** used to delete the set.

  Important: All users and groups with whom the set was shared will lose access once the set is deleted. Deleting the set does not delete or void any agreements.

See these topics to learn more about managing your agreement sets:

- [Edit Agreement Set Details](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=idk1736186497685.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to update the details for an agreement set, including the set name and description.")
- [Edit Smart Filters for an Agreement Set](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=fev1750785436374.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to update the smart filters for an agreement set.")
- [Update Share Settings for an Agreement Set](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=swq1750785657450.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to update the sharing settings for an agreement set, including granting and revoking access to a set.")
- [Delete an Agreement Set](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=dgr1750785460659.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to delete an agreement set.")

title: Obligation Management
text:
Read about obligation management, including how to use this functionality to capture, track, and fulfill your agreement obligations. This topic answers the question: How can I find and track my agreement obligations?

Obligation management helps contract managers to capture, track, and fulfill their contract
commitments. Obligations are the actions or commitments that one or more
parties agree to fulfill as part of an agreement. They are made up of
attributes and extracted contract data that is surfaced from uploaded and
ingested documents.

title: Obligation Management
text:
Obligation management is primarily used by contract managers. This is a broad persona that
includes procurement managers, category managers, and legal
operations. Consider these pain points for each role:

- Identifying important obligations can be tedious and time-consuming
- No easy way to monitor and collaborate on obligations
- Lack of fulfillment tracking often leads to lost revenue

title: Obligation Management
text:
Contractual data points, or attributes, are surfaced either manually or with AI and provide an in-depth view of the obligations contained in all agreement types. With these insights, contract managers can capture, track, and fulfill obligations from signature to expiration.

The obligation management process includes these steps:

1. **Capture obligations:** obligations are surfaced with AI or are created manually by contract
   managers.
2. **Monitor obligations:** obligations are tracked at the agreement- and
   party-level.
3. **Subscribe to email report notifications:** set up one-time or recurring
   email reports to get notified of upcoming due dates.
4. **Report on obligations:** the Obligations dashboard
   provides a visual summary of agreement obligations and access to a variety
   of reports.

title: Obligation Management
text:
During processing, key data points are surfaced in your agreements, including payment, renewal,
and termination obligations. Each obligation is made up of a number
of attributes (fields) that are also surfaced in your
agreements.

Note: The obligations listed
in the table are extracted from your documents if they
exist within the document.

| Obligation type | Obligation name | Extracted attributes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Payment | One-Time Payment | Obligation Name (One-Time Payment), Type (Payment), Status, Payment Value, Due Date, Frequency (One Time), Obligated Party, Discounts, and Repercussions |
| Deposit Payment | Obligation Name (Deposit Payment), Type (Payment), Status, Payment Value, Due Date, Frequency (One Time), Obligated Party, Discount, and Repercussions |
| Renewal | Renewal Notice | Obligation Name (Renewal Notice), Type (Renewal), Status, Notice Date, Notice Period, Obligated Party, and Repercussions |
| Termination | Termination for Cause Notice | Obligation Name (Termination for Cause Notice), Type (Termination), Status, Notice Date, Notice Period, Obligated Party, and Repercussions |
| Termination for Convenience Notice | Obligation Name (Termination for Convenience Notice), Type (Termination), Status, Notice Date, Notice Period, Obligated Party, and Repercussions |

Important:

Obligations are surfaced in English (U.S.) agreements only. The AI model that processes obligations is run from a U.S. server and can only process English agreements in this region.

If your language and region are not supported, manually create the obligations to include this information in dashboards, reports, and other insights.

title: Obligation Management
text:
A number of obligation types can be manually created from an agreement:![A snippet of the Agreement preview page that highlights the Create Obligation option.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Obligation-mgmt_Create-obligation-example.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcxMzcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.xebPLFG5xouk8WgT_edHtnaQzKSLJ1KC31tOCOPX4Wk&_LANG=enus)

- Compliance obligations: specific rules, laws, regulations, or policies that an organization is legally or ethically required to follow.
- Confidentiality obligations: duty to use confidential information only for its intended purpose.
- Data breach obligations: legal requirement for an organization to notify affected individuals and relevant authorities when a data breach occurs.
- Escalation obligations: provisions that allow for increases in wages or prices if economic conditions change throughout the contract.
- Indemnification obligations: agreements that require one party to compensate another party for incurred losses.
- Insurance obligations: duties or
  responsibilities that either the insurer or the policyholder
  has under an insurance contract.
- Limitation of liability obligations: clauses that restrict the amount of damages one party can claim from another in the event of a breach or legal issue.
- Miscellaneous obligations: types of documents used in the financial systems of some government agencies, or a type of payment made by the government.
- Notification obligations: legal or contractual requirements for one party to inform another party about specific events, circumstances, or actions.
- Payment obligations: requirement to make payments on a transaction or debt.
- Renewal notification obligations: legal requirements to send a clear and timely notice to customers about their upcoming renewals (that will automatically renew unless canceled).
- Service level agreements obligations: commitments made by a service provider to a customer, outlining the specific standards of service they will deliver. Often these obligations include details like response times and resolution procedures.
- Subcontracting obligations: the legal
  responsibility an organization (subcontractor) assumes when
  they agree to perform a part of a larger project for another
  organization (main contractor).
- Termination obligations: clauses that
  require one party to provide advance notice to another party
  before terminating the contract due to a specific breach or
  violation.
- Warranty obligations: legal
  commitments made by a seller or manufacturer to repair,
  replace, or provide compensation for a product that fails to
  meet certain quality standards.

Tip:

See the [Manually Create an Obligation](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=rrf1727128588540.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to create an obligation from the Agreement preview page for a selected agreement.") article to learn more about creating obligations from clauses in your agreements.

title: Obligation Management
text:
Obligation statuses provide a quick look at where the obligation is within its life cycle.

![A snippet of an obligation report that highlights the Obligation Status filter and column.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Obligation-mgmt_Obligation-Status_higlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcxMzcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.xebPLFG5xouk8WgT_edHtnaQzKSLJ1KC31tOCOPX4Wk&_LANG=enus)

Each obligation has a status. These statuses are calculated based on the data points that are surfaced in your agreements:

| Status | Calculation explanation |
| --- | --- |
| Active | This status is applied to obligations that do not have a due date. Active obligations are extracted from active and in-effect agreements:  - Effective date is equal to or before the current date (the agreement is "active") - Expiration date is equal to or after the current date (the agreement has not "expired") |
| Scheduled | This status is applied to obligations with due dates that are greater than 30 days in the future from the current date. Scheduled obligations are extracted from active and in-effect agreements:  - Effective date is equal to or before the current date (the agreement is "active") - Expiration date is equal to or after the current date (the agreement has not "expired") |
| Due soon | This status is applied to obligations with due dates that are equal to or less than 30 days in the future from the current date. Due soon obligations are extracted from active and in-effect agreements:  - Effective date is equal to or before the current date (the agreement is "active") - Expiration date is equal to or after the current date (the agreement has not "expired") |
| Passed | This status is applied to obligations with due dates that are before the current date. |
| Inactive | This status is applied to obligations where the associated agreements are either not yet effective or expired:  - Effective date is after the current date (the agreement and associated   obligations are not yet effective) Note: If an   obligation has a due date but the associated   agreement is not yet effective, the obligation   status is   Inactive. - Expiration date is before the current date and the obligations do not have a due date value Note: If the obligation is missing a due date and the associated agreement is expired, the obligation status is Inactive. |

Important: The obligation status is calculated continuously based on the extracted agreement data. When editing an obligation's status, only the possible status options are displayed - based on the ongoing calculations.![A snippet of the Edit Obligation pane that highlights the Status dropdown.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Obligation-mgmt_Edit-Obligation_Status-dropdown_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcxMzcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.xebPLFG5xouk8WgT_edHtnaQzKSLJ1KC31tOCOPX4Wk&_LANG=enus)

The remaining statuses are not calculated. Users are responsible for marking obligations with these statuses:

- Dependent: the obligation is dependent on another event happening (circumstantial obligation)
- Fulfilled: the obligation has been completed (the obligated party fulfilled the obligation)
- Unfulfilled: the obligation has been breached (the obligated party failed to fulfill the obligation)

title: Obligation Management
text:
There are three primary ways to access your obligation information:

- **Obligations dashboard:** located under the Reports menu, the Obligations dashboard displays obligations by due date and type.

  You can filter the obligations list, subscribe to notifications, and download or save obligation reports.

  See the [Obligations Dashboard](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=qal1734545813030.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the Obligations dashboard, including available reports and filter options.") to learn more about the obligations dashboard reports.
- **Party profile - Obligations tab:** located in the party
  hub, the Obligations tab shows a list
  of obligations that are tied to a specific party or alias
  group.

  See the [View Obligations for Selected Parties and Groups](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=brh1742456493450.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to view all the obligations that are tied to a specific party or alias group.") to learn more about viewing obligations from the
  party hub.
- **Agreement preview - Obligations pane:** located on the
  Agreement preview page, the
  Obligations pane displays
  obligations that are associated with a selected agreement.

  The pane displays details for both AI-assisted
  and manually created obligations, including the
  name, type, status, value, and frequency.

  See
  the [Complete a Data Review for Extracted Obligations](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=fwg1749056504311.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to complete a data review to verify the obligations that were surfaced in your agreements.") to learn
  more about reviewing obligations from the
  Agreement preview page.

title: Obligation Management
text:
The Obligations dashboard consists of various charts. When you select any of the charts, a detailed report view opens. From this view, select Subscribe and complete the fields in the Create subscription dialog box.

![A screenshot of the Create subscription dialog box that shows the subscription configuration fields.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Create-Subscription.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcxMzcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.xebPLFG5xouk8WgT_edHtnaQzKSLJ1KC31tOCOPX4Wk&_LANG=enus)

After completing the necessary fields, select Schedule. You will now receive report notifications about the obligations in the selected report. See the [Subscribe to Receive Report Notifications](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=wfw1734545937270.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to set up one-time or recurring email reports for yourself or others to receive notifications about your obligations, renewals, or agreements.") to learn more about subscribing to receive email notifications for your obligations.

title: Install Apps in the Docusign App Center
heading_path: Install Apps in the Docusign App Center
text:
Learn how to install Docusign extension apps in the Docusign App Center.

Your access level determines the types of apps you can install.

Docusign account administrators

You must be an account administrator to install and manage packaged and partner apps from l Docusign App Center. See the [Organization Administrators](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=rrf1583359212854&topicId=god1583359152535.html&_LANG=enus) article to learn more about these permissions.

Non-admin users

If your organization has a Docusign plan that includes packaged apps, you can install these approved apps. See the [Use Approved Apps](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=aak1747679824594.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about Docusign approved extension apps and who can install and use.") article for detailed information.

You install apps at the account level. View and change accounts by selecting your profile picture and selecting Switch Account.

![A screenshot of the switch account option.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/switch-account.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTgwNTcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.h4pmEHAfgXFoQQtKWimS6GsR4djf84Ae4GHLtlnti3M&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the switch account option.")

l Docusign workflows and eSignature templates are affected when you uninstall and reinstall apps and their connections.

Important:

- - If you uninstall and reinstall, an app, you must reinstall and reconfigure the eSignature template or the app step in the workflow.
  - If you delete an app's connection, you must create a new connection and reconfigure the app step with this new connection. See the [Create Docusign App Connections](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=zsb1714159452358.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to create Docusign apps connections for different user credentials in Docusign agreement workflows. You can create connections that are shared by all account users or used privately by the user who created the app connection.") article for details.
  - You can refresh an installed app's connection without deleting without affecting a workflow or template. See the [Reconnect a Docusign App Connection](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=qkv1716320270335.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to use the Docusign app Reconnect a connection feature to reestablish the Docusign app’s connection to workflows.") article for details.
  - Reinstall any apps that were previously installed from the Docusign App Center. Due to version issues, backward compatibility is not currently possible
  - Reconnect all Workflow Builder fields after upgrading.

You can install Docusign App Center apps:

- - On the Docusign App Center home page: Select the app tile and select Install App.
- - From an app's detail page: Select  Install App from any detail page.

1. Access  [Docusign App Center](https://apps.docusign.com/app-center) to begin using extension apps in your Docusign agreement workflows.

   Here is an example of the Docusign App Center page.

   ![A screenshot of the Docusign App Center dashboard.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/ac-newest-dash.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTgwNTcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.h4pmEHAfgXFoQQtKWimS6GsR4djf84Ae4GHLtlnti3M&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Docusign App Center dashboard.")
2. Select Login to log on to your account.

   - Note the following:

title: Install Apps in the Docusign App Center
heading_path: Install Apps in the Docusign App Center
text:
F6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.h4pmEHAfgXFoQQtKWimS6GsR4djf84Ae4GHLtlnti3M&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Docusign App Center dashboard.")
2. Select Login to log on to your account.

   - Note the following:

     - Some apps do not require authentication or an account connection.
     - The Install App  option is grayed out If you are not authorized to use an app. A message banner provides the reasoning. See the [Docusign App Center Banner Messages and App Labels](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=map1704218548543.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about the messages and availability app labels that you encounter when using Docusign App Center and the actions you should take.") to learn more.
3. Optionally refine your search as described in the [Locate Docusign Apps for Your Workflow Processes](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=ljy1744998611944.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to locate apps you can use in your workflow processes by using the Docusign Search feature and Works With and Industry filters.")  article.
4. Select an app to install.

   The app's detail page displays. From this page, you can:

   - Install and authorize the app to use with platform services.
   - Review the app's overview, requirements, and resources.
5. Select Install App.
6. At the prompt, select  Install and Authorize to connect the app.
7. Select Continue.
8. If the app requires authorization, select Connect Account to connect the app to your Docusign account.
9. If you have Administrator privileges, select Manage > Add New Connection and specify the App connection type. See the [Create Docusign App Connections](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=zsb1714159452358.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to create Docusign apps connections for different user credentials in Docusign agreement workflows. You can create connections that are shared by all account users or used privately by the user who created the app connection.") article for detailed information.

   These connection types are available:

   - Shared: Created by an Admin. Any account user can view and use the connection.
   - Private: Created by an Admin or an account user. Only the account user who created the connection can view and use the connection.
10. Authorize the application for use in Docusign by entering your credentials for the app.
11. Select Continue.

    The app is now ready to use to customize and extend agreement processes across multiple Docusign product surfaces, including Docusign Workflow Builder, the workflow builder tool.
12. To use the app in a platform service, such as Workflow Builder, select Use this app.

    The platform service displays. For example, if you want to use the app in a workflow, the workflows page displays.

    To learn more about using workflows, see the [Workflow Builder Templates](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=irb1736981148403.html&_LANG=enus) article.

title: View and Download Processing Results
text:
Learn how to view and download results for processing jobs.

The Monitor All Jobs function gives you access to job details for agreement processing jobs. These jobs are initiated when historical agreements are processed for new insights or attributes (fields). For example, when enabling AI detection for custom fields and agreement types.

Access the Monitor All Jobs function from the More actions menu on the Completed Documents page.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Completed Documents page opens. A list of documents to which the logged-in user has access is displayed in the table.
4. Expand the More actions menu located to the right of the filters and select the Monitor All Jobs option:

   ![A screenshot of the Completed Documents page that shows the Uploads and Processing Status option highlighted in the Settings (gear) menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-documents-view_Actions_Monitor-All-Jobs-option.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY4NTgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.gXAubmAMALCtSWb71Le8cfiAv_leUirzTHDzgl0rtIg&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Completed Documents page that shows the Uploads and Processing Status option highlighted in the Settings (gear) menu.")

   The job monitoring hub opens and the All jobs section is displayed. This section has two tabs: Uploaded and Processing. The Uploaded tab is displayed by default.
5. Select the Processing tab:

   ![A snippet of the All jobs section that highlights the Processing tab.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Monitor-All-Jobs_All-jobs_select-Processing-tab.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY4NTgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.gXAubmAMALCtSWb71Le8cfiAv_leUirzTHDzgl0rtIg&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the All jobs section that highlights the Processing tab.")

   The Processing tab opens. A list of processing jobs are displayed in the Processing agreements table.

   ![A screenshot of the Processing agreements table that shows the job details for each processing job.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Monitor-All-Jobs_Processing_Processing-agreements.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY4NTgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.gXAubmAMALCtSWb71Le8cfiAv_leUirzTHDzgl0rtIg&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Processing agreements table that shows the job details for each processing job.")
6. Review the list of processing jobs. The created date, job name, user, insight, number of files processed, and the status are displayed for each job.
7. Use the filters to locate details for relevant processing jobs:

   ![A snippet of the Processing agreements table that highlights the Created date and Status filters.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Monitor-All-Jobs_Processing_Processing-agreements_Filters.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY4NTgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.gXAubmAMALCtSWb71Le8cfiAv_leUirzTHDzgl0rtIg&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Processing agreements table that highlights the Created date and Status filters.")

   - Expand the Created date filter, select an identifier tab, and complete the appropriate date fields.

     Identifier tab choices are: Between, Before, and After.

     Tip: If you choose Between, you must complete the From and To date fields.
   - Expand the Status filter and select the relevant status.

title: View and Download Processing Results
text:
Identifier tab choices are: Between, Before, and After.

     Tip: If you choose Between, you must complete the From and To date fields.
   - Expand the Status filter and select the relevant status.

     Choices are: Completed, Failed, and Processing.

   The processing jobs that match the applied filters are displayed in the table.

   Tip: Use the sorting arrows located in the column headers to sort the table by the corresponding column. You can sort by Created Date, Job Name, Files, and Status.
8. Select the View details button to view processing results for the corresponding job.

   The processing details page opens for the selected job. The Processing status and Job details are displayed.

   ![A screenshot of the Processing status page that shows processing results for the corresponding job and a download option.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Monitor-All-Jobs_Proessing_Processing-with-Docusign-AI_Processing-status.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY4NTgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.gXAubmAMALCtSWb71Le8cfiAv_leUirzTHDzgl0rtIg&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Processing status page that shows processing results for the corresponding job and a download option.")

   Tip: If any files could not be processed, select the Download CSV option to download the processing results:![A snippet of the processing details page that shows the Download CSV option in the Processing status section.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Job-monitoring_Processing-status_Download-CSV-highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY4NTgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.gXAubmAMALCtSWb71Le8cfiAv_leUirzTHDzgl0rtIg&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the processing details page that shows the Download CSV option in the Processing status section.")

   The file is downloaded to your local Downloads folder.
9. A list of processed files is displayed in the Processing status details table. Use the available actions to:

   ![A snippet of the Processing status details table that lists the files that were processed in the selected job.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Job-monitoring_Processing-details_Processing-status-details-table.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY4NTgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.gXAubmAMALCtSWb71Le8cfiAv_leUirzTHDzgl0rtIg&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Processing status details table that lists the files that were processed in the selected job.")

   1. Perform a keyword search to locate the file that you want to review. Enter the file name in the search field and select Enter.

      A type-ahead dropdown opens, and a list of files that match the keyword are displayed.
   2. Filter the table by Status and Error Message to refine the table. Expand the filter, select or enter a value, and select Apply.

      A list of files that match the applied filters are displayed in the table.
   3. Select the View File option associated with the file that you want to view.

      The Agreement preview page opens in a new tab. The Details pane lists the attribute values that were surfaced within the selected file.

      Close the tab to return to the processing details page.
10. Select the Close (X) icon from the top-right corner to return to the Processing agreements table.

You have successfully viewed and downloaded processing results from the job monitoring hub.

title: Upload Documents to Agreement Manager
text:
Learn how to use the Upload documents function to upload agreements to Agreement Manager. This topic answers the question: How do I upload documents to Agreement Manager?

You can upload up to five documents directly to the repository without being assigned Agreement Manager
permissions. Once uploaded, the documents are accessible for users and groups with
the appropriate permissions.

Important: Limitations of single document uploads include:

- File names must contain fewer than 255 characters
- Files must be less than 100 MB each

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed.
4. Expand the Add (+) dropdown from the top-right and select the Upload option:

   ![A screenshot of the Add (+) dropdown menu that highlights the Upload option.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-documents-table_Upload-icon-highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY3MzAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.rbPC0MLHc-v66ODrOOcXb6d1fF-LItwkuGdtRbo9siE&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Add (+) dropdown menu that highlights the Upload option.")

   The Upload documents dialog box opens.

   ![A screenshot of the Upload documents dialog box.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-documents-view_Upload-documents-dialog-box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY3MzAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.rbPC0MLHc-v66ODrOOcXb6d1fF-LItwkuGdtRbo9siE&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Upload documents dialog box.")
5. Search for and select up to five files to upload to Agreement Manager:
   - Drag the files to the dialog box inside of the Drag and drop file here box.
   - Expand the Select File dropdown and select the Browse option to search for and select files from local folders to upload:

     ![A screenshot of the Select File dropdown that shows the installed apps and the Browse option.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Mini-App-Center_Select-File-dropdown.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY3MzAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.rbPC0MLHc-v66ODrOOcXb6d1fF-LItwkuGdtRbo9siE&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Select File dropdown that shows the installed apps and the Browse option.")

   The selected files are displayed in the Upload documents dialog box.

   ![A screenshot of the Upload documents dialog box that shows the selected files were successfully uploaded.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-documents-view_Upload-documents-dialog-box_with-docs.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY3MzAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.rbPC0MLHc-v66ODrOOcXb6d1fF-LItwkuGdtRbo9siE&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Upload documents dialog box that shows the selected files were successfully uploaded.")

title: Upload Documents to Agreement Manager
text:
InNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.rbPC0MLHc-v66ODrOOcXb6d1fF-LItwkuGdtRbo9siE&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Upload documents dialog box that shows the selected files were successfully uploaded.")

   Important: If you are assigned the *bulk upload* permission, the Upload
   documents dialog box is similar to the bulk upload
   experience. You have the option to enter a Job
   name and Add more files before
   proceeding to the Next step.Complete the
   Allow access step to choose how to share the
   uploaded documents with others:![A screenshot of the Upload documents dialog box that shows the Allow access step.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Upload-documents_Bulk-upload_Step-2.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY3MzAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.rbPC0MLHc-v66ODrOOcXb6d1fF-LItwkuGdtRbo9siE&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Upload documents dialog box that shows the Allow access step.")See the [Bulk Upload Documents and Set Access Controls](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=leq1712700300298.html&_LANG=enus "This topic answers the question: How do I bulk upload documents to Agreement Manager? Learn how to use the bulk upload function to upload more than five documents to Agreement Manager.") topic to learn more
   about access options for uploaded documents.
6. Select Done.

   The dialog box closes and you are returned to the Completed Documents view. A message states that the upload was successful and the files are being processed with AI.

title: View the Overview Dashboard for Selected Parties and Groups
heading_path: View the Overview Dashboard for Selected Parties and Groups
text:
Learn how to view a visual summary of all agreements and obligations that are tied to a
specific party or alias group.

The "party hub" provides a unified summary of all agreements and obligations that are
tied to parties and groups. Access the party hub from various places in Agreement Manager, including:

- The Parties column (Completed Documents
  view)
- The Details pane (Agreement preview page)
- The Name column (Parties page)

Note: This topic reviews the steps to access the party hub from
the Completed Documents view. You can also search for and
select the relevant party from the Parties page.

![A snippet of the Parties page that highlights the Search parties field.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Party-hub_Parties-page_Search-parties-highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc4MDQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.vfO-rsXGliffUpJjfdo3EUhhsuKKAKYbz3DlWAKOOJU&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Parties page that highlights the Search parties field.")

- Go to Home  >  Agreements and select All from the PARTIES section in the navigation pane.
- Enter search criteria in the Search parties
  field.
- Select a party from the search results.

The party hub opens for the selected party or group.

**Access the party hub from the Completed Documents view:**

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign
   Agreements page opens. The Inbox view
   is displayed by default.
3. Select Completed from the
   AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Completed Documents view opens. Filter this view
   for a specific party.
4. Filter the agreement list table for a specific party:

   ![A screenshot of the Parties filter that shows the Search field, Party Role dropdown, and the party list.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-documents-table_Parties-dialog-box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc4MDQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.vfO-rsXGliffUpJjfdo3EUhhsuKKAKYbz3DlWAKOOJU&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Parties filter that shows the Search field, Party Role dropdown, and the party list.")

   1. Expand the Filters option and select the Parties filter.
   2. Enter a keyword or search string in the Search field.

      As you enter search criteria, the list of parties updates to match the keyword.
   3. Select the relevant party.

      Important: When using the Parties filter, you must also select a
      Party Role. If you do not know
      which role to select, accept the default value, All.
   4. Apply the Party Role filter to view agreements where the selected party plays a specific role. If you do not know which role to select, accept the default value, All.

      Select a role from the Party Role dropdown. Choose from: Buyer, Seller, or Other.
   5. Select the Apply option.

   The table updates and agreements that are tied to the selected party or group are
   displayed. The names in the Parties column are clickable
   and link directly to the party hub.

   ![A snippet of the Completed Documents view that highlights the Parties column as an entry point for the party hub.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Party-hub_Entry-point_Completed-example.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc4MDQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.vfO-rsXGliffUpJjfdo3EUhhsuKKAKYbz3DlWAKOOJU&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Completed Documents view that highlights the Parties column as an entry point for the party hub.")
5. Select a name from the Parties column.

   The party hub opens for the selected party or group. The
   Overview tab is displayed by default.

title: View the Overview Dashboard for Selected Parties and Groups
heading_path: View the Overview Dashboard for Selected Parties and Groups
text:
he Parties column as an entry point for the party hub.")
5. Select a name from the Parties column.

   The party hub opens for the selected party or group. The
   Overview tab is displayed by default.

   ![A screenshot of the party hub's Overview dashboard that shows high-level agreement insights for a selected party or group.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Party-management_Party-hub_Overview_Fontara-example.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc4MDQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.vfO-rsXGliffUpJjfdo3EUhhsuKKAKYbz3DlWAKOOJU&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the party hub's Overview dashboard that shows high-level agreement insights for a selected party or group.")

   The Overview dashboard displays a visual summary of all
   active agreements that are tied to a specific party or alias group.

   Tip: Use the Favorites (star) icon to "star" your most active, time-sensitive, or high-value parties and groups. Each user can add up to 50 parties and groups to their Starred list.

   Your "favorite" parties and groups are listed under the Starred tab on the Parties page.

**Filter the Overview dashboard:**

6. Filter the dashboard by Agreement Type and
   Time Period:

   ![A snippet of the Overview dashboard that highlights the Agreement Type and Time Period filters.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Party-hub_Overview-dashboard_Filters.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc4MDQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.vfO-rsXGliffUpJjfdo3EUhhsuKKAKYbz3DlWAKOOJU&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Overview dashboard that highlights the Agreement Type and Time Period filters.")

   1. Expand the filter.
   2. Select a filter value.
   3. Select the Apply option.

   The selected filters are applied to the entire dashboard.

   Note:

   Use the Party Role dropdown to filter the dashboard to show agreement insights where the selected party plays a specific role in an agreement:![A screenshot of the Party Role filter that shows the role options, Buyer, Seller, and Other.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Party-hub_Overview-dashboard_Party-Role-filter_expanded.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc4MDQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.vfO-rsXGliffUpJjfdo3EUhhsuKKAKYbz3DlWAKOOJU&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Party Role filter that shows the role options, Buyer, Seller, and Other.")

**View the Party Summary and download a report:**

7. Review the Party Summary section to view agreement insights for the selected party or group, including:

   ![A snippet of the Overview dashboard that shows the Party Summary section for a specific party or alias group.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Party-hub_Overview-dashboard_Party-Summary.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc4MDQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.vfO-rsXGliffUpJjfdo3EUhhsuKKAKYbz3DlWAKOOJU&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Overview dashboard that shows the Party Summary section for a specific party or alias group.")

   - Summary statement about the selected party or group
   - Agreement counts based on the next 90 days
     - All active agreements
     - Expiring soon
     - Up for renewal

   1. Select a tile from the In the Next 90 Days section to view the list of associated agreements. For example, view a list of Active agreements:

title: View the Overview Dashboard for Selected Parties and Groups
heading_path: View the Overview Dashboard for Selected Parties and Groups
text:
greements
     - Expiring soon
     - Up for renewal

   1. Select a tile from the In the Next 90 Days section to view the list of associated agreements. For example, view a list of Active agreements:

      ![A screenshot of the In the Next 90 Days section that shows the cursor hovering over the Active agreement tile.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Party-hub_Overview-dashboard_In-the-Next-90-Days_tile-selected.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc4MDQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.vfO-rsXGliffUpJjfdo3EUhhsuKKAKYbz3DlWAKOOJU&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the In the Next 90 Days section that shows the cursor hovering over the Active agreement tile.")

      The report view opens and a list of relevant agreements are displayed
      in the table.
   2. Use the filters to refine the agreement list as needed.

      Note: Select the All
      Filters option to choose additional filters to apply
      to the report.
   3. Select the Customize columns (toggle) icon and
      choose the columns to display in the table:

      ![A screenshot of the Customize columns dialog box that shows the columns you can choose to display in the agreements table.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Party-hub_Overview-dashboard_Customize-columns.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc4MDQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.vfO-rsXGliffUpJjfdo3EUhhsuKKAKYbz3DlWAKOOJU&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Customize columns dialog box that shows the columns you can choose to display in the agreements table.")
      - Select the toggles to turn the associated field (column) on (blue) or off
        (white).
      - Re-order the columns as needed. Hover the cursor over the Reorder (dots) icon and drag the field (column) to the appropriate location.
      - Select the Save option.

      The dialog box closes and the columns are updated according to your
      configuration.
   4. Expand the More actions (three dots) menu from the top right and select
      Download:

      ![A snippet of the Active agreements report that highlights the Download option in the More actions (three dots) menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Party-hub_Parties-page_Overview-dashboard-report_Download-option.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc4MDQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.vfO-rsXGliffUpJjfdo3EUhhsuKKAKYbz3DlWAKOOJU&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Active agreements report that highlights the Download option in the More actions (three dots) menu.")

      The associated report is downloaded to your local
      Downloads folder.
   5. Select the party name from the breadcrumb navigation at the top of the
      page.

      You are returned to the Overview
      dashboard.

**View Recent activity:**

8. Review the Recent activity section to view your most recently active agreements over a 12-month period:

   ![A screenshot of the Recent activity section that lists the party's most recently active agreements over a 12-month period.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Party-hub_Overview-dashboard_Recenty-activity-section.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc4MDQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.vfO-rsXGliffUpJjfdo3EUhhsuKKAKYbz3DlWAKOOJU&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Recent activity section that lists the party's most recently active agreements over a 12-month period.")

   Agreements are included in the chart based on the effective and expiration dates. Documents without effective or expiration dates are not included.

title: View the Overview Dashboard for Selected Parties and Groups
heading_path: View the Overview Dashboard for Selected Parties and Groups
text:
ntly active agreements over a 12-month period.")

   Agreements are included in the chart based on the effective and expiration dates. Documents without effective or expiration dates are not included.

   1. Use the Previous Month and Next Month arrows to scroll through each month in the chart.
   2. Hover your cursor over an item in the chart to view high-level agreement details, such as:

      ![A snippet of the Recent activity section that displays agreement details modal that opens when you hover over an item in the chart.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Party-hub_Overview-dashboard_Recent-activity_agreement-details_hover.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc4MDQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.vfO-rsXGliffUpJjfdo3EUhhsuKKAKYbz3DlWAKOOJU&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Recent activity section that displays agreement details modal that opens when you hover over an item in the chart.")

      - Total contract value
      - Agreement name
      - Effective date
      - Expiration date
      - Renewal types
      - Renewal term

      Use the View Agreement option to view the document from the Agreement preview page.

**View dashboard charts and download a report**

9. Review the dashboard charts to see a visual summary of Agreement types, Renewal
   types, Payment terms, and
   Obligations by due date:

   ![A snippet of the Overview dashboard that shows charts for Agreement types, Renewal types, Payment terms, and Obligations by due date.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Party-hub_Overview-dashboard_Charts.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc4MDQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.vfO-rsXGliffUpJjfdo3EUhhsuKKAKYbz3DlWAKOOJU&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Overview dashboard that shows charts for Agreement types, Renewal types, Payment terms, and Obligations by due date.")

   1. Hover your cursor over a chart segment or bar to view the number of
      representative agreements or obligations.

      Tip: The segments in the
      charts are clickable. Select a segment to view
      the associated agreements. Select the party name from the breadcrumb
      navigation at the top of the page to return to the dashboard.
   2. Expand the More actions (three dots)
      and select the View Report option:

      ![A snippet of the Renewals types chart that highlights the View Report option in the More actions (three dots).](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Party-hub_Overview-dashboard_Actions-menu_View-Report.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc4MDQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.vfO-rsXGliffUpJjfdo3EUhhsuKKAKYbz3DlWAKOOJU&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Renewals types chart that highlights the View Report option in the More actions (three dots).")

      Note: The More actions (three dots) menu also has the option to View as table
      or Download as png:
      - Select View as table to display the
        data in a table instead of a chart.
      - Select Download as png to download an
        image of the chart to your local
        Downloads folder.

      The report view opens and a list of relevant agreements or obligations
      are displayed in the table.
   3. Use the filters to refine the agreement or obligation list as
      needed.

      Note: Select the All
      Filters option to choose additional filters to apply
      to the report.
   4. Use the Customize columns (toggle) icon to choose
      the columns to display in the table:

text:
Note: Select the All
      Filters option to choose additional filters to apply
      to the report.
   4. Use the Customize columns (toggle) icon to choose
      the columns to display in the table:

      - Select the toggles to turn the associated field (column) on (blue) or off
        (white).
      - Re-order the columns as needed. Hover the cursor over the Reorder (dots) icon
        and drag the field (column) to the appropriate
        location.
      - Select the Save option.

      The dialog box closes and the columns are updated according to your
      configuration.
   5. Expand the More actions (three dots) menu from the top right and select the
      Download option:

      ![A snippet of the Renewal types report that highlights the Download option.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Party-hub_Parties-page_Overview-dashboard_Download-option-2.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc4MDQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.vfO-rsXGliffUpJjfdo3EUhhsuKKAKYbz3DlWAKOOJU&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Renewal types report that highlights the Download option.")

      The associated report is downloaded to your local
      Downloads folder.
   6. Select the party name from the breadcrumb navigation at the top of the
      page.

      You are returned to the Overview
      dashboard.
10. Select Parties from the breadcrumb navigation at the top
    of the page.

    You are returned to the Parties page.

    Tip: Select Completed
    from the navigation pane on the left to view your agreement list. The
    Completed Documents view opens. The agreements
    to which the user has access are displayed in the table.

title: Delete an Agreement Set
text:
Learn how to delete an agreement set.

Contract managers and any users who are assigned the Organize and manage all agreements permission can create and update agreement sets. This topic reviews the steps to delete an agreement set.

Note: An organization can have up to 50 agreement sets per account. If you exceed this limit, an error message is displayed.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents page is displayed.
4. Expand the Settings (gear) menu from the top right and select the Sets option:

   ![A screenshot of the Settings (gear) menu on the Agreement Manager homepage that highlights the Sets option.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-sets_entry-point_Manage-sets-action-menu.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc0MjEsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.IhDFtR0J66Z1uoq5YhMzgXcpduzGVHP8rQGOoONxsyI&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Settings (gear) menu on the Agreement Manager homepage that highlights the Sets option.")

   The Agreement sets page opens. The All agreement sets view is displayed. The table lists the sets that were shared with or created by the logged-in user.
5. Locate the set that you want to delete.

   Tip: Select the sorting arrows in the Name column to sort the table by set name.
6. Expand the corresponding More actions (three dots) menu and select the Delete option:

   ![A snippet of the All agreement sets view that highlights the Delete option in the More actions menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Manage-Sets-dialog_All-agreement-sets_Delete.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc0MjEsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.IhDFtR0J66Z1uoq5YhMzgXcpduzGVHP8rQGOoONxsyI&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the All agreement sets view that highlights the Delete option in the More actions menu.")

   The Delete this set? dialog box opens. You are prompted to confirm that you want to delete the set.

   Important: All users and groups with whom the set was shared will lose access once the set is deleted. Keep in mind, deleting the set does not delete or void any agreements.
7. Review the message and select the Delete Set option.

   The dialog box closes. The set is no longer displayed in the agreement set table.
8. Return to the Agreement Manager homepage. Select Completed Documents from the breadcrumb navigation at the top of the page.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed.

You have successfully deleted a selected agreement set.

title: Create Agreement Sets from the Agreement List Table
heading_path: Create Agreement Sets from the Agreement List Table
text:
Learn how to create a new agreement set and apply smart filters from the agreement list table on the Completed Documents page.

Smart filters can be created from the filters that you apply to the agreement list table on the Completed Documents page. Keep in mind that not all filters are supported by the *Smart Filters* function.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed. A list of documents to which the logged-in user has access is displayed in the table.
4. Apply filters to locate the documents that you want to add to the agreement set:

   ![A snippet of the Completed Documents page that shows the expanded Filters option, including suggested filters.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Create-agreement-sets_Completed-Documents_Apply-Filters_Filters-expanded.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTczMzAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.TLtvMDQ8NasCOc74QkGEfv1h58VLrKuTz3Ret7DMEuI&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Completed Documents page that shows the expanded Filters option, including suggested filters.")

   1. Select the Filters option. The menu expands and a list of applied and suggested filters is displayed.
   2. Locate the filter that you want to apply. Scroll through the list of suggested filters or enter a search term in the Filter by field.
   3. Expand (click) the relevant filter and select one or more values.

      Important: Not all filters can be used as a smart filter. For example, the smart filter function does not support rolling date and duration-based filters. If you select an unsupported filter, the filter name is displayed with a strike-through when you create the set. The filter is not included in the smart filtering for the set.
   4. Select the Apply option.
   5. Continue applying filters as needed.
   6. Select the Save option to save your filters.

   The agreement list table updates and agreements that match the applied filters are displayed.
5. Select one or more agreements to add to the agreement set:
   - Select individual agreements to update.
   - Select the Choose all checkbox from the header row to select all the agreements on the page.

   Tip: You can select all the agreements in the results list, not just the agreements on the first page of results. The Selected dropdown displays a counter to indicate the number of agreements that are currently selected. Expand the dropdown and select the Select all that match your filters option:![A snippet of the agreement list table that highlights the Select all that match your filters option in the Selected dropdown.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-documents_Selected-dropdown_Select-all-that-match-your-filters.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTczMzAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.TLtvMDQ8NasCOc74QkGEfv1h58VLrKuTz3Ret7DMEuI&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the agreement list table that highlights the Select all that match your filters option in the Selected dropdown.")All the agreements in the table are selected.

   Additional options are displayed when one or more agreements are selected from the list, including the Organize option.
6. Expand the Organize dropdown and select the + Create New Set option:

title: Create Agreement Sets from the Agreement List Table
heading_path: Create Agreement Sets from the Agreement List Table
text:
ted.

   Additional options are displayed when one or more agreements are selected from the list, including the Organize option.
6. Expand the Organize dropdown and select the + Create New Set option:

   ![A screenshot of the Organize dropdown that highlights the Create New Set option.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-documents_Organize-expanded_Create-New-Set_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTczMzAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.TLtvMDQ8NasCOc74QkGEfv1h58VLrKuTz3Ret7DMEuI&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Organize dropdown that highlights the Create New Set option.")

   The New agreement set dialog box opens.
7. Complete the required fields and turn on smart filtering for the set:

   ![A screenshot of the New agreement set dialog box that shows the Name, Description, and smart filtering toggle.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-set-management_New-agreement-set_turn-on-smart-filtering-toggle.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTczMzAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.TLtvMDQ8NasCOc74QkGEfv1h58VLrKuTz3Ret7DMEuI&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the New agreement set dialog box that shows the Name, Description, and smart filtering toggle.")

   1. Enter a name for the agreement set in the Name field.

      Tip: The Name field is limited to 60 characters.
   2. Enter a description for the agreement set in the Description field.

      Tip: The Description field is limited to 200 characters. This field is optional. The entered value is displayed in the All agreement sets table.
   3. Turn on (blue position) the toggle in the Turn on smart filtering for this set section.

      The filters that you applied to the agreement list are displayed in this section. Turn on the toggle to add agreements to the set based on your applied filters.

      Important: If you applied an unsupported filter, the filter name is displayed with a strike-through. The filter is not included in the smart filtering for the set.
8. Select Create or Create and Share:
   - Select the Create option to create the new agreement set without sharing the set with other users or groups.
   - Select the Create and Share option to create the agreement set and share it with users and groups.

     The Share with others dialog box opens. Locate the user or group to whom you want to share the set and assign an Access level. Select Share to complete the process.

     Note: See the [Share an Agreement Set](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=pvb1736186431453.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to share an agreement set with individual users and groups. This topic answers the question: How do I share groups of agreements with the appropriate people?") topic to learn more about sharing agreement sets.

   The dialog box closes and you are returned to the Completed Documents page.

You have successfully created an agreement set from the agreement list table, including applying smart filters.

title: Agreement Manager for Administrators
text:
Read about how administrators interact with Agreement Manager, including typical administrator tasks and example use cases.

Account administrators automatically have full secure access to all Agreement Manager features by
default. This access includes bulk ingestion and agreement set management
functionality. For example, administrators can use agreement sets to ensure that
only the appropriate users have access to sensitive documents.

Restriction: While administrators automatically have
full access to all the Agreement Manager features, they do not have access to
view the agreements. Access is explicitly assigned to administrators for them to
view any of the agreements in the account. See the [Agreement Sharing and Downloads](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=hrr1722978886188.html&_LANG=enus "This topic answers the question: What are my direct sharing and download options for Agreement Manager documents, including the required permissions?") article to learn more about sharing agreements
with others.

Note: This varies by organization. In some cases, the administrator is an IT persona who only manages users and settings. In smaller organizations, the administrator plays a bigger role and might manage agreements, permissions, and settings.

Typical administrator tasks:

- Manage user and group access to agreements in the repository
- Assign bulk upload permission to users
- Give users and groups access to Agreement Manager agreements and features
- Enable cloud storage providers from the Docusign App Center

See the use case topics in the Related Content section on the right to learn how to complete a few basic administrator tasks in Agreement Manager.

title: Grant Users Access to Bulk Upload Documents
heading_path: Grant Users Access to Bulk Upload Documents
text:
This topic answers the question: How do I give users access to bulk upload agreements to
Agreement Manager? Learn how to give users access to upload more than five files to
Agreement Manager.

The Upload function is available to
all Agreement Manager users. With this function, users can upload up to five third-party
documents. However, to upload more than five documents, you need an extra
permission. The *bulk upload* permission is granted by an administrator from
the Admin menu. This permission is assigned manually from the
User profile page or with a permission profile.

Once granted, users can upload up to 1,000 files at a time to
Agreement Manager. Additionally, this permission also gives users access to upload documents
from third-party cloud storage providers.

Important: The *bulk upload* permission is set at the user-level. This
permission is not available at the group-level. Keep in mind that user and group permissions are managed at the account-level as opposed to the organization-level.

1. Log in to your Docusign account using administrator
   credentials.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Admin from the global header.

   The Admin menu opens and the Accounts view is displayed.

**Select an account (for organizations with multiple accounts)**

Important: User and group permissions are managed at the *account*-level. Make sure that you are updating records from Accounts administration instead of Organization administration. If your organization has multiple accounts, verify that you are updating the correct account.

3. Expand the Accounts dropdown from the top of the navigation pane and select the relevant account:

   ![A snippet of the Accounts view that highlights the Accounts dropdown in the pane on the left.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Permissions_Admin-menu_Accounts-dropdown_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTYzMDUsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.8Apv5tFph-y2j5aGsYr6IITD0bAeIibNZUFlt4U50xc&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Accounts view that highlights the Accounts dropdown in the pane on the left.")

   The Accounts view refreshes and the Overview page is displayed for the selected account. Locate the user that you want to update.

**Locate the relevant user**

Bulk permissions are granted at the user-level. This permission is not available at the group-level. Locate
the user that you want to update.

4. Select the Users option from the USERS AND GROUPS section in the navigation pane.

   The Users page opens and the account's users are displayed in the table.
5. Locate the user that you want to update:
   1. Enter the name of the user in the Search Here field.

      A dropdown list of search results is displayed.
   2. Select a name from the type-ahead dropdown or press the Enter key.
   3. Adjust the filters as needed. Filter options include: Status, Permissions, and Groups.

   The Users page refreshes and the users who matched the search criteria are displayed.

**Update Agreement Manager permissions**

6. Expand (select) the Actions option for the relevant user and select the Manage Agreement Manager Access item.

   The User profile page opens and the Agreement Manager Access tab is displayed by default.
7. Assign bulk upload permissions to the selected user. Select the Upload up to 1,000 files at a time toggle so it is in the "on" (blue) position.

title: Grant Users Access to Bulk Upload Documents
heading_path: Grant Users Access to Bulk Upload Documents
text:
Agreement Manager Access tab is displayed by default.
7. Assign bulk upload permissions to the selected user. Select the Upload up to 1,000 files at a time toggle so it is in the "on" (blue) position.

   ![A snippet of the User profile page that highlights the bulk upload permission in the Agreement Manager access section.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Navigator-Access_Bulk-upload-toggle_enabled.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTYzMDUsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.8Apv5tFph-y2j5aGsYr6IITD0bAeIibNZUFlt4U50xc&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the User profile page that highlights the bulk upload permission in the Agreement Manager access section.")

   This permission gives users access to *bulk upload* up to 1,000 files at a time. Users also have access to upload agreements to Agreement Manager from third-party cloud storage locations.

   Tip: Turn this toggle off (white position) to revoke the user's
   access to bulk upload agreements to Agreement Manager.
8. Select the Save Changes option.

   A message box opens to confirm that the Agreement Manager access changes were saved.

You have successfully granted bulk upload permission to a
selected user.

title: Agreement Manager Help FAQs
text:
Look up answers to frequently asked questions related to Agreement Manager, including capabilities by permission, agreement ingestion, and dashboard reports.

The purpose of this support topic is to provide answers to common Agreement Manager questions, including permissions, agreement ingestion, and dashboard reports.

title: Agreement Manager Help FAQs
heading_path: How do I access Agreement Manager? Where is it?
text:
Your completed documents are ingested or uploaded to Agreement Manager. Docusign IAM users can now access their completed documents, as well as their completed eSignature envelopes, from the same place.

Log into Docusign and access your completed documents (Agreement Manager):

![A snippet of the Completed Documents page that highlights the navigation path to access Agreement Manager, Agreements > Completed.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Help-FAQs_General_Navigator-entry-point.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc5MzUsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.hGeFuPSycPqqvBN88HJeRc7C0MNfh4_lB5Rab9K21EE&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Completed Documents page that highlights the navigation path to access Agreement Manager, Agreements > Completed.")

1. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens. The Inbox view is displayed by default.
2. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the pane on the left.

   The Completed Documents page (Agreement Manager) opens. A list of documents to which the logged-in user has access is displayed in the table.

See the [Access Your Completed Envelopes and Documents](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=arm1738168238587.html) support topic to learn more about accessing your completed documents (Agreement Manager).

title: Agreement Manager Help FAQs
heading_path: How many permission levels does Agreement Manager have?
text:
There are two permission levels for granting access to agreements in an account:

1. **View all agreements in this account:** authorizes users or groups to view and download all agreements in the account (to which they have access).
2. **Organize and manage all agreements:** authorizes users or groups to view, edit, download, and delete all agreements in the account. This permission level also grants access to manage agreement sets and attributes, like agreement types and fields.

Note: There is a third permission that gives users access to bulk upload up to 1,000 files at a time to Agreement Manager. The Upload up to 1,000 files at a time toggle is turned on (blue) at the user-level.

Restriction: While administrators automatically have
full access to all the Agreement Manager features, they do not have access to
view the agreements. Access is explicitly assigned to administrators for them to
view any of the agreements in the account. See the [Agreement Sharing and Downloads](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=hrr1722978886188.html&_LANG=enus "This topic answers the question: What are my direct sharing and download options for Agreement Manager documents, including the required permissions?") article to learn more about sharing agreements
with others.

See the [User and Group Management](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=eut1712157553977.html) support topic to learn more about Agreement Manager permissions.

title: Agreement Manager Help FAQs
heading_path: What's the difference between the Agreement Manager permission levels?
text:
The View all agreements in this account permission limits users and groups to viewing and downloading all the agreements in the account to which they have access.

The Organize and manage all agreements permission gives users and groups expanded capabilities, such as:

- Agreement set management
- Agreement type management
- Custom field management
- Duplicate party management

See the [Capabilities by Agreement Manager Permissions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=vhx1743523538507.html) support topic to learn more about the capabilities to which each permission has access.

title: Agreement Manager Help FAQs
heading_path: Can administrators automatically access Agreement Manager agreements and functionality?
text:
No. While administrators automatically have full access to all Agreement Manager features by default, they do not have access to view the agreements.

See the [Agreement Manager Permissions, Profiles, and Roles](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=eut1712157553977.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the available Agreement Manager permissions, including how users and groups are granted access to Agreement Manager agreements and example roles for each permission.") support topic to learn more about Agreement Manager permissions.

title: Agreement Manager Help FAQs
heading_path: Can I use Agreement Manager without assigned permissions?
text:
Yes.

When organizations purchase an Intelligent Agreement Management (IAM) plan with Agreement Manager, by default, all users can access their
own shared, received, sent, and uploaded agreements. However, specific Agreement Manager access is required to view, organize, and manage all the agreements in the account.

See the [User and Group Management](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=eut1712157553977.html) support topic to learn more about Agreement Manager permissions.

title: Agreement Manager Help FAQs
heading_path: How are agreement types identified during agreement processing?
text:
Docusign AI models are designed to detect agreement types by considering the title and content of ingested and uploaded files. This includes recognizing synonyms and phrase variations.

See the [Agreement Type Management](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=uie1724446326038.html) support topic to learn more about agreement types.

title: Agreement Manager Help FAQs
heading_path: What is the limit for creating custom fields and agreement types?
text:
Customers with IAM Professional, IAM Enterprise, and IAM for Sales plans can create up to 50 custom extractions per account. This includes any combination of custom agreement types and fields that are enabled with AI detection:

- The limit represents the combined number of custom agreement types and fields with AI detection.
- The limit can represent any combination of custom agreement types and fields that equal 50 - taking into account the customer agreement type limit.
- For custom agreement types with AI detection, the limit is 15 per account.

.

Messages are displayed when you reach the total number of custom agreement types and fields allowed per account.

Important: Without AI detection, customers can create up to 100 custom fields and agreement types in total. Field values for custom fields without AI detection are populated manually during data review.

See these topics to learn more about custom types and fields:

- [Custom extraction and AI detection limits](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=yrx1747083411531.html&anchor=yrx1747083411531__section_c32_fc1_5fc)
- [Agreement Type Management](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=uie1724446326038.html)
- [Custom Extraction and Field Detection](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=yrx1747083411531.html)

title: Agreement Manager Help FAQs
heading_path: How long does it take to process my documents?
text:
The processing time for agreement uploads, ingestion, and reprocessing is dependent on a number of variables. Processing jobs can take from hours to days, depending on:

- Number of documents being processed or reprocessed
- Size of the data set being extracted (standard versus standard plus expanded attributes)
- Number and size of jobs currently in the processing queue
- Increased activity in the processing queue due to period close dates

See these topics to learn more about processing Agreement Manager agreements:

- [Agreement Uploads and Ingestion](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=rrs1712255668350.html&_LANG=enus "This topic answers the question: What are my upload options for Agreement Manager, including limitations and required permissions?")
- [Processing Agreement Manager Agreements](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=rvw1736364838500.html&_LANG=enus "Read about agreement processing and how to opt in for additional processing to surface new insights within your agreements.")

title: Agreement Manager Help FAQs
heading_path: Can I view or create a document hierarchy to map the relationship between agreements?
text:
Yes. Hierarchies are created and managed from the Hierarchies tab in the party hub. You can also view and create hierarchies from the Related agreements pane on the Agreement preview page.

See the [Agreement Hierarchies](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=con1b654754-bd6d-4fbd-ac0c-8c44a9b1d666.html&_LANG=enus "Read about agreement hierarchies, including their purpose, use cases, entry points, and common tasks.") topic to learn more about using hierarchies.

title: Agreement Manager Help FAQs
