   3. Re-order the columns as needed. Hover the cursor over the Reorder (dots) icon and drag the column (field) to the appropriate location.
   4. Select the Save option.

   The dialog box closes and the columns are updated according to your configuration.

**Download agreements and export agreement data:**

9. Select one or more agreements to download the agreements or export agreement data to a CSV file.

   ![A snippet of the Agreements tab that highlights the download and export options for a group of selected agreements.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Party-hub_Agreements_Download-or-export-selected-agreements.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc4MDksInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.AC8vVSf_-2QBmOtn31UX-GVUkVru8oVhzfnTrzhG_fk&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Agreements tab that highlights the download and export options for a group of selected agreements.")

   1. Select the agreements or agreement data that you want to download or export.
   2. Select the Download Documents (down arrow) icon.

      A zipped folder containing the selected agreements is downloaded to your local Downloads folder.
   3. Select the Export to CSV icon.

      The CSV file is downloaded to your local Downloads folder. When exporting agreement data, all the standard and custom fields are included in the CSV file.

**View additional agreement actions:**

10. Expand the More actions (three dots) menu for an agreement and choose from these options:
    - Select the Download option. The agreement downloads to your local Downloads folder.
    - Select the Share option. The Share with others dialog box opens. Choose the user or group with whom you want to share the agreement, assign a permission level, and select Save.
    - Select the Remove from Account option. A confirmation message opens. Review the message and
      select the Remove Agreement option to remove the
      agreement and its data.

      Important: Once the agreement is permanently removed, all
      users and administrators will lose access to the agreement and its
      data. This action does not remove the agreement from its original
      envelope or void the contract.
    - Select the View Activity Log option. The Activity Log opens and a log of agreement-level events that were performed by a user.
11. Select Parties from the breadcrumb navigation at the top of the page.

    You are returned to the Parties view.

    Tip: Select Completed from the navigation pane on the left to view your agreement list. The Completed Documents view opens. The agreements to which the user has access are displayed in the table.

title: Configure New Custom Agreement Types
text:
Learn how to create custom agreement types to support organizational use cases.

Agreement Manager includes a number of standard, Docusign-provided agreement types. However, since every organization is unique, these types do not cover every type of agreement. Administrators and contract managers can create custom agreement types to support their organizational use cases.

Remember: Administrators automatically have access to configure agreement data. For non-administrator users, agreement type management tasks require Organize and manage all agreements access to Agreement Manager agreements and functionality.

This task is completed from the *Agreement Types Manager*. Access this feature from the Agreement Manager homepage under the Settings (gear) menu.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Completed Documents view opens.
4. Expand the Settings (gear) dropdown from the top right of the page and select the Agreement Types option:

   ![A snippet of the Agreement Manager homepage that highlights the Agreement Types option in the Settings (gear) menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-types-manager_Settings-menu_Agreement-Types_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc1OTUsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.WA1i8qnnUlWae-zoU-IbDLLvAEaCJ3ge6iut3c2wbgM&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Agreement Manager homepage that highlights the Agreement Types option in the Settings (gear) menu.")

   The Manage Agreement Types page opens. A list of the account's standard and custom agreement types is displayed in the Agreement Types table.
5. Select the + Agreement Type option from the top right:

   ![A screenshot of the Agreement Types view that highlights the + Agreement Type option in the top right.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-Types-Manager_Agreement-Types_Add-agreement-type-option_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc1OTUsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.WA1i8qnnUlWae-zoU-IbDLLvAEaCJ3ge6iut3c2wbgM&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Agreement Types view that highlights the + Agreement Type option in the top right.")

   The New agreement type dialog box opens.

   ![A screenshot of the New agreement type dialog box that shows the Name, Description, and Category fields.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Manage-Agreement-Types_Add-new-agreement-type.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc1OTUsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.WA1i8qnnUlWae-zoU-IbDLLvAEaCJ3ge6iut3c2wbgM&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the New agreement type dialog box that shows the Name, Description, and Category fields.")
6. Enter a name for the agreement type in the Name field. The name cannot exceed 50 characters.
7. Enter a description for the agreement type in the Description field. The description cannot exceed 130 characters.

   Note: This information displays on the Agreement type details view and helps others understand the purpose of the agreement type.
8. Expand the Category dropdown and select a category for the agreement type. Choose from:

   - Business Services
   - Human Resources
   - Miscellaneous

   Agreement types are grouped based on their primary use case or category.
9. Select Next.

   The Select fields for [agreement type] dialog box opens. Select the fields that you want to associate with the agreement type. These data points will be surfaced in all documents that are identified as this agreement type.

title: Configure New Custom Agreement Types
text:
[agreement type] dialog box opens. Select the fields that you want to associate with the agreement type. These data points will be surfaced in all documents that are identified as this agreement type.

   ![A screenshot of the Select fields dialog box that shows the available and selected fields.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Manage-Agreement-Types_Add-new-agreement-type_Select-fields.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc1OTUsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.WA1i8qnnUlWae-zoU-IbDLLvAEaCJ3ge6iut3c2wbgM&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Select fields dialog box that shows the available and selected fields.")

   Tip: A number of standard fields are selected by default, such as: Title, Parties, Execution Date, Effective Date, and Expiration Date. These data points are surfaced in all documents and cannot be removed from an agreement type.
10. Choose the fields that you want to associate with the agreement type:
    1. Use the Search field to locate the relevant fields.
    2. Select the fields that you want to add to the agreement type.

       The selected fields are added to the Selected Items tab.
    3. Select Save.

    The agreement type details view opens for the new custom agreement type. The selected fields are displayed in the table.

    ![A screenshot of a new custom agreement type that shows the standard fields that were added to the type.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-Types-Manager_Agreement-type-detail-page_Distribution-Agreement-example.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc1OTUsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.WA1i8qnnUlWae-zoU-IbDLLvAEaCJ3ge6iut3c2wbgM&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of a new custom agreement type that shows the standard fields that were added to the type.")
11. Review the list of agreement fields. Once AI detection is enabled, these data points, or attributes, are surfaced in your documents for this agreement type.

    Tip: If the agreement type does not contain all the necessary fields, use the + Add Fields option to add more fields. See the [Add and Remove Fields from Agreement Types](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tskda96f0fc-0abb-4ac9-b138-c52e4e8ee713.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to add and remove standard and custom fields from agreement types.") topic to learn more about adding fields to agreement types.

    When you first create a custom agreement type, the AI detection is inactive by default. Complete the AI detection setup process to surface the new data points in your agreements. See the [Custom Agreement Type Extractions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=con6f74f3c5-e52b-4611-9d7b-8455c97725b4.html&_LANG=enus "Read about custom agreement type extractions, including their use, purpose, and entry points in Agreement Manager.") topic to learn more about activating your custom types for AI detection.
12. Return to the Agreement Types view. Select the Agreement Types option from the breadcrumb navigation in the top left.

    The Agreement Types view opens. The new custom agreement type is displayed in the table under the relevant category tab.
13. Return to the Agreement Manager homepage. Select the Close (x) icon from the top right.

    You are returned to the Completed Documents view.

You have successfully configured a new custom agreement type. The next step is to enable the AI detection for the custom extraction.

title: Add Custom Fields to Agreement Types
heading_path: Add Custom Fields to Agreement Types
text:
Learn how to create and add custom fields to the custom and standard agreement types in your account.

When necessary, administrators and contract managers can add fields to the agreement types in their account. For example, if a use case requires additional data points.

Note: Administrators automatically have access to configure agreement data. For non-administrator users, this feature requires Organize and manage all agreements access to Agreement Manager. These tasks are primarily completed by administrators and contract managers.

Tip: Both custom and standard fields can be added to the agreement types in the account. Likewise,
these fields can be added to custom and standard agreement types.

There are a number of entry points for adding custom fields to agreement types. For example, from the Agreement Types Manager and the Agreement preview page. This topic reviews the steps to add custom fields from the Agreement Types Manager.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed.
4. Expand the Settings (gear) dropdown from the top right of the page and select the Agreement Types option:

   ![A snippet of the homepage that highlights the Agreement Types option in the Settings (gear) menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-types-manager_Settings-menu_Agreement-Types_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2NTEsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.etYGtRkrtjn_Q4xP7XvwWjccZ2ji0OuqnsQB2RGCuSo&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the homepage that highlights the Agreement Types option in the Settings (gear) menu.")

   The Manage Agreement Types page opens. A list of the account's standard and custom agreement types is displayed in the Agreement Types table.
5. Locate the agreement type that you want to update:

   ![A snippet of the Manage Agreement Types page that highlights the search field and filter options.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Manage-Agreement-Types_Agreement-Types_Fields_search-filters_generic.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2NTEsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.etYGtRkrtjn_Q4xP7XvwWjccZ2ji0OuqnsQB2RGCuSo&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Manage Agreement Types page that highlights the search field and filter options.")

   1. Search for the relevant custom agreement type. Enter a keyword in the Search types... field. The table updates to show agreement types that match the entered search term.
   2. Apply filters to locate the relevant custom agreement type. For example, filter the table by Created By, Category, or AI Status. Expand a filter, choose the relevant value, and select the Apply option.

      The table updates and the agreement types that match the applied filters are displayed.
6. Select (click) the relevant agreement type from the results table.

   The Agreement type details view opens for the selected agreement type. The associated custom and standard fields are displayed in the Fields table.
7. Select the + Add Fields option:

   ![A snippet of the agreement type details that highlights the + Add Fields option.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Manage-Agreement-Types_Agreement-type-details_Add-Fields-option_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2NTEsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.etYGtRkrtjn_Q4xP7XvwWjccZ2ji0OuqnsQB2RGCuSo&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the agreement type details that highlights the + Add Fields option.")

title: Add Custom Fields to Agreement Types
heading_path: Add Custom Fields to Agreement Types
text:
iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2NTEsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.etYGtRkrtjn_Q4xP7XvwWjccZ2ji0OuqnsQB2RGCuSo&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the agreement type details that highlights the + Add Fields option.")

   The Add a field dialog box opens.![A screenshot of the Add a field dialog box that is used to add a field to an existing agreement type.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-type-management_create-type_add-a-field_dist-agreements-example.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2NTEsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.etYGtRkrtjn_Q4xP7XvwWjccZ2ji0OuqnsQB2RGCuSo&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Add a field dialog box that is used to add a field to an existing agreement type.")
8. Enter a name for the custom field in the Field name field.

   As you enter a name, a type-ahead dropdown list opens and fields that match the entered name
   are displayed.

   Tip: Review the type-ahead dropdown list to verify
   that the custom field does not already exist. If the field exists, you are
   unable to create a new one with the same name.
9. Select the + Create [Field name] option to create a custom field:

   ![A snippet of the Add a field dialog box that shows an example of a custom field name being created.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-type-management_add-custom-field_example.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2NTEsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.etYGtRkrtjn_Q4xP7XvwWjccZ2ji0OuqnsQB2RGCuSo&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Add a field dialog box that shows an example of a custom field name being created.")
10. Expand the Field type dropdown and select the type of field that you are adding:

    ![A snippet of the Add a field dialog box that shows the available field types for your custom field.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Add-custom-field_Field-type-options.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2NTEsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.etYGtRkrtjn_Q4xP7XvwWjccZ2ji0OuqnsQB2RGCuSo&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Add a field dialog box that shows the available field types for your custom field.")

    Choices are: Text, Number,
    Date, and
    Dropdown.

    Note: If you select
    Dropdown as the field type, enter options
    for the dropdown in the Options text-entry box:![A snippet of the Add a field dialog box that shows the required Options field for adding a dropdown field type.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Add-custom-field_Options-for-dropdown-type.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2NTEsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.etYGtRkrtjn_Q4xP7XvwWjccZ2ji0OuqnsQB2RGCuSo&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Add a field dialog box that shows the required Options field for adding a dropdown field type.")**Important:** Use a semicolon to separate each option.
11. Apply the field to the relevant agreement types. Click in the Agreement types field and select the agreement types to which you want to add the field. The current agreement type is displayed in the Agreement types field by default.

title: Add Custom Fields to Agreement Types
heading_path: Add Custom Fields to Agreement Types
text:
agreement types. Click in the Agreement types field and select the agreement types to which you want to add the field. The current agreement type is displayed in the Agreement types field by default.

    ![A snippet of the Add a field dialog box that shows the expanded Agreement types field.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-type-management_Add-a-field_Agreement-types-Dist-Agreement-ex.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2NTEsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.etYGtRkrtjn_Q4xP7XvwWjccZ2ji0OuqnsQB2RGCuSo&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Add a field dialog box that shows the expanded Agreement types field.")Choose the relevant agreement types and select the Apply option.
12. Expand the Category dropdown and select a classification for the custom field:

    ![A snippet of the Add a field dialog box that shows the Category options for the new field.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Add-custom-field_Category-options.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2NTEsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.etYGtRkrtjn_Q4xP7XvwWjccZ2ji0OuqnsQB2RGCuSo&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Add a field dialog box that shows the Category options for the new field.")Choices are: General, Termination, Renewal, Payment, Legal and Compliance, Human Resources, and Other.
13. Select the Save option.

    The dialog box closes. The  Agreement type details view opens for the selected agreement type. The custom field is displayed in the field list table.

    ![A snippet of a custom agreement type details page that highlights the new custom field.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Manage-agreement-types_Custom-field-added-to-custom-type-highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2NTEsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.etYGtRkrtjn_Q4xP7XvwWjccZ2ji0OuqnsQB2RGCuSo&_LANG=enus "A snippet of a custom agreement type details page that highlights the new custom field.")

    When you first create a custom field, the AI detection is inactive by default. Complete the AI detection setup process so that data for the field is surfaced in your documents. See the [Custom Field Extractions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=condece8d7e-2da8-4e5f-8ba4-3f0175c2a625.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the custom field extractions, including entry points for creating new fields and a review of the AI detection setup wizard.") topic to learn more about activating your custom fields for AI detection.
14. Return to the Agreement Types view. Select the Agreement Types option from the breadcrumb navigation in the top left.

    You are returned to the Manage Agreement Types page. The Agreement Types view is displayed.
15. Return to the Agreement Manager homepage. Select the Close (X) icon from the top right.

    The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed.

You have successfully added a custom field to an existing agreement type. The next step is to enable the AI detection for the custom extraction. See the [Enable AI Detection for Your Custom Fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tsk02bd31a2-b125-4d53-b632-20d9972163a8.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to enable the AI detection for your custom fields. Once activated, the field is surfaced in your new agreements automatically.") topic to start the AI detection set-up process.

title: Renewals Management
text:
Read about how Agreement Manager supports renewal management, including available tools, use
cases, and key features. This topic answers the question: What are my renewal management
options for Agreement Manager agreements, including tools and key features?

Renewals management helps you use agreement data to act on renewals and terminations.

 

This functionality helps organizations achieve:

- **Operational efficiency:** provides one source of truth and automated processes that help
  keep stakeholders informed of terminated or auto-renewed agreements.
- **Cost savings:** makes you aware of upcoming renewals and terminations.

  Organizations
  waste money when they unintentionally miss renegotiation and termination windows
  for agreements that no longer serve any purpose.
- **Increased revenue:** gives you the chance to contact customers proactively for
  renewals and cuts down on unintentional terminations.
- **Compliance:** ensures that you don’t miss important dates so you stay compliant.
- **Competitive advantage:** increases your sales cycle and gives you a competitive
  advantage.

Three main tools or functions support renewals management:

1. Renewals dashboard displays filterable charts about your
   agreements:
   - Upcoming renewals
   - Renewals by type
2. Renewal-related email report subscriptions and document-level notifications.
3. Data validation ensures the accuracy of renewal-related attribute values within agreements.
   These values drive insight into agreement renewals and feed into the
   Renewals dashboard and the email notifications.

The primary users of renewals management functionality are contract managers, procurement
managers, legal operations managers, and sales operations managers. The table lists the
use cases for each role.

| **Role** | **Use cases** |
| --- | --- |
| Contract managers | Visibility into which agreements automatically renew to ensure enough time to:  - Renegotiate for more favorable terms - Terminate the contract window   Ability to recognize the status or relationship with a counter-party when an agreement has been renewed or terminated.  The ability to see when renewal notices are due to better plan workloads. |
| Procurement managers | Ability to identify when agreements are up for renewal ahead of the due date notice. Extra time is required to determine if the organization wants to renew, terminate, or amend. Visibility into the dollar amount involved in a renewal, including cost savings by renegotiating or terminating the contract. |
| Legal operations managers | An accurate and systematic way to track renewal information without resorting to manual processes. Access management to ensure that the right stakeholders have access to agreements for upcoming renewals or expiration. |
| Sales operations managers | Sales support to ensure that teams have accurate information about renewals so they can proactively secure the renewal or upsell. |

title: Renewals Management
heading_path: Expiration date extension for auto-renewing agreements
text:
When an auto-renewing agreement reaches its expiration date, the date is extended based on the agreement's renewal term. This action happens automatically and requires no user input. For example, if the expiration date is January 1, 2025 with a 1-year renewal term, the expiration date updates to January 1, 2026 when the current date equals January 1, 2025.

Important: This feature relies on the accuracy of the agreement's Expiration
Date, Renewal Type, and Renewal Term values. Although
these values are extracted from your agreements, you should review and validate
them for accuracy. See the [Validate Renewal-Related Attribute Values for an Agreement](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=sah1712251075016.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to validate renewal-related attribute values for a selected agreement.") article to learn
more about completing a data review.

The expiration date extension feature applies to agreements with a Renewal Type of Auto Renew or Evergreen.

Agreements with auto-renewal continue to auto-renewal until the agreement is no longer active or in effect. See the [Update the Agreement Status for a Document](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tsk70e560e8-5e5d-4738-8bb6-0b815994274f.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to update the agreement status for a document.") article to learn how to change an agreement's status.

CAUTION: If an auto-renewing agreement does not have a Renewal Term value, the agreement remains active however, the Expiration Date is not extended.

title: Configure Docusign Apps in an Agreement Workflow
heading_path: Configure Docusign Apps in an Agreement Workflow
text:
You can integrate Docusign apps to automate your agreement workflows and boost productivity.

Add apps from Docusign App Center when creating agreement workflows in Docusign Workflow Builder. With Docusign apps, you can customize and automate your agreement processes as described in the [Creating Workflows](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=tco1697826811758.html&_LANG=enus) article.

Additionally, you can use the Google Docs app with Docusign CLM as described in the  [Google Docs for Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=alb1642965087302&topicId=gcn1714403494382.html&_LANG=enus) article.

title: Configure Docusign Apps in an Agreement Workflow
heading_path: Configure Docusign Apps in an Agreement Workflow
text:
If apps were previously installed from the Docusign App Center, you must uninstall and reinstall them. Due to versioning issues, backward compatibility is not currently possible.

Additionally, you must reconnect all Workflow Builder fields after upgrading.

title: Manage Upcoming Renewals from Email Notifications
heading_path: Manage Upcoming Renewals from Email Notifications
text:
This topic answers the question: How do I view my upcoming renewals from an email notification and share an agreement with others?

Renewal email notifications are sent every other Monday at 9am according to the user’s time zone setting. The email contains a summary of upcoming renewals for the selected notification time period. During notification configuration, you had the option of selecting a notification time period.

Tip: Notification settings give you control over the email notifications that you receive. By default, all the notification types related to the completed agreements in your account are selected, or enabled. Deselect a checkbox if you no longer want to receive email notifications for the corresponding notification type.

See the [Customize Notification Settings for Your Completed Agreements](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tsk155b1ed7-7b45-43cd-be92-2a5279630c0f.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to customize your email notification settings from the Notification Preferences page.") article to learn more about updating your notification settings. Be sure to periodically check the Notification Preferences page as new notification types will be added in the future.

1. Open the renewal email notification.
2. Review the Renewal Summary, including the number of agreements that are renewing within the selected notification time period.
3. Select View Renewals.

   The Insights hub opens. The Renewals dashboard is displayed by default. The dashboard is pre-filtered to show agreements referenced in the email notification.

   Note: If you are not already signed in to Docusign, log in to your account.
4. Access the detailed report view for the Upcoming renewals chart:
   - Select the chart title.
   - Hover the cursor over the chart, expand the More actions (three dots) menu and select the View Report option.

   The detailed report view opens for the Upcoming renewals chart. The agreements represented in the chart are listed in the table.
5. Expand the menu More actions (three dots) for an agreement and select the Download option.

   The agreement is downloaded to your local Downloads folder.
6. Select (click) an agreement.

   The Agreement preview page opens for the selected agreement. The Details pane displays attributes, including values helpful for the negotiation or renewal processes.
7. Expand the dropdown in the top right and select the Share option:

   ![A snippet of the Agreement preview page that highlights the dropdown in the top right where the Share option is selected.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-preview_Dropdown_Share.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcyOTUsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.w9u6l7PtO6CHdYOvBLNWLCY0dG58Xd5rfTHv_pVRwqE&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Agreement preview page that highlights the dropdown in the top right where the Share option is selected.")

   The Share with others dialog box opens. Use this dialog box to share the agreement with key stakeholders involved in the renewal or negotiation process.

title: Manage Upcoming Renewals from Email Notifications
heading_path: Manage Upcoming Renewals from Email Notifications
text:
right where the Share option is selected.")

   The Share with others dialog box opens. Use this dialog box to share the agreement with key stakeholders involved in the renewal or negotiation process.

   ![A screenshot of the Share with others dialog box.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Share-with-others-dialog-box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcyOTUsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.w9u6l7PtO6CHdYOvBLNWLCY0dG58Xd5rfTHv_pVRwqE&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Share with others dialog box.")
8. Search for and select the user or group with whom you want to share the agreement.
   1. Enter the name of the user or group in the Add user or group search field. A type-ahead dropdown displays names matching the entered criteria.
   2. Select a user or group from the list.
9. Expand the Access dropdown and assign the appropriate access level for the agreement. Choices are: Can Manage, Can Edit, and Can View.
10. The Notify users by email option is selected by default. Accept the default to notify users that a document was shared with them.
11. Select the Share option.

    The dialog box closes. The agreement displays in the agreement list table for the user or group with whom you shared the agreement. The users also receive a notification letting them know an agreement has been shared with them.

    Tip: Use the Copy link option to share a link to the agreement with users who have access to the agreement. The link can only be opened by users with access to the agreement.

You have successfully accessed your renewing agreements from an email notification. You also downloaded the agreement and shared it with key stakeholders to assist in the renewal process.

title: Agreement Sharing and Downloads
text:
This topic answers the question: What are my direct sharing and download options for Agreement Manager documents, including the required permissions?

Users can share agreements with other users and groups within the same account or workspace. Likewise, all users can use the Download function for the agreements to which they have access.

The Share function gives users access to share an agreement and assign an access level for the recipients.

![A screenshot of the Share with others dialog box.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Share-with-others-dialog-box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY2NDQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.WDhGGH-tXDj5I68-O5QPL8SsCLPH88poZ_hElyWAXm8&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Share with others dialog box.")

title: Agreement Sharing and Downloads
text:
Before using the Share function, or sharing agreements in general, it's important to recognize the different access levels. They are: Can Manage, Can Edit, and Can View.

![A screenshot of the agreement access permissions for the direct sharing function.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Share-an-agreement_available-access-permissions.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY2NDQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.WDhGGH-tXDj5I68-O5QPL8SsCLPH88poZ_hElyWAXm8&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the agreement access permissions for the direct sharing function.")

These permissions provide varying access to the agreements being shared:

- Can Manage:: Users can edit attribute values, and delete, share, and remove access to the agreement. Users with this access can also add the agreement to agreement sets.
- Can Edit:: Users can edit attribute values and share and download the agreement.
- Can View:: Users can read and download the agreement.

Important: These document permissions are used when setting access controls for
document uploads and shared agreement sets. See these topics to read more about access controls:

- [Agreement Uploads and Ingestion](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=rrs1712255668350.html&_LANG=enus "This topic answers the question: What are my upload options for Agreement Manager, including limitations and required permissions?")
- [Agreement Set Management](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=zed1730843285476.html&_LANG=enus "Read about agreement set management including how to use sets to control access to agreements. This topic answers the question: What are agreement sets and how can I use them?")
- [Share an Agreement Set](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=pvb1736186431453.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to share an agreement set with individual users and groups. This topic answers the question: How do I share groups of agreements with the appropriate people?")

By default, all users have Can Manage access to their uploaded and completed agreements. This includes the documents that are ingested from eSignature. Both senders and recipients are given Can Manage access to their completed documents that are synced from eSignature.

Note: Users can only share an agreement with individuals and groups within their account. Users must have Can Edit or Can Manage access to an agreement to share the agreement with others:

- Users with *manage* access can share an agreement and assign *edit*, *manage*, or *view* access.
- Users with *edit* access can share an agreement and assign *edit* or *view* access.
- Users with *manage* access can revoke user and group access to an agreement.

title: Agreement Sharing and Downloads
text:
Users are notified by email when an agreement is shared with them.

![A screenshot of the Add users dialog box that shows the Notify users by email checkbox and the Copy link option.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Share-an-agreement_Add-users-dialog-box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY2NDQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.WDhGGH-tXDj5I68-O5QPL8SsCLPH88poZ_hElyWAXm8&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Add users dialog box that shows the Notify users by email checkbox and the Copy link option.")

The Notify users by email option is selected by default. Leave this checkbox selected to notify users that an agreement was shared with them. If you de-select the checkbox, users are not notified of shared agreements.

The Copy link option provides you with a direct link to the agreement. When you select this option, a link to the agreement is copied to your clipboard. This link can be pasted into a message or email and shared with other users. Keep in mind, the user must have access to the agreement to view the link.

title: Agreement Sharing and Downloads
text:
The Download function is available to all users for any agreement to which they have access. For example, you may want to download an agreement to share with key stakeholders during negotiations.

The Download function is available from two locations in Agreement Manager:

- The agreement list table (the Completed Documents view)
- The Agreement preview page

The agreement is downloaded to your local Downloads folder, where you can
share it with key stakeholders or save it for future use.

title: Remove Agreements from Existing Agreement Sets
heading_path: Remove Agreements from Existing Agreement Sets
text:
Learn how to remove agreements from an existing agreement set.

Agreements can be removed from existing sets from a number of places within Agreement Manager, such as:

- Agreement list table on the Completed Documents page
- Agreement sets pane on the Agreement preview page

This topic shows how to remove agreements from existing sets from both the agreement list table (Completed Documents page) and the Agreement preview page.

**Remove agreements from sets from the Completed Documents page:**

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed. Locate the set that contains the agreements you want to remove.
4. Apply filters to locate the relevant set:

   ![A snippet of the Completed Documents page that displays the expanded Sets filter.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-sets_entry-point_Sets-filter.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTczNTEsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.mkabalaSdMrXqrosnohCmB-UVBsah0i59ht1WdTmxl8&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Completed Documents page that displays the expanded Sets filter.")

   1. Select the Filters option. The menu expands and a list of applied and suggested filters is displayed.
   2. Select the Sets filter from the list of Suggested filters.
   3. Select a set from the list.
   4. Select the Apply option.

   The filter closes and the agreements contained in the set are displayed in the table.
5. Select the agreements that you want to remove from the set. You can remove one or more agreements at a time.

   When one or more agreements are selected, additional actions are displayed in the bar at the top of the table.
6. Expand the Organize dropdown and select Remove from Set:

   ![A screenshot of the expanded Organize dropdown that highlights the Remove from Set option.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-documents-table_Sets-filter_Remove-highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTczNTEsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.mkabalaSdMrXqrosnohCmB-UVBsah0i59ht1WdTmxl8&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the expanded Organize dropdown that highlights the Remove from Set option.")

   A dialog box opens and you are prompted to confirm that you want to remove the agreements from the set.
7. Review the message and select the Remove option.

   The dialog box closes and the selected agreements are removed from the set.

**Remove an agreement from sets from the Agreement preview page:**

8. Select an agreement from the agreement list table on the Completed Documents page.

   The Agreement preview page opens for the selected agreement. The Details pane displays a list of attributes and field values that were surfaced based on the agreement type.

   ![A snippet of the Agreement preview page that highlights the Agreement sets icon located on the left-side of the page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-Preview-page_Agreement-sets-icon-highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTczNTEsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.mkabalaSdMrXqrosnohCmB-UVBsah0i59ht1WdTmxl8&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Agreement preview page that highlights the Agreement sets icon located on the left-side of the page.")
9. Select the Agreement sets icon from the tray of icons on the left-side of the page.

   The Agreement sets pane opens and a list of sets to which the agreement has been added is displayed.

title: Remove Agreements from Existing Agreement Sets
heading_path: Remove Agreements from Existing Agreement Sets
text:
page.")
9. Select the Agreement sets icon from the tray of icons on the left-side of the page.

   The Agreement sets pane opens and a list of sets to which the agreement has been added is displayed.

   ![A snippet of the Agreement preview page that highlights the Agreement sets pane, including available actions.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-Preview-page_Agreement-sets-pane.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTczNTEsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.mkabalaSdMrXqrosnohCmB-UVBsah0i59ht1WdTmxl8&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Agreement preview page that highlights the Agreement sets pane, including available actions.")
10. Select the Remove (x) icon associated with the set from which you want to remove the agreement.

    A message displays stating that the agreement has been removed from the set.
11. Select the Close (x) icon from the top-left corner.

    You are returned to the Agreement Manager homepage. Your search criteria and filters are still applied.

You have successfully removed agreements from an existing agreement set.

title: Edit Agreement Set Details
text:
Learn how to update the details for an agreement set, including the set name and description.

Contract managers and any users who are assigned the Organize and manage all agreements permission can create and update agreement sets. This topic reviews the steps to update the set details for an agreement set.

Note: An organization can have up to 50 agreement sets per account. If you exceed this limit, an error message is displayed.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents page is displayed.
4. Expand the Settings (gear) menu from the top right and select the Sets option:

   ![A screenshot of the Settings (gear) menu on the Agreement Manager homepage that highlights the Sets option.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-sets_entry-point_Manage-sets-action-menu.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTczOTgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.d8ldvJIQWynR24rbA4LV1fWDapArudEiC0hvs8KBs88&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Settings (gear) menu on the Agreement Manager homepage that highlights the Sets option.")

   The Agreement sets page opens. The All agreement sets view is displayed. The table lists the sets that were shared with or created by the logged-in user.
5. Locate the set that you want to update.

   Tip: Select the sorting arrows in the Name column to sort the table by set name.
6. Expand the More actions (three dots) menu for the set that you want to update and select Edit details:

   ![A snippet of the All agreement sets view that highlights the Edit details option in the More actions menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Manage-Sets-dialog_All-agreement-sets_Edit-Details.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTczOTgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.d8ldvJIQWynR24rbA4LV1fWDapArudEiC0hvs8KBs88&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the All agreement sets view that highlights the Edit details option in the More actions menu.")

   The Edit agreement set dialog box opens.

   ![A screenshot of the Edit agreement set dialog box that shows the Name and Description fields for the selected set.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-set-management_Updaed-details_Edit-agreement-set-dialog-box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTczOTgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.d8ldvJIQWynR24rbA4LV1fWDapArudEiC0hvs8KBs88&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Edit agreement set dialog box that shows the Name and Description fields for the selected set.")
7. Update the Name and Description for the set as needed.
8. Select Save.

   The dialog box closes. The updated set name and description are displayed in the agreement set table.
9. Select Completed Documents from the breadcrumb navigation at the top of the page.

   You are returned to the Agreement Manager homepage.

You have successfully edited the details for a selected agreement set.

title: Verify User and Group Changes from the Audit Logs
heading_path: Verify User and Group Changes from the Audit Logs
text:
This topic answers the question: How do I verify the user and group permission changes that I made? Learn how to access the Audit Logs to verify permission and user management changes to your account.

Audit logs provide a record of the changes made to your account. You can select a date range and
use the Export to CSV option to export up to 1,000 records at
a time.

In Agreement Manager, audit log events are captured at the account-level. Make sure that you
are updating records for the correct account.

1. Log in to your Docusign account using administrator credentials.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Admin from the global header.

   The Admin menu opens and the Accounts view is displayed.

**Select an account (for organizations with multiple accounts)**

Important: User and group permissions are managed at the *account*-level. Make sure that you are updating records from Accounts administration instead of Organization administration. If your organization has multiple accounts, verify that you are updating the correct account.

3. Expand the Accounts dropdown from the top of the navigation pane and select the relevant account:

   ![A snippet of the Accounts view that highlights the Accounts dropdown in the pane on the left.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Permissions_Admin-menu_Accounts-dropdown_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTYzMjYsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.maJA__-9QCvb2u4UoTwUL05BrjNqrpeloVctopSJOpo&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Accounts view that highlights the Accounts dropdown in the pane on the left.")

   The Accounts view refreshes and the Overview page is displayed for the selected account.

**View audit logs**

4. Select the Audit Logs option from the AUDITING section in the navigation pane.

   The Audit Logs view opens. The audit log table displays records of changes made to your account. The list is filtered to display events over the last seven days.
5. Filter the table to locate events that occurred over a specific time period.
   1. Expand the Event Date filter located above the table. The Filter Audit Logs dialog box opens.
   2. Expand the Event Date dropdown and select a time period. Choices include: Custom, Last 7 Days, Last 30 Days,
      Last 6 Months, and Last 12 Months.

      Note: If you select Custom, enter a date range in the From and To date fields. Complete the From Time and End Time fields to locate specific events.
   3. Select Apply.

      The Filter Audit Logs dialog box closes. Events for the specified time period are displayed in the Audit Logs table.
6. Review the table to verify the user and group management changes for your account.

   The table displays the Event Date, User, Description, Field, Old Value, and New Value for each change.

**Export table data**

7. Export the Audit Logs table data. Select the Export to CSV option.

   The auditLog.csv file is downloaded to your local Downloads folder.

You have successfully viewed the audit log to verify permission and user management changes for your account.

title: Monitor and Report on Renewals
text:
Learn how to monitor and report on renewals from the Renewals dashboard, including subscribing to receive notifications. This topic answers the question: How do I report on my renewals?

The Renewals dashboard provides a visual summary of your upcoming agreement renewals through the use of bar and pie charts. After applying filters, you can go to the detailed report view for any of the dashboard charts.

Note: Subscribe to receive daily report notifications about your agreement renewals. This feature is available from the detailed report view. Review the [Subscribe to Receive Report Notifications](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=wfw1734545937270.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to set up one-time or recurring email reports for yourself or others to receive notifications about your obligations, renewals, or agreements.") article to learn more about this feature.

**Access Renewals dashboard:**

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Insights from the global header.

   The Insights hub opens and the Overview is displayed.
3. Select the Renewals option from the navigation pane on the left.

   The Renewals dashboard opens.

   ![A screenshot of the Renewals dashboard that shows the agreement charts and filter options.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Renewals-dashboard.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwNDQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.2WuSxrIxJSxVJfkB4QaJ37g_NinZILfJCyJWv0kMw6I&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Renewals dashboard that shows the agreement charts and filter options.")

**Apply filters to the dashboard:**

4. Filter the Renewals dashboard to locate upcoming renewals:

   ![A snippet of the Renewals dashboard that highlights the dashboard filters.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Renewals-dashboard_Filter-pills.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwNDQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.2WuSxrIxJSxVJfkB4QaJ37g_NinZILfJCyJWv0kMw6I&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Renewals dashboard that highlights the dashboard filters.")

   Filters include: Parties, Renewal Notice Date, Renewal Owner, Renewal Type, and Status.

   1. Expand the filter that you want to apply.
   2. Select or enter the filter value.
   3. Select the Apply option.

   Important: When using the Parties filter, you must also select a Party Role value. If you do not know which role to select, choose the All option. If no role is selected, the search returns agreements where the party is not the buyer or the seller.

   Tip: Use the Filters option to access additional filters:![A screenshot of the Filters dialog box that shows additional filters that you can apply to the agreement list.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-documents-table_All-Filters-dialog-box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwNDQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.2WuSxrIxJSxVJfkB4QaJ37g_NinZILfJCyJWv0kMw6I&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Filters dialog box that shows additional filters that you can apply to the agreement list.")

   The charts are updated to reflect the filters that you applied.

**View dashboard charts:**

5. Review the dashboard charts: Upcoming renewals and Renewals by type.

title: Monitor and Report on Renewals
text:
apply to the agreement list.")

   The charts are updated to reflect the filters that you applied.

**View dashboard charts:**

5. Review the dashboard charts: Upcoming renewals and Renewals by type.

   Hover your cursor over a segment or bar to view the representative agreement count and percentage:![A screenshot of the Renewals by type chart that shows the hover messaging for a selected chart segment.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Renewals_Renewal-types_hover-on-segment.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwNDQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.2WuSxrIxJSxVJfkB4QaJ37g_NinZILfJCyJWv0kMw6I&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Renewals by type chart that shows the hover messaging for a selected chart segment.")

**Access the detailed report view:**

6. Access the detailed report view for a dashboard chart:

   ![A snippet of the Renewals dashboard that shows the View Report option in the More actions (three dots) menu for a dashboard chart.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Renewals-dashboard_Chart_Actions_View-Report.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwNDQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.2WuSxrIxJSxVJfkB4QaJ37g_NinZILfJCyJWv0kMw6I&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Renewals dashboard that shows the View Report option in the More actions (three dots) menu for a dashboard chart.")

   - Select the chart title.
   - Hover the cursor over the chart, expand the More actions (three dots) menu and select the View Report option.

   The detailed report view opens for the selected chart. The agreements represented in the chart are listed in the table.

   ![A screenshot of the detailed report view for the Renewals by type dashboard chart.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Renewals-dashboard_detailed-report-example.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwNDQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.2WuSxrIxJSxVJfkB4QaJ37g_NinZILfJCyJWv0kMw6I&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the detailed report view for the Renewals by type dashboard chart.")

**Customize and download the report:**

7. Review the table to verify that the report contains the relevant renewals. If necessary, use the filters to refine the agreement list table.
8. Customize the information that is displayed in the table:

   ![A screenshot of the Show or Hide Fields dialog box that shows the columns you can choose to display in the agreements table.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Party-hub_Overview-dashboard_Customize-columns.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwNDQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.2WuSxrIxJSxVJfkB4QaJ37g_NinZILfJCyJWv0kMw6I&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Show or Hide Fields dialog box that shows the columns you can choose to display in the agreements table.")

   1. Select the Show or Hide Fields (toggle) icon. This icon is located on the right-side of the header row in the table.

      The Show or Hide Fields dialog box opens. These fields represent the columns in the table.
   2. Select the toggles to turn the associated field on (blue) or off (white).
   3. Re-order the columns as needed. Hover the cursor over the Reorder (dots) icon for the field that you want to move. Drag it to the appropriate location.
   4. Select the Save option.

   Continue applying filters and customizing the table data as needed.
9. Download the report. Expand the More actions (three dots) menu in the top right and select the Download option:

title: Monitor and Report on Renewals
text:
Save option.

   Continue applying filters and customizing the table data as needed.
9. Download the report. Expand the More actions (three dots) menu in the top right and select the Download option:

   ![A snippet of a detailed report view that shows the Download option in the expanded dropdown.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Renewals_Detailed-report_Download.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwNDQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.2WuSxrIxJSxVJfkB4QaJ37g_NinZILfJCyJWv0kMw6I&_LANG=enus "A snippet of a detailed report view that shows the Download option in the expanded dropdown.")

   The associated report is downloaded to your local Downloads folder.

**Subscribe to the report:**

10. Set up one-time and recurring email reports for yourself and others. Select the Manage Notification (bell) icon and complete the Create subscription dialog box:

    ![A screenshot of the Create subscription dialog box that is used to subscribe to report notifications.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Renewals-dashboard_Report-view_Create-subscriptions.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwNDQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.2WuSxrIxJSxVJfkB4QaJ37g_NinZILfJCyJWv0kMw6I&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Create subscription dialog box that is used to subscribe to report notifications.")

    Note: When you create a subscription, you have the option to add individual users and groups. Once created, the subscription is displayed in the Manage report subscriptions dialog box for any individual users who were added. Currently, the subscription is not visible to users if they were added as part of a group.

    Review the [Subscribe to Receive Report Notifications](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=wfw1734545937270.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to set up one-time or recurring email reports for yourself or others to receive notifications about your obligations, renewals, or agreements.") article to learn more about this feature.

**Add the report to Your Favorites:**

11. Add the report to Your Favorites list. Select the Favorite (star) icon located in the top right:

    ![A snippet of a detailed report view that highlights the Favorite (star) icon.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Renewals-dashboard_Report-view_Mark-favorite_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwNDQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.2WuSxrIxJSxVJfkB4QaJ37g_NinZILfJCyJWv0kMw6I&_LANG=enus "A snippet of a detailed report view that highlights the Favorite (star) icon.")Your starred dashboards and reports are displayed in the Your Favorites section on the Overview dashboard.

**Return to the Renewals dashboard:**

12. Return to the Renewals dashboard. Select the Renewals option from the breadcrumb navigation:

    ![A snippet of the detailed report view that highlights the breadcrumb navigation.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Renewals-dashboard_Report-view_Renewals_breadcrumb.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwNDQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.2WuSxrIxJSxVJfkB4QaJ37g_NinZILfJCyJWv0kMw6I&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the detailed report view that highlights the breadcrumb navigation.")

    You are returned to the Renewals dashboard.

You have successfully reviewed the renewal monitoring and reporting features of the Insights hub.

title: View and Download an Agreement Report
heading_path: View and Download an Agreement Report
text:
Learn how to view an agreement chart, drill down to the detailed report view, apply filters, and download agreement data. This topic answers the question: What are my reporting options for Agreement Manager agreements, including applying filters and downloading results?

When you apply filters to the Agreements Dashboard, the agreements that match the filters are reflected in the insights and charts.

Note: **Downloading reports to CSV files**

When downloading reports to CSV files from the Insights reporting hub, there is an agreement limit of 25,000 agreements for all IAM reports. This count includes your custom reports.

**Suggested workaround:** Adjust the report filters to run the report in batches until all the relevant agreements are downloaded. For example, run the report in batches according to varying date ranges. Once downloaded, combine the data into one CSV file.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Insights from the global header.

   The Insights hub opens and the Overview dashboard is displayed.
3. Select Agreements from the navigation pane on the left.

   The Agreements Dashboard view opens.
4. Use the default filters to filter the dashboard by Agreement
   Type, Sets,
   Parties, and Time Period

   ![A snippet of the Agreements Dashboard showing the default filter options, Agreement Type, Sets, Parties, and Time Period.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Agreements-Dashboard_Filters.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY5MjEsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.tx_EfTBFRm5eVhqRsccpqtfY7weLSTJahZf8xMnJ6u8&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Agreements Dashboard showing the default filter options, Agreement Type, Sets, Parties, and Time Period.")

   Tip: Filter the Agreements Dashboard by
   Party Role to view insights for agreements where
   the selected party played a specific role:

   ![A screenshot of the Parties filter that highlights the Party Role dropdown and available roles.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Filter-by-party-role_Parties-filter_Party-Role_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY5MjEsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.tx_EfTBFRm5eVhqRsccpqtfY7weLSTJahZf8xMnJ6u8&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Parties filter that highlights the Party Role dropdown and available roles.")

   - Expand the Parties filter and select the
     relevant party.

     Be sure to select the party before choosing a
     role. At least one party must be selected before the
     Party Role filter can be
     applied.
   - Select the relevant role from the Party
     Role dropdown.

     If you do not know which role
     to select, choose the All option. If
     no role is selected, the dashboard reflects agreements where
     the party is not the buyer or seller.
   - Select the Apply option.

   The Agreements Dashboard updates and insights based
   on the applied filters are displayed.

   The dashboard insights and report charts update automatically as filters are
   applied. Filters can also be applied at the report-level.
5. Review the agreement insights to view a count of your active, expiring, and renewing agreements:

title: View and Download an Agreement Report
heading_path: View and Download an Agreement Report
text:
rts update automatically as filters are
   applied. Filters can also be applied at the report-level.
5. Review the agreement insights to view a count of your active, expiring, and renewing agreements:

   ![A snippet of the Agreements Dashboard that shows a count of active, expiring, and renewing agreements.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Agreements_Agreements-Dashboard_toplevel-insights.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY5MjEsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.tx_EfTBFRm5eVhqRsccpqtfY7weLSTJahZf8xMnJ6u8&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Agreements Dashboard that shows a count of active, expiring, and renewing agreements.")
6. Hover your cursor over the sections or bars in the dashboard charts to see summary details. For example, the agreement count of the agreements represented in the segment:

   ![A snippet of an Agreements Dashboard chart that shows the hover text for a bar or section in a chart.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Agreements-Dashboard_Hover-on-bar-chart.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY5MjEsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.tx_EfTBFRm5eVhqRsccpqtfY7weLSTJahZf8xMnJ6u8&_LANG=enus "A snippet of an Agreements Dashboard chart that shows the hover text for a bar or section in a chart.")

   Note: Select a chart segment or bar to view the agreements represented in the bar or section. The report view opens and the corresponding agreements are displayed in the table.
7. Go to the detailed report view for a selected insight or chart. Select (click) the title of the insight or chart that you want to view.

   You can also expand the More actions (three dots) menu and select the View Report option:

   ![A snippet of an Agreements Dashboard chart that shows the View Report option in the More actions (three dots) menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-dashboard_Expanded-More-actions-menu_View-Report.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY5MjEsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.tx_EfTBFRm5eVhqRsccpqtfY7weLSTJahZf8xMnJ6u8&_LANG=enus "A snippet of an Agreements Dashboard chart that shows the View Report option in the More actions (three dots) menu.")

   Note: For charts, you have the option to View as Bar Chart, View as Stacked Bar Chart, or Download as png.

   The detailed report view opens for the corresponding insight or chart.

   ![A screenshot of the detailed report view for the Duration of agreements chart.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Agreements_Detailed-report-example-for-download.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY5MjEsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.tx_EfTBFRm5eVhqRsccpqtfY7weLSTJahZf8xMnJ6u8&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the detailed report view for the Duration of agreements chart.")
8. Verify that the report contains the appropriate agreements. Use the filters to refine the agreement list as needed.

title: View and Download an Agreement Report
heading_path: View and Download an Agreement Report
text:
"A screenshot of the detailed report view for the Duration of agreements chart.")
8. Verify that the report contains the appropriate agreements. Use the filters to refine the agreement list as needed.

   Tip: Select the All Filters option to choose additional filters to apply to the report. For example, filter the report by agreement status. Expand the Status filter, choose the relevant statuses, and select Apply:![A screenshot of the All Filters dialog box that shows the Status filter.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-statuses_All-Filters_Status-filter.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY5MjEsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.tx_EfTBFRm5eVhqRsccpqtfY7weLSTJahZf8xMnJ6u8&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the All Filters dialog box that shows the Status filter.")
9. Customize the columns in the table:

   Important: The Customize columns function only applies to the data that is displayed in the agreement list table. When downloading or exporting the report, all fields are included in the output file. This includes both custom and standard fields.

   ![A screenshot of the Customize columns dialog box that shows the columns you can choose to display in the agreements table.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Party-hub_Overview-dashboard_Customize-columns.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY5MjEsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.tx_EfTBFRm5eVhqRsccpqtfY7weLSTJahZf8xMnJ6u8&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Customize columns dialog box that shows the columns you can choose to display in the agreements table.")

   1. Select the Customize columns (toggle) icon.

      The Customize columns dialog box opens. These fields represent the columns in the table.
   2. Select the toggles to turn the associated field on (blue) or off (white).
   3. Re-order the columns as needed. Hover the cursor over the Reorder (dots) icon for the column that you want to move and drag it to the appropriate location.
   4. Select the Save option.
10. Download or save the report:

    ![A snippet of the report view that highlights the More actions (three dots) menu used to download or save the report.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Report-view_Dropdown-expanded.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY5MjEsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.tx_EfTBFRm5eVhqRsccpqtfY7weLSTJahZf8xMnJ6u8&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the report view that highlights the More actions (three dots) menu used to download or save the report.")

    - Expand the More actions (three dots) menu in the top right and select the Download.option.

      The associated report is downloaded to your local Downloads folder.
    - Expand the More actions (three dots) menu and select the Save option. The Save Report dialog box opens.

      - Enter a unique name for the report in the Report name field. The field defaults to the name of the selected report.
      - Enter a description about the report's purpose in the Description field.
      - Select the Save option.The report is saved to the Custom tab on the Reports view.
11. Set up one-time and recurring email reports for yourself and others. Select the Manage Notification (bell) icon and complete the Create subscription dialog box:

title: View and Download an Agreement Report
heading_path: View and Download an Agreement Report
text:
the Custom tab on the Reports view.
11. Set up one-time and recurring email reports for yourself and others. Select the Manage Notification (bell) icon and complete the Create subscription dialog box:

    ![A screenshot of the Create subscription dialog box that is used to subscribe to report notifications.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Create-Subscription.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY5MjEsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.tx_EfTBFRm5eVhqRsccpqtfY7weLSTJahZf8xMnJ6u8&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Create subscription dialog box that is used to subscribe to report notifications.")

    Note: When you create a subscription, you have the option to add individual users and groups. Once created, the subscription is displayed in the Manage report subscriptions dialog box for any individual users who were added. Currently, the subscription is not visible to users if they were added as part of a group.

    See the [Subscribe to Receive Report Notifications](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=wfw1734545937270.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to set up one-time or recurring email reports for yourself or others to receive notifications about your obligations, renewals, or agreements.") topic to learn more about this feature.
12. Add the report to Your Favorites list. Select the
    Favorite (star) icon located in the top right:

    ![A snippet of the detailed report view for a selected report that highlights the Favorite (star) icon.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-dashboard_Report-details_Favorite-icon_selected.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY5MjEsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.tx_EfTBFRm5eVhqRsccpqtfY7weLSTJahZf8xMnJ6u8&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the detailed report view for a selected report that highlights the Favorite (star) icon.")Your starred dashboards and reports are displayed in the Your
    Favorites section on the Overview
    dashboard.
13. Return to the Agreements Dashboard. Select the Agreements Dashboard option from the breadcrumb navigation at the top of the page.

    The Agreements Dashboard opens.
14. Repeat this process to view, download, or save other agreement reports.

title: Create an Agreement Set
text:
Learn how to create a new agreement set to ensure that the right people have access to relevant agreements. This topic answers the question: How can I make sure that the appropriate people have access to relevant agreements?

Contract managers use agreement sets to make sure the right people have access to relevant agreements. They add agreements into sets and share sets with the appropriate users and groups. Sets also serve organizational purposes by providing a way to group agreements.

Note: An organization can have up to 50 agreement sets per account. If you exceed this limit, an error message is displayed.

This topic shows how to create a new agreement set from the Sets option in the Settings (gear) menu.

Remember: Agreements sets can also be created from the Agreement sets pane on the Agreement preview page.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed.
4. Expand the Settings (gear) menu from the top right and select the Sets option:

   ![A screenshot of the Settings (gear) menu on the Agreement Manager homepage that highlights the Sets option.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-sets_entry-point_Manage-sets-action-menu.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTczMjAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.LmZcInbj68rfCvIlZZvD7xM2xXjMNjU-hXdUQrogvRA&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Settings (gear) menu on the Agreement Manager homepage that highlights the Sets option.")

   The Agreement sets manager opens. The All agreement sets page is displayed by default. The table lists the sets that were either shared with or created by the logged-in user.

   ![A screenshot of the All agreement sets page that displays the sets that were created by or shared with the logged-in user.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Manage-Sets-dialog_All-agreement-sets_New-Agreement-Set.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTczMjAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.LmZcInbj68rfCvIlZZvD7xM2xXjMNjU-hXdUQrogvRA&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the All agreement sets page that displays the sets that were created by or shared with the logged-in user.")
5. Select the + New Agreement Set option from the top right.

   The New agreement set page opens.
6. Complete the Set details step of the set creation process:

   ![A screenshot of the New agreement set page that shows the first step of the set creation process, Set details.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Manage-Agreement-Sets_Add-new-agreement-set.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTczMjAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.LmZcInbj68rfCvIlZZvD7xM2xXjMNjU-hXdUQrogvRA&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the New agreement set page that shows the first step of the set creation process, Set details.")

   1. Enter a name for the agreement set in the Set name field.

      Tip: The Set name field is limited to 60 characters.
   2. Enter a description for the agreement set in the Description field.

      Tip: The Description field is limited to 200 characters. This field is optional. The entered value is displayed in the All agreement sets table.
   3. Select the Next option.

   The next step of the process, *Smart filters*, is displayed.
7. Select the Choose filters option:

title: Create an Agreement Set
text:
nal. The entered value is displayed in the All agreement sets table.
   3. Select the Next option.

   The next step of the process, *Smart filters*, is displayed.
7. Select the Choose filters option:

   ![A snippet of the New agreement set page that shows the second step in the set creation process, Smart filters.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-set-management_create-new-set_step-2-choose-filters.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTczMjAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.LmZcInbj68rfCvIlZZvD7xM2xXjMNjU-hXdUQrogvRA&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the New agreement set page that shows the second step in the set creation process, Smart filters.")

   Note: Select Skip for now to skip this step. You can add or update smart filters at any time.
