   The names in the Parties column are clickable and provide access to the party hub.

   The party hub opens for the selected party or alias group. The Overview tab displays a visual summary of all active agreements that are tied to a specific party or alias group.
7. Review the top-level insights, such as the Party summary and agreement counts for Active agreements, Expiring soon, and Up for renewal:

   ![A snippet of the Overview dashboard that displays the top-level agreement insights for a specific party or alias group.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Party-hub_Overview-dashboard_Highlevel-insights.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTYyNzMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.p4TJcbmzpcp9wMPtGxWI4viI04qdq9ZbiDfE5PW3Pv4&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Overview dashboard that displays the top-level agreement insights for a specific party or alias group.")

   1. Expand the More actions (three dots) menu for the All active agreements section and select View Report.

      The report view opens. All active agreements tied to the selected party or group are displayed in the table.
   2. Use the filters to refine the agreement list as needed.

      Note: Select the All Filters option to choose additional filters to apply to the report.
   3. Select the Show or Hide Fields (gear) icon and choose the columns to display in the table:

      - Select the toggles to turn the associated field on (blue) or off (white).
      - Re-order the columns as needed:
        - Hover the cursor over the dots to the right of a field.

          The cursor updates to an open hand cursor, indicating that the element can be grabbed.
        - Drag the field to the appropriate location.
      - Select Save.

      The dialog box closes and the columns are updated according to your configuration.

      Note: The Show or Hide Fields function only applies to the data that is displayed in the agreement list table. When downloading or exporting the report, all fields are included in the output file. This includes both custom and standard fields.
   4. Select the Download option.

      The associated report is downloaded to your local Downloads folder.
   5. Return to the Agreement Manager homepage. Select the Completed option from the navigation pane on the left.

      You are returned to the Agreement Manager homepage and the Completed Documents view is displayed.

You have successfully located all active agreements for a specific customer. You can share this information for sales reporting.

title: Bulk Upload Documents and Set Access Controls
heading_path: Bulk Upload Documents and Set Access Controls
text:
This topic answers the question: How do I bulk upload documents to Agreement Manager? Learn how to use the bulk upload function to upload more than five documents to Agreement Manager.

The *bulk upload* process is used when you want to upload more than five files, such as uploading a folder of files.

Important: Limitations of the bulk upload process:

- File names must contain fewer than 255 characters
- Maximum of 1,000 files per bulk upload job
- Folder structure is not maintained when uploading folders with sub-folders
- Files must be less than 100 MB each

Note: The Upload up to 1,000 files at a time permission is required for users to bulk upload files and folders to Agreement Manager. This toggle is turned on from the user's profile page by an administrator.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed.
4. Expand the Add (+) menu in the top right and select the Upload option:

   ![A screenshot of the Add (+) menu that highlights the Upload option.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-documents-table_Upload-icon-highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY3MzEsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.GWnI5W2dVycbyBVNxK6YW1Qhr9iKe2xgOhyjkivom54&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Add (+) menu that highlights the Upload option.")

   The Upload documents dialog box opens.

   ![A screenshot of the Upload documents dialog box.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-documents-view_Upload-documents-dialog-box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY3MzEsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.GWnI5W2dVycbyBVNxK6YW1Qhr9iKe2xgOhyjkivom54&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Upload documents dialog box.")
5. Search for and select up to 1,000 files to bulk upload to Agreement Manager:
   - Drag the files or folders to the dialog box inside of the Drag and drop file here box.
   - Expand the Select File dropdown and select
     Browse: to search for and select files from local
     folders to upload:

     ![A screenshot of the Select File dropdown that shows the installed apps and the Browse option.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Mini-App-Center_Select-File-dropdown.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY3MzEsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.GWnI5W2dVycbyBVNxK6YW1Qhr9iKe2xgOhyjkivom54&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Select File dropdown that shows the installed apps and the Browse option.")

   The Upload documents dialog box opens.

   ![A screenshot of the Upload documents dialog box that shows the number of selected files.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Upload-documents_Bulk-upload_Step-1.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY3MzEsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.GWnI5W2dVycbyBVNxK6YW1Qhr9iKe2xgOhyjkivom54&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Upload documents dialog box that shows the number of selected files.")
6. An auto-generated name is displayed in the Job name field. Accept the default value or enter a new name.

   Tip: Enter a unique job name so you can easily find it in the job monitoring hub.
7. Add more files to the upload as needed:
   1. Select the Add option.
   2. Search for and select additional files to bulk upload to Agreement Manager.

title: Bulk Upload Documents and Set Access Controls
heading_path: Bulk Upload Documents and Set Access Controls
text:
can easily find it in the job monitoring hub.
7. Add more files to the upload as needed:
   1. Select the Add option.
   2. Search for and select additional files to bulk upload to Agreement Manager.

      Choices:
      - Drag the files or folders that you want to upload to the dialog box.
      - Select Upload and browse for documents to upload.
8. Select Next.

   The Allow access step is displayed in the Upload documents dialog box. You have three options:

   - Add the uploaded documents to an agreement set.
   - Share the files with others, including setting agreement-level permissions.
   - Skip this step to upload the documents without sharing or organizing into sets.

   ![A screenshot of the Upload documents dialog box that shows the Allow access step.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Upload-documents_Bulk-upload_Step-2.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY3MzEsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.GWnI5W2dVycbyBVNxK6YW1Qhr9iKe2xgOhyjkivom54&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Upload documents dialog box that shows the Allow access step.")

**Add the uploaded documents to an agreement set:**

Important: The Organize and manage all agreements
permission is required to add uploaded documents to agreement sets. See the
[Capabilities by Agreement Manager Permissions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=vhx1743523538507.html) topic to learn
more about Agreement Manager permissions for various features.

9. Add the uploaded documents to an agreement set:

   ![A snippet of the Upload documents dialog box that shows the Add to agreement sets option.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Upload-documents_Cloud-storage_Allow-access_agreement-sets-option.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY3MzEsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.GWnI5W2dVycbyBVNxK6YW1Qhr9iKe2xgOhyjkivom54&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Upload documents dialog box that shows the Add to agreement sets option.")

   1. Select the Add to agreement sets option.
   2. Enter a set name in the Search sets field and select the set from the type-ahead dropdown.
   3. Select Upload and Add to Sets.
   4. Follow the prompts to start the upload process.

      A dialog box opens. The upload job is initiated and your files are being processed with AI.

   Tip: If you cannot locate the relevant set, select the + Create Set option and create a new set:
   - Complete the Name and Description fields from the New agreement set dialog box.
   - Select either Create or Create and Share.

   See the [Create an Agreement Set](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=jay1736181359265.html) support topic to learn more about creating agreement sets.

**Share the uploaded documents:**

10. Share the uploaded documents with other users and groups:

    ![A snippet of the Upload documents dialog box that shows the Share files option.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Upload-documents_Cloud-storage_Allow-access_share-files-option.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY3MzEsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.GWnI5W2dVycbyBVNxK6YW1Qhr9iKe2xgOhyjkivom54&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Upload documents dialog box that shows the Share files option.")

    1. Select the Share files option.
    2. Enter a name or email address in the Add user or group search field.

title: Bulk Upload Documents and Set Access Controls
heading_path: Bulk Upload Documents and Set Access Controls
text:
us "A snippet of the Upload documents dialog box that shows the Share files option.")

    1. Select the Share files option.
    2. Enter a name or email address in the Add user or group search field.

       A type-ahead dropdown opens, and the users and groups that match the entered search criteria are displayed.
    3. Select a user or group name from the dropdown. Continue adding users or groups as needed.
    4. Expand the Access dropdown and select an access level for each of the selected users and groups.

       Choices are: Can View, Can Edit, and Can Manage.

       Note: Select the Delete icon to remove the associated with a user or group from the list.
    5. Select Upload and Share.
    6. Follow the prompts to start the upload process.

       A dialog box opens. The upload job is initiated and your files are being processed with AI.

**Skip this step:**

11. Skip the Allow access step to continue without adding an agreement set or sharing the documents.
    1. Select the Skip this step option.
    2. Select Upload.
    3. Follow the prompts to start the upload process. Select the Upload Privately option.

       The files are added to Agreement Manager but they are not added to a set or shared with others.

    A dialog box opens. The upload job is initiated and your files are being processed with AI.

    ![A screenshot of the Upload Complete dialog box that shows the number of documents being added to Agreement Manager.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Upload-documents_Bulk-upload_Upload-Complete-modal.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY3MzEsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.GWnI5W2dVycbyBVNxK6YW1Qhr9iKe2xgOhyjkivom54&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Upload Complete dialog box that shows the number of documents being added to Agreement Manager.")
12. Select Monitor progress.

    The job monitoring hub opens and the All jobs view is displayed. The Uploaded Agreements table shows job details for each upload job.

    ![A screenshot of the Uploaded Agreements table that shows job details for all upload and sync jobs.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-documents-view_Monitor-All-Jobs_All-JobsUploaded-tab.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY3MzEsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.GWnI5W2dVycbyBVNxK6YW1Qhr9iKe2xgOhyjkivom54&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Uploaded Agreements table that shows job details for all upload and sync jobs.")

    The created date, job name, source, number of files, and status details are displayed in the table for uploaded and ingested agreements.
13. Use the sorting arrows and filters to locate your upload job. You can filter the table by Created date, Status, and Source:
    - Expand the Created date filter, select an identifier tab, and complete the appropriate date fields.Select Apply.

      Identifier tab choices are: Between, Before, and After.

      Tip: If you choose Between, you must complete the From and To date fields.
    - Expand the Status filter, choose the preferred status, and select Apply.

      Choices are: Deleted, Upload job complete, Upload job failed, and Uploading documents.
    - Expand the Source filter, choose the relevant source, and select
      Apply.

      Choices are: Box,
      Dropbox, Email
      Ingestion, Google Drive,
      OneDrive, SharePoint,
      and Unknown.

    Upload jobs that match the applied filters are displayed in the table.
14. Review processing results, including the number of files successfully uploaded and the number of failures.
15. If any of your files failed to upload:
    1. Expand the Actions dropdown associated with your upload job and select View Details.

title: Bulk Upload Documents and Set Access Controls
heading_path: Bulk Upload Documents and Set Access Controls
text:
number of files successfully uploaded and the number of failures.
15. If any of your files failed to upload:
    1. Expand the Actions dropdown associated with your upload job and select View Details.

       The Job status page opens.
    2. Expand the Download and Fix dropdown and select the Download CSV option:

       ![A snippet of the Upload status page that shows error details and highlights the Download CSV option.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Upload-documents_Bulk-upload_Upload-status_Failed-documents.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY3MzEsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.GWnI5W2dVycbyBVNxK6YW1Qhr9iKe2xgOhyjkivom54&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Upload status page that shows error details and highlights the Download CSV option.")

       A .csv file containing the document name and associated error message is downloaded to your local Downloads folder.
    3. Select the Back arrow from the top-left corner.

       You are returned to the All jobs view.

    See the [View Upload Results and Exceptions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=srq1712255787588.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to view processing results and details for an upload job.") topic to learn more about job monitoring.
16. Select the Back arrow from the top-left corner.

    You are returned to the Completed view.

**Auto-suggestion for custom agreement types:**

When uploading agreements to Agreement Manager, if an agreement does not match an existing standard or custom type, it is identified as *Miscellaneous*. If 10 or more similar agreements are identified as *Miscellaneous*, a custom agreement type is suggested.

For example, 10 charter agreements were uploaded and identified as *Miscellaneous*. The agreements are recognized as being similar and a custom agreement type, *Charter Agreement*, is suggested:

![A snippet of the agreement list table that shows a suggested custom agreement type for a Miscellaneous document.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Autosuggest_New-suggestion-available_dialog-box_charter-agreement-example.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY3MzEsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.GWnI5W2dVycbyBVNxK6YW1Qhr9iKe2xgOhyjkivom54&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the agreement list table that shows a suggested custom agreement type for a Miscellaneous document.")

17. If necessary, use the Create option to create the suggested custom agreement type. See the [Use Auto-Suggestion to Create Custom Agreement Types](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tsk2093dc24-1da8-4393-b4cd-526990bc7118.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to create a custom agreement type from the auto-suggestion feature.") topic for steps to use this feature.

title: Locate Docusign Apps for Your Workflow Processes
heading_path: Locate Docusign Apps for Your Workflow Processes
text:
Learn how to locate apps you can use in your workflow processes by using the Docusign Search feature and Works With and Industry filters.

You can locate extension apps you can use in your workflow processes using the Docusign App Center.

You can also find available  [Workflow Builder templates](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=con8f35c78f-56b7-466c-91e6-c0ea7d03c516.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to select and initiate a Docusign Workflow Builder template from the Docusign App Center page.").

To use the Search feature:

1. Go to the App Center as described in the [Access Docusign Apps](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=bkt1740757069317.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to browse for and connect to Docusign extension apps from various Docusign feature locations.")
   article.
2. Enter the name in the Search field.

   ![A screenshot of the Docusign App Center search bar.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/ac-new-search.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTgxODcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.h6jw4KRUGgtT_QIQcVnY6OVIRD_bT9zhGN2pFpkAn9A&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Docusign App Center search bar.")

   Any apps corresponding to your entry display.

   ![A screenshot showing the results of the Docusign App Center Search feature.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/app-center-search-features.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTgxODcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.h6jw4KRUGgtT_QIQcVnY6OVIRD_bT9zhGN2pFpkAn9A&_LANG=enus "A screenshot showing the results of the Docusign App Center Search feature.")
3. Select the app that you want.

   The app's Detail Listing page displays.
4. Install the app as described in the [Install Apps in the App Center](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=ctg1698170340729.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to install Docusign extension apps in the Docusign App Center.") article.

title: Locate Docusign Apps for Your Workflow Processes
heading_path: Locate Docusign Apps for Your Workflow Processes
Use filters to refine your apps search
text:
1. Go to the App Center as described in the [Access Docusign Apps](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=bkt1740757069317.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to browse for and connect to Docusign extension apps from various Docusign feature locations.") article.
2. Select the Works With or Industry
   filter to display a list of Docusign products as shown in this
   Works With filter example.

   ![A screenshot showing the Docusign App Center Works with filter.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/ac-newest-workswith.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTgxODcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.h6jw4KRUGgtT_QIQcVnY6OVIRD_bT9zhGN2pFpkAn9A&_LANG=enus "A screenshot showing the Docusign App Center Works with filter.")
3. Select one or more Docusign products from the list.
   - Select Apply to display apps that work with
     your product.
   - Select Clear to start over.
4. Select from the resulting apps that work with the selected Docusign products.

   ![A screenshot showing the resulting apps for the Works With Agreement Manager Filter entry.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/ac-works-with-result-pg.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTgxODcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.h6jw4KRUGgtT_QIQcVnY6OVIRD_bT9zhGN2pFpkAn9A&_LANG=enus "A screenshot showing the resulting apps for the Works With Agreement Manager Filter entry.")
5. Select the app that you want.

   The app's Detail Listing page displays.
6. Select one of these options:

   - Install the app as described in the [Install Apps in the App Center](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=ctg1698170340729.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to install Docusign extension apps in the Docusign App Center.")
     article.
   - If the app is already installed, select Use This
     App.

title: Locate Docusign Apps for Your Workflow Processes
heading_path: Locate Docusign Apps for Your Workflow Processes
Use the Approved for you filter to customize your apps search
text:
1. Go to the App Center as described in the [Access Docusign Apps](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=bkt1740757069317.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to browse for and connect to Docusign extension apps from various Docusign feature locations.") article.
2. Select the Approved for you filter and select one of more of these apps.

   ![A screenshot of the App Center Approved for you filter options.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/approved-for-you-filter.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTgxODcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.h6jw4KRUGgtT_QIQcVnY6OVIRD_bT9zhGN2pFpkAn9A&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the App Center Approved for you filter options.")

   - Approved for you filter: Displays apps your
     Admin has approved for your organization. See the [Use Approved Apps](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=aak1747679824594.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about Docusign approved extension apps and who can install and use.") topic for
     details about using these apps.
     - Administrator approved: All Docusign users can install and use these
       approved apps in eSignature feature and Webforms.
     - Private apps: Custom apps created
       exclusively by and for your organization.

title: Filter the Agreement List Table to Locate Documents
heading_path: Filter the Agreement List Table to Locate Documents
text:
Learn how to use the available filters to refine the agreement list table to locate agreements. This topic answers the question: How can I find my documents in Agreement Manager?

Instead of performing a keyword search, filter the agreement list table to locate documents. Use the Filters option to locate and apply filters to the table:

![A snippet of the Completed Documents view that highlights the Filters option, including suggested filters.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-documents-view_Available-filter-pills.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY0NjQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.4_xghyYiJtYK1HZsk4Vk-El9C2g35fY8CUa8B9lXQSw&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Completed Documents view that highlights the Filters option, including suggested filters.")

This topic begins from the Agreement Manager homepage. The Completed Documents view is displayed.

Remember: The Completed Documents view is located under the Agreements tab in the global header:

- Log in to your Docusign account and select the
  Agreements tab from the global header.
- Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

The Agreement Manager homepage opens.

A list of documents to which the logged-in user has access is displayed in the table. The total document count is displayed in the bottom-right corner of the view. This count is updated when you perform searches or apply filters.

**Filter by Sets**

1. Expand (click) the Filters option and select the Sets filter.

   The Sets filter opens and a list of sets is displayed. These sets are either created by or shared with the logged-in user.

   ![A screenshot of the Sets filter that shows the Search options and the agreement set list.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-documents-table_Set-filter_expanded.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY0NjQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.4_xghyYiJtYK1HZsk4Vk-El9C2g35fY8CUa8B9lXQSw&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Sets filter that shows the Search options and the agreement set list.")

   1. Update the search operator as needed. The equals to (=) operator is selected by default. Other options are Does not exist, Exists, and Does not exist.
   2. Enter a keyword or search string in the Search field.

      As you enter search criteria, sets that match the entered keyword are displayed in the list.
   3. Select the set that contains the agreements that you want to view.
   4. Select the Apply option.

      The filter closes and the agreements that matched the applied filter value are displayed in the table.
   5. (Optional) Reset the filter. Select the Delete (x) icon in the Sets filter. The filter is cleared and the table is reset.

**Filter by Expiration Date**

2. Expand (select) the Filters option and search for the Expiration Date filter. Select the Expiration Date filter from the results list.

   The Expiration Date filter opens and a list of date ranges is displayed, including the Custom Date Range option.

   ![A screenshot of the Expiration Date filter that shows the available date range options, including the Custom Date Range option.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-documents-table_Expiration-Date-dialog-box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY0NjQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.4_xghyYiJtYK1HZsk4Vk-El9C2g35fY8CUa8B9lXQSw&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Expiration Date filter that shows the available date range options, including the Custom Date Range option.")

title: Filter the Agreement List Table to Locate Documents
heading_path: Filter the Agreement List Table to Locate Documents
text:
k0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.4_xghyYiJtYK1HZsk4Vk-El9C2g35fY8CUa8B9lXQSw&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Expiration Date filter that shows the available date range options, including the Custom Date Range option.")

   1. Update the search operator as needed. The equals to (=) operator is selected by default. Other options are: Exists and Does not exist.
   2. Select the relevant date range from the list of options. Choices include: Today, Next 24 Hours, Next 7 Days, Next 14 Days, and Next 30 Days.

      Note: Select Custom Date Range to search for expiring agreements over a specific time period.

      The dialog box updates to show the From and To fields, as well as Between, Before, and After options.

      Select an option from the top of the dialog box and enter a date or date range in the From and To fields.
   3. Select the Apply option.

      The filter closes and the agreements that matched the applied date range are displayed in the table.
   4. (Optional) Reset the filter. Select the Delete (x) icon in the Expiration Date filter. The filter is cleared and the table is reset.

**Filter by Status**

3. Expand (click) the Filters option and select the Status filter.

   The Status filter opens and the available statuses are displayed.

   ![A screenshot of the Status filter that shows the available filter options, such as Active and Inactive.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-statuses_Status-filter_expanded_no-selections.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY0NjQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.4_xghyYiJtYK1HZsk4Vk-El9C2g35fY8CUa8B9lXQSw&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Status filter that shows the available filter options, such as Active and Inactive.")

   1. Select the relevant statuses. For example, choose to view agreements that are effective, expiring, or renewing soon.
   2. Select the Apply option.

      The filter closes and the agreements that matched the applied statuses are displayed in the table.
   3. (Optional) Reset the filter. Select the Delete (x) icon in the Status filter. The filter is cleared and the table is reset.

   See the [Agreement Statuses](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=con6a3bd080-da17-4d1c-a92a-b9b9340c44fd.html&_LANG=enus "Read about agreement statuses, including how they are calculated, visibility within Agreement Manager, and which types of statuses are being tracked.") topic to learn more about agreement statuses and the Status filter.

**Filter for Parties**

4. Expand (click) the Filters option and select the Parties filter.

   The Parties filter opens and a list of parties is displayed.

   ![A screenshot of the Parties filter that shows the Search field, Party Role dropdown, and the party list.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-documents-table_Parties-dialog-box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY0NjQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.4_xghyYiJtYK1HZsk4Vk-El9C2g35fY8CUa8B9lXQSw&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Parties filter that shows the Search field, Party Role dropdown, and the party list.")

   1. Update the search operator as needed. The = ANY
      operator is selected by default. This operator means that if multiple
      parties are selected there is an *OR* matching requirement.
      Documents must contain at least one of the selected parties to be
      included in the table.

      Tip: Select the = ALL option to
      locate documents that contain all the selected parties. For example,
      to view agreements between two specific parties.
   2. Locate the relevant parties. Enter a keyword or search string in the Search field.

      As you enter search criteria, the list of parties updates to match the keyword.
   3. Select the relevant party or parties.

title: Filter the Agreement List Table to Locate Documents
heading_path: Filter the Agreement List Table to Locate Documents
text:
levant parties. Enter a keyword or search string in the Search field.

      As you enter search criteria, the list of parties updates to match the keyword.
   3. Select the relevant party or parties.

      Note: When multiple values are selected within the same filter, there is either an *OR* or an *AND* relationship depending on the selected search operator.

      Important: When using the Parties filter, you must also select a
      Party Role. If you do not know
      which role to select, accept the default value, All.
   4. Apply the Party Role filter to view agreements where the selected party plays a specific role. For example, to view your buy-side or sell-side agreements with a specific party.

      Select a role from the Party Role dropdown. Choose from: Buyer, Seller, and Other.

      Tip: If you do not know which role to select, accept the default value, All. See the [Filter for Agreements by Specific Party Role](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tsk6f2903c9-7ae7-4ad5-8acd-1c4948c98d10.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to update the agreement list table to view agreements based on the role played by a specific party.") topic to learn more about using this filter.
   5. Select the Apply option.

      The agreements that match the applied filter are displayed in the agreement list table.
   6. (Optional) Reset the filter. Select the Delete (x) icon in the Parties filter. The filter is cleared and the table is reset.

**Filter by Agreement Type**

5. Expand (click) the Filters option and select the Agreement Type filter.

   The Agreement Type filter opens and a list of available agreement types is displayed.

   ![A screenshot of the Agreement Type filter that shows the field and the agreement type list.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-documents-table_Document-Type-dialog-box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY0NjQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.4_xghyYiJtYK1HZsk4Vk-El9C2g35fY8CUa8B9lXQSw&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Agreement Type filter that shows the field and the agreement type list.")

   1. Locate the relevant agreement types. Enter a keyword or search string in the Search field.

      As you enter the search criteria, the agreement types that match the keyword are displayed.
   2. Select the agreement types from the list.

      Note: Select multiple options to include multiple agreement types. Multiple values within the same filter imply an *OR* relationship.
   3. Clear the search criteria. Select the Clear Search (x) icon in the Search field. The field is cleared and the filter is reset. The agreement types that you selected previously remain selected.
   4. Repeat these steps to select additional agreement types.
   5. Select the Apply option.

      The table updates and the agreements that match the applied filter are displayed.
   6. (Optional) Reset the filter. Select the Delete (x) icon in the Agreement Type filter. The filter is cleared and the table is reset.

**Additional filters: View All Filters**

6. Expand (click) the Filters option and select the View All Filters option at the bottom of the dropdown menu.

   The Filters dialog box opens.

title: Filter the Agreement List Table to Locate Documents
heading_path: Filter the Agreement List Table to Locate Documents
text:
e is reset.

**Additional filters: View All Filters**

6. Expand (click) the Filters option and select the View All Filters option at the bottom of the dropdown menu.

   The Filters dialog box opens.

   ![A screenshot of the Filters dialog box that shows the Applied and Available filters.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-documents-table_All-Filters-dialog-box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY0NjQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.4_xghyYiJtYK1HZsk4Vk-El9C2g35fY8CUa8B9lXQSw&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Filters dialog box that shows the Applied and Available filters.")
7. Scroll through the list to locate and apply additional filters to the agreement list table:
   1. Expand the filter that you want to apply.
   2. Enter or select filter criteria.
   3. Select the Apply option.

      The filter is highlighted and the selected criteria is displayed.
   4. Repeat this step to apply additional filters.
8. Select the Save option.

   The Filters dialog box closes. The agreements that match the applied filters are displayed in the table. The document count is updated based on your filters.

   Note: Any filters that were applied from the Filters dialog box are displayed at the top of the table. Expand these filters and select the Clear option to remove the applied filter.

You have successfully filtered the agreement list table to locate documents.

title: Upload Documents from Cloud Storage Locations
heading_path: Upload Documents from Cloud Storage Locations
text:
This topic answers the question: How do I add documents from cloud storage locations to Agreement Manager? Learn how to select documents from cloud storage locations to upload to Agreement Manager for processing.

Instead of uploading documents from local folders, you can choose files from your organization's cloud storage locations. Keep in mind that:

- File names must contain fewer than 255 characters
- Files must be less than 100 MB each
- Maximum of 1,000 files per bulk upload job

Note: The Upload up to 1,000 files at a time permission must be turned on for users to bulk upload files and folders to Agreement Manager. This toggle is turned on from the user's profile page by an administrator.

Important: An administrator must install third-party cloud storage apps before you can upload files from these locations. Review the [File Ingestion from Third-Party Cloud Storage Providers](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=byx1727376126901.html&_LANG=enus "This topic answers the question: How can I upload selected files to Agreement Manager from third-party cloud storage providers?") topic for information about adding apps from the Docusign App Center.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed.
4. Expand the Add (+) dropdown and select the Upload option:

   ![A screenshot of the Add (+) dropdown menu that highlights the Upload option.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-documents-table_Upload-icon-highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY3NjAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.eka670OSS1D45PHzu62Z9q74iYccb1i4IGFx4GAbMus&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Add (+) dropdown menu that highlights the Upload option.")

   The Upload documents dialog box opens.

   ![A screenshot of the Upload documents dialog box.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-documents-view_Upload-documents-dialog-box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY3NjAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.eka670OSS1D45PHzu62Z9q74iYccb1i4IGFx4GAbMus&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Upload documents dialog box.")
5. Expand the Select File dropdown and select the cloud storage location for the files you want to upload:

   ![A screenshot of the Select File dropdown that shows the installed apps and options to connect to them.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Mini-App-Center_Select-File-dropdown.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY3NjAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.eka670OSS1D45PHzu62Z9q74iYccb1i4IGFx4GAbMus&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Select File dropdown that shows the installed apps and options to connect to them.")

   The available cloud storage apps are displayed in the installed apps list. Before you can upload documents from an app, you must establish a connection to your account.
6. Use the Add Connection link to connect your account to the app.
   1. Select the Add Connection link for the app that you want to use.
   2. Follow the prompts to connect your account to the app.
7. Once connected, select the app from the installed apps list and continue the upload process.

title: Upload Documents from Cloud Storage Locations
heading_path: Upload Documents from Cloud Storage Locations
text:
tion link for the app that you want to use.
   2. Follow the prompts to connect your account to the app.
7. Once connected, select the app from the installed apps list and continue the upload process.

   The Select files from [Cloud Storage] dialog box opens. A list of drives is displayed.
8. Expand the Select a connection dropdown and choose the relevant connection. Select Next.
9. Expand the Select a drive dropdown and select the drive that contains the files you want to upload. Select Next.

   The drive opens. The folders and files in the drive are displayed on the Browse tab.
10. Use the search bar and sorting arrows to locate and select the files that you want to upload:

    ![A screenshot of the Select files dialog box that shows the Browse tab, including selected files.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Upload-documents_cloud-storage_Select-files-from-drive_Browse-tab.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY3NjAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.eka670OSS1D45PHzu62Z9q74iYccb1i4IGFx4GAbMus&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Select files dialog box that shows the Browse tab, including selected files.")

    1. Enter a keyword or search string in the Search field and press [Enter].
    2. Use the sorting arrows to sort the list of documents by Name, Type, or Last Modified.
    3. Select the checkboxes associated with the files that you want to upload.

       You can also choose to upload the entire contents of a folder. If you select a folder, all the files and sub-folders within it are selected.

       As each file is selected the number of Selected Items increases. The breadcrumb navigation updates to display your folder location within the drive.
11. Select the Selected Items tab.

    The list of selected files displays in the dialog box.
12. Review the list of selected files to confirm that all the relevant files are selected.

    ![A screenshot of the Select files dialog box that shows the Selected Items tab.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Upload-documents_cloud-storage_Select-files-from-drive_Selected-Items-tabpng.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY3NjAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.eka670OSS1D45PHzu62Z9q74iYccb1i4IGFx4GAbMus&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Select files dialog box that shows the Selected Items tab.")

    Note: **Regular document upload**

    If you are uploading fewer than five documents, select Submit.

    The Completed Documents view opens and a message states that the upload was successful. The files are being processed. No other actions are required.
13. Select Submit.

    The Upload documents dialog box opens.

    ![A screenshot of the Upload documents dialog box that shows the number of selected files.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Upload-documents_Cloud-storage_Upload-documents-modal_Google-example.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY3NjAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.eka670OSS1D45PHzu62Z9q74iYccb1i4IGFx4GAbMus&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Upload documents dialog box that shows the number of selected files.")
14. An auto-generated name is displayed in the Job name field. Accept the default value or enter a new name.

    Tip: Enter a unique job name so you can easily find it in the job monitoring hub.
15. Add more files to the upload as needed:
    1. Select Add.
    2. Search for and select additional files to bulk upload to Agreement Manager. You may be prompted to select the drive again.
    3. Select the checkboxes associated with the files that you want to upload.
    4. Select Submit.

       The Upload documents dialog box opens.
16. Select Next.

title: Upload Documents from Cloud Storage Locations
heading_path: Upload Documents from Cloud Storage Locations
text:
rompted to select the drive again.
    3. Select the checkboxes associated with the files that you want to upload.
    4. Select Submit.

       The Upload documents dialog box opens.
16. Select Next.

    The Allow access step is displayed in the Upload documents dialog box. You have three options:

    ![A screenshot of the Upload documents dialog box that shows the Allow access step.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Upload-documents_Bulk-upload_Step-2.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY3NjAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.eka670OSS1D45PHzu62Z9q74iYccb1i4IGFx4GAbMus&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Upload documents dialog box that shows the Allow access step.")

    - Add the uploaded documents to an agreement set.
    - Share the files with others, including setting agreement-level permissions.
    - Skip this step to upload the documents without sharing or organizing into sets.

**Add the uploaded documents to agreement sets:**

Important: The Organize and manage all agreements permission is required to add uploaded documents to agreement sets. See the [Capabilities by Agreement Manager Permissions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=vhx1743523538507.html) topic to learn more about Agreement Manager permissions for various features.

17. Add the uploaded documents to an agreement set:

    ![A snippet of the Upload documents dialog box that shows the Add to agreement sets option.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Upload-documents_Cloud-storage_Allow-access_agreement-sets-option.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY3NjAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.eka670OSS1D45PHzu62Z9q74iYccb1i4IGFx4GAbMus&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Upload documents dialog box that shows the Add to agreement sets option.")

    1. Select the Add to agreement sets option.
    2. Enter a set name in the Search sets field and select the set from the type-ahead dropdown.
    3. Select Upload and Add to Sets.
    4. Follow the prompts to start the upload process.

       A dialog box opens. The upload job is initiated and your files are being processed with AI.

    Tip: If you cannot locate the relevant set, select the + Create Set option and create a new set:
    - Complete the Name and Description fields from the New agreement set dialog box.
    - Select either Create or Create and Share.

    See the [Create an Agreement Set](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=jay1736181359265.html) support topic to learn more about creating agreement sets.

**Share the uploaded documents:**

18. Share the uploaded documents with other users and groups:

    ![A snippet of the Upload documents dialog box that shows the Share files option.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Upload-documents_Cloud-storage_Allow-access_share-files-option.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY3NjAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.eka670OSS1D45PHzu62Z9q74iYccb1i4IGFx4GAbMus&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Upload documents dialog box that shows the Share files option.")

    1. Select the Share files option.
    2. Enter a name or email address in the Add user or group search field.

       A type-ahead dropdown opens, and the users and groups that match the entered search criteria are displayed.
    3. Select a user or group name from the dropdown. Continue adding users or groups as needed.
    4. Expand the Access dropdown and select an access level for each of the selected users and groups.

       Choices are: Can View, Can Edit, and Can Manage.

title: Upload Documents from Cloud Storage Locations
heading_path: Upload Documents from Cloud Storage Locations
text:
tinue adding users or groups as needed.
    4. Expand the Access dropdown and select an access level for each of the selected users and groups.

       Choices are: Can View, Can Edit, and Can Manage.

       Note: Select the Delete icon to remove the associated with a user or group from the list.
    5. Select Upload and Share.
    6. Follow the prompts to start the upload process.

       A dialog box opens. The upload job is initiated and your files are being processed with AI.

**Skip this step:**

19. Skip the Allow access step to continue without adding an agreement set or sharing the documents.
    1. Select the Skip this step option.
    2. Select Upload.
    3. Follow the prompts to start the upload process.

       The files will be added to Agreement Manager but they won't be organized or
       shared with others.

    A dialog box opens. The upload job is initiated and your files are being processed with AI.

    ![A screenshot of the Adding your files dialog box that states your files are being processing with AI and will be added to Agreement Manager once finished.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Upload-documents_Cloud-storage_Adding-files-from-Google-Drive.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY3NjAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.eka670OSS1D45PHzu62Z9q74iYccb1i4IGFx4GAbMus&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Adding your files dialog box that states your files are being processing with AI and will be added to Agreement Manager once finished.")
20. Select Monitor progress.

    The job monitoring hub opens and the All jobs view is displayed. The Uploaded Agreements table shows job details for each upload job.

    See the [View Upload Results and Exceptions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=srq1712255787588.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to view processing results and details for an upload job.") topic to learn more about job monitoring.

title: Docusign App Center Banner Messages and App Labels
heading_path: Docusign App Center Banner Messages and App Labels
text:
Learn about the messages and availability app labels that you encounter when using Docusign App Center and the actions you should take.

Here are messages that you can encounter when using Docusign App
Center.

title: Docusign App Center Banner Messages and App Labels
heading_path: Docusign App Center Banner Messages and App Labels
text:
These messages are only shown to Docusign administrators who manage
App Center apps for their organization.

| Message | Meaning | Action |
| --- | --- | --- |
| App successfully installed. | You successfully entered credentials to access the app. | Start using the app in workflow processes. Create app connections as described in the [Create Docusign App Connections](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=zsb1714159452358.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to create Docusign apps connections for different user credentials in Docusign agreement workflows. You can create connections that are shared by all account users or used privately by the user who created the app connection.") article. |
| Contact <trusted partner >, our trusted partner, to build your < custom application > solution for Docusign IAM. | The trusted partner will build the solution specifically for your needs. | Contact the listed trusted partner. |
| This app is approved to install for your organization. | Docusign Administrators and Non-admin users can install this application. | See the [Use Approved Apps](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=aak1747679824594.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about Docusign approved extension apps and who can install and use.") article to learn more about approved apps. |
| Your Docusign plan doesn't support installing this app. | Your organization's plan does not support access to this app. | Contact your Docusign account team to request access to the products that work with this app. |
| Your plan does not include an appname | You can't approve this app for your organization until you add the app to your plan. Your account plan does not include the app. | Contact the [Docusign sales team](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales). |

title: Docusign App Center Banner Messages and App Labels
heading_path: Docusign App Center Banner Messages and App Labels
text:
| Message | Meaning | Action |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Approved | The app is approved for use in your account. | You can install and use the app with Docusign features. |
| Contact your organization's Docusign Admin to manage this app. | This app is only managed by your organization's Docusign Admin. You cannot install or manage this app. | Request that your Docusign organization administrator:  - Installs this app for you. - Approves the app as described in the [Administrator: View and Manage Approved Apps](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=oct1747682633917.html&_LANG=enus "As a Docusign App Center administrator, learn how to access, view, and manage Docusign approved apps.") article. |
| Contact your Docusign administrator to uninstall and manage the apps. |
| Contact your Docusign administrator to manage shared connections. | This app's shared connections are only managed by your organization's Docusign administrator. | Request that your Docusign organization administrator manages the connection for you. Alternatively, create a private connection that you can manage yourself. See [Create Docusign App Connections](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=zsb1714159452358.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to create Docusign apps connections for different user credentials in Docusign agreement workflows. You can create connections that are shared by all account users or used privately by the user who created the app connection.") for details. |
| This app is approved to install for your organization. | Docusign Administrators and Non-admin users can install this application. | See the [Use Approved Apps](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=aak1747679824594.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about Docusign approved extension apps and who can install and use.") article to learn more about approved apps. |
| Log in to your account to start using this app, | You can't use the app in agreement processes until you connect to your account on the app. | Select Connect Account access the app and enter your credentials. If you don't have an account with the app, select Sign up. |
| This app is currently in our Beta program. | The app is in the testing phase and may be available to a select group of users. | If you cannot access the app, contact your Docusign organization administrator about accessing the app's Beta program. |
| We encountered an unexpected error. Refresh the page and try again. | An unexpected error prevented the page from properly displaying. | Check your network connection and refresh the page. |
| You are not authorized to install this app. | You attempted to create a connection that is not accepted by the app to which you want to connect. | Select Browse Approved Apps to find authorized apps. |
| You're connecting to an unverified app. | The app developers have not verified the app details. Make sure you trust the developer before connecting your account. | Contact your administrator if you have questions about using the app. |
| n connections need to be reconnected | Changes in the app may require that you reconnect the connections. | Reconnect connections as described in the [Reconnect a Docusign App Connection](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=qkv1716320270335.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to use the Docusign app Reconnect a connection feature to reestablish the Docusign app’s connection to workflows.") article. |

title: Docusign App Center Banner Messages and App Labels
heading_path: Docusign App Center Banner Messages and App Labels
text:
These labels identify the types of App Center apps and integrations.

- AI connectors: Data-driven connectors that enhance your Docusign
  workflows.
- Approved: Approved by your account administrator to install and use.
- eSignature integration: Third-party integrations that add Docusign
  functions to your favorite software tools without leaving them.
- Extension Apps: Docusign apps that extend the Docusign platform by integrating third-party capabilities.
- FedRAMP-certified: Apps certified when used within the Docusign
  FedRAMP boundary.

title: Docusign App Center Banner Messages and App Labels
heading_path: Docusign App Center Banner Messages and App Labels
text:
These labels define the App Center app's availability status.

- No tag: The app is available to you. However, there may
  be restrictions based on regional availability or product compatibility.
- Beta: The app supports a limited set of features, or
  includes features that are still being developed and tested.
- Coming soon: The app is not yet available to use.
- Custom solution: A Systems Integrator(SI) partner built a
  custom app that it distributes to select customers.

  Note: Custom solution apps are only distributed in the U.S..
- Early Access: The app is only available to a limited
  selection of customers.
- Private: A customer built the app for their own accounts. The
  app is not publicly distributed.

title: The Agreement Preview Page
text:
Read about the purpose, usage, and key components of the Agreement preview page. This topic answers the question: Where can I see my agreement details and obligations?

The Agreement preview page has two primary components:

![A screenshot of the Agreement preview page that shows the Details pane, including data points that were surfaced within the document.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Navigator_Agreement-Preview-page.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY1OTMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.gbvNuKu9YJ3xgo939Q_amfJ12P78Lej3E64bsF2iJ80&_LANG=enus)

1. The pane on the left displays Details, Obligations, Agreement sets, and Related agreements for the selected agreement:
   - Details **pane:** Displays attributes and attribute values that were either manually created or surfaced within the agreement.
   - Obligations **pane:** Displays a list of obligations that were either manually created or surfaced within the agreement.
   - Agreement sets **pane:** Displays a list of sets to which the agreement has been added.
   - Related agreements **pane:** Displays a list of documents that belong to the same hierarchy as the selected agreement.

   Note: The CLM Attributes pane displays a list of CLM-specific attributes that are associated with the selected document.

   ![A snippet of the Agreement preview page that highlights the CLM Attributes icon in the pane on the left.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-Preview-page_CLM-Attributes-tab_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY1OTMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.gbvNuKu9YJ3xgo939Q_amfJ12P78Lej3E64bsF2iJ80&_LANG=enus)

   This pane is read only and is available for documents that were brought into Agreement Manager from Docusign CLM. Once surfaced in Agreement Manager, you can use these attributes to search and filter the agreement list table.

   **Availability:** This feature is only available to CLM customers who purchased an IAM Enterprise plan. Also, the integration between CLM and IAM must be enabled. See the [Docusign CLM Integration with the IAM Platform](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=cona491d69c-2859-4515-882c-db04db58d439.html&_LANG=enus "Read about Docusign CLM integration with the Intelligent Agreement Management (IAM) platform. CLM customers can extract more value from agreements and use Docusign Iris for important agreement insights.") article to learn more about this integration.
2. The Preview pane on the right displays an image of the full agreement.

The first time you access the Agreement preview page, an onboarding callout is displayed. This callout prompts you to review the data suggestions:

![A snippet of the Agreement preview page that shows the onboarding callout that displays when first-time users access the page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-preview-page_firsttime-onboarding-callout.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY1OTMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.gbvNuKu9YJ3xgo939Q_amfJ12P78Lej3E64bsF2iJ80&_LANG=enus)

Data review is integral to accurate reporting, forecasting, and agreement management.

Tip: Choose to download or share the agreement:![A snippet of the Agreement preview page that shows the Share option in the expanded dropdown.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-preview_Dropdown_Share.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY1OTMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.gbvNuKu9YJ3xgo939Q_amfJ12P78Lej3E64bsF2iJ80&_LANG=enus)

title: The Agreement Preview Page
text:
en=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY1OTMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.gbvNuKu9YJ3xgo939Q_amfJ12P78Lej3E64bsF2iJ80&_LANG=enus)

- Select Download from the top-right corner to download a PDF version of the selected document.
- Expand the dropdown and select Share to share the agreement with other users or groups.
- Select Activity Log to view agreement-level events for the agreement.

For contract managers and analysts, the Agreement preview page and the Details pane provide a look into the actual agreement.

Note: The Agreement type value determines which attributes are surfaced within the agreement. Only attributes that are relevant to the identified agreement type are surfaced and displayed in the Details pane. Review the [Agreement Type Management](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=uie1724446326038.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how agreement type management is being used to unify the way Docusign capitalizes on agreement types and associated fields. This topic answers the question: What is agreement type management and how does it benefit me?") topic to learn more about agreement types.

Key details are suggested based on the document contents. Review and validate the suggested values to find, manage, and report on your completed agreements. If an attribute is not listed in the Details pane, you can search for it in the document:

![A snippet of the Agreement preview page that shows an example search term that was found within the document.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-preview-page_Search-dialog-box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY1OTMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.gbvNuKu9YJ3xgo939Q_amfJ12P78Lej3E64bsF2iJ80&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Agreement preview page that shows an example search term that was found within the document.")

- Select the Search (magnifying glass) icon from the top-right corner of the preview pane. A dialog box opens.
- Enter a keyword or search string in the Find in Document field. If the value exists, the keyword or search string is highlighted in the agreement.
- Use the Next and Previous arrows to scroll through the results.

Tip: Select (click) an attribute to view the value within the context of the agreement. The agreement jumps to the location of the value and highlights it:![A snippet of the Agreement preview page that shows a selected attribute value that is highlighted in the agreement.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-preview-page_Review-and-edit-details_example-of-value-highlighted-in-doc.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY1OTMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.gbvNuKu9YJ3xgo939Q_amfJ12P78Lej3E64bsF2iJ80&_LANG=enus)

Note: ![An image of the spark icon is used to denote the use of AI.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/AI-Spark-icon.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY1OTMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.gbvNuKu9YJ3xgo939Q_amfJ12P78Lej3E64bsF2iJ80&_LANG=enus) The spark icon is used to denote the attribute values that were surfaced with AI. Review these extractions and confirm that the values are accurate.

Data reviews are completed to validate the attribute values that were surfaced within the agreement. This is an important aspect of Agreement Manager. Business decisions, negotiations, obligations, and renewals rely on the accuracy of these insights.

title: The Agreement Preview Page
text:
Document-level email notifications help you stay informed about upcoming milestones for individual documents. These notifications can be set from the Agreement preview page. Select the Set a notification (bell) icon in the top-right corner to access this function:![A snippet of the Agreement preview page that shows the Set a notification icon.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-preview_Manage-Set-a-notification_snippet.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY1OTMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.gbvNuKu9YJ3xgo939Q_amfJ12P78Lej3E64bsF2iJ80&_LANG=enus)When you select the bell icon, the Renewal Notice Date and Expiration Date notifications are enabled by default. Other notification types can be configured by using the + Add option.

Note: When you enable the notification, you are designated as the recipient and all frequency options are selected. Use the Edit link to update the frequency and recipients for the notifications as needed.

![A screenshot of the Notifications function that shows options for tracking agreement milestones.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Document-level-notifications_Notifications-window.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY1OTMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.gbvNuKu9YJ3xgo939Q_amfJ12P78Lej3E64bsF2iJ80&_LANG=enus)

- Renewal notice dates
- Expiration dates

Note: If a notification already exists for an agreement, the Manage notifications (bell) icon is displayed. Select this icon to update the notifications for the agreement.

See the [Set Document-Level Notifications](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tsk62370c10-0d3b-4218-a325-4383b9d5f7cd.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to set and manage notifications to track upcoming milestones for an individual document.") topic to learn more about setting and managing document-level notifications.

title: The Agreement Preview Page
text:
The attributes and field values that were manually created or surfaced with AI are displayed in the Details pane. When the Details pane is in *edit* mode, the attribute values can be updated, deleted, and verified.

![A screenshot of the Agreement preview page that highlights the Details pane that is in the edit mode.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-Preview-page_Review-and-edit-details-pane.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY1OTMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.gbvNuKu9YJ3xgo939Q_amfJ12P78Lej3E64bsF2iJ80&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Agreement preview page that highlights the Details pane that is in the edit mode.")

When you select either the Edit (pencil) icon or the Review All option, the attribute values become editable. Attributes are grouped based on their usage.

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| A snippet of the Details pane that shows there are four suggested attribute values in the General section that require review. | Review the suggested details for each group.   - A progress bar is displayed at the top of each group, including the number of suggestions that require review. - An image of the spark icon used to denote the use of AI. A spark icon is displayed next to the values that require validation. - An image of the checkmark indicator that signifies the suggestion has been reviewed. A Review completed (checkmark) icon displays once the value is verified to indicate that the attribute was reviewed. A purple checkmark is also displayed. - Groups with no suggested attribute values are collapsed by default. |

Note: The Party Role is surfaced during agreement processing. This extraction identifies the role that a specific party plays within the context of an agreement. Verify the surfaced Party Role value and if necessary, select a new role from the dropdown:

![A snippet of the Details pane that shows the Party Role dropdown and available roles.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-preview_edit-Details_Party-Roles-highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY1OTMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.gbvNuKu9YJ3xgo939Q_amfJ12P78Lej3E64bsF2iJ80&_LANG=enus)Once identified and validated, you can filter agreements to view the agreements where a selected party plays a specific role.

Important: Any changes made to the attribute values in the Details pane do *not* change the content of the actual document.

During a data review, you can:

- Accept the suggested attribute value.
- Delete the suggested value and enter a new attribute value.
- Enter a custom value if the suggested value is not an exact match (applies to attributes with dropdowns).
- View the suggested value within the agreement to validate it against the agreement. Select (click) an attribute to view the value in the context of the agreement. The agreement jumps to the location of the value and highlights it.

When you accept a value, that value becomes saved as the attribute value and the spark no longer displays.

Important: When you place the Details pane in *edit* mode, the ENVELOPE ID field no longer displays in the pane. You cannot update the ENVELOPE ID field for an agreement.

title: The Agreement Preview Page
text:
When organizations are first onboarded to Agreement Manager, their historical documents are ingested and processed. During processing, attributes and field values are surfaced based on the current AI model. By default, when a new AI model is available, the previously ingested and uploaded agreements are *not* reprocessed for the new insights.

When users interact with insights that were released since their initial onboarding, they are prompted to update their AI model. This messaging also displayed in the Details pane on the Agreement preview page:

![A snippet of the Agreement preview page that highlights the message about the latest AI model.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reprocessing_Agreement-preview_AI-update-available_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY1OTMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.gbvNuKu9YJ3xgo939Q_amfJ12P78Lej3E64bsF2iJ80&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Agreement preview page that highlights the message about the latest AI model.")Use the Update Details option to update your AI model, including processing outdated agreements for new attributes and insights. See the [Agreement Manager Reprocessing Experience](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=con44590246-0f0e-43b4-9692-caadb57335ac.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about the Agreement Manager reprocessing experience, including its purpose, trigger points, and job size considerations.") topic to learn more about the reprocessing feature.

title: The Agreement Preview Page
heading_path: Saving changes to the Details pane
text:
Whether you enter a value or accept the suggested value, be sure to save the Details pane before leaving the page. The Save option becomes active after you add a value or accept a suggested value.

The spark icon displays until a user validates the corresponding value:

- Select the Accept AI Suggestion checkmark and Save your changes to validate the attribute value.
- Update the attribute value manually and Save your changes.

  The entered value is now the verified attribute value - as indicated with a ![An image of the checkmark indicator that signifies the suggestion has been reviewed.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-preview-page_Details-pane_suggestion-reviewed-checkmark.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY1OTMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.gbvNuKu9YJ3xgo939Q_amfJ12P78Lej3E64bsF2iJ80&_LANG=enus) Review completed (checkmark) icon and a purple checkmark.

The document remains in the "review queue" until all the suggested field values have been accepted or updated. Once you Save your changes, the Details pane is reverted back to read-only mode.

title: The Agreement Preview Page
text:
When preparing agreements for an envelope or template, the preparer can add these agreement detail fields:

- Automatic Renewal (checkbox)
- Contract Value
- Effective Date
- Expiration Date
- Renewal Notice Period
- Renewal Term

By adding these fields and entering their values, the preparer simplifies the review process for the completed agreement and ensures greater accuracy. These values display in the Details pane in Agreement Manager for the completed document. Once in Agreement Manager, these attribute values are used for contract analytics, forecasting, renewals management, and reporting purposes.

See these topics to learn more about adding these fields to your eSignature envelopes and templates:

- [Agreement Details for Docusign Agreement Manager](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gbo1643332197980&topicId=eab1715968407258.html)
- [Add Agreement Detail Fields To Specify Values for Agreement Manager﻿](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gbo1643332197980&topicId=wds1725500036394.html)
- [Map Template Fields to Agreement Details﻿](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gbo1643332197980&topicId=vro1725500093108.html)

title: The Agreement Preview Page
text:
Obligations, such as payments, renewal notices, and termination notices are surfaced in agreements with AI. These AI-assisted insights are displayed in the Obligations pane on the Agreement preview page.

![A screenshot of the Agreement preview page that highlights the Obligations pane.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Navigator_Agreement-Preview_Obligations-pane-highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY1OTMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.gbvNuKu9YJ3xgo939Q_amfJ12P78Lej3E64bsF2iJ80&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Agreement preview page that highlights the Obligations pane.")

Note: Users can manually capture and define obligations from the Agreement preview page.

Obligations are surfaced in English (U.S.) agreements only. The AI model that processes obligations is run from a U.S. server and can only process English agreements in this region.

If your language and region are not supported, manually create the obligations to include this information in dashboards, reports, and other insights.

Review the [Obligation Management](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=csm1727128259486.html&_LANG=enus "Read about obligation management, including how to use this functionality to capture, track, and fulfill your agreement obligations. This topic answers the question: How can I find and track my agreement obligations?") topics to learn more about creating, managing, and tracking obligations.

title: The Agreement Preview Page
text:
The Agreement sets pane displays the agreement sets to which the agreement was added. Contract managers can add agreements to existing sets or create new agreement sets from this pane.

![A snippet of the Agreement preview page that highlights the Agreement sets pane, including available actions.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-Preview-page_Agreement-sets-pane.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY1OTMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.gbvNuKu9YJ3xgo939Q_amfJ12P78Lej3E64bsF2iJ80&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Agreement preview page that highlights the Agreement sets pane, including available actions.")

Review the [Agreement Set Management](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=zed1730843285476.html&_LANG=enus "Read about agreement set management including how to use sets to control access to agreements. This topic answers the question: What are agreement sets and how can I use them?") topics to learn more about creating, sharing, and managing agreement sets.

Note: You must have the Organize and manage all agreements permission to create and manage agreement sets. However, an agreement set can be shared with any user or group, regardless of their assigned permissions.

title: The Agreement Preview Page
text:
The CLM Attributes pane displays a list of CLM-specific attributes that are associated with the selected document. This option is only available to documents that were brought into Agreement Manager from Docusign CLM.

Attention: This feature is only available to CLM customers who purchased an IAM Enterprise plan. Also, the integration between CLM and IAM must be enabled. See the [Docusign CLM Integration with the IAM Platform](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=cona491d69c-2859-4515-882c-db04db58d439.html&_LANG=enus "Read about Docusign CLM integration with the Intelligent Agreement Management (IAM) platform. CLM customers can extract more value from agreements and use Docusign Iris for important agreement insights.") article to learn more about this integration.
