Review the list of attributes as needed. This pane is read only. If necessary, use the Edit in CLM option to update the attribute values for your document.

![A snippet of the Agreement preview page that highlights the CLM Attributes pane.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-Preview-page_CLM-Attributes-pane.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY1OTMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.gbvNuKu9YJ3xgo939Q_amfJ12P78Lej3E64bsF2iJ80&_LANG=enus)

See the [Add Attribute Values to a Document in Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=yeo1576610114693.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to add attribute values to a document in Docusign CLM.") topic to learn more about working with CLM attributes.

title: The Agreement Preview Page
text:
The Related agreements pane displays a list of documents that belong to the same hierarchy as the selected agreement.

Users can add documents to existing hierarchies or create new ones, however, they must have Can Edit access to the underlying agreements. Hierarchies are managed from the party hub pages.![A screenshot of the Agreement preview page that highlights the Related agreements pane.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-Preview-page_Related-agreements_Fontara-example.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY1OTMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.gbvNuKu9YJ3xgo939Q_amfJ12P78Lej3E64bsF2iJ80&_LANG=enus)

Review the [Agreement Hierarchies](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=con1b654754-bd6d-4fbd-ac0c-8c44a9b1d666.html&_LANG=enus "Read about agreement hierarchies, including their purpose, use cases, entry points, and common tasks.") topic to learn more about creating and maintaining agreement hierarchies.

title: Locate Agreements by Asking Questions
text:
Learn how to locate data about your agreements by entering a question in the search field.

With natural language (NL) queries, you can enter questions or key phrases directly into the search field on the Agreement Manager homepage. The query returns a direct answer and a list of relevant agreements.

Important: Depending on the nature of the question posed, not all queries yield both direct answers and a list of agreements. Some queries may only provide a list of relevant agreements.

Note: The NL search function is currently available in beta. See
the *Opt-in for NL search (Join Beta)* section of the [Natural Language Search Queries](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=fas1739987568042.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how natural language search queries, including components of the Search Results view.") topic to join the beta.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the pane on the left.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed. A list of documents to which the logged-in user has access is displayed in the table. The total document count is displayed the bottom right. This count is updated when you perform a search or apply filters.
4. Enter a question or key phrase in the search field and press the Enter key or select an option from the dropdown list:

   ![A snippet of the Agreement Manager homepage that shows an example search phrase to locate the value of master service agreements (MSAs) that auto renew this year.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/NL-Search_Example-key-phrase-with-dropdown.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY0MjAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.r1E5Hy0nWIhbXtRm_fjK98Uxbg_HEIVT_y3VsioUrMc&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Agreement Manager homepage that shows an example search phrase to locate the value of master service agreements (MSAs) that auto renew this year.")

   Tip: Here are some sample questions that you can enter into the search field:
   - Do I have an amendment with [Party X]?
   - Do I have an active NDA with [Party X]?
   - Which MSAs will auto-renew?
   - Which agreements do not have a governing law?

   The query returns an answer to the question and a list of
   relevant agreements. The document count is updated
   based on your search query.
5. Review the generated answer:

   ![A snippet of the search results view that shows the detected filters and a direct answer to the query question.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/NL-Search_Review-answer-to-question.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY0MjAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.r1E5Hy0nWIhbXtRm_fjK98Uxbg_HEIVT_y3VsioUrMc&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the search results view that shows the detected filters and a direct answer to the query question.")

   1. If filters were applied while answering the question, the Filters applied field displays the relevant filters. In this example, a number of filters were applied:

      - Expiration Date: >2025-01-01 AND <2026-01-01
      - Agreement Type: Master Service Agreement
      - Renewal Type: Auto Renew
      - Currency Code: USD

title: Locate Agreements by Asking Questions
text:
le, a number of filters were applied:

      - Expiration Date: >2025-01-01 AND <2026-01-01
      - Agreement Type: Master Service Agreement
      - Renewal Type: Auto Renew
      - Currency Code: USD

      Note: Select a filter to view the selected values. For example: ![A screenshot of the Expiration Date filter that is expanded to show the selected values.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/NL-Search_Detected-filter_Selected-expanded.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY0MjAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.r1E5Hy0nWIhbXtRm_fjK98Uxbg_HEIVT_y3VsioUrMc&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Expiration Date filter that is expanded to show the selected values.")

      Restriction: The NL search feature is restricted to agreements where the detected Language is *English* and the Currency Code is *USD*.
   2. The answer is generated based on the data points that were surfaced in your agreements and may require further review.
   3. Use the Copy this answer icon to copy the answer to your clipboard for use in another application.
   4. Provide feedback about the answer. Use the
      Rate as helpful (thumbs up) and
      Rate as unhelpful (thumbs down)
      icons to rate the quality of the generated
      answer.
   5. Select the Use keyword search instead option to perform a regular search if the query did not answer your question.
6. Review the agreement list table to see the agreements that were returned in the search.

   Tip: If you routinely search for the same type of agreements or agreement data, save your search criteria for future use. See the [Save Search Criteria for Future Use](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tskc38121a1-bbf8-4da8-8ed7-b364991f7e50.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to save the search criteria for the queries that you frequently perform.") topic to learn more about the saved search feature.
7. Download selected agreements or export agreement data to a CSV file:

   ![A screenshot of the search results view that highlights selected agreements and the download and export options.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/NL-Search_Example-key-phrase_download-export-search-results.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY0MjAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.r1E5Hy0nWIhbXtRm_fjK98Uxbg_HEIVT_y3VsioUrMc&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the search results view that highlights selected agreements and the download and export options.")

   1. Select the checkboxes associated with the agreements that you want to download or export. You can also select the Choose all checkbox from the header row to select all the agreements on the page.

      The Download Documents and Export to CSV icons are displayed.
   2. Select the Download Documents (arrow) icon.

      The selected agreements are downloaded to your local Downloads folder.
   3. Select the Export to CSV icon to export agreement data for the selected files. All the available attributes and field values for the selected agreements are exported to a CSV file.

      The file is downloaded to your local Downloads folder.
8. Reset the search field to perform a new query. Select the Clear (x) icon located in the search field.

   The search field resets and all the agreements to which the logged-in user has access are displayed.

   Important: The natural language search does not support the use of filters together with questions. If you apply filters to the agreement list table, any natural language question entered in the search box is treated as a standard keyword search.

You have successfully located agreements and agreement data by asking questions.

title: Update the Agreement Type for an Agreement
heading_path: Update the Agreement Type for an Agreement
text:
Learn how to update the agreement type value for an agreement.

When documents are synced or imported to Agreement Manager, the agreement type is identified and the associated fields are surfaced. Although rare, you can update the agreement type. For example, changing a "miscellaneous" agreement to a custom agreement type.

Note: When updating the agreement type, the agreement is processed again to surface content that is relevant to the selected type.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed. A list of documents to which the logged-in user has access is displayed in the table.

   Tip: The total document count is displayed the bottom-right corner of the view. This count is updated when you perform a search or apply filters.
4. Locate the agreement that you want to update.

   ![A snippet of the Completed Documents view that highlights the search field and available filters.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Navigator_Completed-documents_search-field-and-filters-highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY2MzgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.z1Ara0eKCa6c4pSxYHbxzYqYoe8XtP4RR-Z8FFtY6wA&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Completed Documents view that highlights the search field and available filters.")

   1. Enter a keyword or search string in the search field and select Enter.
   2. Use the Filters option to refine the list of search results.

      Select the Filters option and locate the filter that you want to apply. Expand a filter, select or enter a value, and select Apply.

      The agreements that match your search criteria and applied filters are displayed in the table.
5. Select (click) the agreement that you want to update.

   The Agreement preview page opens for the selected agreement. The Details pane is displayed by default and shows a list of attributes and suggested values. These attributes and values are displayed based on the identified agreement type.

   A summary displays at the top of the Details pane. The summary includes the identified agreement type and the number of fields and suggestions to review:

   ![A snippet of the Details pane that shows the identified agreement type and the number of attribute fields and suggestions that require review.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-Preview-page_Details-pane_summary-statement-snippet.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY2MzgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.z1Ara0eKCa6c4pSxYHbxzYqYoe8XtP4RR-Z8FFtY6wA&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Details pane that shows the identified agreement type and the number of attribute fields and suggestions that require review.")

   Tip: Use the Find details search field to locate specific attributes or values.
6. Place the Details pane into *edit* mode.
   - Select the Edit (pencil) icon.
   - Select the Review All option.

   Note: You must have Organize and manage all agreements access to perform data reviews. For all other users, the Edit (pencil) icon and the Review All option are grayed out.

   The Review & edit details pane opens for updates.

   Important: When you accept or edit the suggested attribute values, the content in the agreement does not change.
7. Update the Agreement type for the document.

title: Update the Agreement Type for an Agreement
heading_path: Update the Agreement Type for an Agreement
text:
edit details pane opens for updates.

   Important: When you accept or edit the suggested attribute values, the content in the agreement does not change.
7. Update the Agreement type for the document.

   ![A snippet of the Review & edit details pane that highlights a selected value in the Agreement type dropdown.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-Preview-page_Details-pane_Update-Agreement-type.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY2MzgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.z1Ara0eKCa6c4pSxYHbxzYqYoe8XtP4RR-Z8FFtY6wA&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Review & edit details pane that highlights a selected value in the Agreement type dropdown.")

   1. Expand the Agreement type dropdown.

      The suggested value is highlighted in the preview pane and a dropdown list of available types displays.
   2. Search for and select the relevant agreement type from the list.

   The Change agreement type dialog box opens.

   ![A screenshot of the Change agreement type dialog box that states the agreement will be processed to surface content specific to the type.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-Preview-page_Details-pane_Change-agreement-type-dialog-box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY2MzgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.z1Ara0eKCa6c4pSxYHbxzYqYoe8XtP4RR-Z8FFtY6wA&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Change agreement type dialog box that states the agreement will be processed to surface content specific to the type.")
8. Review the message and select Change.

   The agreement is processed to surface content that is specific to the selected type. Review
   these topics to learn more about agreement types and AI processing:
   - [IAM Agreement Type and Field Management](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=uie1724446326038.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how agreement type management is being used to unify the way Docusign capitalizes on agreement types and associated fields. This topic answers the question: What is agreement type management and how does it benefit me?")
   - [Processing Agreement Manager Agreements](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=rvw1736364838500.html&_LANG=enus "Read about agreement processing and how to opt in for additional processing to surface new insights within your agreements.")

   The dialog box closes. The agreement type was successfully updated and the document is being processed. The Details pane is reverted to read-only mode.
9. Select the Close (X) icon from the top left.

   You are returned to the Completed Documents view. Your search criteria and filters are still applied.

You have successfully updated the agreement type value for a selected document.

title: Subscribe to Renewal Reports
text:
Learn how to set up one-time or recurring email reports for yourself or others to receive notifications about your renewals.

You can subscribe to receive one-time or recurring email reports for any of the charts on the Renewals dashboard.

Tip: Notification settings give you control over the email notifications that you receive. By default, all the notification types related to the completed agreements in your account are selected, or enabled. Deselect a checkbox if you no longer want to receive email notifications for the corresponding notification type.

See the [Customize Notification Settings for Your Completed Agreements](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tsk155b1ed7-7b45-43cd-be92-2a5279630c0f.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to customize your email notification settings from the Notification Preferences page.") article to learn more about updating your notification settings. Be sure to periodically check the Notification Preferences page as new notification types will be added in the future.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Insights from the global header.

   The Insights hub opens and the Overview is displayed.
3. Select Renewals from the navigation pane on the left.

   The Renewals dashboard view opens.

   ![A screenshot of the Renewals dashboard that shows the agreement charts and filter options.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Renewals-dashboard_No-navigation-pane.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcyNzUsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.Ny3mhhY5SFPmcsfMzzTM9A-0ltJn1vYXxpO7NPjruR0&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Renewals dashboard that shows the agreement charts and filter options.")
4. Filter the Renewals dashboard to the relevant renewal information:

   ![A snippet of the Renewals dashboard that highlights the dashboard filters.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Renewals-dashboard_Filter-pills.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcyNzUsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.Ny3mhhY5SFPmcsfMzzTM9A-0ltJn1vYXxpO7NPjruR0&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Renewals dashboard that highlights the dashboard filters.")

   1. Expand the filter that you want to apply.
   2. Select or enter the filter value.
   3. Select the Apply option.

   Important: When using the Parties filter, you must also select a Party Role value. If you do not know which role to select, choose the All option. If no role is selected, the search returns agreements where the party is not the buyer or the seller.

   Tip: Use the Filters option to access additional filters:![A screenshot of the Filters dialog box that shows additional filters that you can apply to the agreement list.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-documents-table_All-Filters-dialog-box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcyNzUsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.Ny3mhhY5SFPmcsfMzzTM9A-0ltJn1vYXxpO7NPjruR0&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Filters dialog box that shows additional filters that you can apply to the agreement list.")

   The charts are updated to reflect the filters that you applied.
5. Go to the detailed report view for the dashboard chart to which you want to subscribe:

title: Subscribe to Renewal Reports
text:
hat you can apply to the agreement list.")

   The charts are updated to reflect the filters that you applied.
5. Go to the detailed report view for the dashboard chart to which you want to subscribe:

   ![A snippet of the Renewals dashboard that highlights the View Report option in the More actions (three dots) menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Renewals-dashboard_Chart_Actions_View-Report.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcyNzUsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.Ny3mhhY5SFPmcsfMzzTM9A-0ltJn1vYXxpO7NPjruR0&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Renewals dashboard that highlights the View Report option in the More actions (three dots) menu.")

   - Select the chart heading.
   - Hover the cursor over the chart, expand the More actions (three dots) menu and select the View Report option.

   The detailed report view opens for the selected chart. The agreements represented in the chart are listed in the table.
6. Filter the table to refine the results list as needed or select Reset to clear existing filters.
7. Customize the information that is displayed in the table, if necessary:

   ![A screenshot of the Show or Hide FIeldsShow or Hide Fields dialog box that shows the columns you can choose to display in the agreements table.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Party-hub_Overview-dashboard_Customize-columns.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcyNzUsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.Ny3mhhY5SFPmcsfMzzTM9A-0ltJn1vYXxpO7NPjruR0&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Show or Hide FIeldsShow or Hide Fields dialog box that shows the columns you can choose to display in the agreements table.")

   Note: If you made changes to the report, expand the More actions (three dots) menu and select the Save option:![A snippet of an agreement dashboard report that shows the Save option in the More actions (three dots) menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Agreements-Dashboard_Dashboard-report_Save-as.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcyNzUsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.Ny3mhhY5SFPmcsfMzzTM9A-0ltJn1vYXxpO7NPjruR0&_LANG=enus "A snippet of an agreement dashboard report that shows the Save option in the More actions (three dots) menu.")

   Once saved, access the report from the Custom tab located under the Reports view. Continue to the next step to subscribe to your custom report.

   The report view updates and the applied filters and customizations are reflected in the chart and agreement list table. Next, subscribe to receive email reports.
8. Select the Manage Notification (bell) icon from the top-right corner.

   The Create Subscription dialog box opens.

   ![A snippet of the Renewals by type report that shows the Manage Notification (bell) icon and the Create subscription dialog box.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Renewals-dashboard_Report-view_Subscribe-to-report.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcyNzUsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.Ny3mhhY5SFPmcsfMzzTM9A-0ltJn1vYXxpO7NPjruR0&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Renewals by type report that shows the Manage Notification (bell) icon and the Create subscription dialog box.")
9. Complete the fields in the Create subscription dialog box and select Schedule:
   1. Enter a descriptive name in the Subscription Name field. This field defaults to the name of the report.
   2. Indicate how often and when you want to receive the report notification. Choose from Daily, Weekly, Monthly, and Once.

title: Subscribe to Renewal Reports
text:
the Subscription Name field. This field defaults to the name of the report.
   2. Indicate how often and when you want to receive the report notification. Choose from Daily, Weekly, Monthly, and Once.

      Depending on the option you selected, you may be prompted to choose a send day.
   3. If necessary, turn on the Choose start and end date toggle and enter a Start date and End date.
   4. The notification is sent to you by default. Enter additional recipients in the Add a user or group field as needed.

      Note: When you create a subscription, you have the option to add individual users and groups. Once created, the subscription is displayed in the Manage report subscriptions dialog box for any individual users who were added. Currently, the subscription is not visible to users if they were added as part of a group.
   5. Select the Attach to email as CSV option as needed.
   6. Select Schedule.

   The dialog box closes. You have successfully signed up to receive report notifications.

title: Subscribe to Renewal Reports
text:
Once created, you can manage the existing subscriptions for a selected report. Go to the report view for the subscription that you want to update and select the Manage Notification (bell) icon:

![A snippet of the detailed report view for a dashboard chart that highlights the editing options for an existing subscription.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Renewals_Renewals_Dashboard_Manage_Notification.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcyNzUsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.Ny3mhhY5SFPmcsfMzzTM9A-0ltJn1vYXxpO7NPjruR0&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the detailed report view for a dashboard chart that highlights the editing options for an existing subscription.")

Use the Manage Notification option to manage any existing subscriptions for the selected report, such as:

- Edit subscription details, including the subscription name, frequency, and recipient list.
- Delete an existing subscription.

You can also use the Create New option to create a new subscription for the selected report.

title: Create Custom Obligation Types
text:
Learn how to create custom obligation types to support organizational and departmental use cases.

Docusign provides a set of standard obligation types, however, you can create custom types to more accurately categorize your obligations.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed.
4. Expand the Settings (gear) menu from the right-side of the page and select the Obligations option:

   ![A screenshot of the Settings (gear) menu that highlights the Obligation option in the expanded](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Obligation-type-management_Manage-Obligation-Types_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcxOTEsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.W6GNA4Es2x1MbkGIrUaJqzYpTh9lTaj2gsiE02xobB8&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Settings (gear) menu that highlights the Obligation option in the expanded")

   The Manage Obligation Types page opens. The Obligation Types view is displayed. A list of custom and standard obligation types is displayed in the table.
5. Select the + New Obligation Type option from the top right.

   ![A screenshot of the Obligation Types view that shows the list of available standard and custom obligation types for the account.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Manage-Obligation-Types_New-Obligation-Type.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcxOTEsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.W6GNA4Es2x1MbkGIrUaJqzYpTh9lTaj2gsiE02xobB8&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Obligation Types view that shows the list of available standard and custom obligation types for the account.")

   The New obligation type dialog box opens.

   ![A screenshot of the New obligation type dialog box.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Manage-Obligation-Types_New-obligation-type-dialog-box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcxOTEsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.W6GNA4Es2x1MbkGIrUaJqzYpTh9lTaj2gsiE02xobB8&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the New obligation type dialog box.")
6. Enter a name for the obligation type in the Name field. The name cannot exceed 50 characters.
7. Select the Save option.

   The dialog box closes. The new obligation type is displayed in the Obligation Types table.

   Tip: The CREATED BY column displays "user" for custom obligation types and "Docusign" for standard types.
8. Continue to create new obligation types as needed.
9. Return to the Agreement Manager homepage. Select the Close (x) icon from the top right.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed. The search criteria and filters are still applied.

You have successfully created a new obligation type.

title: View Agreements in a Set
text:
Learn how to view the agreements in an agreement set.

When an agreement set is shared with a user or group, they have access to all the agreements in the set. There are two primary ways to view the agreements in a set:

- Filter the agreement list table on the Completed Documents page to display the agreements in a selected set.
- Using the Open in New Tab option from the Manage sets option.

  Note: This option is only available to users that are assigned the Organize and manage all agreements permission. For example, this option is not applicable to non-contract managers.

This topic reviews both ways to view agreements in an agreement set.

**View set agreements from the Completed Documents page:**

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed.
4. Apply filters to locate the relevant set:

   ![A snippet of the Completed Documents page that displays the expanded Sets filter.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-sets_entry-point_Sets-filter.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTczNzQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.JQeMKCNpRUlgxADYu3FgSC9txb_Tq2VljfFPfxOeLE8&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Completed Documents page that displays the expanded Sets filter.")

   1. Select the Filters option. The menu expands and a list of applied and suggested filters is displayed.
   2. Select the Sets filter from the list of Suggested filters.
   3. Select a set from the list.
   4. Select the Apply option.

   The filter closes and the agreements contained in the set are displayed in the table.

**View set agreements from the "Sets" option:**

5. Expand the Settings (gear) menu in the top right of the Agreement Manager homepage and select the Sets option:

   ![A screenshot of the Settings (gear) menu on the Agreement Manager homepage that highlights the Sets option.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-sets_entry-point_Manage-sets-action-menu.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTczNzQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.JQeMKCNpRUlgxADYu3FgSC9txb_Tq2VljfFPfxOeLE8&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Settings (gear) menu on the Agreement Manager homepage that highlights the Sets option.")

   The Agreement sets page opens. The All agreement sets view is displayed. The table displays sets that were shared with or created by the logged-in user.
6. Locate the set that you want to view, expand the More actions (three dots) menu, and select Open in new tab:

   ![A snippet of the All agreement sets view that highlights the Open in new tab option in the More actions menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Manage-Sets-dialog_All-agreement-sets_Open-in-New-Tab.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTczNzQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.JQeMKCNpRUlgxADYu3FgSC9txb_Tq2VljfFPfxOeLE8&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the All agreement sets view that highlights the Open in new tab option in the More actions menu.")

   The Completed Documents page opens in a new browser tab. The agreements contained within the selected set are displayed in the table.

title: View Agreements in a Set
text:
hlights the Open in new tab option in the More actions menu.")

   The Completed Documents page opens in a new browser tab. The agreements contained within the selected set are displayed in the table.

   ![A snippet of the Completed Documents page that displays the agreements contained within a selected set.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-documents-table_filtered-by-set_view-agreements.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTczNzQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.JQeMKCNpRUlgxADYu3FgSC9txb_Tq2VljfFPfxOeLE8&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Completed Documents page that displays the agreements contained within a selected set.")

You have successfully viewed the agreements in a selected agreement set.

title: Administrator: View and Manage Approved Apps
heading_path: Administrator: View and Manage Approved Apps
text:
As a Docusign App Center administrator, learn how to access, view, and manage Docusign approved apps.

As a Docusign App Center administrator, you can access, view, and manage Docusign approved apps.

1. From the  [Docusign App Center page](https://apps.docusign.com/app-center), select Extension Apps > Approved Apps.

   Tip: You can alternately select Manage > Approved Apps.

   The List of approved apps section shows apps that are available for your organization's users.

   ![A screenshot of the App Center Approved Apps page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/ac-newest-approved-pg.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTgyMzYsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.9eC09A3avE-9WXGr2Kbolp1KbexTm_zQvstardg5qnY&_LANG=enus)
2. For apps that are approved but not installed, select  Install App to install the app for all account users.
3. Use the overflow menu to manage your approved apps.

   | Function | Select to: |
   | --- | --- |
   | View Details | Display the [App Detail page](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=xkg1702568563800.html&_LANG=enus "Use a Docusign app's detail page to view important information such as an app summary, features, permissions, and resources.") for detailed information about the app. |
   | Remove App | Remove from the  **List of approved apps**. Also removes the ability to install the app. Note: Existing users can continue to use the app. To restrict all access to an app, uninstall the app. |
   | Uninstall | Uninstall the app from App Center and remove the app for all users. |

title: Administrator: View and Manage Approved Apps
heading_path: Administrator: View and Manage Approved Apps
text:
You can add apps to an account’s approved apps list from the Select Apps to Approve  section.

1. From the  [Docusign App Center page](https://apps.docusign.com/app-center), select Manage > Approved Apps.

   Note: You can optionally access the Approved Apps page by selecting Extension Apps > Approved Apps.
2. From the Select apps to approve  section, select an app using one of these methods:
   - Select Approve for the app that you want.
   - Search for the app by typing part of the app's name. When the app displays, select Approve.

     ![A screenshot of searching for an app to approve.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/ac-approved-apps-adm-sel-search.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTgyMzYsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.9eC09A3avE-9WXGr2Kbolp1KbexTm_zQvstardg5qnY&_LANG=enus)
   - Select one, multiple, or all available apps and choose Add to Approved List.

     ![A screenshot of an App Center approved apps selection example.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/ac-new-sel-apps-to-approve.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTgyMzYsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.9eC09A3avE-9WXGr2Kbolp1KbexTm_zQvstardg5qnY&_LANG=enus)

     The apps move to the List of approved apps.
3. In the overflow menu, select Views Details  to display the [app's Detail page](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=xkg1702568563800.html&_LANG=enus "Use a Docusign app's detail page to view important information such as an app summary, features, permissions, and resources.")  for detailed information.

title: Administrator: View and Manage Approved Apps
heading_path: Administrator: View and Manage Approved Apps
text:
Install an approved app to provide access to account users.

1. From the [Docusign App Center page](https://apps.docusign.com/app-center), select Manage > Approved Apps.

   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/ac-approve-install-tag.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTgyMzYsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.9eC09A3avE-9WXGr2Kbolp1KbexTm_zQvstardg5qnY&_LANG=enus)
2. Select Install App.

   The Approved  tag changes to Installed and the app is ready for account user access.

title: Administrator: View and Manage Approved Apps
heading_path: Administrator: View and Manage Approved Apps
text:
You can remove an app from the approved apps listing.

1. From the [Docusign App Center page](https://apps.docusign.com/app-center), select Manage > Approved Apps.

   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/ac-approve-install-tag.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTgyMzYsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.9eC09A3avE-9WXGr2Kbolp1KbexTm_zQvstardg5qnY&_LANG=enus)
2. Select the overflow menu and select Remove app.
3. At the confirmation dialog box, select Remove App  to move the app to the Select Apps to approve list.

   ![A screenshot of the Remove app from approved list dialog box.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/ac-remove-app-db.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTgyMzYsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.9eC09A3avE-9WXGr2Kbolp1KbexTm_zQvstardg5qnY&_LANG=enus)

   The app is no longer available for account users.

title: Test the Extraction Accuracy for Your Custom Agreement Types
heading_path: Test the Extraction Accuracy for Your Custom Agreement Types
text: Learn how to test the accuracy of the AI detection for your custom agreement types.

title: Test the Extraction Accuracy for Your Custom Agreement Types
heading_path: Test the Extraction Accuracy for Your Custom Agreement Types
text:
This topic reviews the next step of the AI detection set-up process, *Test for accuracy*. The steps in this task are completed by administrators and contract managers.

Remember: Administrators automatically have access to configure agreement data. For non-administrator users, agreement type management tasks require Organize and manage all agreements access to Agreement Manager agreements and functionality.

Complete these topics before attempting this task:

- [Configure New Custom Agreement Types](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=gke1727282238086.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to create custom agreement types to support organizational use cases.")
- [Define Your Custom Agreement Types](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tske5fe1ad5-2832-4e6c-9177-f33a9ffade15.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to initiate the AI detection set-up process and complete the first step to define the custom type.")
- [Add Examples of Your Custom Agreement Types](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tskd38465c6-c8c0-4588-bfb3-fdb6916a1926.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to complete the second step of the AI detection set-up process, Add examples of this agreement type.")

Before you activate the AI detection for custom extractions, the setup wizard prompts you to test for accuracy. This step is optional, however, it is highly recommended. The results are a good indicator of the quality and accuracy that you can expect from the AI detection before activating it.

In the previous step, you selected documents that exemplify the custom agreement type. After you selected the Save Examples option, the AI detection set-up wizard advanced to the next step.

You are prompted to test the accuracy of the AI detection:

![A screenshot of the dialog box that states that AI detection is now available for your custom agreement type.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-Types-Manager_AI-detect-setup_Test-for-accuracy_dialog-box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2MDMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.h5uUIBxzOpW79oGDCHVEgK2Dr-un4K32fiNgfa_emUw&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the dialog box that states that AI detection is now available for your custom agreement type.")The Test for accuracy option is selected by default. Use this option to review how well the AI detection can find the agreement type in your documents.

1. Your AI detection is now available for the custom agreement type. Before
   activating the AI detection, Docusign recommends that you test it for accuracy.
   Select the Confirm option.

   Tip: Choose a broader set of documents to test
   whether the system can correctly recognize and differentiate similar
   agreement types. For example, can the system correctly identify a
   *non-short-term lease* while testing the AI detection of the
   *short-term lease* type? Did the system classify the documents as
   the same type, *short-term lease*?

   The Select relevant documents page opens. Choose
   documents to confirm if the AI detection can accurately locate the agreement
   type.
2. Locate the documents that you want to use to test the AI detection accuracy:

   ![A snippet of the Select relevant documents page that shows the search field and filter options to locate agreements for testing.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-Types-Manager_AI-detect-setup_Locate-documents_search-filter-options.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2MDMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.h5uUIBxzOpW79oGDCHVEgK2Dr-un4K32fiNgfa_emUw&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Select relevant documents page that shows the search field and filter options to locate agreements for testing.")

title: Test the Extraction Accuracy for Your Custom Agreement Types
heading_path: Test the Extraction Accuracy for Your Custom Agreement Types
text:
0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.h5uUIBxzOpW79oGDCHVEgK2Dr-un4K32fiNgfa_emUw&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Select relevant documents page that shows the search field and filter options to locate agreements for testing.")

   1. Search for the relevant agreements. Enter a keyword or search string in the search field. The dropdown opens and search suggestions are displayed. Select a search suggestion or press the Enter key.
   2. Filter the agreement list table to find the relevant agreements. For example, filter by Sets, Status, Parties, Agreement Type, or AI Suggestions.

      Tip: Use the All Filters option to apply additional filters to the results table.

      Expand the filter, choose the relevant filter value, and select the Apply option.

   The results table updates and the documents that match the search criteria and applied filters are displayed.
3. Choose the documents that you want to use in the test.
4. Select the Start Testing option in the bottom right.

   The Agreement preview opens for the first document. The pane on the left shows the agreement type that the AI detection identified in the document.

   ![A screenshot of the Agreement preview page that highlights the agreement type that the AI detection identified for the document.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Manage-Agreement-Type_Test-for-accuracy_Confirm-accuracy-of-identified-types-in-selected-documents.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2MDMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.h5uUIBxzOpW79oGDCHVEgK2Dr-un4K32fiNgfa_emUw&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Agreement preview page that highlights the agreement type that the AI detection identified for the document.")
5. Review the identified agreement type in the context of the document. Verify that the AI detection surfaced the correct value.
   - Select Yes if the AI detection identified the correct agreement type.
   - Select No if the AI detection did not identify the correct agreement type.

   Note: If a different agreement type is identified, a message is displayed:
   ![A screenshot of the message that displays when a different agreement type is identified.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Manage-Agreement-Type_Test-for-accuracy_Identified-other-type.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2MDMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.h5uUIBxzOpW79oGDCHVEgK2Dr-un4K32fiNgfa_emUw&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the message that displays when a different agreement type is identified.")

   Review the document and locate the type. Select
   Yes or No to verify
   whether or not the AI detection was accurate.

   The Agreement preview page updates and the next document is displayed. Repeat this step to verify the identified agreement type in the remaining documents.
6. Select the Done option at the bottom of the pane. This option becomes active only after all the identified agreement types have been verified.

   The Results dialog box opens.

   ![A screenshot of the Results dialog box that shows the results of the accuracy test.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Manage-Agreement-Type_Test-for-accuracy_Results.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2MDMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.h5uUIBxzOpW79oGDCHVEgK2Dr-un4K32fiNgfa_emUw&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Results dialog box that shows the results of the accuracy test.")

   Important: If the results do not meet your accuracy requirements, use the Refine definition and examples option to refine your definition and add more examples. Retest the AI detection. Continue refining the definition and examples until the AI detection achieves the expected results.

title: Test the Extraction Accuracy for Your Custom Agreement Types
heading_path: Test the Extraction Accuracy for Your Custom Agreement Types
text:
nition and examples option to refine your definition and add more examples. Retest the AI detection. Continue refining the definition and examples until the AI detection achieves the expected results.

   The last part of the set-up process is activating the AI detection for the custom agreement type. Complete the [Activate Your Custom Agreement Types](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=cgn1740452596841.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to activate the AI detection for a custom agreement type and surface it in selected historical agreements.") task to activate the type and find it in your historical agreements.

You have successfully tested the AI detection accuracy for the custom agreement type.

title: Custom Obligation Types
text:
Read about custom obligation types, including use cases, permissions, capabilities, and entry points.

Custom obligation types are unique categories of obligations that are tailored to meet organizational or departmental needs. These obligations can relate to compliance, performance, or other critical contract aspects.

Docusign provides a set of standard obligation types, however, you can create custom types that more accurately categorize your obligations. These custom obligation types provide:

- **Enhanced compliance and risk management:** Track compliance and adherence to legal and regulatory requirements.
- **Improved performance training:** Monitor and measure the performance of involved parties against their commitments.
- **Streamlined contract management:** Organize, track, and manage various aspects of an agreement.
- **Tailored reporting:** Customize and generate reports that are relevant to your specific use case and obligation type.
- **Assists strategic decision-making:** Use insights and data to support strategic decisions, such as renegotiating terms, extending contracts, or terminating agreements.

title: Custom Obligation Types
text:
This feature is primarily used by contract managers, and possibly administrators. Here are a few examples of how various roles are using obligation types:

- **Procurement Managers**

  Uses standard and custom obligation types to assess the health and performance of vendors:

  - Track obligations ahead of due dates to determine the appropriate actions to take.
  - Visualize that payment obligations are being met to avoid missed payments and penalty fees.
  - Report on obligations to ensure that the organization is taking advantage of negotiated terms, discounts, and rebates.
- **Contract Managers**

  Creates custom obligation types and manages obligations, including assignments and reminders:

  - Monitor and track obligations to ensure fulfillment
  - Assign obligations to relevant team members
  - Communicate obligation requirements
- **Legal Operations Manager**

  Uses obligation types to flag high-risk obligations and follow up on services levels and renegotiation rights:

  - Locate relevant documents and obligations more quickly
  - Track obligations in a systematic and accurate way
  - Ensure that the appropriate stakeholders have access to important obligations

title: Custom Obligation Types
text:
Obligation type management requires the Organize and manage all agreements permission. This permission is the highest access level for Agreement Manager users and includes creating and managing obligation types.

See the [Agreement Manager Permissions, Profiles, and Roles](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=eut1712157553977.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the available Agreement Manager permissions, including how users and groups are granted access to Agreement Manager agreements and example roles for each permission.") topic to learn more about Agreement Manager access levels.

title: Custom Obligation Types
text:
The Manage Obligation Types feature is accessed from the Settings (gear) menu on the Completed Documents page:

![A screenshot of the Settings (gear) menu that highlights the Obligation option in the expanded](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Obligation-type-management_Manage-Obligation-Types_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcxNzAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.9rEoZ9PMYB1oNwrl5ktW9kvzGKsrexL0AOcw4bzAvFY&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Settings (gear) menu that highlights the Obligation option in the expanded")

The Manage Obligation Types page opens. The Obligation Types view is displayed. A list of custom and standard obligation types is displayed in the table.

![A screenshot of the Obligation Types view that shows the list of available standard and custom obligation types for the account.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Manage-Obligation-Types_Obligation-Types-view.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcxNzAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.9rEoZ9PMYB1oNwrl5ktW9kvzGKsrexL0AOcw4bzAvFY&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Obligation Types view that shows the list of available standard and custom obligation types for the account.")

Use this page to manage the types of obligations in your account:

- Create new (custom) obligation types
- Rename custom obligation types
- Hide or unhide custom and Docusign-provided (standard) obligation types

  Note: When an obligation type is hidden, it is hidden from the:
  - Type dropdown on the Obligations pane (Agreement preview page)
  - Obligation Type dropdown on the Obligations dashboard

  If an agreement contains a hidden obligation type, those obligations are still visible. Existing obligation instances with the hidden type are also visible.

title: Customize and Save Reports for Future Use
heading_path: Customize and Save Reports for Future Use
text:
Learn how to customize and save an agreement report for future use.

Create your own reports by applying filters and customizing the agreement table for an existing report. Once saved, the report is listed under the Custom tab on the Reports view.

![A snippet of the Reports view that shows custom reports found on the Custom tab.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Reports-view_Custom-tab-snippet.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY5ODQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.k7EMRfqjm6ASvoM4tXaaXTFvlW9_2AZxGHN9pXL-jhA&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Reports view that shows custom reports found on the Custom tab.")

Tip: Add the report to Your Favorites list. Select the Favorite (star) icon located in the top-right corner: ![A snippet of a detailed report view that highlights the Favorite (star) icon.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreements-dashboard_Report-details_Favorite-icon-selected_generic.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY5ODQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.k7EMRfqjm6ASvoM4tXaaXTFvlW9_2AZxGHN9pXL-jhA&_LANG=enus "A snippet of a detailed report view that highlights the Favorite (star) icon.")Your starred dashboards and reports are displayed in the Your Favorites section on the Overview dashboard.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Insights from the global header.

   The Insights hub opens and the Overview is displayed.

   Note: **Suggested Reports (Beta)**

   The Suggested Reports section provides AI-generated custom report recommendations based on the
   custom fields in your account. Use these suggestions to create custom reports. See the [Create Custom Reports from Suggested Reports (Beta)](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tskfc9f9485-65b6-4a2c-95e3-15bd13ce8562.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to create a custom report from one of the reports in the Suggested Reports (Beta) section on the Overview dashboard.") to learn more about this feature.
3. Select Agreements from the navigation pane on the left.

   The Agreements Dashboard view opens.
4. Go to the detailed report view for the dashboard chart (report) that you want to customize. Select (click) the title of the chart that you want to customize.

   You can also expand the More actions (three dots) menu and select the View Report option:

   ![A snippet of the Agreements Dashboard that highlights the View Report option in the Actions menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-dashboard_Actions_View-Report_generic.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY5ODQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.k7EMRfqjm6ASvoM4tXaaXTFvlW9_2AZxGHN9pXL-jhA&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Agreements Dashboard that highlights the View Report option in the Actions menu.")

   The detailed report view opens for the selected chart or insight.
5. Refine the report until it reflects the relevant agreement data. Expand a filter, choose a value or date range, and select the Apply option.
6. Use the All Filters option to apply additional filters to the report:

   ![A screenshot of the All Filters dialog box that shows a list of available and applied filters.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Agreements-Dashboard_Report-View_All-Filters-dialog-box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY5ODQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.k7EMRfqjm6ASvoM4tXaaXTFvlW9_2AZxGHN9pXL-jhA&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the All Filters dialog box that shows a list of available and applied filters.")

title: Customize and Save Reports for Future Use
heading_path: Customize and Save Reports for Future Use
text:
DE3OTY5ODQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.k7EMRfqjm6ASvoM4tXaaXTFvlW9_2AZxGHN9pXL-jhA&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the All Filters dialog box that shows a list of available and applied filters.")

   1. Select the All Filters
      option at the top of the report.

      The All Filters dialog
      box opens.
   2. Expand the filter that you want to apply, such as the Status filter.
   3. Select or enter the filter value.
   4. Select Apply.
   5. Repeat these steps until all necessary filters are applied.
   6. Select Save.

      The dialog box closes. The report is updated to reflect the filters
      applied.
7. Customize the columns in the table:

   Important: The Customize columns function only applies to the data that is displayed in the agreement list table. When downloading or exporting the report, all fields are included in the output file. This includes both custom and standard fields.

   ![A screenshot of the Customize columns dialog box that shows the columns you can choose to display in the agreements table.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Party-hub_Overview-dashboard_Customize-columns.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY5ODQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.k7EMRfqjm6ASvoM4tXaaXTFvlW9_2AZxGHN9pXL-jhA&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Customize columns dialog box that shows the columns you can choose to display in the agreements table.")

   1. Select the Customize columns (toggle) icon.

      The Customize columns dialog box opens. These fields represent the columns in the table.
   2. Select the toggles to turn the associated field on (blue) or off (white).
   3. Re-order the columns as needed. Hover the cursor over the Reorder (dots) icon for the column that you want to move and drag it to the appropriate location.
   4. Select the Save option.

   Tip: If the Status column is not displayed by default, use the Show or Hide Fields (toggle) option to add it to the table. From the Show or Hide Field dialog box, turn on the Status toggle and drag the field to the appropriate location. Be sure to Save your changes.

   Continue to apply filters and customize the table until the report reflects the relevant data.
8. Save the report. Expand the More actions (three dots) menu in the top right and select the Save option:

   ![A snippet of an agreement dashboard report that shows the Save As option in the dropdown.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Report-view_Dropdown-expanded.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY5ODQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.k7EMRfqjm6ASvoM4tXaaXTFvlW9_2AZxGHN9pXL-jhA&_LANG=enus "A snippet of an agreement dashboard report that shows the Save As option in the dropdown.")

   The Save Report dialog box opens.

   ![A screenshot of the Save Report dialog box that shows the Report name field.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreements-Dashboard_Customize-report_Save-Report-dialog-box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY5ODQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.k7EMRfqjm6ASvoM4tXaaXTFvlW9_2AZxGHN9pXL-jhA&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Save Report dialog box that shows the Report name field.")
9. Enter a unique name for the report in the Report name field.

   Note: The name of the selected report is displayed by default. Overwrite this value to reflect your customized report.
10. Enter a description about the report's purpose in the Description field.
11. Select the Save option.

    The dialog box closes and the custom report is saved. Your new report is available from the Custom tab under the Reports view:

port's purpose in the Description field.
