    The dialog box closes and the custom report is saved. Your new report is available from the Custom tab under the Reports view:

    ![A snippet of the Reports view that shows the Custom tab, including custom reports and the View option.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreements-Dashboard_Customized-report_Highlighted-in-Custom-section.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY5ODQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.k7EMRfqjm6ASvoM4tXaaXTFvlW9_2AZxGHN9pXL-jhA&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Reports view that shows the Custom tab, including custom reports and the View option.")Custom reports can be updated or deleted at any time from the report details page. See the [View and Update Your Custom Reports](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=yuf1727801071281.html&_LANG=enus "This topic answers the question: How can I review and update my custom reports?") topic to learn about editing options for your custom reports.

title: Use Case: Verify the Payment Terms for a Customer Agreement
heading_path: Use Case: Verify the Payment Terms for a Customer Agreement
text:
Learn how to locate a specific customer agreement and verify the payment terms. This topic answers the question: "How do I locate payment terms for one of my customer agreements?

As a contract viewer, you want to verify the payment terms for a customer agreement. Use the filter options to locate the agreement and verify the payment terms.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens. The Inbox view is displayed by default.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed. Use the filters to locate all active agreements for a specific customer.
4. Expand the Filters option and select the Parties filter.
5. Enter the name of the party in the Search field.
6. Choose the relevant party or alias group from the list and select Apply.

   The agreements that are associated with the selected party or alias group are displayed in the table.
7. Review the list and select the agreement that you want to review for payment terms.

   Tip: If you know the file name, perform a keyword search to locate the agreement. Enter the file name into the search field and press [Enter] or select a file from the dropdown list.

   The Agreement preview page opens for the selected agreement. The Details pane is displayed by default. The attributes and data points that were surfaced with AI are listed in the pane.
8. Locate the payment terms within the agreement.
   1. From the Details pane, locate the PAYMENT TERMS field in the Payment  section.
   2. If there is no payment terms value, locate the value within the agreement.

      - Select the Search (magnifying glass) icon from the top-right corner of the preview pane.
      - Enter Payment Terms in the Search field.
      - Use the Next and Previous arrows to scroll through the search results. The instances of the search term are highlighted in the agreement.

        Continue scrolling until you locate the payment term value.
9. Select the Close (x) icon in the top-left corner to return to the previous page.

   The Completed Documents view opens. Your previously entered search criteria and applied filters are still in effect.

You have successfully located payment terms for a specific customer agreement.

title: Agreement Manager Activity Logs
text:
Read about the account- and agreement-level activity logs, including the types of events that are captured in the log.

Audits are essential for:

- Minimizing errors
- Detecting security breaches
- Ensuring compliance
- Troubleshooting issues

Every agreement has an Activity Log that captures and logs user-performed events. Agreement Manager provides two types of activity logs:

- **Account-level:** provides a centralized location to view logged events for all agreements in an account.
- **Agreement-level:** displays logged events for an individual agreement.

Tip: **More about the Activity Log**

- The events are displayed in reverse chronological order with the most recent at the top.
- The time zone is localized based on the account's time zone preference.
- The table displays a maximum of 200 recorded events at a time.
- Events can take up to 15 minutes to display in the log.

title: Agreement Manager Activity Logs
text:
The Activity Log records and displays agreement-related events that were performed by a user, including:

- Access updates (agreement and agreement set sharing)
- Agreement downloads
- Agreement set operations (set creation and updates)
- Agreement views

  Tip: Agreement views are recorded on a per-session basis. If a user views the document multiple times, a single view is recorded in the log. If the file changes, a new session is initiated and a second "view" event is recorded.
- Attribute updates for select standard fields

  Tip: See the [Extracted Agreement Insights and Definitions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=uwk1702945852405.html&_LANG=enus "Look up the names and definitions for the attributes and values that are surfaced in Agreement Manager agreements with AI. This topic answers the question: What data is being extracted from my Agreement Manager agreements?") topic to learn more about agreement attributes.
- Document uploads

These user-performed events are recorded the Activity Log at the account- and agreement-levels.

title: Agreement Manager Activity Logs
text:
The account-level Activity Log provides a centralized location to view aggregate, user-performed events across all the agreements in an account.

![A screenshot of the account-level Activity Log that logs events related to all the agreements in the account.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Accountlevel-Activity-Log--example.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc5MjgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.6o--FfHnwvnvgeyq4e9ghUh-H-QbK6HSrgHOcBA_-CI&_LANG=enus)

The table displays 200 rows of recorded events. If additional events exist, you are prompted to download the table data to a CSV to view the full list of events:![A screenshot of the dialog box that prompts you to download the activity log to a CSV.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Activity-Log_Prompt-to-download-to-csv.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc5MjgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.6o--FfHnwvnvgeyq4e9ghUh-H-QbK6HSrgHOcBA_-CI&_LANG=enus)

The columns in the table represent the data fields that are captured for each event, including:

- Date
- Resource
- User
- Action
- Field

Important: The actual event time stamp is localized in the system, however, the dates are shown in the user interface (UI) in Coordinated Universal Time (UTC).

This centralized Activity Log is accessed from the Agreement Manager homepage on the Completed Documents view:

![A snippet of the Completed Documents view that shows the View Activity Log highlighted in the Settings (gear) menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Account-audit_Actions_View-Activity-Log.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc5MjgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.6o--FfHnwvnvgeyq4e9ghUh-H-QbK6HSrgHOcBA_-CI&_LANG=enus)

Expand the Settings (gear) menu located above the agreement list table and select the View Activity Log option.

Important: You must have the Organize and manage all agreements permission to access the account-level Activity Log. An administrator grants permissions from the user or group profile pages located under the Admin tab.

title: Agreement Manager Activity Logs
text:
The agreement-level Activity Log records and displays user-performed events for a specific agreement.

![A screenshot of the Activity Log that shows agreement-level events that were performed by a user.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Activity-Log-example.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc5MjgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.6o--FfHnwvnvgeyq4e9ghUh-H-QbK6HSrgHOcBA_-CI&_LANG=enus)

The table displays 200 rows of recorded events. If additional events exist, you are prompted to download the table data to a CSV to view the full list of events.

The columns in the table represent the data fields that are captured for each event, including:

- Date
- User
- Action
- Item
- Details

Important: The actual event time stamp is localized in the system, however, the dates are shown in the user interface (UI) in Coordinated Universal Time (UTC).

There are two entry points for the agreement-level Activity Log:

- From the More actions (three dots) menu located in the agreement list table (Completed Documents view):![A snippet of the agreement list table that shows the Activity Log option in the More actions (three dots) menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Activity-Log_access-from-Actions-menuCompleted-documents.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc5MjgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.6o--FfHnwvnvgeyq4e9ghUh-H-QbK6HSrgHOcBA_-CI&_LANG=enus)
- From the dropdown located in the top-right corner of the Agreement preview page:![A snippet of the Agreement preview page that shows the Activity Log option in the expanded dropdown.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Activity-Log_access-from-Agreement-Preview_Downloads-dropdown.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc5MjgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.6o--FfHnwvnvgeyq4e9ghUh-H-QbK6HSrgHOcBA_-CI&_LANG=enus)

title: Agreement Manager Activity Logs
text:
The Activity Log displays pagination for the first 200 logged events in the table. If the table contains more than 200 events, a message displays and you are prompted to download the CSV file to view all the events:![A screenshot of the dialog box that prompts you to download the activity log to a CSV.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Activity-Log_Prompt-to-download-to-csv.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc5MjgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.6o--FfHnwvnvgeyq4e9ghUh-H-QbK6HSrgHOcBA_-CI&_LANG=enus)

Note: When using the Download CSV option, there is a 5,000 row limitation for downloading logged events.

title: Agreement Manager Activity Logs
text:
This table displays a list of updates that were made to the Activity Log capability, including the date and description of the change.

| **Date** | **Description of change** |
| --- | --- |
| July 18, 2025 | The account-level Activity Log includes user-performed events related to agreement sets:  - Access updates (agreement set sharing updates) - Set management operations |
| May 30, 2025 | The account-level Activity Log provides a centralized location to view aggregate, user-performed events across all the agreements in an account:  - Agreement uploads - Attribute (field) updates - Document access (sharing) updates - Document views and downloads |
| March 7, 2025 | The Activity Log records user-performed events at the agreement-level:   - Access updates (agreement sharing updates) - Agreement downloads - Agreement uploads - Agreement views - User-performed attribute updates (for selected fields) |

title: Export Data from the Agreement List Table
heading_path: Export Data from the Agreement List Table
text:
Learn how to export agreement data to a CSV file for selected agreements.

Use the Export to CSV feature to export agreement data from the agreement list table. Before exporting the data, customize the columns in the table to make sure the export captures the relevant data.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed.
4. Perform a search and apply filters to locate the relevant agreements:
   1. Enter a keyword or string in the search field and select a suggestion from the dropdown or press the Enter key.

      The agreements containing the keyword or search string that you entered are displayed in the table.
   2. Use the filters to refine the list of search results. For example, use the Parties filter to view a list of agreements for a specific party.

      1. Expand the Filters option and select the filter that you want to apply.
      2. Choose a filter value.
      3. Select the Apply option.

      The agreements that match the applied filters are displayed in the table.
5. Select the agreements that you want to export:

   ![A snippet of the agreement list table that shows selected agreements and options to download documents or export agreement data.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-documents-table_Download-or-export-selected-agreements.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY0OTAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.ZdtKjt3HXdvKphTZr2Ymn0QzEmerKXE1Oucc3k1tyz8&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the agreement list table that shows selected agreements and options to download documents or export agreement data.")

   - Select the individual agreements.
   - Select the Choose all checkbox from the header row to select all the agreements on that page of results.

     Tip: The Choose all option only selects agreements on the current page of search results. It does not select any agreements on subsequent pages of results.
   - Expand the Selected dropdown and select the Select all that match your filters option. All the agreements in the table that matched your applied filters are selected.

     Tip: The Select all that match your filters option is only available if filters are applied to the agreement list table.

   When at least one document is selected, the actions bar opens. The Download Documents and Export to CSV options are displayed. The document count indicates the number of selected agreements.
6. Select Export to CSV.

   The CSV file is downloaded to your local Downloads folder. The file contains agreement data (custom and standard fields) for the selected documents.

   Tip: Update the CSV file to further customize your information. For example, hide and reorder the columns in the downloaded file until the relevant information is displayed.

You have successfully exported the agreement data for a group of selected documents, including custom and standard fields.

title: Share an Agreement Set
text:
Learn how to share an agreement set with individual users and groups. This topic answers the question: How do I share groups of agreements with the appropriate people?

Contract managers create and manage sets to make sure the right people have access to relevant agreements. After you add agreements to the set, share the set with the appropriate users and groups.

These permissions provide varying access to the agreements being shared:

- Can Manage:: Users can edit attribute values, and delete, share, and remove access to the agreement. Users with this access can also add the agreement to agreement sets.
- Can Edit:: Users can edit attribute values and share and download the agreement.
- Can View:: Users can read and download the agreement.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens.
4. Expand the Settings (gear) menu from the top right and select the Sets option.

   The Agreement sets page opens. The All agreement sets view is displayed. The table displays sets that were shared with or created by the logged-in user.

   ![A snippet of the All agreement sets view that displays sets that were created by or shared with the logged-in user.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Manage-Sets-dialog_All-agreement-sets_New-Agreement-Set.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTczNzAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9._U92rzZFXvNx6HOfobN3RiGtFHQJERl-f0Pkc0YNKKY&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the All agreement sets view that displays sets that were created by or shared with the logged-in user.")
5. Locate the set that you want to share, expand the More actions (three dots) menu, and select Share:

   ![A snippet of the All agreement sets view that shows the Share option in the More actions menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Manage-Sets-dialog_All-agreement-sets_Share-option.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTczNzAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9._U92rzZFXvNx6HOfobN3RiGtFHQJERl-f0Pkc0YNKKY&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the All agreement sets view that shows the Share option in the More actions menu.")

   The Share with others dialog box opens.

   ![A screenshot of the Share with others dialog box that shows the users and groups that currently have access to the selected set.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Manage-Sets-dialog_All-agreement-sets_Share-with-others-dialog.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTczNzAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9._U92rzZFXvNx6HOfobN3RiGtFHQJERl-f0Pkc0YNKKY&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Share with others dialog box that shows the users and groups that currently have access to the selected set.")
6. Enter the name of the user or group with whom you want to share the set in the Add user or group search field.

   A type-ahead dropdown displays names matching the criteria that you entered.
7. Select a user or group from the dropdown list.

   The Add users view is displayed.

   ![A screenshot of the Add users view that shows the notification and access-level options.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-sets_share-set_Add-users-dialog-box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTczNzAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9._U92rzZFXvNx6HOfobN3RiGtFHQJERl-f0Pkc0YNKKY&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Add users view that shows the notification and access-level options.")

   Important:

title: Share an Agreement Set
text:
AsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9._U92rzZFXvNx6HOfobN3RiGtFHQJERl-f0Pkc0YNKKY&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Add users view that shows the notification and access-level options.")

   Important:

   The Notify users by email option is selected by default. Leave this checkbox selected to notify users that an agreement set was shared with them.

   If you de-select this option, users are not notified of the shared agreement set.
8. Expand the Access dropdown and select the appropriate access level for agreements in the set.

   Choices are: Can Manage, Can Edit, and Can View.

   Note: To remove user or group access to the set:
   - Expand the Access dropdown for the user or group that you want to remove.
   - Select Remove.
   - Select Save.

   The user or group name is removed from the People with access list.
9. Select Share.

   The dialog box closes and you are returned to the Agreement sets page. The agreement set has been shared with the selected users or groups.
10. Select Completed Documents from the breadcrumb navigation at the top of the page.

    You are returned to the Agreement Manager homepage.

You have successfully shared an agreement set with selected users or groups, including setting an acces level.

title: Obligations Dashboard
text: Read about the Obligations dashboard, including available reports and filter options.

title: Obligations Dashboard
text:
The Obligations dashboard provides a visual summary of your agreement obligations.

![A screenshot of the Obligations dashboard that shows the available charts and filter options.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Obligations-Dashboard.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwMDIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.PmgSr4Y1zeA7a91Ac3OysLlCY-aitlVSs5tcPNLKG70&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Obligations dashboard that shows the available charts and filter options.")

These obligations include:

- Payment, renewal, and termination obligations surfaced with AI
- Other types of obligations that were manually created

title: Obligations Dashboard
text:
The Obligations dashboard contains two charts (reports):

- Obligations by due date: Shows the number of obligations with upcoming due dates by month for the next six months. Select a segment of the bar or pie chart to view the associated obligations.![A screenshot of the Obligations by due date chart that shows the number of obligations with upcoming due dates by month.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Obligations-Dashboard_Obligations-by-due-date.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwMDIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.PmgSr4Y1zeA7a91Ac3OysLlCY-aitlVSs5tcPNLKG70&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Obligations by due date chart that shows the number of obligations with upcoming due dates by month.")
- Obligations by type: Shows all the agreement obligations
  for the account, grouped by type. The default time period is *All Time*.
  Select a segment from the bar or pie chart to view the associated
  obligations.![A screenshot of the Obligations by type chart that shows the number of obligations with upcoming due dates by type.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Obligations-Dashboard_Obligations-by-type.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwMDIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.PmgSr4Y1zeA7a91Ac3OysLlCY-aitlVSs5tcPNLKG70&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Obligations by type chart that shows the number of obligations with upcoming due dates by type.")

Tip: Use the Due Date filter to update the time period that is reflected in the charts.

For each chart, expand the More actions (three dots) menu and select from the available options:

![A snippet of the Obligations dashboard that shows the available chart actions, such as View Report, View as table, and Download as png.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Obligations-Dashboard_Expanded-Actions-menu.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwMDIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.PmgSr4Y1zeA7a91Ac3OysLlCY-aitlVSs5tcPNLKG70&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Obligations dashboard that shows the available chart actions, such as View Report, View as table, and Download as png.")

- View Report: Opens a detailed report view of the chart, including a list of obligations and filters.
- View as table: Displays the chart data in a table instead of a chart.
- Download as png: Downloads an image of the chart to your local Downloads folder.

Note: The dashboard charts can be viewed as regular bar charts or as stacked bar charts. Expand the More actions (three dots) menu and select the View as Stacked Bar Chart option:![A screenshot of the More actions (three dots) menu for a dashboard chart.that highlights the View as Stacked Bar Chart option.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Dashboard-Reports_Dashboard-charts_View-as-stacked-bar-chart_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwMDIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.PmgSr4Y1zeA7a91Ac3OysLlCY-aitlVSs5tcPNLKG70&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the More actions (three dots) menu for a dashboard chart.that highlights the View as Stacked Bar Chart option.")

title: Obligations Dashboard
text:
The data reflected in the charts can be filtered to show relevant obligations. Filters are applied at the Obligations dashboard level and once applied, both charts update based on the selected filters. Available filters include:

![A snippet of the Obligations dashboard that shows the filter options, including Due Date, Obligation Type, Parties, and Obligation Owner.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Obligations-Dashboard_Filter-options.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwMDIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.PmgSr4Y1zeA7a91Ac3OysLlCY-aitlVSs5tcPNLKG70&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Obligations dashboard that shows the filter options, including Due Date, Obligation Type, Parties, and Obligation Owner.")

- Due Date: Use this filter to specify a time period by which to filter the obligation charts. Options include: Today, Next 7 Days, Next 14 Days, Next 30 Days, Next 6 Months, and Custom Date Range.
- Obligation Type: Use this filter to view obligations based on the type of obligation, such as Compliance, Confidentiality, Indemnification, and Payment.
- Parties: Use this filter to view obligations for a specific party and party role.

  Important: When using the Parties filter, you must also select a Party Role value. If you do not know which role to select, choose the All option. If no role is selected, the search returns agreements where the party is not the buyer or the seller.
- Obligation Owner: Use this filter to view obligations by the user who created the obligation. For obligations that were surfaced with AI, the agreement owner is the obligation owner.

Note: The detailed report view offers two additional filter options: Obligation Status and Agreement Name. Use these filters to refine the report to locate relevant obligations.

title: Obligations Dashboard
text:
The Favorite (star) icon is displayed on all the dashboards and on the detailed report view for each of the dashboard reports and insights. Use this feature to "star" the insights that you regularly track:

![A snippet of a detailed report view that highlights the Favorite (star) icon.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreements-dashboard_Report-details_Favorite-icon-selected_generic.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwMDIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.PmgSr4Y1zeA7a91Ac3OysLlCY-aitlVSs5tcPNLKG70&_LANG=enus "A snippet of a detailed report view that highlights the Favorite (star) icon.")

Your "starred" dashboards and reports are displayed in the Your Favorites list on the Overview dashboard:

![A snippet of the Overview dashboard that highlights the Your Favorites section.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reporting-hub_Overview_Your-Favorties_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwMDIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.PmgSr4Y1zeA7a91Ac3OysLlCY-aitlVSs5tcPNLKG70&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Overview dashboard that highlights the Your Favorites section.")

title: Obligations Dashboard
text:
Features that use Docusign Iris display the AI-Assisted denotation badge within the product. This denotation badge is clickable. Select the badge to access additional information about how Docusign uses AI functionality:

![A screenshot of the expanded AI-Assisted denotation badge that shows additional information about how Docusign uses AI functionality.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Obligations-Dashboard_AIAssisted-pillexpanded.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwMDIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.PmgSr4Y1zeA7a91Ac3OysLlCY-aitlVSs5tcPNLKG70&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the expanded AI-Assisted denotation badge that shows additional information about how Docusign uses AI functionality.")

Review the [AI Denotation in Agreement Manager](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=rsz1722973735168.html&_LANG=enus "This topic answers the question: In what way does Docusign indicate AI-assisted insights or AI functionality in Agreement Manager?") topic to learn more about how Docusign denotes AI functionality.

title: Obligations Dashboard
text:
When downloading reports to CSV files from the Insights reporting hub, there is an agreement limit of 25,000 agreements for all IAM reports. This count includes your custom reports.

**Suggested workaround:** Adjust the report filters to run the report in batches until all the relevant agreements are downloaded. For example, run the report in batches according to varying date ranges. Once downloaded, combine the data into one CSV file.

Note: The eSignature reports have a different set of limitations. See the [Using Reports](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=cnu1642812012214&topicId=wou1578456440763.html) article to learn more about eSignature reports for senders and signers.

title: Obligations Dashboard
text:
Obligations are surfaced in English (U.S.) agreements only. The AI model that processes obligations is run from a U.S. server and can only process English agreements in this region.

If your language and region are not supported, manually create the obligations to include this information in dashboards, reports, and other insights.

See these support articles to learn more about obligation management:

- [Obligation Management](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=csm1727128259486.html&_LANG=enus "Read about obligation management, including how to use this functionality to capture, track, and fulfill your agreement obligations. This topic answers the question: How can I find and track my agreement obligations?")
- [Manually Create an Obligation](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=rrf1727128588540.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to create an obligation from the Agreement preview page for a selected agreement.")

title: Download the Upcoming Renewals Report
text:
Learn how to locate upcoming renewals from the Renewals dashboard, including viewing and downloading agreements. This topic answers the question: How do I find my upcoming renewals in Agreement Manager?

The Renewals dashboard contains charts about agreements in Agreement Manager. The charts reflect the agreements that match any applied filters. Use this dashboard to locate upcoming renewals, including viewing and downloading an agreements list.

Important: The dashboard refreshes every 15 minutes to account for new agreements or agreements with manually updated renewal-related attribute values. The refresh is designed to ensure that all agreements are represented in the dashboard.

**Access Renewals dashboard:**

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Insights from the global header.

   The Insights hub opens and the Overview dashboard is displayed.
3. Select Renewals from the navigation pane on the left.

   The Renewals dashboard view opens.

   ![A screenshot of the Renewals dashboard that shows filter options and renewal-related charts.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Renewals-dashboard_No-navigation-pane.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcyNTQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.WaqII30O9-n6EF8KvEA5d1g3j9sBZ8RlElUUrg3FZdw&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Renewals dashboard that shows filter options and renewal-related charts.")

**Apply filters to the dashboard:**

4. Filter the Renewals dashboard to locate upcoming renewals:

   ![A snippet of the Renewals dashboard that highlights the dashboard filters.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Renewals-dashboard_Filter-pills.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcyNTQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.WaqII30O9-n6EF8KvEA5d1g3j9sBZ8RlElUUrg3FZdw&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Renewals dashboard that highlights the dashboard filters.")

   Filters include: Parties, Renewal Notice Date, Renewal Owner, Renewal Type, and Status.

   1. Expand the filter that you want to apply.
   2. Select or enter the filter value.
   3. Select the Apply option.

   Important: When using the Parties filter, you must also select a Party Role value. If you do not know which role to select, choose the All option. If no role is selected, the search returns agreements where the party is not the buyer or the seller.

   Tip: Use the Filters option to access additional filters:![A screenshot of the Filters dialog box that shows additional filters that you can apply to the agreement list.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-documents-table_All-Filters-dialog-box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcyNTQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.WaqII30O9-n6EF8KvEA5d1g3j9sBZ8RlElUUrg3FZdw&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Filters dialog box that shows additional filters that you can apply to the agreement list.")

   The charts are updated to reflect the filters that you applied.

**View the 'Upcoming renewals' chart:**

The Upcoming renewals chart provides an aggregate count of agreements with upcoming renewals. These agreements are grouped by Renewal Notice Date and match any applied filters, such as the Renewal Type.

5. Review the Upcoming renewals chart. For example, view the number of agreements that are represented in a particular chart segment. Hover your cursor over a segment in the chart.

   A hover message shows the break-down of the documents that are represented in the segment:

title: Download the Upcoming Renewals Report
text:
ements that are represented in a particular chart segment. Hover your cursor over a segment in the chart.

   A hover message shows the break-down of the documents that are represented in the segment:

   ![A snippet of the Upcoming renewals dashboard chart that shows the hover message for a selected bar.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Renewals-dashboard_Upcoming-renewals_hover-message.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcyNTQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.WaqII30O9-n6EF8KvEA5d1g3j9sBZ8RlElUUrg3FZdw&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Upcoming renewals dashboard chart that shows the hover message for a selected bar.")

   Tip: Select (click) a bar to view the agreements that are represented in that part of the chart. The detailed report view opens. The chart and agreement list are filtered to show the agreements that are reflected in the selected bar.

**Access the detailed report view:**

6. Access the detailed report view for the Upcoming renewals chart:

   ![A screenshot of the Upcoming renewals chart that shows the View Report option in the More actions (three dots) menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Renewals_Renewals-Dashboard_Chart-ellipses-menu.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcyNTQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.WaqII30O9-n6EF8KvEA5d1g3j9sBZ8RlElUUrg3FZdw&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Upcoming renewals chart that shows the View Report option in the More actions (three dots) menu.")

   - Select the chart heading.
   - Hover the cursor over the chart, expand the More actions (three dots) menu and select the View Report option.

   The detailed report view opens for the Upcoming renewals chart. The agreements represented in the chart are listed in the table.

**Download an agreement:**

7. Download an agreement to your local Downloads folder:

   ![A snippet of the Renewals dashboard that highlights the Download option in the Actions menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Renewals_Renewals-Dashboard_table-ellipses-menu.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcyNTQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.WaqII30O9-n6EF8KvEA5d1g3j9sBZ8RlElUUrg3FZdw&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Renewals dashboard that highlights the Download option in the Actions menu.")

   1. Expand the More actions (three dots) menu for the agreement that you want to download.
   2. Select the Download option.

   The agreement is downloaded to your Downloads folder.
8. View the Agreement preview page for a selected agreement:
   1. Select (click) an agreement from the table.

      The Agreement preview page opens for the selected agreement. The Details pane is displayed by default.
   2. Review the details, obligations, hierarchies, and agreement sets for the agreement.
   3. Return to the previous page. Select the Back arrow from the top-left corner.

   You are returned to the Upcoming renewals report.

**Customize and download the report:**

9. Customize the information that is displayed in the table:

   ![A screenshot of the Show or Hide Fields dialog box that shows the columns you can choose to display in the agreements table.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Party-hub_Overview-dashboard_Customize-columns.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcyNTQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.WaqII30O9-n6EF8KvEA5d1g3j9sBZ8RlElUUrg3FZdw&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Show or Hide Fields dialog box that shows the columns you can choose to display in the agreements table.")

   1. Select the Show or Hide Fields (toggle) icon.

title: Download the Upcoming Renewals Report
text:
Urg3FZdw&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Show or Hide Fields dialog box that shows the columns you can choose to display in the agreements table.")

   1. Select the Show or Hide Fields (toggle) icon.

      The Show or Hide Fields dialog box opens. These fields represent the columns in the table.
   2. Select the toggles to turn the associated field on (blue) or off (white).
   3. Re-order the columns as needed. Hover the cursor over the Reorder (dots) icon for the field that you want to move and drag it to the appropriate location.
   4. Select the Save option.

   Continue applying filters and customizing the table data until the report reflects relevant data.
10. Download the report. Expand the More actions (three dots) menu in the top right and select the Download option:

    ![A snippet of a detailed report view that shows the Download option in the expanded dropdown.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Renewals_Detailed-report_Download.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcyNTQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.WaqII30O9-n6EF8KvEA5d1g3j9sBZ8RlElUUrg3FZdw&_LANG=enus "A snippet of a detailed report view that shows the Download option in the expanded dropdown.")

    The associated report is downloaded to your local Downloads folder.

**Subscribe to the report:**

11. Set up one-time and recurring email reports for yourself and others. Select the Manage Notification (bell) icon and complete the Create subscription dialog box:

    ![A screenshot of the Create subscription dialog box that is used to subscribe to report notifications.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Renewals-dashboard_Report-view_Create-subscriptions.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcyNTQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.WaqII30O9-n6EF8KvEA5d1g3j9sBZ8RlElUUrg3FZdw&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Create subscription dialog box that is used to subscribe to report notifications.")

    Note: When you create a subscription, you have the option to add individual users and groups. Once created, the subscription is displayed in the Manage report subscriptions dialog box for any individual users who were added. Currently, the subscription is not visible to users if they were added as part of a group.

    Review the [Subscribe to Receive Report Notifications](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=wfw1734545937270.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to set up one-time or recurring email reports for yourself or others to receive notifications about your obligations, renewals, or agreements.") article to learn more about this feature.

**Add the report to 'Your Favorites':**

12. Add the report to Your Favorites list. Select the Favorite (star) icon located in the top-right:

    ![A snippet of the Upcoming renewals report view that highlights the Favorite (star) icon.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Renewals-dashboard_Upcoming-renewals-report_Mark-favorite_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcyNTQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.WaqII30O9-n6EF8KvEA5d1g3j9sBZ8RlElUUrg3FZdw&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Upcoming renewals report view that highlights the Favorite (star) icon.")Your starred dashboards and reports are displayed in the Your Favorites section on the Overview dashboard.

**Return to the Renewals dashboard:**

13. Return to the Renewals dashboard. Select the Renewals option from the breadcrumb navigation:

played in the Your Favorites section on the Overview dashboard.

**Return to the Renewals dashboard:**

13. Return to the Renewals dashboard. Select the Renewals option from the breadcrumb navigation:

    ![A snippet of the Upcoming renewals report that highlights the breadcrumb navigation.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Renewals-dashboard_Upcoming-renewals-report_Renewals-breadcrumb_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcyNTQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.WaqII30O9-n6EF8KvEA5d1g3j9sBZ8RlElUUrg3FZdw&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Upcoming renewals report that highlights the breadcrumb navigation.")

You have successfully reviewed and downloaded the Upcoming renewals report.

title: Reconnect a Docusign App Connection
text:
Learn how to use the Docusign app Reconnect a connection feature to reestablish the Docusign app’s connection to workflows.

If there is a connection disconnect, select Docusign app Reconnect a connection to reestablish the app’s connection to workflows.

For information about reconnecting Salesforce connections, see [Reconnect a Salesforce Organization to Your Workflow Builder App](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=kam1719258759323.html&_LANG=enus)

1. Select the Docusign app.
2. On the app detail page, select Manage Connections to access the
   Connections page.

   ![A screenshot of a sample App Center Connections page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/ac-newest-conn-page-ex.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTgxNjksInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.t8qOwSc3PPh-EmO6mI4_yTDX5sN3xUFqh1SS_3FwryY&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of a sample App Center Connections page.")
3. For the connection you want to reconnect, select Reconnect from the overflow menu.
4. At the Reconnect this connection dialog box, type RECONNECT and select Reconnect.

The Docusign for Salesforce app connection to
workflows is available or re-established.

title: Filter Your Completed Documents for Custom Agreement Types
heading_path: Filter Your Completed Documents for Custom Agreement Types
text:
Learn how to view, sort, and filter your completed documents by a custom agreement type.

Use the Agreement Type filter to filter the agreement list table for specific agreement types, both custom and standard. This filter is located on the Completed Documents page in the Filters option.

This topic reviews the steps to filter your completed documents by custom agreement types.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed. Perform a search and apply filters to locate the relevant agreements.
4. Apply the Agreement Type filter to view documents that are associated with a selected custom type:

   ![A screenshot of the Agreement Type filter that shows a selected custom agreement type.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Navigator_Completed-documents_search-field-and-filters-highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc1MTAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.yqMhU5-GP_cDYT-CvwjCNPmhhJ4mb9j49DvKfFTLrtk&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Agreement Type filter that shows a selected custom agreement type.")

   1. Expand (click) the Filters option and select the Agreement Type filter.

      The Agreement Type filter expands and available types are displayed.
   2. Search for the relevant custom agreement type. Enter a keyword or search string in the Search field.

      As you enter the search criteria, a list of agreement types that matched the keyword are displayed.
   3. Select the custom agreement type by which you want to filter your documents.
   4. Select the Apply option.

   The agreement list table updates, and agreements that match the selected type are displayed.
5. After locating agreements for the selected agreement type, choose to download, share, or export the agreements:
   - See the [Download Documents from the Agreement List Table](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=ach1724363496037.html&_LANG=enus "This topic answers the question: How do I download agreements from Agreement Manager?") topic to learn more about downloading agreements.
   - See the [Share an Agreement with Others](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=vyz1722979030156.html&_LANG=enus "This topic answers the question: How do I share agreements with others? Learn how to share an agreement with other users or groups, including assigning access levels for each recipient.") topic to learn how to share a selected agreement.
   - See the [Export Data from the Agreement List Table](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=pqf1724363438241.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to export agreement data to a CSV file for selected agreements.") topic to learn more about exporting agreement data.

title: Customize the Agreement List Table to Show Relevant Data
heading_path: Customize the Agreement List Table to Show Relevant Data
text:
This topic answers the question: How can I see relevant agreement data in the Agreement Manager table? Learn how to configure the columns in the agreement list table to display relevant data.

The agreement list table is customizable. You can select which attributes (fields) are displayed in the table. This topic begins from the Agreement Manager homepage. The Completed Documents view is displayed.

Remember: The Completed Documents view is located under the Agreements tab in the global header:

- Select Agreements from the global header.
- Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

![A snippet of the Completed Documents view that highlights the Show of Hide Fields (toggle) icon on the right-side of the page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-documents-table_Customize-columns-gear-icon.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY0NzUsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.FTvOCeRjD3gTc0FhZ1-1yasnGabcXQKsKBo5Epa4fGE&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Completed Documents view that highlights the Show of Hide Fields (toggle) icon on the right-side of the page.")

1. Select the Show or Hide Fields (toggle) icon located to the right of the column headings.

   The Show or Hide Fields dialog box opens. A number of fields are turned on
   (blue) by default. These fields represent the columns in the agreement list
   table. The field order in the dialog box reflects the column order in the
   table.

   Important: If you previously customized the columns in the agreement list table, you must manually display the Status column in the table. See the [Display the Status Column in the Agreement List Table](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tsk57b92226-d6e4-4725-99c4-79ebf42e9f8b.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to customize the agreement list table to display agreement status information.") topic to learn about this task.

   ![A screenshot of the Show or Hide Fields dialog box that shows the default selections.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-documents-view_Customize-columns-dialog-box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY0NzUsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.FTvOCeRjD3gTc0FhZ1-1yasnGabcXQKsKBo5Epa4fGE&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Show or Hide Fields dialog box that shows the default selections.")

   Note: The Show or Hide Fields function only applies to the data that is displayed in the agreement list table. When downloading or exporting the report, all fields are included in the output file. This includes both custom and standard fields.
2. Select the toggles to turn the corresponding field on (blue) or off (white).
3. Re-order the columns as needed. Hover the cursor over the Reorder (dots) icon for the field that you want to move. Drag the field to the appropriate location:

   ![A snippet of the Show or Hide Fields dialog box that highlights the 'drag to' feature for reordering column headers.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-documents_Customize-columns-dialog-box_drag-and-drop.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY0NzUsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.FTvOCeRjD3gTc0FhZ1-1yasnGabcXQKsKBo5Epa4fGE&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Show or Hide Fields dialog box that highlights the 'drag to' feature for reordering column headers.")

   Tip: The "grabbing" cursor displays when you hover over an element that can be dragged or moved.
4. Select the Save option.

   The dialog box closes. The new configuration is reflected in the agreement list table.

You have successfully updated the agreement list table to reflect the data that is relevant to you.

title: Agreement Manager Search Capabilities
text:
Read about Agreement Manager search capabilities, including enhanced search options.

With Agreement Manager, you have a single place to go to search across all of your completed agreements within your account. The search experience shows contextual help to craft search queries using synonyms, concepts, and everyday language.

 

Note:

Select one or more documents from the agreement list table to display the Download Documents and Export to CSV options:

![A screenshot of the agreement list table that shows selected documents and the download and export options in the actions bar.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-list-table_Download-DocumentsExport-to-CSV_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTYzNDcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.3v63rFp1K1P6bokQ414GAO9Fif1G1N6s5LFWuYqcnQw&_LANG=enus)

Use these options to download the selected agreements or to export the custom and standard field values for the selected agreements.

title: Agreement Manager Search Capabilities
text: The Agreement Manager search capabilities and features provide a robust search experience, including advanced search queries and filters.

title: Agreement Manager Search Capabilities
text:
The Agreement Manager search capabilities and features provide a robust search experience, including advanced search queries and filters.

| **Capability** | **Description** |
| --- | --- |
| **Advanced search queries** | Enter Boolean operators, proximity searches, term boosting, and wildcards directly in the search field to combine multiple conditions in a single query. Advanced search queries give you more control over how results are matched and ranked.  See the [Advanced Search Queries](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=conce9d9428-f27e-49ad-9001-3c7a0820ae78.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the advanced search query feature that gives you control over how your results are matched and ranked.") article to learn more about these queries. |
| **Boolean operators** | Use Boolean operators when performing advanced search queries to combine search terms. The supported operators, *AND*, *OR*, and *NOT*, enable you to specify must-have, optional, and excluded terms in your search query. You can also require or exclude terms with the + (plus) and - (minus) signs.  See the [Advanced Search Queries](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=conce9d9428-f27e-49ad-9001-3c7a0820ae78.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the advanced search query feature that gives you control over how your results are matched and ranked.") article to learn more about Boolean operators. |
| **Boosting terms** (Boosting) | Boost terms to give extra weight to specific words or phrases in your advanced search queries. Agreements that contain those terms rank higher in the search results list, without excluding other relevant matches.  This search feature uses a caret (^) symbol followed by a boost factor to prioritize the preceding term. For example, place greater importance on "patient" when searching for "patient registration form". Enter patient^2 registration form in the search field.  See the [Advanced Search Queries](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=conce9d9428-f27e-49ad-9001-3c7a0820ae78.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the advanced search query feature that gives you control over how your results are matched and ranked.") article to learn more about boosting terms. |
| **Exact phrase matching** | Use quotes to search for an exact word or phrase. Exact searches match only the specific characters that you type. They do not include variations such as plurals, verb tenses, or partial words.  Note: Quoted searches look for the exact sequence of characters and ignore other matching logic. As a result, these searches often return fewer results than regular keyword searches. See the [Advanced Search Queries](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=conce9d9428-f27e-49ad-9001-3c7a0820ae78.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the advanced search query feature that gives you control over how your results are matched and ranked.") article to learn more about exact phrase matching. |
| **Keyword search** | When users search for a keyword or phrase, the system searches the full agreement content to find exact keywords.  Tip: When using quotation marks in a query, the search returns documents that contain the exact word or phrase inside the quotation marks. |
| **Natural language (NL) search queries** | Using conversational language and tone, enter a question or phrase into the search field on the Completed Documents page. The query returns a direct answer to the question and a list of relevant agreements.  Important: Depending on the nature of the question posed, not all queries yield both direct answers and a list of agreements. Some queries may only provide a list of relevant agreements.  Note: The NL search function is currently available in beta. See the *Opt-in for NL search (Join Beta)* section of the [Natural Language Search Queries](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=fas1739987568042.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how natural language search queries, including components of the Search Results view.") article to join the beta.  See the [Natural Language Search Queries](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=fas1739987568042.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how natural language search queries, including components of the Search Results view.") article to learn more about NL queries. |
| **Natural language search suggestions** | Users can enter search queries using conversational, everyday language. As you enter keywords or phrases, a list of personalized suggestions is displayed. These suggestions are designed to enhance the discoverability and help users find relevant information more easily. |
| **Party role filter** | The Party Role is surfaced during agreement processing. Once identified, users can filter their agreements, agreement data, and insights by Party Role: A screenshot of the Parties filter that highlights the Party Role dropdown, including filter options.  For example, filter for agreements where a selected party played a specific role, such as Buyer or Seller.  Tip: This filter is available from:  - Agreement list table (on the **Completed Documents** view) - Agreements Dashboard (under the **Insights** tab) - Party list table (on the Parties page) |
| **Proximity search** | Find agreements where two or more words appear near each other, even if they are not side by side.  This search uses a quoted phrase followed by a tilde (~) symbol and a proximity number (N). The search returns results where the terms occur within *N* words of each other.  See the [Advanced Search Queries](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=conce9d9428-f27e-49ad-9001-3c7a0820ae78.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the advanced search query feature that gives you control over how your results are matched and ranked.") article to learn more about proximity searches. |
| **Query string search** | Find agreements by combining multiple operators and features in a single query string. You can enter any combination of Boolean logic, phrases, proximity, boosts, and wildcards into a single expression.  See the [Advanced Search Queries](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=conce9d9428-f27e-49ad-9001-3c7a0820ae78.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the advanced search query feature that gives you control over how your results are matched and ranked.") article to learn more about query string searches. |
| **Recent searches** | The search remembers recent searches and displays them for easy future reference. Users can also clear recent searches. |
| **Related terms** | The keyword search automatically includes synonyms and related concepts in the search results. A snippet of the Completed Documents page that shows search results and the Show related terms dialog box.  Users can capture all relevant documents even if the exact search term is not used. |
| **Saved searches** | The Save option gives you the option of saving your search criteria for future use. Saved searches include entered keywords, applied filters, and customized columns. |
| **Search feedback** | The Rate as helpful (thumbs up) and Rate as unhelpful (thumbs down) icons give users a chance to rate the search results:  A snippet of the Completed Documents page that shows the feedback icons used to rate search results.  After you select an initial rating, a dialog box opens and you are encouraged to provide additional details.  Restriction: The feedback rating icons only display when you perform a keyword search, including when filters are detected based on the search terms. This function is not available when you use only applied filters. |
| **Semantic search** | Semantic search improves the accuracy of search results by understanding:  - the intent of the search query - the contextual meaning of search terms |
| **Spelling assist** | The search automatically corrects spelling errors in queries. Users can find relevant information even if mistakes exist. |
| **Text normalization** | The search finds the most relevant agreements regardless of the specific word form used in the query. |
| **Type-ahead suggestions** | The search bar provides type-ahead suggestions as the user enters a search query. |
| **Wildcard search** | Perform a wildcard search to match variations of a word without typing every form and related terms. You can use wildcards to capture spelling or naming variations. For example,  - Use an asterisk (\*) to match one or more characters. - Use a question mark (?) to match exactly one character.  See the [Advanced Search Queries](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=conce9d9428-f27e-49ad-9001-3c7a0820ae78.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the advanced search query feature that gives you control over how your results are matched and ranked.") article to learn more about wildcard searches. |

title: Agreement Manager Search Capabilities
text:
-9001-3c7a0820ae78.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the advanced search query feature that gives you control over how your results are matched and ranked.") article to learn more about wildcard searches. |

These search capabilities lead to a more accurate, flexible, and efficient retrieval of information.

title: Agreement Manager Search Capabilities
text:
Quoted searches look for the exact sequence of characters and they ignore other matching logic. These searches return fewer results than regular keyword searches.

| Exact match (quoted) searches | Regular (keyword) searches |
| --- | --- |
| **Purpose:** Used to search for an exact work or phrase. | **Purpose:** Used to search for a general keyword, including variations and related word forms. |
| **Results list:** Small, narrow, focused. This search produces fewer results than the regular keyword search. | **Results list:** Broad, can contain related word forms and variations. This search produces more results than exact searches. |
| **Search behavior:** Exact searches match only the specific characters that you type. | **Search behavior:** The keyword search includes variations, such as plurals, verb tenses, or partial words. |

title: Agreement Manager Search Capabilities
text:
The feedback icons give you the chance to rate the quality of the search results. These rating icons are displayed when you perform a keyword search, including when filters are detected based on the search terms.

Choose the relevant icon, either Rate as helpful (thumbs up) or Rate as unhelpful (thumbs down), to get started. You are prompted to Share your reason to help us improve:

![A snippet of the Completed Documents page that shows the feedback icons and a dialog box to provide additional details.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Search-feedback_thumbs-up_Share-your-reason-dialog-box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTYzNDcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.3v63rFp1K1P6bokQ414GAO9Fif1G1N6s5LFWuYqcnQw&_LANG=enus)

Choose a reason for the rating, provide additional details in the Comments section, and select the Submit option.

Tip: Choose the Skip option to submit your rating without providing any additional information. Your rating is submitted for review.

When you submit feedback, your search terms, criteria, and search results are sent to Docusign along with your comments. Your data is used to improve the platform and fix any issues. See the [Reporting Bugs and Unexpected Issues FAQ](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fzd1707173174972&topicId=aif1751068986497.html) article to learn more about how Docusign uses your feedback.

title: Agreement Manager Search Capabilities
text:
Saved searches are stored sets of search criteria that include keywords, filters, and custom columns. The search defines how to find documents and it displays the data that is relevant to you.

When you run a saved search, the search criteria are applied to all the documents in the repository. Since agreements are regularly added to Agreement Manager, the search results can change over time. You can run a saved search to retrieve an updated list of documents that match the saved criteria.

Important: The Saved Search feature is available at the user level. All account users have permission to save their frequently used search queries.

When saving search criteria, all search parameters are saved, including:

- Keywords or phrases
- Filters
- Date ranges
- Natural language (NL) questions
- Custom column layouts

Important: When saving your search criteria, the exact search term (keyword, phrase, or question) is saved.

Access your saved searches from the Open Saved Search (bookmark) option:

![A snippet of the Completed Documents page that highlights the Open Saved Search option, including your recently saved searches.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Saved-searches_Your-Recently-Saved_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTYzNDcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.3v63rFp1K1P6bokQ414GAO9Fif1G1N6s5LFWuYqcnQw&_LANG=enus)

Select a search to view agreements that match the saved criteria. The view updates and the documents that match the selected search are listed in the table.![A snippet of a saved search that highlights the More actions (three dots) menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Saved-searches_More-saved-search-options_expanded-highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTYzNDcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.3v63rFp1K1P6bokQ414GAO9Fif1G1N6s5LFWuYqcnQw&_LANG=enus)

From this view, you can:

- Navigate back to the Completed Documents page
- Save a new version of the search
- Edit the search name and description
- Delete the saved search
- View all saved searches

See these articles to learn more about using the saved search feature:

- [Save Search Criteria for Future Use](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tskc38121a1-bbf8-4da8-8ed7-b364991f7e50.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to save the search criteria for the queries that you frequently perform.")
- [Use Saved Searches to Find Agreement Data](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tskca2d74f2-823d-403d-84a6-d990537cd187.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to access and run a saved search, including steps to edit and delete searches.")

title: Remove Parties from Party Groups
text:
This topic answers the question: How do I remove parties from an alias group that were mistakenly merged?

Party management tasks are completed by users with Organize and manage all agreements access to Agreement Manager, such as contract managers. Common tasks include reviewing and merging potential duplicate parties. You can also unmerge parties if they were merged by mistake.

These tasks are completed from the Manage parties page. This topic reviews the steps to remove a party from a merged party group.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Parties from the navigation pane on the left-side of the page.

   The Parties page opens.
4. Select the Manage Parties option from the top right:

   ![A snippet of the Parties page that highlights the Manage Parties option in the top right.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Manage-parties_entry-point_Parties-view.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc3ODUsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9._4nScrWepIItP9dJOEfEkHzxNOWZqmqqchqLjbIW7XA&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Parties page that highlights the Manage Parties option in the top right.")

   The Manage parties page opens and a list of individual and merged parties is displayed.
5. Locate the merged party group that you want to update. Enter the group party name in the Search parties field.
   1. Enter the name of the party group in the Search parties field.

      As you enter the name, a dropdown opens and the parties that match the search term are listed.
   2. Select a party name from the dropdown or select the Vew all results option at the bottom of the list.

      The parties that matched the search term are displayed in the table.
6. Expand (click) the More actions (three dots) menu for the relevant party group and select the Manage Party option:

   ![A snippet of the Manage parties view that shows the Manage Party option for a merged party.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Manage-Parties_All-Parties_Select-alias-group-to-unmerge.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc3ODUsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9._4nScrWepIItP9dJOEfEkHzxNOWZqmqqchqLjbIW7XA&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Manage parties view that shows the Manage Party option for a merged party.")

   The Review Duplicate Parties page opens. The parties that were merged to
   form the party group are displayed in the Select parties to
   merge section.

   Note: The Choose a name
   section is grayed out. You cannot update the group name from this
   step.

   ![A screenshot of the Review Duplicate Parties page that shows a deselected party to unmerge.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Manage-Parties_Manage-party-dialog-box_deselect-party.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc3ODUsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9._4nScrWepIItP9dJOEfEkHzxNOWZqmqqchqLjbIW7XA&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Review Duplicate Parties page that shows a deselected party to unmerge.")
7. Deselect the parties that you want to remove from the group and select the Save option.

   The page closes. You are returned to the Manage parties view. A message states that the changes were saved.

   Important:

   This action can take up to an hour before the changes are reflected in the various locations across the IAM platform.

   The unmerged party is no longer consolidated under the party group.
8. Select Parties from the breadcrumb navigation at the top of the page.

   You are returned to the Parties page.

title: Remove Parties from Party Groups
text:
IAM platform.

   The unmerged party is no longer consolidated under the party group.
8. Select Parties from the breadcrumb navigation at the top of the page.

   You are returned to the Parties page.

   Tip: Select Completed from the navigation pane on the left to view your agreement list. The Completed Documents view opens. The agreements to which the user has access are displayed in the table.

You have successfully removed a party from a merged party group.

title: Add Individual Agreements to Existing Agreement Sets
heading_path: Add Individual Agreements to Existing Agreement Sets
text:
Learn how to add agreements to an existing set from various places within Agreement Manager. This topic answers the question: How do I add additional agreements to an existing set?

Agreements can be added to existing sets from a number of places within Agreement Manager, such as:

- Agreement list table on the Completed Documents page
- Agreement sets pane on the Agreement preview page

This topic reviews both ways to add agreements to existing sets.

**Add agreements to sets from the Completed Documents page:**

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents page is displayed. Perform a search and apply filters to locate agreements.
4. Enter a keyword or search string in the search field and select Enter.

   Agreements containing the keyword or search string that you entered are displayed in the table.
5. Use filters to refine the list of search results:

   ![A snippet of the Completed Documents page that highlights the Filters option and suggested filters.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Navigator_Completed-documents_search-field-and-filters-highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTczNDYsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.3G-f5zeEKnsljFG61xwbaLbeO_kvtoDWRMFRLPdfiFk&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Completed Documents page that highlights the Filters option and suggested filters.")

   1. Select the Filters option and locate the filter that you want to apply.
   2. Select the relevant filter.
   3. Choose the filter value or enter a date range if applicable.
   4. Select the Apply option.
   5. Repeat this step until the relevant agreements are displayed in the agreement list table.

   The table displays agreements matching the filters that you applied.
6. Select the agreements that you want to add to the set. You can add one or more agreements at a time.

   When one or more agreements are selected, additional actions are displayed in the bar at the top of the table.
7. Add the selected agreements to a set. Expand the Organize dropdown, choose the relevant set, and select the Add option:

   ![A screenshot of the expanded Organize dropdown that shows a list of sets to which the agreements can be added.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-documents_Organize-expanded_Add-to-sets_Select-set.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTczNDYsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.3G-f5zeEKnsljFG61xwbaLbeO_kvtoDWRMFRLPdfiFk&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the expanded Organize dropdown that shows a list of sets to which the agreements can be added.")

   The dropdown closes and the selected agreements are added to the set.

   You are returned to the Completed Documents page. The agreements are no longer selected, however, your filters are still applied.

**Add agreements to sets from the Agreement preview page:**

Individual agreements can be added to sets from the Agreement preview page.

8. Select an agreement from the agreement list table on the Completed Documents page.

   The Agreement preview page opens for the selected agreement. The Details pane displays the attributes (fields) and values that were surfaced in the agreement.

title: Add Individual Agreements to Existing Agreement Sets
heading_path: Add Individual Agreements to Existing Agreement Sets
text:
able on the Completed Documents page.

   The Agreement preview page opens for the selected agreement. The Details pane displays the attributes (fields) and values that were surfaced in the agreement.

   ![A snippet of the Agreement preview page that highlights the Agreement sets icon in the tray on the left-side of the page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-Preview-page_Agreement-sets-icon-highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTczNDYsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.3G-f5zeEKnsljFG61xwbaLbeO_kvtoDWRMFRLPdfiFk&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Agreement preview page that highlights the Agreement sets icon in the tray on the left-side of the page.")
9. Select the Agreement sets icon from the tray of icons on the left-side of the page.

   The Agreement sets pane opens and a list of sets to which the agreement has been added is displayed.

   ![A snippet of the Agreement preview page that highlights the Agreement sets pane, including available actions.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-Preview-page_Agreement-sets-pane.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTczNDYsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.3G-f5zeEKnsljFG61xwbaLbeO_kvtoDWRMFRLPdfiFk&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Agreement preview page that highlights the Agreement sets pane, including available actions.")

   From this pane, you can:

   - Add the agreement to another set
   - Remove the agreement from a set
   - Create a new set
10. Search for and select the set to which you want to add the agreement:
    1. Enter a keyword or search string in the Search sets field.

       When you click in the field, a list of sets displays. These sets were either shared with you or created by you. The list updates as you enter search criteria.
    2. Select (click) a set from the list of search results.

       The agreement is added to the selected set.

    Note: If the agreement was already added to a set, select Add to Another Set to add it to another set. The Search sets field displays. Perform a query to locate the set to which you want to add the agreement.

    Repeat this step to add the agreement to additional sets.
11. Select the Close (x) icon from the top-left corner.

    You are returned to the Completed Documents page. Your search criteria and filters are still applied.

You have successfully added individual agreements to existing agreement sets.

title: 2025 Agreement Manager Release Notes
text:
Look up past release notes from 2025 for Docusign Agreement Manager, including features, bug fixes, and other changes.
