Note: The features and enhancements referenced in this topic are available in Demo accounts, Production accounts, or both. Availability is dependent on the ramp schedule and can vary across regions and languages.

title: 2025 Agreement Manager Release Notes
text: The following fixes, new features, and product enhancements have been delivered since the last release.

title: 2025 Agreement Manager Release Notes
text:
The following fixes, new features, and product enhancements have been delivered since the last release.

| **Type** | **Category** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Feature | Search | **Saved searches:** Saved searches are stored sets of search criteria that include keywords, filters, and custom columns. The search defines how to find documents and what agreement data to display in the table.  When you run a saved search, the search criteria are applied to the repository. Since agreements are regularly added to Agreement Manager, the search results can change over time.  See these articles to learn more about this feature:  - [Agreement Manager Search Capabilities](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=qzl1712244340883.html&_LANG=enus "Read about Agreement Manager search capabilities, including enhanced search options.") - [Save Search Criteria for Future Use](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tskc38121a1-bbf8-4da8-8ed7-b364991f7e50.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to save the search criteria for the queries that you frequently perform.") - [Use Saved Searches to Find Agreement Data](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tskca2d74f2-823d-403d-84a6-d990537cd187.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to access and run a saved search, including steps to edit and delete searches.") |
| Enhancement | Search | **Feedback mechanism for keyword searches:** When performing a keyword search, use the Rate as helpful (thumbs up) and Rate as unhelpful (thumbs down) icons to rate the quality of the search results.  Select a feedback rating icon to get started. After selecting a rating, you are prompted to choose a reason and provide comments.  When you submit feedback, your search terms, criteria, and search results are sent to Docusign, along with your rating, reason, and comments. Your data is used to improve the platform and fix any issues.  See these articles to learn more about this enhancement:  - [Agreement Manager Search Capabilities](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=qzl1712244340883.html&_LANG=enus "Read about Agreement Manager search capabilities, including enhanced search options.") - [Reporting Bugs and Unexpected Issues FAQ](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=aif1751068986497.html&_LANG=enus "Look up frequently asked questions related to providing feedback and reporting bugs and unexpected issues.") |
| Enhancement | Analyze | **Custom extraction improvements:** Improvements to the custom extraction process provide greater flexibility and drive accuracy.  - Refine Results option: During setup, the 'set definition' and 'example selection' steps are designed to "teach" the system. If you are not achieving the expected results, use the Refine Results option to adjust the AI setup. This feature is available for custom types and fields in English (IU.S.) and English (global) agreements.  See the [Refine the Detection for Custom Extractions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tskc13b6287-31ac-4687-8b35-b170680f50e0.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to refine the AI detection results for your custom extractions, including fine-tuning the definition and selecting new samples.") article to learn more about this enhancement. - Delete AI Setup option: Use   this option to delete the detection for your   custom types and fields. For example, if you no   longer want to extract the custom type in your   agreements.  See the [Delete the AI Setup for Custom Extractions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tskb5fc515f-a73d-4611-ac4b-db576656040e.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to delete the AI detection setup for a custom extraction.") article to learn more about this enhancement. - Find on Specific Documents option: Use this option to process selected   documents for custom types and fields. If the   custom type or field is located in any of the   documents, the associated data points are   surfaced.  See these articles to learn more:    - [Find Custom Agreement Types in Specific Documents](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tskb991328c-dc5f-4d45-9617-dada91a5672a.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to use the Find On Specific Documents function to surface custom agreement types in selected agreements.")   - [Find Custom Fields in Specific Documents](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tsk79824e36-d51d-4477-a5ca-7228cde30fb2.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to use the Find On Specific Documents function to surface custom fields in selected agreements.") |

title: 2025 Agreement Manager Release Notes
text:
The following fixes, new features, and product enhancements have been delivered since the last release.

| **Type** | **Category** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Enhancement | Search | **Natural language search (Beta):** Using conversational language and tone, enter a question or phrase into the search field on the Completed Documents page. The query returns a direct answer to the question and a list of relevant agreements.  Note: The NL search function is currently available in beta. See the *Opt-in for NL search (Join Beta)* section of the [Natural Language Search Queries](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=fas1739987568042.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how natural language search queries, including components of the Search Results view.") article to join the beta.  Important: Depending on the nature of the question posed, not all queries yield both direct answers and a list of agreements. Some queries may only provide a list of relevant agreements. |
| Enhancement | Analyze | **Status filter/column for reports:** The detailed report view for dashboard insights and charts now contains the Status filter and column. Choose to filter the dashboard reports by agreement status.    See the [Agreement Statuses](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=con6a3bd080-da17-4d1c-a92a-b9b9340c44fd.html&_LANG=enus "Read about agreement statuses, including how they are calculated, visibility within Agreement Manager, and which types of statuses are being tracked.") article to learn more about the Status filter and column. |
| UI change | Navigation | **The "Insights" hub:** The new Insights tab replaces the Reports tab in the global navigation at the top of the page. The new name, Insights, more closely reflects the true user experience.    See the [IAM Dashboards and Reports](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=jyq1749000353004.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the Insights hub, including an overview of the available dashboards and reports.") article to learn more about this change. |

title: 2025 Agreement Manager Release Notes
text:
The following fixes, new features, and product enhancements have been delivered since the last release.

| **Type** | **Category** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Enhancement | Analyze | **Agreement Manager reprocessing experience:** As new or updated AI models are released, your historical agreements can be processed again to surface these data points. Depending on the type of new insights that are available, users can choose to:  - Opt in for reprocessing from the New Insights Available badge (obligation-related attributes and insights) - Reprocess all the outdated agreements for the account (new or updated AI model) - Reprocess selected agreements (standard or custom fields)    See the [Agreement Manager Reprocessing Experience](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=con44590246-0f0e-43b4-9692-caadb57335ac.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about the Agreement Manager reprocessing experience, including its purpose, trigger points, and job size considerations.") article to learn more about this enhancement. |

title: 2025 Agreement Manager Release Notes
text:
The following fixes, new features, and product enhancements have been delivered since the last release.

| **Type** | **Category** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Enhancement | Analyze | **Agreement statuses:** Users can gain clarity on which agreements are active or inactive and quickly understand the state of an agreement:  - The primary statuses, Active and Inactive, are calculated based on the current date versus an agreement's effective and expiration dates. - Secondary statuses are used to further define the current state of an agreement, including: Expiring Soon, Renewing Soon, and Effective Soon.  See the [Agreement Statuses](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=con6a3bd080-da17-4d1c-a92a-b9b9340c44fd.html&_LANG=enus "Read about agreement statuses, including how they are calculated, visibility within Agreement Manager, and which types of statuses are being tracked.") article to learn more about this enhancement. |
| Enhancement | Analyze | **Auto-suggestion for custom agreement types:** When uploading agreements to Agreement Manager, if an agreement does not match an existing standard or custom type, it is identified as *Miscellaneous*. If 10 or more similar agreements are identified as *Miscellaneous*, a custom agreement type is suggested.  For example, 10 charter agreements were uploaded and identified as *Miscellaneous*. The agreements are recognized as being similar and a custom agreement type, *Charter Agreement*, is suggested:  A snippet of the agreement list table that shows a suggested custom agreement type for a Miscellaneous document.  Use the Create option to create the suggested custom agreement type. See the [Use Auto-Suggestion to Create Custom Agreement Types](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tsk2093dc24-1da8-4393-b4cd-526990bc7118.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to create a custom agreement type from the auto-suggestion feature.") article for steps to complete this task. |
| Enhancement | Analyze | **Expiration date extension for auto-renewing agreements:** When an auto-renewing agreement reaches its expiration date, the date is extended based on the agreement's renewal term. This action happens automatically and requires no user input.  For example, if the expiration date is January 1, 2025 with a 1-year renewal term, the expiration date updates to January 1, 2026 when the current date equals January 1, 2025.  Important: This feature relies on the accuracy of the agreement's Expiration Date, Renewal Type, and Renewal Term values. Although these values are extracted from your agreements, you should review and validate them for accuracy. See the [Validate Renewal-Related Attribute Values for an Agreement](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=sah1712251075016.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to validate renewal-related attribute values for a selected agreement.") article to learn more about completing a data review.   The expiration date extension feature applies to agreements with a Renewal Type of Auto Renew or Evergreen.  See the [Renewals Management](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=hjg1712251035061.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how Agreement Manager supports renewal management, including available tools, use cases, and key features. This topic answers the question: What are my renewal management options for Agreement Manager agreements, including tools and key features?") article to learn more about this enhancement. |

title: 2025 Agreement Manager Release Notes
text: The following fixes, new features, and product enhancements have been delivered since the last release.

title: 2025 Agreement Manager Release Notes
text:
The following fixes, new features, and product enhancements have been delivered since the last release.

| **Type** | **Category** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| New feature | Access | **Agreement Manager is GovRAMP authorized:** For customers in regulated industries, Agreement Manager is GovRAMP (Government Risk and Authorization Management Program) authorized. However, the Agreement Manager email ingestion feature is not supported for GovRAMP accounts.  Note: GovRAMP is a standardized cyber security framework used by state and local governments across the United States See the [Benefits and Features of Agreement Manager](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=ycv1702944444424.html&_LANG=enus "Read about Docusign Agreement Manager, a smart repository built specifically for agreements.") article to learn more about this authorization. |
| Enhancement | Analyze | **Delete custom agreement types:** Users can delete their unused and irrelevant custom agreement types. Once deleted, you cannot recover the custom agreement type:  A snippet of the Agreement Types view that highlights the Delete option for a custom agreement type.  - The documents that are currently associated with the agreement type remain intact. - The custom agreement type is not surfaced in new documents.  See these articles to learn more about this enhancement:  - [IAM Agreement Type and Field Management](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=uie1724446326038.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how agreement type management is being used to unify the way Docusign capitalizes on agreement types and associated fields. This topic answers the question: What is agreement type management and how does it benefit me?") - [Delete Unused Custom Agreement Types](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tske81e0c3b-34a3-4872-8ecf-e3a76a8950b5.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to delete custom agreement types that are no longer relevant.") |
| Enhancement | Analyze | **Add fields to multiple agreement types:** Custom and standard fields can be added to multiple agreement types at a time. When adding a field to a selected agreement type, use the Agreement types dropdown to select as many custom and standard agreement types as necessary:  A screenshot of the Agreement types dropdown that shows two selected agreement types to which the field will be added.  See these articles to learn more about this enhancement:  - [IAM Agreement Type and Field Management](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=uie1724446326038.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how agreement type management is being used to unify the way Docusign capitalizes on agreement types and associated fields. This topic answers the question: What is agreement type management and how does it benefit me?") - [Add Fields to Multiple Agreement Types](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tsk37e570f8-2428-4d26-a78b-b1239aca35a5.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to add fields to multiple agreement types from the Add a field dialog box. You can add fields to additional agreement types while you are updating a selected agreement type.") |
| Enhancement | Analyze | **All, Recent, and Starred (Parties page):** The tabs at the top of the Parties page give you quick access to your recently viewed and favorite parties and groups:  A snippet of the Parties page that highlights the All, Recent, and Starred tabs from the top of the page.  - Select the Recent tab to view your most recently viewed parties and groups. Up to 15 parties or groups are displayed in the table. - Select the Starred tab to access your favorite parties and groups. Each user can "star" up to 50 parties or groups.  See the [The Parties List](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=uzq1744241293689.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the enhanced Parties list, including access points and features.") article to learn more about the All, Recent, and Starred tabs. |
| Enhancement | Analyze | **Filter by "Party Side":** During processing, parties are identified as either the first-party (internal entity) or the third-party (external entity). Once identified, you can filter the Parties page to view agreement counts based on the party's side:  A screenshot of the Party Side filter that shows the two options, Internal and External.Expand the Party Side filter and choose to filter the party list table by Internal or External parties.  See the [The Parties List](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=uzq1744241293689.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the enhanced Parties list, including access points and features.") article to learn more about this enhancement. |
| Enhancement | Analyze | **Party hub data refresh:** The data that populates the party hub pages is updated every five minutes:  A snippet of the Parties page that highlights the data refresh message.  The data used to populate the party hub pages, including the Parties list, is refreshed every five minutes. If you've made recent changes, you will see the updated data shortly.  See the [IAM Party Hub](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=bpv1742428468463.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the IAM party hub, a centralized location for party-related pages. This topic answers the question: Where can I find a unified summary of all agreements and obligations for a customer or vendor?") article to learn more about this enhancement. |
| Enhancement | Analyze | **Party hub Overview dashboard:** The Overview dashboard displays a visual summary of all active agreements that are tied to a party or group, including:  - Party summary details - Agreement counts of the active, expiring soon, and renewing agreements for the next 90 days - Recent activity graph that shows your most recently active agreements over a 12-month period - Dashboard charts that show a breakdown of your agreement types, renewal types, payment terms, and obligations by due date  See these articles to learn more about the Overview dashboard:  - [IAM Party Hub](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=bpv1742428468463.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the IAM party hub, a centralized location for party-related pages. This topic answers the question: Where can I find a unified summary of all agreements and obligations for a customer or vendor?") - [View the Overview Dashboard for Selected Parties and Groups](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=dak1742429856906.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to view a visual summary of all agreements and obligations that are tied to a specific party or alias group.") |
| Enhancement | Act | **Expanded subscription support:** The *Subscription* feature provides visibility into subscription details for the individual users who were added to subscriptions by another user.  A screenshot of the Manage report subscriptions dialog box that's used to manage subscriptions, including those created by other users to which you were added.  The subscription is displayed in the Manage report subscriptions dialog box for the individual users who were added to subscriptions.  See the [Report Notifications and Subscriptions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=kwc1749570593935.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the notification and subscription features and how they can help you proactively manage your agreements, obligations, and renewals.") article to learn more about subscribing to report notifications. |
| Enhancement | Analyze | **Reporting hub Overview dashboard:** The Overview dashboard is the landing page for the reporting hub. The dashboard is updated in actual time to provide personalized, actionable insights at a glance.  Key features include:  - Find reports or dashboards: search for reports and dashboards by name - Your Recents: recently accessed reports - Your Favorites: starred reports and dashboards - Review your agreements: agreement insights, including agreement charts and an option to view the Agreements Dashboard - Explore Docusign Insights: links to renewal data, envelope metrics, and dashboard and report customizations - Resources: links to suggest new features and view help documentation  See the [Overview Dashboard](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=con36bb809c-725e-42fa-aeae-7b7e0f4faee4.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the Overview dashboard, including key components and available resources.") article to learn more about this dashboard. |
| Enhancement | Analyze | **Favorite (star) icon for dashboards and reports:** The Favorite (star) icon is displayed on every IAM dashboard and detailed report page:  A snippet of a detailed report view that highlights the Favorite (star) icon.  This feature gives you quick access to the insights that you regularly track. Use the Favorite (star) icon to add dashboards and reports to the Your Favorites section on the Overview dashboard.  See the [Star Your Favorite Reports and Dashboards](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tsk78ef32ef-890c-4e97-96e6-87f9a251d640.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to add your frequently used dashboards and reports to the Your Favorites section on the Overview dashboard.") article to learn more about "starring" your favorite reports and dashboards. |

title: 2025 Agreement Manager Release Notes
text:
The following fixes, new features, and product enhancements have been delivered since the last release.

| **Type** | **Category** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| New feature | Access | **Agreement Manager is FedRAMP authorized:** For customers in regulated industries, Agreement Manager is FedRAMP (Federal Risk and Authorization Management Program) authorized. However, the Agreement Manager email ingestion feature is not supported for FedRAMP accounts.    See the [FedRAMP: Docusign for Government](https://support.docusign.com/s/articles/FedRamp-FAQ) article to learn more about the FedRAMP authorization for the IAM platform. |
| Enhancement | Analyze | **Party role identification:** The Party Role is surfaced during agreement processing. This extraction identifies the relationship between parties within the context of an agreement. This includes:  - Buyer - Seller - Other  You can manually enter party role values from the Details pane on the Agreement preview page. Once identified, you can locate agreements, insights, and party data based on the Party Role.    See these articles to learn more about party roles:  - [The Agreement List Table](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=yjz1702945473104.html&_LANG=enus "This topic answers the question: What can I do from the agreement list table?") - [Filter for Agreements by Specific Party Role](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tsk6f2903c9-7ae7-4ad5-8acd-1c4948c98d10.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to update the agreement list table to view agreements based on the role played by a specific party.") - [View Your Buy-Side and Sell-Side Agreements](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tske5d68969-d052-4fd5-b50c-eb5bc8215e0a.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to use the Parties and Party Role filters to view your buy-side and sell-side agreements.") - [The Parties List](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=uzq1744241293689.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the enhanced Parties list, including access points and features.") |

title: 2025 Agreement Manager Release Notes
text: The following fixes, new features, and product enhancements have been delivered since the last release.

title: 2025 Agreement Manager Release Notes
text:
The following fixes, new features, and product enhancements have been delivered since the last release.

| **Type** | **Category** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Enhancement | Access | **Agreement set smart filters help you automate access for your teams:** The smart filters enhancement for agreement sets is now available to all users with an IAM plan that includes Agreement Manager.  Smart filters are used to add agreements to your sets automatically. This feature streamlines the agreement management process by updating sets based on agreement attribute values. Once created, sets can be shared with relevant users and groups.  During set creation or at any point afterwards, you can apply recurring Smart filters to an agreement set. When agreements match these filters, they are added to the set.  Smart filters support:  - Agreement types - Custom fields - Parties - Standard fields   Note: The smart filters feature does *not* support rolling date and duration-based filters.  See these support articles to learn more about smart filters:  - [Agreement Set Management](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=zed1730843285476.html&_LANG=enus "Read about agreement set management including how to use sets to control access to agreements. This topic answers the question: What are agreement sets and how can I use them?") - [Create an Agreement Set](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=jay1736181359265.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to create a new agreement set to ensure that the right people have access to relevant agreements. This topic answers the question: How can I make sure that the appropriate people have access to relevant agreements?") - [Edit Smart Filters for an Agreement Set](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=fev1750785436374.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to update the smart filters for an agreement set.") |
| Enhancement | Analyze | **Envelope ID column and field:** If an agreement was imported from a completed eSignature envelope, the ID is extracted during processing. The ID is displayed in:  - The Envelope ID column, located in the agreement list table (**Completed Documents** page) - The Envelope ID field, located in the Details pane (Agreement preview page)  The Envelope ID value is clickable. Select the ID to view the associated envelope details. Tip: Use the Customize columns feature to display and re-order the table columns as needed.  See the [Customize the Agreement List Table to Show Relevant Data](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=qrh1702945502942.html&_LANG=enus "This topic answers the question: How can I see relevant agreement data in the Agreement Manager table? Learn how to configure the columns in the agreement list table to display relevant data.") article to learn more about this feature.  See the [View Envelope Details from the Agreement List Table](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tsk15aecb4b-98ec-4b40-a739-61a3715cba02.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to view the envelope details for an agreement that was imported from a completed eSignature envelope.") article to learn more about viewing envelope details. |
| Enhancement | Access | **IAM party hub access point from CLM:** The IAM party hub is a centralized location for party-related pages. These pages provide a unified summary of all agreements and obligations for a party or group.  You can access the IAM party hub pages from Docusign CLM:  - Select Parties from the global header. The IAM party hub opens and the Parties view is displayed. - Select the PARTY name from the Attributes pane in the CLM document viewer. The IAM party hub opens and the Overview dashboard is displayed for the selected party or group.  See the [IAM Party Hub](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=bpv1742428468463.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the IAM party hub, a centralized location for party-related pages. This topic answers the question: Where can I find a unified summary of all agreements and obligations for a customer or vendor?") article to learn more about the IAM party hub. |

title: 2025 Agreement Manager Release Notes
text: The following fixes, new features, and product enhancements have been delivered since the last release.

title: 2025 Agreement Manager Release Notes
text:
The following fixes, new features, and product enhancements have been delivered since the last release.

| **Type** | **Category** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Feature | Standardize | **On-demand processing for custom data:** When new data points are available, you can reprocess a group of selected agreements to surface the custom data.  For example, new custom agreement types and fields were enabled for AI detection. You want to reprocess agreements that are likely to contain this data.  Select the agreements that you want to process from the table on the Completed Documents page and click Update Details.  See the [Custom Extractions and Field Detection](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=yrx1747083411531.html&_LANG=enus "Read about custom extractions, including how these data points bring value to our organization.") article to learn more about this process. |
| Enhancement | Analyze | **Custom dashboards and reports:** Customizable dashboards and reports give you control over the agreement insights that you see - giving you the right data to make smarter decisions.  - *Custom dashboards* can be created from scratch or build from using an existing template. - *Custom reports* can be created from scratch by choosing the relevant data, metrics, and visualization options. They can also be created by updating and saving a standard report.  See the [Custom Dashboards and Reports](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=conac48cc6c-d92c-4187-a733-08968c218a74.html&_LANG=enus "Read about custom dashboards and reports, including use cases and limitations.") article to learn more about customizing dashboards and reports. |
| Enhancement | Find | **Dismiss merge suggestions:** When users dismiss merge suggestions, those rejections are remembered by the system and that combination of parties is not suggested again.  See the [Duplicate Party Management and Standardization](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=ssf1712255476566.html&_LANG=enus "This topic answers the question: What does party management in Agreement Manager entail? Read about the party management features designed to help you manage parties that have similar names. For example, party standardization and consolidation at the point of ingestion.") article to learn more about dismissing merge suggestions. |
| Enhancement | Find | **Party sides:** During processing, parties are identified as either the first-party or the third-party:  - First-party designation, *Internal*, indicates the user's organization - Third-party designation, *External*, indicates external entities  The Party Side column is displayed in the party list table on the Manage parties page. This information provides the context to manage relationships, risks, and obligations effectively.  See the [Duplicate Party Management and Standardization](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=ssf1712255476566.html&_LANG=enus "This topic answers the question: What does party management in Agreement Manager entail? Read about the party management features designed to help you manage parties that have similar names. For example, party standardization and consolidation at the point of ingestion.") article to learn more about the party sides enhancement. |
| Enhancement | Search | **Natural language (NL) search query beta:** The NL search query function is available in beta. See the *Opt-in for NL search (Join Beta)* section of the [Natural Language Search Queries](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=fas1739987568042.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how natural language search queries, including components of the Search Results view.") article to join the beta. |
| Enhancement | Standardize | **Support for custom dropdown fields:** Agreement Manager supports four custom field types: Text, Number, Date, and Dropdown.  When using the Dropdown field type, you must provide a list of options for the dropdown.  See the [Custom Extractions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=yrx1747083411531.html&_LANG=enus "Read about custom extractions, including how these data points bring value to our organization.") article to learn more about custom fields. |
| Enhancement | Standardize | **Expanded agreement type management functionality:** Agreement types and custom fields can be updated, when necessary, to meet new use cases. This includes creating and maintaining custom types and fields.  Agreement type and field management tasks include:  - Edit agreement type details - Edit custom field details - Add fields to agreement types - Remove custom fields from agreement types - Update the dropdown options for a custom field  See the [Maintain Your Custom Extractions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=conb70da869-2165-47b3-89a5-e44371c76bb1.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the typical maintenance tasks related to your custom extractions.") article to learn more about updating agreement types and fields. |
| Enhancement | Ingestion | **Import email attachments (U.S. only):** Executed agreements can be imported directly from email attachments into Agreement Manager. This streamlined approach to ingestion reduces manual effort, improves data accuracy, and drives better collaboration.  Restriction: Email ingestion is currently available to U.S.-based customers only. Users outside of the U.S. will experience the existing user interface until this feature is generally available in early 2026.  Include the repository's unique email address in your ongoing email threads to import the attached documents automatically.  See the [Import Documents from Email Attachments](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=ask1749568023897.html&_LANG=enus "This topic answers the question: How do I add documents from email attachments to Agreement Manager? Learn how to import documents from email attachments to Agreement Manager for processing.") article to learn more about importing email attachments. |
| Enhancement | Ingestion | **Bulk user experience for single document uploads:** When users with the *bulk upload* permission perform a single document upload (fewer than five documents), they are prompted to assign access to the documents  See these articles to learn more about the upload process:  - [Upload Documents to Agreement Manager](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=czx1712255764999.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to use the Upload documents function to upload agreements to Agreement Manager. This topic answers the question: How do I upload documents to Agreement Manager?") - [Bulk Upload Documents and Set Access Controls](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=leq1712700300298.html&_LANG=enus "This topic answers the question: How do I bulk upload documents to Agreement Manager? Learn how to use the bulk upload function to upload more than five documents to Agreement Manager.") |

title: 2025 Agreement Manager Release Notes
text:
The following fixes, new features, and product enhancements are being delivered with this release.

| **Type** | **Category** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Enhancement | Access | **Agreements Dashboard supports filtering by Sets:** The Agreements Dashboard reports can be filtered to show report data for agreements in a selected set:  - Go to the report view for one of the charts or insights on the Agreements Dashboard. - Expand the Sets filter and select the relevant set. - Click Apply.  The report updates and the data for the agreements in the selected set is displayed.  See the [View Report Data for a Selected Set of Agreements](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tskf1577eb7-c826-4181-8a66-d0e9ff353b86.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to filter an agreement report to display data for a selected set of agreements.") article to learn more about filtering agreement reports by sets. |
| Enhancement | Act | **Document-level notifications:** Document-level email notifications help you stay informed about upcoming milestones for individual documents.  Select the Set a notification (bell) icon and choose to be notified of major agreement milestones:  - Renewal notice dates - Expiration dates  See the [Set Document-Level Renewal Notifications](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tsk62370c10-0d3b-4218-a325-4383b9d5f7cd.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to set and manage notifications to track upcoming milestones for an individual document.") article to learn more about enabling these notifications.  Note: If a notification already exists for an agreement, the Manage notifications (bell) icon is displayed. Select this icon to update the existing notifications for the selected agreement. |
| Enhancement | Access | **Account-level audit includes agreement set events:** The account-level Activity Log includes audit events related to agreement set sharing and operations.  Set operations can include set creation and management operations. For example, updating a set name or adjusting the smart filters.  See the [Agreement Manager Activity Logs](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=pne1747684858805.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the account- and agreement-level activity logs, including the types of events that are captured in the log.") article to learn more about the type of audit events that are captured at the account-level. |
| Enhancement | Analyze | **Notification obligations:** Obligation management supports a number of obligation types that can be created from an agreement, including **Notification** obligations.  This type of obligation is a legal or contractual requirement for one party to inform another party about specific events, circumstances, or actions.  See the [Obligation Management](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=csm1727128259486.html&_LANG=enus "Read about obligation management, including how to use this functionality to capture, track, and fulfill your agreement obligations. This topic answers the question: How can I find and track my agreement obligations?") article to learn more about supported obligation types. |

title: 2025 Agreement Manager Release Notes
text:
The following fixes, new features, and product enhancements are being delivered with this release.

| **Type** | **Category** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Enhancement | Access | **Smart filters for agreement sets:** You can set up smart filters that add agreements to your sets automatically. This feature streamlines the agreement management process by updating sets based on agreement attribute values.  During set creation, complete the Smart filters step to apply recurring filters to the agreement set. When new agreements match these smart filters, they are added to the set.  Note: The smart filters feature does *not* support rolling date and duration-based filters.  See the [Agreement Set Management](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=zed1730843285476.html&_LANG=enus "Read about agreement set management including how to use sets to control access to agreements. This topic answers the question: What are agreement sets and how can I use them?") article to learn more about smart filters. |
| Fix | Standardize | **Missing "Expiration Date" value:** The "Expiration Date" was not being surfaced in uploaded or ingested documents.  **Resolution:** The "Expiration Date" value is now accurately extracted from quotes, purchase orders, invoice, and order forms. |

title: 2025 Agreement Manager Release Notes
text:
The following fixes, new features, and product enhancements are being delivered with this release.

| **Type** | **Category** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Enhancement | Upload and Import | **File size limit for uploading and ingestion documents:** The file size limit for uploading or ingesting documents to Agreement Manager was increased from 25 MB to 100 MB per file. |
| Enhancement | Standardize | **"Languages" in filters and tables:** During processing, suggested Languages are surfaced in your agreements.  Once surfaced, you can filter your agreements by language, including customizing the table data to display agreement languages.  Tip: See the [Language detection and processing](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=xvp1729795957126.html&anchor=xvp1729795957126__section_sxx_xqz_ffc&_LANG=enus) article to learn more about how languages are detected in your agreements. |
| Fix | Standardize | **Incorrect "Automatic Renewal" extraction:** "Automatic Renewal" was mistakenly extracted in documents that explicitly stated that the agreement would *not* auto-renew.  **Resolution:** "Automatic Renewal" is only extracted when it is truly present in a document, preventing misinterpretations from negative language. |
| Fix | Standardize | **Filtering out generic party names:** Generic party names were being surfaced in uploaded and ingested documents.  **Resolution:** Generic party names are filtered out and not surfaced in documents. For example, "Supplier," "Agency," "Company," "Customer," "Vendor," and "Partner" are no longer extracted from documents as party names. |
| Enhancement | Standardize | **Improved detection within the "Miscellaneous" agreement types:** The agreement type detection has been improved to more accurately identify the type within "Miscellaneous" agreements. For example:   - "Order Form" to "Form" - "Event" to "Marketing" - "Order Form" to "Invoice" |

title: 2025 Agreement Manager Release Notes
text:
The following fixes, new features, and product enhancements are being delivered with this release.

| **Type** | **Category** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Enhancement | Analyze | **Streamlined Reports view:** The navigation pane on the left-side of the Reports view has been reorganized in a more efficient and user-friendly layout:   - Dashboards section: Lists the available envelope, agreements, obligations and renewals dashboards. - Reports: Groups available reports under category tabs, such as All Envelope, Envelope, Recipient, Usage, Custom, and Web Forms.   Note: See the [IAM Dashboards and Reports](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=jyq1749000353004.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the Insights hub, including an overview of the available dashboards and reports.") article to learn more about the Reports feature and how to use it to gain valuable insights into your data. |
| Enhancement | Standardize | **Spanish and Brazilian Portuguese beta program:** Starting in July 2025, AI-assisted insights will be available in beta for agreements in Spanish and Brazilian Portuguese.  Customers with IAM plans that include Agreement Manager can request beta access.  Notice: See the [Language and Regional Support](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=xvp1729795957126.html&_LANG=enus "Read about language support and regional availability for Agreement Manager AI models and features.") article to learn more about the beta program. |

title: 2025 Agreement Manager Release Notes
text:
The following fixes, new features, and product enhancements are being delivered with this release.

| **Type** | **Category** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Enhancement | Access | **Account-level activity log:** The account-level Activity Log provides a centralized location to view aggregate, user-performed events across all the agreements in an account:   - Agreement uploads - Attribute (field) updates - Document access (sharing) updates - Document views and downloads   This Activity Log is accessed from the Completed Documents view. Expand the More actions (three dots) menu and select View Activity Log. |
| Enhancement | Access | **Download CSV for activity logs:** The Download CSV option is available from the account- and agreement-level activity logs. Use this button to download user-performed events for:   - All the agreements in an account (account-level) - An individual agreement (agreement-level)   See the [Agreement Manager Activity Logs](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=pne1747684858805.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the account- and agreement-level activity logs, including the types of events that are captured in the log.") article to learn more about these activity logs. |
| Enhancement | Access | **Agreement set sharing notifications:** A screenshot of the Add users dialog box that highlights the Notify users by email option. When you share an agreement set with other users or groups, the Notify users by email option is selected by default. Leave this option selected to notify users that an agreement set was shared. |
| Enhancement | Find | **Document count:** The Completed Documents view displays the total count of documents to which the logged-in user has access. This count is located in the bottom-right corner of the view.  The document count is dynamically updated based on the user's access permissions, entered search criteria, and applied filters. |
| Enhancement | Standardize | **Language detection in agreements:** During processing, suggested Languages are surfaced in your agreements.  These suggestions are listed in the Details pane on the Agreement preview pane. Be sure to validate all the data points that were surfaced in your agreement.  Once surfaced, you can filter your agreements by language, including customizing the table data to display agreement languages.  Tip: See the [Language detection and processing](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=xvp1729795957126.html&anchor=xvp1729795957126__section_sxx_xqz_ffc&_LANG=enus) section to learn more about how languages are detected in your agreements. |
| Enhancement | Analyze | **Global entry for the party hub:** A snippet of the Completed Documents view that highlights the Parties option in the navigation pane. The Parties global entry point provides a centralized, relationship-centric experience where users can access key information, insights, and actions.  Access the party hub from the Agreements page. Select Parties from the navigation pane on the left side of the page. |

title: 2025 Agreement Manager Release Notes
text:
The following fixes, new features, and product enhancements are being delivered with this release.

| **Type** | **Category** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Enhancement | Upload | **Bulk upload supports agreement sets:** The bulk upload feature now includes the ability for users to organize and manage their agreements at scale during ingestion. Contract managers, and anyone with the appropriate access, can organize agreements into flexible sets during the bulk upload process. This enhancement aims to boost internal efficiency.  Note: Bulk uploads and agreement set management require two Agreement Manager permissions:  - Upload up to 1,000 files at a time - Organize and manage all agreements   Users must have both permissions to use agreement sets during the bulk upload process. |
| Enhancement | Standardize | **Language detection in agreements:** During processing, suggested Languages are surfaced in your agreements. These suggestions are listed in the Details pane on the Agreement preview pane. Be sure to validate all the data points that were surfaced in your agreement.  Once surfaced, you can filter your agreements by language, including customizing the table data to display agreement languages.  Tip: See the [Language detection and processing](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=xvp1729795957126.html&anchor=xvp1729795957126__section_sxx_xqz_ffc&_LANG=enus) section to learn more about how languages are detected in your agreements. |
| Feature | Analyze | **Obligation type management:** Custom obligation types are unique categories of obligations that are tailored to meet organizational or departmental needs. These obligations can relate to compliance, performance, or other critical contract aspects. The Manage Obligation Types feature gives users access to:   - Create new (custom) obligation types - Rename custom obligation types - Hide and un-hide custom and standard obligation types   The Organize and manage all agreements permission is required for this feature. |
| Enhancement | Access, View | **UX enhancements:** The Agreement preview page now displays:  - The Close (X) icon in the top-left corner when you access this page from the Completed Documents view. Select the Close (X) icon to return to the Completed Documents view. Any applied filters or entered search criteria are still applied. - The Back arrow in the top-left corner when you access the page from other locations. Select the Back arrow to return to the previous page. For example, if you accessed the Agreement preview page from the Obligations dashboard, you are returned to that location. |

title: 2025 Agreement Manager Release Notes
text:
The following fixes, new features, and product enhancements are being delivered with this release.

| **Type** | **Category** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Enhancement | Access, Analyze | **Guided flow and onboarding callouts:** As a new user, a series of onboarding callouts is displayed when you first access Agreement Manager. These callouts provide a quick tour of Agreement Manager and walk you through a guided flow, starting from the Completed Documents page.  Featured items include:   - Completed agreements in the File Name column - Parties filter options - Renewing contracts banner and messaging - Document upload feature - Agreement processing for missing details - Data review for attribute (field) suggestions |
| Enhancement | Upload and import | **Document uploads from cloud storage locations:** Users can connect to third-party cloud storage apps directly from the upload process: A screenshot of the Select File dropdown that shows the installed apps and options to add connections to them.  Note: An administrator must install the cloud storage apps before users can use the app to upload files. See the [File Ingestion from Third-Party Cloud Storage Providers](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=byx1727376126901.html) article to learn more about enabling apps. |
| Enhancement | Find | **Party management:** The party list table on the Parties page displays agreement counts for each party, including:  - Number of active agreements - Number of expiring agreements - Number of renewing agreements - Number of agreements taking effect |

title: 2025 Agreement Manager Release Notes
text: The following fixes, new features, and product enhancements are being delivered with this release.

title: 2025 Agreement Manager Release Notes
text:
The following fixes, new features, and product enhancements are being delivered with this release.

| **Type** | **Category** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Enhancement | Standardize | **Agreement type management:** New Docusign-provided agreement types and limits are available.  - **10 new non-agreement types:** Non-agreement types are used to categorize non-contractual documents that are uploaded and synced to Agreement Manager. Supported types include: Form, policy, application, certificate, report, notice, plan, invoice, handbook, summary, and non-agreement/other. - **Limits and restrictions:** Warning messages are displayed when you reach the total number of custom agreement types and fields allowed per account. |
| Feature | View and organize | **Bulk edit feature:** Select and edit multiple agreements from the agreement list table (Completed Documents view). This feature currently supports bulk editing the agreement type for a group of selected agreements. |
| Enhancement | Standardize | **Custom fields:** Create custom fields from within agreement type management. Custom fields can be added to both standard and custom agreement types. |
| Feature | Standardize | **AI detection for custom agreement types and fields:** Enable or improve AI detection for custom agreement types and fields for more precise searches and reporting. |
| Enhancement | Analyze | **Global attribute support:** A number of additional attributes (fields) are now supported globally in English, French, and German. See the [Language and Regional Support](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=xvp1729795957126.html&_LANG=enus "Read about language support and regional availability for Agreement Manager AI models and features.") article to learn more about the supported attributes and insights. |
| Enhancement | Analyze | **Job monitoring hub:** The Monitor All Jobs feature gives you access to job details for agreement uploads and processing jobs. This job monitoring hub provides a centralized location to view and track jobs:   - The Uploaded view displays job details for uploaded and synced agreements. These jobs are initiated when agreements are added to Agreement Manager. - The Processing view displays details for AI processing jobs. These jobs are triggered when documents are processed for new attributes or fields. Note: The Processing tab is dependent on regional availability and is tied to the roll out of custom field detection. If you do not have access to this feature, the tab is not displayed. |
| Enhancement | Find | **Natural language search queries:** Natural language (NL) queries offer a more intuitive and efficient way for you to find relevant agreement information.  Enter a question into the search field to receivea list of relevant agreements. |
| Feature | Analyze | **Party Hub - Overview dashboard:** The "party hub" displays a unified summary of all agreements and obligations that are tied to a party or alias group. The Overview dashboard displays a visual summary of all active agreements for a specific party or group:   - Top-level insights show a count of all active, expiring, and renewing agreements. - Dashboard charts show renewal types, payment terms, and obligations by due date for a specified time period. - View and download the reports for the top-level insights and charts. |
| Enhancement | Entry point | **Agreement Manager moved to the "Completed" view:**  - **New entry point:** Agreement Manager is now accessed by selecting Completed instead of the Agreement Manager option. - **Switch to completed envelopes:** Once users select Completed, the Agreement Manager view is displayed by default. Use the Select a View dropdown to switch the display to show a list of completed envelopes.   Note: The Envelopes section, under the Agreements global header, was renamed to Agreements and it contains both documents and envelopes.  These changes will be live for all Agreement Manager customers by April 3, 2025. |

title: 2025 Agreement Manager Release Notes
text:
The following fixes, new features, and product enhancements are being delivered with this release.

| **Type** | **Category** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Enhancement | Access | **Agreement-level audit:**  - **New recorded event:** User-performed attribute updates are now recorded in the Activity Log. When users update the attribute values for select attributes, the event is recorded in the log. - **Additional entry point in Agreement Manager:** The Activity Log can be accessed from the Actions menu in the agreement list table (Completed Documents view). |
| Enhancement | View and organize | **Agreement preview and data review:**  - On the Details pane, attributes are grouped by usage and a progress bar displays the number of suggested values for each group. Verify all the suggested values. - Groups with no suggested values are collapsed by default. - An image of the Review completed checkmark indicator that signifies the suggestion has been reviewed. Once a suggested value is accepted, the Review completed (checkmark) indicator is displayed to indicate that the attribute was reviewed. - Use the thumbs up and thumbs down icons to rate the accuracy of the suggested values. |
| Enhancement | Entry point | **Agreement Manager moving to the Completed view:**  - **New entry point:** Agreement Manager is now accessed by selecting Completed instead of the Agreement Manager option. - **Switch to completed envelopes:** Once users select Completed, the Agreement Manager view is displayed by default. Use the Select a View dropdown to switch the display to show a list of completed envelopes.   Note: The Envelopes section, under the Agreements global header, was renamed to Agreements and it contains both documents and envelopes.  These changes are live for a subset of customers as of early March. All Agreement Manager customers will see these changes by early April. |

title: 2025 Agreement Manager Release Notes
text:
The following fixes, new features, and product enhancements are being delivered with this release.

| **Type** | **Category** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Enhancement | Access | **Agreement-level audit:** An agreement-level activity log is available from inside of Agreement Manager. Users with Can Manage access to a document can view the associated activity log. The Activity Log records and displays agreement-level events that were performed by a user, such as:  - Access updates - Agreement downloads - Agreement views - Document uploads |

title: 2025 Agreement Manager Release Notes
text:
The following fixes, new features, and product enhancements are being delivered with this release.

| **Type** | **Category** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Enhancement | Find | **Party management:** The party profile page gives you visibility into the agreements and obligations tied to a specific party or party alias group. |
| Feature | Access | **Agreement sets:** Contract managers, and anyone with the appropriate access level, can organize agreements into flexible sets to manage access at scale and boost internal efficiency. |
| Feature | Analyze | **Obligation management:** Contractual data points, or attributes, are surfaced either manually or with AI and provide an in-depth view of the obligations contained in all agreement types. These insights empower contract managers to capture, track, and fulfill obligations from signature to expiration. |
| Feature | Analyze | **Payment obligation tracking:** Stay on top of payments and maximize agreement value with AI. New payment obligation insights are available to help organizations reduce procurement risks, enhance operational efficiency, and improve financial impact. |
| Enhancement | Analyze | **Opt-in for agreement processing:** As new AI-assisted insights are released to support additional use cases, agreements can be processed with AI again to surface the newly added data points. Users must *opt in* to start processing new and existing agreements to surface additional data points with AI. |
| Enhancement | Analyze | **Subscribe to reports:** Subscribe to receive any of Agreement Manager's pre-built agreement, obligation, and renewal reports by email and enable other users or groups to receive them too. |
| Feature | Access | **Agreement sharing notifications:** Automatically receive an email whenever a user directly shares an agreement with you. |

title: Troubleshoot Spreadsheet Upload Errors
text:
Look up troubleshooting steps for resolving errors during the metadata spreadsheet upload.

Look up troubleshooting steps to resolve your validation errors from the metadata spreadsheet upload.

title: Troubleshoot Spreadsheet Upload Errors
heading_path: Error: Invalid file. Upload the CSV file you generated in Docusign.
text:
If you attempt to upload external spreadsheets, including files generated from other Intelligent Agreement Management (IAM) features, the files are rejected:

![A screenshot of the Import data from a spreadsheet dialog box that shows the "Invalid file" error message.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Bulk-metadata-editing_Import-data-from-spreadsheet_Invalid-file.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY3ODgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.VReYtu1_7vAeAJAFiZeCIj73u3C9yQv4lUnMV2qwnZo&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Import data from a spreadsheet dialog box that shows the \"Invalid file\" error message.")

**Resolution**

Use the Select File option to search for and select the pre-formatted spreadsheet file. This is the file that you downloaded from Agreement Manager and updated with agreement data for the selected agreements.

Complete these tasks to start with a fresh spreadsheet file:

1. [Download the Pre-Formatted Spreadsheet File](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tsk1b94afc6-712f-4d71-868e-0787867354d8.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to download the pre-formatted spreadsheet file for a group of documents.")
2. [Edit the Metadata Spreadsheet](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tsk8710c3f2-c881-4219-96b0-2b287e8b4e03.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to edit the metadata spreadsheet file to bulk update the agreement data for the selected documents.")
3. [Import Your Edited Metadata Spreadsheet](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tsk5ab1cd5b-00f7-42a9-bd77-728baadff903.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to import your edited metadata spreadsheet using the Add (+) menu located on the Agreement Manager homepage.")

title: Troubleshoot Spreadsheet Upload Errors
heading_path: Error: You need permission to edit this agreement
text:
This error message is displayed if you do not have permission to edit the metadata for an agreement. Any of these permissions grant users access to update the agreement metadata:

- Can Manage access to the agreement
- Can Edit access to the agreement
- Organize and manage all agreements access to Agreement Manager agreements and functionality

**Resolution**

Remove the agreement from the metadata spreadsheet file and re-import the data:

1. Open the metadata file and locate the row that contains the relevant agreement.
2. Delete the row from the spreadsheet.
3. Save your changes and close the spreadsheet.
4. Upload the spreadsheet file to Agreement Manager.

title: Troubleshoot Spreadsheet Upload Errors
heading_path: Error: [Field Name] must follow the format: [Number][Space][Currency]
text:
This error message is displayed when a field has an invalid currency format:

![A snippet of the Job status page that highlights the error message for a file that could not be updated.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Bulk-metadata-editing_Job-status_Error-details_NumberSpaceCurrency.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY3ODgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.VReYtu1_7vAeAJAFiZeCIj73u3C9yQv4lUnMV2qwnZo&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Job status page that highlights the error message for a file that could not be updated.")

**Resolution**

Update the cell value in the metadata spreadsheet file and re-import the data:

1. Open the metadata file and locate the row that contains the relevant agreement.
2. Scroll to locate the column and cell for the field with the invalid currency format.
3. Update the value so it follows the required format, [Number][Space][Currency code]. For example, 10000 USD.
4. Save your changes and close the spreadsheet.
5. Upload the spreadsheet file to Agreement Manager.

title: Troubleshoot Spreadsheet Upload Errors
heading_path: Error: [Field Name] must be a valid date and follow the format: MM/DD/YYYY
text:
This error message is displayed when a field has an invalid date format. The date follows the U.S. format, MM/DD/YYYY. It is not localized based on the user's location.

![A snippet of the Job status page that highlights the error message for a file that could not be updated.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Bulk-metadata-editing_Job-status_Error-details_MM-DD-YYYY.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY3ODgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.VReYtu1_7vAeAJAFiZeCIj73u3C9yQv4lUnMV2qwnZo&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Job status page that highlights the error message for a file that could not be updated.")

**Resolution**

Update the cell value in the metadata spreadsheet file and re-import the data:

1. Open the metadata file and locate the row that contains the relevant agreement.
2. Scroll to locate the column and cell for the field with the invalid date format.
3. Update the value so it follows the required format, MM/DD/YYYY.
4. Save your changes and close the spreadsheet.
5. Upload the spreadsheet file to Agreement Manager.

title: Troubleshoot Spreadsheet Upload Errors
heading_path: Error: [Field Name] must follow the format: [Number][Space][Time Unit]
text:
This error message is displayed when a field has an invalid duration format:

![A snippet of the Job status page that highlights the error message for a file that could not be updated.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Bulk-metadata-editing_Job-status_Error-details_NumberSpaceTimeUnit.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY3ODgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.VReYtu1_7vAeAJAFiZeCIj73u3C9yQv4lUnMV2qwnZo&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Job status page that highlights the error message for a file that could not be updated.")

**Resolution**

Update the cell value in the metadata spreadsheet file and re-import the data:

1. Open the metadata file and locate the row that contains the relevant agreement.
2. Scroll to locate the column and cell for the field with the invalid duration format.
3. Update the value so it follows the required format, [Number][Space][Time Unit].

   Note: Common time units are: Years, Months, Weeks, and Days. The time unit value is case-sensitive. Capitalize the first letter. For example, "5 Years" instead of "5 Y" or "5 years".
4. Save your changes and close the spreadsheet.
5. Upload the spreadsheet file to Agreement Manager.

title: Troubleshoot Spreadsheet Upload Errors
heading_path: Error: [Field Name] must follow the format: [Name] | [Role]
text:
This error message is displayed when a Parties cell contains an invalid format or is missing information:

![A snippet of the Job status page that highlights the error message for a file that could not be updated.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Bulk-metadata-editing_Job-status_Error-details_NameRole.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY3ODgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.VReYtu1_7vAeAJAFiZeCIj73u3C9yQv4lUnMV2qwnZo&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Job status page that highlights the error message for a file that could not be updated.")

**Resolution**

Update the cell value in the metadata spreadsheet file and re-import the data:

1. Open the metadata file and locate the row that contains the relevant agreement.
2. Scroll to locate the column and cell for the field with the invalid parties format.
3. Update the value so it follows the required format:

   1. Enter the party name and role, separated with the pipe (vertical line) divider. For example, Fontara | Buyer.

      Important: The Role is required when populating the Parties column. If you are unsure of the role or do not want to include one, use the format: [Party Name] | [None]. For example, Fontara | None.
   2. Enter additional party names and roles as needed. Use semicolons to separate the party values. For example, Fontara | Buyer; Liberty Innovations | Seller;
4. Save your changes and close the spreadsheet.
5. Upload the spreadsheet file to Agreement Manager.

title: Troubleshoot Spreadsheet Upload Errors
heading_path: Error: [Field Name] must be a valid number
text:
This error message is displayed when a field has an invalid number format. Numbers are entered without commas.

Note: Decimals are approved where the field supports them. For example, 2500.75.

**Resolution**

Update the cell value in the metadata spreadsheet file and re-import the data:

1. Open the metadata file and locate the row that contains the relevant agreement.
2. Scroll to locate the column and cell for the field with the invalid number format.
3. Update the value so it follows the required format. Enter digits only, no commas.
4. Save your changes and close the spreadsheet.
5. Upload the spreadsheet file to Agreement Manager.

title: Troubleshoot Spreadsheet Upload Errors
text:
This error message is displayed if one of your cell values exceeds the maximum character limit for a text field. The spreadsheet supports up to 2,000 characters for custom fields and 400 characters for standard fields.

Important: Depending on how the standard fields are used across the platform, the maximum character limit can vary.

**Resolution**

Update the cell value in the metadata spreadsheet file and re-import the data:

1. Open the metadata file and locate the row that contains the relevant agreement.
2. Scroll to locate the cell that exceeds the maximum character limit.
3. Edit the value so that it is less than the maximum character limit.
4. Save your changes and close the spreadsheet.
5. Upload the spreadsheet file to Agreement Manager.

title: Troubleshoot Spreadsheet Upload Errors
heading_path: Error: Something went wrong when attempting to update the agreement
text:
This generic error message is displayed when an unidentified error occurs. Unidentified errors are issues that do not match any of the specific cases that are logged:

![A snippet of the Job status page that highlights the error message for a file that could not be updated.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Bulk-metadata-editing_Job-status_Error-details_Something-went-wrong.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY3ODgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.VReYtu1_7vAeAJAFiZeCIj73u3C9yQv4lUnMV2qwnZo&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Job status page that highlights the error message for a file that could not be updated.")

**Resolution**

If you receive this error message, try to upload the file again. If the error message persists, contact your system administrator for help. Your administrators have access to logs on their end to triage and monitor such cases.

title: 2026 Agreement Manager Release Notes
text:
Look up past release note entries for Docusign Agreement Manager features, bug fixes, and other changes.

Note: The features and enhancements referenced in this topic are available in Demo accounts, Production accounts, or both. Availability is dependent on the ramp schedule and can vary across regions and languages.

title: 2026 Agreement Manager Release Notes
text:
The following fixes, new features, and product enhancements have been delivered since the last release.

| **Type** | **Category** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Feature | Analyze | **Bulk metadata editing:** Bulk edit your agreement data with a Docusign-generated spreadsheet file:  - Download a pre-formatted spreadsheet file for a group of selected documents. - Edit the spreadsheet to include the new metadata values. - Return to Agreement Manager and import the file.  Once successfully processed, the new and updated values are reflected in the selected documents. See the [Bulk Metadata Editing](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=concb493b18-adf8-4fe5-8b99-dd90cbc28399.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the Bulk Metadata Editing feature, including use cases, typical roles and permissions, and a process overview.") article to learn more about this feature. |

title: 2026 Agreement Manager Release Notes
text: The following fixes, new features, and product enhancements have been delivered since the last release.

title: 2026 Agreement Manager Release Notes
text:
The following fixes, new features, and product enhancements have been delivered since the last release.

| **Type** | **Category** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Enhancement | Access | **Agreement access level changes:** By default, users have Can Manage access to their completed agreements, including documents ingested from eSignature:  A screenshot of the agreement access permissions for the direct sharing function.  - Senders, signers, and recipients are given Can Manage access to their completed documents that are synced from eSignature. - Users who currently have Can Edit access to their agreements will be updated to Can Manage access.  Users with Can Manage access have full control over the agreement and can add it to agreement sets. See the [Agreement Sharing and Downloads](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=hrr1722978886188.html&_LANG=enus "This topic answers the question: What are my direct sharing and download options for Agreement Manager documents, including the required permissions?") article to learn more about agreement access levels. |
| Enhancement | Analyze | **Party name clean-up:** Duplicate party management helps keep party data clean and consistent. Party names are identified and standardized during ingestion. When duplicate parties are detected, you are prompted to group the parties under one preferred party name.  Note: The standardization process ensures that parties are not considered distinct because of case sensitivity, whitespaces, punctuation, and legal entity descriptors.  See the [Duplicate Party Management and Standardization](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=ssf1712255476566.html&_LANG=enus "This topic answers the question: What does party management in Agreement Manager entail? Read about the party management features designed to help you manage parties that have similar names. For example, party standardization and consolidation at the point of ingestion.") article to learn more about this enhancement. |
| Enhancement | Access | **Intelligent Agreement Management (IAM) Permission Profiles:** Permission profiles are groups of settings that determine the behavior and actions available for a user.  When creating or updating permission profiles, administrators have the option to include Intelligent Agreement Management (IAM) permissions, such as Agreement Manager Permissions:  A screenshot of the Add Permission Profile dialog box that highlights the available Agreement Manager Permissions.  See the [Permission Profiles](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=pof1583277362435.html) article to learn more about using permission profiles. |
| Enhancement | Access | **Expanded administrator permissions:** Account administrators automatically have full secure access to all Agreement Manager features by default. This access includes bulk ingestion and agreement set management functionality. For example, administrators can use agreement sets to ensure that only the appropriate users have access to sensitive documents.  Restriction: While administrators automatically have full access to all the Agreement Manager features, they do not have access to view the agreements. Access is explicitly assigned to administrators for them to view any of the agreements in the account. See the [Agreement Sharing and Downloads](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=hrr1722978886188.html&_LANG=enus "This topic answers the question: What are my direct sharing and download options for Agreement Manager documents, including the required permissions?") article to learn more about sharing agreements with others.  See the [Agreement Manager Permissions, Profiles, and Roles](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=eut1712157553977.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the available Agreement Manager permissions, including how users and groups are granted access to Agreement Manager agreements and example roles for each permission.") article to learn more about this enhancement. |
| Enhancement | Act | **In-product "Insight" bar and card prompts:** The Insights bar is an interactive deck of cards on the Agreement Manager homepage that surfaces relevant, actionable insights and prompts. These cards include links to reports or next steps.  During Agreement Manager onboarding, administrators are prompted to assign a contract manager if one hasn't already been assigned:  A screenshot of the Insights card that prompts administrators to assign at least one contract manager.  Once assigned, both administrators and contract managers are prompted to:  - Create agreement sets to secure access to sensitive documents - Run the latest AI model to reprocess agreements for the latest extractions and insights  See the [Agreement Manager Permissions, Profiles, and Roles](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=eut1712157553977.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the available Agreement Manager permissions, including how users and groups are granted access to Agreement Manager agreements and example roles for each permission.") article to learn more about this enhancement. |
| Enhancement | Analyze | **Suggested reports (Overview dashboard):** The Overview dashboard includes Suggested Reports (Beta) and Weekly Insights.  - The Suggested Reports (Beta) section on the Overview dashboard provides AI-generated custom report notifications. These suggestions are auto-generated based on the custom fields in your account. When fields (custom or standard) are located in at least five agreements, they are included when generating custom report suggestions. - The Weekly Insights section displays the agreement counts for all the agreements in the account, including All agreements, Expiring agreements, and Upcoming renewals.  See the [Overview Dashboard](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=con36bb809c-725e-42fa-aeae-7b7e0f4faee4.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the Overview dashboard, including key components and available resources.") article to learn more about these enhancements. |
