| Enhancement | Analyze | **Dashboard bar charts (viewing options):** Depending on the number of data points, the dashboard bar charts have two viewing options:  A screenshot of a dashboard chart that shows the Stacked Bar Chart view.  - Bar Chart view: Shows a single metric per category for each time bucket. - Stacked Bar Chart view: Shows the same totals, but each bar is segmented to show the composition by a second field.  See the [IAM Dashboards and Reports](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=jyq1749000353004.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the Insights hub, including an overview of the available dashboards and reports.") article to learn more about this enhancement. |
| Feature | Analyze | **IAM Agreement Types Manager:** The *Agreement Types Manager* is the central location for configuring and maintaining agreement types and their fields. These agreement types are used across the IAM platform, not just in Agreement Manager.  This feature is accessed from the Agreement Manager homepage. See the [IAM Agreement Types Manager](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=con22821364-8601-4fc5-b768-e19f07c43462.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the Agreement Types manager, including its purpose, use, and role within the Intelligent Agreement Management (IAM) platform.") article to learn more about this enhancement. |
| Feature | Analyze | **IAM Fields Manager:** The *Fields Manager* is the single source of truth for the account's custom and standard fields. It is the central place for administrators and contract managers to define, organize, and manage the fields in their account. These custom and standard fields are reused across the Docusign Intelligent Agreement Management (IAM) platform.  This feature is accessed from the Agreement Manager homepage. See the [IAM Fields Manager](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=con7092ce57-207f-4d06-9228-c7bdd2ccff2a.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the Fields manager, including its purpose, use, and role within the Intelligent Agreement Management (IAM) platform.") article to learn more about this enhancement. |
| Enhancement | Analyze | **New entry points for 'Add a field' function:**  The Add a field function is used to configure new custom fields. This function is launched from:  - Fields Manager - Agreement Types Manager - Agreement preview  Additionally, the Agreement preview has two options for creating custom fields. See the [Custom Field Extractions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=condece8d7e-2da8-4e5f-8ba4-3f0175c2a625.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the custom field extractions, including entry points for creating new fields and a review of the AI detection setup wizard.") article to learn more about this enhancement. |
| Feature | Analyze | **Track this field with AI:** When you create custom fields from the Agreement preview page, the Track this field with AI wizard launches. You are guided through the AI detection set-up process:  A screenshot of the Track this field with AI setup wizard that displays when you create custom fields from the Agreement preview page.  - Define the field - Select examples - Test for accuracy  See the [Custom Field Extractions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=condece8d7e-2da8-4e5f-8ba4-3f0175c2a625.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the custom field extractions, including entry points for creating new fields and a review of the AI detection setup wizard.") article to learn more about this feature. |

title: Formatting Requirements for Metadata Editing
text: Look up spreadsheet rules and formatting requirements for using the Bulk Metadata Edit.

title: Formatting Requirements for Metadata Editing
text:
When editing the agreement metadata, always use the Docusign-generated, pre-formatted spreadsheet that you downloaded from Agreement Manager. Use the spreadsheet as a template for updating the metadata for the selected documents.

Attention: Before downloading the spreadsheet, be sure that your custom fields are enabled in Agreement Manager. The pre-formatted spreadsheet contains all the custom and standard fields in the account. If you plan on adding custom data, the spreadsheet must contain a column for the custom field. You cannot add columns and rows to the pre-formatted spreadsheet.

Use this reference when you are editing the metadata spreadsheet file and must look up field formatting requirements.

title: Formatting Requirements for Metadata Editing
text:
Explains the structural requirements for the Docusign-generated, pre-formatted spreadsheet so your upload is accepted.

Do not change the structure of the pre-formatted spreadsheet. If the structure of your spreadsheet does not match the original template, the upload is rejected.

| Component | Rules |
| --- | --- |
| Headers | - Do not change the header row. - Do not change the field names in the header row. |
| Columns | - Do not add columns. - Do not rename the column headers. - Do not edit, reorder, or remove the first three columns, Agreement ID, File Name, and Agreement Type. |
| Rows | - Do not add rows. - One row per agreement. |

While you can remove and reorder the columns, it is not recommended. Retain the original file structure as much as possible to avoid errors.

Tip: Instead of removing or reordering the columns in the spreadsheet, use the Hide function to hide the unnecessary columns.

title: Formatting Requirements for Metadata Editing
text:
Identifies the "no-edit" areas of the spreadsheet. These areas must stay exactly as downloaded so your upload is accepted.

| Components | Rules |
| --- | --- |
| Header row (Field names) | Do not edit the field names in the header row. |
| Field-mapping row (second row) | Do not edit or delete the field-mapping values. Agreement Manager relies on this value when updated the field values with new data. |
| Agreement ID column | - Do not delete the Agreement ID column. - Do not edit the IDs in the Agreement ID column.   Agreement Manager uses the agreement ID to match the data rows to the appropriate agreements. |
| Agreement Type column | - Do not delete the Agreement Type column. - Do not edit the Agreement Type value.  Important: Associate your custom fields with the relevant agreement types in Agreement Manager before downloading the spreadsheet. If the field is not associated with the current agreement type, any values that you enter will fail validation. |
| Column layout | - Do not edit, reorder, or remove the first three columns. These values are required for mapping the metadata to the appropriate agreements. - Do not add columns to the spreadsheet. The columns require the correct field-mapping value. This value is generated when you download the pre-formatted spreadsheet.  Keep the original column layout to ensure a successful upload. Removing and reordering the columns is not recommended. |
| Row structure | - Do not add extra rows. Every row represents one of the selected agreements. - Do not delete the agreement ID value from any row. |

title: Formatting Requirements for Metadata Editing
text:
You can safely edit the data cells in the existing agreement rows. However, you cannot edit the header row, the field-mapping values, or the Agreement ID and Agreement Type columns.

| Question | Rule |
| --- | --- |
| Which cells can I edit? | You can edit any of the data cells under the existing field columns (header row and field-mapping row) for each agreement.  **Exceptions:**  - Do not update the header row. - Do not update the field-mapping row (second row). - Do not update the Agreement ID values. These values are important to the upload process.  Tip: Follow the required field formatting rules so your updates pass validation. |
| Can I change a field name in the header row? | No. The names default from Agreement Manager. Do not edit or delete the field names in the header row. |
| Can I replace update the Agreement ID or Agreement Type for an agreement? | No. Agreement Manager uses these fields to match rows to agreements and to verify that field data is appropriate for the type. |
| Can I overwrite existing values? | No. Do not overwrite any of the pre-populated data in the spreadsheet. This includes the Agreement ID and Agreement Type values, and the field-mapping data in the second row. |
| Can I leave the fields blank? | Yes, you can leave fields blank if the field is not relevant to the agreement or the agreement type. |

title: Formatting Requirements for Metadata Editing
text:
Lists the required formats for each field type so your updates pass validation.

| Field type | Rule or requirement | Example |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Text (length) | Enter up to 2,000 characters for custom text fields and up to 400 characters for standard fields.  Important: Depending on how the standard fields are used across the platform, the maximum character limit can vary.  Longer values fail validation and an error is logged in the job monitoring hub | Northern District of California |
| Date | Enter dates in the standard, U.S. format, MM-DD-YYYY. | 01-01-2026 (not "2026-01-01") |
| Number | Enter digits only, no commas. Note: Decimals are approved where the field supports them. For example, 2500.75. | 10000 (not "10,000") |
| Currency (and Amount) | Enter an amount followed by the [ISO 4217 currency code](https://www.iso.org/iso-4217-currency-codes.html) using the format, [Number][Space][Currency code]. | 25 USD (not "25.00 U.S. dollars") |
| Duration | Enter a value using the format, [Number][Space][Time Unit]. Common time units are: Year, Month, Week, and Day.  Important: Capitalize the time unit value. This value is case-sensitive. | - 11 Months (not "11 M" or "11 months") - 4 Years (not "4 Y" or "4 years") |
| Multiple-value | Enter comma-separated values for multiple-value cells. | English, German, French (not "English, German, and French") |
| Party and Role fields | Enter party values using the format, [Name] | [Role]. Important:  - The party name and role values are separated by the vertical line (pipe) divider. - Use semicolon-separated entries when adding more than one party value. - The party role is required. Use the format, [Party Name] | [None], if you are unsure of the role. | For example, Party A is the buyer and Party B is the seller. This value is entered as: Party A | Buyer; Party B | Seller |
| Dropdown fields | Values in dropdown fields (cells) must match at least one of the options in the dropdown. If the value is not a valid option for the dropdown, the file is skipped and an error is logged. | For example, if your custom dropdown field has three options, the cell must contain one of those options. |

title: 2024 Agreement Manager Release Notes
text:
Look up past release notes from 2024 for Docusign Agreement Manager, including features, bug fixes, and other changes.

Note: The features and enhancements referenced in this topic are available in Demo accounts, Production accounts, or both. Availability is dependent on the ramp schedule and can vary across regions and languages.

title: 2024 Agreement Manager Release Notes
text:
IAM Core and IAM for Sales are now globally available with Agreement Manager, including:

- Agreement Manager AI-assisted insights support agreements in English (global), French, and German
- The localized Agreement Manager user interface supports usability in 14 Docusign-supported languages
- Localized user experience reflects global variations in agreement details, such as date formats and currencies
- Agreement Manager product documentation localized in French and German

title: 2024 Agreement Manager Release Notes
text:
The following fixes, new features, and product enhancements are being delivered with this release:

| **Type** | **Category** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Feature | Upload and Import | - **Cloud storage upload:** Administrators can now upload agreements from cloud storage locations. This simplifies storage processes, improves collaboration, and provides agreement visibility across your portfolio. - **Agreement ingestion filtering:** Administrators can use filters to determine which Docusign historical completed agreements will be ingested into Agreement Manager. This allows for greater control over the agreements stored in the account. |
| Feature | AI/Intelligence | **Manage custom agreement types:** Create custom agreement types that reflect your organization's specific agreements. This allows for greater flexibility, enhanced organization, and more accurate reporting. |
| Enhancement | AI/Intelligence | - **Expanded AI-assisted data insights:** Automatically capture more of the contractual data points that matter to your organization with AI-assisted insights. These expanded insights drive greater efficiency and improve risk management. - **Data enrichment:** Drag standard fields onto eSignature agreement templates to capture attributes up front. This ensures agreements are more discoverable post-signature. |
| Enhancement | Reporting | **Expanded reporting:** Capitalize on additional pre-built reports and advanced reporting capabilities to gain more agreements insights and to improve efficiency. |
| Enhancement | Search | **Search enhancements:**  - Natural language searches - Download selected agreements - Export and share search results |
| Enhancement | View and Organize | **Agreement preview updates:** Experience a cleaner and more relevant view of agreement details for a selected agreement. Key attributes are prominently displayed based on the agreement type so relevant attributes are displayed first. |

title: 2024 Agreement Manager Release Notes
text:
The following fixes, new features, and product enhancements are being delivered with the May 30, 2024 release.

| **Type** | **Category** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Feature | Upload and Import | - **Automatic sync with eSignature:** Automatically access your completed and newly signed agreements from Docusign eSignature. - **Bulk agreement upload:** Easily upload historical agreements completed outside of Docusign in bulk. - **Individual agreement upload:** All users can upload agreements ad hoc and associate them with relevant parties. |
| Feature | View and Organize | - **Centralized storage:** Store your completed agreements in one location to improve agreement organization and visibility. - **Agreement list:** See your individual completed agreements in a simplified view and sort or filter by various attributes. - **Agreement preview:** Preview any given agreement with key details clearly visible and surfaced by AI technology. - **Party deduplication:** Store, organize, and reconcile party information to access agreements associated with a specific party. |
| Feature | Search | - **Comprehensive search:** Find relevant agreements by searching for key attributes, keywords, or related terms within the content of an agreement. - **Attribute search:** Locate an agreement by searching for key attributes associated, such as the agreement type or date ranges. - **Keyword search:** Locate an agreement by searching for specific text or phrases within the content of the agreement. - **Related concepts search:** Enhance your search with an advanced technique that identifies agreements containing related terms and concepts. |
| Feature | AI/Intelligence | **AI-assisted data insights:** Rely on Docusign AI to identify and store agreement attributes, resulting in standardized data that makes it easy to find crucial information, understand key points, and efficiently search and filter your agreements. |
| Feature | Security and Compliance | - **Access permissions:** Administrators can grant users access to completed agreements as relevant to their role. - **Direct sharing:** Users have the ability to share or revoke access to individual agreements that they own. |
| Feature | Reporting | - **Out-of-the-box reporting:** Discover important insights about your agreements with an agreement dashboard that displays essential data, leading to more informed decisions. - **Renewal management:** Access a renewal dashboard and subscribe to agreement reminders to identify cost-saving opportunities and prevent unwanted auto-renewals. |
| Feature | Notifications and Alerts | **Agreement reminders:** Stay ahead of important dates with reminders for agreement milestones, such as renewals and terminations. |

title: Agreement Manager Page Elements
text:
Read about the basic elements of the Docusign Agreement Manager Completed Documents view. This topic answers the question: What kind of information or features are found in Agreement Manager?

When you first access Agreement Manager, the Completed Documents view is displayed:

![A screenshot of the Agreement Manager homepage that displays the Completed Documents view.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Docusign-Navigator_Completed-documents-view.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTYwNzYsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.nm_9oheHsh9DSW-XrEGaMnuE44k-MmqeFtAQJf5diL4&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Agreement Manager homepage that displays the Completed Documents view.")

title: Agreement Manager Page Elements
text:
This view contains a number of helpful page elements, including:

- **Agreement list table:** Displays all the agreements to which the logged-in user has access.
- **Total document count:** Displays the number of documents in the table. The count is located
  in the bottom-right corner of the page. It is updated when you perform a
  search or apply filters.
- **Search field:** Used to perform a keyword search to locate agreements and data.
- **Filters option:** Used to refine the agreement list table for specific data points.
- **Customize columns (toggle) option:** Used to choose which attributes (fields) to display in the agreement list table.
- **Sort items (arrows) option:** Used to sort the agreement list table by a specific column. The Sort items (arrows) option is not available for all column headers.
- The names in the Parties column link to the party hub pages.
- Each agreement in the table has a More actions (three dots) menu. Use this menu to view, download, and share the corresponding agreement.

  Tip: You can also access the document-level Activity Log from this menu. This log tracks any changes that are made to the document or its attributes.
- The View renewing contracts feature used to access the Renewals dashboard.
- The Settings (gear) menu located in the top right of the page provides access to manage agreement data, such as fields, agreement types, parties, obligations, and sets. The job monitoring hub is also accessed from this menu.

title: Agreement Manager Page Elements
text:
The Insights bar is an interactive deck of cards on the Agreement Manager homepage. Select the Show Insights option to view relevant, actionable insights with links to reports or next steps.

![A snippet of the Completed Documents page that shows the expanded Insights bar.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-documents_Insights-bar_expanded.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTYwNzYsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.nm_9oheHsh9DSW-XrEGaMnuE44k-MmqeFtAQJf5diL4&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Completed Documents page that shows the expanded Insights bar.")

Select the Hide Insights option to collapse the bar.

Tip: See the [The Agreement List Table](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=yjz1702945473104.html&_LANG=enus "This topic answers the question: What can I do from the agreement list table?") support article to learn more about the Insights bar.

title: Agreement Manager Page Elements
text:
The Settings (gear) menu contains options for managing your agreement data, such as: managing agreement types, fields, obligations, and sets. Here are the options in the menu:

text:
3ODE3OTYwNzYsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.nm_9oheHsh9DSW-XrEGaMnuE44k-MmqeFtAQJf5diL4&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Settings (gear) menu that shows the options for managing agreement data.")

title: Agreement Manager Page Elements
text:
The Add (+) menu is available to all users. Use this function to upload single documents to Agreement Manager:

![A screenshot of the Add (+) dropdown menu that shows the Upload, Import from Email, and Import Data options.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-view_Add-Documents-dropdown_expanded.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTYwNzYsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.nm_9oheHsh9DSW-XrEGaMnuE44k-MmqeFtAQJf5diL4&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Add (+) dropdown menu that shows the Upload, Import from Email, and Import Data options.")
