title: Manually Create an Obligation
text:
Learn how to create an obligation from the Agreement preview page for a selected agreement.

Some obligations are automatically surfaced in your agreements, such as payments, renewal notices, and termination notices. These obligations are displayed in the Obligations pane on the Agreement preview page. Obligations can also be manually created from the Agreement preview page.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed.
4. Perform a search and apply filters to locate the relevant agreements:
   1. Enter a keyword or string in the search field and select a suggestion from the dropdown or press the Enter key.

      The agreements containing the keyword or search string that you entered are displayed in the table.
   2. Use the filters to refine the list of search results:

      1. Expand the Filters option and select the filter that you want to apply.
      2. Choose a filter value.
      3. Select the Apply option.

      The agreements that match the applied filters are displayed in the table.
5. Select an agreement from the list.

   The Agreement preview page opens for the selected agreement. The Details pane is displayed by default and shows a list of attributes and suggested values. Attributes, or fields, are displayed based on the identified agreement type.

   Before you create a new obligation, review the list of existing obligations.
6. Select the Obligations (flag) icon from the icon tray to the left of the Details pane.

   The Obligations pane opens. A list of obligations that were surfaced with AI or manually created is displayed in the pane.

   ![A screenshot of the Agreement preview page that highlights the Obligations pane.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Navigator_Agreement-Preview_Obligations-pane-highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcxNjAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.UbWqUHm8HaSYiMXK_fujM6_V45Eodv6hg7DSEheyZoI&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Agreement preview page that highlights the Obligations pane.")

   The name, owner, due date, and status are displayed for each obligation.
7. Before creating a new obligation, review the list to make sure that you are not adding a duplicate. If necessary, select an obligation to review additional details.
8. Highlight the clause associated with the obligation that you want to create from the preview pane. A dropdown menu of options opens. Select the Create Obligation option:

   ![A snippet of the document preview pane that shows the Create Obligation option for a highlighted clause.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Obligation_Create-obligation-option_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcxNjAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.UbWqUHm8HaSYiMXK_fujM6_V45Eodv6hg7DSEheyZoI&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the document preview pane that shows the Create Obligation option for a highlighted clause.")

   The Create Obligation pane opens and a list of data fields is displayed.
9. Complete the required data fields in the Create Obligation pane:

title: Manually Create an Obligation
text:
he Create Obligation option for a highlighted clause.")

   The Create Obligation pane opens and a list of data fields is displayed.
9. Complete the required data fields in the Create Obligation pane:

   ![A screenshot of the Agreement preview page that highlights the Create Obligation pane including the obligation's data fields.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Obligations_Create-Obligation-panehighlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcxNjAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.UbWqUHm8HaSYiMXK_fujM6_V45Eodv6hg7DSEheyZoI&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Agreement preview page that highlights the Create Obligation pane including the obligation's data fields.")

   1. Enter a name for the obligation in the Obligation name field. This field is required.
   2. Select an obligation type from the Type dropdown.
   3. Select an obligation status from the Status dropdown.
   4. Indicate the Notice period for the obligation. The Notice date field is auto-calculated by subtracting the notice period from the expiration date.

      Note: The Notice period and Notice date fields are specific to renewal and termination obligations. All other obligation types have a Due date field that serves the same purpose.
   5. Enter or select a Due date for the obligation.
   6. Select a frequency from the Frequency dropdown. This field is associated with payment obligations.
   7. Enter the name or email address of the Owner.
   8. Select a party from the Obligated party dropdown.
   9. Select a party from the Benefiting party dropdown.
   10. Enter the repercussions for not fulfilling the obligation in the Repercussions  field.
   11. Enter a description for the obligation in the Description field.
10. Select Save.

    The new obligation is saved and displays in the Obligations pane.
11. Repeat this process to create additional obligations as needed.
12. Return to the Agreement Manager homepage. Select the Close (x) icon from the top left.

    The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed. Your search criteria and filters are still applied.

You have successfully created an obligation from the Agreement preview page for an agreement.

title: Agreement Uploads and Ingestion
text:
This topic answers the question: What are my upload options for Agreement Manager, including limitations and required permissions?

Uploading third-party agreements is an important part of Agreement Manager. There are two upload or ingestion options based on the number of documents:

- **Bulk upload** (more than five documents)
- **Single upload** (up to five documents)

The Add (+) function is available to all users. You can perform single uploads without being assigned Agreement Manager permissions, however, bulk uploads require an additional permission.

An administrator turns on the Upload up to 1,000 files at a time toggle
(blue position) from the user profile page. See the [Agreement Manager Permissions, Profiles, and Roles](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=eut1712157553977.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the available Agreement Manager permissions, including how users and groups are granted access to Agreement Manager agreements and example roles for each permission.") topic for more information about
Agreement Manager permissions.

During processing, attribute (field) values are surfaced from all the agreements uploaded, ingested, or synced to Agreement Manager.

Note: The processing time for agreement uploads and ingestion is dependent on the
number of documents being processed. When processing large data sets, the total
processing time can exceed a week. See the [Processing Agreement Manager Agreements](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=rvw1736364838500.html&_LANG=enus "Read about agreement processing and how to opt in for additional processing to surface new insights within your agreements.")
article to learn more about agreement processing.

title: Agreement Uploads and Ingestion
text:
All historical, completed documents in an existing eSignature account are auto-ingested into Agreement Manager by default upon provisioning. These documents are displayed in the agreement list table on the Completed Documents page.

The number of Docusign historical completed documents that you can ingest is based on the Intelligent Agreement Management (IAM) plan that was purchased. The document ingestion thresholds are:

- 500,000 (North American customers)
- 100,000 (International customers)

Important: If your document count exceeds the ingestion threshold, contact your Docusign account representative to submit a special request.

title: Agreement Uploads and Ingestion
text:
During the ingestion and upload processes, your documents are moved to Agreement Manager and processed to surface key data points. The primary sources of document uploads and ingestion are:

- Docusign eSignature
- Docusign CLM
- Cloud storage providers
- Email attachments

After the initial ingestion of historical documents, new agreements are stored in Agreement Manager
automatically when they are signed. This includes agreements that use other
Intelligent Agreement Management (IAM) features, such as:

- New signed agreements from eSignature and CLM
- Workflow Builder workflow sends
- API sends
- Integration sends, such as from Microsoft Dynamics and Salesforce

These ongoing agreements are stored in Agreement Manager as they are completed.

CAUTION:

Optical character recognition (OCR) converts images of text into machine-readable text. OCR is limited to the first 100 pages of a document. For documents with more than 100 pages, embed the text directly in a document before ingesting or uploading the file to Agreement Manager. For example, a text-based PDF.

Data extraction and AI processing considers the first 500 pages of "text". This limitation ensures performance and accuracy as large documents can impact processing times.

title: Agreement Uploads and Ingestion
text:
The agreement upload process supports these file formats:![A screenshot of the Upload documents dialog box that shows a list of supported file formats.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-documents-view_Upload-documents-dialog-box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY3MDksInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.qAQx_cuf-u0qP4YPfOU11-tlmOBQno6BCBss3Y2Euyg&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Upload documents dialog box that shows a list of supported file formats.")

- Word (DOC, DOCX, RTF)
- Excel (XLS, XLSX, XLSB)
- HTML (HTML)
- Images (JPG, PDF, PNG, TIF, TIFF)
- PowerPoint (PPSX, PPT, PPTX)

title: Agreement Uploads and Ingestion
text:
Users can upload documents from third-party cloud storage providers, such as:

- Box
- Dropbox
- Google Drive
- OneDrive
- SharePoint

An administrator must install the cloud storage app from the Docusign App Center before you can upload files from these locations. Once installed, the apps are listed in the Select File dropdown during document uploads:

![A screenshot of the Select File dropdown that shows the installed apps and options to add connections to them.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Mini-App-Center_Select-File-dropdown.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY3MDksInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.qAQx_cuf-u0qP4YPfOU11-tlmOBQno6BCBss3Y2Euyg&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Select File dropdown that shows the installed apps and options to add connections to them.")

Use the Add Connection link to establish a connection to the corresponding
cloud storage app and upload your documents. See the [File Ingestion from Third-Party Cloud Storage Providers](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=byx1727376126901.html&_LANG=enus "This topic answers the question: How can I upload selected files to Agreement Manager from third-party cloud storage providers?") article to learn more about this feature.

title: Agreement Uploads and Ingestion
text:
All users can upload up to five documents to Agreement Manager from their local drives and third-party cloud storage apps. Once uploaded, these documents are visible to users and groups with permission to view the user's sent agreements. These users must have at least View all agreements in this account access to Agreement Manager.

Limitations of single document upload include:

- File names must be fewer than 255 characters
- Files must be less than 100 MB
- Only supported file types can be uploaded

When successfully uploaded, the file displays in a list view. If processing exceptions exist, the
user receives a notification.

Important: Users with *bulk upload*
permission are prompted to assign access to the documents they are uploading -
even if fewer than five documents are being uploaded. See the *Access controls
for bulk document uploads* section in this topic to learn more about
these controls.

title: Agreement Uploads and Ingestion
text:
Bulk upload is available when you want to upload more than five files, such as uploading a folder of files. An administrator must grant the Bulk upload permission for users to use this function. This is done by turning on the Upload up to 1,000 files at a time toggle for a user.

Limitations of bulk upload:

- File names must be fewer than 255 characters
- Maximum of 1,000 files per bulk upload job
- Folder structure is not maintained when uploading folders and sub-folders
- Files must be less than 100 MB
- Only supported file types can be uploaded

title: Agreement Uploads and Ingestion
heading_path: Access controls for bulk document uploads
text:
During the bulk upload process, the Allow access step gives you the option to:

- Add the uploaded documents to an agreement set
- Share files with other users and groups
- Skip this step

Important: The Allow access step always displays for users with bulk upload permissions, regardless of the number of documents being uploaded.

**Add to agreement sets:**

Agreement sets are named groups of agreements that can be shared with users and groups. Sets are used to organize agreements for productivity and data privacy, including managing access at scale.

Tip: See the [Agreement Set Management](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=zed1730843285476.html) article to learn more about agreements sets.

**Share files with others:**

When sharing files with other users and groups, an access level is assigned. The choices are: Can View, Can Edit, and Can Manage. When users upload documents to Agreement Manager, they are set as the *owner* for the files in the upload job.

Note: By default, users are given Can Manage access to their uploaded and completed agreements. This includes the documents that are ingested from eSignature.

Tip: See the Direct Sharing and Agreement Downloads article to learn more about agreement access levels.

If permissions are not set during the upload, the files are visible by the owner and anyone with the View all agreements in this account permission.

**Skip this step:**

If you choose to skip the Allow access step, the files are added to Agreement Manager but they won't be organized or shared with others.

title: Agreement Uploads and Ingestion
text:
Import your executed agreements from email attachments directly into Agreement Manager. This streamlined approach to ingestion reduces manual effort, improves data accuracy, and drives better collaboration.

Restriction: Email ingestion is currently available to U.S.-based customers only. Users outside of the U.S. will experience the existing user interface until this feature is generally available in early 2026.

Include the repository's unique email address in your ongoing email threads to import the
attached documents automatically:![A screenshot of the Import agreement straight from your inbox dialog box that highlights the Copy Address option.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Email-ingestion_Copy-Addresss-highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY3MDksInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.qAQx_cuf-u0qP4YPfOU11-tlmOBQno6BCBss3Y2Euyg&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Import agreement straight from your inbox dialog box that highlights the Copy Address option.")

Tip: **Agreement Manager email addresses**

- Add the repository email address to your Contacts list for ongoing use.

  The next time you import agreements to Agreement Manager, the email address is already in your Contacts
  list. You do not have to copy the email address every time
  you want to import email attachments.
- Add the Agreement ManagerUploader@docusign.net email alias to your Safe Senders list.

  If your ingestion fails, this email alias will send you a notification. Depending on your security policies, the email may be blocked or marked as spam. Add the email address to your Safe Senders list to ensure that you receive failure notifications.

Important: **License and permission requirements**

By default, all users can upload or import up to five agreements at a time. However, to ingest email attachments, the email must come from a registered account user. Only registered account users with the appropriate upload permissions can use this feature.

The Upload up to 1,000 files at a time permission is required to ingest more than five email attachments at a time. This toggle is turned on from the user profile page by an administrator.

Limitations of email attachment ingestion:

- File names must be fewer than 255 characters
- Only supported file types can be ingested

Attention: The maximum file size for email
attachment ingestion is limited by your email provider. The common file size
limit for popular providers, like Gmail, Outlook, and Yahoo is 25 MB. Some
providers, like Outlook, might have lower limits for certain account types, such
as accounts with internet service providers (ISPs). For these accounts, the file
size limit is 20 MB.

See the [Import Documents from Email Attachments](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=ask1749568023897.html&_LANG=enus "This topic answers the question: How do I add documents from email attachments to Agreement Manager? Learn how to import documents from email attachments to Agreement Manager for processing.") article to learn more about using this function.

title: Agreement Uploads and Ingestion
text:
When uploading or ingesting agreements to Agreement Manager, if an agreement does not match an existing standard or custom type, it is identified as Miscellaneous. If 10 or more similar agreements are identified as Miscellaneous, a custom agreement type is suggested.

For example, 10 charter agreements were uploaded and identified as *Miscellaneous*. The agreements are recognized as being similar and a custom agreement type, *Charter Agreement*, is suggested:

![A snippet of the agreement list table that shows a suggested custom agreement type for a Miscellaneous document.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Autosuggest_New-suggestion-available_dialog-box_charter-agreement-example.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY3MDksInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.qAQx_cuf-u0qP4YPfOU11-tlmOBQno6BCBss3Y2Euyg&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the agreement list table that shows a suggested custom agreement type for a Miscellaneous document.")

Use the Create option and follow the steps to create a new custom agreement type. See the [Use Auto-Suggestion to Create Custom Agreement Types](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tsk2093dc24-1da8-4393-b4cd-526990bc7118.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to create a custom agreement type from the auto-suggestion feature.") article to learn more about using this feature.

Once created, the custom type is applied to all the relevant documents that were previously identified as Miscellaneous. Depending on the processing queue, it may take an hour or longer for the agreements to reflect the new type.

You can filter the agreement list table using the new custom type:

![A snippet of the agreement list table that shows the new custom type in the Agreement Type filter.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Autosuggest_Agreement-Type-filter_Charter-Agreement-example.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY3MDksInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.qAQx_cuf-u0qP4YPfOU11-tlmOBQno6BCBss3Y2Euyg&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the agreement list table that shows the new custom type in the Agreement Type filter.")

Important: The agreements contain attributes and field values that were surfaced with AI. Complete a data review to validate these values, including the Agreement Type. See the [Validate Extracted Field Values (Complete a Data Review)](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=yqq1702945693043.html&_LANG=enus "This topic answers the question: How can I make sure that the suggested attribute (field) values are accurate? Learn how to complete a data review to verify suggested field values.") article to learn more about validating AI-surfaced values.

title: Agreement Uploads and Ingestion
text:
The Monitor All Jobs function gives you access to job details for agreement uploads and processing jobs. This job monitoring hub provides a centralized location to view and track the status of these jobs.

Access this function from the Settings (gear) menu on the Agreement Manager homepage:

![A snippet of the Completed Documents view that shows the Uploads and Processing Status option in the Settings dropdown.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-documents-view_Actions_Monitor-All-Jobs-option.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY3MDksInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.qAQx_cuf-u0qP4YPfOU11-tlmOBQno6BCBss3Y2Euyg&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Completed Documents view that shows the Uploads and Processing Status option in the Settings dropdown.")

The All Jobs page opens. The Uploaded Agreements view is displayed, including the job details and status information the upload jobs.

![A screenshot of the All jobs page that is used to monitor all upload jobs, including the job name, number of documents, and status.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-documents-view_Monitor-All-Jobs_All-JobsUploaded-tab.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY3MDksInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.qAQx_cuf-u0qP4YPfOU11-tlmOBQno6BCBss3Y2Euyg&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the All jobs page that is used to monitor all upload jobs, including the job name, number of documents, and status.")

The created date, job name, number of documents, and status for all uploads are displayed in the table. Use this view to:

- Monitor job progress
- Cancel an upload job
- View upload failures and processing exceptions
- Download a list of failed, skipped, or canceled files
- View the status of initiated upload jobs, such as in-progress, completed, failed, and canceled
- View the documents that were uploaded for a selected job

Tip: Use the Actions dropdown to Browse
Files, View Details, and
Delete an upload job.

title: AI Denotation in Agreement Manager
text: This topic answers the question: In what way does Docusign indicate AI-assisted insights or AI functionality in Agreement Manager?

title: AI Denotation in Agreement Manager
text:
Agreement Manager relies on Docusign Iris to surface key data points and provide insight to your agreements. Docusign uses visual indicators to denote AI-assisted data or AI functionality, such as:

- ![A screenshot of the bloom icon.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/AI_Bloom-icon.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTYwNjMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.2J_iCX50UT8vMw7pGlh975XUFXoydGPck01dJtk-yU4&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the bloom icon.") the bloom icon to indicate an AI-assisted action
- ![Image of the spark icon used to denote AI functionality.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/AI-Spark-icon.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTYwNjMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.2J_iCX50UT8vMw7pGlh975XUFXoydGPck01dJtk-yU4&_LANG=enus "Image of the spark icon used to denote AI functionality.") the spark icon to denote field data that was surfaced with AI
- ![A screenshot of the AI-Assisted denotation badge.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/AIassisted-badge.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTYwNjMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.2J_iCX50UT8vMw7pGlh975XUFXoydGPck01dJtk-yU4&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the AI-Assisted denotation badge.") the AI-Assisted denotation badge to indicate that the page contains features that use Docusign Iris.

![A snippet of the Completed Documents view that shows various manners of AI denotation, including a clickable AI-Assisted denotation badge.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Navigator_AI-denotation-example.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTYwNjMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.2J_iCX50UT8vMw7pGlh975XUFXoydGPck01dJtk-yU4&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Completed Documents view that shows various manners of AI denotation, including a clickable AI-Assisted denotation badge.")

Methods of denoting AI functionality include:

- Bloom icon
- Spark icon
- AI-Assisted denotation badge
- Dotted lines under AI-suggested attribute (field) values
- Purple highlighting around AI-suggested party names

Important: The dotted lines and purple highlights indicate that the associated values were AI-assisted and must be validated by the user. Once the values are validated against the agreement, the purple dotted lines or highlighting goes away.

The AI-Assisted  denotation badge is clickable. Select this badge to access additional information about how Docusign uses AI functionality:

![A screenshot of the expanded AI-Assisted denotation badge showing additional information about how Docusign uses AI functionality.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Obligations-Dashboard_AIAssisted-pillexpanded.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTYwNjMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.2J_iCX50UT8vMw7pGlh975XUFXoydGPck01dJtk-yU4&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the expanded AI-Assisted denotation badge showing additional information about how Docusign uses AI functionality.")

title: AI Denotation in Agreement Manager
text:
When new obligation-related attributes are available, the Track obligations badge is displayed on the Completed Documents page. For example, when an account is enabled for obligations. This badge indicates that you are not using the latest AI model.

Select the Track obligations badge to get started:

![A screenshot of the expanded Track obligations badge that shows information related to processing agreements for added insights.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reprocessing_New-Insights-pill_expanded.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTYwNjMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.2J_iCX50UT8vMw7pGlh975XUFXoydGPck01dJtk-yU4&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the expanded Track obligations badge that shows information related to processing agreements for added insights.")

Select Start Now to opt in for additional processing. Another dialog box opens:

![A screenshot of the dialog box that contains the opt-in toggle for processing your agreements for new insights.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reprocessing_Turn-on-processing-toggle.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTYwNjMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.2J_iCX50UT8vMw7pGlh975XUFXoydGPck01dJtk-yU4&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the dialog box that contains the opt-in toggle for processing your agreements for new insights.")

Turn on the Process for additional insights toggle (blue position) and select the Save Changes option. The dialog box closes and a message states that your agreements are being processed for additional AI insights.

Note: Once the toggle is activated, it cannot be turned off. Also, you can no longer deactivate processing. Agreements will be processed based on the user's account access. For example, if the user has obligation management as part of their plan, opting in for additional processing includes obligation-related insights.

Review the [Extracted Agreement Insights and Definitions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=uwk1702945852405.html&_LANG=enus "Look up the names and definitions for the attributes and values that are surfaced in Agreement Manager agreements with AI. This topic answers the question: What data is being extracted from my Agreement Manager agreements?") article to learn more about the insights being surfaced in your agreements with AI.

title: Update Custom Obligation Types
text:
Learn how to rename your custom obligation types that more accurately reflect your obligations.

If necessary, you can rename your custom obligation types to support specific organizational or departmental use cases.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed.
4. Expand the Settings (gear) menu from the right-side of the page and select the Obligations option:

   ![A screenshot of the Settings (gear) menu that highlights the Obligation option in the expanded](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Obligation-type-management_Manage-Obligation-Types_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcxOTIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.RuHLJIoLlkt_Thfk6weCHZF-fNSECf0GhaWO2k1Fhes&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Settings (gear) menu that highlights the Obligation option in the expanded")

   The Manage Obligation Types page opens. The Obligation Types view is displayed. A list of custom and standard obligation types is displayed in the table.
5. Locate the custom obligation type that you want to update.

   Only user-created, custom obligation types can be updated. View the CREATED BY column to verify that the type is user-created.
6. Expand the More actions (three dots) menu and select the Rename option:

   ![A snippet of the Obligation Types table that shows the Rename option in the More actions (three dots) menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Obligation-type-management_Manage-Obligation-Types_Rename.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcxOTIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.RuHLJIoLlkt_Thfk6weCHZF-fNSECf0GhaWO2k1Fhes&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Obligation Types table that shows the Rename option in the More actions (three dots) menu.")

   The Rename obligation type dialog box opens.

   ![A screenshot of the Rename obligation type dialog box that shows the new name for the obligation type.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Obligation-type-management_Rename-obligation-type-dialog-box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcxOTIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.RuHLJIoLlkt_Thfk6weCHZF-fNSECf0GhaWO2k1Fhes&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Rename obligation type dialog box that shows the new name for the obligation type.")
7. Enter the new name for the obligation type in the Name field and select the Save option.

   The dialog box closes and the Obligation Type view is displayed. The custom obligation type is listed in the table under the new name.

title: Processing Agreement Manager Agreements
text:
Read about agreement processing and how to opt in for additional processing to surface new insights within your agreements.

When historical and new agreements are imported or uploaded into Agreement Manager, they are processed to surface key data points. These values are surfaced based on the current AI model.

Currently, Agreement Manager supports a number of standard
attributes (fields) related to:

- Assignment and change of control
- Compensation and payment
- Confidentiality
- Governing law, jurisdiction, and dispute resolution
- Limitation of liability
- Objective information
- Renewals
- Term
- Termination

AI insights help you:

- **Drive efficiency:** help increase productivity by reducing manual reviews and data management.
- **Utilize cost-saving opportunities:** identify areas where agreement value can be optimized by uncovering values, like hidden payment terms.
- **Reduce risk:** identify risks within your agreements, such as legal, financial, compliance, strategic, and confidentiality risks.

title: Processing Agreement Manager Agreements
text:
As new or updated AI models are released, the previously ingested agreements can be processed again to surface these data points. Depending on the type of new insights that are available, users can choose to:

- Opt in for reprocessing from the Track obligations badge (obligation-related attributes and insights)
- Reprocess all the outdated agreements for the account (new or updated AI model)
- Reprocess selected agreements (custom agreement types and fields)

Note: See the [Agreement Manager Reprocessing Experience](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=con44590246-0f0e-43b4-9692-caadb57335ac.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about the Agreement Manager reprocessing experience, including its purpose, trigger points, and job size considerations.") article to learn more about these reprocessing features.

Processing happens in the background. While it does not impact your current experience, processing may take some time depending on the number and size of agreements. Once started, processing cannot be canceled.

title: Processing Agreement Manager Agreements
text:
Historical documents, such as completed agreements from eSignature envelopes, are imported into Agreement Manager automatically. These documents are displayed in the agreement list table on the Completed page.

The number of Docusign historical documents that you can ingest is based
on the IAM plan that was purchased. Limits for historical ingestion:

- 500,000 (North American customers)
- 100,000 (International customers)

The processing time for agreement uploads and ingestion is dependent on the
number of documents being processed. When processing or reprocessing large data
sets, the total processing time can exceed a week.

Depending on how an agreement is uploaded, the processing time can take from hours to days. Consider these examples:

- *Increased processing time due to expanded attribute sets, such as
  Obligation-related attributes.*

  - Processing documents for standard attributes and obligations
    takes longer than processing without obligations.
  - This applies to documents added to Agreement Manager from the
    + Add (+) option.
- *Increased processing time due to congestion in the processing
  queue.*

  - Processing times for documents that are ingested from Docusign eSignature are dependent on the size
    of the data set and the processing queue.
  - This includes the number and size of the jobs already in the
    processing queue.

    Tip: The
    processing queue for documents ingested from eSignature is
    more congested during period close dates, such as month- or
    year-end. You should expect for processing jobs to take
    longer during these times. The typical processing time in
    this scenario is 4–5 days.

title: Processing Agreement Manager Agreements
text:
Consider these examples of when you might want to process agreements for new insights:

- **Updating the agreement type for an agreement:** During data review, the reviewer notices that the surfaced Agreement Type is incorrect and updates the value.
  - Making this type of change automatically initiates a processing job.
  - A dialog box opens. A message informs you about processing the agreement to show specific content for the new agreement type:![A screenshot of the Change agreement type dialog box that shows information about agreement processing.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reprocessing_Change-agreement-type-dialog-box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc4NTksInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.y6-6KSvi7Z3KyRVb4kh9_UUuR3TNzowDaoUhDV61SLQ&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Change agreement type dialog box that shows information about agreement processing.")
  - After selecting Change, the dialog box closes. A message states that the agreement type has been changed and the agreement is being processed.
- **Adding new fields to an agreement type:** The contract manager updates an agreement type by adding standard or custom fields to the type. The additional fields are surfaced in any new agreements that are associated with that type. However, the historical agreements that are associated with the agreement type must be processed again.
- **Releasing new attributes (fields) in Agreement Manager:** As new attributes and insights become available, your historical agreements must be reprocessed to surface the new data points. This reprocessing ensures accurate reporting and agreement management.

title: Processing Agreement Manager Agreements
text:
Docusign uses visual indicators, such as the ![Image of the spark icon used to denote AI functionality.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/AI-Spark-icon.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc4NTksInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.y6-6KSvi7Z3KyRVb4kh9_UUuR3TNzowDaoUhDV61SLQ&_LANG=enus "Image of the spark icon used to denote AI functionality.") spark icon, to denote AI-assisted data or AI functionality. The icon has a number of different states that indicate the processing status of the corresponding agreement.

![A screenshot of the Completed Documents view that highlights the spark icon, a processing indicator for the the corresponding agreement.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Processing-Navigator-Agreements_spark-processing-indicators.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc4NTksInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.y6-6KSvi7Z3KyRVb4kh9_UUuR3TNzowDaoUhDV61SLQ&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Completed Documents view that highlights the spark icon, a processing indicator for the the corresponding agreement.")

Processing stages include:

- ![An image of the spark icon in the solid state.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/AI-Spark-icon.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc4NTksInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.y6-6KSvi7Z3KyRVb4kh9_UUuR3TNzowDaoUhDV61SLQ&_LANG=enus "An image of the spark icon in the solid state.") (solid-state): the agreement was processed and contains values suggested with AI. You are prompted to review, edit, and approve suggestions.
- ![An image of the spark icon in the spinner state.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Processing-Navigator-Agreements_spinner-state.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc4NTksInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.y6-6KSvi7Z3KyRVb4kh9_UUuR3TNzowDaoUhDV61SLQ&_LANG=enus "An image of the spark icon in the spinner state.") (spinner state): the agreement is either in queue or is currently being processed with AI.
- ![An image of the spark icon in the outlined state.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Processing-Navigator-Agreements_outline-statepng.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc4NTksInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.y6-6KSvi7Z3KyRVb4kh9_UUuR3TNzowDaoUhDV61SLQ&_LANG=enus "An image of the spark icon in the outlined state.") (outlined state): the agreement has not been processed with AI but is either in queue or is currently being processed.
- **Blank** (no icon): the agreement was processed or it does not have any AI-suggested values.

title: Validate Renewal-Related Attribute Values for an Agreement
heading_path: Validate Renewal-Related Attribute Values for an Agreement
text:
Learn how to validate renewal-related attribute values for a selected agreement.

Data review is an important part of renewal management. If the renewal-related data points that were surfaced in your agreements are inaccurate, the data displayed in the Renewals dashboard is not accurate. This process relies on the verification of both the AI-assisted insights and the manually entered attribute values.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed.
4. Use the filters to locate the agreements that you want to review:
   1. Select the Filters option.

      The Filters option expands and list of suggested filters is displayed. You can also search for the filter you want to apply.
   2. Select the AI Suggestions filter from the list of Suggested filters.

      The AI Suggestions filter opens and filter values are displayed.

      ![A screenshot of the expanded AI Suggestions filter that shows the filter options, Needs Review and No Suggestions.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-documents-table_AI-Suggestions-dialog-box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcyMzcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.5oXPRHnSx08kHvvXHLx9Ed5ypYmrqwVbR7gVAR1eSUI&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the expanded AI Suggestions filter that shows the filter options, Needs Review and No Suggestions.")
   3. Choose the Needs Review value and select the Apply option.

   The dialog box closes and the filter is applied to the agreement list table. The agreements that matched the applied filters are displayed in the table.

   Tip: ![An image of the spark icon used to denote the use of AI.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/AI-Spark-icon.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcyMzcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.5oXPRHnSx08kHvvXHLx9Ed5ypYmrqwVbR7gVAR1eSUI&_LANG=enus "An image of the spark icon used to denote the use of AI.") The spark icon indicates that the agreement contains suggested values. Review, edit, and approve these values to ensure accurate agreement data for reporting, forecasting, and other business purposes.
5. Select (click) the agreement that you want to review from the list.

   The Agreement preview page opens for the selected agreement. The Details pane is displayed by default and shows a list of attributes and suggested values. Attributes, or fields, are displayed based on the identified agreement type.

   A summary displays at the top of the Details pane. The summary includes the identified agreement type and the number of fields and suggestions to review:

   ![A snippet of the Details pane that shows the identified agreement type and the number of suggested field values that require review.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-Preview-page_Details-pane_summary-statement-snippet.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcyMzcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.5oXPRHnSx08kHvvXHLx9Ed5ypYmrqwVbR7gVAR1eSUI&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Details pane that shows the identified agreement type and the number of suggested field values that require review.")

   Validate the suggested renewal-related attribute values.
6. Place the Details pane into *Edit* mode.
   - Select the Edit (pencil) icon.
   - Select the Review All option.

title: Validate Renewal-Related Attribute Values for an Agreement
heading_path: Validate Renewal-Related Attribute Values for an Agreement
text:
s that require review.")

   Validate the suggested renewal-related attribute values.
6. Place the Details pane into *Edit* mode.
   - Select the Edit (pencil) icon.
   - Select the Review All option.

   Note: Data reviews are completed by users with either of these access levels:
   - Agreement-level access: Can Manage or Can Edit access to the agreement being reviewed.
   - Account-level access: Organize and manage all agreements access to Agreement Manager.For all other users, the Edit (pencil) icon and Review All button are grayed out.

   The Review & edit details pane opens for updates. Attributes are grouped based on usage and purpose. A progress bar is displayed at the top of each group and shows the number of suggestions that require review.

   Important: Updating the suggested attribute values does not change the content in the agreement.
7. Locate the renewal-related attributes. Scroll to the bottom of the pane to the Renewal section.

   ![A screenshot of the Agreement preview page that highlights the Renewal section in the Review & edit pane.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Renewal-Management_Validate-Renewal-attributes_ReviewEdit-details-pane.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcyMzcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.5oXPRHnSx08kHvvXHLx9Ed5ypYmrqwVbR7gVAR1eSUI&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Agreement preview page that highlights the Renewal section in the Review & edit pane.")

   The Renewal section contains renewal-related attributes, such as:
   - Renewal type
   - Renewal Notice Period
   - Renewal Notice Date
   - Renewal Term
   - Extension Period
   - Renewal Owner
   - Additional Info
8. Accept or edit the suggested renewal-related values until each attribute in the Renewal section has been validated. If an attribute does not have a value, leave the field empty for now.
   1. Compare the suggested attribute values against the data in the agreement.

      - Select (click) an attribute to view the suggested value within the agreement.

        The value is highlighted within the agreement and a list of suggested values is displayed.
      - Select the Accept AI Suggestion (checkmark) icon if the value is *correct*.
      - Delete the existing value and enter the correct value if the suggestion is *incorrect*.
   2. Continue reviewing and updating values until all the attributes in the Renewal section have been verified against the agreement.
9. If a field value is missing, find the value in the agreement and update the attribute.

   ![A snippet of the Agreement preview page that shows an example search term that was found within the document.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-preview-page_Search-dialog-box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcyMzcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.5oXPRHnSx08kHvvXHLx9Ed5ypYmrqwVbR7gVAR1eSUI&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Agreement preview page that shows an example search term that was found within the document.")

   1. Select the Search (magnifying glass) icon from the top-right corner. A dialog box opens.
   2. Enter a keyword or search string in the Find in Document field. If the value exists, the keyword or search string is highlighted in the agreement.
   3. Once you locate the value, enter it as the attribute value.

      Tip: If the value does not exist in the agreement, leave the field blank and move to the next attribute.
10. When you are finishing with the data review, save your changes. Select the Save option at the bottom of the pane.

    The agreement details are saved. The Details pane is reverted to read-only mode.
11. Return to the Agreement Manager homepage. Select the Close (x) icon in the top-left corner.

title: Validate Renewal-Related Attribute Values for an Agreement
heading_path: Validate Renewal-Related Attribute Values for an Agreement
text:
ottom of the pane.

    The agreement details are saved. The Details pane is reverted to read-only mode.
11. Return to the Agreement Manager homepage. Select the Close (x) icon in the top-left corner.

    The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed. Your search criteria and filters are still applied. The agreements requiring review are displayed in the table.

You have successfully validated the renewal-related attribute values for a selected agreement.

title: Search for and Merge Parties
text:
This topic answers the question: How do I merge parties under one name? Learn how to search for and merge duplicate parties.

Party management tasks are completed by users with Organize and manage all agreements access to Agreement Manager, such as contract managers. Common tasks include:

- Review and merge duplicate party names.
- Set a preferred name for a merged group of parties.
- Remove parties from merged groups.

These tasks are completed from the Manage parties page. This topic reviews the steps to search for specific parties that you want to merge.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Parties from the navigation pane on the left.

   The Parties page opens.
4. Select the Manage Parties option from the top right:

   ![A snippet of the Parties page that highlights the Manage Parties option in the top right.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Manage-parties_entry-point_Parties-view.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc3ODIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.vLKDkrYKIo-_9-q3Yz1H88aJre8iCaJ7sM3VcDHaCjA&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Parties page that highlights the Manage Parties option in the top right.")

   The Manage parties page opens and a list of individual and merged parties is displayed.

**Select parties to merge**

Parties can be merged manually from the Manage parties page.

![A screenshot of the Manage parties view that highlights the search field, selected parties, and the Merge option.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Manage-Parties_Manage-parties_Selected-parties--and-Merge-highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc3ODIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.vLKDkrYKIo-_9-q3Yz1H88aJre8iCaJ7sM3VcDHaCjA&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Manage parties view that highlights the search field, selected parties, and the Merge option.")

5. Locate and select the parties that you want to merge:
   1. Enter a party name in the Search parties field.

      As you enter the name, a dropdown opens and the parties that match the search term are listed.
   2. Select a party name from the dropdown list or select the View all results option at the bottom of the list.

      The parties list updates and the parties that matched the search term are displayed.
   3. Select the checkboxes for the parties that you want to merge.

   Tip: The Party Side column indicates whether the party is *internal* or *external* to the user's organization. During processing, parties are identified as either the first-party or the third-party:
   - The first-party designation, *Internal*, indicates the user's organization.
   - The third-party designation, *External*, indicates external entities.Review this column to help you determine if parties should be merged under a party alias group.

   When two or more parties are selected, the Merge option becomes active.

**Merge selected parties**

6. Select the Merge option.

   The Review Duplicate Parties page opens. The Select parties to merge and Choose a name sections are displayed.

title: Search for and Merge Parties
text:
Merge option becomes active.

**Merge selected parties**

6. Select the Merge option.

   The Review Duplicate Parties page opens. The Select parties to merge and Choose a name sections are displayed.

   ![A screenshot of the Review Duplicate Parties page that shows the suggested duplicate parties and naming options.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Manage-Parties_Review-Duplicate-Parties.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc3ODIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.vLKDkrYKIo-_9-q3Yz1H88aJre8iCaJ7sM3VcDHaCjA&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Review Duplicate Parties page that shows the suggested duplicate parties and naming options.")
7. Choose the parties that you want to merge from the Select parties to merge section.
   - Deselect the checkboxes for the parties that you do not want to merge. This step is applicable when there are three or more possible duplicate parties.

     Tip: When there are only two suggested duplicate parties, the checkboxes are grayed out.
   - Select the Cancel option from the bottom right if you do not want to merge the selected parties.
8. Select a preferred party name from the Choose a name section. Expand the Party name dropdown and select the preferred name from the list.

   Tip: The merged parties are consolidated under one name. Thi name is used throughout the Intelligent Agreement Management (IAM) platform. For example, this name is displayed in the Parties filter and column on the Completed Documents page in Agreement Manager.
9. Select the Merge Parties option from the bottom right. The button label reflects the number of parties being merged.

   Important:

   This action can take up to an hour before the changes are reflected in the various locations across the IAM platform.

   The Review Duplicate Parties page closes and you are returned to the Manage parties view.
10. Clear the existing search criteria. Select the Clear (x) icon located in the search field.

    The search field is cleared and the party list table is reset.
11. Repeat these steps to merge additional parties as needed.
12. Select the Parties option from the breadcrumb navigation located in the top left.

    You are returned to the Parties page.

    Tip: Select Completed from the navigation pane on the left to view your agreement list. The Completed Documents view opens. The agreements to which the user has access are displayed in the table.

title: Hide Obligation Types
text:
Learn how to hide and unhide your custom and standard obligation types.

Custom and standard obligation types can be hidden if they are no longer relevant. When an obligation type is hidden, it no longer displays as a filter option in various dropdowns.

Note: If an agreement contains a hidden obligation type, those obligations are still visible. Existing obligation instances with the hidden type are also visible.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed.
4. Expand the Settings (gear) menu from the right-side of the page and select the Obligations option:

   ![A screenshot of the Settings (gear) menu that highlights the Obligation option in the expanded](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Obligation-type-management_Manage-Obligation-Types_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcyMTIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9._EwXMQCXvhO3bGzdRR46wE7KVr__lIge1lCRXF2k8N0&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Settings (gear) menu that highlights the Obligation option in the expanded")

   The Manage Obligation Types page opens. The Obligation Types view is displayed. A list of custom and standard obligation types is displayed in the table.
5. Locate the custom obligation type that you want to hide.
6. Expand the More actions (three dots) menu and select the Hide option:

   ![A snippet of the Obligation Types table that shows the Hide option in the More actions (three dots) menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Obligation-type-management_Manage-Obligation-Types_Hide.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcyMTIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9._EwXMQCXvhO3bGzdRR46wE7KVr__lIge1lCRXF2k8N0&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Obligation Types table that shows the Hide option in the More actions (three dots) menu.")

   The table is updated and indicates that the obligation type is *hidden*.

**Unhide an obligation type:**

7. To show a hidden obligation type:

   ![A snippet of the Obligation Types table that shows the Unhide option in the More actions (three dots) menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Obligation-type-management_Manage-Obligation-Types_Unhide.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcyMTIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9._EwXMQCXvhO3bGzdRR46wE7KVr__lIge1lCRXF2k8N0&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Obligation Types table that shows the Unhide option in the More actions (three dots) menu.")

   1. Locate the obligation type that you want to show.
   2. Expand the More actions (three dots) menu and select the Unhide option.

   The table is updated and the obligation type is no longer hidden.

**Return to homepage**

8. Repeat these steps to hide or display obligation types as needed.
9. Return to the Agreement Manager homepage. Select the Close (x) icon in the top right.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed. The search criteria and filters are still applied.

title: Agreement Manager for Contract Viewers and Preparers
heading_path: Agreement Manager for Contract Viewers and Preparers
text:
Read about how contract viewers and preparers interact with Agreement Manager, including example job titles and use cases. This topic answers the question: How do contract viewers and preparers use Agreement Manager to do their jobs?

Contract viewers and preparers use Agreement Manager to locate specific agreement data to perform job duties. Example tasks by department include:

- Looking up a customer agreement (Support)
- Reviewing a vendor agreement (Procurement)
- Verifying payment terms (Accounting)
- Referring to purchased products to verify if discounts were applied (Sales)

See the use case topics in the Related Content section on the right to learn how to complete a few basic tasks using Agreement Manager.

title: Standard Fields for Agreement and Non-Agreement Types
heading_path: Standard Fields for Agreement and Non-Agreement Types
text:
This topic answers the question: What fields are associated with Docusign-provided agreement types? Look up the standard fields that are
associated with each agreement and non-agreement type.

Each Docusign-provided agreement type is pre-populated with a list of relevant standard
fields. These standard fields (attributes) provide structure to help organizations
create custom agreement types.

A number of non-agreement types are also included with the Docusign-provided agreement types. These types are also pre-populated with a list of relevant standard fields.

Tip: The standard fields that are associated with the Docusign-provided agreement and non-agreement types cannot be removed from the type. You can add additional fields to the type, including standard and custom fields. These fields can be removed as needed.

title: Standard Fields for Agreement and Non-Agreement Types
heading_path: Standard Fields for Agreement and Non-Agreement Types

title: Standard Fields for Agreement and Non-Agreement Types
heading_path: Standard Fields for Agreement and Non-Agreement Types
text:
This section lists the standard fields associated with the Docusign-provided non-agreement types.

| **Non-agreement type** | **Standard fields (attributes)** |
| --- | --- |
| **Application**  **Certificate**  **Form**  **Handbook**  **Notice**  **Plan**  **Policy**  **Report**  **Summary** | Title, Parties, Execution Date, Effective Date, Expiration Date, Status, Line of Business |
| **Invoice** | Title, Parties, Total Contract Value, Execution Date, Effective Date, Expiration Date, Status, Line of Business, Renewal, Payment Terms, Late Payment Fee, Price Cap Increase % |

Important: All the custom and standard fields that are associated with an agreement type are surfaced in the relevant documents. However, not all non-agreement types are surfaced in your documents. This includes the custom and standard fields that are associated with the type. These non-agreement types are not surfaced during agreement processing: Certificate, Handbook, Notice, and Policy.

title: Display the Envelope ID in the Agreement List Table
heading_path: Display the Envelope ID in the Agreement List Table
text:
Learn how to configure the agreement list table to display the Envelope ID column.

The agreement list table is customizable. Update the table to display the envelope IDs for your completed documents that were ingested from eSignature envelopes.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed.

**Display the Envelope iD column in the table**

4. Select the Show or Hide Fields (toggle) icon located to the right of the filters:

   ![A snippet of the Completed Documents view that highlights the Show or Hide FIelds (toggle) icon on the right-side of the page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-documents-table_Customize-columns-gear-icon.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY0ODAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.kZXsDhVP84e3BeUzCMT9MkiZ9j1ELc8S3rb2i9R8NSs&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Completed Documents view that highlights the Show or Hide FIelds (toggle) icon on the right-side of the page.")

   The Show or Hide FIelds dialog box opens. A number of fields are turned on (blue) by default. These fields represent the columns in the agreement list table. Columns display in the order that the fields are represented in the dialog box.
5. Scroll to locate the Envelope ID column and select the toggle to turn it on (blue). You can also search for the field using the search field at the top of the dialog box.

   ![A snippet of the Show or Hide Fields dialog box that highlights the Envelope ID column.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed_view_Customize_columns-dialog_box_Envelope_ID.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY0ODAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.kZXsDhVP84e3BeUzCMT9MkiZ9j1ELc8S3rb2i9R8NSs&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Show or Hide Fields dialog box that highlights the Envelope ID column.")
6. Re-order the columns so the Envelope ID is displayed in the appropriate location. Hover the cursor over the Reorder (dots) icon and drag the Envelope ID

   ![A snippet of the Show or Hide Fields dialog box that displays the Envelope ID column in the process of being dragged to a new location.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-view_Customize-columns-dialog-box_Envelope-ID-Reorder.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY0ODAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.kZXsDhVP84e3BeUzCMT9MkiZ9j1ELc8S3rb2i9R8NSs&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Show or Hide Fields dialog box that displays the Envelope ID column in the process of being dragged to a new location.")

   For example, drag the Envelope ID up to the top five spots to display it in the agreement list table without scrolling.

   Tip: The "grabbing" cursor displays when you hover over an element that can be dragged or moved.
7. Select the Save option from the bottom of the dialog box.

   The dialog box closes. The new configuration is reflected in the agreement list table.

   ![A snippet of the Completed Documents view that highlights the Envelope ID column in the agreement list table.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-view_Customize-columns_Envelope-ID-in-table.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY0ODAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.kZXsDhVP84e3BeUzCMT9MkiZ9j1ELc8S3rb2i9R8NSs&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Completed Documents view that highlights the Envelope ID column in the agreement list table.")

title: Display the Envelope ID in the Agreement List Table
heading_path: Display the Envelope ID in the Agreement List Table
text:
ZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.kZXsDhVP84e3BeUzCMT9MkiZ9j1ELc8S3rb2i9R8NSs&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Completed Documents view that highlights the Envelope ID column in the agreement list table.")

   Note: If an agreement was imported from a completed eSignature envelope, the envelope ID is displayed in the Envelope ID column for the agreement. Select the Envelope ID link to view the associated envelope details.

   See the [View Envelope Details from the Agreement List Table](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tsk15aecb4b-98ec-4b40-a739-61a3715cba02.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to view the envelope details for an agreement that was imported from a completed eSignature envelope.") topic to learn more about viewing envelope details from the agreement list table.

You have successfully used the Show or Hide Fields (toggle) feature to display the Envelope ID column in the agreement list table.

title: View Upload Results and Exceptions
text:
Learn how to view processing results and details for an upload job.

The Monitor All Jobs function gives you access to job details for agreement uploads and processing jobs. From the Completed Documents page, expand the Settings (gear) menu located in the top-right corner to access this function.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Completed Documents page opens. A list of documents to which the logged-in user has access is displayed in the table.
4. Expand the More actions menu located in the top-right corner of the view and select the Uploads and Processing Status option:

   ![A screenshot of the Completed Documents view that shows the Uploads and Processing Status option highlighted in the Settings (gear) menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-documents-view_Actions_Monitor-All-Jobs-option.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY4MzcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.jcPfRp4eBxfNRrTMBa4kjPkMRHINDLMhFL9raRhRj4Y&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Completed Documents view that shows the Uploads and Processing Status option highlighted in the Settings (gear) menu.")

   The job monitoring hub opens. The All jobs view has two tabs: Uploaded and Processing. The Uploaded tab displays job details for all agreement upload jobs.

   ![A screenshot of the Uploaded Agreements table that shows job details for all upload and sync jobs.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-documents-view_Monitor-All-Jobs_All-JobsUploaded-tab.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY4MzcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.jcPfRp4eBxfNRrTMBa4kjPkMRHINDLMhFL9raRhRj4Y&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Uploaded Agreements table that shows job details for all upload and sync jobs.")

   The created date, job name, number of documents, and status details are displayed in the table for uploaded and synced agreements.
5. Use the filters to locate your upload job:

   ![A snippet of the Uploaded Agreements table that highlights the Created date, Status, and Source filters.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Monitor-uploads_All-jobs_Filters.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY4MzcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.jcPfRp4eBxfNRrTMBa4kjPkMRHINDLMhFL9raRhRj4Y&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Uploaded Agreements table that highlights the Created date, Status, and Source filters.")

   - Expand the Created date filter, select an identifier tab, and complete the appropriate date fields. Select Apply.

     Identifier tab choices are: Between, Before, and After.

     Tip: If you choose Between, you must complete the From and To date fields.
   - Expand the Status filter, choose the preferred status, and select Apply..

     Choices are: Deleted, Upload job complete, Upload job failed, and Uploading documents.
   - Expand the Source filter, choose the relevant source, and select Apply.

     Choices are: Box, Dropbox, Email Ingestion, Google Drive, OneDrive, SharePoint, and Unknown.

   Upload jobs that match the applied filters are displayed in the table.
6. Review processing results, including the number of files successfully uploaded and the number of failures.
7. Expand the Actions dropdown associated with your upload job and choose from the available actions: Browse Files, View Details, and Delete.

title: View Upload Results and Exceptions
text:
of files successfully uploaded and the number of failures.
7. Expand the Actions dropdown associated with your upload job and choose from the available actions: Browse Files, View Details, and Delete.

   ![A snippet of the All jobs view that shows the additional actions that are available for each job.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-documents-view_Monitor-All-Jobs_Uploaded-agreements_Actions-menu.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY4MzcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.jcPfRp4eBxfNRrTMBa4kjPkMRHINDLMhFL9raRhRj4Y&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the All jobs view that shows the additional actions that are available for each job.")
8. Select Browse Files from the Actions dropdown.

   A list of agreements that were uploaded as a result of the selected job are displayed in the Search Results table. The table displays the attributes and field values that were surfaced for each file.![A snippet of the Completed Documents view that shows the agreements that were successfully added to Agreement Manager for the selected job.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Monitor-All-Jobs_Actions_Browse-Files_Agreement-list.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY4MzcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.jcPfRp4eBxfNRrTMBa4kjPkMRHINDLMhFL9raRhRj4Y&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Completed Documents view that shows the agreements that were successfully added to Agreement Manager for the selected job.")

   Select the browser's Back arrow to return to the Uploaded Agreements table.
9. Select View Details from the Actions dropdown.

   The Job status view opens and processing results are displayed.

   ![A screenshot of the Job status view that shows the processing results for a selected upload job.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Monitor-All-Jobs_Actions_View-Details_Upload-status.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY4MzcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.jcPfRp4eBxfNRrTMBa4kjPkMRHINDLMhFL9raRhRj4Y&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Job status view that shows the processing results for a selected upload job.")

   1. Select Manage Files to view the files that were successfully uploaded to Agreement Manager.
   2. Select Download CSV to download the document name and error message for the files that could not be uploaded. The file downloads to your local Downloads folder.
   3. View a table of users and groups that have access to the uploaded files from the Shared access section.
   4. View a list of agreement sets to which that the uploaded files were added from the Added to agreement sets section.
   5. Select the Back arrow in the top-left corner to return to the Uploaded Agreements table.
10. Select Delete from the Actions dropdown.

    The Delete upload details dialog box opens.

    ![A screenshot of the Delete upload details dialog box that explains the implications of deleting the upload details for the selected job.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Monitor-All-Jobs_Actions_Delete_dialog-box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY4MzcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.jcPfRp4eBxfNRrTMBa4kjPkMRHINDLMhFL9raRhRj4Y&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Delete upload details dialog box that explains the implications of deleting the upload details for the selected job.")

    1. Review the message and note that you will not be able to recover any of these details later.
    2. Select Delete.

    The upload details are removed from the Uploaded Agreements table. The uploaded files are not deleted, but you cannot recover any of the upload details.

title: View Upload Results and Exceptions
text:
these details later.
    2. Select Delete.

    The upload details are removed from the Uploaded Agreements table. The uploaded files are not deleted, but you cannot recover any of the upload details.

You have reviewed upload results and processing exceptions for a selected upload job.

title: Duplicate Party Management and Standardization
text:
This topic answers the question: What does party management in Agreement Manager entail? Read about the party management features designed to help you manage parties that have similar names. For example, party standardization and consolidation at the point of ingestion.

"Party management" is the umbrella term for Agreement Manager features that help you manage your relationships with various clients, vendors, and partners.

title: Duplicate Party Management and Standardization
text:
Duplicate party management helps keep party data clean and consistent. Party names are surfaced and standardized during ingestion. This process reduces the number of duplicate parties due to:

- Case sensitivity
- Legal entity descriptors
- Punctuation
- Whitespaces

When duplicate parties are detected, you are prompted to Review and Merge the parties under one preferred party name:

![A snippet of the Parties page that highlights the Review and Merge option in the insights banner.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Parties-page_Review-and-Merge_highlighted-in-banner.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc3NjAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.PQ5M9BAJ-9KPEcSqx00EbQ2XLz-kPRq3xjz-WWBIwVA&_LANG=enus)

See the [Review and Merge Duplicate Parties](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=upw1712255598663.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to merge parties to ensure data integrity across your organization. This topic answers the question: Where can I review duplicate party suggestions and merge parties that are the same?") article for steps to merge similar parties into a party group under one preferred name. The preferred name is displayed in various filters and columns throughout Agreement Manager and the Intelligent Agreement Management (IAM) platform. For example:

- Agreement Manager: Displays in the Parties filter and column on the Completed Documents page
- Agreement preview: Displays in the Details pane
- IAM dashboards and reports: Displays in the Parties filter and column for various dashboards and reports
- IAM party hub: Displays on the Parties list and party hub pages

The preferred party name is also included in search queries and results.

title: Duplicate Party Management and Standardization
text:
In addition to duplicate party management, existing customers can opt in to clean up their party data.

Restriction: The party clean-up option is available only for English-language documents.

Access the opt-in message from the Insights bar on the Completed Documents page:

![A snippet of the Insights bar that highlights the opt-in message for the normalization and deduplication process.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Normalization-deduplication_Opt-In-card_Clean-Up-Parties.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc3NjAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.PQ5M9BAJ-9KPEcSqx00EbQ2XLz-kPRq3xjz-WWBIwVA&_LANG=enus)

Select the Clean Up Parties option and follow the prompts to clean up your parties list. Standardization rules are applied to your party names and duplicate parties are consolidated into a single party.

Important: Party clean-up differs from reviewing and merging parties under a preferred party group name. When merging parties into groups, the individual parties retain their names and can be unmerged from the group.
