The party clean-up process permanently consolidates the duplicate parties into a single party.

The party name is assigned automatically based on the date that the party record was originally created. The duplicate parties are consolidated under the name of the oldest party.

Warning: When you opt in for party clean-up, the process is permanently enabled and the processing job cannot be paused, reverted, or stopped. Any parties that are consolidated as a result of the clean-up process cannot be unmerged or separated.

Once completed, a confirmation message opens to inform you that the updated party list is ready to view.

See the [Opt-In for Party Clean Up](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tsk63a801de-59cb-4a65-ae4d-a866d34b11fb.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to opt in to reprocess the organization party names in your account for party normalization and deduplication.") article to learn more about enabling this process.

title: Duplicate Party Management and Standardization
text:
Standardization rules ensure that consistent party names are used throughout the IAM platform. These rules are used to clean up your party names and improve data accuracy:

| Rule | Example |
| --- | --- |
| Whitespaces | Party names are not considered distinct due to spacing discrepancies. For example, "Fontara[space][space]Inc." becomes "Fontara[space]Inc." |
| Case sensitivity | Party names are not considered distinct because of case discrepancies. For example, "Fontara" and "FONTARA" are considered the same party. |
| Punctuation and symbols | Party names are not considered distinct due to punctuation differences. This rule standardizes the use of punctuation and symbols in party names.  For example, "Fontara\_Tech Solutions, NA" and "Fontara Tech Solutions NA" are not considered distinct. They are considered the same party and the first occurrence is retained. |
| Legal entity descriptors | Party names are not considered distinct due to differences in the abbreviations of legal entity descriptors. This rule normalizes how these descriptors are referenced in party names. For example:  - "Corporation" becomes "Corp." - "Incorporated" becomes "Inc." - "Limited Liability Company" becomes "LLC"  Note: When parties have different legal entity descriptors, they are considered distinct and are treated as two separate parties. For example, Fontara Inc and Fontara LLC remain two separate parties. |

title: Duplicate Party Management and Standardization
text:
Party management tasks are completed by users with Organize and manage all agreements access to Agreement Manager, such as contract managers. Common tasks include:

- Review and merge duplicate party names.
- Set a preferred name for a merged group of parties.
- Remove parties from merged groups.

These party management tasks are completed from the Manage parties page:

![A screenshot of the Manage parties page that lists all your party names, including merged parties.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Party-management_Manage-parties-page.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc3NjAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.PQ5M9BAJ-9KPEcSqx00EbQ2XLz-kPRq3xjz-WWBIwVA&_LANG=enus)

Search for and select the parties or merged party groups that you want to manage. You can also use the Review and Merge option to merge duplicate parties under one preferred name.

Tip: The party normalization and deduplication process results in curated, normalized party names across the IAM platform. If the merged parties have different display names, they are added to the list of suggested duplicate parties. Use the Review and Merge option to merge the suggested duplicate parties under one preferred name.

title: Duplicate Party Management and Standardization
text:
Party management tasks are completed from the Manage parties view. Access this functionality from:

- The Parties page in the IAM party hub (go to Home  >  Agreements  >  Parties):![A snippet of the Parties page that highlights the Manage Parties option in the top right.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Manage-parties_entry-point_Parties-view.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc3NjAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.PQ5M9BAJ-9KPEcSqx00EbQ2XLz-kPRq3xjz-WWBIwVA&_LANG=enus)
- The Settings (gear) menu on the Completed Documents page (Go to Home  >  Agreements  >  Completed):![A snippet of the Completed Documents view that highlights the Manage Parties option in the More actions (three dots) menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-documents-view_Select-Manage-Parties.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc3NjAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.PQ5M9BAJ-9KPEcSqx00EbQ2XLz-kPRq3xjz-WWBIwVA&_LANG=enus)

title: Duplicate Party Management and Standardization
text:
During processing, parties are identified as either the first-party or the third-party:![A screenshot of the Manage parties page that highlights the Party Side column in the party list table.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Manage-parties_Party-Side_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc3NjAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.PQ5M9BAJ-9KPEcSqx00EbQ2XLz-kPRq3xjz-WWBIwVA&_LANG=enus)

- The first-party designation, *Internal*, indicates the user's organization.
- The third-party designation, *External*, indicates external entities.

The Party Side column is displayed in the party list table on the Manage parties page. This information provides context to help you manage relationships, risks, and obligations effectively.

Tip: Change a party's designation by using the Set as option. From the Manage parties page, search for and select the party that you want to update:![A snippet of the Manage parties page that highlights the Set as Internal Party option for a selected party.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Manage-parties_Set-as-Internal-Party_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc3NjAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.PQ5M9BAJ-9KPEcSqx00EbQ2XLz-kPRq3xjz-WWBIwVA&_LANG=enus) Once you select a party from the list, the Set as [Internal Party or External Party] option is displayed. Select the Set as Internal Party to indicate the part is part of your organization. The Party Side column is updated and reflects the new designation, *Internal*.

Once identified, you can filter the Parties list table by Party Side:

![A snippet of the Parties page that highlights the Party Side filter.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Parties-view_Party-Side-dropdown_page-snippet.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc3NjAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.PQ5M9BAJ-9KPEcSqx00EbQ2XLz-kPRq3xjz-WWBIwVA&_LANG=enus)

title: Update Share Settings for an Agreement Set
heading_path: Update Share Settings for an Agreement Set
text:
Learn how to update the sharing settings for an agreement set, including granting and revoking access to a set.

Contract managers and any users who are assigned the Organize and manage all agreements permission can create and update agreement sets. This includes managing access to agreement sets.

This topic describes the steps to manage the sharing settings for a selected agreement set, including granting and revoking access to a set.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents page is displayed.
4. Expand the Settings (gear) menu from the top right and select the Sets option.

   ![A screenshot of the Settings (gear) menu on the Agreement Manager homepage that highlights the Sets option.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-sets_entry-point_Manage-sets-action-menu.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc0MTgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.pLYBLkq_y-mXOGJiSoy2h8Gzp6lkreJKUslMxeGDYPI&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Settings (gear) menu on the Agreement Manager homepage that highlights the Sets option.")

   The Agreement sets page opens. The All agreement sets view is displayed. The table lists the sets that were shared with or created by the logged-in user.
5. Locate the set that you want to update.

   Tip: Select the sorting arrows in the Name column to sort the table by set name.
6. Expand the More actions (three dots) menu for the set that you want to update and select Share:

   ![A snippet of the All agreement sets view that shows the Share option in the More actions (three dots) menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Manage-Sets-dialog_All-agreement-sets_Share-option.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc0MTgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.pLYBLkq_y-mXOGJiSoy2h8Gzp6lkreJKUslMxeGDYPI&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the All agreement sets view that shows the Share option in the More actions (three dots) menu.")

   The Share with others dialog box opens. The People with access section shows the users and groups that currently have access to the agreement set. This includes the assigned access level for the agreements in the set.

   ![A screenshot of the Share with others dialog box that shows the users and groups that currently have access to the selected set.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Manage-Sets-dialog_All-agreement-sets_Share-with-others-dialog.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc0MTgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.pLYBLkq_y-mXOGJiSoy2h8Gzp6lkreJKUslMxeGDYPI&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Share with others dialog box that shows the users and groups that currently have access to the selected set.")

   Update the sharing settings for the set and select Save or Share.

**Share the set with additional users or groups:**

7. Enter the name of the user or group with whom you want to share the set in the Add user or group field.

   A type-ahead dropdown displays names matching the criteria that you entered.
8. Select a user or group from the dropdown list.

   The Add users view is displayed.

title: Update Share Settings for an Agreement Set
heading_path: Update Share Settings for an Agreement Set
text:
in the Add user or group field.

   A type-ahead dropdown displays names matching the criteria that you entered.
8. Select a user or group from the dropdown list.

   The Add users view is displayed.

   ![A screenshot of the Add users view that shows the notification and access-level options.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-sets_share-set_Add-users-dialog-box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc0MTgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.pLYBLkq_y-mXOGJiSoy2h8Gzp6lkreJKUslMxeGDYPI&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Add users view that shows the notification and access-level options.")

   Important:

   The Notify users by email option is selected by default. Leave this checkbox selected to notify users that an agreement set was shared with them.

   If you de-select this option, users are not notified of the shared agreement set.
9. Expand the Access dropdown and select the appropriate access level for agreements in the set.

   Choices are: Can Manage, Can Edit, and Can View.
10. Select the Share option.

    The dialog box closes and you are returned to the Agreement sets page. The agreement set has been shared with the selected users or group.

**Update the "Access" level for users and groups with access to the set:**

Begin from the Agreement sets page.

11. Expand the More actions (three dots) menu for the set that you want to update and select Share.

    The Share with others dialog box opens. The People with access section shows the users and groups that currently have access to the agreement set.
12. Update the Access level for users and groups displayed in the People with access list:

    ![A snippet of the Share with others dialog box that highlights the Can View access level in the dropdown.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-set-management_Update-access-level_example.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc0MTgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.pLYBLkq_y-mXOGJiSoy2h8Gzp6lkreJKUslMxeGDYPI&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Share with others dialog box that highlights the Can View access level in the dropdown.")

    1. Expand the Access dropdown for the user or group that you want to update.
    2. Choose an access level. Choices are: Can View, Can Edit, and Can Manage.
    3. Select the Save option.

    The dialog box closes and you are returned to the Agreement sets page. The access level for the selected user or group is updated to reflect the new value.

**Revoke user or group access to an agreement set:**

Begin from the Agreement sets page.

13. Expand the More actions (three dots) menu for the set that you want to update and select Share.

    The Share with others dialog box opens. The People with access section shows the users and groups that currently have access to the agreement set.
14. Revoke user or group access to the selected agreement set:

    ![A snippet of the Share with others dialog box that highlights the Remove option in the dropdown.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-set-management_Update-access-level_Remove.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc0MTgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.pLYBLkq_y-mXOGJiSoy2h8Gzp6lkreJKUslMxeGDYPI&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Share with others dialog box that highlights the Remove option in the dropdown.")

    1. Expand the Access dropdown for the user or group that you want to update.
    2. Select the Remove option.
    3. Select the Save option.

       The dialog box closes. The user or group is no longer displayed in the People with access list for the agreement set.
15. Select Completed Documents from the breadcrumb navigation at the top of the page.

title: Update Share Settings for an Agreement Set
heading_path: Update Share Settings for an Agreement Set
text:
dialog box closes. The user or group is no longer displayed in the People with access list for the agreement set.
15. Select Completed Documents from the breadcrumb navigation at the top of the page.

    You are returned to the Agreement Manager homepage.

You have successfully updated the *Share* settings for a selected agreement set.

title: Display Clause Columns in the Agreement List Table
heading_path: Display Clause Columns in the Agreement List Table
text:
Learn how to display clause-related columns in the agreement list table.

Display clause-related columns in the agreement list table so that you can quickly scan which agreements contain key terms. For example, Termination for Convenience or specific liability caps across a group of contacts.

title: Display Clause Columns in the Agreement List Table
heading_path: Display Clause Columns in the Agreement List Table
text:
- Make sure that you can open Agreement Manager and see the agreements that you want to review. The clause filters only apply to agreements that you already have permission to view.
- If your organization uses AI-extracted clauses, confirm that clause extraction is enabled in your environment. This is required to use the clause-related columns and filters.

  Note: Clause extraction for Agreement Manager is available in Beta. Contact your Docusign account representative to inquire about participating in the Beta.
- If you want to edit or validate clause values, confirm that you have Organize and manage all agreements access to Agreement Manager. Users with Can Manage or Can Edit access to an agreement can also edit of validate clause presence.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed.
4. Select the Show or Hide Fields (toggle) icon located to the right of the filters:

   ![A snippet of the Completed Documents view that highlights the Show or Hide FIelds (toggle) icon on the right-side of the page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-documents-table_Customize-columns-gear-icon.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY0NzcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.uD1c9JHe4rz_5g3lyjUHFnHnqY7Ly3FqJyVZ23iKyJA&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Completed Documents view that highlights the Show or Hide FIelds (toggle) icon on the right-side of the page.")

   The Show or Hide FIelds dialog box opens. A number of fields are turned on (blue) by default. These fields represent the columns in the agreement list table. Columns display in the order that the fields are represented in the dialog box.

**Show the relevant clause-related columns**

5. Locate the relevant clause-related columns. Enter the name of the clause in the search field.

   Tip: Enter *Clause* in the Search Fields field to view all the clause-related columns.

   The fields (columns) that matched the search criteria are displayed.
6. Turn on the clause fields (columns) that you want to display in the table and select the Save option. Select the toggles to turn on (blue position) the relevant clauses:

   ![A snippet of the Show or Hide Fields dialog box that shows the clause-related columns being displayed in the table.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-documents-table_Show-Hide-Fields_clause-columns.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY0NzcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.uD1c9JHe4rz_5g3lyjUHFnHnqY7Ly3FqJyVZ23iKyJA&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Show or Hide Fields dialog box that shows the clause-related columns being displayed in the table.")

   The dialog box closes and you are returned to the Completed Documents view. The clause-related columns are displayed in the table.

**Re-order the table columns**

The column order in the agreement list table can be updated and saved for future use. For example, display the clauses that you want to track and reorder the columns so they are easy to locate. Use the Save option to save your filters and column customizations for future use.

7. Re-order the clause-related columns so they are easier to locate in the table:

title: Display Clause Columns in the Agreement List Table
heading_path: Display Clause Columns in the Agreement List Table
text:
olumns so they are easy to locate. Use the Save option to save your filters and column customizations for future use.

7. Re-order the clause-related columns so they are easier to locate in the table:

   ![A snippet of the Show or Hide Fields dialog box that highlights a clause column in the process of being dragged to a new location.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-documents-table_Show-Hide-Fields_reorder-columns_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY0NzcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.uD1c9JHe4rz_5g3lyjUHFnHnqY7Ly3FqJyVZ23iKyJA&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Show or Hide Fields dialog box that highlights a clause column in the process of being dragged to a new location.")

   1. Select the Show of Hide Fields (toggle) icon located on the right-side of the table.

      The Show or Hide Fields dialog box opens.
   2. Locate the clause-related fields (columns).
   3. Hove the cursor over the Reorder (dots) icon for the clause that you want to move and drag it to the appropriate location.
   4. Continue reordering the columns as needed.
   5. Select the Save option.

   The dialog box closes and the columns are reordered in the table.

**Save your column layout**

The New saved search feature saves your search terms, applied filters, and your column customizations. You can save the search without entering search criteria or applying filters, basically saving only the table layout.

8. Save your table layout for future use. Select the Save option and complete the New saved search dialog box:
   1. Enter a unique Name for the saved search. For example, "Clause-focused layout".
   2. Provide a Description for the saved search. For example, "The clause columns are displayed first in the table".
   3. Select the Save Search option.

   The dialog box closes and the saved search is displayed. The clause-related columns are displayed prominently in the table.
9. Return to the Agreement Manager homepage. Select the Completed Documents option from the breadcrumb navigation in the top left.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed.

   Tip: Since you saved the table layout, you can update the columns and the column order while still having access to the clause-forward layout. See the [Save Search Criteria for Future Use](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tskc38121a1-bbf8-4da8-8ed7-b364991f7e50.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to save the search criteria for the queries that you frequently perform.") article to learn more about saving searches.

You have successfully displayed clause-related columns in the agreement list table. These columns indicate the presence or absence of key clause language.

title: Filter Your Agreements by Clause Language
heading_path: Filter Your Agreements by Clause Language
text:
Learn how to use clause-related filters to find agreements that contain, or do not contain, specific clause language.

Filter your completed agreements to find agreements that contain (or do not contain) specific clause language. For example, you can confirm that a standard data protection clause appears in all active vendor contracts. You can also identify agreements where that language is missing so you can follow up and reduce risk exposure.

The clause-related filters are located in the Filters option on the Agreement Manager homepage. This task shows you how to apply these filters to confirm the presence of required clauses and identify any gaps.

title: Filter Your Agreements by Clause Language
heading_path: Filter Your Agreements by Clause Language
text:
- Make sure that you can open Agreement Manager and see the agreements that you want to review. The clause filters only apply to agreements that you already have permission to view.
- If your organization uses AI-extracted clauses, confirm that clause extraction is enabled in your environment. This is required to use the clause-related columns and filters.

  Note: Clause extraction for Agreement Manager is available in Beta. Contact your Docusign account representative to inquire about participating in the Beta.
- If you want to edit or validate clause values, confirm that you have Organize and manage all agreements access to Agreement Manager. Users with Can Manage or Can Edit access to an agreement can also edit of validate clause presence.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed.

**Apply clause-related filters**

The clause-related filters indicate the presence or absence of specific clause language in your agreements. Use these filters to confirm required clause language and to identify gaps.

4. Expand the Filters option and locate the filter that you want to apply:

   ![A snippet of the Completed Documents table that highlights a clause filter in the Filters option.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-documents-table_Filter-by-clause_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY0NjcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.hb0AmqAC1uxZCAxlGTWcpXUUKiOCxAvg2DHKUFQf3FA&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Completed Documents table that highlights a clause filter in the Filters option.")

   1. Enter the name of the clause in the Filter by field:

      Tip: Enter *Clause* in the Filter by field to view all the available clause filters.

      A list of suggested filters is displayed.
   2. Select the relevant clause filter.

   The filter expands and the available filter options are displayed. For clause-related filters, there are two options: Found in agreements and Not found in agreements.
5. Choose a filter value and select the Apply option:

   ![A snippet of the Completed Documents table that shows the filter values for a selected clause filter.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-documents-table_Filters_Clause-filter-options.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY0NjcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.hb0AmqAC1uxZCAxlGTWcpXUUKiOCxAvg2DHKUFQf3FA&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Completed Documents table that shows the filter values for a selected clause filter.")

   - Select the Found in agreements option to view the agreements that contain the clause language.
   - Select the Not found in agreements option to view the agreements that are missing the clause language.

   The filter closes and the agreements that matched the applied filter are listed in the table. For example, the agreements that contain the selected clause language.

**Verify clause presence in specific agreements**

Apply additional filters to verify the clause presence in a subset of agreements, such as master service agreements (MSAs) or non-disclosure agreements (NDAs).

6. Narrow the agreement list table for a specific subset of agreements. For example, to verify the presence of clause language across all your MSAs.
   1. Expand the Filters option and select the filter that you want to apply. For example, select the Agreement Type filter.

title: Filter Your Agreements by Clause Language
heading_path: Filter Your Agreements by Clause Language
text:
or example, to verify the presence of clause language across all your MSAs.
   1. Expand the Filters option and select the filter that you want to apply. For example, select the Agreement Type filter.

      The filter expands and the available filter values are displayed.
   2. Select the relevant filter value. For example, select the Master Service Agreement value.
   3. Select the Apply option.

   The filter closes and the agreements that matched the applied filter are listed in the table. For example, MSAs that contain Data Breach clause language.

**Identify gaps in your agreements**

Identify gaps where the clause is not present in your agreements. Update the clause filter to show agreements where the clause was not found.

7. Update the clause filter to identify gaps where the clause is not present. For example, locate the MSAs that are missing the Data Breach clause language.

   ![A snippet of the Completed Documents table that shows the filter values for the selected clause filter.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-documents-table_Update-clause-filter_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY0NjcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.hb0AmqAC1uxZCAxlGTWcpXUUKiOCxAvg2DHKUFQf3FA&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Completed Documents table that shows the filter values for the selected clause filter.")

   1. Select the applied clause filter from the top of the page.

      The filter expands and available values are displayed.
   2. Select the Not found in agreements value.
   3. Select the Apply option.

   The filter closes and the agreements that matched the applied filter are listed in the table. For example, MSAs that are missing the Data Breach clause language.
8. Review the agreement list and identify the agreements that you want to investigate further.

   Note: Download the agreements that require follow-up:
   - Select the checkboxes associated with the agreements that you want to download.

     The actions bar opens when at least one agreement is selected.
   - Select the Download Documents (arrow) icon from the action bar.

     A zipped folder containing the selected agreements is downloaded to your local Downloads folder.

You have successfully used clause-related filters to confirm the presence of specific clauses, including identifying any gaps.

title: Perform a Keyword Search to Locate Agreements
heading_path: Perform a Keyword Search to Locate Agreements
text:
This topic answers the question: How can I locate agreements by performing a keyword search, using type-ahead suggestions, or by selecting from my search history?

Perform a keyword search to locate agreements in Agreement Manager. After reviewing the results, use the feedback icons to rate the quality of the search. You can also download selected agreements or export agreement data.

Tip: If you routinely search for the same type of agreements or agreement data, save your search criteria for future use. See the [Save Search Criteria for Future Use](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tskc38121a1-bbf8-4da8-8ed7-b364991f7e50.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to save the search criteria for the queries that you frequently perform.") topic to learn more about the saved search feature.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed. The documents to which the logged-in user has access are listed in the table. The total document count is displayed the bottom right. This count is updated when you perform a search or apply filters.

   ![A screenshot of the Completed Documents page that shows a list of documents to which the logged-in user has access.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-view_updated-with-document-count.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTYzNTAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.RwMUimzqQC9WVWfJuO7yTLNWyWuWWxfgB3dJR37wo9I&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Completed Documents page that shows a list of documents to which the logged-in user has access.")

   Tip: The search field supports the use of advanced search queries to locate relevant agreements. Enter Boolean operators, proximity searches, term boosting, and wildcards directly in the search field to combine multiple conditions in a single query. See the [Advanced Search Queries](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=conce9d9428-f27e-49ad-9001-3c7a0820ae78.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the advanced search query feature that gives you control over how your results are matched and ranked.") article to learn more about these search queries.
4. Use the search field to locate the relevant agreements:

   ![A snippet of the type-ahead dropdown that shows the search history, suggested questions, and recent documents.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-documents-table_Search_typeahead-dropdown.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTYzNTAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.RwMUimzqQC9WVWfJuO7yTLNWyWuWWxfgB3dJR37wo9I&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the type-ahead dropdown that shows the search history, suggested questions, and recent documents.")

   1. Click in the search field. A dropdown opens and your search history and recent documents are displayed.

      Tip: Access your Saved Searches from the dropdown to run an existing query. See the [Save Search Criteria for Future Use](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tskc38121a1-bbf8-4da8-8ed7-b364991f7e50.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to save the search criteria for the queries that you frequently perform.") topic to learn more about this feature.
   2. Enter a keyword or phrase in the search field.

      As you enter a keyword in the search field, suggestions are displayed in the dropdown.
   3. Select a suggestion from the dropdown or press the [Enter] key.

title: Perform a Keyword Search to Locate Agreements
heading_path: Perform a Keyword Search to Locate Agreements
text:
yword or phrase in the search field.

      As you enter a keyword in the search field, suggestions are displayed in the dropdown.
   3. Select a suggestion from the dropdown or press the [Enter] key.

   Important: Keyword searches are different from applying filters. The search reviews the contents of agreements, while the filters display agreements based on surfaced attributes and field values.

   By default, the query attempts to match the search criteria with filter values in your documents. The number of results and the identified filters are displayed. This is not the same as performing a traditional keyword search and the results may vary between the two types of searches.

   Tip: The File Name column lists the attributes (fields) where the keyword or phrase matched the agreement data.
5. Select the Use keyword search instead option:

   ![A snippet of the agreement list table that shows query results and the 'Use keyword search instead' option.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Keyword-search_results_Use-keyword-search-instead_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTYzNTAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.RwMUimzqQC9WVWfJuO7yTLNWyWuWWxfgB3dJR37wo9I&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the agreement list table that shows query results and the 'Use keyword search instead' option.")

   Note: If the query is unable to match the search criteria with any filters, the regular keyword search is displayed.

   The agreements that matched the keyword or phrase are displayed in the agreement list table. The document count is updated based on your search criteria. In this version of the search results, the query returned agreements that contained the entered keyword or phrase. It did not use the search criteria as an applied filter.

   Tip: The keyword search automatically includes synonyms and related concepts in the search results. Select the related terms link to view additional information: ![A snippet of the agreement list table that shows query results and the Show related terms dialog box.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Search_Keyword-search_Related-terms-dialog.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTYzNTAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.RwMUimzqQC9WVWfJuO7yTLNWyWuWWxfgB3dJR37wo9I&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the agreement list table that shows query results and the Show related terms dialog box.") The Related terms toggle is turned on (blue position) by default. Select the toggle to turn this feature off (white position).
6. Review the search results to determine if the query returned the relevant agreements.
7. Use the feedback icons to rate your search experience:

   ![A snippet of the Completed Documents page that highlights the feedback icons and options to provide a reason and comments.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Keyword-search_results_share-feedback-modal_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTYzNTAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.RwMUimzqQC9WVWfJuO7yTLNWyWuWWxfgB3dJR37wo9I&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Completed Documents page that highlights the feedback icons and options to provide a reason and comments.")

   1. Choose the appropriate feedback icon. Select either the Rate as helpful (thumbs up) icon or the Rate as unhelpful (thumbs down) icon.

      The Share your reason to help us improve dialog box opens.
   2. Select a reason for choosing the rating. You can select more than one reason. The choices are: Complete, Relevant, Accurate, and Other.
   3. Enter additional details in the Comments text box.
   4. Select the Submit option.

title: Perform a Keyword Search to Locate Agreements
heading_path: Perform a Keyword Search to Locate Agreements
text:
sing the rating. You can select more than one reason. The choices are: Complete, Relevant, Accurate, and Other.
   3. Enter additional details in the Comments text box.
   4. Select the Submit option.

      Tip: Choose the Skip option to submit your rating without providing any additional information. Your rating is submitted for review.

   The dialog box closes and your feedback is submitted to Docusign.
8. If the search did not return the expected agreements, perform another keyword search:
   1. Select the Clear Search (x) icon located in the search field.

      The search criteria is cleared and the agreement list table resets.
   2. Enter new search criteria in the search field.
   3. Select a suggestion from the type-ahead dropdown or press the [Enter] key.

   The agreement list table updates and the agreements that matched the entered keyword or phrase are displayed. Continue repeating this process until you locate the relevant agreements.

   Note: After locating the relevant agreements, choose to download selected agreements or export agreement data. See these support articles to learn more about these options:
   - [Download Documents from the Agreement List Table](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=ach1724363496037.html&_LANG=enus "This topic answers the question: How do I download agreements from Agreement Manager?")
   - [Export Data from the Agreement List Table](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=pqf1724363438241.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to export agreement data to a CSV file for selected agreements.")

You have successfully performed a keyword search to locate relevant agreements.

title: Best Practice: Writing Definitions for AI Detection
heading_path: Best Practice: Writing Definitions for AI Detection
text:
Read about tips for writing good definitions for the AI detection setup process. These
definitions are an important part of the process and help to "teach" the AI
detection.

During AI detection setup, the input definition step helps to "teach" the
system and enables the AI detection to "learn" by example. Writing a "good" definition
is imperative to the AI detection process.

title: Best Practice: Writing Definitions for AI Detection
heading_path: Best Practice: Writing Definitions for AI Detection
text:
When adding a definition during AI detection setup, adhere to the following
guidelines. A "good" definition is:

- **Clear and concise:** The definition explains the extraction in a
  straightforward way so that someone without expertise can understand it.
- **General and adaptable:** The definition is not tied to a specific industry
  or use case.
- **Simple language:** The definition does not contain complex legal or
  financial jargon.
- **Exclusion:** The definition provides details about what not to include in
  the AI detection.
- **100–1,000 characters:** The definition is clear without excessive detail
  and does not exceed 1,000 characters.

title: Best Practice: Writing Definitions for AI Detection
heading_path: Best Practice: Writing Definitions for AI Detection
text:
**NDA**

A Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA) is a legal contract that establishes confidentiality
obligations between parties. It restricts the sharing or use of specified
confidential information and protects sensitive business, financial, or personal
data from unauthorized disclosure.

**Total Contract Value**

Total Contract Value (TCV) refers to the total financial commitment of an agreement. This
includes all fees, charges, and recurring payments over the contract’s full term. It
helps assess the overall value of an agreement for budgeting, financial planning,
and reporting purposes.

title: Best Practice: Writing Definitions for AI Detection
heading_path: Best Practice: Writing Definitions for AI Detection
text:
**NDA**

An NDA is a contract that you sign when joining a startup to make sure you don’t talk
about internal projects. It usually lasts for two years and applies to employees,
contractors, and sometimes investors.

Why is this bad?

- **Too specific:** The definition focuses only on startups and employment
  scenarios.
- **Inaccurate:** NDAs apply to many contexts beyond hiring.
- **Limiting:** The definition does not cover broader use cases like
  partnerships, M&A, or business deals.

**Total Contract Value**

TCV is the total amount a SaaS company expects to make from a single customer’s
three-year subscription, including all renewals and upsells. It helps SaaS finance
teams plan revenue projections.

Why is this bad?

- **Too narrow:** The definition is tied to SaaS businesses and ignores other
  industries.
- **Overly specific:** The definition mentions *three-year subscriptions*
  and *SaaS finance teams*, which limit applicability.
- **Misses the broader purpose:** The definition does not generalize how TCV
  applies across different contract types.

title: Enable AI Detection for Your Custom Fields
heading_path: Enable AI Detection for Your Custom Fields
text:
Learn how to enable the AI detection for your custom fields. Once activated, the field is surfaced in your new agreements automatically.

When you first create a custom field, the AI detection is inactive by default. Complete the Set Up process to activate the custom field. This process is designed to "teach" the AI model and enable the AI detection to "learn" by example. Once activated, the custom data point is surfaced in your new agreements and selected historical documents.

Important: This task requires a user-defined custom field that is not activated for AI detection. See the [Add Custom Fields to Agreement Types](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=hcn1736879099815.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to create and add custom fields to the custom and standard agreement types in your account.") article for steps to configure new fields.

Locate the custom field that you want to enable for AI detection and complete the Set Up process. This task is completed by users with Organize and manage all agreements access to Agreement Manager agreements and functionality. For example, administrators and contract managers.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed.

**Locate the custom field**

If you know the agreement type for the field, you can locate it from the *Agreement Types Manager*. If you know the name of the field, you can search for it in the *Fields Manager*.

4. Expand the Settings (gear) menu from the top right and select either Agreement Types or Fields:

   ![A snippet of the Settings dropdown that highlights the Fields and Agreement Types options.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Manage-Agreement-Types_More-actions-menu_expanded.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2NTQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.1vno5LRovFsIhyzJY4sZtK5fstwGHTxDAVsVnoRdEhQ&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Settings dropdown that highlights the Fields and Agreement Types options.")Depending on which option you selected, either the Mange Fields page or the Manage Agreement Types page is displayed.
5. Search for the relevant custom field:
   - From the Manage Fields page: Enter a search term in the Search fields... field or use the filters to locate the relevant field. Filter options are: Created By, Category, Field Type, and AI Status. Expand the filter, choose a value, and select the Apply option.

     The fields that matched the entered search term and applied filters are displayed in the FIelds table.
   - From the Manage Agreement Types page: Search for and select (click) the agreement type that contains the custom field. The agreement type details page opens and the associated fields are listed in the Fields table. Perform a keyword search and apply filters to locate the relevant field.

     The fields that matched the search term and applied filters are displayed in the Fields table.
6. Select the Set Up option for the custom field that you want to enable for AI detection.

   The AI detection set-up wizard launches.

   ![A screenshot of the message box that provides high-level instructions for enabling AI detection.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-type-management_enable-AI-for-custom-fields_informational-message.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2NTQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.1vno5LRovFsIhyzJY4sZtK5fstwGHTxDAVsVnoRdEhQ&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the message box that provides high-level instructions for enabling AI detection.")

**Define the field**

title: Enable AI Detection for Your Custom Fields
heading_path: Enable AI Detection for Your Custom Fields
text:
HF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.1vno5LRovFsIhyzJY4sZtK5fstwGHTxDAVsVnoRdEhQ&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the message box that provides high-level instructions for enabling AI detection.")

**Define the field**

7. Select the Get Started option.

   The Set definition dialog box opens and asks you to define the custom field.
8. Define the custom field.
   1. Enter a detailed definition for the custom field. This field is limited to 2,000 characters. The definition describes the purpose of the field.

      Note: See the [Best Practice: Writing Definitions for AI Detection](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tmk1741317734856.html&_LANG=enus "Read about tips for writing good definitions for the AI detection setup process. These definitions are an important part of the process and help to \"teach\" the AI detection.") article to review the
      guidelines for writing definitions. Follow these guidelines to get
      the most out of the AI detection and to ensure accuracy.

      When the definition exceeds 50 characters, the Refine with AI option becomes available. Use this option to update the definition for consistency and accuracy.
   2. Fine-tune the definition. Select the Refine with AI option. The dialog box refreshes and a suggested definition is displayed.
   3. Confirm the definition. Review and edit the suggested definition as needed. You can also select the Undo option to revert to the original version of the definition.
   4. Select the Next option.

   Note: If you are enabling a custom dropdown field, you are prompted to add details for each option. The descriptions help the AI model to identify the data points in your documents. Enter a description for each of the options in the dropdown and select the Next option:![A screenshot of the Add details for each option dialog box that shows example descriptions.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-type-management_Enable-AI_Add-details-for-options_Dropdowns.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2NTQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.1vno5LRovFsIhyzJY4sZtK5fstwGHTxDAVsVnoRdEhQ&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Add details for each option dialog box that shows example descriptions.")

   The AI detection set-up wizard advances to the next step and you are prompted to provide contextual examples for the custom field.

**Add examples of the field**

Important:

When you provide example field values, include specific phrases or paragraphs so the AI
models can detect the data in a contextual manner. If you provide a single word, the model is unable
to detect the data due to lack of context.

9. Choose your preferred method for providing examples and select the Next option.
   - Select the Paste examples from your documents option and click Next.

     The Paste examples of the field dialog box opens.

     1. Paste examples of the field. Be sure to include whole sentences or paragraphs.
     2. Confirm the field value. Locate the field value within the provided example and enter it in the text-entry field.
     3. Repeat these steps for each example. Add at least 3–5 examples for the best results.
     4. Select Save after providing the necessary examples.
   - Select the Highlight examples directly in documents option and click Next.

     The Select relevant documents page opens.

     1. Enter search criteria and apply filters to locate documents that likely contain the custom field.
     2. Choose the documents that contain the relevant examples and select the Start Highlighting option.

        The Agreement preview page opens for the first document. You are prompted to locate the field in each document that you selected.
     3. Highlight the entire section that contains the custom field from the preview pane and select Confirm selection.

title: Enable AI Detection for Your Custom Fields
heading_path: Enable AI Detection for Your Custom Fields
text:
cument. You are prompted to locate the field in each document that you selected.
     3. Highlight the entire section that contains the custom field from the preview pane and select Confirm selection.

        You are prompted to confirm the field value.
     4. Locate the field value from within the agreement and enter it in the text-entry field. Select Confirm.

        Continue highlighting the example content and confirming the field value for each document that you selected.
     5. Select the Next option from the bottom of the pane.

        The Paste examples of the field dialog box opens.
     6. Review the examples that you provided, including the confirmed field values. Select Save.

   A dialog box opens and a message confirms that AI detection is now available for the custom field. You are prompted to test for accuracy or activate AI detection.

   ![A screenshot of the AI detection is now available dialog box that confirms AI detection is available for the custom field.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Manage-Agreement-Type_Enable-AI-detection-custom-fields_test-or-activate.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2NTQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.1vno5LRovFsIhyzJY4sZtK5fstwGHTxDAVsVnoRdEhQ&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the AI detection is now available dialog box that confirms AI detection is available for the custom field.")

**Test for accuracy**

Before you activate the AI detection for custom extractions, the setup wizard prompts you to test for accuracy. This step is optional, however, it is highly recommended. The results are a good indicator of the quality and accuracy that you can expect from the AI detection before activating it.

10. Choose the Test for accuracy option and select the Confirm option. This option is selected by default.

    The Select relevant documents page opens. Select at least one document that is likely to contain the custom field.
11. Select the documents that you want to use to test the AI detection accuracy:
    1. Search for the relevant agreements. Enter a keyword or search string in the search field. The dropdown opens and search suggestions are displayed. Select a search suggestion or press the Enter key.
    2. Filter the agreement list table to refine the results list. For example, filter by Sets, Status, Parties, Agreement Type, or AI Suggestions.

       Tip: Use the All Filters option to apply additional filters to the results table.

       Expand the filter, choose the relevant filter value, and select the Apply option.
    3. Choose the documents that you want to use in the test.
    4. Select the Start Testing option.

    The Agreement preview opens for the first document. The pane on the left shows the agreement type that the AI detection identified in the document.
12. Rate the accuracy of the findings.
    1. Review the identified field in the context of the document. Verify that the AI detection surfaced the correct value. Select Yes if the AI detection was correct or No if the field was not correctly identified.

       Note: If the field was not located in a selected document, a message is displayed. Be sure to include agreements that do not contain the field when testing the accuracy. It's important for the AI model to recognize when the field is not present.

       The Agreement preview page updates and the next document is displayed.
    2. Repeat this step until you are finished verifying the identified field in all the selected documents.
    3. Select the Done option.

    The Results dialog box opens and the AI detection test results are displayed.

title: Enable AI Detection for Your Custom Fields
heading_path: Enable AI Detection for Your Custom Fields
text:
p until you are finished verifying the identified field in all the selected documents.
    3. Select the Done option.

    The Results dialog box opens and the AI detection test results are displayed.

    ![A screenshot of the Results dialog box that shows the AI detection test results.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Fields-Manager_Enable-AI-detection_Set-Up_Results-dialog-box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2NTQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.1vno5LRovFsIhyzJY4sZtK5fstwGHTxDAVsVnoRdEhQ&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Results dialog box that shows the AI detection test results.")

    Important: If the results do not meet your accuracy requirements, use the Refine definition and examples option to refine your definition and add more examples. Retest the AI detection. Continue refining the definition and examples until the AI detection achieves the expected results.

**Activate the AI detection**

You are prompted to activate the AI detection for the custom field.

13. Activate the AI detection for the field. The
    Activate AI detection option is selected by default. Select the Confirm option.

    The Activate AI detection dialog box opens. Use this dialog box to choose when the system should look for the custom field. The All new agreements option is selected by default. This option cannot be deselected.

    ![A screenshot of the Activate AI detection dialog box that prompts you to choose when to look for the field.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Manage-Agreement-Type_Enable-AI-detection-custom-fields_Activate.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2NTQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.1vno5LRovFsIhyzJY4sZtK5fstwGHTxDAVsVnoRdEhQ&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Activate AI detection dialog box that prompts you to choose when to look for the field.")
14. Choose when to look for the custom field and select Activate.

    Tip: Select Activate to continue without selecting historical documents to process. The dialog box closes and the AI detection is active for the custom field. The definitions and examples that you provided will be used to identify the agreement type in your documents.

    In this example, accept the default option, All new agreements, and select Activate.

    Note: The Find on specific agreements option can be used at any time to process specific documents for custom fields. See the [Find Custom Fields in Specific Documents](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tsk79824e36-d51d-4477-a5ca-7228cde30fb2.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to use the Find On Specific Documents function to surface custom fields in selected agreements.") article to learn more about this function.

    The dialog box closes and the custom field is published.
15. Return to the Agreement Manager homepage. Select the Close (X) icon from the top left.

    The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents
    view is displayed.

You have successfully enabled the AI detection for a custom field by completing the Set Up process. The definitions and examples that you provided will be used to identify the custom field across your documents.

title: Create Custom Fields from the Agreement Preview
heading_path: Create Custom Fields from the Agreement Preview
text:
Learn how to create custom fields from the Agreement preview page for a selected agreement.

Custom field extractions are used to capture business-specific data points that standard extractions might not track. For example, use custom fields to capture terms, dates, amounts, and other details that are important to your organization.

Note: Administrators automatically have access to configure agreement data. For non-administrator users, this feature requires Organize and manage all agreements access to Agreement Manager. These tasks are primarily completed by administrators and contract managers.

Use the Add a field function to configure new custom fields. This feature has three main entry points:

- Fields Manager
- Agreement Types Manager
- Agreement preview page

This topic reviews the steps to create custom fields from the Agreement preview page. When you add custom fields from this location, the *Define the field* step combines field configuration and definition.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed. A list of documents to which the logged-in user has access is displayed in the table.

**Select an agreement**

4. Locate the relevant agreement. For example, the agreement that contains the data points for the new custom field.

   ![A snippet of the Agreement Manager homepage that shows the search field and the Filters option.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-documents_search-field_filters-option-expanded_snippet.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2NTYsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.0rhCh0UBLBKwB_PF1WdjbyqZLsbcsDvYJo3bxf8yvpQ&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Agreement Manager homepage that shows the search field and the Filters option.")

   1. Search for the relevant agreement. Enter a keyword in the search field. As you enter a value, the dropdown opens and a list of suggested searches is displayed. Select a suggested search from the dropdown or press the Enter key.

      The agreement list table updates and agreements that matched the search term are displayed.
   2. Filter the table to locate the relevant agreement. Expand (click) the Filters option and select the filter that you want to apply. Choose your filter values and select the Apply option.

      The agreement list table updates and agreements that matched the applied filters are displayed.
5. Select (click) an agreement from the table.

   The Agreement preview page opens for the selected agreement.

**Add the custom field**

6. Launch the Add a Field function. There are two ways to access this function from the Agreement preview page.
   - From the Details pane: Select the Add (+) icon and select the Add a Field option.

     ![A snippet of the Agreement preview page that highlights the Add (+) icon in the Details pane.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Fields-Manager_Agreement-preview_Details_Add-icon_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2NTYsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.0rhCh0UBLBKwB_PF1WdjbyqZLsbcsDvYJo3bxf8yvpQ&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Agreement preview page that highlights the Add (+) icon in the Details pane.")

     Tip: Use this option to add metadata to an agreement without tying it to a specific sentence or clause. For example, you want to create a field to capture data about the agreement, like 'internal owner' or 'risk rating'. This information is not tied to a specific sentence or clause in the document.

title: Create Custom Fields from the Agreement Preview
heading_path: Create Custom Fields from the Agreement Preview
text:
use. For example, you want to create a field to capture data about the agreement, like 'internal owner' or 'risk rating'. This information is not tied to a specific sentence or clause in the document.

     .
   - From the Preview pane: Highlight the text that contains the relevant data points and select Track with AI from the dropdown list.

     ![A snippet of the Agreement preview page that demonstrates the text highlighting option for adding a field.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Custom-field-extraction_Agreement-preview_text-highlighting_animated-gif.gif?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2NTYsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.0rhCh0UBLBKwB_PF1WdjbyqZLsbcsDvYJo3bxf8yvpQ&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Agreement preview page that demonstrates the text highlighting option for adding a field.")

     Tip: Use this option when you want to track content that is directly associated with specific text in the agreement. For example, you want to track a specific clause, sentence, or value in the agreement.

   The Add a Field dialog box opens. When accessing the function from the Agreement preview page, you have the option to initiate the AI detection setup.

   ![A screenshot of the Add a Field dialog box that shows the required fields for creating a field.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Fields-Manager_Agreement-preview_Add-a-Field-dialog-box_Full.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2NTYsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.0rhCh0UBLBKwB_PF1WdjbyqZLsbcsDvYJo3bxf8yvpQ&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Add a Field dialog box that shows the required fields for creating a field.")

**Define the field details**

7. Enter a name for the custom field in the Field name field:

   ![A snippet of the Add a Field dialog box that highlights the Create Field option for the custom field.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Custom-field-extraction_Add-a-Field_Create-field-name_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2NTYsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.0rhCh0UBLBKwB_PF1WdjbyqZLsbcsDvYJo3bxf8yvpQ&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Add a Field dialog box that highlights the Create Field option for the custom field.")

   As you enter a name, a type-ahead dropdown list opens and fields that match the entered name are displayed.

   Tip: Review the type-ahead dropdown list to verify that the custom field does not already exist. If the field exists, you are unable to create a new one with the same name.

   If the field does not exist, select the + Create [Field name] option.
8. Expand the Field type dropdown and select the type of field that you are adding:

   ![A snippet of the Add a field dialog box that shows the available field types for your custom field.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Add-custom-field_Field-type-options.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2NTYsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.0rhCh0UBLBKwB_PF1WdjbyqZLsbcsDvYJo3bxf8yvpQ&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Add a field dialog box that shows the available field types for your custom field.")

   Choices are: Text, Number, Date, and Dropdown.

   Tip: If you are adding a Dropdown field, enter the dropdown options in the text-entry box. Use a semicolon to separate each option.
9. Apply the field to the relevant agreement types. Click in the Agreement types field, choose the agreement types for the field, and select the Apply option:

title: Create Custom Fields from the Agreement Preview
heading_path: Create Custom Fields from the Agreement Preview
text:
Use a semicolon to separate each option.
9. Apply the field to the relevant agreement types. Click in the Agreement types field, choose the agreement types for the field, and select the Apply option:

   ![A snippet of the Add a Field dialog box that highlights the Agreement types dropdown for adding field to multiple types.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Custom-field-extractions_Add-a-field_Agreement-types_expanded.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2NTYsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.0rhCh0UBLBKwB_PF1WdjbyqZLsbcsDvYJo3bxf8yvpQ&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Add a Field dialog box that highlights the Agreement types dropdown for adding field to multiple types.")

   Note: The current agreement type is displayed in the Agreement types field by default. This value is pulled from the selected agreement.
10. Assign a classification to the field. Expand the Category dropdown and select an option from the list.

    Choices are: General, Termination, Renewal, Payment, Legal and Compliance, Human Resources, and Other.

**Without AI detection (optional)**

11. If AI detection is not necessary for the custom field, you are finished with this process:

    ![A screenshot of the Add a Field dialog box that shows a custom field with no AI detection.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Custom-field_Add-a-field_No-AI-setup_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2NTYsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.0rhCh0UBLBKwB_PF1WdjbyqZLsbcsDvYJo3bxf8yvpQ&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Add a Field dialog box that shows a custom field with no AI detection.")

    1. Deselect the Use AI to find the field in future agreements option.

       The remaining fields collapse and are no longer displayed.
    2. Select the Save option.

    The dialog box closes. The new field is displayed in the Details pane for the agreement.

    Note: You can complete the AI detection setup from the data management pages. See the [Enable AI Detection for Your Custom Fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tsk02bd31a2-b125-4d53-b632-20d9972163a8.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to enable the AI detection for your custom fields. Once activated, the field is surfaced in your new agreements automatically.") topic to learn about activating custom fields for AI detection.

**Initiate the AI detection setup (recommended)**

After defining the field
details, you are prompted to initiate the AI detection setup process. Leave this
option selected if you want to surface the custom field in other agreements. For
example, in newly completed agreements and in selected historical documents.

![A screenshot of the Add a Field dialog box that shows the field details and the AI detection setup option.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Custom-field-extraction_Add-a-field_Initiate-AI-detection.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2NTYsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.0rhCh0UBLBKwB_PF1WdjbyqZLsbcsDvYJo3bxf8yvpQ&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Add a Field dialog box that shows the field details and the AI detection setup option.")

12. The Use AI to find this field in future agreements option is selected by default. Accept the default selection.
13. Describe the purpose of the field. Enter a detailed definition for the field in the text-entry box. This field is limited to 2,000 characters.

title: Create Custom Fields from the Agreement Preview
heading_path: Create Custom Fields from the Agreement Preview
text:
is selected by default. Accept the default selection.
13. Describe the purpose of the field. Enter a detailed definition for the field in the text-entry box. This field is limited to 2,000 characters.

    ![A snippet of the Add a Field dialog box that shows a detailed definition for the new field.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Custom-field-extraction_Add-a-field_Enter-detailed-definition.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2NTYsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.0rhCh0UBLBKwB_PF1WdjbyqZLsbcsDvYJo3bxf8yvpQ&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Add a Field dialog box that shows a detailed definition for the new field.")

    Note: See the [Best Practice: Writing Definitions for AI Detection](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tmk1741317734856.html&_LANG=enus "Read about tips for writing good definitions for the AI detection setup process. These definitions are an important part of the process and help to \"teach\" the AI detection.") support topic to review the guidelines for writing definitions. Follow these guidelines to get the most our of the AI detection and to ensure accuracy.

    When the definition exceeds 50 characters, the Refine with AI option becomes available. Use this option to update the definition for consistency and accuracy.
14. Fine-tune the definition.

    ![A snippet of the Add a Field dialog box that highlights the Refine with AI option.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Fields-Manager_Agreement-preview_Add-a-Field_Refine-with-AI_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2NTYsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.0rhCh0UBLBKwB_PF1WdjbyqZLsbcsDvYJo3bxf8yvpQ&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Add a Field dialog box that highlights the Refine with AI option.")

    1. Select the Refine with AI option.

       The dialog box refreshes and a suggested definition is displayed.
    2. Review and edit the suggested definition as needed.

       ![A snippet of the Add a Field dialog box that shows the updated definition that was refined with AI.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Custom-field-extraction_Add-a-Field_Refine-with-AI_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2NTYsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.0rhCh0UBLBKwB_PF1WdjbyqZLsbcsDvYJo3bxf8yvpQ&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Add a Field dialog box that shows the updated definition that was refined with AI.")

       Tip: Select the Undo option to revert to the original version of the definition.
15. Select the Save option.

    The dialog box closes and a message confirms that the field is saved.

    The Track this field with AI setup wizard launches:

    ![A screenshot of the Track this field with AI setup wizard that displays when you create custom fields from the Agreement preview page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Custom-field-extractions_Track-this-field-with-AI_setup-wizard.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2NTYsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.0rhCh0UBLBKwB_PF1WdjbyqZLsbcsDvYJo3bxf8yvpQ&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Track this field with AI setup wizard that displays when you create custom fields from the Agreement preview page.")

    There are three steps to enable the AI detection for the custom field: Define the field,
    select examples, and test for accuracy. By creating the field from the
    Agreement preview page, you completed the first step
    when you configured the field.
16. Select the Set Up option.

title: Create Custom Fields from the Agreement Preview
heading_path: Create Custom Fields from the Agreement Preview
text:
ld,
    select examples, and test for accuracy. By creating the field from the
    Agreement preview page, you completed the first step
    when you configured the field.
16. Select the Set Up option.

    The set-up wizard advances to the next step and you are prompted to select examples of the field.

    Complete the [Add Examples of Your Custom Fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tskcb56face-b8de-4cf9-85ce-6eac3669dd82.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to complete the second step of the AI detection set-up process, Select examples.") task to provide examples of the field. This step helps the AI model to find the field in different contexts.

You have successfully created a new custom field from the Agreement preview page. You also completed the first step of the AI detection set-up wizard, *Define the field*.

title: Customize Notification Settings for Your Completed Agreements
heading_path: Customize Notification Settings for Your Completed Agreements
text:
Learn how to customize your email notification settings from the Notification Preferences page.

Update your notification settings from the Notification Preferences page. These preferences give you control over the email notifications that you receive. By default, all the notification types in the Completed Agreements section are enabled.

Tip: Periodically review your Notification Preferences to ensure that you are receiving the relevant email notifications. Additional notification types will be added in the future.

The Notification Preferences page is accessed from the My Preferences option in the user profile menu.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select your user profile avatar located in the top-right corner of the
   page.

   The user profile menu opens.
3. Select the My Preferences option.

   The General Settings page opens. Your default account details and ID card are displayed.
4. Select Notification Preferences from the navigation pane on the left.

   The Notification Preferences page opens. Use this page to customize your
   email notification settings.
5. Expand the Completed Agreements section and update your email notification settings as needed:

   ![A screenshot of the Notification Preferences page that highlights the selected notification types for completed agreements.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/My-preferences_Notification-Preferences_For-Completed-Agreements_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcxMTQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.WvXYgdXlqwuasRsrqqKouEofxMiJdSinowWU2mD1fhc&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Notification Preferences page that highlights the selected notification types for completed agreements.")

   1. Select (click) the Completed Agreements section heading.

      The section expands and the available notification types for completed agreements are displayed.
   2. Review the Notification Type list and select the checkboxes associated with the email notifications that you want to receive. All notification types are selected, or enabled, by default.
   3. Deselect a checkbox if you no longer want to receive email notifications for the corresponding notification type.

   By default, all the notification types in the Completed Agreements section are enabled.
6. Select the Save option.

   A message box opens and confirms that the updated preferences were saved.

You have successfully updated your notification settings from the Notification Preferences page.

title: View Envelope Details from the Agreement List Table
heading_path: View Envelope Details from the Agreement List Table
text:
Learn how to view the envelope details for an agreement that was imported from a completed eSignature envelope.

If an agreement was imported from a completed eSignature envelope, the envelope ID is displayed in the agreements list in the Envelope ID column. Select the hyperlinked envelope ID to view the corresponding envelope details.

Tip: See these topics to learn more about customizing the agreement list table, including displaying the Envelope ID column:

- [Display the Envelope ID in the Agreement List Table](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=sqs1748881628076.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to configure the agreement list table to display the Envelope ID column.")
- [Customize the Agreement List Table to Show Relevant Data](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=qrh1702945502942.html&_LANG=enus "This topic answers the question: How can I see relevant agreement data in the Agreement Manager table? Learn how to configure the columns in the agreement list table to display relevant data.")

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed.
4. Perform a search and apply filters to locate the relevant agreements:
   1. Enter a keyword or string in the search field and select a suggestion from the dropdown or press the Enter key.
