      The agreements containing the keyword or search string that you entered are displayed in the table.
   2. Use the filters to refine the list of search results. For example, use the Parties filter to view a list of agreements for a specific party.

      1. Expand the Filters option and select the filter that you want to apply.
      2. Choose a filter value.
      3. Select the Apply option.

      The agreements that match the applied filters are displayed in the table.
5. Verify that the Envelope ID column is displayed in the agreement list table.

   If necessary, follow the steps in the [Display the Envelope ID in the Agreement List Table](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=sqs1748881628076.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to configure the agreement list table to display the Envelope ID column.") topic to display the column.
6. Locate the relevant agreement and select the associated Envelope ID link:

   ![A snippet of the Completed Documents view that highlights the Envelope ID for an agreement.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-view_Customize-columns_Envelope-ID-in-table.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY0ODMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.HmhaxUDWsBBfl575NV4O2LQCD_7lq39RFTT4E_Wk_D8&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Completed Documents view that highlights the Envelope ID for an agreement.")

   Tip: The Envelope ID column is only populated if the agreement was imported from a completed eSignature envelope.

   The envelope details page opens and information about the selected envelope ID is displayed.

   ![A screenshot of the envelope details page for a selected envelope ID.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Envelope-ID_envelope-details-page.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY0ODMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.HmhaxUDWsBBfl575NV4O2LQCD_7lq39RFTT4E_Wk_D8&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the envelope details page for a selected envelope ID.")
7. Review the envelope details as needed, including the:

   - Envelope status
   - Recipient list and status
   - Message

title: View Envelope Details from the Agreement List Table
heading_path: View Envelope Details from the Agreement List Table
text:
enus "A screenshot of the envelope details page for a selected envelope ID.")
7. Review the envelope details as needed, including the:

   - Envelope status
   - Recipient list and status
   - Message

   Note: See these topics to learn more about viewing and managing envelope details:
   - [Managing Envelopes](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=oeq1643226594604&topicId=ghu1578456429097.html)
   - [View Envelope Details](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=oeq1643226594604&topicId=otr1578456349846.html)
8. Select the Agreements tab in the global navigation at the top of the page.

   You are returned to the Agreement Manager homepage. The Completed Documents view is displayed.

You have successfully located the envelope details for an agreement that was imported from a completed eSignature envelope.

title: Download the Pre-Formatted Spreadsheet File
text: Learn how to download the pre-formatted spreadsheet file for a group of documents.

title: Download the Pre-Formatted Spreadsheet File
text:
Verify that you have the appropriate permissions before you use the *Bulk Metadata Editing* feature. This feature requires bulk upload access and either Can Manage or Can Edit access to the agreements being updated. See the [Bulk Metadata Editing](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=concb493b18-adf8-4fe5-8b99-dd90cbc28399.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the Bulk Metadata Editing feature, including use cases, typical roles and permissions, and a process overview.") article to learn more about this feature.

Attention: Before downloading the spreadsheet, be sure that your custom fields are enabled in Agreement Manager. The pre-formatted spreadsheet contains all the custom and standard fields in the account. If you plan on adding custom data, the spreadsheet must contain a column for the custom field. You cannot add columns and rows to the pre-formatted spreadsheet.

The *Bulk Metadata Editing* feature is used to update the agreement data for a group of documents all at once. This is accomplished by using a pre-formatted, Docusign-generated spreadsheet file. Use this spreadsheet as a template for editing your agreement data in bulk.

Begin by downloading the pre-formatted spreadsheet file for a group of documents. This task is completed from the agreement list table on the Agreement Manager homepage.

Important: The function used to download the metadata spreadsheet is not the same as the Download files and Export to CSV functions. The metadata spreadsheet is designed for the bulk metadata editing process. You can only download it from the Edit (pencil) dropdown on the Agreement Manager homepage. This option becomes available after selecting at least one document from the agreement list table.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed. The agreements to which you have access are listed in the table.
4. Filter the agreement list table to locate the documents that you want to update. For example, filter the table by agreement set or upload job.
   - Locate agreements by upload job (recommended for editing recently migrated documents):![A snippet of the Uploaded Agreements table that highlights the Browse Files option for an upload job.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Bulk-metadata-editing_locate-docs_filter-by-upload-job.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY3OTMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.NzWGIzHDdPM3JB0zHYVKKvaD_9LE8f4mZMN7yvg4jHQ&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Uploaded Agreements table that highlights the Browse Files option for an upload job.")

     - Begin from the Agreement Manager homepage. Expand the Settings (gear) menu and select the Uploads and Processing Status option. The job monitoring hub opens to the All jobs page. The Uploaded Agreements view is displayed.
     - Apply filters or scroll to locate the relevant upload job. Filter options include: Created date, Status, and Source. You can also use the Sort items (arrows) icons to sort the table by column.
     - Expand the Actions option for the upload job and select the Export Agreement Data option.

title: Download the Pre-Formatted Spreadsheet File
text:
d date, Status, and Source. You can also use the Sort items (arrows) icons to sort the table by column.
     - Expand the Actions option for the upload job and select the Export Agreement Data option.

     The View or edit agreement data in a spreadsheet dialog box opens. Proceed to step 7.
   - Locate the agreements by set. Expand the Filters option, select the Sets filter, choose the relevant set, and select the Apply option:![A snippet of the Agreement Manager homepage that shows the Sets filter to locate agreements by set.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Bulk-metadata-editing_locate-docs_filter-by-set.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY3OTMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.NzWGIzHDdPM3JB0zHYVKKvaD_9LE8f4mZMN7yvg4jHQ&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Agreement Manager homepage that shows the Sets filter to locate agreements by set.")

     The agreement list table updates and the documents that matched the applied filters are displayed.

   Tip: You can edit up to 1,000 agreements at a time in a single upload job. Larger data sets are processed in batches.
5. Select the documents that you want to update:
   - Select the Select all checkbox from the header row. This option selects all the agreements on the current page of results.
   - Select the checkboxes for the documents that you want to update.
   - Select all the documents that match the applied filters. After you select at least one document from the table, additional actions are displayed, including the Selected dropdown. This option shows a count of the selected documents:

     Expand the Selected dropdown and select the Select all that match your filters option:

     ![A snippet of the Completed Documents page that shows the Select all that match your filters option in the agreement list table.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Bulk-metadata-editing_Locate-docs_Select-all-that-match-your-filters.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY3OTMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.NzWGIzHDdPM3JB0zHYVKKvaD_9LE8f4mZMN7yvg4jHQ&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Completed Documents page that shows the Select all that match your filters option in the agreement list table.")

   Warning: Be sure to select all the documents that you want to update. The pre-formatted spreadsheet contains a row for each of the selected agreements. Due to mapping issues, you cannot add a row to the spreadsheet once it is downloaded. The row must contain a Docusign-generated agreement ID to map the edited metadata to the correct agreement.
6. Expand the Edit (pencil) dropdown and select the Agreement Data option:

   ![A snippet of the Completed Documents page that shows the Edit Agreement Data option in the Edit (pencil) dropdown.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Bulk-metadata-editing_Edit-dropdown_Edit-Agreement-Data_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY3OTMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.NzWGIzHDdPM3JB0zHYVKKvaD_9LE8f4mZMN7yvg4jHQ&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Completed Documents page that shows the Edit Agreement Data option in the Edit (pencil) dropdown.")

   The View or edit agreement data in a spreadsheet dialog box opens. The File name field defaults to a system-generated name.
7. Download the pre-formatted spreadsheet for the selected documents:

title: Download the Pre-Formatted Spreadsheet File
text:
")

   The View or edit agreement data in a spreadsheet dialog box opens. The File name field defaults to a system-generated name.
7. Download the pre-formatted spreadsheet for the selected documents:

   ![A screenshot of the View or edit agreement data in a spreadsheet dialog box that shows the Download and Edit option.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Bulk-metadata-editing_View-edit-agreement-data-spreadsheet_Download-and-Edit.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY3OTMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.NzWGIzHDdPM3JB0zHYVKKvaD_9LE8f4mZMN7yvg4jHQ&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the View or edit agreement data in a spreadsheet dialog box that shows the Download and Edit option.")

   1. Enter a unique name for the spreadsheet in the File name field or accept the system-generated default value.
   2. Select the Download and Edit option.

   The pre-formatted spreadsheet file is downloaded to your local Downloads folder and the dialog box advances to the next step, *Import data*. You can skip this step for now and come back to it later.
8. After adding the relevant metadata to the spreadsheet, import the same file back into your account. For now, select the Skip for Now option.

   The dialog box closes and you are returned to the Agreement Manager homepage.

You have successfully downloaded the Docusign-generated, pre-formatted spreadsheet file. Use this spreadsheet to update the metadata for the selected documents. Continue to the next step of the process, [Edit the Metadata Spreadsheet](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tsk8710c3f2-c881-4219-96b0-2b287e8b4e03.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to edit the metadata spreadsheet file to bulk update the agreement data for the selected documents.").

title: Create a Hierarchy of Related Agreements
heading_path: Create a Hierarchy of Related Agreements
text:
Learn how to create a hierarchy of related agreements for a selected party or group.

Hierarchies show how agreements are legally related. If there are no existing hierarchies for the selected party or group, use the Create Hierarchy option to build one.

This task reviews the steps to create an agreement hierarchy from the party hub pages.

Tip: You can also initiate the Create Hierarchy process from the Related agreements pane on the Agreement preview page: ![A snippet of the Agreement preview page that highlights the Create Hierarchy option on the Related agreements pane.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-Preview-page_Create-Hierarchy_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc4MzUsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.MvZVzAbqx6mXekq_J8WSd7yFM2IvH9VxIBjzTNvGMnc&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Agreement preview page that highlights the Create Hierarchy option on the Related agreements pane.")Select the Create Hierarchy option to initiate the process. The Create Hierarchy page opens in the party hub for the selected party. In this case, the first party that was listed in the Details pane on the Agreement preview page.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens. The Inbox view is displayed by default.
3. Expand the PARTIES section in the navigation pane on the left and select the All option.

   The Parties page opens and a list of individual and merged parties is displayed in the table.
4. Select the relevant party or group from the parties list.

   ![A snippet of the Parties page that highlights the entered keyword and the relevant party in the search results list.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Contract-Hierarchy_Parties_Select-Party_search-highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc4MzUsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.MvZVzAbqx6mXekq_J8WSd7yFM2IvH9VxIBjzTNvGMnc&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Parties page that highlights the entered keyword and the relevant party in the search results list.")

   1. Enter the name of the party or group in the Search parties field and press [Enter].

      Parties and groups matching the keyword are displayed in the table.
   2. Select the party or group from the table.

   The party hub opens for the selected party or group. The Overview tab is displayed by default.
5. Select the Hierarchies tab.

   Hierarchies are created and managed from the IAM party hub under the Hierarchies tab. From this page, you are prompted to create new hierarchies or update existing ones.
6. Create a new hierarchy for the selected party or group. Select the Create Hierarchy option.

   The Create Hierarchy wizard opens and you are prompted to define the hierarchy.
7. Complete the Define step by providing Hierarchy details, such as the title and description.

   ![A screenshot of the Create Hierarchy wizard that shows the Define step for providing hierarchy details.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Contract-Hierarchy_Create-Hierarchy_Define-step-1.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc4MzUsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.MvZVzAbqx6mXekq_J8WSd7yFM2IvH9VxIBjzTNvGMnc&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Create Hierarchy wizard that shows the Define step for providing hierarchy details.")

   1. Verify the Party field. This field defaults based on the selected party or group.

title: Create a Hierarchy of Related Agreements
heading_path: Create a Hierarchy of Related Agreements
text:
enus "A screenshot of the Create Hierarchy wizard that shows the Define step for providing hierarchy details.")

   1. Verify the Party field. This field defaults based on the selected party or group.

      Tip: If the Party field is incorrect, go back to the Parties page and select the correct party or group. Select the Parties option from the breadcrumb navigation at the top of the page.
   2. Enter a title for the hierarchy in the Title field. This field is required.
   3. Enter a description of the hierarchy in the Description field. Use this field to explain how the agreements are related.
   4. Select the Next option.

   The Create Hierarchy wizard advances to the Add step. You are prompted to add related agreements to the hierarchy.
8. Complete the Add step by adding related agreements to the hierarchy.

   ![A screenshot of the Create HIerarchy wizard that shows the Add step for adding related agreements to the hierarchy.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Contract-Hierarchy_Create-Hierarchy_Add-step-2.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc4MzUsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.MvZVzAbqx6mXekq_J8WSd7yFM2IvH9VxIBjzTNvGMnc&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Create HIerarchy wizard that shows the Add step for adding related agreements to the hierarchy.")The agreement list is pre-filtered to show agreements for the selected party or group.

   Tip: Remove the pre-applied filter to add documents that are associated with other parties.

   1. Search and filter the agreement list to locate the relevant agreements. Enter a keyword in the search field and apply filters as needed.
   2. Review the list of agreements and select the + Add option for the agreements that you want to add to the hierarchy. If necessary, scroll to the bottom of the page and use the vertical scroll bar to access the + Add option.

      Restriction: You can only add documents to which you have Can Edit or Can Manage access. Also, you cannot add agreements that are already associated with another hierarchy.

      The selected agreements are displayed in the Add related Agreements section for the hierarchy.
   3. Repeat this step until all the relevant documents have been added to the hierarchy.

      Tip: Remove agreements from the hierarchy if they were added by mistake. Locate the agreement that you want to remove from the Add related Agreements section. Expand the associated Actions (vertical ellipsis) menu and select the Remove from Hierarchy option.
   4. Select the Next option from the bottom of the page.

   The Create Hierarchy wizard advances to the Arrange step. You are prompted to provide a structure for the hierarchy. The structure is used to define the relationships between the related agreements.
9. Complete the Arrange step by creating relationships between the agreements.

   ![A screenshot of the Create Hierarchy wizard that shows the Arrange step for creating relationships between the agreements.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Contract-Hierarchy_Create-Hierarchy_Arrange-step-3.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc4MzUsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.MvZVzAbqx6mXekq_J8WSd7yFM2IvH9VxIBjzTNvGMnc&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Create Hierarchy wizard that shows the Arrange step for creating relationships between the agreements.")

   1. Update the Primary agreement as needed. The Primary designation defaults to the first agreement that was added to the hierarchy.

      Expand the Arrange actions (vertical ellipsis) menu and select the Make Primary option.

title: Create a Hierarchy of Related Agreements
heading_path: Create a Hierarchy of Related Agreements
text:
ement as needed. The Primary designation defaults to the first agreement that was added to the hierarchy.

      Expand the Arrange actions (vertical ellipsis) menu and select the Make Primary option.

      ![A snippet of the hierarchy details page that highlights the Arrange actions menu options.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Contract-Hierarchy_Create-Hierarchy_Arrange-actions-menu.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc4MzUsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.MvZVzAbqx6mXekq_J8WSd7yFM2IvH9VxIBjzTNvGMnc&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the hierarchy details page that highlights the Arrange actions menu options.")
   2. Rearrange the agreements to create structured relationships between them. Consider these options:

      - Hover the cursor over the dots in the File Name column for the agreement that you want to move. When the "grabber" cursor displays, drag the agreement to the appropriate location.
      - Expand the Arrange actions menu and choose to move the agreement up, down, or to a different level.Repeat this step until the hierarchy reflects the appropriate agreement relationships.
   3. Remove agreements that were added by mistake as needed. Expand the Arrange actions menu and select the Remove from Hierarchy option.
   4. Select the Finish option.

   The wizard closes and you are returned to the Hierarchies tab. The new hierarchy is displayed in the table.

   ![A screenshot of the Hierarchies tab in the party hub that highlights the new hierarchy.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Contract-Hierarchy_Hierarchies-tab_Fontara-example_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc4MzUsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.MvZVzAbqx6mXekq_J8WSd7yFM2IvH9VxIBjzTNvGMnc&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Hierarchies tab in the party hub that highlights the new hierarchy.")

You have successfully created a new hierarchy for a group of legally related agreements.

title: Use Auto-Suggestion to Create Custom Agreement Types
heading_path: Use Auto-Suggestion to Create Custom Agreement Types
text:
Learn how to create a custom agreement type from the auto-suggestion feature.

When uploading or ingesting agreements to Agreement Manager, if an agreement does not match an existing standard or custom type, it is identified as Miscellaneous. If 10 or more similar agreements are identified as Miscellaneous, a custom agreement type is suggested:![A snippet of the agreement list table that shows a suggested custom agreement type for a Miscellaneous document.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Autosuggest-custom-agreement-type_Charter-agreement-example_messaging.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2MjIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.yB2EBrA5aWPu7ZOx99UtnDJv1kaRgMUfo1IpojMuB0w&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the agreement list table that shows a suggested custom agreement type for a Miscellaneous document.")Follow the steps in this topic to create a custom agreement type from the auto-suggestion feature.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed.
4. Use the Upload function to add documents to Agreement Manager:

   ![A snippet of the Completed view that highlights the Upload option in the Add (+) dropdown.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Upload-agreements_Add-Documents_Upload-option_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2MjIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.yB2EBrA5aWPu7ZOx99UtnDJv1kaRgMUfo1IpojMuB0w&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Completed view that highlights the Upload option in the Add (+) dropdown.")

   1. Expand the Add (+) dropdown and select the Upload option.

      The Upload documents dialog box opens.
   2. Drag the files that you want to upload to the Drag and Drop
      files here box. You can also use the Select
      File to select the files from cloud storage locations or
      local folders.

      The Upload documents dialog box opens.

      Remember: The auto-suggestion feature is triggered when 10
      or more similar Miscellaneous agreements are
      uploaded or ingested to Agreement Manager. The Upload up to 1,000
      files at a time permission is required to upload
      more than five documents.
   3. Complete the Select Files step and select Next.
   4. Complete the Allow access step and select the relevant Upload option.
   5. Follow the prompts to complete the upload process.

   The dialog box closes and a confirmation message is displayed.

   Tip: Monitor progress and view uploaded files.
   - Select the Monitor Progress option located in the confirmation message. The All jobs page opens and the Uploaded Agreements table is displayed.
   - Locate the upload job in the Uploaded Agreements table.
   - Expand the Actions dropdown for the job and select Browse Files.

   The uploaded files are displayed in the agreement list table.

   ![A screenshot of the agreement list table that highlights the auto-suggestion messaging in the Agreement Type column.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Autosuggest_Completed-view_Agreement-Type-with-autosuggest-messaging_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2MjIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.yB2EBrA5aWPu7ZOx99UtnDJv1kaRgMUfo1IpojMuB0w&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the agreement list table that highlights the auto-suggestion messaging in the Agreement Type column.")
5. Review the suggested custom agreement type:

title: Use Auto-Suggestion to Create Custom Agreement Types
heading_path: Use Auto-Suggestion to Create Custom Agreement Types
text:
DJv1kaRgMUfo1IpojMuB0w&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the agreement list table that highlights the auto-suggestion messaging in the Agreement Type column.")
5. Review the suggested custom agreement type:

   ![A snippet of the agreement list table that shows a suggested custom agreement type for a Miscellaneous document.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Autosuggest_New-suggestion-available_dialog-box_charter-agreement-example.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2MjIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.yB2EBrA5aWPu7ZOx99UtnDJv1kaRgMUfo1IpojMuB0w&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the agreement list table that shows a suggested custom agreement type for a Miscellaneous document.")

   1. Select the New suggestion available message from the Agreement Type column.
   2. Review the suggested custom agreement type. The new type is suggested based on key data points within the uploaded agreements. This includes all the similar agreements in the upload job that were identified as Miscellaneous.
6. Select the Create option.

   Note: The auto-suggestion feature is also accessed from the Agreement preview page for a selected agreement. Select the New suggestion available message from the Details pane and use the Create option to create the suggested type.

   The Create new agreement type page opens. Use this page to create a custom agreement type from the auto-suggestion feature.

   ![A screenshot of the Create new agreement type page that shows the default configuration values and examples for the new type.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Autosuggest_Create-new-agreement-type_full-page.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2MjIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.yB2EBrA5aWPu7ZOx99UtnDJv1kaRgMUfo1IpojMuB0w&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Create new agreement type page that shows the default configuration values and examples for the new type.")
7. Review and update the Agreement Type, Category, and Definition values for the new type, if necessary:

   ![A snippet of the Create new agreement type page that shows the type, category, and definition details for the new type.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Autosuggest_Create-new-agreement-type_default-configuration-values.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2MjIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.yB2EBrA5aWPu7ZOx99UtnDJv1kaRgMUfo1IpojMuB0w&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Create new agreement type page that shows the type, category, and definition details for the new type.")These values were generated with AI based on the agreement details from the upload job. This includes details from "similar" agreements in the upload job that were identified as Miscellaneous.
8. Complete the Provide examples of this agreement type section to enable the AI detection for the custom agreement type:

   ![A snippet of the Create new agreement type page that shows the pre-selected examples for the agreement type.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Autosuggest_Create-new-agreement-type_Provide-examples-of-this-agreement-type.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2MjIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.yB2EBrA5aWPu7ZOx99UtnDJv1kaRgMUfo1IpojMuB0w&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Create new agreement type page that shows the pre-selected examples for the agreement type.")

   This section is pre-populated with a sampling of "similar" agreements from the upload job, including the current agreement.

title: Use Auto-Suggestion to Create Custom Agreement Types
heading_path: Use Auto-Suggestion to Create Custom Agreement Types
text:
type page that shows the pre-selected examples for the agreement type.")

   This section is pre-populated with a sampling of "similar" agreements from the upload job, including the current agreement.

   1. Select an example (tile) to view the agreement in the Agreement preview page.
   2. Select the Delete (x) icon to remove an example from the list.
   3. Use the + Add example option to add other examples of the agreement type.
9. Select the Save option from the top right of the
   page.

   You are returned to the **Completed Documents**
   page. The new agreement type is created and enabled for AI detection.

   Once created, the custom type is applied to all the relevant documents that were previously identified as Miscellaneous. Depending on the processing queue, it may take an hour or longer for the agreements to reflect the new type.

   You can filter the agreement list table using the new custom type:

   ![A snippet of the agreement list table that shows the new custom type in the Agreement Type filter.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Autosuggest_Agreement-Type-filter_Charter-Agreement-example.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2MjIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.yB2EBrA5aWPu7ZOx99UtnDJv1kaRgMUfo1IpojMuB0w&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the agreement list table that shows the new custom type in the Agreement Type filter.")

   Important: The agreements contain attributes and field values that were surfaced with AI. Complete a data review to validate these values, including the Agreement Type. See the [Validate Extracted Field Values (Complete a Data Review)](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=yqq1702945693043.html&_LANG=enus "This topic answers the question: How can I make sure that the suggested attribute (field) values are accurate? Learn how to complete a data review to verify suggested field values.") article to learn more about validating AI-surfaced values.

You have successfully created a custom agreement type from the auto-suggestion feature.

title: Update Your Agreements to the Latest AI Model
heading_path: Update Your Agreements to the Latest AI Model
text:
Learn how to reprocess the agreements in your account using the latest AI model.

When organizations are first onboarded to Agreement Manager, their historical documents are ingested and processed based on the current AI model. As new or updated models become available, the previously ingested and uploaded documents are not automatically reprocessed for the new insights.

When users interact with insights that were released since their initial onboarding, they are prompted to update their AI model:![A snippet of the Completed Documents view that highlights the banner prompt to Update Now to the latest AI version.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reprocessing_banner-messaging_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc5MDIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.xuEQ225F6_C5PRhHgfyYpL4p6d7D2nzHGrY8D61O9nk&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Completed Documents view that highlights the banner prompt to Update Now to the latest AI version.")

Note: The banner message is triggered when users apply relevant filters from the agreement list table located on the Completed Documents page. Using these filters from other locations, such as in the party hub or under the Insights menu, does *not* trigger the banner message.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed. A list of documents to which the logged-in user has access is displayed in the table.

   Note: The reprocessing banner message and prompt are triggered when the certain criteria are met:

   - The user applies a filter from the Completed Documents page that is not
     available with their current AI model. For example, the banner is
     triggered if you apply the Line of Business,
     Party Role, or Status
     filters.
   - The account contains agreements with outdated extractions.

   Every user has access to initiate the reprocessing job for all the agreements in their account -
   regardless of the account size.
4. Apply filters to locate the relevant agreements. When you apply a filter that is not available with your current AI model, you are prompted to update to a new version.

   Filters that may trigger the banner include:
   - Line of Business (located in the All Filters list)
   - Party Role (located under the Parties filter)
   - Status  (displays by default but is also located in the All Filters list)

   Expand the filter that you want to apply, choose a filter value, and select
   Apply.

   The agreement list table updates and agreements that match the applied filters are displayed. A banner message prompts you to: *Update to the latest AI version to find and show all documents matching these filters*.
5. Select the Update Now option:

   ![A snippet of the Completed Documents view that highlights the Update Now option in the banner message.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reprocessing_Completed-page_Update-Now_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc5MDIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.xuEQ225F6_C5PRhHgfyYpL4p6d7D2nzHGrY8D61O9nk&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Completed Documents view that highlights the Update Now option in the banner message.")

   The Update AI version dialog box opens.
6. Expand and review the What to expect section:

title: Update Your Agreements to the Latest AI Model
heading_path: Update Your Agreements to the Latest AI Model
text:
s "A snippet of the Completed Documents view that highlights the Update Now option in the banner message.")

   The Update AI version dialog box opens.
6. Expand and review the What to expect section:

   ![A screenshot of the Update AI version dialog box that highlights the What do expect section.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reprocessing_Update-AI-version_dialog-box_what-to-expect_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc5MDIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.xuEQ225F6_C5PRhHgfyYpL4p6d7D2nzHGrY8D61O9nk&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Update AI version dialog box that highlights the What do expect section.")

   Once the reprocessing job is initiated, it cannot be canceled. These updates happen in the background and can take several minutes (small jobs) to days (1,000 files or more). See the [Agreement Manager Reprocessing Experience](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=con44590246-0f0e-43b4-9692-caadb57335ac.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about the Agreement Manager reprocessing experience, including its purpose, trigger points, and job size considerations.") topic to learn more about job size considerations.

   Note: For enterprise accounts, agreements are reprocessed in batches of 25,000 files at a time.
7. Select the Start Update option.

   The dialog box closes and the reprocessing job is initiated. A message confirms that your agreements are being processed with the latest AI model.

   Note: The reprocessing job may surface and overwrite your agreement data. However, any values that were manually entered or marked as "accepted" are not overwritten or altered. The processing job skips any documents that are up to date.
8. Track your job progress from the job
   monitoring hub. Select the View
   Progress option from the message
   box:

   ![A screenshot of the confirmation message that highlights the View Progress option.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reprocessing_Completed-page_Confirmation-message_View-Progress.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc5MDIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.xuEQ225F6_C5PRhHgfyYpL4p6d7D2nzHGrY8D61O9nk&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the confirmation message that highlights the View Progress option.")

   The job details page opens. The
   Processing status and
   Job details are displayed for
   the processing job.

title: Update Your Agreements to the Latest AI Model
heading_path: Update Your Agreements to the Latest AI Model
text:
screenshot of the confirmation message that highlights the View Progress option.")

   The job details page opens. The
   Processing status and
   Job details are displayed for
   the processing job.

   Note: The Uploads and Processing Status function gives you access to job details for agreement uploads and processing jobs. This job monitoring hub provides a centralized location to view and track the status of these jobs. Access this function from the Settings (gear) menu on the Completed Documents page:![A screenshot of the Completed Documents view that shows the Uploads and Processing Status option highlighted in the Settings menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-documents-view_Actions_Monitor-All-Jobs-option.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc5MDIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.xuEQ225F6_C5PRhHgfyYpL4p6d7D2nzHGrY8D61O9nk&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Completed Documents view that shows the Uploads and Processing Status option highlighted in the Settings menu.")See the [Job Monitoring for Agreement Uploads and Processing](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=ahg1742251814403.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the job monitoring hub that is used to track the progress for agreement uploads and processing jobs.") topic to learn more about the job monitoring hub.
9. Review the job's status and details as needed:
   - Perform a keyword search to locate processing status details for a specific file. Enter search criteria in the Search for a file name field and select a file from the type-ahead dropdown or press [Enter].
   - Apply filters to sort the list of files by Status and Error Message.
   - Select the View File option to view the corresponding file from the Agreement preview page.
10. Select Close (X) from the top-right corner to return to the previous page.

    You are returned to the Completed Documents page.

You have successfully reprocessed your out-of-date agreements with the latest AI
model.

title: Delete Custom Dashboards
text:
Learn how to delete a custom dashboard.

Custom dashboards give you access to the data that's tailored for your priorities. If you are no longer using a custom dashboard, it can be deleted.

1. Log in to your **Docusign** account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Insights from the global header.

   The Insights hub opens and the Overview is displayed.

   Your custom dashboards are displayed in the navigation pane on the left, under the Custom Dashboards grouping.
3. Select the dashboard that you want to delete from the Custom Dashboards grouping in the navigation pane.

   The custom dashboard view opens for the selected dashboard.
4. Expand the More actions (three dots) menu from the top-right corner of the page and select Delete Dashboard:

   ![A snippet of a custom dashboard page that highlights the Delete Dashboard option in the Dashboard Actions menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Delete-custom-dashboard_Delete-Dashboard_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwOTcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.ahio8Vrq-Kye0S7EKhkkBpPJvSvXgSyO8avzvsLZW0k&_LANG=enus "A snippet of a custom dashboard page that highlights the Delete Dashboard option in the Dashboard Actions menu.")The Delete dashboard dialog box opens.
5. Review the message and select the Delete option.

   CAUTION: When you delete a dashboard, you are also deleting the user-created reports that were added from the dashboard.

   The dialog box closes. The custom dashboard is no longer displayed in the navigation pane under the Custom Dashboards grouping.

You have successfully deleted a custom dashboard. The dashboard no longer displays in the navigation pane on the left-side of the Insights page.

title: Create Custom Dashboards from Templates
text:
Learn how to build a custom dashboard by using an existing Agreement or Renewal dashboard as a template.

Dashboards are collections of standard
and custom reports that show a larger picture of a
particular focus area. When creating a custom
dashboard, you can add up to 20 reports to the
dashboard. This includes both standard and custom
reports. Once created, the dashboard is displayed
in the navigation pane under the Custom
Dashboards group.

This topic
reviews the steps to build a custom dashboard
by using an existing dashboard as a template.

Restriction: Currently, only the Agreements and Renewals dashboards can be used as templates for creating custom dashboards.

1. Log in to your **Docusign** account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Insights from the global header.

   The Insights hub opens and the Overview is displayed.
3. Expand the Create dropdown and select the Create Dashboard option:

   ![A snippet of the reporting hub that highlights the Create dropdown with options to create reports and dashboards.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Custom-report-dashboard_Create-expanded.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwODgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.7y8l9u7t62gpoEQZTF9tjYuvlxiHmbAcX0nggUKKKUM&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the reporting hub that highlights the Create dropdown with options to create reports and dashboards.")The Untitled dashboard page opens.![A screenshot of the Untitled dashboard page that shows the options to create a dashboard from scratch or from a template.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Create-custom-dashboard_from-scratch_Untitled-page.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwODgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.7y8l9u7t62gpoEQZTF9tjYuvlxiHmbAcX0nggUKKKUM&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Untitled dashboard page that shows the options to create a dashboard from scratch or from a template.")
4. Enter a name for the dashboard and add a description:

   ![A snippet of the Untitled dashboard page that shows the Edit Dashboard Name.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Create-custom-dashboard_Untitled_Enter-name.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwODgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.7y8l9u7t62gpoEQZTF9tjYuvlxiHmbAcX0nggUKKKUM&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Untitled dashboard page that shows the Edit Dashboard Name.")

   1. Hover your cursor over the page title
      ("Untitled") and select the Edit
      Dashboard Name (pencil) icon or select
      (click) the page title.

      The title field opens for edits.
   2. Enter a name for the dashboard in the text-entry field.

      The text-entry field closes for edits. The entered name is displayed at the top of the page.
   3. Select the + Add description option. Enter a description in the text-entry field and press [Enter].

      The text-entry field closes. The entered description is displayed below the dashboard title.

      Tip: Update the dashboard title and description at any time:
      - Hover the cursor over the information that you want to update, either the title or the description.
      - Select the Edit (pencil) icon or select (click) the title or description
        to access the text-entry field.
      - Update the title or description by typing directly into the text-entry field and press [Enter].

title: Create Custom Dashboards from Templates
text:
(pencil) icon or select (click) the title or description
        to access the text-entry field.
      - Update the title or description by typing directly into the text-entry field and press [Enter].

      After entering a title and description, you're ready to build your custom dashboard. There are two options for creating a custom dashboard:
      - Use the Start from scratch option to get started by adding reports to this dashboard.
      - Use the Start from a template option to make a copy of an existing dashboard to get started.

        Restriction: Currently, only the Agreements and Renewals dashboards can be used as templates for creating custom dashboards.For purposes of this topic, use the Start from a template option to build a custom dashboard from an existing dashboard.
5. Choose a dashboard to use as a template:

   ![A screenshot of the Start from a template section that highlights the available dashboard templates.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Create-custom-dashboard_Start-from-a-template_Select-dropdown.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwODgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.7y8l9u7t62gpoEQZTF9tjYuvlxiHmbAcX0nggUKKKUM&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Start from a template section that highlights the available dashboard templates.")Use the Start from a template box to select the dashboard that you want to use as a template. Select a dashboard from the dropdown.

   Your custom dashboard is populated with the reports associated with the selected dashboard. For example, either the Agreements or Renewals dashboard reports.

   ![A snippet of a custom dashboard that shows the reports that were added from the selected dashboard template.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Create-custom-dashboard_populated-from-template.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwODgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.7y8l9u7t62gpoEQZTF9tjYuvlxiHmbAcX0nggUKKKUM&_LANG=enus "A snippet of a custom dashboard that shows the reports that were added from the selected dashboard template.")Using an existing dashboard as a template is a quick way to get started with building your custom dashboard. After initially populating the dashboard, update the reports so the dashboard reflects the insights that you want to see, including:
   - Add existing custom and standard reports to the dashboard
   - Add a new custom report to the dashboard
   - Reorganize the report charts and graphs on the dashboard

     Important: If the dashboard becomes
     cluttered, you can delete any of the reports that
     are no longer relevant. Expand the
     Report Options menu for the
     report that you want to delete and select the
     Delete option:![A snippet of a dashboard page that highlights the Delete option in the Report Options menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Create-custom-dashboard_Delete-report-from-dashboard.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwODgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.7y8l9u7t62gpoEQZTF9tjYuvlxiHmbAcX0nggUKKKUM&_LANG=enus "A snippet of a dashboard page that highlights the Delete option in the Report Options menu.")**Performing this action will delete the report, not just remove it from the dashboard.** Once deleted, it is no longer available to
     view or add to a dashboard.

**Add an existing report to the dashboard**

6. Add an existing custom or standard report to the dashboard:

title: Create Custom Dashboards from Templates
text:
he dashboard.** Once deleted, it is no longer available to
     view or add to a dashboard.

**Add an existing report to the dashboard**

6. Add an existing custom or standard report to the dashboard:

   ![A snippet of a custom dashboard page that highlights options to add additional reports.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Create-custom-dashboard_template_Add-reports.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwODgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.7y8l9u7t62gpoEQZTF9tjYuvlxiHmbAcX0nggUKKKUM&_LANG=enus "A snippet of a custom dashboard page that highlights options to add additional reports.")

   1. Hover the cursor over either side of the dashboard and select the + (add) option or expand the Add dropdown from the bottom of the dashboard.
   2. Select the Existing Report option from the list.

      The Add Existing Report dialog box opens.

      ![A screenshot of the Add Existing Report dialog box that shows the reports that can be added to the dashboard.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Create-custom-dashboard_from-scratch_Add-Existing-Report-dialog-bpx.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwODgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.7y8l9u7t62gpoEQZTF9tjYuvlxiHmbAcX0nggUKKKUM&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Add Existing Report dialog box that shows the reports that can be added to the dashboard.")
7. Locate the report that you want to add to the dashboard and select the associated Add option.

   Tip: Enter a keyword in the Search for a report field or scroll to locate the report that you want to add.

   The dialog box closes and the selected report is displayed on the dashboard.

**Add a new custom report to the dashboard**

8. Expand the Add dropdown and select the + New Report option.

   The Untitled report page opens. Use this page to create a custom report, including choosing the data, metric, and visualization that matter to you.

   Note: See the [Create Custom Reports from Scratch](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tskfa65c658-fd7d-4ce8-b26b-08a9ec950aad.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to create a custom report from scratch, including choosing the relevant data, metrics, and visualization options.") topic to learn more about creating a custom report.
9. Complete the sections on the Untitled report page and save the report:
   1. Complete the Data source section:

      - Expand the Data source dropdown and choose the data that you want to use to build the report:
      - Use the + Add filter option to apply filters to the agreement data.The agreement count shows the number of agreements that match the applied filters in the selected data source.
   2. Complete the Build your report section:

      - Expand the Measure dropdown and choose an attribute to start building a report.

        Important: Currently, custom reports only support numeric values for metrics or key measures.
      - Select the Group by option to choose a field by which to group or break down your metric. Select the attribute or field by which you want to group the selected metric.The pane on the right is updated to reflect the selected metrics. For example, a bar chart might show the agreements that match the applied filters grouped by the selected Group by field value.
   3. Complete the Visualization section.

      Expand the Visualization dropdown and choose how the report is displayed.
      Choices include:
      - Single metric (not
        available when the Group by
        option is used)
      - Bar chart
      - Pie chart

title: Create Custom Dashboards from Templates
text:
ation dropdown and choose how the report is displayed.
      Choices include:
      - Single metric (not
        available when the Group by
        option is used)
      - Bar chart
      - Pie chart

      The pane on the right updates to reflect the selected chart.
10. After refining the report so that it reflects
    the agreement data that you want to capture, save
    the report:
    1. Select the Save option
       from the top-right corner of the page.

       The Save report dialog
       box opens.
    2. Enter a name for the report in the Report name field. This step is required.
    3. Enter a description about the report's purpose in the Description field.
    4. Select the Save option.

       The dialog box closes and the new report is
       displayed on your custom dashboard.

       Tip: Your custom reports are available from
       the Custom tab under the
       Reports view. See the [View and Update Your Custom Reports](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=yuf1727801071281.html&_LANG=enus "This topic answers the question: How can I review and update my custom reports?") topic to learn how
       to view your custom reports.

**Organize the dashboard reports**

11. Re-organize the dashboard so it reflects the agreement insights that you want to see:

    ![A snippet of a custom dashboard page that highlights the "grab" cursor that is used to reorganize dashboard reports.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Create-custom-dashboard_from-scratch_reorganize-dashboard_Grab-cursor.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwODgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.7y8l9u7t62gpoEQZTF9tjYuvlxiHmbAcX0nggUKKKUM&_LANG=enus "A snippet of a custom dashboard page that highlights the \"grab\" cursor that is used to reorganize dashboard reports.")

    1. Hover the cursor over the report chart or graph that you want to relocate.

       A grid icon displays to the left of the report name.
    2. Place the cursor over the grid icon. The cursor turns into the "grab" cursor.
    3. Drag the report to the desired location on
       the dashboard.
    4. Continue moving reports around on the
       dashboard until it reflects the agreement insights
       that you want to see.

**Add the dashboard to Your Favorites list**

12. Add the dashboard to Your Favorites list. Select the Favorite (star) icon located in the top-right corner:

    ![A snippet of a detailed report view that highlights the Favorite (star) icon.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreements-dashboard_Report-details_Favorite-icon-selected_generic.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwODgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.7y8l9u7t62gpoEQZTF9tjYuvlxiHmbAcX0nggUKKKUM&_LANG=enus "A snippet of a detailed report view that highlights the Favorite (star) icon.")Your starred dashboards and reports are displayed in the Your Favorites section on the Overview dashboard.

You have successfully built a custom dashboard by using an existing dashboard as a template.

title: Add Fields to Multiple Agreement Types
heading_path: Add Fields to Multiple Agreement Types
text:
Learn how to add fields to multiple agreement types from the Add a field dialog box. You can add fields to additional agreement types while you are updating a selected agreement type.

Administrators and contract managers can add fields to a selected agreement type. This includes both custom and standard fields. When completing the Add a field dialog box, the Agreement types field supports multiple selections.

The Add a field function is accessed from these locations:

- Agreement Type Manager
- Fields Manager
- Agreement Preview page

Note:

This task is completed by users with Organize and manage all agreements access to Agreement Manager and the account's agreements.

This topic reviews the steps to add fields to multiple agreement types from the *Agreement Types Manager*. Access this feature from the Agreement Manager homepage, under the Settings (gear) menu.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Completed Documents view opens. A list of documents to which the logged-in user has access is displayed in the table.
4. Expand the Settings (gear) dropdown from the top right and select the Agreement Types option:

   ![A screenshot of the Agreement Manager homepage that highlights the Agreement Types option in the Settings (gear) menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-types-manager_Settings-menu_Agreement-Types_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc1NTYsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.ZcSvHc8ssGUrnmaHwyz3v7r0o_vh6EZdkAfDBX5rtZo&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Agreement Manager homepage that highlights the Agreement Types option in the Settings (gear) menu.")

   The Manage Agreement Types page opens. The account's standard and custom agreement types are displayed in the Agreement Types table.
5. Locate the agreement type that you want to update. You can add fields to custom and standard agreement types.

   ![A snippet of the Manage Agreement Types page that shows the search and filter options for locating agreement types.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Manage-Agreement-Types_Agreement-Types_search-filters.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc1NTYsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.ZcSvHc8ssGUrnmaHwyz3v7r0o_vh6EZdkAfDBX5rtZo&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Manage Agreement Types page that shows the search and filter options for locating agreement types.")

   - Search for the relevant agreement type. Enter a keyword in the Search types... field. As you enter the search term, the table updates and agreement types that matched the entered criteria are displayed.
   - Filter the table for the relevant agreement type. Filter options are: Created By, Category, and AI Status. Expand the filter, choose the relevant value, and select the Apply option.The table updates and the agreement types that matched the applied filters are displayed.
6. Select (click) the relevant agreement type from the table.

   The Agreement type details view opens for the selected agreement type. The Fields table displays the fields that are associated with the agreement type.
7. Select the + Add Fields option.

   The Add a field dialog box opens.

title: Add Fields to Multiple Agreement Types
heading_path: Add Fields to Multiple Agreement Types
text:
s view opens for the selected agreement type. The Fields table displays the fields that are associated with the agreement type.
7. Select the + Add Fields option.

   The Add a field dialog box opens.

   ![A screenshot of the Add a field dialog box that is used to add a field to existing agreement types.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-type-management_create-type_add-a-field-dialog-box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc1NTYsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.ZcSvHc8ssGUrnmaHwyz3v7r0o_vh6EZdkAfDBX5rtZo&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Add a field dialog box that is used to add a field to existing agreement types.")The selected agreement type is displayed in the Agreement types field by default.
8. Complete the required fields and select the Save option.
   1. Enter the name of the field in the
      Field name field.

      As you enter a name, a type-ahead dropdown
      list opens, and fields that match the entered name
      are displayed.
   2. Select the name from the type-ahead dropdown
      list or select the + Create [Field
      name] option to add a custom field to
      the agreement type.

      For existing fields, the Field
      type and
      Category fields default
      based on the selected field.
   3. For custom fields, complete the
      Field type and
      Category fields:

      - Expand the Field type dropdown and select the type of field that you are adding. Choices are: Text, Number, Date, and Dropdown.

        Important: If you
        select the Dropdown field type, you must provide a list of options for
        the dropdown. Enter a list of options for the
        dropdown in the Options
        text-entry box:![A snippet of the Add a field dialog box that shows the Options field for adding dropdown options.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Add-custom-field_Options-for-dropdown-type.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc1NTYsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.ZcSvHc8ssGUrnmaHwyz3v7r0o_vh6EZdkAfDBX5rtZo&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Add a field dialog box that shows the Options field for adding dropdown options.")**Important:** Separate each option with
        a semicolon.
      - Expand the Category dropdown and select a classification for the new custom field:

        Choices are: General, Termination, Renewal, Payment, Legal and Compliance, Human Resources, and Other.
   4. Add the field to multiple agreement types.

      ![A screenshot of the Agreement types dropdown that shows two selected agreement types to which the field will be added.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Manage-agreement-types-fields_Add-a-field_Agreement-types-dropdown.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc1NTYsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.ZcSvHc8ssGUrnmaHwyz3v7r0o_vh6EZdkAfDBX5rtZo&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Agreement types dropdown that shows two selected agreement types to which the field will be added.")

      - Click in the Agreement types field to expand the dropdown.
      - Enter search criteria or scroll to locate and select the relevant agreement types. You can select more than one agreement type.
      - Select the Apply option.The dropdown closes and the selected types are displayed in the Agreement types field.
   5. Select the Save option.

      The dialog box closes and you are returned to the
      Agreement type details view.
      The new field
      is displayed in the table.
9. Return to the Agreement Types view. Select the Agreement Types  option from the breadcrumb navigation in the top left.

title: Add Fields to Multiple Agreement Types
heading_path: Add Fields to Multiple Agreement Types
text:
eement type details view.
      The new field
      is displayed in the table.
9. Return to the Agreement Types view. Select the Agreement Types  option from the breadcrumb navigation in the top left.

   You are returned to the Manage Agreement Types page. The Agreement Types view is displayed.
10. Return to the Agreement Manager homepage. Select the Close (x)
    icon from the top right.

    The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed.

You have successfully added a field to multiple agreement types at the same time, including both custom and standard types.

title: Activate Your Custom Fields
text: Learn how to activate the AI detection for a custom field and surface it in selected historical agreements.

title: Activate Your Custom Fields
text:
This topic reviews the final part of the *Test for accuracy* step, activating the
custom field. You also have the option to find the custom field in past agreements.
These steps are completed by users with Organize and manage all
agreements access to Agreement Manager agreements and functionality. For
example, an administrator or contract manager.

Complete these topics before attempting
this task:

- [Create Custom Fields from the Agreement Preview](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tsk0830ea60-9b29-4efa-b73f-9eb15b2d6561.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to create custom fields from the Agreement preview page for a selected agreement.")
- [Add Examples of Your Custom Fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tskcb56face-b8de-4cf9-85ce-6eac3669dd82.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to complete the second step of the AI detection set-up process, Select examples.")
- [Test the Extraction Accuracy for Your Custom Fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=wjr1740452628309.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to test the AI detection accuracy for your custom fields and refining the results to improve accuracy.")

Important: Use the Finish Later option to save your progress and finish the AI detection set-up later. Using this option ends the Track this field with AI experience. See the [Enable AI Detection for Your Custom Fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tsk02bd31a2-b125-4d53-b632-20d9972163a8.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to enable the AI detection for your custom fields. Once activated, the field is surfaced in your new agreements automatically.") topic for steps to finish the process.

In the previous step, you tested the accuracy of the AI detection against a group of selected documents. After verifying the identified fields, you selected the Done option. The test results were displayed.

![A screenshot that shows the results of the accuracy test, including options for activating the field.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Fields-Manager_Agreement-preview_Activate-Find-past-docs.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc3MTgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.dT_kIBMR_iAewZBtqqRFCSEG01NZX_uqEX8oRPJr2T0&_LANG=enus "A screenshot that shows the results of the accuracy test, including options for activating the field.")

After achieving the target accuracy score, you are prompted to activate the field. Once activated, the field is identified in new agreements and any future agreement types. For example, if you add the field to additional agreement types, it is surfaced in those agreements too.

You are ready to activate the custom field.

1. Surface the new field in your historical agreements. Select the Find in past agreements option:

   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Fields-Manager_Agreement-preview_Activate-Find-past-docs_selected.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc3MTgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.dT_kIBMR_iAewZBtqqRFCSEG01NZX_uqEX8oRPJr2T0&_LANG=enus)

   As part of the activation process, you can select past agreements to check for the new field.
2. Select the Activate option.

   The AI detection is published and the field is active for new agreements.

   The agreement list table opens. Use this page to search for the documents that you want to process for the new field.
3. Locate the documents that you want to process for the new field:

title: Activate Your Custom Fields
text:
greements.

   The agreement list table opens. Use this page to search for the documents that you want to process for the new field.
3. Locate the documents that you want to process for the new field:

   ![A snippet of the agreement list table that shows the search field and filter options to locate the relevant documents.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Fields-Manager_Agreement-preview_Activate_Search-for-past-docs.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc3MTgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.dT_kIBMR_iAewZBtqqRFCSEG01NZX_uqEX8oRPJr2T0&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the agreement list table that shows the search field and filter options to locate the relevant documents.")

   1. Search for the relevant agreements. Enter a keyword or search string in the search field. The dropdown opens and search suggestions are displayed. Select a search suggestion or press the Enter key.
   2. Filter the agreement list table to find the relevant agreements. For example, filter by Sets, Status, Parties, Agreement Type, or AI Suggestions.

      Tip: Use the Filters option to apply additional filters to the results table.

      Expand the filter, choose the relevant filter value, and select the Apply option.

   The results table updates and the documents that match the search criteria and applied filters are displayed.
4. Choose the documents that you want to process for the new field.
5. Select the Start Processing option in the bottom right.

   Your processing job is submitted and you are returned to the Agreement preview page. The new field is displayed in the Details pane:

   A message confirms that the selected documents are being refreshed for custom data.

   ![A screenshot of the message box, confirming that the selected documents are being refreshed for custom data.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Fields-Manager_Agreement-preview_Field-actived_Message-box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc3MTgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.dT_kIBMR_iAewZBtqqRFCSEG01NZX_uqEX8oRPJr2T0&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the message box, confirming that the selected documents are being refreshed for custom data.")

   Tip: Select the View Progress option to view the processing status for the job. The job monitoring hub opens, and the Processing status and Job details are displayed. Review the processing results, including any error messages.Select the Close (x) icon in the top right to return to the Agreement Manager homepage.
6. Return to the Agreement Manager homepage. Select the Close (x) icon in the top left.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed.

You have successfully activated the AI detection for a custom field and processed historical agreements for the new data point.

title: Display the Status Column in the Agreement List Table
heading_path: Display the Status Column in the Agreement List Table
text:
Learn how to customize the agreement list table to display agreement status information.

The Status column and filter are associated with the agreement list table and can be found on the:

- Completed page
- Agreements page in the party hub
- Detailed report view for a selected dashboard or report

The column and filter are displayed in the agreement list table by default. However, if your table contains column customizations, you must manually add the Status column to the table.

Use the Customize columns feature to display the Status column in the agreement list table.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed.

   ![A snippet of the Completed Documents page that highlights the Customize columns (toggles) icon on the right-side of the page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-documents-table_Customize-columns-gear-icon.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY1NjgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.GXQPMViGA-3HV22Smz3GzVdoqBI8PzUH_NPxJKuTXWo&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Completed Documents page that highlights the Customize columns (toggles) icon on the right-side of the page.")
4. Select the Show or Hide Fields (toggle) icon located to the right of the column headings.

   The Show or Hide Fields dialog box opens. A number of fields are turned on (blue) by default. These fields represent the columns in the agreement list table. Columns display in the order that the fields are represented in the dialog box.

   Important: The Show or Hide Fields function only applies to the data that is displayed in the agreement list table. When downloading or exporting the report, all fields are included in the output file. This includes both custom and standard fields.
5. Perform a query to locate and turn on the Status column:

   ![A screenshot of the Show or Hide Fields dialog box that shows the Status toggle.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-statuses_Customize-columns_show-Status.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY1NjgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.GXQPMViGA-3HV22Smz3GzVdoqBI8PzUH_NPxJKuTXWo&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Show or Hide Fields dialog box that shows the Status toggle.")

   1. Enter Status in the Find columns field.
   2. Select the Status toggle to turn it on (blue position).
   3. Select Save.

   The dialog box closes. The Status column is displayed in the agreement list table.
6. Re-order the columns so you can view the Status column in the agreement list table without scrolling:

   ![A snippet of the Show or Hide Fields dialog box that highlights the re-ordered Status column.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-statuses_Customize-columns_move-Status_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY1NjgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.GXQPMViGA-3HV22Smz3GzVdoqBI8PzUH_NPxJKuTXWo&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Show or Hide Fields dialog box that highlights the re-ordered Status column.")

   1. Locate the Status column and place your cursor over the dotted grid to the right of the name.

      The "grabbing" cursor displays when you hover over an element that can be dragged or moved.
   2. Drag the Status column to the appropriate location in the list.
   3. Select Save.

   The dialog box closes and the Status column is displayed in the new location within the table.

   2. Drag the Status column to the appropriate location in the list.
   3. Select Save.

   The dialog box closes and the Status column is displayed in the new location within the table.

   ![A snippet of the Completed Documents page that highlights the Status column in the agreement list table.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-statuses_Status-column-displayed-table_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY1NjgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.GXQPMViGA-3HV22Smz3GzVdoqBI8PzUH_NPxJKuTXWo&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Completed Documents page that highlights the Status column in the agreement list table.")

title: Import Your Edited Metadata Spreadsheet
text: Learn how to import your edited metadata spreadsheet using the Add (+) menu located on the Agreement Manager homepage.

title: Import Your Edited Metadata Spreadsheet
text:
Import the updated spreadsheet file and resolve any upload errors.

Important: When you import the updated agreement data, the current data is overwritten. This action cannot be undone once saved. The user-provided data is final. The cells must contain a valid value to overwrite the current value. If the cell is empty in the spreadsheet, the current field value is retained.

This task is completed after you update the pre-formatted spreadsheet file with the relevant metadata. If you haven't updated the spreadsheet, see the [Edit the Metadata Spreadsheet](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tsk8710c3f2-c881-4219-96b0-2b287e8b4e03.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to edit the metadata spreadsheet file to bulk update the agreement data for the selected documents.") article to learn more about this task.

Note: The *Bulk Metadata Editing* process is completed by users with Organize and manage all agreements access to Agreement Manager and bulk upload permissions.

Import the spreadsheet file to update the metadata for the selected agreements. Once successfully imported, the agreement data is synced and the updated values are reflected across the Intelligent Agreement Management (IAM) platform.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
