3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed.
4. Expand the Add (+) dropdown and select the Import Data option:

   ![A snippet of the Completed Documents page that highlights the Import Data option in the Add (+) dropdown menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Bulk-metadata-editing_Add-menu_Import-Data_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY4MTUsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.I_8q6999MpteYMlP4HBkYTsVAEyPc_o3XoyeTM5Erq0&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Completed Documents page that highlights the Import Data option in the Add (+) dropdown menu.")

   The Import data from a spreadsheet dialog box opens. You are prompted to drop or select your edited file.
5. Upload the pre-formatted spreadsheet file that you updated with metadata values:

   ![A screenshot of the Import data from a spreadsheet dialog box that highlights the selected file and the Import Data option.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Bulk-metadata-editing_Import-data-from-spreadsheet_Select-file_Import-Dtata.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY4MTUsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.I_8q6999MpteYMlP4HBkYTsVAEyPc_o3XoyeTM5Erq0&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Import data from a spreadsheet dialog box that highlights the selected file and the Import Data option.")

   - Locate the metadata spreadsheet file and drop it in the dialog box.
   - Use the Select File option to search for and select the file.

   Important: Upload the same file that you downloaded previously. If the file structure does not match the original spreadsheet template, the upload is rejected.

   The dialog box updates and the selected file is displayed in a green box. This indicates that the file was accepted by the import process. If the file failed this initial validation, a red box is displayed and the messaging indicates that the file was rejected.

   Note: Keep in mind that the file can be accepted by the import process but still fail validation due to formatting errors.
6. Select the Import Data option.

   The dialog box closes and you are returned to the Agreement Manager homepage. A confirmation message indicates that the data was imported.

title: Import Your Edited Metadata Spreadsheet
text:
e to formatting errors.
6. Select the Import Data option.

   The dialog box closes and you are returned to the Agreement Manager homepage. A confirmation message indicates that the data was imported.

   Tip: Use the View Details option to view the Job status for the upload job, including files that failed to update and the associated error messages. You can access this page from the email notification too.

   When the upload is completed, a system-generated email notification is sent. The email contains a brief summary of the import results, including the number of files that failed to update.

You have successfully imported the updated metadata spreadsheet. Continue to the next step of the process, [Review Job Results from the Notification Email](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tske7ac3afe-c04e-4acc-95b1-9f83b1b66ed5.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to view the results of an upload job from the email notification. A system-generated email is sent when the upload process is completed.").

title: Set Document-Level Notifications
text:
Learn how to set and manage notifications to track upcoming milestones for an individual document.

Use the Notifications function to stay informed about major milestones for an individual agreement. There are two entry points for this function: the agreement list table and the Agreement preview page.

Tip: Notification settings give you control over the email notifications that you receive. By default, all the notification types related to the completed agreements in your account are selected, or enabled. Deselect a checkbox if you no longer want to receive email notifications for the corresponding notification type.

See the [Customize Notification Settings for Your Completed Agreements](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tsk155b1ed7-7b45-43cd-be92-2a5279630c0f.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to customize your email notification settings from the Notification Preferences page.") article to learn more about updating your notification settings. Be sure to periodically check the Notification Preferences page as new notification types will be added in the future.

This topic reviews the steps to set a notification from the agreement list table. Begin by locating the relevant agreements.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed.
4. Perform a search and apply filters to locate the relevant agreements:
   1. Enter a keyword or string in the search field and select a suggestion from the dropdown or press the Enter key.

      The agreements containing the keyword or search string that you entered are displayed in the table.
   2. Use the filters to refine the list of search results. For example, use the Parties filter to view a list of agreements for a specific party.

      1. Expand the Filters option and select the filter that you want to apply.
      2. Choose a filter value.
      3. Select the Apply option.

      The agreements that match the applied filters are displayed in the table.
5. Hover your cursor over the agreement that you want to track and select the Set a notification (bell) icon:

   ![A screenshot of the Completed view that highlights the Set a notification option in the agreement list table.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-list-table_Set-a-notification_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY1MzQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.LcTzqQNvO9EsWVf86FlQlW88QzMIgXYfPrFQPmsJRIY&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Completed view that highlights the Set a notification option in the agreement list table.")

   Tip: If a notification already exists, the Manage notifications (bell) icon is displayed. Select the bell icon to update existing notifications for the agreement.

   The Notifications function opens. The Renewal Notice Date and Expiration Date notifications, including the reminder intervals, are enabled by default. You are designated as the recipient.

   ![A screenshot of the Notifications window that shows options for tracking agreement milestones.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Document-level-notifications_Notifications-window.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY1MzQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.LcTzqQNvO9EsWVf86FlQlW88QzMIgXYfPrFQPmsJRIY&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Notifications window that shows options for tracking agreement milestones.")

title: Set Document-Level Notifications
text:
DE3OTY1MzQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.LcTzqQNvO9EsWVf86FlQlW88QzMIgXYfPrFQPmsJRIY&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Notifications window that shows options for tracking agreement milestones.")

   Tip: Use the + Add option to configure other notification types, such as Effective Date and Execution Date notifications.
6. Update the reminder interval and recipients for the notification. Select the Edit link.

   The Edit notification dialog box opens.![A screenshot of the Edit notification dialog box that shows configuration options for the selected notification.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Document-level-notifications_Edit-notification-dialog-box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY1MzQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.LcTzqQNvO9EsWVf86FlQlW88QzMIgXYfPrFQPmsJRIY&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Edit notification dialog box that shows configuration options for the selected notification.")
7. Update notification settings and select the Save
   option.
   1. Update the reminder interval. Expand the Remind me prior to date dropdown and choose when you want to
      receive notifications for the selected milestone. Click an interval to deselect it:

      ![A snippet of the Edit notification dialog box that shows the selected reminder intervals, 30 days and 60 days.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Set-Notification_Edit-notification_Reminder-interval-dropdown.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY1MzQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.LcTzqQNvO9EsWVf86FlQlW88QzMIgXYfPrFQPmsJRIY&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Edit notification dialog box that shows the selected reminder intervals, 30 days and 60 days.")
   2. Specify the users that should also receive these
      notifications. Type a name or email address in the Add a
      user field and select a name from the dropdown
      list.

      Note: The Self option is selected by default and cannot be removed.
   3. Select the Save option.

   The dialog box closes and you are returned to the
   Completed view. The notification is saved and the
   Manage notifications (bell) icon is displayed when you hover
   over the document.
8. Use the Manage notifications (bell) icon to update your document-level notifications, including:

   - Add new notifications
   - Update existing notification settings
   - Manage the distribution list
   - Delete the notification

You have successfully set a document-level notification, including customizing the reminder interval and recipient list.

title: Opt-In for Party Clean Up
text:
Learn how to opt in to reprocess the organization party names in your account for party normalization and deduplication.

In addition to duplicate party management, existing customers can opt in to clean up their party data.

Restriction: The party clean-up option is available only for English-language documents.

Access the opt-in message from the Insights bar on the Completed Documents page.

Warning: When you opt in for party clean-up, the process is permanently enabled and the processing job cannot be paused, reverted, or stopped. Any parties that are consolidated as a result of the clean-up process cannot be unmerged or separated.

When parties are consolidated into a single party, the party name is assigned based on the party's creation date. The parties are consolidated under the name of the party with the oldest record.

This task is completed by users with Organize and manage all agreements access to Agreement Manager agreements and functionality.

1. Log in to your Docusign account

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Completed Documents page opens.
4. Expand the purple Insights bar at the top of the page. Select the Show Insights option:

   ![A snippet of the Completed Documents page that highlights the Show Insights option at the top of the page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Party-normalization-deduplication_Opt-in_Show-Insights_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTgwNTgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.8bsqLFMzerS160aN6ySeXiB-HammxiebzZK9t4Ha2lk&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Completed Documents page that highlights the Show Insights option at the top of the page.")

   The My Insights bar expands to show relevant actionable insights and prompts with links to reports or next steps. For example, upcoming renewals and activity trends.
5. Locate the interactive card that prompts you to clean up your organization party names:

   ![A snippet of the Insights bar that highlights the opt-in message for the normalization and deduplication process.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Normalization-deduplication_Opt-In-card_Clean-Up-Parties.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTgwNTgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.8bsqLFMzerS160aN6ySeXiB-HammxiebzZK9t4Ha2lk&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Insights bar that highlights the opt-in message for the normalization and deduplication process.")
6. Select the Clean Up Parties option from the Insights card.

   The Clean up your parties wizard opens and the process is explained. During processing, the party names in your account are reviewed and standardized. After the update, the process continues to check your party names with AI.

   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Party-normalization-deduplication_Opt-in_Clearn-up-your-parties-dialog.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTgwNTgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.8bsqLFMzerS160aN6ySeXiB-HammxiebzZK9t4Ha2lk&_LANG=enus)
7. Review the information in the wizard to learn more about the process.
   1. Expand the What we're looking for section and review the messaging. The process looks for minor differences in the same party names. Normalization rules standardize party names around casing, suffices, and punctuation.
   2. Expand the What to expect section and review the messaging. The processing job cannot be canceled once it is initiated. Updates happen in the background and processing time is dependent on the number of party records being reviewed.

title: Opt-In for Party Clean Up
text:
n and review the messaging. The processing job cannot be canceled once it is initiated. Updates happen in the background and processing time is dependent on the number of party records being reviewed.

      Tip: The party normalization and deduplication process will not interrupt your work or overwrite insights that were reviewed manually.
8. Opt in for the party normalization and deduplication process. Select the Clean Up Parties option.

   The wizard closes and the process is initiated.
9. Message boxes provide you with status updates:
   1. During processing, select the View Details option to view the status and job details for the processing job:

      ![A screenshot of a message box that indicates the process is cleaning up the parties.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Party-normalization-deduplication_Opt-in_message-box_View-Details.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTgwNTgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.8bsqLFMzerS160aN6ySeXiB-HammxiebzZK9t4Ha2lk&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of a message box that indicates the process is cleaning up the parties.")

      The All jobs monitoring hub opens and the Processing agreements view is displayed. See the [View and Download Processing Results](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=cvd1742251951627.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to view and download results for processing jobs.") article to learn more about the job monitoring hub.
   2. Once processing is completed, use the View Parties option to view your updated party list.

      ![A screenshot of a message box that indicates the updated party list is ready to view.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Party-normalization-deduplication_Opt-in_message-box_View-Parties.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTgwNTgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.8bsqLFMzerS160aN6ySeXiB-HammxiebzZK9t4Ha2lk&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of a message box that indicates the updated party list is ready to view.")

      The Parties page opens in the Intelligent Agreement Management (IAM) party hub. The updated organization party names are reflected in the party list table. See the [The Parties List](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=uzq1744241293689.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the enhanced Parties list, including access points and features.") article to learn more about the Parties page.

You have successfully opted-in for party clean-up.

title: View Hierarchies for Selected Parties and Groups
heading_path: View Hierarchies for Selected Parties and Groups
text:
Learn how to locate the agreement hierarchies for selected parties and groups.

Hierarchies are created and maintained at the party-level from the Hierarchies tab. While hierarchies are associated with a specific party, documents from other parties can be added to the hierarchy. This feature supports the more complex contractual multi-party arrangements.

Important:

Access to various hierarchy-related functionalities is based on a
user's document-level access. For example:

- **Viewing documents in a hierarchy.** Users can view the documents in a hierarchy if they have Can View, Can Edit, or Can Manage access to the document.

  If you do not have the appropriate document-level access, the documents are not visible in the
  hierarchy.
- **Adding documents.** When creating and updating hierarchies, users can only add documents to which they have Can Edit or Can Manage access.

  If you do not have the appropriate document-level access, you cannot add the agreement to the hierarchy.
- **Editing hierarchies.** Users can only edit a hierarchy if they have Can Edit or Can Manage access to all the documents in the hierarchy.

  If you do not have the appropriate document-level access, you cannot edit the hierarchy. For
  example, you cannot update details, add documents,
  or remove documents.
- **Rearranging document relationships in a hierarchy.** Users must have Can Edit or Can Manage access to all the documents in a hierarchy to rearrange or change the relationship between documents.

  If you do not have the appropriate document-level access, you cannot rearrange or update the document relationships for a hierarchy.

This topic reviews the steps to access hierarchy details from the party hub. Begin by locating the relevant party or group from the Parties page.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign
   Agreements page opens. The Inbox view
   is displayed by default.
3. Expand the PARTIES section in the navigation pane on the left and select the All option.

   The Parties page opens and a list of individual and merged parties
   is displayed in the table.
4. Select the relevant party or group from the parties list.
   1. Enter the name of the party or group in the Search parties field and press [Enter].

      Parties and groups matching the keyword are displayed in the table.
   2. Select the party or group from the table.

   The party hub opens for the selected party or group. The
   Overview tab is displayed by default.
5. Select the Hierarchies tab.

   Hierarchies are created and managed from the IAM party hub under the Hierarchies tab. From this page, you are prompted to create new hierarchies or update existing ones.
6. Create a new hierarchy for the selected party or group as needed.
   1. Select the Create Hierarchy option.

      The Create HIerarchy wizard opens. The Define step is displayed.
   2. Complete the Define step. Enter a Title and Description for the hierarchy and select the Next option.

      The Create Hierarchy wizard advances to the Add step.
   3. Complete the Add step. Locate the relevant agreements and select the + Add option for the agreements that you want to add to the hierarchy. Select the Next option.

      The Create Hierarchy wizard advances to the Arrange step.
   4. Complete the Arrange step. Designate the Primary agreement and reorder the other agreements to reflect the appropriate relationship structure. Select the Finish option.

title: View Hierarchies for Selected Parties and Groups
heading_path: View Hierarchies for Selected Parties and Groups
text:
nces to the Arrange step.
   4. Complete the Arrange step. Designate the Primary agreement and reorder the other agreements to reflect the appropriate relationship structure. Select the Finish option.

   The wizard closes and you are returned to the Hierarchies tab. The new hierarchy is displayed in the table. See the [Create a Hierarchy of Related Agreements](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tsk1f4c26ae-7477-4293-8b06-b2354f54271c.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to create a hierarchy of related agreements for a selected party or group.") article to learn more about creating hierarchies.
7. Update an existing hierarchy as needed.

   Important: You must have Can Edit or Can Manage access to all the documents in the hierarchy to edit the hierarchy.

   1. Select (click) the hierarchy that you want to update.

      The hierarchy details page opens.
   2. Update the hierarchy details. Select the Edit (pencil) icon located to the right of the hierarchy title. Update the Title and Description fields and select the Save option.

      You are returned to the hierarchy details page for the selected hierarchy.
   3. Use the Add Documents option to add additional agreements to the hierarchy.
   4. Update the relationship structure of the hierarchy. Drag the agreements to the appropriate location within the hierarchy. You can also expand the Arrange actions (vertical ellipsis) menu and use the Move options to reorder the agreements.
   5. Remove agreements from the hierarchy. Expand the Arrange actions menu for the relevant agreement and select the Remove from Hierarchy option.
   6. Select the Update option after making the necessary changes.

   The hierarchy is updated. See the [Manage Your Existing Hierarchies](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tskf2e938fc-cb7f-4c65-a7bf-1cf1433722a2.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to maintain your existing hierarchies, including updating the relationship structure between the related agreements.") article to learn more about updating hierarchies.

You have successfully viewed the hierarchies that are associated with a selected party or group.

title: Edit Details for Selected Parties and Groups
heading_path: Edit Details for Selected Parties and Groups
text:
Learn how to update the details for selected parties and groups, such as the party type and side details.

The "party hub" displays a unified summary of all agreements and obligations that are tied to a party or party group. Access the party hub from various places within Agreement Manager, including:

- The Parties column (Completed Documents view)
- The Details pane (Agreement preview page)
- The Name column (Parties view)

Note: This topic reviews the steps to access the party hub from
the Completed Documents view. You can also search for and
select the relevant party from the Parties page.

![A snippet of the Parties page that highlights the Search parties field.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Party-hub_Parties-page_Search-parties-highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc4MzAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.F6f9YkxGNJ6CgpocP2BogKCoHL4MmJyY1GN0VGUBBQI&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Parties page that highlights the Search parties field.")

- Go to Home  >  Agreements and select All from the PARTIES section in the navigation pane.
- Enter search criteria in the Search parties
  field.
- Select a party from the search results.

The party hub opens for the selected party or group.

**Access the party hub from the Completed Documents view:**

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens. The Inbox view is displayed by default.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed. Filter this view for a specific party.
4. Filter the agreement list table for a specific party:

   ![A screenshot of the Parties filter that shows the Search field, Party Role dropdown, and the party list.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-documents-table_Parties-dialog-box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc4MzAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.F6f9YkxGNJ6CgpocP2BogKCoHL4MmJyY1GN0VGUBBQI&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Parties filter that shows the Search field, Party Role dropdown, and the party list.")

   1. Expand the Filters option and select the Parties filter.
   2. Enter a keyword or search string in the Search field.

      As you enter search criteria, the list of parties updates to match the keyword.
   3. Select the relevant party.

      Important: When using the Parties filter, you must also select a
      Party Role. If you do not know
      which role to select, accept the default value, All.
   4. Apply the Party Role filter to view agreements where the selected party plays a specific role. If you do not know which role to select, accept the default value, All.

      Select a role from the Party Role dropdown. Choose from: Buyer, Seller, or Other.
   5. Select the Apply option.

   The table updates and agreements that are tied to the selected party or group are displayed. The names in the Parties column are clickable and link directly to the party hub.

   ![A snippet of the Completed Documents view that highlights the Parties column as an entry point for the party hub.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Party-hub_Entry-point_Completed-example.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc4MzAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.F6f9YkxGNJ6CgpocP2BogKCoHL4MmJyY1GN0VGUBBQI&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Completed Documents view that highlights the Parties column as an entry point for the party hub.")
5. Select a name from the Parties column.

   The party hub opens for the selected party or group. The Overview tab is displayed by default.

title: Edit Details for Selected Parties and Groups
heading_path: Edit Details for Selected Parties and Groups
text:
s the Parties column as an entry point for the party hub.")
5. Select a name from the Parties column.

   The party hub opens for the selected party or group. The Overview tab is displayed by default.

**Update party details:**

6. Select the Details tab:

   ![A snippet of the party hub that highlights the Overview, Agreements, and Obligations tabs.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Party-hub_Overview-dashboard_highlighted-tabs.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc4MzAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.F6f9YkxGNJ6CgpocP2BogKCoHL4MmJyY1GN0VGUBBQI&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the party hub that highlights the Overview, Agreements, and Obligations tabs.")

   The Details tab opens. The display name, party type, and party side details are displayed for the selected party or group.
7. Select the Edit Details option from the top right.

   The Details tab updates and the Party Type and Party Side fields are open for edits. You can also add an alias for the party or group.

   ![A screenshot of the Details tab that shows the editing options for the selected party or group.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Party-hub_Details-tab_Edit-details.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc4MzAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.F6f9YkxGNJ6CgpocP2BogKCoHL4MmJyY1GN0VGUBBQI&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Details tab that shows the editing options for the selected party or group.")
8. Update the details for the selected party or group as needed and save your changes:
   1. Select the + Add alias option and enter a party alias in the text-entry field.
   2. Expand the Party Type dropdown and select the relevant value, Organization or Individual.
   3. Expand the Party Side dropdown and select the appropriate value, Internal or External.
   4. Select the Save option.

   The changes are saved and the Details tab reflects your updates.

**Return to the Parties page:**

9. Select Parties from the breadcrumb navigation at the top of the page.

   You are returned to the Parties view.

You have successfully updated the details for a selected party or group.

title: Filter for Agreements by Specific Party Role
heading_path: Filter for Agreements by Specific Party Role
text:
Learn how to update the agreement list table to view agreements based on the role played by a specific party.

The Party Role is surfaced during agreement processing. This
extraction identifies the role that a party plays within the context of an agreement.
Once identified, apply the Party Role filter to view agreements
where a selected party plays a specific role. For example, they are the buyer or the
seller in the agreement.

Tip: The
Party Role filter is located under the
Parties filter and it is applied to a selected party:![A screenshot of the Parties filter that highlights the Party Role dropdown and available roles.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Filter-by-party-role_Parties-filter_Party-Role_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTYzOTQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.5ELlRbOopNq6z_vx7PCa38dEPOIGS7Or1OUcjwvrmEQ&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Parties filter that highlights the Party Role dropdown and available roles.")The Party Role defaults to All.
Be sure to select the party first before choosing a role. At least one party must be
selected before the Party Role filter can be
applied.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Completed Documents view opens. A list of documents to which the logged-in user has access is displayed in the table. The total document count is displayed in the bottom right. This count is updated when you perform a search or apply filters.
4. Expand (click) the Filters button and select the Parties option:![A snippet of the Completed Documents page that highlights the Parties option.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Filters-menu_Parties_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTYzOTQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.5ELlRbOopNq6z_vx7PCa38dEPOIGS7Or1OUcjwvrmEQ&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Completed Documents page that highlights the Parties option.")
5. Select the relevant party:

   ![A screenshot of the Parties filter that shows a search term and the selected party.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-documents-table_Parties_Tally-Inc-example.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTYzOTQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.5ELlRbOopNq6z_vx7PCa38dEPOIGS7Or1OUcjwvrmEQ&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Parties filter that shows a search term and the selected party.")

   1. Enter a name in the Search field. As you enter the name, the list is updated to display parties that match the keyword.
   2. Select the relevant party.

      Important: When using the Parties filter, you must also select a
      Party Role. If you do not know
      which role to select, accept the default value, All.
6. The Party Role defaults to All. Expand the dropdown and select the
   appropriate role:

   ![A screenshot of the Parties filter that highlights the Party Role dropdown, including available roles.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Filter-by-party-role_Parties-filter_Party-Role_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTYzOTQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.5ELlRbOopNq6z_vx7PCa38dEPOIGS7Or1OUcjwvrmEQ&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Parties filter that highlights the Party Role dropdown, including available roles.")

   Choose from: Buyer,
   Seller, or Other.

title: Filter for Agreements by Specific Party Role
heading_path: Filter for Agreements by Specific Party Role
text:
bOopNq6z_vx7PCa38dEPOIGS7Or1OUcjwvrmEQ&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Parties filter that highlights the Party Role dropdown, including available roles.")

   Choose from: Buyer,
   Seller, or Other.

   For example:
   - Select Buyer to see all the buy-side
     agreements for the selected party.
   - Select Seller to see all the sell-side
     agreements for the selected party.If you do not know the role, accept the default value, All.
7. Select the Apply option.

   The filter closes and the agreements that match the applied filters are displayed in the table:![A screenshot of the Completed view that highlights the Parties filter and lists the agreements that match the applied filters.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-list-table_filtered-for-party-role_Fontara-Buyer.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTYzOTQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.5ELlRbOopNq6z_vx7PCa38dEPOIGS7Or1OUcjwvrmEQ&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Completed view that highlights the Parties filter and lists the agreements that match the applied filters.")The applied filter is displayed at the top of the page. In this example, the Parties filter indicates that the Fontara and Fontara, Inc values have been applied to the agreement list.

   Tip: Update the filter to view your sell-side agreements with the selected party. Expand (click) the applied filter and select Seller from the Party Role: ![A screenshot of the Parties filter that shows options for updating the party role for the selected party.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-list-table_update-applied-filter_Party-Role_seller.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTYzOTQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.5ELlRbOopNq6z_vx7PCa38dEPOIGS7Or1OUcjwvrmEQ&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Parties filter that shows options for updating the party role for the selected party.")

   Use the Status filter to further refine the agreement list table. For example, choose to view only Active agreements. See the [Agreement Statuses](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=con6a3bd080-da17-4d1c-a92a-b9b9340c44fd.html&_LANG=enus "Read about agreement statuses, including how they are calculated, visibility within Agreement Manager, and which types of statuses are being tracked.") topic to learn more about this feature.

   Tip: If you routinely search for the same type of agreements or agreement data, save your search criteria for future use. See the [Save Search Criteria for Future Use](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tskc38121a1-bbf8-4da8-8ed7-b364991f7e50.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to save the search criteria for the queries that you frequently perform.") topic to learn more about the saved search feature.

You have successfully viewed your agreements with a selected party based on the party's
role.

title: Update Custom Field Details
text:
Learn how to update the details for your custom fields, such as the name and category.

Administrators and contract managers can update the custom fields in their account. For example, update the name and category details for a selected field.

Note: The Edit Details function is available for custom fields only. You cannot update the standard, Docusign-provided fields. This function is completed by users with Organize and manage all agreements access to Agreement Manager and the account's agreements.

Use the *Fields Manager* to update the details for a custom field. Access this function from the Agreement Manager homepage under the Settings (gear) menu.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed. A list of documents to which the
   logged-in user has access is displayed in the table.
4. Expand the Settings (gear) dropdown from the top right and select the Fields option:

   ![A screenshot of the Agreement Manager homepage that highlights the Fields option in the Settings (gear) menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-types-manager_Settings-menu_Fields_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc3MzMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.Szgh_xEp-74SZ_1x_N6VjMvfRS_-i1XwfanALvKO5Y8&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Agreement Manager homepage that highlights the Fields option in the Settings (gear) menu.")

   The Manage Fields page opens and the Fields view is displayed. All the custom and standard fields in the account are displayed in the table.
5. Locate the custom field that you want to update.

   ![A snippet of the Manage Fields page that shows the search field and filter options for the Fields table.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Fields-Manager_Fields-table_search-and-filters.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc3MzMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.Szgh_xEp-74SZ_1x_N6VjMvfRS_-i1XwfanALvKO5Y8&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Manage Fields page that shows the search field and filter options for the Fields table.")

   - Search for the relevant custom field.

     Enter a keyword in the Search fields... field. As you type the keyword, the table updates and the fields that match the search term are displayed.
   - Filter the table to locate the relevant custom field. Filter options are: Created By, Category, Field Type, and AI Status.

     For example, filter the table to view user-created fields. Expand the filter, choose a value, and select the Apply option.

     Important: The Edit Details function is only available for user-created or custom fields. You cannot update the details for Docusign-provided fields.

     The table updates and the fields that matched the applied filters are displayed.
6. Expand the More actions (three dots) menu for the relevant field and select the Edit Details option:

   ![A snippet of the agreement type details view that highlights the Edit Details option for a custom field.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Fields-Manager_Fields-table_More-actions-menu_Edit-Details_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc3MzMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.Szgh_xEp-74SZ_1x_N6VjMvfRS_-i1XwfanALvKO5Y8&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the agreement type details view that highlights the Edit Details option for a custom field.")

title: Update Custom Field Details
text:
sInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.Szgh_xEp-74SZ_1x_N6VjMvfRS_-i1XwfanALvKO5Y8&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the agreement type details view that highlights the Edit Details option for a custom field.")

   The Edit field dialog box opens and a warning (!) message is displayed. Any changes that you make to the field are reflected everywhere that the field is being used.
7. Update the details for the selected field and select the Save option:

   ![A snippet of the Edit field details dialog box that shows you can update the field's Name and Category.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Manage-Agreement-Types_Agreement-type-details_Edit-field-details-dialog-box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc3MzMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.Szgh_xEp-74SZ_1x_N6VjMvfRS_-i1XwfanALvKO5Y8&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Edit field details dialog box that shows you can update the field's Name and Category.")

   1. Enter a new Name for the custom field as needed.
   2. Update the Category for the custom field as needed. Expand the Category dropdown and select the relevant category.
   3. Select the Save option.

   Tip: The Field type and Agreement types fields are closed to edits. You cannot update these fields from this location. However, you can add the field to multiple agreement types from the *Agreement Types Manager*. See the [Add Fields to Multiple Agreement Types](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tsk37e570f8-2428-4d26-a78b-b1239aca35a5.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to add fields to multiple agreement types from the Add a field dialog box. You can add fields to additional agreement types while you are updating a selected agreement type.") article to learn about adding fields to multiple agreement types.
8. Select the Save option.

   The dialog box closes and you are returned to the Manage Fields page. The custom field is listed in the Fields table and it reflects the updated details.
9. Return to the Agreement Manager home page. Select the Close (x) icon from the top right.

   You are returned to the Agreement Manager homepage and the Completed Documents view is displayed.

You have successfully updated the name and category details for a custom field.

title: Update the Agreement Status for a Document
heading_path: Update the Agreement Status for a Document
text:
Learn how to update the agreement status for a document.

Agreement statuses provide a quick look at where the agreement is within its life cycle. These statuses are calculated based on an agreement's extracted Effective
Date, Expiration Date, and Renewal
Type.

Note: A Renewal Type value is not required for calculating an agreement's status. However, if an extracted value exists, it is taken into consideration when calculating the status. For example, if an agreement has an Expiration Date and the Renewal Type is Evergreen, the agreement is always Active.

When necessary, you can manually update an agreement's status. For example, update the status for a terminated agreement to *Inactive*.

Important: When you update an agreement's Status, it is no longer automatically calculated and updated. The calculation process stops, regardless of the Effective Date, Expiration Date, and Renewal Type values.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed. A list of documents to which the logged-in user has access is displayed in the table.
4. Locate the agreement that you want to update.

   ![A snippet of the Completed Documents view that highlights the search field and available filters.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Navigator_Completed-documents_search-field-and-filters-highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY1NzMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.Xgs2K1cmfDpOKVBVxaDb2-lIBGNfoTmzMYY2PngQx4w&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Completed Documents view that highlights the search field and available filters.")

   1. Enter a keyword or search string in the search field and select Enter.
   2. Use the Filters option to refine the list of search results. Locate the filter in the Filters list and choose or enter values. Select the Apply option.

      The agreements that match your search criteria and filters are displayed in the table. The document count reflects the entered search criteria and applied filters.
5. Select (click) the agreement that you want to update.

   The Agreement preview page opens for the selected agreement. The Details pane is displayed by default and shows a list of attributes and suggested values.
6. Place the Details pane into *edit* mode:

   ![A snippet of the Agreement preview page that highlights ways to place the Details pane into edit mode.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-statuses_Agreement-Preview_Details_Edit-options_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY1NzMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.Xgs2K1cmfDpOKVBVxaDb2-lIBGNfoTmzMYY2PngQx4w&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Agreement preview page that highlights ways to place the Details pane into edit mode.")

   - Select the Edit (pencil) icon.
   - Select the Review All option.

title: Update the Agreement Status for a Document
heading_path: Update the Agreement Status for a Document
text:
MYY2PngQx4w&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Agreement preview page that highlights ways to place the Details pane into edit mode.")

   - Select the Edit (pencil) icon.
   - Select the Review All option.

   Note: The ability to edit attribute values requires Can Manage or Can Edit access to the agreement:![A screenshot of the Access dropdown for an agreement that highlights the Can Edit and Can Manage options.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-access_Can-Manage_Can-Edit_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY1NzMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.Xgs2K1cmfDpOKVBVxaDb2-lIBGNfoTmzMYY2PngQx4w&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Access dropdown for an agreement that highlights the Can Edit and Can Manage options.")
   - Can Manage users can edit attribute values, as well as delete, share, and remove access to the agreement.
   - Can Edit users can edit attribute values, and share and download the agreement.

   The Review & edit details pane opens for updates.
7. Update the agreement's Status and select the Save option:

   ![A snippet of the Agreement preview page that highlights the Status field in the Review & edit details pane.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-statuses_Agreement-Preview_Review-edit-details_Status-highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY1NzMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.Xgs2K1cmfDpOKVBVxaDb2-lIBGNfoTmzMYY2PngQx4w&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Agreement preview page that highlights the Status field in the Review & edit details pane.")

   1. Scroll to the Status field, located under the General section.
   2. Select the toggle to turn off (white position) the automatic status calculation feature. This feature is turned on by default to calculate the status based on related details.
   3. Expand the dropdown and select the relevant status, Active or Inactive.
   4. Select the Save option.

   The Details pane reverts to read-only mode and the agreement's status is updated.

   Remember: When you update an agreement's Status, it is no longer automatically calculated and updated. The calculation process stops, regardless of the Effective Date, Expiration Date, and Renewal Type values.
8. Select the Close (x) icon from the top-left corner.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed. Your search criteria and filters are still applied.

You have successfully updated the status for a selected agreement.

Important: You can turn on the calculation again by using this same process. Place the
Details pane into *edit*
mode and update the Status
toggle so it is in the blue position. ![A snippet of the Agreement preview page that highlights the Status toggle in the Review & edit details pane.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-statuses_Agreement-Preview_Details_Status-snippet_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY1NzMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.Xgs2K1cmfDpOKVBVxaDb2-lIBGNfoTmzMYY2PngQx4w&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Agreement preview page that highlights the Status toggle in the Review & edit details pane.")Be sure to Save your
changes.

title: Download and Fix Your Metadata Validation Errors
heading_path: Download and Fix Your Metadata Validation Errors
text: Learn how to download and fix your metadata validation errors from the upload process.

title: Download and Fix Your Metadata Validation Errors
heading_path: Download and Fix Your Metadata Validation Errors
text:
This task reviews the steps to resolve the errors from the upload process. It relies on you completing the previous phases, including:

- [Download the Pre-Formatted Spreadsheet File](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tsk1b94afc6-712f-4d71-868e-0787867354d8.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to download the pre-formatted spreadsheet file for a group of documents.")
- [Edit the Metadata Spreadsheet](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tsk8710c3f2-c881-4219-96b0-2b287e8b4e03.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to edit the metadata spreadsheet file to bulk update the agreement data for the selected documents.")
- [Import Your Edited Metadata Spreadsheet](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tsk5ab1cd5b-00f7-42a9-bd77-728baadff903.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to import your edited metadata spreadsheet using the Add (+) menu located on the Agreement Manager homepage.")
- [Review Job Results from the Notification Email](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tske7ac3afe-c04e-4acc-95b1-9f83b1b66ed5.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to view the results of an upload job from the email notification. A system-generated email is sent when the upload process is completed.")

When you import the new data, the existing values are overwritten, including the AI-extracted values. Agreement Manager applies all valid cell values and flags the formatting errors in the job monitoring hub.

Resolve these formatting errors and re-import the spreadsheet file. Repeat this step until all the field values are updated successfully.

In the previous topic, you reviewed how to access the error details from the notification email. This topic reviews the steps to access the job monitoring hub from the Agreement Manager homepage.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed.

**Access the job status**

4. Expand the Settings (gear) menu and select the Uploads and Processing Status option:

   ![A screenshot of the Completed Documents view that shows the Uploads and Processing Status option highlighted in the Settings (gear) menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-documents-view_Actions_Monitor-All-Jobs-option.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY4MjAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.uIbpCRE9sdkaTET0hP0DZGAuSd7Px3DhfG30HwcZwas&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Completed Documents view that shows the Uploads and Processing Status option highlighted in the Settings (gear) menu.")

   The job monitoring hub opens and the All jobs page is displayed. Use this page to access the status of any job, including uploads and processing.

**Review the error details**

5. Go to the Job status page for your upload job:

   ![A snippet of the All jobs page that highlights the View Details option for an upload job.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Bulk-metadata-editing_Uploaded-Agreements_Actions_View-Details_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY4MjAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.uIbpCRE9sdkaTET0hP0DZGAuSd7Px3DhfG30HwcZwas&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the All jobs page that highlights the View Details option for an upload job.")

   1. Locate the upload job in the Uploaded Agreements table. Apply filters and use the sorting arrows as needed.
   2. Expand the corresponding Actions dropdown and select the View Details option.

title: Download and Fix Your Metadata Validation Errors
heading_path: Download and Fix Your Metadata Validation Errors
text:
)

   1. Locate the upload job in the Uploaded Agreements table. Apply filters and use the sorting arrows as needed.
   2. Expand the corresponding Actions dropdown and select the View Details option.

   The Job status page opens for the upload job. The files that failed to update are listed in the Error details table, including the reason the data failed to update.
6. Review the errors listed in the Error details table:

   ![A snippet of the Job status page that shows the Error details table, including the reason the files failed to update.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Bulk-metadata-editing_Job-status_Error-details_Fontara-example.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY4MjAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.uIbpCRE9sdkaTET0hP0DZGAuSd7Px3DhfG30HwcZwas&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Job status page that shows the Error details table, including the reason the files failed to update.")

**Fix the errors**

The Errors column describes the reasons that the files were not updated. Use the Download and Fix option to update the field values that failed validation.

Tip: Leave your Agreement Manager window open so you can reference the Error details table. Use the View option to access the associated document. The file opens in the Agreement preview page.

See the [Troubleshoot Spreadsheet Upload Errors](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=ref4dcc5ebd-8791-48fd-98e6-5c5d6bae7bc5.html&_LANG=enus "Look up troubleshooting steps for resolving errors during the metadata spreadsheet upload.") article for steps to resolve common validation errors.

7. Select the Download and Fix option.

   A pre-formatted spreadsheet file is downloaded to your local Downloads folder. This spreadsheet is pre-populated with the agreements that failed validation and were not updated.

   Important: The same formatting requirements and spreadsheet rules apply to this spreadsheet file.
8. Open the pre-formatted spreadsheet.

   Tip: Use the Freeze Panes option under the View ribbon to freeze the first two rows of the spreadsheet. Freezing the Agreement ID and File Name rows helps you find the correct fields to update.

   The spreadsheet opens. It is pre-formatted with the agreements from the Error details table. Update the fields that were noted in the Errors column.
9. Resolve the first error in the list. In this example, the error is, 'Usage fees must be a valid number." This error is displayed when the entered number did not follow the formatting requirement.

   Locate the agreement row and field column for the first error. Enter a value using the correct format:

   ![A snippet of the pre-formatted spreadsheet that highlights the updated agreement and value for one of the errors in the list.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Bulk-metadata-editing_Download-and-fix_Update-usage-fee_example.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY4MjAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.uIbpCRE9sdkaTET0hP0DZGAuSd7Px3DhfG30HwcZwas&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the pre-formatted spreadsheet that highlights the updated agreement and value for one of the errors in the list.")

   Remember: For number fields, enter digits only with no commas. Decimals are approved where the field supports them.

   For example, enter *10000* in the Usage fees (NUMBER) column for the appropriate agreement. The header row includes the field type to help you populate the spreadsheet.
10. Resolve the next error in the list. In this example, the error is, "Parties must follow the format: [Name] | [Role]." This error message is displayed when the Parties value was entered incorrectly or is missing information.

    Locate the agreement row and field column for the error. Enter a value using the correct format:

title: Download and Fix Your Metadata Validation Errors
heading_path: Download and Fix Your Metadata Validation Errors
text:
ror message is displayed when the Parties value was entered incorrectly or is missing information.

    Locate the agreement row and field column for the error. Enter a value using the correct format:

    ![A snippet of the pre-formatted spreadsheet that highlights the updated agreement and value for one of the errors in the list.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Bulk-metadata-editing_Download-and-fix_Update-parties-example.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY4MjAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.uIbpCRE9sdkaTET0hP0DZGAuSd7Px3DhfG30HwcZwas&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the pre-formatted spreadsheet that highlights the updated agreement and value for one of the errors in the list.")

    Remember: When populating the Parties column, enter a party name and role. If you are unsure of the role or do not want to include one, us the format: [Name] | [None]. Use semicolon-separated entries when adding multiple party values in the same cell.

    For example, enter *Fontara | Buyer; Liberty Innovate | Seller* in the Parties column for the appropriate agreement.
11. Resolve the next error in the list. In this example, the error is, "Effective Date must be a valid date and follow the format: MM/DD/YYYY." This error message is displayed when a date value was entered with using the incorrect format.

    Locate the agreement row and field column for the error. Enter a value using the correct format:

    ![A snippet of the pre-formatted spreadsheet that highlights the updated agreement and value for two of the errors in the list.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Bulk-metadata-editing_Download-and-fix_Update-date-field-example.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY4MjAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.uIbpCRE9sdkaTET0hP0DZGAuSd7Px3DhfG30HwcZwas&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the pre-formatted spreadsheet that highlights the updated agreement and value for two of the errors in the list.")

    Remember: When populating date fields, enter dates in the standard, U.S. format: *MM-DD-YYYY*.

    For example, enter *01/01/2026* in the Effective Date (DATE) column for the appropriate agreements. There are two agreements in the Error details table with this issue.
12. Continue updating the pre-formatted spreadsheet to fix the errors noted in the Error details table. When all the errors are resolved, save your changes and close the spreadsheet.

**Import the updated spreadsheet**

After populating the pre-formatted spreadsheet with the relevant data values, import the metadata into your account.

13. Return to the Agreement Manager homepage:
    1. From the Job status page, select the Back arrow in the top left. You are returned to the All Jobs page.
    2. From the All Jobs page, select the Back arrow in the top left. You are returned to the Agreement Manager homepage.
14. Expand the Add (+) dropdown and select the Import Data option:

    ![A snippet of the Completed Documents page that highlights the Import Data option in the Add (+) dropdown menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Bulk-metadata-editing_Add-menu_Import-Data_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY4MjAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.uIbpCRE9sdkaTET0hP0DZGAuSd7Px3DhfG30HwcZwas&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Completed Documents page that highlights the Import Data option in the Add (+) dropdown menu.")

title: Download and Fix Your Metadata Validation Errors
heading_path: Download and Fix Your Metadata Validation Errors
text:
WFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.uIbpCRE9sdkaTET0hP0DZGAuSd7Px3DhfG30HwcZwas&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Completed Documents page that highlights the Import Data option in the Add (+) dropdown menu.")

    The Import data from a spreadsheet dialog box opens. You are prompted to drop or select your edited file.
15. Upload the pre-formatted spreadsheet file that you populated with the correct metadata values. This is the spreadsheet that you downloaded from the Job status page. Drop the file in the dialog box of use the Select File option to select the relevant file.
16. Select the Import Data option.

    The dialog box closes and you are returned to the Completed Documents page. A confirmation message indicates that the data was imported.
17. Select the View Details option from the confirmation message.

    The Job status page opens for the upload job.

    ![A screenshot of the Job status page for the upload job that shows all the files were successfully updated.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Bulk-metadata-editing_Job-status_All-files-successfully-updated.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY4MjAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.uIbpCRE9sdkaTET0hP0DZGAuSd7Px3DhfG30HwcZwas&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Job status page for the upload job that shows all the files were successfully updated.")
18. If errors persist, repeat this process until all the errors are resolved.

You have successfully resolved the validation errors, imported the metadata, and updated the files that previously failed. This completes the process for using the *Bulk Metadata Editing* feature.

title: Star Your Favorite Reports and Dashboards
heading_path: Star Your Favorite Reports and Dashboards
text:
Learn how to add your frequently used dashboards and reports to the Your Favorites section on the Overview dashboard.

The Your Favorites section gives you quick access to your personalized list of starred dashboards and reports. Select the Favorite (star) icon to "star" your frequently accessed dashboards and reports. This icon is available for custom and standard dashboards and reports.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Insights from the global header.

   The Insights hub opens and the Overview dashboard is displayed.

**Star your favorite dashboards:**

3. Locate the dashboards that you want to add to the Your Favorites list:
   - Select a standard dashboard from the navigation pane on the left. The standard dashboards include: Agreements, Obligations, and Renewals.
   - Select a custom dashboard from the navigation pane on the left. Expand the Custom Dashboards group from the navigation pane and select a custom dashboard.
   - Select a standard or custom dashboard from the Overview dashboard. Perform a keyword search to locate the relevant dashboard. Enter a keyword in the search field at the top of the page and select a value from the type-ahead dropdown. Results are grouped by Reports and Dashboards.

   The selected dashboard opens and the Favorite (star) icon is displayed
   in the top right.
4. Select the Favorite (star) icon:

   ![A snippet of a selected dashboard that highlights the Favorite (star) icon in the top right corner.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Overview_star-favorite-dashboard_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY4OTMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.s425S7HzdUhKyHuze7u1PhwlXs3H-UGqd4lKTFtrKlg&_LANG=enus "A snippet of a selected dashboard that highlights the Favorite (star) icon in the top right corner.")The Favorite (star) icon turns blue, indicating that the dashboard is "starred".

   Tip:

   Select the Favorite (star) icon again to remove the dashboard from the Your Favorites list.

**Star your favorite reports:**

5. Locate the reports that you want to add to the Your Favorites list:
   - Select a report from the Overview dashboard:

     - Enter a keyword in the search field at the top of the dashboard and select the relevant report from the dropdown.
     - Select a report from the Your Recents section. This section provides a list of your most recently viewed reports.
     - Select a dashboard chart to view the corresponding report.
     - Select a chart segment to view the corresponding report, filtered for the selected segment.
   - Choose a report from another dashboard. Select a dashboard from the navigation pane on the
     left. From the dashboard page, drill down to the detailed report view.
     Expand the More actions menu (ellipsis) for
     the relevant dashboard chart and select View
     Report.

   The detailed report view opens for the selected chart and the Favorite
   (star) icon is displayed in the top right.
6. Select the Favorite (star) icon:

   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Overview_Star-favorite-report_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY4OTMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.s425S7HzdUhKyHuze7u1PhwlXs3H-UGqd4lKTFtrKlg&_LANG=enus)

   The Favorite (star) icon turns blue, indicating that the dashboard is "starred".

   Tip:

   Select the Favorite (star) icon again to remove the report from the Your Favorites list.

You have successfully "starred" your favorite dashboards and reports.

title: Find Custom Fields in Specific Documents
heading_path: Find Custom Fields in Specific Documents
text: Learn how to use the Find On Specific Documents function to surface custom fields in selected agreements.

title: Find Custom Fields in Specific Documents
heading_path: Find Custom Fields in Specific Documents
text:
Once a custom field is enabled for AI detection, you can find it in specific documents at any time. This requires you to complete these tasks first:

- [Add Custom Fields to Agreement Types](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=hcn1736879099815.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to create and add custom fields to the custom and standard agreement types in your account.")
- [Add Custom Fields to Standard Agreement Types](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=vju1740452710836.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to create and add custom fields to standard agreement types.")
- [Test the Extraction Accuracy for Your Custom Fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=wjr1740452628309.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to test the AI detection accuracy for your custom fields and refining the results to improve accuracy.")

The Find On Specific Documents function is used to
process specific documents for a custom field. If the field is located in the document, the associated value is surfaced.

Important: The selected documents are reprocessed *only* for that specific custom field. If the field is not found in the document, there is no further processing.

This function is found under the Fields option in the Settings (gear) menu.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed.
4. Expand the Settings (gear) dropdown from the
   top right and select the Fields
   option:

   ![A snippet of the Agreement Manager homepage that highlights the Fields option in the Settings menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Fields-Manager_Settings-menu_Fields-option_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc3MjIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.l-5FDwGrSKi9J5cfHtZcRv3DG8AnmsmPrrD_mySzR-k&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Agreement Manager homepage that highlights the Fields option in the Settings menu.")

   The Manage Fields page opens. The
   Fields view is displayed. The standard and custom fields that are associated with the account are listed in the table.
5. Locate the field that you want to find in specific documents:

   ![A snippet of the Manage Fields page that shows the search field and filter options for the Fields table.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Fields-Manager_Fields-table_search-and-filters.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc3MjIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.l-5FDwGrSKi9J5cfHtZcRv3DG8AnmsmPrrD_mySzR-k&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Manage Fields page that shows the search field and filter options for the Fields table.")

   - Search for the relevant custom field.

     Enter a keyword in the Search fields... field. As you type the keyword, the table updates and the fields that match the search term are displayed.
   - Filter the table to locate the relevant custom field. Filter options are: Created
     By, Category, Field
     Type, and AI Status. For example, use
     the Created By filter the table for user-created
     fields.

     Expand the filter, choose a value, and select the
     Apply option.

     Important: The
     Edit Details function is only available for
     user-created or custom fields. You cannot update the details for Docusign-provided fields.

     The table updates
     and the fields that matched the applied filters are
     displayed.
6. Expand the More actions (three dots) menu for the relevant field and select the Find On Specific Documents option:

title: Find Custom Fields in Specific Documents
heading_path: Find Custom Fields in Specific Documents
text:
e updates
     and the fields that matched the applied filters are
     displayed.
6. Expand the More actions (three dots) menu for the relevant field and select the Find On Specific Documents option:

   ![A snippet of the Fields view that highlights the Find On Specific Documents option in the More actions (three dots) menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Fields-Manager_Fields-table_More-actions_Find-On-Specific-Documents.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc3MjIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.l-5FDwGrSKi9J5cfHtZcRv3DG8AnmsmPrrD_mySzR-k&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Fields view that highlights the Find On Specific Documents option in the More actions (three dots) menu.")

   The Select documents to process page opens. Use this page to search for the documents that you want to process for the field.
7. Choose the documents that you want to process for the custom field:

   ![A screenshot of the Select documents to process page that shows applied filters and selected documents.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Fields-Manager_Fields_Select-docs-to-process_selected.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc3MjIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.l-5FDwGrSKi9J5cfHtZcRv3DG8AnmsmPrrD_mySzR-k&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Select documents to process page that shows applied filters and selected documents.")

   1. Search for the relevant agreements. Enter a keyword or search string in the search field. The dropdown opens and search suggestions are displayed. Select a search suggestion or press the Enter key.

      The table updates and agreements that matched the search term are displayed.
   2. Filter the agreement list table for relevant agreements. For example, filter by Sets, Status, Parties, Agreement Type, or AI Suggestions.

      Tip: Use the Filters option to apply additional filters to the results table.

      Expand the filter, choose a value, and select the Apply option.

      The table updates and the agreements that matched applied filters are displayed.
   3. Select the relevant documents from the table.
8. Select the Start Processing option from the bottom right.

   You are returned to the Fields view. A message confirms that the selected documents are being processed.

   ![A screenshot of the Fields view that shows the confirmation message that the documents are being checked for the custom field.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Fields-Manager_Fields-table_Finding-field-on-docs_confirmation-message.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc3MjIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.l-5FDwGrSKi9J5cfHtZcRv3DG8AnmsmPrrD_mySzR-k&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Fields view that shows the confirmation message that the documents are being checked for the custom field.")

   If the custom field is found in any of the documents, the field value is surfaced in the agreement.

   Tip: Select the View Progress option to view the processing status for the job. The job monitoring hub opens, and the Processing status and Job details are displayed. Review the processing results, including any error messages.Select the Close (x) icon in the top right to return to the Agreement Manager homepage.
9. Return to the Agreement Manager homepage. Select the Close (X) icon located in the top right.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed.

title: Find Custom Fields in Specific Documents
heading_path: Find Custom Fields in Specific Documents
text:
nager homepage.
9. Return to the Agreement Manager homepage. Select the Close (X) icon located in the top right.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed.

   Note: Reprocess your documents for standard and custom
   data on-demand from the agreement list table:![A snippet of the agreement list table that highlights the Custom Data option in the Update dropdown.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Ondemand-reprocessing_Update-dropdown_Custom-data_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc3MjIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.l-5FDwGrSKi9J5cfHtZcRv3DG8AnmsmPrrD_mySzR-k&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the agreement list table that highlights the Custom Data option in the Update dropdown.")See the [Refresh Agreements for New Data Points](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=fog1750877660436.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to reprocess a group of selected agreements to surface new data points. For example, to surface custom agreement types or fields within your agreements.")
   topic to learn how to reprocess selected documents
   for custom data.

You have successfully processed a group of documents for a custom field. If the field is identified in any of the selected documents, the relevant data points are surfaced.

title: Filter the Agreement List Table by Status
heading_path: Filter the Agreement List Table by Status
text:
Learn how to filter the agreement list table by agreement status.

Agreement statuses provide a quick look at where an agreement is within its life cycle.
These statuses are calculated based on an agreement's extracted Effective
Date, Expiration Date, and Renewal
Type.

Note: A Renewal Type value is not required for calculating an agreement's status. However, if an extracted value exists, it is taken into consideration when calculating the status. For example, if an agreement has an Expiration Date and the Renewal Type is Evergreen, the agreement is always Active.

Agreement statuses are re-calculated and updated daily, based on the current
date.

Refine the agreement list table to view agreements based on selected
statuses. For example: Active,
Inactive, Expiring Soon,
Renewing Soon, and Effective
Soon.

The Status column and filter are associated with the agreement list table and can be found on the:

- Completed page
- Agreements page in the party hub
- Detailed report view for a selected dashboard or report

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens. The Inbox view is displayed by default.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed. Perform a search and apply filters to locate the relevant agreements.
4. Apply the Status filter to the agreement list table:

   ![A snippet of the Completed view that shows the Status filter and filter options.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-statuses_Completed-view_Status-filter_expanded.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY1NzAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.Y2e15kI3VAdfXklAm2cNtmyNFT5LO1VZK7SfLbIMxSM&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Completed view that shows the Status filter and filter options.")

   1. Expand (click) the Filters option and select the Status filter.

      The Status filter opens.
   2. Choose the relevant agreement statuses.

      You can select more than one status. For example, select two statuses to view agreements for either status.
   3. Select the Apply option.
