   The agreement list updates and the agreements that match the selected statuses are displayed.

You have successfully filtered the agreement list table to view agreements with a specific status.

title: Edit the Metadata Spreadsheet
text: Learn how to edit the metadata spreadsheet file to bulk update the agreement data for the selected documents.

title: Edit the Metadata Spreadsheet
text:
Edit the spreadsheet to include the new metadata values. When you edit the spreadsheet, follow the formatting rules and do not change the template structure. For example, do not insert columns and rows. And do not edit the first two rows or the first three columns.

Make sure you are editing the Docusign-generated, pre-formatted spreadsheet. See the [Download the Pre-Formatted Spreadsheet File](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tsk1b94afc6-712f-4d71-868e-0787867354d8.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to download the pre-formatted spreadsheet file for a group of documents.") article for steps to download the file.

This topic provides suggestions for editing the metadata spreadsheet. The pre-formatted spreadsheet contains no data values. Populate the spreadsheet with the relevant agreement data for the selected documents.

Important: The spreadsheet is pre-formatted to contain system-generated agreements IDs and field-mapping data. Do not edit or delete these values. This data is located in the first two rows and in the first three columns.

1. Open the pre-formatted spreadsheet file that you downloaded previously.

   The spreadsheet file opens. It is pre-formatted to include the selected agreements, mapping data, and all the custom and standard agreements in the account.

**Hide columns in the spreadsheet**

2. Hide the columns that you are not editing. The spreadsheet contains all the custom and standard fields in your account in a scrolling worksheet. Hide the columns to make it easier to update the relevant columns:

   ![A snippet of the pre-formatted spreadsheet that highlights the Hide option for a selected column.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Bulk-metadata-editing_Hide-columns_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY4MTMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.ffGJ7rxBvf-E9rBKOpx6jZ4yVK-qrfcAaxLkfD0ccs0&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the pre-formatted spreadsheet that highlights the Hide option for a selected column.")

   1. Select the columns that you want to hide. Hold the Shift key and select the columns that you want to hide.
   2. Right-click at the top of a selected column and select the Hide option.
   3. Repeat this process until the spreadsheet reflects the fields that you want to populate.

   The selected columns are hidden in the spreadsheet. While you can remove columns from the spreadsheet, it is not recommended.

   Warning: Do not remove or re-order the first three columns in the spreadsheet. The Agreement ID (GUID), File Name (TEXT), and Agreement Type (TEXT) columns are integral to successfully updating the selected documents with the new metadata.

**Remove unnecessary agreements (rows)**

3. Remove any agreements that you do not want to update. While you cannot add rows to the spreadsheet, when necessary, you can remove them:

   ![A snippet of the pre-formatted spreadsheet that highlights the Delete option for a selected row.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Bulk-metadata-editing_Delete-row_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY4MTMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.ffGJ7rxBvf-E9rBKOpx6jZ4yVK-qrfcAaxLkfD0ccs0&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the pre-formatted spreadsheet that highlights the Delete option for a selected row.")

   1. Select the rows that you want to delete. Hold the Shift key and click on the row numbers for the rows that you want to delete.
   2. Right-click on a selected row select the Delete option.
   3. Repeat this process until the spreadsheet reflects the agreements that you want to update.

   The rows are removed from the table. While you can remove rows from the table, it is not recommended.

title: Edit the Metadata Spreadsheet
text:
3. Repeat this process until the spreadsheet reflects the agreements that you want to update.

   The rows are removed from the table. While you can remove rows from the table, it is not recommended.

   Warning: Do not remove or edit the first two rows in the spreadsheet, including the field names in the header row. The field names in the header row are pre-formatted from Agreement Manager and the second row contains field-mapping data. Both rows ensure that the new metadata is mapped to the correct agreements and fields. Do not alter these rows.

**Populate the data cells**

4. Enter values for the relevant metadata. Be sure to follow the [field-specific formatting requirements](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=refa47e5582-efd8-403b-b39c-45a0c05beb1f.html&_LANG=enus "Look up spreadsheet rules and formatting requirements for using the Bulk Metadata Edit.") when populating the spreadsheet:
   1. Text fields have a 2,000-character limit for custom fields and a 400-character limit for standard fields.

      Important: Depending on how the standard fields are used across the platform, the maximum character limit can vary.
   2. Duration field values are entered using the format, [Number][Space]{Time Unit].
   3. Date field values are entered using the standard, U.S. format, MM-DD-YYYY.
   4. Currency field values are entered using the format, [Number][Space][Currency code].
   5. Multi-values are entered as comma-separated values.

   Tip: If you are entering the same value for all the agreements, populate the cell in row 3 and copy it down to the other rows:![An animated screenshot of the metadata spreadsheet that shows an example of entering the same value for all the agreements.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Bulk-metadata-editing_populate-data-cells_fill-down_example.gif?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY4MTMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.ffGJ7rxBvf-E9rBKOpx6jZ4yVK-qrfcAaxLkfD0ccs0&_LANG=enus "An animated screenshot of the metadata spreadsheet that shows an example of entering the same value for all the agreements.")

**Edit the Parties column**

Use the Parties column to normalize your party data for the selected agreements.

5. Enter the party data in the Parties column. If you copy and paste the values, be sure to follow the correct format, [Party Name] | [Role]. When adding two or more parties, separate the values with a semicolon:

   ![A snippet of the pre-formatted spreadsheet that highlights a value in the Parties column.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Bulk-metadata-editing_Enter-parties-data_format.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY4MTMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.ffGJ7rxBvf-E9rBKOpx6jZ4yVK-qrfcAaxLkfD0ccs0&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the pre-formatted spreadsheet that highlights a value in the Parties column.")

   Important: The party role is required when populating cells in the Parties column. If you are unsure of the role or do not want to include one, enter "None" as the role. Use the format, [Party Name] | [None].

   For example, Fontara | None.

**Editing dropdown fields**

title: Edit the Metadata Spreadsheet
text:
ties column. If you are unsure of the role or do not want to include one, enter "None" as the role. Use the format, [Party Name] | [None].

   For example, Fontara | None.

**Editing dropdown fields**

When populating dropdown fields, the values must match at least one of the options in the dropdown. For example, the NDA Type field accepts either Mutual or One Way.:![A screenshot of the NDA Type field that has two options, Mutual and One Way.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Bulk-metadata-editing_example-drop-dwon-field_dropdown-options.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY4MTMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.ffGJ7rxBvf-E9rBKOpx6jZ4yVK-qrfcAaxLkfD0ccs0&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the NDA Type field that has two options, Mutual and One Way.")

6. Enter values for the dropdown field in the relevant cells:

   ![An animated screenshot of the pre-formatted spreadsheet that shows an example of populating a dropdown field (column).](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Bulk-metadata-editing_Enter-dropdown-value.gif?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY4MTMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.ffGJ7rxBvf-E9rBKOpx6jZ4yVK-qrfcAaxLkfD0ccs0&_LANG=enus "An animated screenshot of the pre-formatted spreadsheet that shows an example of populating a dropdown field (column).")

   Tip: Verify your values against the dropdown options in Agreement Manager before adding the data to the spreadsheet.

   If the value that you enter does not match any of the options, the field fails to update for that agreement. An error is logged on the Job status page.

**Review and save your changes**

7. Review the spreadsheet for formatting errors or file structure issues. For example, additional rows and columns or missing agreement IDs and mapping values. Update your data as needed.
8. Save and close the spreadsheet.

You have successfully edited the pre-formatted spreadsheet file with new agreement data for the selected documents. Import the spreadsheet back into your account to update the selected documents. Continue to the next step of the process, [Import Your Edited Metadata Spreadsheet](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tsk5ab1cd5b-00f7-42a9-bd77-728baadff903.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to import your edited metadata spreadsheet using the Add (+) menu located on the Agreement Manager homepage.").

title: View the Agreement Hierarchy for a Selected Document
heading_path: View the Agreement Hierarchy for a Selected Document
text:
Learn how to view the related agreements in a hierarchy for a selected document.

The Related agreements pane, located on the Agreement preview page, displays the documents that belong in the same hierarchy as the selected agreement.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Completed Documents view opens.
4. Enter search criteria and apply filters to locate the relevant agreements. For example, expand the Filters option and apply the Parties filter to view agreements associated with specific parties or groups.

   The agreements that match the search criteria and filters are displayed in the table.
5. Select (click) the relevant agreement from the table.

   The Agreement preview page opens for the selected agreement. The Details pane is displayed by default.
6. Select the Related agreements icon from the toolbar on the left side of the page:

   ![A snippet of the Agreement preview page that highlights the Related agreements option in the toolbar on the left.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-Preview-page_Related-agreements_highlighted_pointer.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc4MzgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.yUYPRhF0oOLS_4ZnWtC62gTR19IhwfqXIWKH0laDm88&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Agreement preview page that highlights the Related agreements option in the toolbar on the left.")

   The Related agreements pane opens and the agreements in the hierarchy are displayed. The current document is highlighted in the list.

   ![A screenshot of the Agreement preview page that highlights the Related agreements pane, including the hierarchy for the current document.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-Preview-page_Related-agreements_Fontara-MSA-example.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc4MzgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.yUYPRhF0oOLS_4ZnWtC62gTR19IhwfqXIWKH0laDm88&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Agreement preview page that highlights the Related agreements pane, including the hierarchy for the current document.")
7. View the hierarchy from the party hub pages.
   1. Select the View Hierarchy option.

      The hierarchy details page opens in the party hub for the selected party. The Related Agreements section lists the all the agreements in the hierarchy.
   2. Update the hierarchy as needed. See the [Manage Your Existing Hierarchies](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tskf2e938fc-cb7f-4c65-a7bf-1cf1433722a2.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to maintain your existing hierarchies, including updating the relationship structure between the related agreements.") article to learn more about updating your hierarchies.
   3. Select the Update option at the bottom of the page.

      The hierarchy is saved. The changes are reflected on the hierarchy details page.
   4. Select the browser's Back button.

   You are returned to the Agreement preview page for the selected agreement. The Details pane is displayed.
8. View the other agreements in the hierarchy.
   1. Return to the Related agreements pane. Select the Related agreements icon from the toolbar on the left.

      The Related agreements pane opens.
   2. Select the agreement that you want to view.

      The Agreement preview page opens for the selected agreement. The Details pane is displayed by default.
   3. Review the agreement as needed, including the attributes and field
      values that were surfaced.

title: View the Agreement Hierarchy for a Selected Document
heading_path: View the Agreement Hierarchy for a Selected Document
text:
ement preview page opens for the selected agreement. The Details pane is displayed by default.
   3. Review the agreement as needed, including the attributes and field
      values that were surfaced.

      Tip: Continue reviewing the other agreements in the
      hierarchy. Select the Related agreements icon
      from the toolbar on the left and choose another document in the
      hierarchy. The Agreement preview page opens for
      the selected agreement.
   4. Select the browser's Back button.

   You are returned to the Agreement preview page for the selected agreement. The Details pane is displayed.
9. Select the Close (X) option from the top left.

   You are returned to the Completed Documents page. The agreement list table reflects the search criteria and filters that were applied previously.

You have successfully reviewed the hierarchy for a selected document, including viewing the other agreements in the hierarchy.

title: Combine Filters with an Advanced Query String
heading_path: Combine Filters with an Advanced Query String
text: Learn how to combine filters with an advanced search query to locate relevant documents.

title: Combine Filters with an Advanced Query String
heading_path: Combine Filters with an Advanced Query String
text:
Before completing this task, review the support articles related to filters and advanced search queries, such as:

- [Perform a Search and Apply Filters to Locate Agreements](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=kcf1713200340695.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to combine search methods and filters to locate relevant agreements. This topic answers the question: How do I perform a keyword search and apply filters to find my completed agreements?")
- [Advanced Search Queries](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=conce9d9428-f27e-49ad-9001-3c7a0820ae78.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the advanced search query feature that gives you control over how your results are matched and ranked.")
- [Build an Advanced Search Query](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tskf302fc1f-74d3-40be-8ca2-e9b038981aa9.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to build an advanced search query by combining multiple operators and features in a single query string.")

Your advanced query strings work best with limited complexity. Long queries, with many query operators and rules, are more likely to fail or revert to a basic keyword search. As a rule, apply filters for field values instead of encoding the field logic into the query string.

The filters that you apply decide *which* agreements are eligible for inclusion. Your advanced query decides:

- *How* the agreement text matches the criteria.
- *Which* matches should rank higher in the results.

This article shows you how to combine filters with an advanced query string. This task is completed from the  homepage.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The  homepage opens and the Completed Documents page is displayed.

**Apply filters for field values**

Instead of encoding field logic into the query string, filter your agreements for specific field values. For example, filter your agreements by Parties, Agreement Type, or Status.

4. Apply filters to the agreement list table:
   1. Expand (click) the Filters option and select the relevant filter. For example, apply the Parties filter to view agreements for a specific party.

      The filter expands to show the field values.
   2. Choose the appropriate field values from the filter. For example, search for and select all the relevant party names, including variants.
   3. Select the Apply option to apply the selected filter values to your agreement list.
   4. Repeat this step until all the relevant filters are applied.

   The agreement list table updates and the agreements that matched the applied filters are listed in the table.

**Build the advanced query string**

Use a query string to capture search requirements beyond the basic keywords and filters. For example, add requirements to locate specific clause language, capture word variations, find related-terms, and boost relevant terms.

5. Locate agreements that contain specific clause language:
   1. Enter the quoted clause terms in the search field. For example, enter *"liability obligations"* as examples of terms found in force majeure clauses.

      Tip: Choose terms that are typical to the clause, which are
      not next to each other. The presence of these terms indicates the
      existence of the specific clause language.
   2. Add a tilde (~) and proximity number after the quoted terms. For example, update the query string to: *"liability obligations"~5*.
   3. Press the Enter key.

title: Combine Filters with an Advanced Query String
heading_path: Combine Filters with an Advanced Query String
text:
nce of the specific clause language.
   2. Add a tilde (~) and proximity number after the quoted terms. For example, update the query string to: *"liability obligations"~5*.
   3. Press the Enter key.

   The query string is applied to your agreement list table. The agreements that contained the clause terms are displayed in the table.
6. Continue building the query string until the relevant results are reflected in the table. See the [Build an Advanced Search Query](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tskf302fc1f-74d3-40be-8ca2-e9b038981aa9.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to build an advanced search query by combining multiple operators and features in a single query string.") article to learn more about building query strings.

**Save your query string and filters**

Use the Save option to save your query string, including applied filters, for future use. Once saved, run your search at any time to retrieve an updated list of documents that match the query string.

See the [Use Saved Searches to Find Agreement Data](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tskca2d74f2-823d-403d-84a6-d990537cd187.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to access and run a saved search, including steps to edit and delete searches.") article to learn more about running your saved searches.

7. Save your query string for future use:
   1. Select the Save option located to the right of the filters.

      The New saved search dialog box opens. Complete the required fields to save your search terms.
   2. Enter a unique name for the search in the Name field. A system-generated name defaults automatically. Overwrite this value as needed.
   3. Enter a Description of the search in the text-entry box, including its purpose and use.
   4. Select the Save Search option.

   The dialog box closes. Your search is available in the Saved Searches list. The page title and the breadcrumb navigation are updated to reflect the name of the saved search.
8. Return to the Agreement Manager homepage. Select the Completed Documents option from the breadcrumb navigation in the top left.

   You are returned to the Agreement Manager homepage and the Completed Documents page is displayed.

You have successfully applied filters and entered an advanced query string to locate the relevant agreements and metadata.

title: View and Download an Overview Report
heading_path: View and Download an Overview Report
text:
Learn how to view reports from the Overview dashboard, including accessing and downloading the detailed report view.

The Overview dashboard is the landing page for the reporting hub. It
is updated in actual time to provide personalized, actionable insights at a glance.
There are several ways to access the detailed report view for a selected report.

Note: **Downloading reports to CSV files**

When downloading reports to CSV files from the Insights reporting hub, there is an agreement limit of 25,000 agreements for all IAM reports. This count includes your custom reports.

**Suggested workaround:** Adjust the report filters to run the report in batches until all the relevant agreements are downloaded. For example, run the report in batches according to varying date ranges. Once downloaded, combine the data into one CSV file.

This
topic focuses on the ways to access the detailed report view directly from the
Overview dashboard, as opposed to going to another
dashboard first.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Insights from the global header.

   The Insights hub opens and the Overview dashboard is displayed.

   ![A screenshot of the Insights hub that displays the Overview dashboard.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Insights-reporting-hub_Overview_cropped.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY4OTAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.AGqimN929p19iN12pNihvXz8pIZppOF2hGD8KEgn7K4&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Insights hub that displays the Overview dashboard.")
3. Locate and select the report that you want to view. There are a number of ways to find the report that you want to view and download.
   - Use the search bar at the top of the page to search for the report you want to view. Enter a search term in the Find reports or dashboards field and select the relevant report from the dropdown.
   - Select the report from the Your Recents section. Your five most recently viewed reports are listed. Select the View all option to view an expanded list of your recently viewed reports.
   - Select the report from the Your Favorites section. Your "starred" dashboards and reports are displayed in this section. Select the View all option to view all of your favorite dashboards and reports.

     Tip: Be sure to "star" your frequently used dashboards and reports so they are added to the Your Favorites list.
   - Select a report name from the Suggested Reports (Beta) section.
   - Select an agreement insight from the Weekly Insights section.
   - Select the report from the Review your agreements section.
     - Select (click) the report name or select View Report from the More actions (three dot) menu.
     - Select (click) a segment in the bar or pie chart to open the detailed report view for the chart.
       The report is filtered to show the agreements that are
       associated with the selected segment.

   The detailed report view opens for the selected report.

   ![A screenshot of the detailed report view for a selected Overview dashboard report.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Overview_view-detailed-report_example.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY4OTAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.AGqimN929p19iN12pNihvXz8pIZppOF2hGD8KEgn7K4&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the detailed report view for a selected Overview dashboard report.")

title: View and Download an Overview Report
heading_path: View and Download an Overview Report
text:
CJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY4OTAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.AGqimN929p19iN12pNihvXz8pIZppOF2hGD8KEgn7K4&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the detailed report view for a selected Overview dashboard report.")

   Note: The dashboard charts can be viewed as regular bar charts or as stacked bar charts. Expand the More actions (three dots) menu and select the View as Stacked Bar Chart option:![A screenshot of the More actions (three dots) menu for a dashboard chart.that highlights the View as Stacked Bar Chart option.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Dashboard-Reports_Dashboard-charts_View-as-stacked-bar-chart_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY4OTAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.AGqimN929p19iN12pNihvXz8pIZppOF2hGD8KEgn7K4&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the More actions (three dots) menu for a dashboard chart.that highlights the View as Stacked Bar Chart option.")
4. Verify that the report contains the relevant agreements and data. Use the filters to refine the agreement list as needed:

   ![A snippet of a detailed report view that highlights the available filters, including the All Filters option.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Insights-reporting-hub_Overview_Detailed-report-view_Filters.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY4OTAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.AGqimN929p19iN12pNihvXz8pIZppOF2hGD8KEgn7K4&_LANG=enus "A snippet of a detailed report view that highlights the available filters, including the All Filters option.")

   Tip: Filter options may vary from report to report.

   1. Apply the default filters that are displayed at the top of the report. Expand a filter, choose a value, and select the Apply option.
   2. Use the All Filters option to apply additional filters to the table. Select the All Filters option and apply the relevant filters.

      - Expand the filter, choose a value or enter a date, and select the Apply option. For example, to filter the report by agreement status, expand the Status filter and choose the applicable statuses.
      - Continue applying filters as needed.
      - Save the applied filters before closing the dialog box. Select the Save option.

      The All Filters dialog box closes and the report view is updated to reflect the applied filters.
5. Customize the columns in the table.

   Important: The Show or Hide Fields function only applies to the data that is displayed in the agreement list table. When downloading or exporting the report, all fields are included in the output file. This includes both custom and standard fields.

   ![A screenshot of the Show or Hide Fields dialog box that shows the columns you can choose to display in the agreements table.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Party-hub_Overview-dashboard_Customize-columns.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY4OTAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.AGqimN929p19iN12pNihvXz8pIZppOF2hGD8KEgn7K4&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Show or Hide Fields dialog box that shows the columns you can choose to display in the agreements table.")

   1. Select the Show or Hide Fields (toggle) icon.

      The Show or Hide Fields dialog box opens. These fields represent the columns in the table.
   2. Select the toggles to turn the associated fields on (blue) or off (white).
   3. Re-order the columns as needed. Hover the cursor over the
      Reorder (dots) icon for the column that you
      want to move and drag it to the appropriate location.
   4. Select the Save option.

title: View and Download an Overview Report
heading_path: View and Download an Overview Report
text:
3. Re-order the columns as needed. Hover the cursor over the
      Reorder (dots) icon for the column that you
      want to move and drag it to the appropriate location.
   4. Select the Save option.

   Tip: If the Status column is not displayed by default, use the Show or Hide Fields (toggle) option to add it to the table. From the Show or Hide Field dialog box, turn on the Status toggle and drag the field to the appropriate location. Be sure to Save your changes.

   The dialog box closes and the table is updated based on the selected customizations.
6. Download or save the report.

   ![A snippet of the report view that highlights the Report Actions (vertical ellipsis) menu used to download or save the report.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Report-view_Dropdown-expanded.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY4OTAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.AGqimN929p19iN12pNihvXz8pIZppOF2hGD8KEgn7K4&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the report view that highlights the Report Actions (vertical ellipsis) menu used to download or save the report.")

   Tip: The Save As option is not available for all reports.

   - Expand the More actions (three dots) menu in the top right and select the Download option.

     The report is downloaded to your local Downloads folder.
   - Expand the dropdown in the top right and select Save As. The Save Report dialog box opens

     - Enter a unique name in the Report name field. The field defaults to the name of the selected report.
     - Enter a description about the report's purpose in the Description field.
     - Select the Save option.The report is saved to the Custom tab on the Reports view.

     .
7. Set up one-time and recurring email reports for yourself and others. Select the Manage Notification (bell) icon and complete the Create subscription dialog box:

   ![A screenshot of the Create subscription dialog box that is used to subscribe to report notifications.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Overview_View-detailed-report_Create-subscription.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY4OTAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.AGqimN929p19iN12pNihvXz8pIZppOF2hGD8KEgn7K4&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Create subscription dialog box that is used to subscribe to report notifications.")

   Note: When you create a subscription, you have the option to add individual users and groups. Once created, the subscription is displayed in the Manage report subscriptions dialog box for any individual users who were added. Currently, the subscription is not visible to users if they were added as part of a group.

   See the [Subscribe to Receive Report Notifications](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=wfw1734545937270.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to set up one-time or recurring email reports for yourself or others to receive notifications about your obligations, renewals, or agreements.") topic to learn more about this feature.
8. Add the report to the Your Favorites list. Select the
   Favorite (star) icon located in the top right:

title: View and Download an Overview Report
heading_path: View and Download an Overview Report
text:
ns about your obligations, renewals, or agreements.") topic to learn more about this feature.
8. Add the report to the Your Favorites list. Select the
   Favorite (star) icon located in the top right:

   ![A snippet of a detailed report view that highlights the Favorite (star) icon.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreements-dashboard_Report-details_Favorite-icon-selected_generic.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY4OTAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.AGqimN929p19iN12pNihvXz8pIZppOF2hGD8KEgn7K4&_LANG=enus "A snippet of a detailed report view that highlights the Favorite (star) icon.")Your starred dashboards and reports are displayed in the Your
   Favorites section on the Overview
   dashboard.
9. Return to the Overview dashboard. Select Overview from the breadcrumb navigation at the top of the page:

   ![A snippet of the detailed report view that highlights the breadcrumb navigation.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Overview_View-detailed-report_Return-to-Overview_breadcrumb.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY4OTAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.AGqimN929p19iN12pNihvXz8pIZppOF2hGD8KEgn7K4&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the detailed report view that highlights the breadcrumb navigation.")

   You are returned to the Overview dashboard.
10. Repeat this process to view, download, or save other Overview dashboard reports.

You have successfully reviewed, customized, and downloaded an Overview
dashboard report.

title: Delete the AI Setup for Custom Extractions
heading_path: Delete the AI Setup for Custom Extractions
text: Learn how to delete the AI detection setup for a custom extraction.

title: Delete the AI Setup for Custom Extractions
heading_path: Delete the AI Setup for Custom Extractions
text:
Before you can delete the AI setup, the custom extraction must be activated for AI detection. See the [Custom Extractions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=yrx1747083411531.html&_LANG=enus "Read about custom extractions, including how these data points bring value to our organization.") article to learn more about creating custom types.

Use the Delete AI Setup option to delete the detection for custom extractions. For example, if the extraction is no longer relevant.

Remember: Once deleted, you can reset the AI detection at any time. See the [Refine the Detection for Custom Extractions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tskc13b6287-31ac-4687-8b35-b170680f50e0.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to refine the AI detection results for your custom extractions, including fine-tuning the definition and selecting new samples.") article for steps to complete this task.

This task is completed by users with Organize and manage all agreements access to Agreement Manager agreements and functionality. For example, administrators and contract managers.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed.
4. Go to the applicable component manager. Expand the Settings (gear) dropdown from the top right and select the relevant location, Fields or Agreement Types.

   ![A screenshot of the Settings dropdown that highlights the Fields and Agreement Types options.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Manage-Agreement-Types_More-actions-menu_expanded.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc3NTMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.9BWL2uf1bS8MMaYr6dQ7UEemduSgfgJJ7S-0_w5BHHg&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Settings dropdown that highlights the Fields and Agreement Types options.")

   Depending on your selection, either the Manage Agreement Types page or the Manage FIelds page opens.

**Find the applicable custom extraction**

5. Locate the extraction that you want to update.

   Tip: The Delete AI Setup function is not available for the standard, Docusign-provided extractions. Apply the Created By filter to view User-created extractions.

   - Search for the extraction that you want to update. Enter a keyword in the search field and select a suggested search from the dropdown or press the Enter key.
   - Apply filters to locate the relevant extraction. Expand the filter, choose a filter value, and select the Apply option.

   The extractions that matched the search term and applied filters are displayed in the table.

**Delete the AI setup**

6. Expand the More actions (three dots) menu for the applicable extraction and select the Delete AI Setup option.

   The Delete Detection dialog box opens. You are asked to confirm the deletion of the AI setup.
7. Select the Delete option.

   The dialog box closes and the custom extraction is no longer activated for AI detection.

   Note: The custom extraction is still visible in the component table. The Set Up option is displayed in the AI detection column for the custom extraction.
8. Return to the Agreement Manager homepage. Select the Close (X) icon located in the top right of the page.

   You are returned to the Completed Documents view.

You have successfully deleted the AI setup for a custom extraction.

title: Find Custom Agreement Types in Specific Documents
heading_path: Find Custom Agreement Types in Specific Documents
text: Learn how to use the Find On Specific Documents function to surface custom agreement types in selected agreements.

title: Find Custom Agreement Types in Specific Documents
heading_path: Find Custom Agreement Types in Specific Documents
text:
Once activated, the custom extraction is surfaced in your new agreements by default. The data points are included in your agreement insights, such as filters, searches, dashboards, and reports.

Administrators and contract managers can also process specific historical document for the new agreement type.

Remember: Administrators automatically have access to configure agreement data. For non-administrator users, agreement type management tasks require Organize and manage all agreements access to Agreement Manager agreements and functionality.

Be sure to complete these topics before attempting this task:

- [Configure New Custom Agreement Types](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=gke1727282238086.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to create custom agreement types to support organizational use cases.")
- [Define Your Custom Agreement Types](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tske5fe1ad5-2832-4e6c-9177-f33a9ffade15.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to initiate the AI detection set-up process and complete the first step to define the custom type.")
- [Add Examples of Your Custom Agreement Types](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tskd38465c6-c8c0-4588-bfb3-fdb6916a1926.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to complete the second step of the AI detection set-up process, Add examples of this agreement type.")
- [Test the Extraction Accuracy for Your Custom Agreement Types](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=ogw1740503179389.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to test the accuracy of the AI detection for your custom agreement types.")
- [Activate Your Custom Agreement Types](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=cgn1740452596841.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to activate the AI detection for a custom agreement type and surface it in selected historical agreements.")

The Find On Specific Documents function is used to reprocess
selected documents for custom extractions, such as custom types. If the
custom type is located in the document, the associated attributes (fields)
are surfaced.

Important: The selected documents are reprocessed
*only* for that specific custom agreement type.
If the type is not found in the document, there is no
further processing.

This task is completed from the *Agreement Types Manager*.
Access this feature from the  homepage under the Settings
menu.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed.
4. Expand the Settings (gear) menu from the
   top right and select the Agreement Types
   option:

   ![A snippet of the Agreement Manager homepage that highlights the Agreement Types option in the Settings (gear) menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-types-manager_Settings-menu_Agreement-Types_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2MjgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.iiZshJRf0s9EvzpItoxT4gg4BNkgo07aVtwtKwY5LJE&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Agreement Manager homepage that highlights the Agreement Types option in the Settings (gear) menu.")

   The Manage Agreement Types page opens. A list of the account's standard and custom agreement types is displayed in the Agreement Types table.
5. Locate the custom agreement type that you want to find in specific documents.

title: Find Custom Agreement Types in Specific Documents
heading_path: Find Custom Agreement Types in Specific Documents
text:
ypes page opens. A list of the account's standard and custom agreement types is displayed in the Agreement Types table.
5. Locate the custom agreement type that you want to find in specific documents.

   ![A snippet of the Agreement Types view that highlights the search field and applied filters to locate active custom types.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-Types-Manager_Find-on-specific-fields_locate-active-custom-type.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2MjgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.iiZshJRf0s9EvzpItoxT4gg4BNkgo07aVtwtKwY5LJE&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Agreement Types view that highlights the search field and applied filters to locate active custom types.")

   - Search for the relevant custom agreement type. Enter a keyword in the Search types... field. The table updates to show agreement types that match the entered search term.
   - Apply filters to locate the relevant custom agreement type. For example, apply the Created By (User) and AI Status (Active) filters to view eligible agreement types. Expand a filter, choose the relevant value, and select the Apply option.

     Restriction: The Find On Specific Documents option is available only for custom, user-defined agreement types with an activated AI detection. Use the Created By and AI Status filters to view all the active custom agreement types for the account.

     The table updates and the agreement types that match the applied filters are displayed.
6. Expand the More actions (three dots) menu for the relevant agreement type and select the Find On Specific Documents option:

   ![A snippet of the Agreement Types table that shows the Find On Specific Documents option in the More actions (three dots) menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-Types-Manager_More-actions_Find-On-Specific-Documents_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2MjgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.iiZshJRf0s9EvzpItoxT4gg4BNkgo07aVtwtKwY5LJE&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Agreement Types table that shows the Find On Specific Documents option in the More actions (three dots) menu.")

   The Select documents to process page opens and a list of agreements is displayed. You are prompted to select at least one document to process for the agreement type.
7. Choose the documents that you want to process for the custom type:

   ![A screenshot of the Select documents to process page that shows selected agreements to process for the new type.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Custom-agreement-type_Select-docs-to-process_Start-Processing.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2MjgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.iiZshJRf0s9EvzpItoxT4gg4BNkgo07aVtwtKwY5LJE&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Select documents to process page that shows selected agreements to process for the new type.")

   1. Search for the relevant agreements. Enter a keyword in the search field and select a search suggestion from the dropdown or press the Enter key.

      The table updates and the agreements that matched the search term are displayed.
   2. Refine the results list with filters. Expand a filter, choose the relevant values, and select the Apply option.

      The table updates and the agreements that matched the applied filters are displayed.
   3. Select the documents that you want to process.

      A counter indicates the number of selected documents.

      Tip: Use the Show Selected Agreements option to view your selected documents.
8. Select the Start Processing option in the lower right.

title: Find Custom Agreement Types in Specific Documents
heading_path: Find Custom Agreement Types in Specific Documents
text:
A counter indicates the number of selected documents.

      Tip: Use the Show Selected Agreements option to view your selected documents.
8. Select the Start Processing option in the lower right.

   The Agreement Types view opens. A message box opens and indicates that the selected documents are being checked for the custom type.

   ![A screenshot of the confirmation message that states the documents are being checked for the custom type.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-Types-Manager_Find-on-specific-fields_confirmation-message_view-progress.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2MjgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.iiZshJRf0s9EvzpItoxT4gg4BNkgo07aVtwtKwY5LJE&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the confirmation message that states the documents are being checked for the custom type.")
9. Review the processing status. Select the View Progress link from the message box.

   Note:

   **Navigate directly to the job details.**

   You can access the processing status and
   details for any of your processing jobs from the job
   monitoring hub. Access this feature from the
   Agreement Manager homepage. Expand the Settings (gear) menu and select the
   Uploads and Processing
   Status option.

   The job monitoring
   hub opens and the All jobs
   view is displayed. Go to the
   Processing tab and select
   the View details option for
   the job that you want to review:

   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-Types-Manager_Find-on-specific-fields_navigate-to-job-details-manually.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2MjgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.iiZshJRf0s9EvzpItoxT4gg4BNkgo07aVtwtKwY5LJE&_LANG=enus)

   The job monitoring hub opens, and the Processing status and Job details sections are displayed.

   ![A screenshot of the Processing status and Job details for the job that is checking the selected agreements.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-Types-Manager_Find-on-specific-docs_Processing-results.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2MjgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.iiZshJRf0s9EvzpItoxT4gg4BNkgo07aVtwtKwY5LJE&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Processing status and Job details for the job that is checking the selected agreements.")
10. Review the processing job results, including any error messages.

    Tip: Select the View File option to view the document from the Agreement preview page. If the custom type is found in any of the documents, they are reprocessed to surface the relevant attributes and values. Once processed, the Details pane reflects the data points that are associated with the custom type.
11. Return to the Agreement Types view. Select the Close (x) icon located in the top right.

    You are returned to the Agreement Types view.
12. Return to the Agreement Manager home page. Select the Close (x) icon located in the top right.

    The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed.

    If the custom type was identified in any of the selected documents, the associated attributes were surfaced. When you apply filters that are related to the custom type, the documents are included in the results.

title: Find Custom Agreement Types in Specific Documents
heading_path: Find Custom Agreement Types in Specific Documents
text:
om type was identified in any of the selected documents, the associated attributes were surfaced. When you apply filters that are related to the custom type, the documents are included in the results.

    Note: Reprocess your documents for standard and custom
    data on-demand from the agreement list table:![A snippet of the agreement list table that highlights the Custom Data option in the Update dropdown.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Ondemand-reprocessing_Update-dropdown_Custom-data_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2MjgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.iiZshJRf0s9EvzpItoxT4gg4BNkgo07aVtwtKwY5LJE&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the agreement list table that highlights the Custom Data option in the Update dropdown.")See the [Refresh Agreements for New Data Points](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=fog1750877660436.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to reprocess a group of selected agreements to surface new data points. For example, to surface custom agreement types or fields within your agreements.")
    topic to learn how to reprocess selected documents
    for custom data.

You have successfully processed a group of documents for a custom agreement type. If the
type is identified in any of the selected documents, the relevant attributes
(fields) are surfaced.

title: Remove Documents from the Agreement List Table
heading_path: Remove Documents from the Agreement List Table
text:
Learn how to remove documents from the agreement list table on the Completed Documents page. This topic answers the question: How do I delete agreements from Agreement Manager?

The Remove function provides a quick way to delete unnecessary documents from the agreement list table.

Note: **Permission requirements**

The Remove function requires users to have at least one of these permissions:

- Organize and manage all agreements access to *Agreement Manager*
- Can Manage access to the *agreement*

See the [Agreement Manager Permissions, Profiles, and Roles](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=eut1712157553977.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the available Agreement Manager permissions, including how users and groups are granted access to Agreement Manager agreements and example roles for each permission.")ll support topic to learn more about Agreement Manager permissions and document-level access control.

The Remove option is available at the document-level and for a group of selected documents. Both entry points are accessed from the Agreement Manager homepage. This task reviews both entry points.

Once deleted, users and administrators no longer have access to the agreement and its data. This action does not remove the agreement from its original envelope or void the contract.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed.

**Locate the relevant agreements**

4. Perform a search and apply filters to locate the agreements that you want to remove:
   1. Enter a keyword or string in the search field and select a suggestion from the dropdown or press the Enter key.

      The agreements containing the keyword or search string that you entered are displayed in the table.
   2. Use the filters to refine the list of search results. For example, use the Parties filter to view a list of agreements for a specific party.

      1. Expand the Filters option and select the filter that you want to apply.
      2. Choose a filter value.
      3. Select the Apply option.

      The agreements that match the applied filters are displayed in the table.

**Delete a single document**

5. Remove a single document from the agreement list:

   ![A snippet of the agreement list table that highlights the Remove from Account option in the More actions (three dots) menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-list-table_Remove-option_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY1MTQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.a6YqD8IDCmHhYVUgOVqbfy0XggMswhsPAoZhkZ8q3Cg&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the agreement list table that highlights the Remove from Account option in the More actions (three dots) menu.")

   1. Locate the agreement that you want to remove and expand the corresponding More actions (three dots) menu.
   2. Select the Remove from Account option:

   The Permanently remove this agreement? dialog box opens.
6. Review the message and select the Remove Agreement option.

   The agreement is removed from the repository and its data is no longer available in Agreement Manager.

   Note: Removing the agreement from Agreement Manager does not remove it from its original envelope or void the contract.

   You are returned to the Agreement Manager homepage. Your search criteria and filters are still applied to the table.

**Remove a group of documents**

7. Select the agreements that you want to remove from the account.
   - Select the individual agreements.
   - Select the Choose all checkbox from the header row to select all the agreements on that page of results.

title: Remove Documents from the Agreement List Table
heading_path: Remove Documents from the Agreement List Table
text:
greements that you want to remove from the account.
   - Select the individual agreements.
   - Select the Choose all checkbox from the header row to select all the agreements on that page of results.

     Tip: The Choose all option only selects agreements on the current page of search results. It does not select any agreements on subsequent pages of results.
   - Expand the Selected dropdown and select the Select all that match your filters option. All the agreements in the table that matched your applied filters are selected.

     Tip: The Select all that match your filters option is only available if filters are applied to the agreement list table.

   When you select at least one document, an actions bar opens and the Remove (trash) icon is available.
8. Select the Remove (trash) icon from the actions bar:

   ![A snippet of the agreement list table that shows a group of selected documents and the Remove (trash) icon.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-list-table_Actions-bar_Remove-option_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY1MTQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.a6YqD8IDCmHhYVUgOVqbfy0XggMswhsPAoZhkZ8q3Cg&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the agreement list table that shows a group of selected documents and the Remove (trash) icon.")

   The Remove documents from this account? dialog box opens.
9. Review the message and select the Remove from Account option.

   The agreements are removed from the repository and the associated data is no longer available in Agreement Manager.

   Note: Removing agreements from Agreement Manager does not remove them from the original envelope or void the contract.

   You are returned to the Agreement Manager

You have successfully removed documents from the Agreement Manager repository.

title: Refine the Detection for Custom Extractions
heading_path: Refine the Detection for Custom Extractions
text: Learn how to refine the AI detection results for your custom extractions, including fine-tuning the definition and selecting new samples.

title: Refine the Detection for Custom Extractions
heading_path: Refine the Detection for Custom Extractions
text:
The Refine Results function is available to custom extractions with a configured AI setup. See the [Custom Extractions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=yrx1747083411531.html&_LANG=enus "Read about custom extractions, including how these data points bring value to our organization.") article to learn more about creating custom extractions.

Note: Administrators automatically have access to configure agreement data. For non-administrator users, this feature requires Organize and manage all agreements access to Agreement Manager. These tasks are primarily completed by administrators and contract managers.

During setup, the 'set definition' and 'example selection' steps are designed to "teach" the system. These steps enable the AI detection to "learn" by example. If you are not achieving the expected results, use the Refine Results option to adjust the AI setup.

Restriction: The Refine with AI option is only available for English (U.S.) and English (global) agreements. This function is used to fine-tune the definition for your custom extractions.

The Refine Results option is available for all custom extractions with a previously configured AI setup.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed.
4. Go to the applicable component manager. Expand the Settings (gear) dropdown from the top right and select the relevant location, Fields or Agreement Types.

   ![A screenshot of the Settings dropdown that highlights the Fields and Agreement Types options.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Manage-Agreement-Types_More-actions-menu_expanded.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc3MzksInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.y0hmATrDwtQ_INeM9I-BZ6PtYHAfy_XMCxldoZdGUPE&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Settings dropdown that highlights the Fields and Agreement Types options.")

   Depending on your selection, either the Manage Agreement Types page or the Manage FIelds page opens.

**Find the applicable custom extraction**

5. Locate the extraction that you want to update.

   Tip: The Refine Results function is not available for the standard, Docusign-provided extractions. Apply the Created By filter to view User-created extractions.

   - Search for the extraction that you want to update. Enter a keyword in the search field and select a suggested search from the dropdown or press the Enter key.
   - Apply filters to locate the relevant extraction. Expand the filter, choose a filter value,
     and select the Apply option.

   The extractions that matched the search term and applied filters are displayed in the table.
6. Expand the More actions (three dots) menu for the applicable extraction and select the Refine Results option.

   A dialog box opens. You are warned that refining the result will deactivate the custom extraction. After retesting the updated setup, reactivate the extraction.
7. Select the Confirm option.

   The dialog box closes and the custom extraction is deactivated. The Set definition dialog box opens.

**Update the definition**

8. Update the definition for the custom extraction.

   Note: See the [Best Practice: Writing Definitions for AI Detection](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tmk1741317734856.html&_LANG=enus "Read about tips for writing good definitions for the AI detection setup process. These definitions are an important part of the process and help to \"teach\" the AI detection.") article to review the
   guidelines for writing definitions. Follow these guidelines to get
   the most out of the AI detection and to ensure accuracy.

title: Refine the Detection for Custom Extractions
heading_path: Refine the Detection for Custom Extractions
text:
rocess and help to \"teach\" the AI detection.") article to review the
   guidelines for writing definitions. Follow these guidelines to get
   the most out of the AI detection and to ensure accuracy.

   1. Enter a detailed description that explains the purpose of the extraction. This field is limited to 2,000 characters.

      When the definition exceeds 50 characters, the Refine with AI option becomes available. Use this option to update the definition for consistency and accuracy.
   2. Fine-tune the definition. Select the Refine with AI option. The dialog box refreshes and a suggested definition is displayed.
   3. Confirm the definition. Review and edit the suggested definition as needed.

      Tip: Use the Undo option to revert to the original version of the definition.
   4. Select the Next option.

   Note: Depending on the extraction being updated, you can be prompted to enter additional details. The descriptions help the AI model to identify the data points in your documents.

   In addition to a text definition, you are prompted to provide examples of the extraction. The definition explains what the data means and the examples show how the data displays in your agreements.

**Add examples for context**

Important:

When you provide example field values, include specific phrases or paragraphs so the AI
models can detect the data in a contextual manner. If you provide a single word, the model is unable
to detect the data due to lack of context.

9. Choose your preferred method for providing examples and select the Next option.
   - Select the Paste examples from your documents option to copy and paste your examples.
   - Select the Highlight examples directly in documents option to select your examples from existing documents.

   Depending on your selection, you are prompted to either paste examples of the extraction or select the relevant documents.
10. Provide examples of the extraction.
    - Paste examples of the extraction: For each example, add a paragraph of text that contains the extraction, then type out the value and confirm it. Select the Save option after adding your examples.
    - Highlight examples directly in documents: Search for and select the relevant documents. For each document, highlight a paragraph of text that contains the extraction, then type out the value and confirm it. Select the Next option after adding your examples.

      Your examples are transferred to the Paste examples of the field dialog box. Review your examples and select the Save option.

    A dialog box opens and a message confirms that the AI detection is now available for the extraction.

**Test for accuracy**

Before activating the AI detection, retest the setup to confirm its accuracy. The results are a good indicator of the quality and accuracy that you can expect from the AI detection before activating it.

11. Choose the Test for accuracy option and select the Confirm option. This option is selected by default.

    The Select relevant documents page opens. You are prompted to select documents that are likely to contain the extraction.

    Note: Include at least one document that does not contain the extraction. For example, a document with a similar data point to determine if the AI model detects the difference.
12. Test the AI detection against your selected documents:
    1. Locate the relevant documents. Perform a keyword search and apply filters to locate documents that are likely to contain the extraction.
    2. Select the documents that you want to include in the test.
    3. Select the Start Testing option in the bottom right.

title: Refine the Detection for Custom Extractions
heading_path: Refine the Detection for Custom Extractions
text:
ply filters to locate documents that are likely to contain the extraction.
    2. Select the documents that you want to include in the test.
    3. Select the Start Testing option in the bottom right.

    The Agreement preview opens for the first document. The pane on the left shows the extraction that the AI detection identified in the document.
13. Rate the accuracy of the findings.
    1. Review the identified extraction in the context of the document. Verify that the AI detection surfaced the correct value. Select Yes if the AI detection was correct or No if the extraction was not correctly identified.

       Note: If the extraction was not found in a selected document, a message is displayed. Be sure to include agreements that do not contain the extraction when testing the accuracy. It's important for the AI model to recognize when the extraction is not present.

       The Agreement preview page updates and the next document is displayed.
    2. Repeat this step until you are finished verifying the identified extractions in all the selected documents.
    3. Select the Done option.

    The AI detection test results are displayed. The target score is 80%. Use the Refine definition and examples option to improve the accuracy score.

**Activate the AI detection**

After achieving the target accuracy score, activate the AI detection for the custom extraction.

14. Choose the Activate AI detection option and select Confirm.

    The Activate AI detection dialog box opens. Use this dialog box to choose when the system should look for the custom extraction. The All new agreements option is selected by default. You cannot deselect this option.
15. Choose when to look for the custom extraction and select the Activate option.

    Note: The Find on specific agreements option can be used at any time to process your historical agreements for custom data points. See these articles to learn more about this function:
    - [Find Custom Agreement Types in Specific Documents](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tskb991328c-dc5f-4d45-9617-dada91a5672a.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to use the Find On Specific Documents function to surface custom agreement types in selected agreements.")
    - [Find Custom Fields in Specific Documents](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tsk79824e36-d51d-4477-a5ca-7228cde30fb2.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to use the Find On Specific Documents function to surface custom fields in selected agreements.")

    In this example, activate the AI detection without processing historical agreements.

    The dialog box closes and the extraction is published. You are returned to the previous page,
    the AI detection is activated for the extraction.
16. Return to the Agreement Manager homepage. Select the Close (X)
    icon located in the top right of the page.

    You are returned to the Agreement Manager homepage and the Completed Documents
    view is displayed.

You have successfully refined the AI detection results for a custom extraction.

title: Save Search Criteria for Future Use
heading_path: Save Search Criteria for Future Use
text:
Learn how to save the search criteria for the queries that you frequently perform.

Save your search criteria, including keywords, filters, and custom columns, to find documents and
view relevant agreement data (columns) more
quickly and easily.

Important: The Saved Search feature is available at the user level. All account users have permission to save their frequently used search queries.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed. The documents to which the logged-in user has access are listed in the table. The total document count is displayed the bottom right. This count is updated when you perform a search or apply filters.

   ![A screenshot of the Agreement Manager homepage that shows a list of documents to which the logged-in user has access.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-view_updated-with-document-count.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY0NDcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.cQatBL6EUZzenA_Rvi-XtE4XuI913HmmGcotiyoApQo&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Agreement Manager homepage that shows a list of documents to which the logged-in user has access.")
4. Perform a keyword search to locate the relevant agreements.
   1. Enter a keyword or phrase in the search field.

      Tip: When you click in the search field, a dropdown opens. Your search history and recent documents are displayed. You can also access your saved searches from the dropdown.
   2. Select a suggestion from the dropdown or press the Enter key.

   The agreements that contain the keyword or phrase are displayed in the table.
5. Apply filters to refine the agreement list table.
   1. Select the Filters option.
   2. Locate the filter that you want to apply.

      - Search for the filter. Enter a keyword in the Filter by field and select a value from the results list.
      - Select a filter from the list of default filters, including: Sets, Status, Parties, Agreement Type, and AI Suggestions.
      - Use the All Filters option to locate the relevant filter from a list of all available filters.
   3. Choose the relevant filter values or enter a date range.
   4. Select the Apply option.

      Tip: A number displays next to filter values to indicate the number of agreements that match the corresponding value.

   The table updates and agreements that match the applied filters are displayed.
6. Customize the agreement list table, as needed.
   1. Select the Show or Hide Fields (toggle) icon located to the right of the column headings.

      The Show or Hide Fields dialog box opens. A number of fields are turned on
      (blue) by default. These fields represent the columns in the agreement list
      table. The field order in the dialog box reflects the column order in the
      table.
   2. Select the toggles to turn the corresponding field on (blue) or off (white).
   3. Re-order the columns as needed. Hover the cursor over the Reorder (dots) icon for the field that you want to move and drag it to the appropriate location.
   4. Select the Save option.

   The dialog box closes and the new configuration is reflected in the agreement list table. Continue refining the search criteria until it achieves the expected outcome.
7. Select the Save option located to the right of the filters.

   The New saved search dialog box opens.
8. Complete the fields in the dialog box and select the Save Search option:

title: Save Search Criteria for Future Use
heading_path: Save Search Criteria for Future Use
text:
expected outcome.
7. Select the Save option located to the right of the filters.

   The New saved search dialog box opens.
8. Complete the fields in the dialog box and select the Save Search option:

   ![A screenshot of the New saved search dialog box that is used to save your search criteria.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Saved-searches_New-saved-search_generic.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY0NDcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.cQatBL6EUZzenA_Rvi-XtE4XuI913HmmGcotiyoApQo&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the New saved search dialog box that is used to save your search criteria.")

   1. Enter a unique name for the search in the Name field or accept the default value. The Name field is generated based on the applied filters and the time stamp.
   2. Enter a brief description for the search in the Description field.
   3. Select the Save Search option.

   The dialog box closes. Your search is available in the Saved Searches
   list. The page title and the breadcrumb navigation
   are updated to reflect the name of the saved
   search. The description is also displayed.

   ![A screenshot of a saved search that lists the agreements that matched the search criteria.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Saved-searches_search-results_force-majeure-cobranding-ex.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY0NDcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.cQatBL6EUZzenA_Rvi-XtE4XuI913HmmGcotiyoApQo&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of a saved search that lists the agreements that matched the search criteria.")

   Tip: Use the More actions (three dots) menu to:![A snippet of a saved search that highlights the More saved search actions dropdown.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Saved-searches_More-actions_force-majeure-cobranding-ex.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY0NDcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.cQatBL6EUZzenA_Rvi-XtE4XuI913HmmGcotiyoApQo&_LANG=enus "A snippet of a saved search that highlights the More saved search actions dropdown.")
   - Edit the name and description of the search
   - Delete the saved search
   - View all your saved searches
9. Return to the Agreement Manager homepage. Select the Back to Completed option.

   You are returned to the Agreement Manager homepage. The Completed Documents view is displayed.

You have successfully saved your search criteria for future use.

title: Use Saved Searches to Find Agreement Data
heading_path: Use Saved Searches to Find Agreement Data
text:
Learn how to access and run a saved search, including steps to edit and delete searches.

Run your saved searches to retrieve an updated list of documents that match the search criteria. When you run the search, the search criteria (keywords, filters, custom columns) are applied to the repository. Since new agreements are regularly added to Agreement Manager, the search results can change over time.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   TheAgreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed.

   A list of documents to which the logged-in user has access is displayed in the table. The total document count is displayed the bottom right. This count is updated when you perform a search or apply filters.
4. Run a saved search from a list of Your Recently Saved searches.

   ![A snippet of the Agreement Manager homepage that highlights the Open Saved Search (bookmark) icon.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Saved-searches_Access-searches_Open-Saved-Search.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY0NDksInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.j7cv5SmqmdTjxZy9tHNbG323QCK9MYGiujaoYS76HJo&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Agreement Manager homepage that highlights the Open Saved Search (bookmark) icon.")

   1. Select the Open Saved Search (bookmark) icon.

      A dropdown opens and Your Recently Saved searches are displayed.
   2. Select the relevant search from the list of recently saved searches.

   The search is run against the agreement list table and the agreements that matched the search criteria are displayed. The search name and description are displayed at the top of the page.

   ![A screenshot of the results table from the saved search that was run against the agreement list table.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Saved-searches_search-results_distribution-agreements.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY0NDksInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.j7cv5SmqmdTjxZy9tHNbG323QCK9MYGiujaoYS76HJo&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the results table from the saved search that was run against the agreement list table.")

   There are a number of actions that you can take from the search results page, such as:
   - [Download Documents from the Agreement List Table](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=ach1724363496037.html&_LANG=enus "This topic answers the question: How do I download agreements from Agreement Manager?")
   - [Export Data from the Agreement List Table](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=pqf1724363438241.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to export agreement data to a CSV file for selected agreements.")
5. Return to the Agreement Manager homepage.
   - Select the Completed Documents option from the breadcrumb navigation at the top of the page.
   - Select the Back to Completed option located to the right of the filters.

   You are returned to the Agreement Manager homepage and the Completed Documents view is displayed. Next, access your saved searches from the search field.
6. Click in the search field and select the Saved Searches option.

title: Use Saved Searches to Find Agreement Data
heading_path: Use Saved Searches to Find Agreement Data
text:
the Agreement Manager homepage and the Completed Documents view is displayed. Next, access your saved searches from the search field.
6. Click in the search field and select the Saved Searches option.
