   ![A snippet of the Completed Documents page that highlights the Saved Searches option in the search dropdown.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Saved-searches_Access-searches_Search-dropdown.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY0NDksInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.j7cv5SmqmdTjxZy9tHNbG323QCK9MYGiujaoYS76HJo&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Completed Documents page that highlights the Saved Searches option in the search dropdown.")

   The Saved Searches dialog box opens and a list of your searches is displayed.

   Tip: Every saved search has a More actions (three dots) menu. Use this menu to:![A snippet of the Saved Searches dialog box that shows the More actions menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Saved-Searches-dialog-box_Manage-search-menu_expanded.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY0NDksInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.j7cv5SmqmdTjxZy9tHNbG323QCK9MYGiujaoYS76HJo&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Saved Searches dialog box that shows the More actions menu.")
   - Save a new version of the search with the updated search criteria and column customizations
   - Edit the name and description of the search
   - Delete the search
7. Select (click) the search that you want to run.

   The agreements that matched the selected search are displayed in the table. The search name and description are displayed at the top of the page.
8. Adjust the search criteria for the saved search as needed.
   1. Enter a new keyword or phrase in the search field.
   2. Update the applied filters. Select an existing filter and choose new values or clear the existing selection.
   3. Use the Filters option to apply additional filters to the search. Select the relevant filter, choose the filter values, and select the Apply option.
   4. Use the Show or Hide Fields (toggle) icon to update the columns in the agreement list table. Select toggles to turn the corresponding field on (blue) or off (white). Drag the fields to re-order the columns as needed.

   Be sure to save your unsaved changes.
9. Expand the Save dropdown and choose the relevant option:

   ![A snippet of a saved search that highlights the Save options, including Save Changes and Restore Original.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Saved-search_Update-search-criteria_Save-dropdown_expanded.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY0NDksInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.j7cv5SmqmdTjxZy9tHNbG323QCK9MYGiujaoYS76HJo&_LANG=enus "A snippet of a saved search that highlights the Save options, including Save Changes and Restore Original.")

   - Select the Save Changes option to save
     the changes to the current search.
   - Select the Save as New Version option
     to create a new saved search with the updated search
     criteria.
   - Select the Restore Original option to
     revert to the original version of the saved search.

   The results table updates and agreements that match the updated search
   criteria are displayed.
10. Update the name and description for the search to reflect the updated search criteria.

title: Use Saved Searches to Find Agreement Data
heading_path: Use Saved Searches to Find Agreement Data
text:
search.

   The results table updates and agreements that match the updated search
   criteria are displayed.
10. Update the name and description for the search to reflect the updated search criteria.

    ![A screenshot of the Edit name and description dialog box.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Saved-search_Edit-name-and-description-dialog-box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY0NDksInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.j7cv5SmqmdTjxZy9tHNbG323QCK9MYGiujaoYS76HJo&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Edit name and description dialog box.")

    1. Expand (select) the More actions (three dots) menu in the top right.
    2. Select the Edit Name and Description option.

       The Edit name and description dialog box opens.
    3. Update the Name and Description of the saved search to reflect the changes to the search criteria.
    4. Select the Save option.

    The dialog box closes and the search results page is updated to reflect the new name and description.
11. Delete the search if it is no longer relevant.

    ![A screenshot of the Delete this saved search? dialog box.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Saved-search_Delete-this-saved-search-dialog-box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY0NDksInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.j7cv5SmqmdTjxZy9tHNbG323QCK9MYGiujaoYS76HJo&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Delete this saved search? dialog box.")

    1. Expand (select) the More actions (three dots) menu in the top right.
    2. Select the Delete option.

       The Delete this saved search? dialog box opens. A message confirms that removing the search will not delete or void any agreements.
    3. Select the Delete option.

    The dialog box closes. A message confirms that the saved search was deleted. You are returned to the Completed Documents view. The search no longer displays in your saved search list.

You have successfully used a saved search to locate agreement data, including a review of additional search actions.

title: Add Examples of Your Custom Fields
heading_path: Add Examples of Your Custom Fields
text: Learn how to complete the second step of the AI detection set-up process, *Select examples*.

title: Add Examples of Your Custom Fields
heading_path: Add Examples of Your Custom Fields
text:
This topic reviews the next step of the AI detection set-up process, *Select examples*. These steps are completed by users with Organize and manage all agreements access to Agreement Manager agreements and functionality.

Note: Administrators automatically have access to configure agreement data. For non-administrator users, this feature requires Organize and manage all agreements access to Agreement Manager. These tasks are primarily completed by administrators and contract managers.

Important: Complete the [Create Custom Fields from the Agreement Preview](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tsk0830ea60-9b29-4efa-b73f-9eb15b2d6561.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to create custom fields from the Agreement preview page for a selected agreement.") topic before attempting this task. When you create a field from the Agreement preview page, the Track this field with AI wizard is launched.

When you set up custom extractions, you are prompted to provide examples, in addition to a text
definition. The definition tells the AI model what the data means and the examples
show how the data displays in your agreements.

In the previous task, you added field details and provided a definition to initiate the AI setup. When you add a field from the Agreement preview page, the first step of the AI detection set-up is completed during field configuration. After saving the configuration, the Track this field with AI wizard opened:

![A screenshot of the Track this field with AI setup wizard that displays when you create custom fields from the Agreement preview page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Custom-field-extractions_Track-this-field-with-AI_setup-wizard.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2NzcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.K2tsSnRuAkJjga3n1_DKzSk9Q_syUtYAHUY0altdmdA&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Track this field with AI setup wizard that displays when you create custom fields from the Agreement preview page.")

After you selected the Set Up option, the Track this field with AI wizard advanced to the *Select examples* step. This topic completes the *Select examples* step of the AI detection set-up process.

1. From the previous step in this process, select the Set Up option from the Track this field with AI wizard.

   The Select examples step opens. You are prompted to select two or more agreements that contain the field. These examples help the AI model locate the data point in different contexts.

   ![A screenshot of the Select examples step that prompts you to select examples of the custom field.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Fields-Manager_Agreement-preview_Select-examples.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2NzcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.K2tsSnRuAkJjga3n1_DKzSk9Q_syUtYAHUY0altdmdA&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Select examples step that prompts you to select examples of the custom field.")

   Important: Use the Finish Later option to save your progress and finish the AI detection set-up later. Using this option ends the Track this field with AI experience. See the [Enable AI Detection for Your Custom Fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tsk02bd31a2-b125-4d53-b632-20d9972163a8.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to enable the AI detection for your custom fields. Once activated, the field is surfaced in your new agreements automatically.") topic for steps to finish the process.
2. Select the Select Documents option.

   The page refreshes and an agreement list table is displayed. Select two or more documents that include the custom field.

**Select examples of the custom field**

3. Locate the documents that contain good examples of the custom field:

title: Add Examples of Your Custom Fields
heading_path: Add Examples of Your Custom Fields
text:
t list table is displayed. Select two or more documents that include the custom field.

**Select examples of the custom field**

3. Locate the documents that contain good examples of the custom field:

   ![A snippet of the agreement list table that shows the search field and filter options to locate examples.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Fields-Manager_Agreement-preview_Select-examples_search-filter-options.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2NzcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.K2tsSnRuAkJjga3n1_DKzSk9Q_syUtYAHUY0altdmdA&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the agreement list table that shows the search field and filter options to locate examples.")

   1. Search for the relevant agreements. Enter a keyword or search string in the search field. The dropdown opens and search suggestions are displayed. Select a search suggestion or press the Enter key.
   2. Filter the agreement list table to refine the results list. For example, filter by Sets, Status, Parties, Agreement Type, or AI Suggestions.

      Tip: Use the All Filters option to apply additional filters to the results table.

      Expand the filter, choose the relevant filter value, and select the Apply option.

   The agreement list table updates and the documents that matched the search criteria and applied filters are displayed. The original agreement is selected by default. This is the agreement that you were viewing in the Agreement preview when you added the field.
4. Choose at least two agreements from the agreement list table:

   ![A snippet of the agreement list table that shows a number of selected examples.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Fields-Manager_Agreement-preview_Select-examples_selected-docs.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2NzcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.K2tsSnRuAkJjga3n1_DKzSk9Q_syUtYAHUY0altdmdA&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the agreement list table that shows a number of selected examples.")

   Tip: Select the Show Selected Agreements option to review your selected documents. If necessary, select the Return to Search Results option and make changes to your selections.
5. Select the Start Highlighting option at the bottom of the page.

   An agreement preview view opens and the selected documents are listed in the pane on the left. For each of the examples in the list, highlight where the field is displayed in the document.

**Highlight the field in your examples**

6. Highlight the field in your example documents and confirm the field value:

   ![An animated image of the Select examples step that highlights the field value in an example document.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Fields-Manager_Agreement-preview_Select-examples_animated.gif?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2NzcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.K2tsSnRuAkJjga3n1_DKzSk9Q_syUtYAHUY0altdmdA&_LANG=enus "An animated image of the Select examples step that highlights the field value in an example document.")The first example is displayed in the Preview pane.

   1. Locate the field in the document. Scroll to find the field value or use the Search (magnifying glass) icon to perform a keyword search.
   2. Highlight the field value from the Preview pane and select the Confirm selection option from the dropdown.

      You are prompted to confirm the custom field value.
   3. Enter the selected text in the Confirm [Field Name] field.

      Note: Highlight the field value in the Preview pane and select the Copy Text option from the dropdown. Paste the text into the field.
   4. Select the Confirm option:

title: Add Examples of Your Custom Fields
heading_path: Add Examples of Your Custom Fields
text:
onfirm [Field Name] field.

      Note: Highlight the field value in the Preview pane and select the Copy Text option from the dropdown. Paste the text into the field.
   4. Select the Confirm option:

      The next example is displayed.
   5. Continue highlighting the field value in your documents until all the examples are confirmed.

      Tip: If necessary, use the Remove icon to remove an example from the list. For example, if the agreement does not contain the custom field.
7. Select the Next option.

   You are returned to the set-up wizard. Your examples were saved and are displayed in the Add examples of list.

   ![A screenshot of the Add examples of list that displays the examples that you provided for the custom field.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Fields-Manager_Agreement-preview_Confirm-examples.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2NzcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.K2tsSnRuAkJjga3n1_DKzSk9Q_syUtYAHUY0altdmdA&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Add examples of list that displays the examples that you provided for the custom field.")
8. Review each of the examples for accuracy:
   - Use the Edit (pencil) icon to update the
     example.
   - Use the Remove icon to delete the
     example.
   - Use the + Add Example option to add more
     examples. For example, highlight the field in additional documents or paste
     your examples.

   Note: If necessary, use the +Add Example option to add
   more examples of the custom field. You have the option of highlighting
   examples in documents or pasting examples into the list.
9. Select the Next option from the bottom of the page.

   Your examples are saved. The Track this field with AI wizard advances to the next step, *Test for accuracy*.

   Complete the [Test the Extraction Accuracy for Your Custom Fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=wjr1740452628309.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to test the AI detection accuracy for your custom fields and refining the results to improve accuracy.") task to test the accuracy of the AI detection. This step is required.

You have successfully completed the second step of the AI detection set-up process, *Select examples*.

title: Add Examples of Your Custom Agreement Types
heading_path: Add Examples of Your Custom Agreement Types
text: Learn how to complete the second step of the AI detection set-up process, *Add examples of this agreement type*.

title: Add Examples of Your Custom Agreement Types
heading_path: Add Examples of Your Custom Agreement Types
text:
This topic reviews the next step of the AI detection set-up process, *Add examples of this agreement type*. These steps are completed by administrators and contract managers.

Remember: Administrators automatically have access to configure agreement data. For non-administrator users, agreement type management tasks require Organize and manage all agreements access to Agreement Manager agreements and functionality.

Complete these topics before attempting this task:

- [Configure New Custom Agreement Types](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=gke1727282238086.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to create custom agreement types to support organizational use cases.")
- [Define Your Custom Agreement Types](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tske5fe1ad5-2832-4e6c-9177-f33a9ffade15.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to initiate the AI detection set-up process and complete the first step to define the custom type.")

When you set up custom extractions, you are prompted to provide examples, in addition to a text
definition. The definition tells the AI model what the data means and the examples
show how the data displays in your agreements.

In the previous step, you provided a definition for your custom agreement. After you selected the
Next option, the AI
detection set-up wizard advanced to the next step.
You are prompted to select examples of the custom
type.

1. Review the dialog box message for a brief
   overview of the next step. Select the
   Select Examples
   option:

   ![A screenshot of the introduction message for the next step of the AI detection set-up process.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-Types-Manager_AI-detect-setup_Need-examples_prompt.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2MDAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.3oI_mUYMDrZL_dZJfPlwv9aXwP7qh9SdZzvpRQP6kNg&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the introduction message for the next step of the AI detection set-up process.")

   The Select relevant
   documents page opens. Use this page to
   select documents that are good examples of the
   custom type.
2. Locate documents that are good examples of the custom agreement type:

   ![A snippet of the Select relevant documents page that shows the search field and filter options to locate examples.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-Types-Manager_AI-detect-setup_Select-examples_search-filter-options.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2MDAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.3oI_mUYMDrZL_dZJfPlwv9aXwP7qh9SdZzvpRQP6kNg&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Select relevant documents page that shows the search field and filter options to locate examples.")

   1. Search for the relevant agreements. Enter a keyword or search string in the search field. The dropdown opens and search suggestions are displayed. Select a search suggestion or press the Enter key.
   2. Filter the agreement list table to refine the results list. For example, filter by Sets, Status, Parties, Agreement Type, or AI Suggestions.

      Tip: Use the All Filters option to apply additional filters to the results table.

      Expand the filter, choose the relevant filter value, and select the Apply option.

   The agreement list table updates and the documents that matched the search criteria and applied filters are displayed.
3. Choose the documents that exemplify the custom agreement type:

title: Add Examples of Your Custom Agreement Types
heading_path: Add Examples of Your Custom Agreement Types
text:
ply option.

   The agreement list table updates and the documents that matched the search criteria and applied filters are displayed.
3. Choose the documents that exemplify the custom agreement type:

   ![A snippet of the Select relevant documents page that shows a number of selected examples.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-Types-Manager_AI-detect-setup_Selected-documents_Save-Eamples.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2MDAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.3oI_mUYMDrZL_dZJfPlwv9aXwP7qh9SdZzvpRQP6kNg&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Select relevant documents page that shows a number of selected examples.")

   Tip: Select the Show Selected Agreements option to review your selected documents. If necessary, select the Return to Search Results option and make changes to your selections.
4. Select the Save Examples option.

   The AI detection set-up wizard advances to the next step and you are prompted to Test for accuracy.

   Complete the [Test the Extraction Accuracy for Your Custom Agreement Types](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=ogw1740503179389.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to test the accuracy of the AI detection for your custom agreement types.") task to test the accuracy of your AI
   detection.

You have successfully completed the second step of the AI detection set-up process, *Add examples of this agreement type*.

title: Add and Remove Fields from Agreement Types
heading_path: Add and Remove Fields from Agreement Types
text:
Learn how to add and remove standard and custom fields from agreement types.

Administrators and contract managers can add and remove fields from the agreement types in their account. This function requires Organize and manage all agreements access to Agreement Manager agreements and features.

Fields can be added to both custom (user-defined) and standard (Docusign-provided) agreement types. Likewise, fields can be removed from agreement types if they are no longer relevant or required for the type.

Important: Each Docusign-provided agreement type is pre-populated with a list of relevant standard fields. You cannot remove these default fields from the agreement type as they provide structure to the type.

Use the *Agreement Types Manager* to add and remove fields from the agreement types in your account. This task is completed from the Agreement Types option in the Settings (gear) menu.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Completed Documents page opens. A list of documents to which the
   logged-in user has access is displayed in the table.
4. Expand the Settings (gear) dropdown from the top right and select the Agreement Types option:

   ![A screenshot of the Agreement Manager homepage that highlights the Agreement Types option in the Settings (gear) menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-types-manager_Settings-menu_Agreement-Types_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc1NDksInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.BEE4guT9h3E0PlEbQUEslcpbgc6_g0ofm1RYeNf4Kq4&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Agreement Manager homepage that highlights the Agreement Types option in the Settings (gear) menu.")

   The Manage Agreement Types page opens. A list of the account's standard and custom agreement types is displayed in the Agreement Types table.

**Locate the relevant agreement type**

5. Locate the agreement type that you want to update.

   ![A snippet of the Manage Agreement Types page that shows the search and filter options for locating agreement types.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Manage-Agreement-Types_Agreement-Types_search-filters.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc1NDksInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.BEE4guT9h3E0PlEbQUEslcpbgc6_g0ofm1RYeNf4Kq4&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Manage Agreement Types page that shows the search and filter options for locating agreement types.")

   - Search for the relevant agreement type. Enter a keyword in the Search types... field. As you enter the search term, the table updates and agreement types that matched the entered criteria are displayed.
   - Filter the table for the relevant agreement type. Filter options are: Created By, Category, and AI Status. Expand the filter, choose the relevant value, and select the Apply option.The table updates and the agreement types that matched the applied filters are displayed.
6. Select (click) the relevant agreement type from the table.

   The Agreement type details view opens for the selected agreement type. The fields associated with the agreement type are listed in the table.

**Add a field to the agreement type**

7. Select the + Add Fields option from the top right.

   The Add a field dialog box opens.

title: Add and Remove Fields from Agreement Types
heading_path: Add and Remove Fields from Agreement Types
text:
e fields associated with the agreement type are listed in the table.

**Add a field to the agreement type**

7. Select the + Add Fields option from the top right.

   The Add a field dialog box opens.

   ![A screenshot of the Add a field dialog box that is used to add a field to existing agreement types.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-type-management_create-type_add-a-field-dialog-box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc1NDksInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.BEE4guT9h3E0PlEbQUEslcpbgc6_g0ofm1RYeNf4Kq4&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Add a field dialog box that is used to add a field to existing agreement types.")The selected agreement type is displayed in the Agreement types field by default.
8. Complete the required fields and select the Save option.
   1. Enter the name of the field in the Field name field.

      As you enter a name, a type-ahead dropdown list opens, and fields that match the entered name are displayed.
   2. Select the name from the type-ahead dropdown list or select the + Create [Field name] option to add a new custom field to the agreement type.

      For existing fields, the Field type and Category fields default based on the selected field.
   3. For custom fields, complete the Field type and Category fields:

      - Expand the Field type dropdown and select the type of custom field that you are adding. Choices are: Text, Number, Date, and Dropdown.

        Note: If you select Dropdown as the field type, you must provide a list of options for the dropdown. Enter a list of dropdown options in the Options text-entry box:![A snippet of the Add a field dialog box that shows the Options field for adding dropdown options.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Add-custom-field_Options-for-dropdown-type.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc1NDksInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.BEE4guT9h3E0PlEbQUEslcpbgc6_g0ofm1RYeNf4Kq4&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Add a field dialog box that shows the Options field for adding dropdown options.")**Important:** Separate each option with a semicolon.
      - Expand the Category dropdown and select a classification for the custom field.

      Choices are: General, Termination, Renewal, Payment, Legal and Compliance, and Other.
   4. Add the field to multiple agreement types if necessary. The selected type is displayed in the field automatically.

      Click in the Agreement types field to expand the dropdown. Choose the agreement types to which the field is being added and select the Apply option. You can select one or more custom and standard agreement types:

      ![A screenshot of the Agreement types dropdown that shows a selected agreement type to which the field will be added.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Manage-agreement-types-fields_Add-a-field_Agreement-types-dropdown.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc1NDksInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.BEE4guT9h3E0PlEbQUEslcpbgc6_g0ofm1RYeNf4Kq4&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Agreement types dropdown that shows a selected agreement type to which the field will be added.")
   5. Select the Save option.

      The dialog box closes. The Agreement type details view opens for the selected agreement type. The newly added field is displayed in the Fields table.

**Remove a field from the agreement type**

This task continues with the same agreement type that was used in the previous steps.

To choose a new agreement type, return to the Agreement Types view and select a new agreement type. Select Agreement Types from the breadcrumb navigation at the top of the page. You are returned to the Agreement Types view.

title: Add and Remove Fields from Agreement Types
heading_path: Add and Remove Fields from Agreement Types
text:
nt type, return to the Agreement Types view and select a new agreement type. Select Agreement Types from the breadcrumb navigation at the top of the page. You are returned to the Agreement Types view.

9. Locate the field that you want to remove from the selected agreement type. Any fields that were previously added to an agreement type can be removed.

   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-type-details_Fields_search-and-filters.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc1NDksInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.BEE4guT9h3E0PlEbQUEslcpbgc6_g0ofm1RYeNf4Kq4&_LANG=enus)

   - Search for the relevant field. Enter a keyword in the Search fields... field. The table updates and the fields that matched the search criteria are displayed.
   - Filter the table for the relevant field. Filter options are: Created By, Category, Field Type, and AI Status. Expand the filter, choose the relevant value, and select the Apply option.

     The table updates and the fields that matched the applied filters are displayed.
10. Expand the More actions (three dots) menu for the field that you want to remove and select the Remove option:

    ![A snippet of the Fields table that highlights the Remove option in the More actions (three dots) menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-type-details_Fields_More-actions_Remove.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc1NDksInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.BEE4guT9h3E0PlEbQUEslcpbgc6_g0ofm1RYeNf4Kq4&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Fields table that highlights the Remove option in the More actions (three dots) menu.")

    The Remove field dialog box opens.
11. Review the message and select the Remove option:

    ![A screenshot of the Remove field dialog box that prompts you to confirm that you want to remove the field.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Manage-Agreement-Type_Remove-field-dialog-box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc1NDksInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.BEE4guT9h3E0PlEbQUEslcpbgc6_g0ofm1RYeNf4Kq4&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Remove field dialog box that prompts you to confirm that you want to remove the field.")

    The dialog box closes and the Agreement type details view is displayed. The field is no longer associated with the agreement type.
12. Return to the Agreement Types view. Select the Agreement Types option from the breadcrumb navigation in the top left.

    You are returned to the Agreement Types view. Continue updating your agreement types as needed.
13. Return to the Agreement Manager homepage. Select the Close (x) icon from the top right.

    The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed.

You have successfully added and removed fields from custom (user-defined) and custom (Docusign-provided) agreement types.

title: View Your Buy-Side and Sell-Side Agreements
heading_path: View Your Buy-Side and Sell-Side Agreements
text:
Learn how to use the Parties and Party Role filters to view your buy-side and sell-side agreements.

The Party Role is surfaced during agreement processing. This extraction identifies the role that a party plays within the context of an agreement. Once identified, apply the Parties and Party Role filters to view agreements where your organization is either the buyer or seller.

Tip: The
Party Role filter is located under the
Parties filter and it is applied to a selected party:![A screenshot of the Parties filter that highlights the Party Role dropdown and available roles.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Filter-by-party-role_Parties-filter_Party-Role_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY0MTUsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.0Qkhogg61hmywTp6y92b5PVWy6wZ-vWpT5DNLNT8mM0&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Parties filter that highlights the Party Role dropdown and available roles.")The Party Role defaults to All.
Be sure to select the party first before choosing a role. At least one party must be
selected before the Party Role filter can be
applied.

**Access your completed agreements**

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Completed Documents view opens. A list of documents to which the logged-in user has access is displayed in the table. The total document count is displayed in the bottom right. This count is updated when you perform a search or apply filters.

**View your buy-side agreements**

4. Apply filters to view all your buy-side agreements. These are agreements where your organization is the buyer in the agreement.

   ![A screenshot of the Parties filter that shows the selected party and party role.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Parties-filter_Fontara_Buy-side-example.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY0MTUsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.0Qkhogg61hmywTp6y92b5PVWy6wZ-vWpT5DNLNT8mM0&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Parties filter that shows the selected party and party role.")

   1. Expand (select) the Filters option.
   2. Select the Parties filter.
   3. Enter your organization's name in the Search field. The parties list updates as you enter search criteria.
   4. Select your organization from the results list.

      Tip: At least one party must be selected before the Party Role
      filter can be applied.
   5. Expand the Party Role dropdown and select the Buyer option.
   6. Select the Apply option.

   The filter closes. The buy-side agreements that your organization has with other parties are displayed in the table.

   Next, update the agreement list table to view all the sell-side agreements that your organization has with other parties.

**View your sell-side agreements**

5. Update the Parties filter to view your organization's sell-side agreements:

   ![A screenshot of the Parties filter that shows options for updating the party role for the selected party.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-list-table_update-applied-filter_Party-Role_seller.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY0MTUsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.0Qkhogg61hmywTp6y92b5PVWy6wZ-vWpT5DNLNT8mM0&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Parties filter that shows options for updating the party role for the selected party.")

   1. Expand (select) the applied filter.

      The Parties filter opens. Your organization's name is already selected.
   2. Expand the Party Role dropdown and select the Seller option.
   3. Select the Apply option.

title: View Your Buy-Side and Sell-Side Agreements
heading_path: View Your Buy-Side and Sell-Side Agreements
text:
ct) the applied filter.

      The Parties filter opens. Your organization's name is already selected.
   2. Expand the Party Role dropdown and select the Seller option.
   3. Select the Apply option.

   The filter closes. The sell-side agreements that your organization has with other parties are displayed in the table.

   Tip:

   Use the Status filter to further refine the agreement list table. For example, choose to view only Active agreements. See the [Agreement Statuses](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=con6a3bd080-da17-4d1c-a92a-b9b9340c44fd.html&_LANG=enus "Read about agreement statuses, including how they are calculated, visibility within Agreement Manager, and which types of statuses are being tracked.") topic to learn more about this feature.

   Tip: If you routinely search for the same type of agreements or agreement data, save your search criteria for future use. See the [Save Search Criteria for Future Use](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tskc38121a1-bbf8-4da8-8ed7-b364991f7e50.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to save the search criteria for the queries that you frequently perform.") topic to learn more about the saved search feature.

You have successfully viewed all the buy-side and sell-side agreements that you have with other parties.

title: Define Your Custom Agreement Types
text:
Learn how to initiate the AI detection set-up process and complete the first step to
define the custom type.

When you configure a new custom agreement type, the AI detection is inactive by default.
Complete the Set Up process to activate the custom type. Once
activated, the custom data point is surfaced in your new agreements and selected
historical documents.

Important: This task requires a user-defined custom agreement type that
is not activated for AI detection. See the [Configure New Custom Agreement Types](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=gke1727282238086.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to create custom agreement types to support organizational use cases.")
topic for steps to add custom agreement types.

The definition and examples that you provide during the setup are designed to
"teach" the AI model.

Important:

Customers do not need to consent to AI training to use this feature. When customers create custom
agreement types and fields, their data is used as **input** to the AI model to help generate
results for their use. This process does not involve AI training.

If a customer has given consent to sharing data for AI
training, anonymized data may be used for training Docusign AI models. This may
include anonymized data from custom extractions.

See the [Docusign FAQs for AI](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fzd1707173174972&topicId=uss1707173279973.html) topic for more information about how
Docusign uses data for AI training.

This topic reviews the steps to launch the AI detection set-up wizard and define
your custom agreement type. This task completed by administrators and contract managers.

Remember: Administrators automatically have access to configure agreement data. For non-administrator users, agreement type management tasks require Organize and manage all agreements access to Agreement Manager agreements and functionality.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Completed Documents view opens.
4. Expand the Settings (gear) dropdown from the top right of the page and select the Agreement Types option:

   ![A snippet of the Agreement Manager homepage that highlights the Agreement Types option in the Settings (gear) menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-types-manager_Settings-menu_Agreement-Types_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc1OTgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.tf8sVppA3b7gNpfUj5dxUoFHhVC5vbnQn-ClM35i0zs&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Agreement Manager homepage that highlights the Agreement Types option in the Settings (gear) menu.")

   The Manage Agreement Types page opens. A list of the account's standard and custom agreement types is displayed in the Agreement Types table.
5. Locate the custom agreement type that you want to set up for AI detection and select the Set Up option:

   ![A screenshot of the Agreement Types view that highlights the Set Up option for a custom agreement type.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-Types-Manager_Agreement-Types-table_Set-Up-option_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc1OTgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.tf8sVppA3b7gNpfUj5dxUoFHhVC5vbnQn-ClM35i0zs&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Agreement Types view that highlights the Set Up option for a custom agreement type.")

   The AI detection set up wizard launches. Follow the prompts to find and categorize your custom extraction.

title: Define Your Custom Agreement Types
text:
the Agreement Types view that highlights the Set Up option for a custom agreement type.")

   The AI detection set up wizard launches. Follow the prompts to find and categorize your custom extraction.

   ![A screenshot of the setup wizard that introduces the three easy steps to enable the custom agreement type extraction.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Custom-agreement-type-extractions_Setup-wizard_Get-Started.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc1OTgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.tf8sVppA3b7gNpfUj5dxUoFHhVC5vbnQn-ClM35i0zs&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the setup wizard that introduces the three easy steps to enable the custom agreement type extraction.")

   This task focuses on the first step, *Define your custom extraction*.
6. Select the Get Started option.

   The Set definition dialog box opens and asks you to define the custom agreement type.

   ![A screenshot of the Set definition dialog box that prompts you to define the custom agreement type.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Manage-Agreement-Type_Enable-AI-detection-for-custom-agreement-type--Add-defniition.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc1OTgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.tf8sVppA3b7gNpfUj5dxUoFHhVC5vbnQn-ClM35i0zs&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Set definition dialog box that prompts you to define the custom agreement type.")
7. Enter a definition for the custom type. This field is limited to 2,000 characters. The definition describes the purpose and usage of the agreement type.

   Note: See the [Best Practice: Writing Definitions for AI Detection](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tmk1741317734856.html&_LANG=enus "Read about tips for writing good definitions for the AI detection setup process. These definitions are an important part of the process and help to \"teach\" the AI detection.") support topic to review the guidelines for writing definitions. Follow these guidelines to get the most our of the AI detection and to ensure accuracy.

   When the definition exceeds 50 characters, the Refine with AI option becomes available. Use this option to update the definition for consistency and accuracy.
8. Fine-tune the definition.

   ![A screenshot of the Set definition dialog box that shows the AI-suggested definition.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Custom-agreement-type_Set-definition_Refine-with-AI_Definition-Suggested.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc1OTgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.tf8sVppA3b7gNpfUj5dxUoFHhVC5vbnQn-ClM35i0zs&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Set definition dialog box that shows the AI-suggested definition.")

   1. Select the Refine with AI option.

      The dialog box refreshes and a suggested definition is displayed.
   2. Review and edit the suggested definition as needed. You can also select the Undo option to revert to the original version of the definition.
9. Select the Next option.

   The AI detection set-up wizard advances to the next step and you are prompted to select
   examples.

   Complete the [Add Examples of Your Custom Agreement Types](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tskd38465c6-c8c0-4588-bfb3-fdb6916a1926.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to complete the second step of the AI detection set-up process, Add examples of this agreement type.") task to provide
   examples to the AI detection set-up process.

You have successfully completed the first step of the AI detection set-up process and
defined your custom agreement type.

title: Review Job Results from the Notification Email
heading_path: Review Job Results from the Notification Email
text: Learn how to view the results of an upload job from the email notification. A system-generated email is sent when the upload process is completed.

title: Review Job Results from the Notification Email
heading_path: Review Job Results from the Notification Email
text:
This task is part of the *Upload and sync* phase of bulk metadata editing in Agreement Manager. It relies on you completing the previous phases, including:

- [Download the Pre-Formatted Spreadsheet File](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tsk1b94afc6-712f-4d71-868e-0787867354d8.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to download the pre-formatted spreadsheet file for a group of documents.")
- [Edit the Metadata Spreadsheet](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tsk8710c3f2-c881-4219-96b0-2b287e8b4e03.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to edit the metadata spreadsheet file to bulk update the agreement data for the selected documents.")
- [Import Your Edited Metadata Spreadsheet](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tsk5ab1cd5b-00f7-42a9-bd77-728baadff903.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to import your edited metadata spreadsheet using the Add (+) menu located on the Agreement Manager homepage.")

When you import the new data, the existing values are overwritten, including the AI-extracted values. Agreement Manager applies all valid cell values and flags formatting errors in the job monitoring hub.

A system-generated email notification is sent when the upload is completed. The email contains a brief summary of the import results, including the number of files that failed to update.

This topic begins from the system-generated email notification:

![A screenshot of the system-generated notification email that provides a brief summary of the import results.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Bulk-metadata-editing_Notification-email_View-Details.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY4MTcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.elPNQUFlbAE6TbuMEgavDhW0zQPUtLV7zTVWWP4-YvY&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the system-generated notification email that provides a brief summary of the import results.")

1. Review the notification email for a high-level summary of the import results. For example, the number of files that failed to update and possible reasons.
2. Select the View Details option.

   The Job status page opens. The files that failed to update are listed in the Error details table.

   ![A screenshot of the Job status page for the upload job, including the error details for the files that failed to update.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Bulk-metadata-editing_Notification-email_View-Job-status.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY4MTcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.elPNQUFlbAE6TbuMEgavDhW0zQPUtLV7zTVWWP4-YvY&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Job status page for the upload job, including the error details for the files that failed to update.")

   When a data value fails validation, that field is not updated in the associated document and an error is logged.
3. Review the error details for the files that failed to update, including the reason that the data value failed:

   ![A snippet of the Job status page that shows the Error details table, including the reason the files failed to update.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Bulk-metadata-editing_Job-status_Error-details_Fontara-example.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY4MTcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.elPNQUFlbAE6TbuMEgavDhW0zQPUtLV7zTVWWP4-YvY&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Job status page that shows the Error details table, including the reason the files failed to update.")

title: Review Job Results from the Notification Email
heading_path: Review Job Results from the Notification Email
text:
cHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.elPNQUFlbAE6TbuMEgavDhW0zQPUtLV7zTVWWP4-YvY&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Job status page that shows the Error details table, including the reason the files failed to update.")

   Tip: Select the View option to go to the Agreement preview page for the corresponding document. This page opens in a new tab.
4. Resolve the errors and import the spreadsheet again. Repeat this process until all the errors are resolved and the files are updated.

You have successfully viewed the results of an upload job from the email notification. Continue to the last step of the process, [Download and Fix Your Metadata Validation Errors](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tsk788b581e-b10b-41cc-818e-04108b62210c.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to download and fix your metadata validation errors from the upload process.").

title: Delete Unused Custom Agreement Types
text:
Learn how to delete custom agreement types that are no longer relevant.

Organizations can create custom agreement types to support their use cases. However, when custom
agreement types are no longer relevant, they can be deleted. Once an agreement type
is deleted, it cannot be recovered.

The documents that are currently associated with the agreement type remain
intact. However, the agreement type is no longer surfaced in new documents.

Tip: Remove unused custom agreement types if your organization is close to reaching the 200 custom agreement type limit per account. Once the custom agreement types are deleted, they no longer count towards the limit.

Delete your custom unused agreement types from the Agreement Types option in the Settings (gear) dropdown. This task requires the Organize and manage all agreements permission and is typically completed by administrators and contract managers.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Completed Documents view opens. A list of documents to which the logged-in user has access is displayed in the table.
4. Expand the Settings (gear) dropdown from the top right and select the Agreement Types option:

   ![A screenshot of the Agreement Manager homepage that highlights the Agreement Types option in the Settings (gear) menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-types-manager_Settings-menu_Agreement-Types_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc1MjgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.xE2xH63gOy2KmKNc_a4fdsuvm3E8vUPogj-JDvomT7A&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Agreement Manager homepage that highlights the Agreement Types option in the Settings (gear) menu.")

   The Manage Agreement Types page opens. A list of the account's standard and custom agreement types is displayed in the Agreement Types table.
5. Locate the custom agreement type that you want to delete. Only user-created, custom agreement types can be deleted.

   ![A snippet of the Manage Agreement Types page that shows the selected Created By value, the User option.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Manage-Agreement-Types_Created-By-filter_User-selected.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc1MjgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.xE2xH63gOy2KmKNc_a4fdsuvm3E8vUPogj-JDvomT7A&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Manage Agreement Types page that shows the selected Created By value, the User option.")

   1. Expand (click) the Created By filter and select the User option.
   2. Select the Apply option.

      The Agreement Types table updates and all the account's custom agreement types are displayed.
   3. Refine the results list as needed. For example, apply the Category and AI Status filters or perform a keyword search.
6. Expand the More actions (three dots) menu for the relevant custom agreement type and select the Delete option:

   ![A snippet of the Agreement Types view that highlights the Delete option in the More actions (three dots) menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-type-management_Delete-custom-agreement-type_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc1MjgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.xE2xH63gOy2KmKNc_a4fdsuvm3E8vUPogj-JDvomT7A&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Agreement Types view that highlights the Delete option in the More actions (three dots) menu.")

   The Delete agreement type? dialog box opens. You are prompted to confirm that you want to delete the agreement type.

title: Delete Unused Custom Agreement Types
text:
view that highlights the Delete option in the More actions (three dots) menu.")

   The Delete agreement type? dialog box opens. You are prompted to confirm that you want to delete the agreement type.

   ![A screenshot of the Delete agreement type? dialog box that prompts you to delete the agreement type.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-type-management_Delete-agreement-type-dialog-box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc1MjgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.xE2xH63gOy2KmKNc_a4fdsuvm3E8vUPogj-JDvomT7A&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Delete agreement type? dialog box that prompts you to delete the agreement type.")
7. Review the message and select the OK option.

   The message states that the agreement type won't be available for future agreements. However, the type still displays on current agreements until you change it.

   The dialog box closes and you are returned to the Manage Agreement Types page. The deleted agreement type is no longer displayed in the Agreement Types table.
8. Repeat these steps to remove other unused custom agreement types.
9. Return to the Agreement Manager homepage. Select the Close (x) icon from the top right.

   You are returned to the Completed Documents page.

You have successfully deleted an unused custom agreement type.

title: Update Custom Dropdown Fields
text:
Learn how to update the dropdown options for custom fields with the Dropdown field type.

Administrators and contract managers can update the custom fields in their account. For example, updating the options for custom dropdown fields.

Note: The Edit Details function is available for custom fields only. You cannot update the standard, Docusign-provided fields. This function is completed by users with Organize and manage all agreements access to Agreement Manager and the account's agreements.

Use the *Fields Manager* to update the options for a custom dropdown field. Access this function from the Agreement Manager homepage under the Settings (gear) menu.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed.
4. Expand the Settings (gear) dropdown from the top right and select the Fields option:

   ![A screenshot of the Agreement Manager homepage that highlights the Fields option in the Settings (gear) menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-types-manager_Settings-menu_Fields_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc3MzYsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.LDHMG4GexASxojP65XWHMklEDYexCCGaE4ch8yPMOvs&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Agreement Manager homepage that highlights the Fields option in the Settings (gear) menu.")

   The Manage Fields page opens and the Fields view is displayed. All the custom and standard fields in the account are displayed in the table.
5. Locate the custom dropdown field that you want to update.
   - Search for the relevant custom dropdown field.

     Enter a keyword in the Search fields... field. As you type the keyword, the table updates and the fields that match the search term are displayed.
   - Filter the table to locate the relevant custom field. Filter options are: Created By, Category, Field Type, and AI Status.

     Use the Created By and Field Type filters to view the custom dropdown fields in your account:

     - Expand the Created By filter, choose the User value, and select the Apply option.
     - Expand the Field Type filter, choose the Dropdown option, and select the Apply option.

     The table updates and the fields that matched the applied filters are displayed.
6. Expand the More actions (three dots) menu for the relevant field and select the Edit Details option:

   ![A snippet of the Fields table that highlights the Edit Details option for a user-created custom dropdown field.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Manage-Agreement-Type_Update-dropdown-field_Edit-Details.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc3MzYsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.LDHMG4GexASxojP65XWHMklEDYexCCGaE4ch8yPMOvs&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Fields table that highlights the Edit Details option for a user-created custom dropdown field.")The Edit field dialog box opens and a warning (!) message is displayed. Any changes that you make to the field are reflected everywhere that the field is being used.

   You can update the Name, Options, and Category details for the selected custom field.

title: Update Custom Dropdown Fields
text:
ge is displayed. Any changes that you make to the field are reflected everywhere that the field is being used.

   You can update the Name, Options, and Category details for the selected custom field.

   Tip: The Field type and Agreement types fields are closed to edits. You cannot update these fields from this location. However, you can add the field to multiple agreement types from the *Agreement Types Manager*. See the [Add Fields to Multiple Agreement Types](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tsk37e570f8-2428-4d26-a78b-b1239aca35a5.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to add fields to multiple agreement types from the Add a field dialog box. You can add fields to additional agreement types while you are updating a selected agreement type.") article to learn about adding fields to multiple agreement types.
7. Update the Options for the custom dropdown field as needed.

   ![A screenshot of the Edit field dialog box that highlights the Options text-entry box.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Manage-Agreement-Type_Edit-field-details_Update-Options.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc3MzYsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.LDHMG4GexASxojP65XWHMklEDYexCCGaE4ch8yPMOvs&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Edit field dialog box that highlights the Options text-entry box.")

   1. Update the options for the custom dropdown field from the Options text-entry box. Separate each option with a semicolon (;).
   2. Select the Save option.

   The dialog box closes and the Agreement type details view is displayed for the selected type. The custom field is listed in the table and reflects the updated details.

   The dialog box closes and you are returned to the Manage Fields page. The
   custom dropdown field has been updated and the changes are reflected across the
   Intelligent Agreement Management (IAM) platform.
8. Return to the Agreement Manager home page. Select the Close (x) icon from the top right.

   You are returned to the Agreement Manager homepage. The Completed Documents view is displayed.

You have successfully updated the options for a custom dropdown field.

title: View Report Data for Sets of Agreements
heading_path: View Report Data for Sets of Agreements
text:
Learn how to filter an agreement report to display data for a selected set of agreements.

The Agreements Dashboard contains a number of charts and high-level insights that can be viewed as detailed reports. Use the Sets filter to view report data for a selected set of agreements.

Filters can be applied from both the dashboard view and the detailed report view. This topic reviews the steps to filter the Agreements Dashboard to view report data for a selected agreement set.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Insights from the global header.

   The Insights hub opens and the Overview is displayed.
3. Select Agreements from the navigation pane on the
   left.

   The Agreements Dashboard opens.
4. Filter the dashboard to show data for a selected set of agreements:

   ![A snippet of an agreement report that highlights the Sets filter and shows a selected set.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-dashboard_Report_Sets-filter.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY5NjgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.uIY2Fcu7CheI4_FsgQ47Q-lbY2T8w7iB5LDV7AH_Nas&_LANG=enus "A snippet of an agreement report that highlights the Sets filter and shows a selected set.")

   1. Expand the Sets filter.
   2. Search for and select the relevant agreement set.
   3. Select the Apply option.
5. Go to the detailed report view for an insight or chart. Select (click) the title of the insight or chart that you want to view.

   You can also expand the More actions (three dot) menu and select the View Report option:

   ![A snippet of the Expiring agreements insight that shows the View Report option in the More actions menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Agreements_snippet-ellipsesView-Report.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY5NjgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.uIY2Fcu7CheI4_FsgQ47Q-lbY2T8w7iB5LDV7AH_Nas&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Expiring agreements insight that shows the View Report option in the More actions menu.")

   The detailed report view opens and it is pre-filtered to show data from the agreements in the selected set:

   - Agreements in the set are displayed in the agreement list table
   - Agreement data is reflected in the chart and the table
6. Download or save the report:

   ![A snippet of the report view that highlights the More actions (three dots) menu used to download or save a report.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Report-view_Dropdown-expanded.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY5NjgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.uIY2Fcu7CheI4_FsgQ47Q-lbY2T8w7iB5LDV7AH_Nas&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the report view that highlights the More actions (three dots) menu used to download or save a report.")

   - Expand the More actions (three dots) menu in the top right and select
     the Download option.

     Note:

     When downloading reports to CSV files from the Insights reporting hub, there is an agreement limit of 25,000 agreements for all IAM reports. This count includes your custom reports.

     The report is downloaded to your local
     Downloads folder. The output file contains all
     the available attributes, including standard and non-standard fields.
     Customize the CSV file as needed.
   - Expand the More actions (three dots) menu in the top right and select the Save option. The Save Report dialog box
     opens.

title: View Report Data for Sets of Agreements
heading_path: View Report Data for Sets of Agreements
text:
ndard and non-standard fields.
     Customize the CSV file as needed.
   - Expand the More actions (three dots) menu in the top right and select the Save option. The Save Report dialog box
     opens.

     - Enter a unique name in the Report name field. The field defaults to the name of the selected report.
     - Enter a description about the report's purpose in the Description field.
     - Select the Save option.The report is saved to the Custom tab on the Reports view.

     Enter a Report name and select
     Confirm. The report is saved to the
     Custom tab on the Reports
     view.
7. Set up one-time and recurring email reports for yourself and others. Select the Manage Notification (bell) icon and complete the Create subscription dialog box:

   ![A screenshot of the Create subscription dialog box that is used to subscribe to report notifications.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Create-Subscription.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY5NjgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.uIY2Fcu7CheI4_FsgQ47Q-lbY2T8w7iB5LDV7AH_Nas&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Create subscription dialog box that is used to subscribe to report notifications.")

   Note: When you create a subscription, you have the option to add individual users and groups. Once created, the subscription is displayed in the Manage report subscriptions dialog box for any individual users who were added. Currently, the subscription is not visible to users if they were added as part of a group.

   See the [Subscribe to Receive Report Notifications](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=wfw1734545937270.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to set up one-time or recurring email reports for yourself or others to receive notifications about your obligations, renewals, or agreements.") topic to learn more about this
   feature.
8. Add the report to Your Favorites list. Select the Favorite (star) icon located in the top right:

   ![A snippet of a detailed report view that highlights the Favorite (star) icon.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreements-dashboard_Report-details_Favorite-icon-selected_generic.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY5NjgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.uIY2Fcu7CheI4_FsgQ47Q-lbY2T8w7iB5LDV7AH_Nas&_LANG=enus "A snippet of a detailed report view that highlights the Favorite (star) icon.")Your starred dashboards and reports are displayed in the Your Favorites section on the Overview dashboard.
9. Return to the Agreements Dashboard. Select
   Agreements Dashboard from the breadcrumb navigation
   at the top of the page.

   The Agreements Dashboard view opens.

You have successfully filtered the detailed report view to show data for a selected set of agreements.

title: Manage Your Existing Hierarchies
text: Learn how to maintain your existing hierarchies, including updating the relationship structure between the related agreements.

title: Manage Your Existing Hierarchies
text:
The selected party or group must have existing hierarchies to complete various maintenance tasks.

Important:

Access to various hierarchy-related functionalities is based on a
user's document-level access. For example:

- **Viewing documents in a hierarchy.** Users can view the documents in a hierarchy if they have Can View, Can Edit, or Can Manage access to the document.

  If you do not have the appropriate document-level access, the documents are not visible in the
  hierarchy.
- **Adding documents.** When creating and updating hierarchies, users can only add documents to which they have Can Edit or Can Manage access.

  If you do not have the appropriate document-level access, you cannot add the agreement to the hierarchy.
- **Editing hierarchies.** Users can only edit a hierarchy if they have Can Edit or Can Manage access to all the documents in the hierarchy.

  If you do not have the appropriate document-level access, you cannot edit the hierarchy. For
  example, you cannot update details, add documents,
  or remove documents.
- **Rearranging document relationships in a hierarchy.** Users must have Can Edit or Can Manage access to all the documents in a hierarchy to rearrange or change the relationship between documents.

  If you do not have the appropriate document-level access, you cannot rearrange or update the document relationships for a hierarchy.

Perform these tasks to maintain your existing hierarchies:

- Update the hierarchy details
- Add documents to the hierarchy
- Change the primary agreement
- Reorder the agreements in the hierarchy
- Remove agreements from the hierarchy

This topic reviews the steps to update an existing hierarchy from the party hub. Begin by locating the relevant party or group from the Parties page.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign
   Agreements page opens. The Inbox view
   is displayed by default.
3. Expand the PARTIES section in the navigation pane on the left and select the All option.

   The Parties page opens and a list of individual and merged parties
   is displayed in the table.
4. Select the relevant party or group from the parties list.
   1. Enter the name of the party or group in the Search parties field and press [Enter].

      Parties and groups matching the keyword are displayed in the table.
   2. Select the party or group from the table.

   The party hub opens for the selected party or group. The
   Overview tab is displayed by default.
5. Select the Hierarchies tab.

   Hierarchies are created and managed from the IAM party hub under the Hierarchies tab. From this page, you are prompted to create new hierarchies or update existing ones.

   ![A screenshot of the Hierarchies tab for a selected party or group that highlights an existing hierarchy.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Contract-Hierarchy_Hierarchies-tab_Select-hierarchy_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc4NTYsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.9jEFiUrDPtdJq67TbNqC-bFBUVYqhiA9-YZMI9HA3GE&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Hierarchies tab for a selected party or group that highlights an existing hierarchy.")
6. Select (click) the hierarchy that you want to update.

   The hierarchy details page opens for the selected hierarchy. Perform the various maintenance tasks as needed.

title: Manage Your Existing Hierarchies
text:
ghts an existing hierarchy.")
6. Select (click) the hierarchy that you want to update.

   The hierarchy details page opens for the selected hierarchy. Perform the various maintenance tasks as needed.

   ![A screenshot of the hierarchy details page for a selected hierarchy that lists the related agreements.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Contract-Hierarchy_Hierarchy-details-page.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc4NTYsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.9jEFiUrDPtdJq67TbNqC-bFBUVYqhiA9-YZMI9HA3GE&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the hierarchy details page for a selected hierarchy that lists the related agreements.")
7. Update the hierarchy details.
   1. Select the Edit (pencil) icon located next to the title at the top of the page.

      The hierarchy details page opens.
   2. Update the Title and Description fields.
   3. Select the Save option.

   The page closes and you are returned to the hierarchy details page. The title and description are updated to reflect the changes.
8. Add documents to the hierarchy.
   1. Select the Add Documents option.

      The Add related agreements page opens.
   2. Scroll to the table at the bottom of the page. This is the table of available documents.
   3. Enter search criteria and apply filters to locate the relevant documents.
   4. Select the + Add option associated with the documents that you want to add to the hierarchy.
   5. Select the Update option.

   The page closes and you are returned to the hierarchy details page. The additional documents are listed in the Related Agreements section.
9. Designate a new primary document.

   ![A snippet of the hierarchy details page that highlights the Arrange actions menu options.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Contract-Hierarchy_Create-Hierarchy_Arrange-actions-menu.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc4NTYsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.9jEFiUrDPtdJq67TbNqC-bFBUVYqhiA9-YZMI9HA3GE&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the hierarchy details page that highlights the Arrange actions menu options.")

   1. Expand the Arrange actions (vertical ellipsis) menu for the relevant agreement.
   2. Select the Make Primary option.

   The Related Agreements section updates and the new Primary document is displayed at the top of the table.
10. Reorder the agreements to create structured relationships between them. Consider these options:

    - Hover the cursor over the dots in the File Name column for the agreement that you want to move. When the "grabber" cursor displays, drag the agreement to the appropriate location.
    - Expand the Arrange actions menu and choose to move the agreement up, down, or to a different level.Repeat this step until the hierarchy reflects the appropriate relationship structure between the agreements. Select the Update option.

    The hierarchy details page reflects the new agreement relationships.
11. Remove unnecessary agreements.
    1. Expand the Arrange actions (vertical ellipsis) menu for the relevant agreement.
    2. Select the Remove from Hierarchy option.
    3. Select the Update option. Your changes are saved.

    The hierarchy details page updates and the agreement no longer displays in the Related Agreements list.
12. Return to the Parties page. Select Parties from the breadcrumb navigation at the top of the page.

    You are returned to the Parties page. The search criteria and filters are no longer applied to the table.

You have successfully updated an existing hierarchy for a selected party or group.

title: Build an Advanced Search Query
text: Learn how to build an advanced search query by combining multiple operators and features in a single query string.

title: Build an Advanced Search Query
text:
Before completing this task, review the [Advanced Search Queries](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=conce9d9428-f27e-49ad-9001-3c7a0820ae78.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the advanced search query feature that gives you control over how your results are matched and ranked.") article to learn about the supported advanced query operators.

Advanced search queries give you more control over how results are matched and
ranked. You can find agreements by combining multiple operators and features in a
single query string.

Enter any combination of Boolean logic, phrases, proximity, boosts, and wildcards
into a single expression. The search returns agreements that match the query
string.

This article walks you through the steps to build an advanced search query.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents page is displayed.

**Start simple and broad**

When you enter a valid query string, the search returns a focused, predictable result set. Test each piece independently and start simple to ensure that your query string is valid and effective.

The goal of this query is to locate vendor agreements that contain non-standard limitation of liability language for legal review.

4. Perform a broad, initial search using a keyword or term. Enter the term in the search field and press the Enter key. For example, search for *liability* to view any agreements that mention "liability" in the text:

   ![A snippet of the Completed Documents page that shows a search term and the number of results.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Advanced-search-queries_start-simple_enter-search-term.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY0MzgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.w3kUHdl2eqIubJgQpFO_-AjSbv5LN2PPwbkYFcvNib8&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Completed Documents page that shows a search term and the number of results.")

   The search returns any agreement that mentions "liability" anywhere in the text.
