   Tip: Apply the Agreement Type filter to limit the scope to specific types of vendor contracts. Expand the Filters option, select the Agreement Type filter, choose the relevant types, and select Apply.

**Target a specific clause**

In the previous step, you located documents based on a broad keyword search to locate the term anywhere in a document. Next, narrow the search results to show agreements that contain an exact phrase.

5. Overwrite the existing query string and enter an exact phrase in the search field. Enter the quoted clause phrase and press the Enter key.

   For example, update the query string to:

   ```
   |  |  |
   | --- | --- |
   |  | "limitation of liability" |
   ```

   ![A snippet of the Completed Documents page that shows an exact phrase query string and the resultant document count.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Advanced-search-queries_add-party-requirement.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY0MzgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.w3kUHdl2eqIubJgQpFO_-AjSbv5LN2PPwbkYFcvNib8&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Completed Documents page that shows an exact phrase query string and the resultant document count.")

   The search results are narrowed to show agreements that contain the exact phrase.

**Capture common variations**

An exact phrase search can narrow your search results list too much by excluding common clause variants. Update your query string to capture common clause variations.

title: Build an Advanced Search Query
text:
.

**Capture common variations**

An exact phrase search can narrow your search results list too much by excluding common clause variants. Update your query string to capture common clause variations.

6. Use Boolean logic to capture common clause variations in your search query. Update the existing query string so the exact phrase is grouped with the clause variants. Separate each quoted variant with a Boolean operator.

   For example, update the query string to:

   ```
   |  |  |
   | --- | --- |
   |  | ("limitation of liability" OR "limitation of damages" OR "liability cap") |
   ```

   ![A snippet of the Completed Documents page that shows the grouped clause variations and the resultant document count.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Advanced-search-queries_add-agreement-type-requirement.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY0MzgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.w3kUHdl2eqIubJgQpFO_-AjSbv5LN2PPwbkYFcvNib8&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Completed Documents page that shows the grouped clause variations and the resultant document count.")

   The search results are expanded to include the common clause variations.

**Add proximity to find clause thresholds**

Your results list contains documents that contain the relevant clause, including clause variations. Next, update your query string to find specific clause language. The proximity search returns documents where specific words are near each other, as is typical in the clause.

For example, find agreements where liability cap language appears near references to contract values or fees. The documents that match this query requirement are likely to contain a defined cap amount.

7. Use the proximity search to locate specific clause details. Add a quoted phrase followed by ~ (tilde) N, where N is the maximum distance (number of words) allowed between terms. Use parentheses to capture variations in the clause details.

   For example, update the query string to:

   ```
   |  |  |
   | --- | --- |
   |  | ("limitation of liability" OR "limitation of damages" OR "liability cap") AND ("annual contract value"~5 OR "fees paid"~5) |
   ```

   ![A snippet of the Completed Documents page that shows the updated query string and the resultant document count.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Advanced-search-queries_add-clause-requirement_proximity.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY0MzgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.w3kUHdl2eqIubJgQpFO_-AjSbv5LN2PPwbkYFcvNib8&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Completed Documents page that shows the updated query string and the resultant document count.")

   The query returns agreements where liability cap language appears *near* references to contract values or fees. For example, when referencing a specific cap amount.

   Tip: Apply the Parties filter to focus on a specific vendor. Expand the Filters option, select the Parties filter, choose the relevant vendor, and select Apply.

**Exclude standard, boilerplate, and template agreements**

After building your query string, consider an additional rule to clean up the search results. For example, use Boolean operators to exclude standard, boilerplate, and template agreements. These agreements add irrelevant noise to the query results.

8. Use Boolean operators to exclude standard, boilerplate, and template agreements from the results. Add a NOT statement to the query string that excludes these types of agreements.

   ```
   |  |  |
   | --- | --- |
   |  | ("limitation of liability" OR "limitation of damages" OR "liability cap") AND ("annual contract value"~5 OR "fees paid"~5) NOT (template OR standard OR boilerplate) |
   ```

title: Build an Advanced Search Query
text:
|
   | --- | --- |
   |  | ("limitation of liability" OR "limitation of damages" OR "liability cap") AND ("annual contract value"~5 OR "fees paid"~5) NOT (template OR standard OR boilerplate) |
   ```

   ![A snippet of the Completed Documents page that shows the updated query string and resultant document count that excludes standard, boilerplate, and template agreements.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Advanced-search-queries_exclude-examples-tests-templates_Boolean.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY0MzgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.w3kUHdl2eqIubJgQpFO_-AjSbv5LN2PPwbkYFcvNib8&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Completed Documents page that shows the updated query string and resultant document count that excludes standard, boilerplate, and template agreements.")

   The search results are updated to exclude the standard, boilerplate, and template agreements.

   Tip: Apply the Status filter to limit the query results to active, live contracts. Expand the Filters option, select the Status filter, choose the Active option, and select Apply.

**Boost preferred terms and phrases**

Boosting is used to favor some terms, variants, or phrases over others when
ranking results. This is done without excluding any agreements outright. Review
the components of your query string and boost any relevant terms or phrases.

9. Boost the term or phrase that you want to prioritize in the search results: Add a caret (^) followed by a boost number. This weighted number indicates the relative importance of the associated term or phrase.

   For example, update the search string to boost the clause name:

   ```
   |  |  |
   | --- | --- |
   |  | ("limitation of liability"^3 OR "limitation of damages" OR "liability cap") AND ("annual contract value"~5 OR "fees paid"~5) NOT (template OR standard OR boilerplate) |
   ```

   A caret (^) and the boost number are added to the "limitation of liability" phrase:

   ![A snippet of the Completed Documents page that highlights the boosted phrase.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Advanced-search-queries_prioritize-term-phrase_Boosting.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY0MzgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.w3kUHdl2eqIubJgQpFO_-AjSbv5LN2PPwbkYFcvNib8&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Completed Documents page that highlights the boosted phrase.")

   1. Locate the term or phrase that you want to prioritize when ranking results.
   2. Add a caret (^) to the end of the quoted term or phrase. The caret (^)
      goes on the outside of the quote.
   3. Add the weighting number to indicate the relative importance of the term or phrase.

      Important: Large boosts can produce unintuitive relevance. Use small integers to start.
      The suggested range is 2–5. Boosts work within the advanced query, however,
      filters still control which agreements are included in the results. If a boosted
      term never appears in the results, it has no effect.
   4. Press the Enter key.

   The query results are re-ordered to account for the boosted term or phrase.

**Save your query string**

Use the Save option to save your query string for future use. This function saved your query string, applied filters, and column customizations. Once saved, run your search at any time to retrieve an updated list of documents that match the query string.

See the [Use Saved Searches to Find Agreement Data](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tskca2d74f2-823d-403d-84a6-d990537cd187.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to access and run a saved search, including steps to edit and delete searches.") article to learn more about running your saved searches.

10. Save your query string for future use:

title: Build an Advanced Search Query
text:
NG=enus "Learn how to access and run a saved search, including steps to edit and delete searches.") article to learn more about running your saved searches.

10. Save your query string for future use:

    ![A screenshot of the New saved search dialog box that is used to save your search criteria, filters, and column customizations for future use.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Advanced-search-queries_New-saved-serach_LoL-example.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY0MzgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.w3kUHdl2eqIubJgQpFO_-AjSbv5LN2PPwbkYFcvNib8&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the New saved search dialog box that is used to save your search criteria, filters, and column customizations for future use.")

    1. Select the Save option located to the right of the filters.

       The New saved search dialog box opens. Complete the required fields to save your search terms.
    2. Enter a unique name for the search in the Name field. A system-generated name defaults automatically. Overwrite this value as needed.
    3. Enter a Description of the search in the text-entry box, including its purpose and use.
    4. Select the Save Search option.

    The dialog box closes. Your search is available in the Saved Searches list. The page title and the breadcrumb navigation are updated to reflect the name of the saved search:

    ![A snippet of the saved search, including the query string and resultant document count.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Advanced-search-queries_saved-search-page.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY0MzgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.w3kUHdl2eqIubJgQpFO_-AjSbv5LN2PPwbkYFcvNib8&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the saved search, including the query string and resultant document count.")
11. Return to the Agreement Manager homepage. Select the Completed Documents option from the breadcrumb navigation in the top left.

    You are returned to the Agreement Manager homepage.

You have successfully built an advanced search query by combining operators, terms, and modifiers into a single query string.

title: Create Custom Dashboards from Scratch
text:
Learn how to create a dashboard from scratch by adding standard and custom reports to the dashboard.

Customizable dashboards and reports give you control over the insights that you see -
giving you access to data that's tailored for your priorities. Dashboards are
collections of standard and custom reports that show a larger picture of a particular
focus area.

When creating a custom dashboard, you can add up to 20 reports to the
dashboard. This includes both standard and custom reports. Once created, the
dashboard is displayed in the navigation pane under the Custom
Dashboards group.

This topic reviews the steps to create a
custom dashboard from scratch by choosing reports for the
dashboard.

1. Log in to your **Docusign** account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Insights from the global header.

   The Insights hub opens and the Overview is displayed.
3. Expand the Create dropdown and select the Create Dashboard option:

   ![A snippet of the reporting hub that highlights the Create dropdown with options to create reports and dashboards.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Custom-report-dashboard_Create-expanded.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwOTAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.GtwNWe8rG-aZuF_aNSBVtHNszubGydDsx20YTbUIVBM&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the reporting hub that highlights the Create dropdown with options to create reports and dashboards.")The Untitled dashboard page opens.![A screenshot of the Untitled dashboard page that shows the options to create a dashboard from scratch or from a template.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Create-custom-dashboard_from-scratch_Untitled-page.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwOTAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.GtwNWe8rG-aZuF_aNSBVtHNszubGydDsx20YTbUIVBM&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Untitled dashboard page that shows the options to create a dashboard from scratch or from a template.")
4. Enter a name for the dashboard and add a description:

   ![A snippet of the Untitled dashboard page that shows the Edit Dashboard Name icon that displays when you hover on the title.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Create-custom-dashboard_Untitled_Enter-name.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwOTAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.GtwNWe8rG-aZuF_aNSBVtHNszubGydDsx20YTbUIVBM&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Untitled dashboard page that shows the Edit Dashboard Name icon that displays when you hover on the title.")

   1. Hover your cursor over the page title
      ("Untitled") and select the Edit
      Dashboard Name (pencil) icon or select
      (click) the page title.

      The title field opens for edits.
   2. Enter a name for the dashboard in the text-entry field.

      The text-entry field closes for edits. The entered name is displayed at the top of the page.
   3. Select the + Add description option. Enter a description in the text-entry field and press [Enter].

      The text-entry field closes. The entered description is displayed below the dashboard title.

      Tip: Update the dashboard title and description at any time:
      - Hover the cursor over the information that you want to update, either the title or the description.
      - Select the Edit (pencil) icon or select (click) the title or description
        to access the text-entry field.
      - Update the title or description by typing directly into the text-entry field and press [Enter].

title: Create Custom Dashboards from Scratch
text:
(pencil) icon or select (click) the title or description
        to access the text-entry field.
      - Update the title or description by typing directly into the text-entry field and press [Enter].

      After entering a title and description, you're ready to build your custom dashboard. There are two options for creating a custom dashboard:
      - Use the Start from scratch option to get started by adding reports to this dashboard.
      - Use the Start from a template option to make a copy of an existing dashboard to get started.

        Restriction: Currently, only the Agreements and Renewals dashboards can be used as templates for creating custom dashboards.For purposes of this topic, select the Start from scratch option to begin adding reports to the dashboard. You can add custom and standard reports to the dashboard. You can add up to 20 reports to the dashboard.

**Add an existing custom or standard report to the dashboard**

5. From the Start from scratch box, expand the Add dropdown and select the Existing Report option:

   ![A screenshot of the Start from scratch section that highlights the Existing Report option in the Add dropdown.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Create-custom-dashboard_from-scratch_Add_Existing-Report.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwOTAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.GtwNWe8rG-aZuF_aNSBVtHNszubGydDsx20YTbUIVBM&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Start from scratch section that highlights the Existing Report option in the Add dropdown.")The Add Existing Report dialog box opens.![A screenshot of the Add Existing Report dialog box that shows the reports that can be added to the dashboard.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Create-custom-dashboard_from-scratch_Add-Existing-Report-dialog-bpx.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwOTAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.GtwNWe8rG-aZuF_aNSBVtHNszubGydDsx20YTbUIVBM&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Add Existing Report dialog box that shows the reports that can be added to the dashboard.")
6. Locate the report that you want to add to the dashboard and select the corresponding Add option.

   Tip: Enter a keyword in the Search for a report field or scroll to locate the report that you want to add.

   The dialog box closes and the selected report is displayed on the dashboard.
7. Continue adding existing reports to the dashboard as needed.

   ![A screenshot of a custom dashboard page that highlights options to add additional reports.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Create-custom-dashboard_from-scratch_report-added_Add-options-highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwOTAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.GtwNWe8rG-aZuF_aNSBVtHNszubGydDsx20YTbUIVBM&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of a custom dashboard page that highlights options to add additional reports.")There are two options for adding reports from the dashboard's page:
   - Select the + (add) option from either side of the dashboard and choose to add a + New Report or an Existing Report.

     Tip: The + (add) options are displayed when you hover the cursor over the report.
   - Expand the Add dropdown and select either + New Report or Existing Report from the list.

**Add a new custom report to the dashboard**

8. Expand the Add dropdown and select the + New Report option.

   The Untitled report page opens. Use this page to create a custom report, including choosing the data, metric, and visualization that matter to you.

title: Create Custom Dashboards from Scratch
text:
Add dropdown and select the + New Report option.

   The Untitled report page opens. Use this page to create a custom report, including choosing the data, metric, and visualization that matter to you.

   Note: See the [Create Custom Reports from Scratch](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tskfa65c658-fd7d-4ce8-b26b-08a9ec950aad.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to create a custom report from scratch, including choosing the relevant data, metrics, and visualization options.") topic to learn more about creating a custom report.

   ![A screenshot of the Untitled report page that shows the fields used to configure a new report.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Create-custom-report_Untitled-report-page.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwOTAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.GtwNWe8rG-aZuF_aNSBVtHNszubGydDsx20YTbUIVBM&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Untitled report page that shows the fields used to configure a new report.")
9. Complete the Data source section to choose the data that you want to use to build the report:
   1. Expand the Data source dropdown and choose the data that you want to use to build the report:
   2. Select the + Add
      filter option to refine the data. For
      example, apply filters to ensure that the
      agreement data contains custom field values.

      The All Filters dialog
      box opens. A list of Available
      Filters is displayed. This list
      includes both custom and standard fields.
   3. Locate the filter that you want to apply. If necessary, perform a keyword search to locate the filter. Enter a keyword in the Search for filters field.
   4. Expand the filter, choose a value, and select
      the Apply option. Repeat
      this step to refine the report data.
   5. Select the Save option to save the applied filters.

      The dialog box closes and the applied filters are listed in the Data Source section. The agreement count shows the number of agreements that match the applied filters in the selected data source.
10. Complete the Build your report section to choose the report metrics and grouped by details:
    1. Expand the Measure dropdown and choose an attribute to start building a report.

       Important: Currently, custom reports only support numeric values for metrics or key measures.
    2. Select the Group by option to choose a field by which to group or break down your metric.

       A dropdown opens and a list of attributes is displayed.
    3. Select the attribute or field by which you want to group the selected metric. Both custom and standard fields are available for selection. If necessary, enter a keyword in the search field to locate the relevant attribute.

       The dropdown closes and the Group by section is displayed. The Group by field reflects the selected attribute.

       The pane on the right is updated to reflect the selected metrics. For example, a bar chart might show the agreements that match the applied filters grouped by the selected Group by field value.
11. Complete the Visualization section to choose how the report is displayed:
    1. Expand the Visualization dropdown.
    2. Select the relevant chart type. Choices include:

       - Single metric (not available when the Group by option is used)
       - Bar chart
       - Pie chart
12. After refining the report so that it reflects
    the agreement data that you want to capture, save
    the report:
    1. Select the Save option
       from the top-right corner of the page.

       The Save report dialog
       box opens.
    2. Enter a name for your report in the
       Report name field. This
       field is required.
    3. Enter a description about the report's
       purpose in the Description
       field.
    4. Select the Save
       option.

title: Create Custom Dashboards from Scratch
text:
port in the
       Report name field. This
       field is required.
    3. Enter a description about the report's
       purpose in the Description
       field.
    4. Select the Save
       option.

       The dialog box closes and the new report is
       displayed on your custom dashboard.

       Tip: Your custom reports are available from
       the Custom tab under the
       Reports view. See the [View and Update Your Custom Reports](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=yuf1727801071281.html&_LANG=enus "This topic answers the question: How can I review and update my custom reports?") topic to learn how
       to view your custom reports.

    ![A screenshot of a custom dashboard that shows two reports have been added to the dashboard.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Create-custom-dashboard_from-scratch_reports-added-to-dashboard.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwOTAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.GtwNWe8rG-aZuF_aNSBVtHNszubGydDsx20YTbUIVBM&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of a custom dashboard that shows two reports have been added to the dashboard.")

    Important: If the dashboard becomes
    cluttered, you can delete any of the reports that
    are no longer relevant. Expand the
    Report Options menu for the
    report that you want to delete and select the
    Delete option:![A snippet of a dashboard page that highlights the Delete option in the Report Options menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Create-custom-dashboard_Delete-report-from-dashboard.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwOTAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.GtwNWe8rG-aZuF_aNSBVtHNszubGydDsx20YTbUIVBM&_LANG=enus "A snippet of a dashboard page that highlights the Delete option in the Report Options menu.")**Performing this action will delete the report, not just remove it from the dashboard.** Once deleted, it is no longer available to
    view or add to a dashboard.

**Organize the dashboard reports**

13. Re-organize the dashboard so it reflects the agreement insights that you want to see:

    ![A snippet of a custom dashboard page that highlights the "grab" cursor that is used to reorganize dashboard reports.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Create-custom-dashboard_from-scratch_reorganize-dashboard_Grab-cursor.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwOTAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.GtwNWe8rG-aZuF_aNSBVtHNszubGydDsx20YTbUIVBM&_LANG=enus "A snippet of a custom dashboard page that highlights the \"grab\" cursor that is used to reorganize dashboard reports.")

    1. Hover the cursor over the report chart or graph that you want to relocate.

       A grid icon displays to the left of the report name.
    2. Place the cursor over the grid icon. The cursor turns into the "grab" cursor.
    3. Drag the report to the desired location on
       the dashboard.
    4. Continue moving reports around on the
       dashboard until it reflects the agreement insights
       that you want to see.

**Add the dashboard to Your Favorites list**

14. Add the dashboard to Your Favorites list. Select the Favorite (star) icon located in the top-right corner:

title: Create Custom Dashboards from Scratch
text:
ment insights
       that you want to see.

**Add the dashboard to Your Favorites list**

14. Add the dashboard to Your Favorites list. Select the Favorite (star) icon located in the top-right corner:

    ![A snippet of a detailed report view that highlights the Favorite (star) icon.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreements-dashboard_Report-details_Favorite-icon-selected_generic.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwOTAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.GtwNWe8rG-aZuF_aNSBVtHNszubGydDsx20YTbUIVBM&_LANG=enus "A snippet of a detailed report view that highlights the Favorite (star) icon.")Your starred dashboards and reports are displayed in the Your Favorites section on the Overview dashboard.

You have successfully created a custom dashboard by adding custom and standard reports to the dashboard.

title: Create Custom Reports from Scratch
text:
Learn how to create a custom report from scratch, including choosing the relevant data, metrics, and visualization options.

Customizable dashboards and reports give you control over the insights that you see -
providing access to data that's tailored for your priorities. Dashboards are
collections of standard and custom reports that show a larger picture of a particular
focus area.

Reports are individual data visualizations, such as charts and graphs.
Create custom reports to focus on the agreement data that is most relevant to your
organization or industry.

1. Log in to your **Docusign** account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Insights from the global header.

   The Insights hub opens and the Overview is displayed.
3. Expand the Create dropdown and select the Create Report option:

   ![A snippet of the Insights page that highlights the Create dropdown with options to create reports and dashboards.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Custom-report-dashboard_Create-expanded.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwNzQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.zRNvOPGsqg4psYI6vuB-UNsI_RmR_fRJOe0SLuhI2xg&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Insights page that highlights the Create dropdown with options to create reports and dashboards.")The Untitled report page opens. Use this page to create a custom report, including choosing the data, metric, and visualization that matter to you.![A screenshot of the Untitled report page that shows the fields used to configure a new report.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Create-custom-report_Untitled-report-page.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwNzQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.zRNvOPGsqg4psYI6vuB-UNsI_RmR_fRJOe0SLuhI2xg&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Untitled report page that shows the fields used to configure a new report.")
4. Complete the Data source section to choose the data that you want to use to build the report:

   ![A snippet of the Untitled report page that shows the Data source dropdown and Add filter option.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Create-custom-report_Untitled-report_Data-source-section.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwNzQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.zRNvOPGsqg4psYI6vuB-UNsI_RmR_fRJOe0SLuhI2xg&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Untitled report page that shows the Data source dropdown and Add filter option.")

   1. Expand the Data source dropdown and choose the data that you want to use to build the report:
   2. Select the + Add
      filter option to refine the data. For
      example, apply filters to ensure that the
      agreement data contains custom field values.

      The All Filters dialog
      box opens. A list of Available
      Filters is displayed. This list
      includes both custom and standard fields.
   3. Locate the filter that you want to apply. If necessary, perform a keyword search to locate the filter. Enter a keyword in the Search for filters field.
   4. Expand the filter, choose a value, and select
      the Apply option. Repeat
      this step to refine the report data.
   5. Select the Save option to save the applied filters.

      The dialog box closes and the applied filters are listed in the Data Source section. The agreement count shows the number of agreements that match the applied filters in the selected data source.
5. Complete the Build your report section to choose the report metrics and grouped by details:

title: Create Custom Reports from Scratch
text:
greement count shows the number of agreements that match the applied filters in the selected data source.
5. Complete the Build your report section to choose the report metrics and grouped by details:

   ![A snippet of the Untitled report page that shows the Measure dropdown and Group by option.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Create-custom-report_Untitled-report_Build-your-report-section.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwNzQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.zRNvOPGsqg4psYI6vuB-UNsI_RmR_fRJOe0SLuhI2xg&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Untitled report page that shows the Measure dropdown and Group by option.")

   1. Expand the Measure dropdown and choose an attribute to start building a report.

      Important: Currently, custom reports only support numeric values for metrics or key measures.
   2. Select the Group by option to choose a field by which to group or break down your metric.

      A dropdown opens and a list of attributes is displayed.
   3. Select the attribute or field by which you want to group the selected metric. Both custom and standard fields are available for selection. If necessary, enter a keyword in the search field to locate the relevant attribute.

      The dropdown closes and the Group by section is displayed. The Group by field reflects the selected attribute.

      The pane on the right is updated to reflect the selected metrics. For example, a bar chart might show the agreements that match the applied filters grouped by the selected Group by field value.
6. Complete the Visualization section to choose how the report is displayed:

   ![A snippet of the Untitled report page that shows the Visualization dropdown.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Create-custom-report_Untitled-report_Visualization-section.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwNzQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.zRNvOPGsqg4psYI6vuB-UNsI_RmR_fRJOe0SLuhI2xg&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Untitled report page that shows the Visualization dropdown.")

   1. Expand the Visualization dropdown.
   2. Select the relevant chart-type. Choices include:

      - Single metric (not available when the Group by option is used)
      - Bar chart
      - Pie chart

   The pane on the right shows what the report chart or graph looks like after choosing the relevant data, metrics, and visualization option.

   Continue refining the report until it reflects the agreement data that you want to capture.
7. Select the Save option from the top-right corner of the page.

   The Save report dialog box opens.![A screenshot of the Save report dialog box that shows the Report name and Description fields.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Create-custom-report_Save-report-dialog-box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwNzQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.zRNvOPGsqg4psYI6vuB-UNsI_RmR_fRJOe0SLuhI2xg&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Save report dialog box that shows the Report name and Description fields.")
8. Enter a name for your report in the Report name field. This field is required.
9. Enter a description about the report's purpose in the Description field.
10. Select the Save option.

    The dialog box closes and the customized report is displayed.

    Tip: Your custom reports are available from the Custom tab under the Reports view. See the [View and Update Your Custom Reports](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=yuf1727801071281.html&_LANG=enus "This topic answers the question: How can I review and update my custom reports?") topic to learn how to view your custom reports.

title: Create Custom Reports from Scratch
text:
em?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=yuf1727801071281.html&_LANG=enus "This topic answers the question: How can I review and update my custom reports?") topic to learn how to view your custom reports.

    The custom report can now be added to a custom dashboard.
11. From the report view, you can:

    ![A snippet of the newly created custom report that highlights the various report management options.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Create-custom-report_Report-view_options-highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwNzQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.zRNvOPGsqg4psYI6vuB-UNsI_RmR_fRJOe0SLuhI2xg&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the newly created custom report that highlights the various report management options.")

    - Apply additional filters to the report.
    - Download the report.
    - Edit the report.
    - Save updates to the report.
    - Delete the custom report.
    - Add the report to Your Favorites list.
    - Subscribe to receive email reports notifications.

You have successfully created a custom report from scratch, including choosing relevant data, metrics, and visualization options.

title: Create Custom Reports from Suggested Reports (Beta)
heading_path: Create Custom Reports from Suggested Reports (Beta)
text:
Learn how to create a custom report from one of the reports in the Suggested Reports (Beta) section on the Overview dashboard.

The Suggested Reports (Beta) section on the Overview dashboard provides AI-generated custom report notifications. These suggestions are auto-generated based on the custom fields in your account. When fields (custom or standard) are located in at least five agreements, they are included when generating custom report suggestions.

This topic reviews the steps to create a custom report from one of the reports in the Suggested Reports section.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Insights from the global header.

   The Insights hub opens and the Overview dashboard is displayed.
3. Go to the Suggested Reports section and review all the custom report recommendations:

   ![A snippet of the Overview dashboard that shows the Suggested Reports (Beta) section, including the scroll bar to view all the recommendations.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Create-custom-report_Suggested-Reports_scrollbar_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwNTMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.LXB6P0f6pC-7hJ8gSkqIBOj_6_GAa-yO_ZQW-FQBVcA&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Overview dashboard that shows the Suggested Reports (Beta) section, including the scroll bar to view all the recommendations.")
4. Select the report heading for the report that you want to create.

   The detailed report view opens for the selected report.
5. Apply filters to customize the report further:

   ![A snippet of the detailed report view for a suggested custom report that highlights the filter options.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Create-custom-report_from-Suggested-Reports_Apply-filters_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwNTMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.LXB6P0f6pC-7hJ8gSkqIBOj_6_GAa-yO_ZQW-FQBVcA&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the detailed report view for a suggested custom report that highlights the filter options.")

   For example, sort the report by Sets, Parties, or Agreement Type. Use the All Filters option to apply additional filters to the report.

   1. Expand the filter that you wan to apply.
   2. Choose a filter value or select a date range.
   3. Select the Apply option.
6. Customize the columns in the table:

   Important: The Customize columns function only applies to the data that is displayed in the agreement list table. When downloading or exporting the report, all fields are included in the output file. This includes both custom and standard fields.

   ![A screenshot of the Customize columns dialog box that shows the columns you can choose to display in the agreements table.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Party-hub_Overview-dashboard_Customize-columns.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwNTMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.LXB6P0f6pC-7hJ8gSkqIBOj_6_GAa-yO_ZQW-FQBVcA&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Customize columns dialog box that shows the columns you can choose to display in the agreements table.")

   1. Select the Customize columns (toggle) icon.

      The Customize columns dialog box opens. These fields represent the columns in the table.
   2. Select the toggles to turn the associated field on (blue) or off (white).
   3. Re-order the columns as needed. Hover the cursor over the Reorder (dots) icon for the column that you want to move and drag it to the appropriate location.
   4. Select the Save option.

title: Create Custom Reports from Suggested Reports (Beta)
heading_path: Create Custom Reports from Suggested Reports (Beta)
text:
(white).
   3. Re-order the columns as needed. Hover the cursor over the Reorder (dots) icon for the column that you want to move and drag it to the appropriate location.
   4. Select the Save option.

   Tip: If the Status column is not displayed by default, use the Show or Hide Fields (toggle) option to add it to the table. From the Show or Hide Field dialog box, turn on the Status toggle and drag the field to the appropriate location. Be sure to Save your changes.

   Continue to apply filters and customize the table until the report reflects the relevant data.
7. Save the report. Expand the More actions (three dots) menu in the top right and select the Save option:

   ![A snippet of a detailed report view for a suggested custom report that highlights the Save option in the More actions (three dots) menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Create-custom-report_from-Suggested-Reports_Save_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwNTMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.LXB6P0f6pC-7hJ8gSkqIBOj_6_GAa-yO_ZQW-FQBVcA&_LANG=enus "A snippet of a detailed report view for a suggested custom report that highlights the Save option in the More actions (three dots) menu.")

   The Save report dialog box opens. The Report name
   and Description fields default based on the selected
   suggestion.

   ![A screenshot of th Save report dialog box that shows the custom report name and description.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Create-custom-report_from-Suggested-Reports_Save-report-modal.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwNTMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.LXB6P0f6pC-7hJ8gSkqIBOj_6_GAa-yO_ZQW-FQBVcA&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of th Save report dialog box that shows the custom report name and description.")
8. Update the Report name field or accept the default value. This field defaults to the name of the suggested report.
9. Update the Description field or accept the default
   value.
10. Select the Save option.

    The dialog box closes and the custom report is saved. Your new report is available from the Custom tab under the Reports view:

    ![A snippet of the Reports view that shows the Custom tab, including custom reports and the View option.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Create-custom-report_from-Suggested-Reports_Report-saved-to-Custom-tab.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwNTMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.LXB6P0f6pC-7hJ8gSkqIBOj_6_GAa-yO_ZQW-FQBVcA&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Reports view that shows the Custom tab, including custom reports and the View option.")

    Custom reports can be updated or deleted at any time from the report details page. See the [View and Update Your Custom Reports](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=yuf1727801071281.html&_LANG=enus "This topic answers the question: How can I review and update my custom reports?") topic to learn about editing options for your custom reports.

You have successfully created a custom report from a recommendation in the Suggested Reports section on the Overview dashboard.

title: Install Cloud Storage Apps for Account Administrators
heading_path: Install Cloud Storage Apps for Account Administrators
text:
Learn how to install various cloud storage providers from the Docusign App Center. This topic answers the question: How do I install cloud storage providers so users can upload documents to Agreement Manager?

When performing single or bulk uploads, users can choose to upload documents located in various cloud storage locations. Cloud storage apps are installed from the Docusign App Center. This task is completed by an account administrator.

Important: If the apps were previously installed from the Docusign App Center, you must uninstall and reinstall them. Due to versioning issues, backward compatibility is not currently possible.

Note: See the [Docusign Organization Administrators](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=rrf1583359212854&topicId=god1583359152535.html) pages to learn more about the required permissions.

You can install cloud provider apps from three locations:

- Docusign App Center main page:
  - Select the app tile.
  - Select Install.
- Cloud storage provider's detail page: Select Install from any detail page.
- Select File dropdown:
  - Select the Upload arrow.
  - Expand the Select File dropdown.
  - Select the install option for the relevant app

1. Go to the  [Docusign App Center](https://apps.docusign.com/app-center).

   ![A screenshot of the Docusign App Center main page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Docusign-App-Center_Main-page.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY3NTgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.t23RlAsqTbyEg0DdvVIUetJ_k_WiXcQsxjDy7gt3M1U&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Docusign App Center main page.")
2. Select Log In located in the top-right corner of the App Center.
3. Log in to Docusign using account administrator credentials.

   The Docusign App Center main page opens.
4. Locate and select the app for the cloud storage provider that you want to install.

   The app's detail page displays.
5. Review the app's overview, requirements, and resources.
6. Select Install.

   If the app has authentication or account connection requirements, you are prompted to install and authorize the connection.
7. If prompted, select Install and Authorize.

   You are connected to the selected cloud storage app.
8. Review the information on the app's detail page and select Continue.
9. If the app requires authorization, select Connect Account.

   The app is connected to your Docusign account. Next, authorize the app for use in Docusign.
10. Enter your credentials for the app and select Continue.

    The cloud storage provider is now ready to use for document uploads.

title: Add Agreements to Sets Based on Applied Filters
heading_path: Add Agreements to Sets Based on Applied Filters
text:
Learn how to apply filters to locate agreements to add to an agreement set. This topic
answers the question: How do I control access to agreements based on attribute
values?

If an agreement set was created without smart filters, agreements can be added to the set
manually. This is done by performing search queries and applying filters to locate
the agreements that you want to add to a set.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign
   Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the
   AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed.

   Tip: Select the Filters option to apply filters to the agreement list table::![A snippet of the Completed Documents page that highlights the Filters option.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-documents-view_Filter-pills_highlighting-All-Filters.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTczNDMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.U3tlKTKLKeTVUtPNrysMg-r1MDeSld20svyO93UE-hc&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Completed Documents page that highlights the Filters option.")
4. Perform a search query or use the available filters to locate agreements to add
   to an agreement set.
   1. Enter a keyword or search string in the search field and select
      Enter.
   2. Apply filters to locate agreements based on attribute values:

      1. Expand the Filters option and locate the filter that you want to apply.
      2. Select the relevant filter. For example, locate agreements for a specific party, agreement type, or
         time period.
      3. Select a filter value or enter a date range if applicable.
      4. Select the Apply option.
   3. Repeat this step until the relevant agreements are displayed in the agreement list
      table.

   The agreements that matched the search criteria and filters are displayed in the table.
5. Select one or more agreements to add to the agreement set:
   - Select individual agreements to update.
   - Select the Choose all checkbox from the header row to select all the agreements on the page.

   Tip: You can select all the agreements in the results list, not just the agreements on the first page of results. The Selected dropdown displays a counter to indicate the number of agreements that are currently selected. Expand the dropdown and select the Select all that match your filters option:![A snippet of the agreement list table that highlights the Select all that match your filters option in the Selected dropdown.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-documents_Selected-dropdown_Select-all-that-match-your-filters.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTczNDMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.U3tlKTKLKeTVUtPNrysMg-r1MDeSld20svyO93UE-hc&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the agreement list table that highlights the Select all that match your filters option in the Selected dropdown.")All the agreements in the table are selected.

   Important: When you use the Select all that match your filters option, the processing time is dependent on the
   volume of agreements that match the applied filters.

   Restriction: The
   Select all that match your filters option only
   works when applying filters. If you performed a keyword search or if no
   active filter is applied, this option is disabled.

   Additional options are displayed when one or more agreements are selected from the list, including the Organize option.
6. Expand the Organize dropdown and select the relevant set for the agreements:

title: Add Agreements to Sets Based on Applied Filters
heading_path: Add Agreements to Sets Based on Applied Filters
text:
Additional options are displayed when one or more agreements are selected from the list, including the Organize option.
6. Expand the Organize dropdown and select the relevant set for the agreements:

   ![A screenshot of the expanded Organize dropdown that shows a list of sets to which the agreements can be added.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-documents_Organize-expanded_Add-to-sets_Select-set.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTczNDMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.U3tlKTKLKeTVUtPNrysMg-r1MDeSld20svyO93UE-hc&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the expanded Organize dropdown that shows a list of sets to which the agreements can be added.")

   Tip: Select + Create New Set to add
   the agreements to a new agreement set. Enter a name and description in the
   New agreement set dialog box and select
   Create. The dialog box closes and the new set is
   available for selection from the Organize
   dropdown.
7. Select the Add option.

   The dropdown closes and the selected agreements are added to the set. The Completed Documents view opens and while the agreements are no
   longer selected, the filters are still applied.

   Tip: Clear the selected filters before
   performing another search query. Select the Clear (x) icon for the filter that you want to reset.

You have successfully added agreements to a set based on the applied filters.

title: View Expiring Agreements for a Specific Party
heading_path: View Expiring Agreements for a Specific Party
text:
This topic answers the question: How do I report on expiring agreements for a specific party? Learn how to access the Upcoming expiring agreements report and use filters to view expiring agreements for a specific party.

The Upcoming expiring agreements  chart shows an aggregated count of your expiring agreements, grouped by expiration date. By default, the chart reflects expiring agreements over the next six months. You can filter this report by time period, sets, parties, and agreement type. These filters can be applied from both the dashboard view and the report view.

Note: **Downloading reports to CSV files**

When downloading reports to CSV files from the Insights reporting hub, there is an agreement limit of 25,000 agreements for all IAM reports. This count includes your custom reports.

**Suggested workaround:** Adjust the report filters to run the report in batches until all the relevant agreements are downloaded. For example, run the report in batches according to varying date ranges. Once downloaded, combine the data into one CSV file.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Insights from the global header.

   The Insights hub opens and the Overview is displayed.
3. Select Agreements from the navigation pane on the left.

   The Agreements Dashboard view opens.

   Tip: Filters can be applied at the dashboard-level and the report-level. For example, if you are searching for generic agreement data, apply the filters at the dashboard level.

   In this example, apply filters at the report-level.
4. View the report details for the Upcoming expiring agreements chart. Select the chart title or expand the More actions (three dots) menu and select the View Report option.

   The detailed report view opens and the Upcoming expiring agreements report is displayed. By default, the report reflects agreements that are expiring over the next six months.
5. Filter the report for a specific party and date range.

   ![A snippet of the Upcoming expiring agreements report that highlights the report filters.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-dashboard_Upcoming-expiring-agreements_filters.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY5NDIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.Di4HfqhS5KgLrWC9vnWXeZ7sHYsdoJAcOBG72rRmboM&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Upcoming expiring agreements report that highlights the report filters.")

   1. Expand (click) the Expiration Date filter, choose the relevant time period, and select the Apply option. For example, select the Next 12 Months option for longer range planning.

      Tip: Use the Custom Date Range option to select a specific date range.
   2. Expand (click) the Parties filter, choose the relevant party, and select the Apply option.

   The report updates automatically. The agreements that match the applied filters are reflected in the chart and displayed in the table.
6. Download or save the report:

   ![A snippet of the report view that highlights the More actions (three dots) menu used to download or save a report.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Report-view_Dropdown-expanded.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY5NDIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.Di4HfqhS5KgLrWC9vnWXeZ7sHYsdoJAcOBG72rRmboM&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the report view that highlights the More actions (three dots) menu used to download or save a report.")

   - Expand the More actions (three dots) menu in the top right and select Download.

     The report is downloaded to your local Downloads folder.
   - Expand the dropdown in the top right and select the Save option. The Save Report dialog box opens.

title: View Expiring Agreements for a Specific Party
heading_path: View Expiring Agreements for a Specific Party
text:
he top right and select Download.

     The report is downloaded to your local Downloads folder.
   - Expand the dropdown in the top right and select the Save option. The Save Report dialog box opens.

     - Enter a unique name in the Report name field. This field defaults to the name of the selected report.
     - Enter a description about the report's purpose in the Description field.
     - Select the Save option.
7. Set up one-time and recurring email reports for yourself and others. Select the Manage Notification (bell) icon and complete the Create subscription dialog box:

   ![A screenshot of the Create subscription dialog box that is used to subscribe to report notifications.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Create-Subscription.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY5NDIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.Di4HfqhS5KgLrWC9vnWXeZ7sHYsdoJAcOBG72rRmboM&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Create subscription dialog box that is used to subscribe to report notifications.")

   Note: When you create a subscription, you have the option to add individual users and groups. Once created, the subscription is displayed in the Manage report subscriptions dialog box for any individual users who were added. Currently, the subscription is not visible to users if they were added as part of a group.

   See the [Subscribe to Receive Report Notifications](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=wfw1734545937270.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to set up one-time or recurring email reports for yourself or others to receive notifications about your obligations, renewals, or agreements.") topic to learn more about this feature.
8. Add the report to Your Favorites list. Select the Favorite (star) icon located in the top-right corner:

   ![A snippet of a detailed report view that highlights the Favorite (star) icon.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreements-dashboard_Report-details_Favorite-icon-selected_generic.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY5NDIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.Di4HfqhS5KgLrWC9vnWXeZ7sHYsdoJAcOBG72rRmboM&_LANG=enus "A snippet of a detailed report view that highlights the Favorite (star) icon.")Your starred dashboards and reports are displayed in the Your Favorites section on the Overview dashboard.
9. Return to the Agreements Dashboard. Select the Agreements Dashboard option from the breadcrumb navigation at the top of the page.

   The Agreements Dashboard view opens.
10. Repeat this process to view, download, or save other agreement reports.

title: Use Case: View Auto-Renewal Agreements with Upcoming Expiration Dates
heading_path: Use Case: View Auto-Renewal Agreements with Upcoming Expiration Dates
text:
Learn how to use the Renewals dashboard to view expiring soon and auto-renewal agreements.

As a contract manager, there are a few reasons to stay on top of your upcoming renewals, such as:

- To avoid paying for something that you don’t need anymore
- To negotiate the best possible deal for the organization

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Insights from the global header.

   The Docusign Insights page opens.
3. Select Renewals from the navigation pane on the left.

   The Renewals dashboard view opens. Upcoming renewals for the next 12 months are displayed by default.
4. Expand the Expiration Date filter and select the time period for the expiring agreements.
   - Select one of the predefined time period options. Choices include: Today, Next 7 Days, Next 14 Days, and Next 30 Days.
   - Select Custom Date Range. The dialog box updates to show the From and To fields, as well as the Between, Before, and After options. Select an option from the top of the dialog box and enter a date or date range in the From and To fields.
5. Select Apply.

   The agreements that match the filters are displayed in the Renewals dashboard.
6. Select Automatic renewal from the Renewal Type filter.
   1. Expand the Renewal Type filter.
   2. Select the Automatic renewal option.
   3. Select Apply.

   The Renewals dashboard updates to reflect the agreements with auto-renewal that are expiring within the applied date range.
7. Review the agreements in the table.

You have successfully filtered the Renewals dashboard to display auto-renewal agreements with upcoming expiration dates.

title: IAM Agreement Type and Field Management
heading_path: IAM Agreement Type and Field Management
text:
Read about how agreement type management is being used to unify the way Docusign capitalizes on agreement types and associated fields. This topic
answers the question: What is agreement type management and how does it benefit
me?

*Agreement Type and Field Management* helps contract managers and administrators standardize how agreements are classified. When agreements are ingested or uploaded to Agreement Manager, key data points are surfaced based on the identified agreement type. The Agreement Types and Fields features provide a central location to manage your standard and custom agreement types and fields.

Key benefits of agreement type and field management:

- Single source of truth for agreement data:

  - A central place to configure and govern your agreement types and fields.
  - Consistent agreement type and fields definitions across the IAM platform.
- Structured, reusable metadata:
  - Standard fields attached to each agreement type.
  - These fields provide a ready-made data model
    to capture agreements in a structured, comparable
    way.
- Tailored data through custom types and fields:

  - Custom agreement types and fields help to capture the attributes that are not covered by the
    standard, predefined fields.
  - Organizations can use custom types and fields to capture business-specific agreements and attributes (fields).
- Operational consistency and governance:

  - Fields can be added to, edited, or removed from agreement types.
  - Custom types can be created, updated, or removed.
  - Documents can be reprocessed when agreement types and fields change, keeping downstream analytics and workflows accurate over time.

title: IAM Agreement Type and Field Management
heading_path: IAM Agreement Type and Field Management
text:
This functionality is accessed from the Settings
(gear) menu on the Agreement Manager homepage:

![A screenshot of the Settings (gear) menu on the Agreement Manager homepage that highlights the Fields and Agreement Types options.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Manage-Agreement-Types_More-actions-menu_expanded.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc0NDEsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.ll9QoDzcOinLOVph2ZmhR1ICDkrCmjwLtQ6hYeDF10g&_LANG=enus)

The top portion of the dropdown displays options for managing your agreement data, including the Fields and Agreement Types options. See the [IAM Agreement Types Manager](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=con22821364-8601-4fc5-b768-e19f07c43462.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the Agreement Types manager, including its purpose, use, and role within the Intelligent Agreement Management (IAM) platform.") and [IAM Fields Manager](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=con7092ce57-207f-4d06-9228-c7bdd2ccff2a.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the Fields manager, including its purpose, use, and role within the Intelligent Agreement Management (IAM) platform.") topics to learn more about these features.

title: IAM Agreement Type and Field Management
heading_path: IAM Agreement Type and Field Management
text:
Docusign AI models are designed to detect agreement types by considering the title and content of ingested and uploaded files. This includes recognizing synonyms and phrase variations.

For example, "Consulting Agreements" are often seen as "Advisory Agreements" or "Consulting Services Agreements" and "Contractor Agreements" as "Independent Contractor Agreements".

If the AI model does not recognize the agreement type, it is assigned to the "Miscellaneous" type.

If necessary, create a custom agreement type to meet your organizational needs. Once created, you can bulk update agreements to the new agreement type. See these topics for more information:

- [Create a New Agreement Type](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=gke1727282238086.html)
- [Bulk Update the Agreement Type for Selected Agreements](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=uwa1742362757142.html)

title: IAM Agreement Type and Field Management
heading_path: IAM Agreement Type and Field Management
text:
The standard Docusign-provided agreement types are pre-populated with the relevant, standard fields (attributes). These standard fields are automatically added to the agreement type to provide structure.

![A screenshot of a Docusign-provided agreement type that shows the standard fields that were automatically added to the type.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-type-management_add-custom-field-to-standard-agreement.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc0NDEsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.ll9QoDzcOinLOVph2ZmhR1ICDkrCmjwLtQ6hYeDF10g&_LANG=enus)

Standard and custom fields can be added to agreement types based on your organization's business needs.

See the [Standard Fields for Agreement and Non-Agreement Types](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=smm1727282189861.html&_LANG=enus "This topic answers the question: What fields are associated with Docusign-provided agreement types? Look up the standard fields that are associated with each agreement and non-agreement type.") topic for a list of standard fields for each Docusign-provided agreement type.

title: IAM Agreement Type and Field Management
heading_path: IAM Agreement Type and Field Management
text:
Custom agreement types and fields capture specific, unique information that isn't covered by standard agreement fields and agreement types. Organizations can use custom agreement types and fields to tailor their extracted data points for varying business needs.

![A screenshot of the Add a field dialog box that is used to add fields to your custom agreement types.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-type-managment_Custom-agreement-type_add-a-field-dialog-box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc0NDEsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.ll9QoDzcOinLOVph2ZmhR1ICDkrCmjwLtQ6hYeDF10g&_LANG=enus)

Custom and standard fields can be added to multiple agreement types at a time, including custom and standard types:

![A screenshot of the Add a field dialog box that highlights the selected agreement types to which the field will be added.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-type-management_Add-field_Multiple-types_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc0NDEsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.ll9QoDzcOinLOVph2ZmhR1ICDkrCmjwLtQ6hYeDF10g&_LANG=enus)

See the [Custom Extractions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=yrx1747083411531.html&_LANG=enus "Read about custom extractions, including how these data points bring value to our organization.") article to learn more about using custom agreement types and fields.

Important: When custom agreement types are no longer relevant, they can be deleted. ![A snippet of the Agreement Types view that highlights the Delete option for a custom agreement type.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-type-management_Delete-custom-agreement-type_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc0NDEsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.ll9QoDzcOinLOVph2ZmhR1ICDkrCmjwLtQ6hYeDF10g&_LANG=enus)Once deleted, the agreement type cannot be recovered. The existing documents that are associated with the agreement type remain intact, however, the type is not surfaced in new agreements.

Example use cases for your custom agreement types and fields:

- **Technology contracts** may include custom fields for *Hardware specifications*, *Software license terms*, or *Maintenance schedule*.
- **Consulting agreements** may include custom fields for *Engagement scope* and *Deliverables timeline*.
- **Real Estate leases** may include custom fields for *Security deposit amount*, *Pet policy details*, or *Parking options*.

Important:

Customers do not need to consent to AI training to use this feature. When customers create custom
agreement types and fields, their data is used as **input** to the AI model to help generate
results for their use. This process does not involve AI training.

If a customer has given consent to sharing data for AI
training, anonymized data may be used for training Docusign AI models. This may
include anonymized data from custom extractions.

See the [Docusign FAQs for AI](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fzd1707173174972&topicId=uss1707173279973.html) topic for more information about how
Docusign uses data for AI training.

title: IAM Agreement Type and Field Management
heading_path: IAM Agreement Type and Field Management
text:
When uploading or ingesting agreements to Agreement Manager, if an agreement does not match an existing standard or custom type, it is identified as Miscellaneous. If 10 or more similar agreements are identified as Miscellaneous, a custom agreement type is suggested.

For example, 10 charter agreements were uploaded and identified as *Miscellaneous*. The agreements are recognized as being similar and a custom agreement type, *Charter Agreement*, is suggested:

![A snippet of the agreement list table that shows a suggested custom agreement type for a Miscellaneous document.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Autosuggest_New-suggestion-available_dialog-box_charter-agreement-example.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc0NDEsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.ll9QoDzcOinLOVph2ZmhR1ICDkrCmjwLtQ6hYeDF10g&_LANG=enus)

Use the Create option and follow the steps to create a new custom agreement type. See the [Use Auto-Suggestion to Create Custom Agreement Types](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tsk2093dc24-1da8-4393-b4cd-526990bc7118.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to create a custom agreement type from the auto-suggestion feature.") article to learn more about using this feature.

Once created, the custom type is applied to all the relevant documents that were previously identified as Miscellaneous. Depending on the processing queue, it may take an hour or longer for the agreements to reflect the new type.

You can filter the agreement list table using the new custom type:

![A snippet of the agreement list table that shows the new custom type in the Agreement Type filter.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Autosuggest_Agreement-Type-filter_Charter-Agreement-example.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc0NDEsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.ll9QoDzcOinLOVph2ZmhR1ICDkrCmjwLtQ6hYeDF10g&_LANG=enus)

Important: The agreements contain attributes and field values that were surfaced with AI. Complete a data review to validate these values, including the Agreement Type. See the [Validate Extracted Field Values (Complete a Data Review)](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=yqq1702945693043.html&_LANG=enus "This topic answers the question: How can I make sure that the suggested attribute (field) values are accurate? Learn how to complete a data review to verify suggested field values.") article to learn more about validating AI-surfaced values.

title: IAM Agreement Type and Field Management
heading_path: IAM Agreement Type and Field Management
text:
By default, all users can view custom agreement types and fields that are surfaced in their agreements. This includes other agreements to which they have access.

Administrators and contract managers create and manage the agreement types and fields for their account. For non-administrator users, this feature requires you to have Organize and manage all agreements access to Agreement Manager agreements and functionality.

See the [Agreement Manager Permissions, Profiles, and Roles](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=eut1712157553977.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the available Agreement Manager permissions, including how users and groups are granted access to Agreement Manager agreements and example roles for each permission.") article to learn more about Agreement Manager access levels.

title: IAM Agreement Type and Field Management
heading_path: IAM Agreement Type and Field Management
text:
When the total number of custom agreement types and fields reaches its limit for the account,
warning messages are displayed. The + Add
Fields and + Agreement
Type options are disabled.

| **Limit type** | **Limit** |
| --- | --- |
| Number of custom agreement types per account | 200 |
| Number of fields that can be added to an agreement type | 100 |

Note: See the [Understanding Your IAM and eSignature Plan Allowances](https://www.docusign.com/iam/plan-allowances) page to learn more about the limits for each plan type.

title: IAM Agreement Type and Field Management
heading_path: IAM Agreement Type and Field Management
text:
"Non-agreement" types are used to categorize non-contractual documents that are uploaded and synced to Agreement Manager. Although not related to contractual agreements, these non-agreement types are associated with relevant fields (attributes). When a document is identified as a specific non-agreement type, the associated fields are surfaced within the file.

The supported non-agreement types are:

- Form: often a tool to collect information
- Policy: generally outlines guidelines or rules
- Application: usually a request for consideration
- Certificate: typically a proof of something, like completion
- Report: a summary of findings or data
- Notice: often a communication or information
- Plan: usually outlines strategies or proposals
- Invoice: a request for payment
- Handbook: guidelines or policies
- Summary: condensed information
- Non-agreement/Other

All non-agreement types are associated with the basic attributes or fields. For example, the
Agreement Type, Effective
Date, Parties, and
Title. Depending on the non-agreement
type, additional date and payment fields can also be associated with
the type. For example, the Invoice type
includes fields related to contract value, expiration and execution
dates, and payment terms.

Important: All the custom and standard fields that are associated with an agreement type are surfaced in the relevant documents. However, not all non-agreement types are surfaced in your documents. This includes the custom and standard fields that are associated with the type. These non-agreement types are not surfaced during agreement processing: Certificate, Handbook, Notice, and Policy.

See the [Standard Fields for Agreement and Non-Agreement Types](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=smm1727282189861.html&_LANG=enus "This topic answers the question: What fields are associated with Docusign-provided agreement types? Look up the standard fields that are associated with each agreement and non-agreement type.") topic for a list of fields that are auto-associated
with the standard agreement types.

title: IAM Agreement Type and Field Management
heading_path: IAM Agreement Type and Field Management
text:
As new or updated AI models are released, the previously ingested agreements can be processed again to surface these data points. Depending on the type of new insights that are available, users can choose to:

- Opt in for reprocessing from the Track obligations badge (obligation-related attributes and insights)
- Reprocess all the outdated agreements for the account (new or updated AI model)
- Reprocess selected agreements (custom agreement types and fields)

Note: See the [Agreement Manager Reprocessing Experience](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=con44590246-0f0e-43b4-9692-caadb57335ac.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about the Agreement Manager reprocessing experience, including its purpose, trigger points, and job size considerations.") article to learn more about these reprocessing features.

Processing happens in the background. While it does not impact your current experience, processing may take some time depending on the number and size of agreements. Once started, processing cannot be canceled.

title: Review and Merge Duplicate Parties
text:
Learn how to merge parties to ensure data integrity across your organization. This topic answers the question: Where can I review duplicate party suggestions and merge parties that are the same?

Organization party names are standardized at ingestion and duplicate party records are consolidated. This process reduces the number of potential duplicate parties, however, duplicate parties can exist. For example, if the consolidated parties have different display names, they are added to the Review Duplicate Parties list.

Tip: See the [Duplicate Party Management and Standardization](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=ssf1712255476566.html&_LANG=enus "This topic answers the question: What does party management in Agreement Manager entail? Read about the party management features designed to help you manage parties that have similar names. For example, party standardization and consolidation at the point of ingestion.") article to learn more about the party normalization and deduplication process.
