Use the Review and Merge option to review and merge duplicate parties. This task is completed by users with Organize and manage all agreements access to Agreement Manager, such as contract managers.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Parties from the navigation pane on the left.

   The Parties page opens.
4. Select the Review and Merge option from the banner message at the top of the page:

   ![A snippet of the Parties view that highlights the Review and Merge option from the banner message.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Parties-page_Review-and-Merge_highlighted-in-banner.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc3NzcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.qiqirVAiwB3jp7L8J0UTdHHErq831FuVByF_mghev68&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Parties view that highlights the Review and Merge option from the banner message.")

   The Review Duplicate Parties page opens and the Are these the same party? view is displayed. Potential duplicates are listed in the table.

   Note: You will not lose any agreements if you merge a party.

   ![A screenshot of the Review Duplicate Parties page that lists potential duplicate parties.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Manage-Parties_Are-these-the-same-party-dialog-box_suggestion-highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc3NzcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.qiqirVAiwB3jp7L8J0UTdHHErq831FuVByF_mghev68&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Review Duplicate Parties page that lists potential duplicate parties.")
5. Locate the parties that you want to review and select the associated Review option.

   Tip: Enter a party name in the Search by Party Name field to merge specific parties.

   The Review Duplicate Parties page updates and the Select
   parties to merge and the Choose a
   name sections are displayed.![A screenshot of the Review Duplicate Parties page that shows the suggested duplicate parties and naming options.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Manage-Parties_Review-Duplicate-Parties.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc3NzcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.qiqirVAiwB3jp7L8J0UTdHHErq831FuVByF_mghev68&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Review Duplicate Parties page that shows the suggested duplicate parties and naming options.")

title: Review and Merge Duplicate Parties
text:
mIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.qiqirVAiwB3jp7L8J0UTdHHErq831FuVByF_mghev68&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Review Duplicate Parties page that shows the suggested duplicate parties and naming options.")

   You are prompted to select parties to merge and choose a party name. The potential duplicates are listed in the Select parties to merge section. They are selected by default.
6. Choose the parties that you want to merge from the Select parties to merge section.
   - Deselect the checkboxes for the parties that you do not want to merge. This step is applicable when there are three or more possible duplicate parties.

     Tip: When there are only two suggested duplicate parties, the checkboxes are grayed out.
   - Select the Dismiss All option from the bottom-right corner if the suggested parties are unrelated and should not be merged.

   Important: Agreement Manager recalls the parties that you didn't select
   and will not suggest them again with this group.
   If you select the Dismiss
   All option, you will not see this
   specific combination of parties in future
   suggestions.
7. Select a preferred party name from the Choose a name section. Expand the Party name dropdown and select the preferred name from the list.

   Tip: The merged parties are consolidated under one name. Thi name is used throughout the Intelligent Agreement Management (IAM) platform. For example, this name is displayed in the Parties filter and column on the Completed Documents page in Agreement Manager.
8. Select the Merge Parties option from the bottom right. The button label reflects the number of parties being merged.
9. Important:

   This action can take up to an hour before the changes are reflected in the various locations across the IAM platform.

   You are returned to the Are these the same party? view. The merged party is displayed in the table with the View Party option.

   ![A screenshot of the Review Duplicate Parties page that shows the View Party option for the merged party.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Manage-Parties_Review-Duplicate-Parties_View-Party-option.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc3NzcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.qiqirVAiwB3jp7L8J0UTdHHErq831FuVByF_mghev68&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Review Duplicate Parties page that shows the View Party option for the merged party.")

   Tip: Select the View Party option to view the party hub pages for the merged party. See the [IAM Party Hub](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=bpv1742428468463.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the IAM party hub, a centralized location for party-related pages. This topic answers the question: Where can I find a unified summary of all agreements and obligations for a customer or vendor?") to learn more about the party hub pages.
10. Repeat this process to continue merging duplicate parties that were surfaced within your agreements.
11. Select Close (X) from the top-right corner of the page.

    You are returned to the Parties page. The Manage parties view is displayed.

    Tip: Select Completed from the navigation pane on the left to view your agreement list. The Completed Documents view opens. The agreements to which the user has access are displayed in the table.

title: Docusign FAQs﻿ for AI
text:
Read frequently asked questions about the practices and policies that apply to the use
of AI in Docusign products and programs.

Trust, privacy, and transparency have always been at the core of Docusign, and our work with AI is no different. The following FAQ
shares additional details about the use of AI in Docusign products
and programs.

title: Docusign FAQs﻿ for AI
heading_path: Does Docusign use my data for AI model training and improvement?
text:
There are limited instances where Docusign uses anonymized data from
customers who provide consent to Docusign to improve our AI models
so our AI-powered features can deliver greater value for our customers. Previously,
Docusign used anonymized data from consenting customers who
used Docusign AI Extension for CLM or participated in select beta
programs. Customers using these products provide explicit contractual consent when
they sign an agreement to purchase or participate in Docusign
programs using AI.

On May 30, 2024, Docusign introduced the AI Data
Controls setting. Moving forward, Docusign will
use anonymized data only from customers who have given explicit consent through
their contract *and* who have the AI Data Controls
setting turned ***on***.

The AI Data Controls setting is an in-product AI data sharing setting
that provides additional, built-in controls for your organization’s account
administrator to manage whether your organization's data is used for training
purposes. For customers who provide contractual consent, account administrators can
turn this setting ***off*** or ***on*** at any time, using the
in-product controls.

If a customer’s authorized representative has not provided contractual consent, the
in-product toggle will be disabled and set to ***off*** and cannot be
changed by an account administrator. Only documents sent or uploaded by consenting
Docusign accounts will be used for training. Additionally, Docusign only uses data that is aggregated and anonymized before
training occurs.

See the [AI Data Controls Setting FAQs](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fzd1707173174972&topicId=dcs1708531444159.html) topic for more information
about this setting.

title: Docusign FAQs﻿ for AI
heading_path: In cases where Docusign uses my data for AI model training and improvement, how is my data protected and secured?
text:
Docusign trains AI models on data from customers only if
a customer has given consent, and the data is anonymized and aggregated before
training occurs.

For customers that have given explicit consent, Docusign takes a
multi-pronged privacy and security-centric approach to protect data privacy and
confidentiality including: anonymization, aggregation, and encryption.

The Docusign anonymization process removes personal identifiers,
such as:

- Name
- Phone numbers
- Email addresses
- Social security numbers (in the U.S.)
- National ID numbers

These measures are designed to prevent data from being used to re-identify or trace
back to an individual or organization. For additional security, data is encrypted in
transit and rest.

title: Docusign FAQs﻿ for AI
heading_path: Which AI models does Docusign use for AI features?
text:
Docusign employs a hybrid-model approach that combines proprietary models
with best-in-class third-party LLMs. Proprietary models are developed in-house and
are customized open-source AI models. [Third-party models](https://www.docusign.com/trust/privacy/subprocessors-list) are provided by third-party
organizations, such as Microsoft Azure OpenAI Service, and accessed via API.

title: Docusign FAQs﻿ for AI
heading_path: When Docusign partners with third-party models such as Microsoft Azure OpenAI Service, how is a customer’s data used and protected?
text:
When working with third-party models such as Microsoft Azure OpenAI Service, Docusign and its partners agree on robust protections to safeguard
the data privacy and security of our customers and our users. For Microsoft Azure
OpenAI Service specifically, there is:

- **No improvements to Microsoft or Azure products:**
  Docusign does not share customer data to improve Microsoft or
  Azure products for other Microsoft customers. Anonymized data may be used to
  customize AI models for Docusign’s AI solutions. Customized
  models are only available for Docusign to use.
- **No persistent data storage during AI processing:** Data from Docusign customers is not persistently stored in the Azure
  OpenAI Service.
- **Bias filtering and responsible AI:**
  Docusign uses the Azure bias and offensiveness detection
  models to process and filter AI output.

title: Update the Agreement Type for Selected Agreements
heading_path: Update the Agreement Type for Selected Agreements
text:
Learn how to update the agreement type value for a group of selected agreements.

When documents are ingested or uploaded to Agreement Manager, the agreement type is identified and the associated fields are surfaced. When the type cannot be identified, the agreements can be assigned as *Miscellaneous*. You can update the agreement type for a group of agreements using the *bulk update* feature. For example, update a group of "miscellaneous" agreements to a custom agreement type.

Note: When updating the agreement type, the agreement is processed again to surface content that is relevant to the selected type.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed. A list of documents to which the logged-in user has access is displayed in the table.
4. Locate the agreements that you want to update. In this example, filter the agreement list table by Agreement Type.

   ![A screenshot of the Agreement Type filter that shows a selected agreement type.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-documents_Filters_Agreement-Type_Misc-example.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc1MTIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.OwZf_lBEK7q2X3D2wSIpQZtVNYtOaGFq0l1T7Z8URVc&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Agreement Type filter that shows a selected agreement type.")

   1. Expand (click) the Filters option and select the Agreement Type filter.

      The Agreement Type filter expands and available types are displayed.
   2. Search for the relevant agreement type. Enter a keyword or search string in the Search field.

      As you enter the search criteria, the list of agreement types updates to match the keyword.
   3. Select the agreement type by which you want to filter your documents.
   4. Select the Apply option.
   5. Repeat this step until the table contains the agreements that you want to update. For example, filter your completed documents by Status or Parties.

   The agreement list table updates, and agreements that match the applied filters are displayed.
5. Select the agreements that you want to update from the list.

   ![A snippet of the agreement list table that shows a group of selected agreements.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Bulk-update-agreement-type_select-agreements.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc1MTIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.OwZf_lBEK7q2X3D2wSIpQZtVNYtOaGFq0l1T7Z8URVc&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the agreement list table that shows a group of selected agreements.")

   - Select the individual agreements that you want to update.
   - Select the Choose all checkbox from the header row.

     All the agreements on that page of results are selected.
   - Once you select at least one agreement, the Selected dropdown is displayed. Expand the Selected dropdown and select the Select all that match your filters option.

     All the agreements in the table that match the applied filters are selected. The selection extends beyond the current page of results.
6. Expand the Edit (pencil) dropdown and select the Agreement Type option:

title: Update the Agreement Type for Selected Agreements
heading_path: Update the Agreement Type for Selected Agreements
text:
greements in the table that match the applied filters are selected. The selection extends beyond the current page of results.
6. Expand the Edit (pencil) dropdown and select the Agreement Type option:

   ![A snippet of the agreement list table that highlights the Agreement Type option in the Edit dropdown menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-documents-table_Edit-option_Agreement-Type_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc1MTIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.OwZf_lBEK7q2X3D2wSIpQZtVNYtOaGFq0l1T7Z8URVc&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the agreement list table that highlights the Agreement Type option in the Edit dropdown menu.")The Edit agreement type dialog box opens.
7. Specify the new agreement type for the selected agreements:

   ![A screenshot of the Edit agreement type dialog box.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Bulk-update-agreement-type_Edit-agreement-type-dialog-box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc1MTIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.OwZf_lBEK7q2X3D2wSIpQZtVNYtOaGFq0l1T7Z8URVc&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Edit agreement type dialog box.")

   1. Enter a keyword in the Select a type field to locate the relevant agreement type.

      As you enter the search criteria, a list of agreement types that matched the keyword are displayed.
   2. Select the relevant Agreement type from the results list.
   3. Select the Save option.

   The Change agreement type dialog box opens.

   ![A screenshot of the Change agreement type dialog box that explains the agreements will be processed for the selected type.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Bulk-update-agreement-type_Change-agreement-type-dialog-box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc1MTIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.OwZf_lBEK7q2X3D2wSIpQZtVNYtOaGFq0l1T7Z8URVc&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Change agreement type dialog box that explains the agreements will be processed for the selected type.")

   Important: This feature is available to users with specific agreement-level or Agreement Manager-level permissions:
   - Users with Can Manage or Can Edit access to an agreement can update that agreement's type.
   - Users with Organize and manage all agreements access to Agreement Manager can update the agreement type for any of the agreements in the account.

   The document name is grayed out and is not included in the update if you do not have the appropriate access.
8. Confirm the proposed changes and select the Update agreements option.

   The dialog box closes, and the Completed Documents page is displayed. The agreements are reprocessed to surface content related to the agreement type that you selected.

   Tip: The results of the processing job are available from the job monitoring hub. See the [View and Download Processing Results](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=cvd1742251951627.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to view and download results for processing jobs.") topic for more information about job monitoring.

You have successfully updated the agreement type for a group of selected agreements.

title: Extracted Agreement Insights and Definitions
text:
Look up the names and definitions for the attributes and values that are surfaced in Agreement Manager agreements with AI. This topic answers the question: What data is being extracted from my Agreement Manager agreements?

Attributes and field values are surfaced with Docusign Iris to capture the contractual terms and data. These key data points provide insights into your agreements, and provide greater efficiency and improved risk management.

With these AI-assisted insights, you can:

- **Boost internal productivity:** spend less time manually capturing and tracking data and more time focused on strategic work.
- **Make more informed decisions:** use Docusign Iris to surface critical agreement terms and data points.
- **Expose more potential risks:** monitor compliance with more consistent and reliable agreement records.

Important:

Docusign's latest release includes new attributes (fields)
along with the standard attributes that were previously available. As new
insights are released to support additional use cases, agreements can be
processed again to surface these data points. However, this processing does not
happen automatically.

You must *opt in* to start processing new and existing agreements to surface additional data points. When opting in for processing, your agreements are automatically processed for new insights as they become available.

Review the [Processing Agreement Manager Agreements](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=rvw1736364838500.html) topic to learn more about processing your agreements for new insights.

Use the extracted attributes and values to:

- Filter the agreement list table (Completed Documents view)
- Perform a search query to locate agreements
- Manage renewals (populated with renewal-related attribute values)
- Track payment obligations
- Filter and view agreement reports

CAUTION: The accuracy and appropriateness of AI-assisted insights may vary depending on the agreement language and
customer use case. Always review any attribute values that were surfaced with AI for
accuracy and appropriateness.

Important: Validate all suggested attributes and field values to ensure accuracy. These attributes are used for filtering, searching, renewals and obligation management, and reporting. Review and validate attribute values from the Details pane for a selected agreement.

title: Extracted Agreement Insights and Definitions
text:
Review these sections to learn more about the attributes that comprise various clause types or
use cases.

title: Extracted Agreement Insights and Definitions
heading_path: Assignment and Change of Control Insights
text:
| **Attribute** | **Description** |
| --- | --- |
| Assignment (Change of Control)\* | Sometimes one or more parties can assign the duties and obligations of the contract to a third party. This assignment might require the consent of the counter-party. This field only concerns assignment upon change of control or merger, or successor in interest. |
| Assignment (General)\* | Sometimes one or more parties can assign the rights, duties, and obligations of an agreement to a third party. This assignment might require the consent of the counter-party. This field only concerns assignment where the nature of the third party is not specified. For example, anything not related to assignment upon change of control or merger. |
| Assignment (Termination Rights)\* | Sometimes one or more parties can assign the duties and obligations of the contract to a third party. If this happens, the contract may allow one or more parties to terminate the agreement early. |

**\*** Included in all IAM plans with Agreement Manager.

title: Extracted Agreement Insights and Definitions
text:
| **Attribute** | **Description** |
| --- | --- |
| Annual Contract Value\* | The total value of all fees due under the agreement during a period of one year, as specified in the agreement. |
| Total Contract Value\* | The total monetary value of an agreement between two or more parties. |
| Late Fees Apply\* | This field indicates whether a party is allowed to charge late fees for payments that are past due. |
| Late Fee %\* | The maximum interest rate that a party is allowed to charge on past due payments. |
| Payment Term\* | This field refers to the general payment terms of an agreement, such as when and how payment for goods or services is expected.  Payment can be due:  - Within a specific period of time - Upon receipt of the invoice - Upon any number of other provisions  Important: For attributes (fields) that use dropdowns with pre-populated values, like Payment Term, only those values are surfaced in your agreements. Agreement processing extracts an attribute only if it matches one of the values in the dropdown. See the [Language and Regional Support](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=xvp1729795957126.html&_LANG=enus "Read about language support and regional availability for Agreement Manager AI models and features.") topic to learn more about this limitation. |
| Price Cap Increase %\* | The maximum amount by which a party can raise their prices when the term is renewed, as a percentage of the previous price. |

**\*** Included in all IAM plans with Agreement Manager.

title: Extracted Agreement Insights and Definitions
text:
| **Attribute** | **Description** |
| --- | --- |
| Confidentiality Duration\* | The duration of time during which one or more parties must keep the information provided under an agreement confidential. |

**\*** Included in all IAM plans with Agreement Manager.

title: Extracted Agreement Insights and Definitions
heading_path: Governing Law, Jurisdiction, and Dispute Resolution Insights
text:
| **Attribute** | **Description** |
| --- | --- |
| Governing Law\* | Refers to the laws of a specific place or the system of law that is applied in the event of a legal dispute. |
| Jurisdiction\* | Refers to the location where any legal disputes related to the agreement are tried. The venue for disagreements is the name or location of the courts where the trial must take place. This field also encompasses locations for arbitration, if it is laid out in the contract. |

**\*** Included in all IAM plans with Agreement Manager.

title: Extracted Agreement Insights and Definitions
text:
| **Attribute** | **Description** |
| --- | --- |
| Liability Cap Amount\* | The maximum explicit monetary cost of damages for which one party is liable under the agreement. |
| Liability Cap Duration\* | The duration of time used to calculate the actual maximum limitation of liability under the agreement. |
| Liability Cap Multiplier\* | The multiplier that is laid out in an agreement to calculate the actual maximum limitation of liability for a party. |

**\*** Included in all IAM plans with Agreement Manager.

title: Extracted Agreement Insights and Definitions
text:
| **Attribute** | **Description** |
| --- | --- |
| Agreement Type\* | Refers to the classification of an agreement according to references in its title and its subject matter. For example, Master Services Agreement (MSA), Non-Disclosure Agreements (NDA), and Statement of Work (SOW). |
| Execution Date\* | Refers to the date on which the contract became a legally binding agreement that was signed by all the necessary parties.  If the execution date is missing from the document and all parties have signed it, the date defaults to the latest signature date. |
| Languages\* | Identifies which languages are present in the agreement. |
| Line of Business | This field indicates the line of business to which the agreement most closely aligns. For example, Sales, Procurement, Human Resources, and unspecified. |
| NDA Type\* | A Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA) is described as: One-way or Mutual.  - Mutual (or Bilateral) NDAs are where both parties are exchanging information. - One way (or Unilateral) NDAs are where one party is receiving information and the other party is   providing it. |
| Parties\* | Refers to a provision naming the individuals, organizations, or entities that enter into a legally binding agreement. |
| Party Role | This field identifies which party is the buyer as opposed to the seller within the context of an agreement. |
| Status | This field provides a look at where the agreement is within its life cycle. It indicates whether the agreement is active, inactive, effective soon, expiring soon, and renewing soon. |
| Title\* | The title of the document. It is usually at the top of the page and describes the subject of the agreement. |

**\*** Included in all IAM plans with Agreement Manager.

title: Extracted Agreement Insights and Definitions
text:
| **Attribute** | **Description** |
| --- | --- |
| Deposit or Initial Payment Amount | Payment value. The first sum of money that is paid upfront when entering into an agreement. |
| Deposit or Initial Payment Amount - Calculated | Payment value. The calculated amount paid upfront when entering into an agreement. This value uses text to calculate the amount if not explicitly written in the agreement. |
| Deposit or Initial Payment - Due Date | Due date. The date the deposit or initial payment is due. |
| Deposit or Initial Payment - Period Due | Due date. The duration of time within which a deposit or initial payment is due. |
| Late Payment Penalties | Repercussion. The penalty for late payments. |
| Late Payment Interest | Repercussion. The interest rate levied on late payments. |
| One Time Payment Amount | Payment value. A single payment made on a single occasion. The payment is only made once for a specific amount. The payment is not part of a recurring payment schedule. |
| One Time Payment - Due Date | Due date. The specific date by which a single, non-recurring payment must be made in full to avoid late fees or penalties. |
| One Time Payment - Period Due | Due date. The duration of time within which a one-time payment is due. |
| Promisor | Party - Promisor. The party that is responsible for fulfilling an obligation. |

title: Extracted Agreement Insights and Definitions
text:
| **Attribute** | **Description** |
| --- | --- |
| Renewal Type\* | Comprised of these data extractions: Auto Renew, Evergreen, Fixed Term, and Renewal Option. |
| Auto Renew (Renewal Type)\* | Renewal type. Refers to provisions that state an agreement is renewed automatically at the end of its term. |
| Evergreen (Renewal Type)\* | Renewal type. Refers to clauses that contemplate a perpetual continuation of the agreement until a party takes action to terminate it or a certain condition is fulfilled. |
| Fixed Term (Renewal Type)\* | Renewal type. Refers to an agreement that is effective for a specified period. |
| Renewal Option (Renewal Type)\* | Renewal type. Refers to provisions that set out a party’s option to extend the term of the contract. |
| Renewal Term\* | Refers to a fixed period for which an agreement is extended after its initial term expires. These provisions set out:  - Duration of an auto-renewal period (Term: Renewal Automatic) - Party's option to extend an agreement for a specified term (Term: Renewal Option) - Additional period of time that an agreement will be extended beyond its initial term (Extension Period) |
| Extension Period\* | Refers to provisions that set out the additional period of time (duration) that an agreement will be extended beyond its initial term. |
| Renewal Notice Date\* | Refers to the date by which one or more parties must provide official notice to continue an agreement. |
| Renewal Notice Period\* | Refers to the period of time that is required for a party to give notice of their intent to renew an agreement. NonRenewal Notice Period refers to the period of time that is required for a party to give notice of their intent not to renew an agreement. |

**\*** Included in all IAM plans with Agreement Manager.

title: Extracted Agreement Insights and Definitions
text:
| **Attribute** | **Description** |
| --- | --- |
| Effective Date\* | Refers to the date on which an agreement begins to apply or take effect. For example, when the terms, obligations, or rights established within an agreement become legally enforceable or operational. |
| Expiration Date\* | Refers to the date on which the agreement comes to an end and is no longer in effect. |
| Initial Term Length\* | Describes the period of time during which an agreement is in effect. |

**\*** Included in all IAM plans with Agreement Manager.

title: Extracted Agreement Insights and Definitions
text:
| **Attribute** | **Description** |
| --- | --- |
| Termination for Cause - Notice Period\* | Refers to provisions that set out the notice period required for a party to terminate an agreement for cause. |
| Termination for Convenience - Notice Period\* | Refers to provisions that set out the notice period required for a party to terminate an agreement for convenience. |

**\*** Included in all IAM plans with Agreement Manager.

title: The Parties List
text:
Read about the enhanced Parties list, including access points and
features.

The Parties page lists the individual and merged parties that were
surfaced in an account's uploaded and ingested agreements.

![A screenshot of the Parties page that lists all the parties and groups for an account.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Manage-Parties_Parties-view_Updated-table.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc4MDIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9._HLAqed6zCTLnAqOS5E2kjff7_eIjH6W2cmvhYEhOHE&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Parties page that lists all the parties and groups for an account.")

From this page, you can:

- Access your Recent and Starred agreements.
- View the party list table by Role View or Party View.
- Search for a specific party or group.
- Filter the party list table by Party Roles, such as Buyer, Seller, or Other.
- Apply the Party Side filter to view Internal or External parties.
- Use the Favorites (star) icon to add a party or group to your Starred list.
- View the number of active, expiring, renewing, and upcoming agreements over a 90-day time period
  for parties and groups.
- Use the Review and Merge option to manage your duplicate parties.
- Manage merged party groups.
- Access the party hub for a selected party or group.

Notice:

The data used to populate the party hub pages, including the Parties list, is refreshed every five minutes. If you've made recent changes, you will see the updated data shortly.

title: The Parties List
text:
Primary ways to access the Parties page:

- Navigate directly to the Parties page. Log into Docusign and select Agreements from the global header. Expand the PARTIES section in the navigation pane on the left and select the All option:

  ![A snippet of the Parties page that highlights the All option in the navigation pane.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Party_hub_Entry_point_navigation_pane.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc4MDIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9._HLAqed6zCTLnAqOS5E2kjff7_eIjH6W2cmvhYEhOHE&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Parties page that highlights the All option in the navigation pane.")
- From Docusign CLM. Select the Parties option from the global header at the top of the page:

  ![A snippet of Docusign CLM that highlights the Parties option in the global header.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Parties-view_CLM-entry-point.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc4MDIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9._HLAqed6zCTLnAqOS5E2kjff7_eIjH6W2cmvhYEhOHE&_LANG=enus "A snippet of Docusign CLM that highlights the Parties option in the global header.")

The Parties page opens and a list of individual and merged parties
is displayed in the table.

Note: Party management tasks are completed by users
with Organize and manage all agreements access to Agreement Manager.
See the [Duplicate Party Management](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=ssf1712255476566.html) topic to learn
more about merging parties and managing party groups.

title: The Parties List
text:
When you first access the Parties page, a series of onboarding callouts is displayed. These callouts provide a quick tour of the Parties page.

Select Next to review each of the callouts or select Skip All to close the dialog boxes:

![A screenshot of the first callout in a series of onboarding callouts that are displayed when you first access the Parties page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Parties-view_Star-parties-messaging_callout.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc4MDIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9._HLAqed6zCTLnAqOS5E2kjff7_eIjH6W2cmvhYEhOHE&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the first callout in a series of onboarding callouts that are displayed when you first access the Parties page.")

This guided flow highlights various features of the Parties page, such as:

- Favorites (star) icon: Star your most active, time-sensitive, or high-value parties.
- Recent and Starred tabs: Find your starred and recently viewed parties in the tabs.
- Role View: Switch to Role View to show party data broken down by role, such as buyer or seller.

title: The Parties List
text:
The tabs at the top of the Parties page give you quick access to your
recently viewed and favorite parties and groups:

![A snippet of the Parties page that highlights the All, Recent, and Starred tabs at the top of the page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Parties-view_All-Recent-Starred_tabs_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc4MDIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9._HLAqed6zCTLnAqOS5E2kjff7_eIjH6W2cmvhYEhOHE&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Parties page that highlights the All, Recent, and Starred tabs at the top of the page.")

For example:

- Select the Recent tab to access your most recently viewed parties and groups. Up to 15 parties or groups are displayed in the table.
- Select the Starred tab to access your favorite parties and groups. Each user can "star" up to 50 parties or groups.

The All tab is displayed by default. This tab lists all the parties and groups that were surfaced in an account's agreements.

title: The Parties List
text:
Use the View dropdown to switch the focus of the Parties list:

![A snippet of the Parties page that highlights the View dropdown, including the Role View and Party View options.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Parties-view_View-dropdown_expanded.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc4MDIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9._HLAqed6zCTLnAqOS5E2kjff7_eIjH6W2cmvhYEhOHE&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Parties page that highlights the View dropdown, including the Role View and Party View options.")

- Role View: Shows agreement data broken down by specific party roles. For
  example, you can see a party's sell-side and buy-side agreement counts
  together in the table.![A snippet of the Parties page that highlights the different roles that the selected party plays within your agreements.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Parties-view_Role-View-example.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc4MDIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9._HLAqed6zCTLnAqOS5E2kjff7_eIjH6W2cmvhYEhOHE&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Parties page that highlights the different roles that the selected party plays within your agreements.")
- Party View: Shows the total agreement counts for each party. For example,
  a party's agreement counts include agreements where the party was the
  seller and the buyer:![A snippet of the Parties page that shows the total agreement accounts for all roles that the party plays.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Parties-view_Party-View-example.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc4MDIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9._HLAqed6zCTLnAqOS5E2kjff7_eIjH6W2cmvhYEhOHE&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Parties page that shows the total agreement accounts for all roles that the party plays.")

title: The Parties List
text:
Party Roles are surfaced during agreement processing. These extractions identify the roles that parties play within the context of your agreements.

Once surfaced, you can filter the Parties list to view agreement counts based on the role that the party played. For example, choose to view the party list and agreement counts based on your buyer-seller relationship.

![A screenshot of the Party Roles dropdown that shows the available roles.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Parties-view_Party-Roles-dropdown_expanded.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc4MDIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9._HLAqed6zCTLnAqOS5E2kjff7_eIjH6W2cmvhYEhOHE&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Party Roles dropdown that shows the available roles.")

Choose the relevant role and select the Apply option.

The table updates based on the selected role. For example, only those agreements where the party's role matches the applied filter are included in the table.

![A screenshot of the Parties page that highlights the applied Party Roles filter.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Parties-view_Filtered-for-role_Buyer.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc4MDIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9._HLAqed6zCTLnAqOS5E2kjff7_eIjH6W2cmvhYEhOHE&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Parties page that highlights the applied Party Roles filter.")

title: The Parties List
text:
During processing, parties are identified as either the first-party or the third-party:

- The first-party designation, Internal, represents the user's organization.
- The third-party designation, External, indicates an external entity.

Once surfaced, you can filter the Parties list to view agreement counts based on the party's side. For example, choose to view the party list and agreement counts for your *internal* entities:

![A screenshot of the Party Side dropdown that shows the two options, Internal and External.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Parties-view_Party-Side-dropdown_expanded.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc4MDIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9._HLAqed6zCTLnAqOS5E2kjff7_eIjH6W2cmvhYEhOHE&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Party Side dropdown that shows the two options, Internal and External.")

Expand the Party Side filter and choose to filter the table by Internal or External parties. Select the Apply option to apply the filter.

The party list table updates based on the selected party side.

title: The Parties List
text:
During the upload process, key data points are surfaced within your agreements,
including the relevant parties and party roles. The Parties
page lists all the party names that were extracted from your account's
agreements.

![A snippet of the Parties page that shows the parties and party groups that matched the search criteria.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Manage-Parties_Parties-view_Acme-example.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc4MDIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9._HLAqed6zCTLnAqOS5E2kjff7_eIjH6W2cmvhYEhOHE&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Parties page that shows the parties and party groups that matched the search criteria.")

Tip: Perform a keyword search to narrow the list of
parties. Enter a party or alias group name in the Search
parties field. The table automatically updates and the parties and
groups that match the search criteria are listed.

High-level details for each party or party group are displayed in the party list
table, including:

- Name, role, and status for each party or party group
- Number of current active agreements
- Number of agreements set to expire or renew over the next 30 days
- Number of agreements that are expected to become active over the next 30
  days

Tip: Use the sorting arrows in the column headers to
sort the Parties list as needed.

Hover the cursor over the
headings of the agreement count columns to view a tool tip. For example:![A screenshot of the tool tip that displays when you hover the cursor over the column headings that are related to agreement counts.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Parties-view_Column-heading_hover_renewing-agreements-example.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc4MDIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9._HLAqed6zCTLnAqOS5E2kjff7_eIjH6W2cmvhYEhOHE&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the tool tip that displays when you hover the cursor over the column headings that are related to agreement counts.")

Select a party or party group from the table to view the associated party hub pages.
You can click anywhere in the row:

![A snippet of the Parties page that highlights a selected table row.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Parties-view_NAME-column-highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc4MDIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9._HLAqed6zCTLnAqOS5E2kjff7_eIjH6W2cmvhYEhOHE&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Parties page that highlights a selected table row.")

The party hub provides a unified summary of all agreements and obligations that are
tied to a party or alias group. See the [IAM Party Hub](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=bpv1742428468463.html) topic to learn more about these
pages, including other entry points and reporting features.

title: Capabilities by Agreement Manager Permissions
text:
Look up the available capabilities for each Agreement Manager permission.

There are two primary options when granting access to agreements in an account:

- **View all agreements in this account:** this permission grants basic access to view and download all agreements in an account. This access is dependent on agreement-level access controls. For example, some agreements contain sensitive information and are not accessible for all users.
- **Organize and manage all agreements:** this permission grants the highest level of access to view, edit, download, and delete all agreements in the account. This permission also grants access to manage agreement sets and attributes, like agreement types and fields.

Important:

When organizations purchase an Intelligent Agreement Management (IAM) plan with Agreement Manager, by default, all users can access their
own shared, received, sent, and uploaded agreements. However, specific Agreement Manager access is required to view, organize, and manage all the agreements in the account.

Restriction: While administrators automatically have
full access to all the Agreement Manager features, they do not have access to
view the agreements. Access is explicitly assigned to administrators for them to
view any of the agreements in the account. See the [Agreement Sharing and Downloads](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=hrr1722978886188.html&_LANG=enus "This topic answers the question: What are my direct sharing and download options for Agreement Manager documents, including the required permissions?") article to learn more about sharing agreements
with others.

See the following table to learn more about the available capabilities for each Agreement Manager permission.

**\***All users can upload up to five 3rd-party agreements to Agreement Manager. The Upload up to 1,000 files at a time toggle must be turned on (blue) to upload more than five files. This toggle gives users (not available at the group-level) access to bulk upload documents locally and from 3rd-party storage locations.
