title: Capabilities by Agreement Manager Permissions
text:
*Free participants* can view their own completed agreements in Agreement Manager. These *free license* users have similar access to Agreement Manager features as those users with View all agreements permission. However, this role cannot use Agreement Manager features that rely on the account's repository and data sets.

For example, a "free participant" may have access to the Insights tab, but no data is loaded. The dashboards and reports are populated from the account's Agreement Manager data set. Access to this information requires Agreement Manager permissions:

- View all agreements in this account permission (minimum requirement)
- Organize and manage all agreements permission

Free participants can gain access to additional agreements through shared agreement sets. Depending on the assigned access level, users can view, edit, or manage the agreements in a shared set.

A full license and additional permission are required to use the majority of Agreement Manager features. See the [License Management in Account Administration](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=bhs1740099648152.html) topic to learn more about IAM plans and license types.

title: Renewals Dashboard
text:
This topic answers the question: What does the Renewals dashboard show me and how does it work? Read about the Renewals dashboard, including how to use the charts to filter for agreements and available sorting options.

The Renewals dashboard displays renewal data based on the attribute (field) values surfaced in your agreements. The dashboard refreshes every 15 minutes to account for new agreements and agreements with updated renewal-related field values. The refresh is designed to ensure that all agreements are represented in the dashboard.

Important: Data validation is an important part of renewal management. If the renewal-related field values that were surfaced in your agreements are inaccurate, the Renewals dashboard is not accurate. This process relies on the verification of suggested attribute (field) values.

The dashboard charts are populated based on the data points that are surfaced in your agreements.
Apply filters to refine the chart data. The
Renewal Notice Date and
Renewal Type filters are
applied by default.

Access the dashboard from the email notification or by selecting Insights > Renewals.

![A screenshot of the Renewals dashboard that shows the agreement charts and filter options.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Renewals-dashboard.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwMjcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.63BIMrYjhkIjFS13cCvy88brmgxMMKWcsiiDUXGmtBI&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Renewals dashboard that shows the agreement charts and filter options.")

title: Renewals Dashboard
text:
The dashboard charts show a visual summary of renewal-related insights, including: Upcoming renewals and Renewals by type.

Upcoming renewals

The Upcoming renewals chart shows agreements with Renewal Notice Dates that match the applied filters:

![A screenshot of the Upcoming Renewals chart that is located on the Renewals dashboard.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Renewals-dashboard_Chart_Upcoming-Renewals.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwMjcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.63BIMrYjhkIjFS13cCvy88brmgxMMKWcsiiDUXGmtBI&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Upcoming Renewals chart that is located on the Renewals dashboard.")

By default, the chart shows agreements where:

- The Renewal Type is Auto Renewal.
- The Renewal Notice Date value is within the next 12 months.

The agreements are groups by Renewal Notice Date and an aggregated count is displayed for each group.

Note: The dashboard charts can be viewed as regular bar charts or as stacked bar charts. Expand the More actions (three dots) menu and select the View as Stacked Bar Chart option:![A screenshot of the More actions (three dots) menu for a dashboard chart.that highlights the View as Stacked Bar Chart option.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Dashboard-Reports_Dashboard-charts_View-as-stacked-bar-chart_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwMjcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.63BIMrYjhkIjFS13cCvy88brmgxMMKWcsiiDUXGmtBI&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the More actions (three dots) menu for a dashboard chart.that highlights the View as Stacked Bar Chart option.")

Renewals by type

The Renewals by type chart shows agreements with Renewal Notice Dates that match the applied filters, grouped by Renewal Type:

![A screenshot of the Renewals by type chart that is located on the Renewals dashboard.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Renewals-dashboard_Chart_Renewals-by-type.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwMjcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.63BIMrYjhkIjFS13cCvy88brmgxMMKWcsiiDUXGmtBI&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Renewals by type chart that is located on the Renewals dashboard.")

By default, the chart shows agreements where:

- The agreement has a Renewal Type value.
- The Renewal Notice Date is within the next 12 months.

The agreements are groups by Renewal Type and an aggregated count is displayed for each group.

**Clickable segments and bars**

The segments or bars in the dashboard charts are clickable:

![A screenshot of the Renewals by type chart that shows the hover messaging for a selected chart segment.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Renewals_Renewal-types_hover-on-segment.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwMjcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.63BIMrYjhkIjFS13cCvy88brmgxMMKWcsiiDUXGmtBI&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Renewals by type chart that shows the hover messaging for a selected chart segment.")

- Hover the cursor over the chart segments or bars to view the associated agreement count.
- Select (click) a chart segment or bar to view the corresponding list of agreements.

  The report
  view opens and the representative agreements are displayed in the table. The
  chart is also displayed.

Note: The information in the dashboard charts is an estimate that is based on both verified and unverified AI suggestions. These estimates are only as accurate as the renewal-related data points that were surfaced in your agreements. Be sure to validate all the suggested agreement attributes and field values.

title: Renewals Dashboard
text:
Filters display at the top of the Renewals dashboard. Your filter selections are applied to the dashboard charts. Apply one or more of the filters to locate the relevant renewal information.

![A snippet of the Renewals dashboard that highlights the dashboard filters.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Renewals-dashboard_Filter-pills.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwMjcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.63BIMrYjhkIjFS13cCvy88brmgxMMKWcsiiDUXGmtBI&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Renewals dashboard that highlights the dashboard filters.")

Filters include:

- Parties
- Renewal Notice Date
- Renewal Owner
- Renewal Type
- Status

Important: When using the Parties filter, you must also select a Party Role value. If you do not know which role to select, choose the All option. If no role is selected, the search returns agreements where the party is not the buyer or the seller.

Additional filters are available from the All Filters dialog box:

![A screenshot of the All Filters dialog box that shows additional filters that you can apply to the agreement list.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-documents-table_All-Filters-dialog-box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwMjcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.63BIMrYjhkIjFS13cCvy88brmgxMMKWcsiiDUXGmtBI&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the All Filters dialog box that shows additional filters that you can apply to the agreement list.")

Note: Multiple filters and search criteria can be used together.

- Multiple values within the same filter imply an "or" relationship. For example, select two parties to view agreements for either contracting party.
- Multiple values in separate filters with entered search criteria imply an "and" relationship.

Review the [Download the Upcoming Renewals Report](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=qke1713569186359.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to locate upcoming renewals from the Renewals dashboard, including viewing and downloading agreements. This topic answers the question: How do I find my upcoming renewals in Agreement Manager?") topic to learn more about these filters.

title: Renewals Dashboard
text:
When downloading reports to CSV files from the Insights reporting hub, there is an agreement limit of 25,000 agreements for all IAM reports. This count includes your custom reports.

**Suggested workaround:** Adjust the report filters to run the report in batches until all the relevant agreements are downloaded. For example, run the report in batches according to varying date ranges. Once downloaded, combine the data into one CSV file.

Note: The eSignature reports have a different set of limitations. See the [Using Reports](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=cnu1642812012214&topicId=wou1578456440763.html) article to learn more about eSignature reports for senders and signers.

title: Renewals Dashboard
text:
The Favorite (star) icon is displayed on all the dashboards and on the detailed report view for each of the dashboard reports and insights. Use this feature to "star" the insights that you regularly track:

![A snippet of a detailed report view that highlights the Favorite (star) icon.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreements-dashboard_Report-details_Favorite-icon-selected_generic.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwMjcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.63BIMrYjhkIjFS13cCvy88brmgxMMKWcsiiDUXGmtBI&_LANG=enus "A snippet of a detailed report view that highlights the Favorite (star) icon.")

Your "starred" dashboards and reports are displayed in the Your Favorites list on the Overview dashboard:

![A snippet of the Overview dashboard that highlights the Your Favorites section.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reporting-hub_Overview_Your-Favorties_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwMjcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.63BIMrYjhkIjFS13cCvy88brmgxMMKWcsiiDUXGmtBI&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Overview dashboard that highlights the Your Favorites section.")

title: Renewals Dashboard
text:
Use the *Subscriptions* feature to sign up to receive report notifications for any of the dashboard reports. For example, create a subscription to receive weekly reports for your Upcoming renewals:

![A snippet of the Upcoming renewals report that shows the Create subscription dialog box.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Renewals-dashboard_Upcoming-renewals_Create-subscription.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwMjcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.63BIMrYjhkIjFS13cCvy88brmgxMMKWcsiiDUXGmtBI&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Upcoming renewals report that shows the Create subscription dialog box.")

Users can sign up to receive daily, weekly, monthly, and one-time email reports. See the [Subscribe to Receive Report Notifications](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=wfw1734545937270.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to set up one-time or recurring email reports for yourself or others to receive notifications about your obligations, renewals, or agreements.") topic to learn more about creating report subscriptions.

title: Renewals Dashboard
text:
Features that use Docusign Iris display the AI-Assisted denotation badge within the product. This denotation badge is clickable. Select the badge to access additional information about how Docusign uses AI functionality:

![A screenshot of the expanded AI-Assisted denotation badge that shows additional information about how Docusign uses AI functionality.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Obligations-Dashboard_AIAssisted-pillexpanded.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwMjcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.63BIMrYjhkIjFS13cCvy88brmgxMMKWcsiiDUXGmtBI&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the expanded AI-Assisted denotation badge that shows additional information about how Docusign uses AI functionality.")

See the [AI Denotation in Agreement Manager](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=rsz1722973735168.html&_LANG=enus "This topic answers the question: In what way does Docusign indicate AI-assisted insights or AI functionality in Agreement Manager?") topic to learn more about how Docusign denotes AI functionality.

title: Add Custom Fields to Standard Agreement Types
heading_path: Add Custom Fields to Standard Agreement Types
text:
Learn how to create and add custom fields to standard agreement types.

Every standard agreement type is associated with a list of fields that capture relevant information for the type. Custom fields capture specific, unique information that isn't covered by standard agreement fields. These fields can be added to standard and custom agreement types to meet specific use cases.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Completed Documents view opens.
4. Expand the Settings (gear) dropdown from the top right and select the Types and Fields option:

   ![A snippet of the Settings dropdown that highlights the Type and Field option.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Manage-Agreement-Types_More-actions-menu_expanded.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc5ODcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.t71OQx2QK0OCeH0HaYaRDf3VolusWMbeGTWZMLCKnhY&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Settings dropdown that highlights the Type and Field option.")

   The Manage Agreement Types page opens. The Agreement Types view is displayed.

   ![A screenshot of the Agreement Types view that shows the agreement types that were grouped under the Business Services tab.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Manage-Agreement-Types_Manage-Types_Agreement-Types.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc5ODcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.t71OQx2QK0OCeH0HaYaRDf3VolusWMbeGTWZMLCKnhY&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Agreement Types view that shows the agreement types that were grouped under the Business Services tab.")
5. Locate the standard agreement type that you want to update and select the View option.

   Tip: Review the CREATED BY column to differentiate between standard and custom agreement types:
   - Standard types are created by Docusign
   - Custom types are created by User

   Tip: Agreement types are categorized and grouped under three tabs: Business Services, Human Resources, and Miscellaneous. Be sure to display the relevant tab when searching for the agreement type that you want to update.

   The Agreement type details view opens for the selected agreement type. The fields that were automatically added to the standard agreement type are displayed in the table.

   ![A screenshot of a selected standard agreement type that shows the fields that were automatically added to the type.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-type-management_add-custom-field-to-standard-agreement.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc5ODcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.t71OQx2QK0OCeH0HaYaRDf3VolusWMbeGTWZMLCKnhY&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of a selected standard agreement type that shows the fields that were automatically added to the type.")
6. Select the + Add Fields option from the top right.

   The Add a field dialog box opens.![A screenshot of the Add a field dialog box that is used to add fields to existing agreement types.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-type-management_add-custom-fields_Franchise-Agreement-example.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc5ODcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.t71OQx2QK0OCeH0HaYaRDf3VolusWMbeGTWZMLCKnhY&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Add a field dialog box that is used to add fields to existing agreement types.")
7. Complete the required fields and select Save:
   1. Enter a name for the custom field in the Field name field.

title: Add Custom Fields to Standard Agreement Types
heading_path: Add Custom Fields to Standard Agreement Types
text:
f the Add a field dialog box that is used to add fields to existing agreement types.")
7. Complete the required fields and select Save:
   1. Enter a name for the custom field in the Field name field.

      As you enter a name, a type-ahead dropdown list opens, and fields that match the entered name are displayed.

      Tip: Review the type-ahead dropdown list to verify that the custom field you are adding does not already exist.
   2. Select the + Create [Field name] option to create a custom field:

      ![A snippet of the Add a field dialog box that shows an example of a custom field name being created.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-type-management_add-custom-field-to-standard-agreement_example.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc5ODcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.t71OQx2QK0OCeH0HaYaRDf3VolusWMbeGTWZMLCKnhY&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Add a field dialog box that shows an example of a custom field name being created.")
   3. Expand the Field type dropdown and select the type of field that you are adding:

      ![A snippet of the Add a field dialog box that shows the available field types for your custom field.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Add-custom-field_Field-type-options.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc5ODcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.t71OQx2QK0OCeH0HaYaRDf3VolusWMbeGTWZMLCKnhY&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Add a field dialog box that shows the available field types for your custom field.")Choices are: Text,
      Number, Date, and
      Dropdown.

      When using the Dropdown field type, the Options field is required.
   4. (Dropdown only) Enter a list of Options for the dropdown in the text-entry box:

      ![A snippet of the Add a field dialog box that shows the Options field for adding a dropdown field type.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Add-custom-field_Options-for-dropdown-type.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc5ODcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.t71OQx2QK0OCeH0HaYaRDf3VolusWMbeGTWZMLCKnhY&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Add a field dialog box that shows the Options field for adding a dropdown field type.")

      Important: Separate each option with a semicolon.
   5. Expand the Category dropdown and select a classification for the field:

      ![A snippet of the Add a field dialog box that shows the Category options for the new field.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Add-custom-field_Category-options.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc5ODcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.t71OQx2QK0OCeH0HaYaRDf3VolusWMbeGTWZMLCKnhY&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Add a field dialog box that shows the Category options for the new field.")

      Choices are: General, Termination, Renewal, Payment, Legal and Compliance, Human Resources, and Other.
   6. The selected agreement type is displayed in the Agreement types field by default. Apply the custom field to additional agreement types as needed:

title: Add Custom Fields to Standard Agreement Types
heading_path: Add Custom Fields to Standard Agreement Types
text:
egal and Compliance, Human Resources, and Other.
   6. The selected agreement type is displayed in the Agreement types field by default. Apply the custom field to additional agreement types as needed:

      ![A snippet of the Add a field dialog box that shows the expanded Agreement types field.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-type-management_Add-a-field_Agreement-types-dropdown.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc5ODcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.t71OQx2QK0OCeH0HaYaRDf3VolusWMbeGTWZMLCKnhY&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Add a field dialog box that shows the expanded Agreement types field.")Choose the relevant agreement types and select the Apply option.
   7. Select Save.

   The dialog box closes. The Agreement type details view opens for the selected agreement type. The custom field is displayed in the field list table.

   ![A snippet of the Franchise Agreement details page that highlights the new custom field in the table.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-type-management_Add-custom-field-to-standard-agreement_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc5ODcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.t71OQx2QK0OCeH0HaYaRDf3VolusWMbeGTWZMLCKnhY&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Franchise Agreement details page that highlights the new custom field in the table.")

   When you first create a custom agreement field, the AI detection is inactive by default. Complete the AI detection setup process so that data for the field is surfaced in your documents.

   See the [Enable AI Detection for Custom Fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=wjr1740452628309.html) topic for more information.
8. Select the Back  arrow located in the top left.

   You are returned to the Manage Agreement Types page. The Agreement Types view is displayed.
9. Select the Close (X) icon from the top right.

   You are returned to the Completed Documents page.

title: Data Review Onboarding Experience
text:
Read about the first-time onboarding experience for data review, including a walk-through of the review process.

AI-assisted insights help you to find answers to critical business questions quicker, mitigate risk, and accelerate document review. Data review is an important part of using Agreement Manager as it ensures that users can make business decisions based on accurate agreement data.

As a new user, when you first access Docusign Agreement Manager, a series of onboarding callouts is displayed. This guided flow gives you a quick tour of Agreement Manager functionality, including data reviews.

Note: See the [Onboarding Callouts for First-Time Users](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=kpg1722973773604.html&_LANG=enus "This topic answers the question: Is there an onboarding experience for first-time users? Read about the walk-through experience for first-time Agreement Manager users.") topic to learn about the guided Agreement Manager tour.

The last callout in the guided flow takes you to the Details pane on the Agreement preview page. You are prompted to Review new data suggestions:

![A snippet of the Agreement preview page showing an onboarding callout prompting you to review new data suggestions.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-preview-page_snippet-Agreement-details_callout-1.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY2MTQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.skQ7q01iIqU4UL9mcbYr7HQj9x7PMZAFNxDehZb-Jko&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Agreement preview page showing an onboarding callout prompting you to review new data suggestions.")

Select OK to continue. The callout closes.

The next callout is displayed once you put the Details pane into *edit* mode. This callout prompts you to view the suggested details in the document.

![A snippet of the Agreement preview page that shows the onboarding callout that opens when you place the page into edit mode.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-preview-page_Review-and-edit-details-pane_callout-2.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY2MTQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.skQ7q01iIqU4UL9mcbYr7HQj9x7PMZAFNxDehZb-Jko&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Agreement preview page that shows the onboarding callout that opens when you place the page into edit mode.")

The attribute values that were surfaced with AI are clickable. Select the suggested value to view the value in the context of the agreement:

![A snippet of the Agreement preview page that shows the suggested value in the context of the agreement.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Onboarding_data-review_view-value-in-doc.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY2MTQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.skQ7q01iIqU4UL9mcbYr7HQj9x7PMZAFNxDehZb-Jko&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Agreement preview page that shows the suggested value in the context of the agreement.")

The suggested value is highlighted in the agreement: Review the value in the context of the agreement to determine if it is accurate.

Tip: Use the *Bulk Metadata Editing* feature to overwrite the extracted attribute values with user-provided agreement data. With this feature, you can edit the metadata for up to 1,000 selected documents in a single processing job. See the [Bulk Metadata Editing](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=concb493b18-adf8-4fe5-8b99-dd90cbc28399.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the Bulk Metadata Editing feature, including use cases, typical roles and permissions, and a process overview.") topic to learn about more about this feature.

title: Grant Access to Agreement Manager Agreements
heading_path: Grant Access to Agreement Manager Agreements
text:
This topic answers the question: How can I give access to Agreement Manager agreements and features, including bulk upload permissions?

When organizations purchase an Intelligent Agreement Management (IAM) plan with Agreement Manager, by default, all users can access their
own shared, received, sent, and uploaded agreements. However, specific Agreement Manager access is required to view, organize, and manage all the agreements in the account.

Administrators are responsible for safeguarding access to the agreements in Agreement Manager. This includes making sure that the appropriate users and groups can view or manage agreements.

Remember:

Agreement sets provide another option for sharing groups of agreements with users. These sets give contract managers and administrators a more granular way to control access to agreements. See the [Share an Agreement Set](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=pvb1736186431453.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to share an agreement set with individual users and groups. This topic answers the question: How do I share groups of agreements with the appropriate people?") topic to learn more about granting access to a set of agreements.

Agreement Manager access is granted from the User and Group profile pages under the Admin menu. This topic reviews the steps to grant individual users and groups access to Agreement Manager agreements and features.

1. Log in to your Docusign account using administrator credentials.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Admin from the global header.

   The Admin menu opens and the Accounts view is displayed.

**Select an account (for organizations with multiple accounts)**

Important: User and group permissions are managed at the *account*-level. Make sure that you are updating records from Accounts administration instead of Organization administration. If your organization has multiple accounts, verify that you are updating the correct account.

3. Expand the Accounts dropdown from the top of the navigation pane and select the relevant account:

   ![A snippet of the Accounts view that highlights the Accounts dropdown in the pane on the left.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Permissions_Admin-menu_Accounts-dropdown_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTYzMDMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.aQYD34WOFRyz1JSsiN4jc1j8b5Hw_vWRA3lPdbHyZXw&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Accounts view that highlights the Accounts dropdown in the pane on the left.")

   The Accounts view refreshes and the Overview page is displayed for the selected account. Locate the user or group that you want to update.

**Locate the relevant user or group**

4. Select either Users or Groups from the USERS AND GROUPS section in the navigation pane.

   Depending on your selection, either the Users or Groups page opens.
5. Locate the user or group that you want to update:
   1. From the Users page:

      - Enter the name of the user in the Search Here field.
      - Select a name from the type-ahead dropdown or press the Enter key.
      - Adjust the filters as needed. Filter options include: Status, Permissions, and Groups.

      The Users page refreshes and the users who matched the search criteria are displayed in the table.
   2. From the Groups page:

      - Enter the name of the group in the Enter Keyword field.
      - Select the Search option.

      The Groups page refreshes and the groups that matched the search criteria are displayed.

**Update Agreement Manager access**

6. Expand (select) the Actions option for the relevant user or group and select the Manage Agreement Manager Access item.

title: Grant Access to Agreement Manager Agreements
heading_path: Grant Access to Agreement Manager Agreements
text:
tched the search criteria are displayed.

**Update Agreement Manager access**

6. Expand (select) the Actions option for the relevant user or group and select the Manage Agreement Manager Access item.

   The User or Group profile page opens. The Agreement Manager Access tab is displayed by default.
7. Turn on the appropriate permissions for the selected user or group. From the Agreement Manager access section:
   - Assign the View all agreements in this account permission. Select the toggle so it is in the "on" (blue) position.

     This permission gives the user or group access to view and download all the agreements in the selected account.
   - Assign the Organize and Manage all agreements permission. Select the toggle so it is in the "on" (blue) position.

     This permission gives the user or group the highest level of Agreement Manager access. This access is typically reserved for contract managers and administrators. With this access, the user or group can create and manage:

     - Agreement sets
     - Agreement types
     - Custom fields
     - Duplicate parties and merged groups
     - Obligations and obligation types

**Assign bulk upload permissions (user-level permission)**

Important: The *bulk upload* permission is set at the user-level. This
permission is not available at the group-level. Keep in mind that user and group permissions are managed at the account-level as opposed to the organization-level.

8. If appropriate, assign bulk upload permissions to the selected user. Select the Upload up to 1,000 files at a time toggle so it is in the "on" (blue) position.

   This permission gives users access to *bulk upload* up to 1,000 files at a time. Users also have access to upload agreements to Agreement Manager from third-party cloud storage locations.
9. Select the Save Changes option.

   A message box opens to confirm that the Agreement Manager access changes were saved.

You have successfully granted users and groups Agreement Manager access to the agreements in a selected account.

title: Share Agreements with Others
text:
This topic answers the question: How do I share agreements with others? Learn how to
share an agreement with other users or groups, including assigning access levels for each
recipient.

The Share function provides an easy way for you to share an
agreement with other users and groups. This function also includes a step to assign
agreement access levels for each recipient.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign
   Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the
   AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed.
4. Perform a keyword search or use the available filters to locate the agreement
   that you want to share.
5. Expand the More actions (three dots) menu
   for the agreement that you want to share and select
   Share:

   ![A snippet of the Completed Documents view that shows the Share option in the expanded More actions (three dots) menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-documents-table_vertical-ellipses_Share.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY2ODUsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.dxl3vDo-ElhyZhdRNyOPWKvpT210gMj9DrP_1fOAZnY&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Completed Documents view that shows the Share option in the expanded More actions (three dots) menu.")

   The Share with others dialog box opens.

   ![A screenshot of the Share with others dialog box that shows the people with access to the agreement.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Share-with-others-dialog-box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY2ODUsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.dxl3vDo-ElhyZhdRNyOPWKvpT210gMj9DrP_1fOAZnY&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Share with others dialog box that shows the people with access to the agreement.")
6. Enter the name of the user or group that you want to share the agreement with
   in the Add user or group search field.

   A type-ahead dropdown displays names matching the criteria that you
   entered.
7. Select a user or group from the dropdown list.

   The Add users view is displayed.

   ![A screenshot of the Add users view that shows the Notify users by email and Access options.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Share-an-agreement_Add-users-dialog-box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY2ODUsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.dxl3vDo-ElhyZhdRNyOPWKvpT210gMj9DrP_1fOAZnY&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Add users view that shows the Notify users by email and Access options.")

   Note:

   The Notify users by email option is selected by
   default. Leave this checkbox selected to notify users that an agreement
   was shared with them.

   If you de-select this option, users are not notified of the shared
   agreement.
8. Expand the Access dropdown and select the appropriate
   access level.

   Choices are: Can Manage, Can
   Edit, and Can View.
9. Select the Copy link option to obtain a link to the
   agreement that can be shared with others. The link is copied to your
   clipboard.

   This link can be pasted into a message or email and shared with other users.
   Keep in mind, the user must have access to the agreement to view the
   link.
10. Select Share.

    The dialog box closes. The agreement is shared with the selected users and displayed in
    their agreement list table.

title: Share Agreements with Others
text:
er must have access to the agreement to view the
   link.
10. Select Share.

    The dialog box closes. The agreement is shared with the selected users and displayed in
    their agreement list table.

    Note: You can also share an agreement from the Agreement
    preview page. Expand the dropdown in the top-right corner and
    select Share:![A snippet of the Agreement preview page that shows the Share option located in the dropdown in the top-right corner.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-preview-page_expanded-Download-button.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY2ODUsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.dxl3vDo-ElhyZhdRNyOPWKvpT210gMj9DrP_1fOAZnY&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Agreement preview page that shows the Share option located in the dropdown in the top-right corner.")

    The Share with others dialog box opens.
    Complete the fields in the dialog box to share an agreement with other
    users or groups.

You have successfully shared an agreement with key
stakeholders.

title: Subscribe to Receive Report Notifications
text:
Learn how to set up one-time or recurring email reports for yourself or others to receive notifications about your obligations, renewals, or agreements.

You can subscribe to receive one-time or recurring email reports for any of the charts on the report dashboards - Agreements, Obligations, and Renewals.

Tip: Notification settings give you control over the email notifications that you receive. By default, all the notification types related to the completed agreements in your account are selected, or enabled. Deselect a checkbox if you no longer want to receive email notifications for the corresponding notification type.

See the [Customize Notification Settings for Your Completed Agreements](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tsk155b1ed7-7b45-43cd-be92-2a5279630c0f.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to customize your email notification settings from the Notification Preferences page.") article to learn more about updating your notification settings. Be sure to periodically check the Notification Preferences page as new notification types will be added in the future.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Insights from the global header.

   The Insights hub opens and the Overview is displayed.
3. Select the dashboard containing the reports to which you want to subscribe. Select either Agreements, Obligations, or Renewals from the navigation pane on the left.

   The selected dashboard opens.
4. Use the available filters to locate the relevant agreement, obligation, or renewals information.

   Tip: To subscribe to a custom report, locate and select the report from the Custom tab located under the Reports view. See the [View and Update Your Custom Reports](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=yuf1727801071281.html&_LANG=enus "This topic answers the question: How can I review and update my custom reports?") article to learn more about locating your custom reports.
5. Access the detailed report view for the chart to which you are subscribing. Select (click) the chart title or select the View Report option from the More actions (three dots) menu.

   The detailed report view opens for the selected chart. Next, apply filters, and customize the table columns until the table displays the data that you want to track.
6. Filter the table to refine the results list as needed or select Reset to clear existing filters.
7. Customize the information that is displayed in the table, if necessary:

   ![A screenshot of the Customize columns dialog box that shows the columns you can choose to display in the agreements table.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Party-hub_Overview-dashboard_Customize-columns.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcxMTksInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.Iy6U99DvoO8oV7VL4cWf2HZL2tncIfes_e-jcuM4JGI&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Customize columns dialog box that shows the columns you can choose to display in the agreements table.")

   1. Select the Customize columns (gear) icon.

      The Customize columns dialog box opens. These fields represent the columns in the table.
   2. Select the toggles to turn the associated field on (blue) or off (white).
   3. Re-order the columns as needed. Hover the cursor over the dots to the right of the field name and drag it to the appropriate location.
   4. Select Save.

title: Subscribe to Receive Report Notifications
text:
sociated field on (blue) or off (white).
   3. Re-order the columns as needed. Hover the cursor over the dots to the right of the field name and drag it to the appropriate location.
   4. Select Save.

   Note: If you made changes to the report, expand the dropdown and select Save As:![A snippet of an agreement dashboard report that shows the Save As option in the dropdown.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Agreements-Dashboard_Dashboard-report_Save-as.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcxMTksInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.Iy6U99DvoO8oV7VL4cWf2HZL2tncIfes_e-jcuM4JGI&_LANG=enus "A snippet of an agreement dashboard report that shows the Save As option in the dropdown.")

   Once saved, access the report from the Custom tab located under the Reports view. Continue to the next step to subscribe to your custom report.
8. Select the Manage Notification (bell) icon from the top-right corner.

   The Create Subscription dialog box opens.

   Note: The Manage report subscriptions dialog box displays when you have existing subscriptions. This includes the subscriptions to which you were added by other users. Expand the More actions (three dots) menu and choose to Edit or Delete the corresponding subscription:![A snippet of the Manage report subscriptions dialog box that shows the available Actions for an existing subscription.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Manage-report-subscriptions_Actions-menuP_EditDelete.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcxMTksInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.Iy6U99DvoO8oV7VL4cWf2HZL2tncIfes_e-jcuM4JGI&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Manage report subscriptions dialog box that shows the available Actions for an existing subscription.")If you were added to a subscription as an individual user, the subscription is displayed in the dialog box. Currently, subscriptions are not visible to users who were added to as part of a group.
9. Complete the fields in the Create subscription dialog box and select Schedule:

   ![A screenshot of the Create subscription dialog box that shows the subscription configuration fields.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Create-Subscription.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcxMTksInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.Iy6U99DvoO8oV7VL4cWf2HZL2tncIfes_e-jcuM4JGI&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Create subscription dialog box that shows the subscription configuration fields.")

   1. Enter a descriptive name in the Subscription Name field. This field defaults to the name of the report.
   2. Indicate how often and when you want to receive the report notification. Choose from Daily, Weekly, Monthly, and Once.

      Depending on the option you selected, you may be prompted to choose a send day.
   3. If necessary, turn on the Choose start and end date toggle and enter a Start date and End date.
   4. The notification is sent to you by default. Enter additional recipients in the Add a user or group field as needed.

      Note: When you create a subscription, you have the option to add individual users and groups. Once created, the subscription is displayed in the Manage report subscriptions dialog box for any individual users who were added. Currently, the subscription is not visible to users if they were added as part of a group.
   5. Select the Attach to email as CSV option as needed.
   6. Select Schedule.

   The dialog box closes. You have successfully signed up to receive report notifications.

You have successfully subscribed to receive a report email notification for a selected dashboard report.

title: Manage Notification Subscriptions
text:
Learn how to manage your report notification subscriptions.

Manage your subscriptions to help tailor your report notifications to fit your needs and preferences. For example, you may want to adjust the frequency of the email notifications or unsubscribe to them. This is done from the detailed report view for the report to which you are subscribed.

The Manage report subscriptions dialog box displays when you have existing subscriptions. This includes the subscriptions to which you were added as an individual user. Currently, subscriptions are not visible to users who were added as part of a group.

Tip: Notification settings give you control over the email notifications that you receive. By default, all the notification types related to the completed agreements in your account are selected, or enabled. Deselect a checkbox if you no longer want to receive email notifications for the corresponding notification type.

See the [Customize Notification Settings for Your Completed Agreements](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tsk155b1ed7-7b45-43cd-be92-2a5279630c0f.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to customize your email notification settings from the Notification Preferences page.") article to learn more about updating your notification settings. Be sure to periodically check the Notification Preferences page as new notification types will be added in the future.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Insights from the global header.

   The Insights hub opens and the Overview is displayed.
3. Select the dashboard containing the subscriptions that you want to manage. Select either Agreements, Obligations, or Renewals from the navigation pane on the left.

   The selected dashboard opens.
4. Access the detailed report view for the chart with the subscription that you want to manage. Select (click) the chart heading or select the View Report option from the More actions (ellipsis) menu.

   Note: To manage a custom report subscription, locate and select the report from the Custom tab located under the Reports view. See the [View and Update Your Custom Reports](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=yuf1727801071281.html&_LANG=enus "This topic answers the question: How can I review and update my custom reports?") article to learn more about locating your custom reports.
5. Select the Manage Notification (bell) icon from the top-right corner.

   The Manage report subscriptions dialog box opens.

   ![A snippet of the Manage report subscriptions dialog box that shows the Actions menu for an existing subscription.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Manage-report-subscriptions_Actions-menuP_EditDelete.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcxMjMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.sqJ0a7Y4Z3S74TTR-h-BRyIZqGSYoAvZ9Edv9RDnbEs&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Manage report subscriptions dialog box that shows the Actions menu for an existing subscription.")

   Note: When you create a subscription, you have the option to add individual users and groups. Once created, the subscription is displayed in the Manage report subscriptions dialog box for any individual users who were added. Currently, the subscription is not visible to users if they were added as part of a group.
6. Expand the More actions (three dots) menu associated with the subscription that you want to update and select the action you want to perform:
   - Select Edit to update the subscription.

     The Edit subscription dialog box opens. Update the subscription as needed, including changing the name, frequency, start and end dates, and recipients. Select Save to save changes to the subscription.
   - Select Delete to delete the subscription.

title: Manage Notification Subscriptions
text:
the subscription as needed, including changing the name, frequency, start and end dates, and recipients. Select Save to save changes to the subscription.
   - Select Delete to delete the subscription.

     The Delete subscription dialog box opens. Select Delete to confirm that you want to unsubscribe to the report notification. This action cannot be undone.

You have successfully managed the notification subscription for a dashboard report, including how to edit and delete a subscription.

title: Test the Extraction Accuracy for Your Custom Fields
heading_path: Test the Extraction Accuracy for Your Custom Fields
text: Learn how to test the AI detection accuracy for your custom fields and refining the results to improve accuracy.

title: Test the Extraction Accuracy for Your Custom Fields
heading_path: Test the Extraction Accuracy for Your Custom Fields
text:
This topic reviews the next step of the Track this field with AI wizard, *Test for accuracy*. These steps are completed by users with Organize and manage all agreements access to Agreement Manager agreements and functionality.

Note: Administrators automatically have access to configure agreement data. For non-administrator users, this feature requires Organize and manage all agreements access to Agreement Manager. These tasks are primarily completed by administrators and contract managers.

Complete these topics before attempting this task:

- [Create Custom Fields from the Agreement Preview](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tsk0830ea60-9b29-4efa-b73f-9eb15b2d6561.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to create custom fields from the Agreement preview page for a selected agreement.")
- [Add Examples of Your Custom Fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tskcb56face-b8de-4cf9-85ce-6eac3669dd82.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to complete the second step of the AI detection set-up process, Select examples.")

Before you activate the AI detection for custom extractions, the setup wizard prompts you to test for accuracy. This step is required when using the Track this field with AI wizard. The results are a good indicator of the quality and accuracy that you can expect from the AI detection before activating it.

In the previous step, you selected documents that contained examples of the custom field. After confirming the field values and saving the examples, the set-up wizard advanced to the last step. The final step of the process is *Test for accuracy*.

You are prompted to test the accuracy of the AI detection:![A screenshot of the final step of the Track this field with AI wizard, Test for accuracy.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Fields-Manager_Agreement-preview_Test-for-accuracy_Start-Test-option.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2OTgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.o9SUg-AB4_xBTE0JD8gGnS3ipSemeZsV7ocSfwzAf2k&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the final step of the Track this field with AI wizard, Test for accuracy.")

Important: Use the Finish Later option to save your progress and finish the AI detection set-up later. Using this option ends the Track this field with AI experience. See the [Enable AI Detection for Your Custom Fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tsk02bd31a2-b125-4d53-b632-20d9972163a8.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to enable the AI detection for your custom fields. Once activated, the field is surfaced in your new agreements automatically.") topic for steps to finish the process.

1. Select the Start Test option.

   The agreement list table opens. You are prompted to select three or more agreements that contain the custom field and at least one agreement that does not.

**Select agreements for the test**

2. Locate the documents that you want to use to test the AI detection accuracy:

   ![A snippet of the Test for accuracy step that shows the search field and filter options to locate documents.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Fields-Manager_Agreement-preview_Test-for-accuracy_search-filters.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2OTgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.o9SUg-AB4_xBTE0JD8gGnS3ipSemeZsV7ocSfwzAf2k&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Test for accuracy step that shows the search field and filter options to locate documents.")

title: Test the Extraction Accuracy for Your Custom Fields
heading_path: Test the Extraction Accuracy for Your Custom Fields
text:
NoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.o9SUg-AB4_xBTE0JD8gGnS3ipSemeZsV7ocSfwzAf2k&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Test for accuracy step that shows the search field and filter options to locate documents.")

   1. Search for the relevant agreements. Enter a keyword or search string in the search field. The dropdown opens and search suggestions are displayed. Select a search suggestion or press the Enter key.
   2. Filter the agreement list table to find the relevant agreements. For example, filter by Sets, Status, Parties, Agreement Type, or AI Suggestions.

      Tip: Use the Filters option to apply additional filters to the results table.

      Expand the filter, choose the relevant filter value, and select the Apply option.

   The results table updates and the documents that match the search criteria and applied filters are displayed.
3. Choose the documents that you want to use in the test:

   ![A snippet of the Test for accuracy step that shows selected agreements for the test.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Fields-Manager_Agreement-preview_Test-for-accuracy_Test-Agreements-option.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2OTgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.o9SUg-AB4_xBTE0JD8gGnS3ipSemeZsV7ocSfwzAf2k&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Test for accuracy step that shows selected agreements for the test.")

   Tip: Select the Show Selected Agreements option to review your selected documents. If necessary, select the Return to Search Results option and make changes to your selections.

**Test the accuracy and verify results**

4. Select the Test Agreements option in the bottom right.

   An agreement preview opens for the first document. The example documents are displayed in the pane on the left.
5. Review the identified field in the context of the document. Verify that the AI detection surfaced the correct value.

   ![An animated screenshot of the agreement preview that shows the user verifying the identified value in the document.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Fields-Manager_Agreement-preview_Test-for-accuracy_confirm-accuracy_animated.gif?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2OTgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.o9SUg-AB4_xBTE0JD8gGnS3ipSemeZsV7ocSfwzAf2k&_LANG=enus "An animated screenshot of the agreement preview that shows the user verifying the identified value in the document.")Scroll to locate the field value in the document. The value is highlighted in the preview pane.

   - Select the Yes option if the AI detection identified the correct field.
   - Select the No option if the AI detection did not identify the correct field.

   The agreement preview updates and the next document is displayed. Repeat this step to verify the identified field in the remaining documents.

**Correct the values for incorrect results**

Provide the correct answer if the identified value is incorrect. After selecting the No option, a series of questions displays. You are prompted to provide the correct context and value for the field.

![An animated screenshot of the agreement preview that shows the user providing the correct value for an incorrect value.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Fields-Manager_Agreement-preview_Test-for-accuracy_Provide-correct-value_animated.gif?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2OTgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.o9SUg-AB4_xBTE0JD8gGnS3ipSemeZsV7ocSfwzAf2k&_LANG=enus "An animated screenshot of the agreement preview that shows the user providing the correct value for an incorrect value.")

title: Test the Extraction Accuracy for Your Custom Fields
heading_path: Test the Extraction Accuracy for Your Custom Fields
text:
F6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.o9SUg-AB4_xBTE0JD8gGnS3ipSemeZsV7ocSfwzAf2k&_LANG=enus "An animated screenshot of the agreement preview that shows the user providing the correct value for an incorrect value.")

6. Locate the correct value within the agreement. Use the Search (magnifying glass) icon in the top right to search for the field value.
7. Indicate whether the field is present in the agreement:
   - Select the No option if the field is not present in the agreement.
   - Select the Yes option if the field is present in the agreement. You are prompted to provide the correct value.
8. Highlight the paragraph or clause that contains the field value and select the Confirm Selection option.
9. Enter the correct value in the text-entry field for the result.

   Tip: For longer values, use the Copy Text option to copy and paste the text into the field.
10. Select the Confirm option.

    The agreement preview updates and the next document is displayed.

**View accuracy results**

After you verify the results for each example, you are ready to view the test results.

11. Select the Done option at the bottom of the pane.

    You are returned to the Track this field with AI wizard. The results of the accuracy test are displayed.

    In this example, the AI detection did not pass the accuracy test.

    ![A screenshot that shows the AI detection did not pass the accuracy test.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Fields-Manager_Agreement-preview_Accuracy-score_Failed.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2OTgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.o9SUg-AB4_xBTE0JD8gGnS3ipSemeZsV7ocSfwzAf2k&_LANG=enus "A screenshot that shows the AI detection did not pass the accuracy test.")You have a few choices at this point:
    - Start over. Use the Update Details option to go back to the first step of the setup wizard.
    - Choose new examples: Use the Modify Examples option to select new example agreements that contain the custom field.
    - Re=run the test: Use the Test Again option (recommended) to perform the
      test again. The corrections that you made when verifying the
      identified values are used to teach the AI detection. Re-run the
      test to see your new test score.
    - Activate the field: Use the Activate option to activate the AI detection for the custom field. This option is not recommended if the accuracy score is less than 80%.
12. Make adjustments as needed and perform the accuracy test again. Select the Test Again option. You are prompted to complete the process again. Return to the beginning of the topic and start again at step 2.

    When you achieve an accuracy score of 80% or better, proceed to the activation part of the set-up process.

    ![A screenshot that shows the results of the accuracy test, including options for activating the field.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Fields-Manager_Agreement-preview_Activate-Find-past-docs.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc2OTgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.o9SUg-AB4_xBTE0JD8gGnS3ipSemeZsV7ocSfwzAf2k&_LANG=enus "A screenshot that shows the results of the accuracy test, including options for activating the field.")

    The last part of the set-up process is activating the AI detection for the custom field and finding it in past agreements. Complete the [Activate Your Custom Fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tsk443eb55b-5980-4fa6-b145-e00b3415cdbe.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to activate the AI detection for a custom field and surface it in selected historical agreements.") task to activate the field and find it in your historical agreements.

You have successfully tested the AI detection accuracy for the custom field.

title: Agreement Manager Release Notes
text:
Look up new features, bug fixes, and other changes for Docusign Agreement Manager.

Note: The features and enhancements referenced in this topic are available in Demo accounts, Production accounts, or both. Availability is dependent on the ramp schedule and can vary across regions and languages.

title: Agreement Manager Release Notes
text: These fixes, features, and product enhancements have been delivered since the last release.

title: Agreement Manager Release Notes
text:
Docusign Navigator was renamed to Docusign Agreement Manager.

Agreement Manager is an AI-powered repository that centralizes signed agreements and uses Docusign Iris to turn them into structured, searchable data. From Agreement Manager, teams can quickly find, analyze, and act on the terms that matter most.

This change is focused on making the product name clearer and more descriptive. Your existing Agreement Manager features, usage, and workflows continue to work as they do today. No customer action is required.

title: Agreement Manager Release Notes
text:
Docusign Maestro was renamed to Workflow Builder across the Intelligent Agreement Management (IAM) platform.

Workflow Builder is the tool used to design and automate your agreement workflows. This tool helps you understand, build, and trigger multi-step agreement processes.

This change is focused on the name only. The underlying functionality and any existing entitlements for your workflows remain the same. You can continue using your current workflows without interruption.

title: Agreement Manager Release Notes
text:
These fixes, features, and product enhancements have been delivered since the last release.

Note: The features and enhancements referenced in this topic are available in Demo accounts, Production accounts, or both. Availability is dependent on the ramp schedule and can vary.

title: Agreement Manager Release Notes
text:
Advanced search queries give you more control over how results are matched and ranked. You can use a combination of Boolean operators, exact and proximity searches, boosting, and wildcards in a single query:

![A snippet of the Completed Documents page that highlights an example query string that combines terms, operators, and modifiers.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Advanced-search-queries_example-querystring_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc5NjksInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.PojXCt6upvZp-tB7eDsX90Pm-cCMDjrUaMW7FrP0aO0&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Completed Documents page that highlights an example query string that combines terms, operators, and modifiers.")

Enter the query string directly in the search field when you want to:

- Combine multiple conditions
- Control how closely words appear together
- Emphasize some terms over others

See the [Advanced Search Queries](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=conce9d9428-f27e-49ad-9001-3c7a0820ae78.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the advanced search query feature that gives you control over how your results are matched and ranked.") article to learn more about using these queries.

title: Use App Detail Pages to Learn About Docusign Apps
heading_path: Use App Detail Pages to Learn About Docusign Apps
text:
Use a Docusign app's detail page to view important information such as
an app summary, features, permissions, and resources.

Each Docusign app has a detail page with important information about the
app, such as a preview features, permissions, required plans, resources.![A screenshot of a sample Docusign App Detail page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/ac-use-this-app-det-page.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTgxOTUsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.V7VmlxhSHrZuLvpLwUtgxuZUDC6YrJiAp1nXJw0XykI&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of a sample Docusign App Detail page.")

title: Use App Detail Pages to Learn About Docusign Apps
heading_path: Use App Detail Pages to Learn About Docusign Apps
text:
The app detail page includes this information:

- Name
- Overview
- Use This App: Select the Docusign feature that you want to use with the app to access the page on which you use the app. For example, selecting Open in Workflow Builder opens a new workflow.
- [Docusign plan requirements](https://ecom.docusign.com/plans-and-pricing/esignature?utm_source=google&utm_medium=cpc&utm_campaign=GBL_XX_PRF_NEW_2401_SEMBranded&utm_term=docusign%20plans&campaignid=14964582893&adgroupid=127314395926&location=9198112&device=c&matchtype=e&extensionid=&creative=615714019859&keyword=docusign%20plans&placement=&network=g&gclid=Cj0KCQjwjJrCBhCXARIsAI5x66U-Tq5EeW33f-pTTNzCzGNWdogOf4m3z9WCSqCcG5BIia90eYEx7uEaAqTEEALw_wcB&gad_source=1&gad_campaignid=14964582893&gbraid=0AAAAADicDKsNWbtN78PfC2n0xdDWf3d-7)
- App Version number
- [Available Regions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=con81e64917-4912-40c5-a4aa-69f2053f312a.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about finding the global regions where Docusign apps are available. These apps are currently deployed across five different datacenters: U.S., CA, AU, EU, and JP."):
  The global regions where Docusign apps are available.
- Certifications: [FedRAMP-certified
  apps](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=cone7f31b50-43c1-40a1-b6ed-d7ae1425ce61.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to identify FedRAMP-certified apps in the Docusign App Center.") if available
- Developer (who builds the app)
- Resources
- Requirements: Any app prerequisites
- [Workflow
  templates](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=con8f35c78f-56b7-466c-91e6-c0ea7d03c516.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to select and initiate a Docusign Workflow Builder template from the Docusign App Center page.") that support this app
- Works with: Docusign features where you use this app
  - Agreement Actions
  - Agreement Manager
  - CLM
  - eSignature
  - Workflow Builder
- Pricing: Option to view [available pricing plans](https://ecom.docusign.com/plans-and-pricing/iam?utm_source=google&utm_medium=cpc&utm_campaign=GBL_XX_PRF_NEW_2401_SEMBranded&utm_term=docusign%20plans&campaignid=14964582893&adgroupid=127314395926&location=9198112&device=c&matchtype=e&extensionid=&creative=615714019859&keyword=docusign%20plans&placement=&network=g&gclid=Cj0KCQjwjJrCBhCXARIsAI5x66U-Tq5EeW33f-pTTNzCzGNWdogOf4m3z9WCSqCcG5BIia90eYEx7uEaAqTEEALw_wcB&gad_source=1&gad_campaignid=14964582893&gbraid=0AAAAADicDKsNWbtN78PfC2n0xdDWf3d-7)

title: Use App Detail Pages to Learn About Docusign Apps
heading_path: Use App Detail Pages to Learn About Docusign Apps
text:
Depending on the app's status, you can:

- Install an app by selecting Install as described in the [Install Apps in the App Center](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=ctg1698170340729.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to install Docusign extension apps in the Docusign App Center.") article.
- Manage an installed app by selecting Manage as described in
  the [Manage Apps in the App Center](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=zxa1698170686717.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to view, install, or uninstall Docusign App Center apps.") article.

title: Language and Regional Support
text:
Read about language support and regional availability for Agreement Manager AI models and features.

Agreement Manager is available to customers globally, however, the features and insights vary by region and language. This page provides an overview of the supported agreement types, attributes, and data points that provide insights into your agreements. Additional information can be found in the [General Limitations and Considerations for Agreement Manager](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=jyn1704248204281.html&_LANG=enus "This topic answers the question: Where can I see a list of the Agreement Manager limitations and considerations associated with this release?") article.

CAUTION: The accuracy and appropriateness of AI-assisted insights may vary depending on the agreement language and customer use case. Customers should review all suggested data points for accuracy and to determine if a value is appropriate for its particular circumstances.

Important:

As new or updated AI models are released, your historical agreements can be processed again to surface these data points. Depending on the type of new insights that are available, users can choose to:

- Opt in for reprocessing from the New Insights Available badge (obligation-related attributes and insights)
- Reprocess all the outdated agreements for the account (new or updated AI model)
- Reprocess selected agreements (standard or custom fields)

See the [Agreement Manager Reprocessing Experience](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=con44590246-0f0e-43b4-9692-caadb57335ac.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about the Agreement Manager reprocessing experience, including its purpose, trigger points, and job size considerations.") article to learn more about this experience.

title: Language and Regional Support
text:
AI-assisted insights are currently available for agreements in English, French, German, Japanese, Brazilian-Portuguese, and Spanish.

Important: English (global) is applied to all customers deployed across Docusign datacenters located in the United States (NA), Europe (EU), Canada (CA), Australia (AU), and Japan (JP).

Note: See the [Extracted Agreement Insights and Definitions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=uwk1702945852405.html&_LANG=enus "Look up the names and definitions for the attributes and values that are surfaced in Agreement Manager agreements with AI. This topic answers the question: What data is being extracted from my Agreement Manager agreements?") article to learn more about the insights that are surfaced with AI.

The language support matrix shows the supported attributes for each agreement language and locale. Supported attributes or fields, are surfaced automatically during agreement processing.

title: Language and Regional Support
heading_path: Limitation: "Dropdown" attributes (fields) with pre-populated value sets
text:
For attributes (fields) that use dropdowns with pre-populated value sets, only those values are surfaced in your agreements. Agreement processing extracts an attribute value only if it matches one of the values in the dropdown.

When processing cannot locate the value within the agreement, the extracted value is *Other*. For example, if an agreement's payment term is 50 days, the surfaced Payment Term value is *Other*:

![A screenshot of the Party Term filter that shows the pre-populated value set for the attribute (field).](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Payment-term-filter_All-options.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTgxMDEsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.o1pGerNKS2kYHbo0ZysjzF3TgYv1W4whmPBXj-Xx6Ak&_LANG=enus)

This limitation applies to these attributes (fields) and values:

| Attribute (Field) | Value Set |
| --- | --- |
| Assignment (Change of Control) | No, or consent required; Yes, notice required; Yes, restrictions apply; Yes, with conditions |
| Assignment (General) | No, or consent required; Yes; Yes, with conditions |
| Assignment (Termination Rights) | Yes |
| Employment Type | Fixed Term; Full Time; Intern; Part Time |
| Late Fees Apply | Yes; No |
| NDA Type | Mutual; One Way |
| Party Role | Buyer, Seller |
| Payment Term | 15 days; 30 days; 45 days; 60 days; 7 days; 90 days; >90 days; On Receipt; Other; Under 7 days |
| Renewal Type | Auto-Renew; Evergreen; Fixed Term; Renewal Option; Unspecified |

title: Language and Regional Support
text: Review the table to learn about limitations and exceptions to the Agreement Manager global expansion.

title: Language and Regional Support
text:
Review the table to learn about limitations and exceptions to the Agreement Manager global expansion.

| Limitation | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Custom agreement types and fields** | Custom agreement types and fields are supported in English (global).  If your language and region are not supported:  - Your custom agreement types are not identified during processing. - The values associated with your custom fields are not extracted.  This also applies to standard agreement types and fields (attributes) that are not supported in your language and region. Important: You can report on custom and standard agreement types even if they are not supported in your language.  Manually add field values for custom and standard fields (attributes) that are not supported in your language. You can also assign custom and standard agreement types to relevant documents. See these support articles to learn more about assigning agreement types and adding field values:  - [Update the Agreement Type for an Agreement](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=nez1737143575705.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to update the agreement type value for an agreement.") - [Enter Values for Empty Attribute Fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=ato1712248318394.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to locate and enter values for attributes with missing or empty values.") |
| **Obligations** | Obligations are surfaced in English (U.S.) agreements only. The AI model that processes obligations is run from a U.S. server and can only process English agreements in this region.  If your language and region are not supported, manually create the obligations to include this information in dashboards, reports, and other insights.  See these support articles to learn more about obligation management:  - [Obligation Management](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=csm1727128259486.html&_LANG=enus "Read about obligation management, including how to use this functionality to capture, track, and fulfill your agreement obligations. This topic answers the question: How can I find and track my agreement obligations?") - [Manually Create an Obligation](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=rrf1727128588540.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to create an obligation from the Agreement preview page for a selected agreement.") |
| **Agreement reports** | Agreement reports that are generated based on unsupported attributes (fields) are not supported for that language and region.  These reports are not supported for Brazilian-Portuguese, Japanese, and Spanish:  - Top liabilities (unsupported attributes: Liability Cap Amount, Liability Cap Duration, and Liability Cap Multiplier) - Agreements with late fee clauses (unsupported attributes: Late Fees Apply and Late Fee %) - Assignment controls (unsupported attributes: Assignment (Change of Control), Assignment (General), and Assignment (Termination Rights)) - Payment terms (unsupported attribute: Payment Terms)  These reports are supported for English, French, and German.  Remember: Unsupported custom and standard fields (attributes) can be updated manually to populate the insights in various dashboards, reports, and other insights. For example, if you manually add payment terms to your agreements, these agreements are reflected in the Payment terms report. See the [Enter Values for Empty Attribute Fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=ato1712248318394.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to locate and enter values for attributes with missing or empty values.") support article to learn more about updating field values manually. |
| **Overview reports** | The Overview dashboard is part of the party hub. It displays a visual summary of all active agreements that are tied to a specific party or group.  Any Overview reports that are generated based on unsupported attributes (fields) are not supported for that language and region.  These reports are not supported for Brazilian-Portuguese, Japanese, and Spanish:  - Payment terms (unsupported attribute: Payment Terms) Note: The Payment terms report is supported for English, French, and German.  Unsupported custom and standard fields (attributes) can be updated manually to populate the insights in dashboards and reports.
