For example, if you manually add payment terms to your agreements, these agreements are reflected in the Payment terms report. - Obligations by due date (unsupported attributes: all attributes in the *Payment Obligation Insights* section in the [Supported attributes (fields)](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=xvp1729795957126.html&_LANG=enus&anchor=xvp1729795957126__section_sxx_xqz_ffc&anchor=xvp1729795957126__section_e22_zm2_fdc) table) Note: Obligations are surfaced in English agreements only.  If your language and region are not supported, manually create the obligations to include this information in dashboards, reports, and other insights.  See the [Manually Create an Obligation](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=rrf1727128588540.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to create an obligation from the Agreement preview page for a selected agreement.") article to learn more about creating obligations. |
| **Search filters** | Search filters exist for all the standard fields (attributes). When supported, these attributes and field values are surfaced in agreements and can be used to filter to agreement list table.  If you apply a filter for an unsupported field, the query only returns results in supported languages. It does not produce results in unsupported languages. |

title: Language and Regional Support
text:
Organizations that operate globally often have agreements in both English and their local language. English is used for international and local business contracts, while the local language is used for domestic agreements. Many of these agreements are available in multiple languages.

During processing, Languages are surfaced based on the data in your agreements.

![A snippet of the Agreement preview page that highlights the LANGUAGES field in the Details pane.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-preview-page_Details-pane_Langauges-highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTgxMDEsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.o1pGerNKS2kYHbo0ZysjzF3TgYv1W4whmPBXj-Xx6Ak&_LANG=enus)

Note: Be sure to validate the suggested Languages in your agreements to ensure accurate reporting and analysis. See the [Validate Extracted Field Values (Complete a Data Review)](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=yqq1702945693043.html&_LANG=enus "This topic answers the question: How can I make sure that the suggested attribute (field) values are accurate? Learn how to complete a data review to verify suggested field values.") article to learn how to perform a data review.

Language detection looks for supported languages within your agreements:

- If a supported language is detected in a significant portion of an agreement, the agreement is processed with AI. The detected language and other supported attributes are surfaced in the agreement.
- If a supported language is missing or insignificant, the agreement is not processed for that language. The agreement is still processed with AI to surface other supported attributes.

See the [Supported attributes (fields)](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=xvp1729795957126.html&anchor=xvp1729795957126__section_zwp_25y_x2c) table to learn about the supported languages.

Important: All detected languages are surfaced and displayed in the Details  pane for the Languages  attribute (field). Agreements are only processed for supported languages that are detected in a significant portion of the agreement.

title: Language and Regional Support
text:
Once surfaced, you can filter your agreements by document language. Locate the Languages filter in the Filters dialog box:

![A screenshot of the Filters dialog box that shows the Languages filter that's used to filter the agreement list by document language.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Languages_and_Regional_Support_All_Filters_Languages_filter.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTgxMDEsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.o1pGerNKS2kYHbo0ZysjzF3TgYv1W4whmPBXj-Xx6Ak&_LANG=enus)

You can also display the Languages column in the agreement list table. Locate and turn on the Languages toggle in the Show or Hide Fields dialog box:

![A screenshot of the Show or Hide Fields dialog box that shows the Languages toggle.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Languages-and-Regional-Support_Customize-columns_Languages-toggle.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTgxMDEsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.o1pGerNKS2kYHbo0ZysjzF3TgYv1W4whmPBXj-Xx6Ak&_LANG=enus)

title: Use Case: Validate Suggested Attribute Values for Your Agreements
heading_path: Use Case: Validate Suggested Attribute Values for Your Agreements
text:
This topic answers the question: How do I review the suggested attribute (field) values for my agreements?

Data reviews are necessary to validate the data that is surfaced during processing and to provide values for empty attributes (fields). This data review is an important part of using Agreement Manager. It ensures accurate agreement data for reporting, business planning, sales forecasting, and negotiations.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Completed Documents view opens. A list of documents to which the logged-in user has access is displayed in the table.
4. Expand the Filters option and select the View All Filters option from the dropdown.

   The Filters dialog box opens and a list of available filters is displayed.
5. Expand the AI Suggestions filter.

   The AI Suggestions dialog box opens.
6. Choose the Needs Review option and select Apply.

   The dialog box closes.
7. Select Save.

   The Filters dialog box closes and the agreements that require an AI data review are displayed in the table.

   Note: ![An image of the spark icon used to denote the use of AI.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/AI-Spark-icon.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTYyNDQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.X4OWOOYe4y1FUNXEAqopT-vxtP9ehAoqEzixWb90WeA&_LANG=enus "An image of the spark icon used to denote the use of AI.") The spark icon is a processing indicator. Hover your cursor over the icon to view the processing status for the agreement. For example:![A screenshot of the text that displays when you hover your cursor over a solid-state spark icon.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Processing-Navigator-Agreements_spark-indicator_hover-message.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTYyNDQsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.X4OWOOYe4y1FUNXEAqopT-vxtP9ehAoqEzixWb90WeA&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the text that displays when you hover your cursor over a solid-state spark icon.")

   See the [Processing Agreement Manager Agreements](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=rvw1736364838500.html&_LANG=enus "Read about agreement processing and how to opt in for additional processing to surface new insights within your agreements.") topic to learn more about processing indicators.
8. Review the list of agreements and select (click) the agreement you want to review.

   The Agreement preview page opens for the selected agreement. The Details pane is displayed by default and shows a list of attributes and suggested values. Attributes, or fields, are surfaced in the agreements based on the identified agreement type.

   Validate the suggested values for each attribute.
9. Place the Details pane into *edit* mode.
   - Select the Edit (pencil) icon.
   - Select the Review All option.

   Note: You must have Organize and manage all agreements access to perform data reviews.

   The Review & edit details pane opens for updates.

   Important: Updating the suggested attribute values does not change the content in the agreement.
10. Accept or edit the suggested values until each attribute is validated. If the attribute value does not exist within the agreement, leave the field empty.
    1. Compare the suggested attribute values against the data in the agreement.

       - Select (click) an attribute to view the suggested value within the agreement.

title: Use Case: Validate Suggested Attribute Values for Your Agreements
heading_path: Use Case: Validate Suggested Attribute Values for Your Agreements
text:
reement, leave the field empty.
    1. Compare the suggested attribute values against the data in the agreement.

       - Select (click) an attribute to view the suggested value within the agreement.

         The value is highlighted in the agreement and a list of suggested values is displayed.
       - Select the Accept AI Suggestion (checkmark) icon if the value is *correct*.
       - Delete the existing value and enter the correct value if the suggestion is *incorrect*.
    2. Continue reviewing and updating attribute values until all the attributes have been verified against the agreement.

       Attributes are grouped based on usage and purpose. A progress bar is displayed at the top of each group and shows the number of suggestions that require review.
11. Select Save.

    The agreement details are saved. The Details pane is reverted to read-only mode.
12. Select the Close (x) icon from the top-left corner.

    You are returned to the Completed Documents view. Your filters are still applied and agreements requiring review are displayed in the table.

title: Work with Docusign App Connections
text:
Learn how to create and manage Docusign app connections. Use
connections to access different accounts and environments.

With Docusign app connections, you can select different accounts
when creating workflow processes.

You can perform these connection actions:

- Add a new connection
- Manage Connections
  - Edit connection name
  - Delete a connection
  - Manage Field Mappings: Review the suggested mappings to ensure that the data is
    correctly mapped to Docusign IAM fields
  - Reload objects: Refresh the Connections display to view the latest connections
    activity
- Refresh

  Note: This function is not available for all
  apps.
- View connection Details: Select an existing connection to view the app's detail page
  and view connection status
- Uninstall the app: Uninstall the app from the App Center

  Attention: When you uninstall an app, workflows and templates that use the app
  are permanently affected.

title: Work with Docusign App Connections
text:
Your role determines the connection types that you can create and manage.

- Administrator: Private or shared connections
- Account user (no Administrator permission): Private connections

title: Work with Docusign App Connections
text:
You can create Docusign apps connections for use in Docusign agreement workflows for different user credentials.

Here are the available app connection types:

Shared
:   Created by an Admin. Any account user can view and use the connection.

Private
:   Created by an Admin or an account user. Only the account user who created
    the connection can view and use the connection.

For the Salesforce app, you can also create connections based on the environment
type.

- Demos (Sandbox) account
- Production account

title: Benefits and Features of Agreement Manager
heading_path: Benefits and Features of Agreement Manager
text:
Read about Docusign Agreement Manager, a smart repository built specifically for agreements.

Docusign Agreement Manager helps organizations to store, manage, and analyze agreements with help from Docusign Iris. It is part of the Docusign Intelligent Agreement Management (IAM) platform. With Agreement Manager, you can:

- **Drive efficiencies for your internal teams:** centralize agreements and empower your teams to find what they need fast.
- **Uncover opportunities to reduce costs:** use AI to handle tedious tasks and uncover cost-saving opportunities by harnessing agreement data.
- **Increase oversight and security:** tighten agreement access with rules that ensure only the right users have access to the right agreements.

title: Benefits and Features of Agreement Manager
heading_path: Benefits and Features of Agreement Manager
text: Agreement Manager supports these features and benefits:

title: Benefits and Features of Agreement Manager
heading_path: Benefits and Features of Agreement Manager
text:
Agreement Manager supports these features and benefits:

| **Feature** | **Description** |
| --- | --- |
| Account-level activity log | The account-level Activity Log provides a centralized location to view aggregate, user-performed events across all the agreements in an account:   - Access updates (agreement and agreement set sharing) - Agreement downloads - Agreement set operations (set creation and updates) - Agreement views |
| Agreement access permissions | Administrators can grant users or groups account-level access to agreements.  - Contract managers with the appropriate permissions can create agreement sets to control access to agreements. - Users can share agreements with other users or groups in their account. |
| Agreement-level audits | Agreement-level audits are essential for minimizing errors, ensuring compliance, and troubleshooting issues.  The Activity Log records and displays agreement-level events that were performed by a user, including document uploads and access updates. |
| Agreement hierarchies | Agreement hierarchies help you gain a comprehensive understanding of how your documents are legally related to one another. With agreement hierarchies, you can more easily track down and understand related documents like amendments and addenda.  See the [Agreement Hierarchies](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=con1b654754-bd6d-4fbd-ac0c-8c44a9b1d666.html&_LANG=enus "Read about agreement hierarchies, including their purpose, use cases, entry points, and common tasks.") article to learn more about this feature. |
| Agreement ingestion | Your Docusign historical completed agreements are imported to Agreement Manager automatically upon account provisioning. The ingestion limits for historical completed agreements are:  - 500.000 (North American customers) - 100,000 (International customers)   There is a 100-MB file size limit when uploading or ingesting documents to Agreement Manager. |
| Agreement list | See all your completed agreements in a single list and sort or filter by various attributes. |
| Agreement preview | Preview any agreement with key information highlighted thanks to AI functionality. |
| Agreement reminders | Stay ahead of important dates with reminders for agreement milestones, such as renewals and terminations. |
| Agreement reprocessing | As new or updated AI models are released, your historical agreements can be processed again to surface these data points. Depending on the type of new insights that are available, users can choose to:  - Opt in for reprocessing from the New Insights Available badge (obligation-related attributes and insights) - Reprocess all the outdated agreements for the account (new or updated AI model) - Reprocess selected agreements (standard or custom fields)    See the [Agreement Manager Reprocessing Experience](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=con44590246-0f0e-43b4-9692-caadb57335ac.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about the Agreement Manager reprocessing experience, including its purpose, trigger points, and job size considerations.") article to learn more about this experience. |
| Agreement set management | Make sure that the right people have access to relevant agreements by organizing documents into sets. Agreement sets can be used as an organizational tool or as a way to control access to agreements. During set creation, apply smart filters to your agreement sets to create a recurring ingestion rule. When new agreements match the applied filters, they are added to the agreement set automatically. |
| Agreement statuses | Gain clarity on which agreements are active or inactive and quickly understand the state of an agreement. Primary and secondary statuses are calculated based on the current date compared to an agreement's effective and expiration dates. See the [Agreement Statuses](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=con6a3bd080-da17-4d1c-a92a-b9b9340c44fd.html&_LANG=enus "Read about agreement statuses, including how they are calculated, visibility within Agreement Manager, and which types of statuses are being tracked.") article to learn more about this feature. |
| Agreement type management | Configure your agreement types and fields in one central place for one source of truth for agreement data throughout the agreement lifecycle. |
| AI-assisted insights | Rely on Docusign Iris to surface key attributes and field values associated with your agreements. This feature facilitates the standardization of agreement data and better reporting. |
| Attribute search | Quickly locate an agreement by searching for key attributes associated with the agreement, such as contract value, agreement type, and date range. |
| Auto-suggestion for custom agreement types | When uploading agreements to Agreement Manager, if 10 or more similar agreements are identified as Miscellaneous, a custom agreement type is suggested.    For example, 10 charter agreements were uploaded and identified as *Miscellaneous*. The agreements are recognized as being similar and a custom agreement type, Charter Agreement, is suggested:  A snippet of the agreement list table that shows a suggested custom agreement type for a Miscellaneous document.  Use the Create option to create the suggested custom agreement type. See the [Use Auto-Suggestion to Create Custom Agreement Types](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tsk2093dc24-1da8-4393-b4cd-526990bc7118.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to create a custom agreement type from the auto-suggestion feature.") article for steps to complete this task. |
| Bulk agreement upload | Bulk upload historical agreements that were completed outside of Docusign or choose agreements from a third-party cloud storage location. There is a 100-MB file size limit when uploading or ingesting documents to Agreement Manager. |
| Bulk metadata editing | Bulk edit your agreement data with a Docusign-generated spreadsheet file:  - Download a pre-formatted spreadsheet file for a group of selected documents. - Edit the spreadsheet to include the new metadata values. - Return to Agreement Manager and import the file.  Once successfully processed, the new and updated values are reflected in the selected documents. See the [Bulk Metadata Editing](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=concb493b18-adf8-4fe5-8b99-dd90cbc28399.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the Bulk Metadata Editing feature, including use cases, typical roles and permissions, and a process overview.") article to learn more about this feature. |
| Centralized storage | Store all your agreements in one location to improve agreement organization and visibility. |
| Custom fields and types | Create custom fields and types within agreement type management to track specific information unique to organizational needs. |
| Duplicate party management | Duplicate party management helps keep party data clean and consistent. Party names are identified and standardized during ingestion. When duplicate parties are detected, you are prompted to group the parties under one preferred party name.  Note: The standardization process ensures that parties are not considered distinct because of case sensitivity, whitespaces, punctuation, and legal entity descriptors.  The preferred name is displayed in various filters and columns throughout Agreement Manager and the IAM platform. See the [Duplicate Party Management and Standardization](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=ssf1712255476566.html&_LANG=enus "This topic answers the question: What does party management in Agreement Manager entail? Read about the party management features designed to help you manage parties that have similar names. For example, party standardization and consolidation at the point of ingestion.") article to learn more about this feature. |
| Individual agreement upload | Users can upload agreements ad hoc and associate them with relevant parties. There is a 100-MB file size limit when uploading or ingesting documents to Agreement Manager. |
| Insights bar | The Insights bar is an interactive deck of cards on the Agreement Manager homepage, located above the agreement list table. It highlights key trends and suggests actions so you can manage agreements in one place. |
| Job monitoring | Access job details for your agreement uploads and processing jobs. The job monitoring hub provides a centralized location to view and track the status of these jobs. |
| Keyword search | Quickly locate an agreement by searching for specific text or phrases within the content of the agreement. |
| Natural language search | Enter a natural language question or phrase into the search field on the Completed Documents page. The query returns a direct answer to the question and a list of relevant agreements. Important: Depending on the nature of the question posed, not all queries yield both direct answers and a list of agreements. Some queries may only provide a list of relevant agreements. |
| Obligation management | Contract managers can take a proactive approach to ensuring that contract commitments are captured, tracked, and fulfilled. |
| Party management | Store, organize, and reconcile party information so you can easily access agreements associated with a specific party. |
| Payment obligation tracking | Stay ahead of due dates, improve compliance, and maximize contract value with AI-assisted payment-related obligations surfaced in your agreements. |
| Related concepts search | Find relevant agreements more precisely using an advanced search technique. This technique uses both synonyms and a knowledge graph to identify related terms and concepts. |
| Renewal management | Simplify renewal tracking with a dedicated dashboard and stay on top of important dates with agreement reminders. |
| Reporting | Discover important insights and key data points about your agreements with IAM dashboards and reports. Locate the Dashboards under the Insights menu. |
| Subscriptions and notifications | Sign up or subscribe to receive daily email reports and notifications to stay ahead of renewals, obligations, and other agreement deadlines. |

title: Benefits and Features of Agreement Manager
heading_path: Benefits and Features of Agreement Manager
Docusign IAM FedRAMP and GovRAMP authorization
text:
For customers in regulated industries, Agreement Manager is FedRAMP and GovRAMP authorized:

- FedRAMP (Federal Risk and Authorization Management Program) authorizes Cloud Service Providers (CSPs) for federal agencies.
- GovRAMP (Government Risk and Authorization Management Program) authorizes CSPs for state, local, and tribal governments.

Both frameworks standardize cloud security assessments and compliance.

Restriction: The Agreement Manager email ingestion feature is not supported for FedRAMP and GovRAMP accounts.

See the [FedRAMP: Docusign for Government](https://support.docusign.com/s/articles/FedRamp-FAQ) article to learn more about the FedRAMP authorization for the IAM platform.

title: Benefits and Features of Agreement Manager
heading_path: Benefits and Features of Agreement Manager
text: Submit a case from the Docusign Support Portal to request help with using Agreement Manager. See the [How do I contact Docusign Customer Support](https://support.docusign.com/s/articles/How-do-I-contact-DocuSign-Customer-Support) article to learn more about contacting support.

title: Add Attribute Values to a Document in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Add Attribute Values to a Document in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to add attribute values to a document in Docusign CLM.

You can add attribute values to documents in Docusign CLM. Attribute values provide content and format validation for
single and multiple entries in a document.

Note: Attribute fields that
are string fields have a limit of 400 characters. An example is the Description field or any
custom attribute that you define as a string field.

1. Log in to Docusign CLM.
2. Select Documents in the navigation header.

   The Documents page opens.
3. Navigate to the folder containing your document.
4. Select the document to open it.

   The document displays in Document Review.
5. Select the Attributes tab on the navigation panel.

   The Attributes panel opens.

   Note: Attributes that are noted with a red asterisk are required.
6. Select the ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Pencil.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTgwMzcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.EIATmDO4CxhktvkpJs8zVPrfnY_EuKxykgiqBxUzGac&_LANG=enus) icon in the
   "Document Details" section or "Groups" section to allow editing.
7. Complete each attribute field as needed according to type:

   - For dropdown menus, select the menu and then choose a value from the options.
   - For freeform entries, enter a value that matches the requested format.
   - For date selectors, manually enter the date in MM/DD/YYYY format, or use the date
     picker.
8. Select Save.

   You have successfully added attribute values to a selected document in Docusign CLM. If any values do not match the requested format, an error
   displays. If any required fields do not have values, a notification displays.

title: Assign Obligations to Team Members
text:
Learn how to assign obligations to other users or team members.

Obligation management provides a centralized platform for you to stay updated on deadlines, assign tasks, and track completion status. Contract managers assign obligations to team members to help improve accountability by ensuring that obligations are tracked, monitored, and completed.

Obligation details are updated from the Agreement preview page. There are a number of ways to access an obligation's details:

- Select an obligation from one of the Obligations dashboard reports.
- Select an obligation from the Obligations tab in the party hub.
- Select an obligation from the Obligations pane on the Agreement preview page.

This topic reviews how to update the obligation details by starting from the Completed Documents view.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed.
4. Apply filters and enter search criteria to locate the relevant agreements.
   1. Enter a keyword or string in the search field and select a suggestion from the dropdown or press the Enter key.

      The agreements containing the keyword or search string that you entered are displayed in the table.
   2. Use the filters to refine the list of search results:

      1. Expand the Filters option and select the filter that you want to apply.
      2. Choose a filter value.
      3. Select the Apply option.

   The agreements that match the entered search criteria and applied filters are displayed in the table.
5. Review the list and select (click) an agreement from the table.

   The Agreement preview page opens. The Details pane is displayed by default.
6. Select the Obligations (flag) icon from the icon tray on the far-left of the page.

   The Obligations pane opens and a list of obligations that were surfaced in the agreement are displayed.
7. Review the list and select (click) the obligation that you want to assign.

   The obligation details are displayed.
8. Place the pane into *edit* mode. Select the Edit (pencil) icon.

   The page refreshes and the Edit Obligation pane is opened for updates.
9. Enter the name or email address of the relevant team member in the Owner field:

   ![A snippet of the Edit Obligations pane that highlights the Owner field.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Obligations_Assign-obligation_Owner-highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcxNjMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.T4o3bgaYpL2EuN7lWzbenRgoUpSzaPgmFiqJYNi3eGg&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Edit Obligations pane that highlights the Owner field.")

   As you enter the name or email address, a type-ahead dropdown list is displayed.
10. Select the appropriate user from the list.
11. Select the Save option from the bottom of the pane.

    A message states that the obligation details have been saved. The obligation details pane reverts back to *read-only*.

    Note: View your assigned obligations from the Obligations dashboard. See the [View Assigned Obligations](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=cpx1745434861979.html) topic to learn more about viewing your assigned obligations.
12. Select < Back from the top of the pane.

    The Obligations pane opens and a list of agreement obligations are displayed.
13. Repeat this process until all the relevant obligations have been assigned.
14. Return to the Agreement Manager homepage. Select the Close (x) icon from the top left.

title: Assign Obligations to Team Members
text:
ent obligations are displayed.
13. Repeat this process until all the relevant obligations have been assigned.
14. Return to the Agreement Manager homepage. Select the Close (x) icon from the top left.

    Tip: If you accessed this page from another location, select the back arrow from the top-left corner to return to the previous page. ![A snippet of the Agreement Preview page that highlights the Back arrow in the top-left corner.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-Preview-page_Example-with-Back-arrow_hilgihghted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcxNjMsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.T4o3bgaYpL2EuN7lWzbenRgoUpSzaPgmFiqJYNi3eGg&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Agreement Preview page that highlights the Back arrow in the top-left corner.")

    For example, select the back arrow to return to the Obligations page in the party hub.

You have successfully assigned an obligation owner from the Agreement preview page.

title: The Agreement List Table
text:
This topic answers the question: What can I do from the agreement list table?

The agreement list table is displayed on the Completed Documents view. The table contains a list of agreements to which the logged-in user has access. This includes their own sent, shared, and received agreements.

![A screenshot of the Completed Documents view that shows a list of documents to which the logged-in user has access.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-view_updated-with-document-count.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY0NTIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.ACZHxzt7_lfXi8KeUbSzXOgjz0spT5QFz54bA6YYN_c&_LANG=enus)

The total document count is displayed in the bottom-right corner of the view. This count is
updated when you perform a search or apply filters.

Tip: The
Envelope ID column displays the envelope ID that is
associated with the corresponding document. Select the Envelope
ID link to view the envelope details page. See the [View Envelope Details from the Agreement List Table](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tsk15aecb4b-98ec-4b40-a739-61a3715cba02.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to view the envelope details for an agreement that was imported from a completed eSignature envelope.") topic to learn more about
viewing envelope details.

title: The Agreement List Table
text:
When you first access the Completed Documents view, a series of onboarding callouts is displayed. These callouts provide a quick tour of Agreement Manager and walk you through a guided flow.

Select the Next option to review each of the callouts or select the
Skip All option to close the dialog boxes:

![A screenshot of the first onboarding callout that points out that you are viewing documents.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Onboarding_first-time-user_callout-1.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY0NTIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.ACZHxzt7_lfXi8KeUbSzXOgjz0spT5QFz54bA6YYN_c&_LANG=enus)

The flow guides you through a quick tour of Agreement Manager functionality. For example:

- Here are all your completed agreements
- Stay organized with AI suggestions
- Track agreements with specific people
- Work smarter with agreement insights
- Upload any of your files to Agreement Manager
- Discover more agreement data with AI suggestions
- Review new data suggestions

Tip: See the [Onboarding Callouts for First-Time Users](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=kpg1722973773604.html&_LANG=enus "This topic answers the question: Is there an onboarding experience for first-time users? Read about the walk-through experience for first-time Agreement Manager users.") topic to learn more about these callouts.

title: The Agreement List Table
text:
The Insights bar is an interactive deck of cards on the Agreement Manager homepage, located above the agreement list table. It highlights key trends and suggests actions so you can manage agreements in one place.

**Rotating messages**

When collapsed, the bar shows a short rotating message about your agreement data: ![An animated gif that shows the rotating messaging that is displayed in the Insights bar when it is collapsed.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Insights-bar_messaging_looped.gif?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY0NTIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.ACZHxzt7_lfXi8KeUbSzXOgjz0spT5QFz54bA6YYN_c&_LANG=enus)

**My Insights (expanded bar)**

Select the Show Insights option to view relevant, actionable insights with links to reports or next steps:

![A snippet of the Completed Documents page that shows the expanded Insights bar.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-documents_Insights-bar_My-Insights_expanded.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY0NTIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.ACZHxzt7_lfXi8KeUbSzXOgjz0spT5QFz54bA6YYN_c&_LANG=enus)

The Insights bar includes these elements:

- An Upcoming renewals chart that shows agreements expiring over the next twelve months.
- One or more insight cards with short messages and action buttons.
- Messages that reflect your data, permissions, and recent Agreement Manager activity.

**Insight cards and calls to action**

Insight cards surface specific recommendations based on your repository and role. The cards you
see can change over time as new features become available.

Common cards are:

| Card topic | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Stay ahead of your renewals | This card is displayed when you have agreements that are expiring or renewing in the next 12 months. You are prompted to review your upcoming renewal data and set up notification in minutes.  Use the Review Data option to review and track your renewals. |
| We found HR agreements | This card is displayed when your repository has HR agreements and non-HR agreements. Create agreement sets to separate your HR-sensitive agreements from the rest and share them only with the necessary people and teams.  Use the Create Sets option to complete this task from the Sets manager. |
| New agreements are HR-related | This card is displayed when you have HR-related agreements. You are encouraged to reprocess existing documents to extract HR-related content. For example, to extract employee metadata fields and HR-specific clauses.  Use the Run Update option to reprocess your HR agreements for new insights and data points. |
| Some parties have multiple names | This card is displayed when duplicate parties are identified in your repository. When the same party has different spellings, each party is listed separately.  Use the Clean Up Parties option to merge duplicates and normalize party names. |

Important: The visibility of these cards is dependent on your Agreement Manager permissions and your Intelligent Agreement Manager (IAM) plan. For example, the HR-related cards are visible for accounts that are enabled with IAM for Human Resources (HR).

title: The Agreement List Table
text:
Use the Filters option to filter the agreement list table by various attributes (fields):

![A snippet of the Completed Documents view that highlights the Filters option, including suggested filters.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-documents-view_Available-filter-pills.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY0NTIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.ACZHxzt7_lfXi8KeUbSzXOgjz0spT5QFz54bA6YYN_c&_LANG=enus)

Select the filter that you want to apply, choose the relevant filter value, and select the Apply option. The filter is applied to the agreement list table.

**Suggested filters**

You can also select a filter from the list of Suggested filters. For example:

- Use the Sets filter to view agreements in a specific agreement set. For
  example, a project or business unit portfolio.

  Note: Only
  sets that were created by or shared with the logged-in user are available
  for selection.
- Use the Status: filter to focus on agreements at a specific lifecycle stage. For example, Active, Inactive,
  Expiring Soon, Renewing Soon,
  and Effective Soon.
- Use the
  Parties: filter to find agreements involving a specific customer, partner, or vendor.
- Agreement Type: use this filter to view only a particular type of agreement. For example, non-disclosure agreements (NDAs), master service agreements (MSAs), or statements of work (SOWs).
- AI Suggestions: use this filter to surface the agreements
  with AI-recommended insights requiring review. You can also find agreements with
  no suggestions.
- Use the Envelope Completed Date filter to find agreements by when the related envelopes were completed. For example, last month's completions.
- Use the Envelope Sent Date filter to focus on agreements by when their envelopes were sent. For example, a specific reporting period.
- Use the Envelope Type filter to narrow the list by the Docusign eSignature envelope type or template.

**The "View All Filters" option**

Select the View All Filters option to access all the filter options. The Filters dialog box lists all the Applied and Available filters:

![A screenshot of the Filters dialog box that shows additional filters that you can apply to the agreement list.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-documents-table_All-Filters-dialog-box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY0NTIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.ACZHxzt7_lfXi8KeUbSzXOgjz0spT5QFz54bA6YYN_c&_LANG=enus)

Scroll through the list or perform a keyword search to locate the filter you want to apply. Expand the filter, choose the relevant filter value, and select the Apply option. Repeat these steps to apply additional filters as needed.

After applying the filters, select the Save option at the bottom of the dialog box. The modal closes and the table is updated to reflect that filters that you applied.

Note: Multiple filters and search criteria can be used together.

- Multiple values within the same filter imply an "or" relationship. For example, select two parties to view agreements for either contracting party.
- Multiple values in separate filters with entered search criteria imply an "and" relationship.

title: The Agreement List Table
text:
The Party Role is surfaced during agreement processing. This extraction identifies the role that a specific party plays within the context of an agreement.

Once surfaced, you can filter the agreement list table to view the agreements where a selected party played a specific role. For example, use the Party Role filter to view your buy-side and sell-side agreements with a specific party.

The Party Role filter is located under the Parties filter:![A screenshot of the Parties filter that highlights the Party Role dropdown and available roles.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Filter-by-party-role_Parties-filter_Party-Role_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY0NTIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.ACZHxzt7_lfXi8KeUbSzXOgjz0spT5QFz54bA6YYN_c&_LANG=enus)

Important: When using the Parties filter, you must also select a Party Role value. If you do not know which role to select, accept the default value, All.

Search for and select at least one party from the list and choose the relevant role. The
Party Role filter requires you to select a party before
it can be applied.

See the [Filter for Agreements by Specific Party Role](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tsk6f2903c9-7ae7-4ad5-8acd-1c4948c98d10.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to update the agreement list table to view agreements based on the role played by a specific party.") topic to learn more about using the Party Role filter.

title: The Agreement List Table
text:
After applying filters to the agreement list table, use the Sort items
(arrows) icons to refine the agreement list table further:

![A snippet of the Completed Documents view that highlights the Sort items (arrows) icons in the column headers of the agreement list table.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-documents-table_sorting-arrows.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY0NTIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.ACZHxzt7_lfXi8KeUbSzXOgjz0spT5QFz54bA6YYN_c&_LANG=enus)

The column headers that display sorting arrows can be used to sort the table. For example, select a column header to sort the table by Total Contract Value, Effective Date, or Expiration Date.

title: The Agreement List Table
text:
A number of columns are displayed in the agreement list table by default, such as:

- File Name
- Status
- Parties
- Agreement Type
- Total Contract Value
- Effective Date
- Expiration Date

Use the Show or Hide Fields (toggle) icon located to the right of the filters to display the relevant data in the table.

The Show or Hide Fields dialog box opens. Select the toggles to show (blue) or hide (white) the corresponding field in the table:

![A screenshot of the Show or Hide Fields dialog box that shows the current selections.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-documents-view_Customize-columns-dialog-box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY0NTIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.ACZHxzt7_lfXi8KeUbSzXOgjz0spT5QFz54bA6YYN_c&_LANG=enus)

**Reorder the fields**

The fields correspond to the columns in the agreement list table. After selecting the relevant fields, reorder the list so that the data is displayed in the desired order.

Hover the cursor over the Reorder (dots) icon for the field that you want to move. Drag the field up or down to re-order the column headers:

![A snippet of the Show or Hide Fields dialog box that shows the "drag" feature to re-order column headers.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-documents_Customize-columns-dialog-box_drag-and-drop.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY0NTIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.ACZHxzt7_lfXi8KeUbSzXOgjz0spT5QFz54bA6YYN_c&_LANG=enus)

title: The Agreement List Table
text:
Agreement statuses provide a quick look at where the agreement is within its life cycle. These statuses are calculated based on an agreement's extracted Effective Date and Expiration Date:

![A snippet of the Completed Documents view that shows the Status filter and filter options.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-statuses_Completed-view_Status-filter_expanded.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY0NTIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.ACZHxzt7_lfXi8KeUbSzXOgjz0spT5QFz54bA6YYN_c&_LANG=enus)

- **Primary status:** indicates whether an agreement is *active* or *inactive*.
- **Secondary status:** defines the agreement's current state, such as *expiring soon*, *renewing soon*, and *effective soon*.

Agreement statuses are re-calculated and updated regularly based on the current date. See the [Agreement Statuses](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=con6a3bd080-da17-4d1c-a92a-b9b9340c44fd.html&_LANG=enus "Read about agreement statuses, including how they are calculated, visibility within Agreement Manager, and which types of statuses are being tracked.") topic to learn more about this feature.

Important: If you previously customized the columns in the agreement list table, you must manually display the Status column in the table. See the [Display the Status Column in the Agreement List Table](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tsk57b92226-d6e4-4725-99c4-79ebf42e9f8b.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to customize the agreement list table to display agreement status information.") topic to learn about this task.

title: The Agreement List Table
text:
After applying filters and customizing the table, use the checkboxes to select agreements to download or export:

![A snippet of the search results table that shows selected agreements and options to download documents or export agreement data to a CSV file.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-documents-table_Download-or-export-selected-agreements.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY0NTIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.ACZHxzt7_lfXi8KeUbSzXOgjz0spT5QFz54bA6YYN_c&_LANG=enus)

Select the checkboxes for the agreements or agreement data that you want to download or export:

- Select individual agreements.
- Select the Choose all checkbox from the header row to select all the agreements on that page of results.

  Tip: The Choose all option only selects agreements on the current page of search results. It does not select any agreements on subsequent pages of results.
- Expand the Selected dropdown and select the Select all that match your filters option. All the agreements in the table are selected.

  Important: Apply at least one filter to use the Select all that match your filters link. If you do not apply a filter, the link is grayed out and not available. Additionally, if you perform a search query, this option is not available.

The actions bar opens when at least one document is selected. The Download Documents and Export to CSV options are available.

Choose to download the selected agreements or export the agreement data for the selected agreements. The files are downloaded to your local Downloads folder.

title: The Agreement List Table
text:
Document-level email notifications help you stay informed about upcoming milestones for individual documents. Access this function from the agreement list table. Hover your cursor over an agreement to display the Set a notification (bell) icon:![A screenshot of the Completed view that highlights the Set a notification option in the agreement list table.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-list-table_Set-a-notification_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY0NTIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.ACZHxzt7_lfXi8KeUbSzXOgjz0spT5QFz54bA6YYN_c&_LANG=enus)

Tip: The document-level Notifications function (bell icon) can also be accessed from the Agreement preview page.

Select the Set a notification (bell) icon to enable notifications for the corresponding agreement. When you select the bell icon, the ​Renewal Notice Date​ and ​Expiration Date​ notifications are enabled by default. Other notification types can be configured by using the ​+ Add option.

Note: When you enable the notification, you are designated as the recipient and all frequency options are selected. Use the Edit link to update the frequency and recipients for the notification as needed.

![A screenshot of the Notifications window that shows options for tracking agreement milestones.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Document-level-notifications_Notifications-window.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY0NTIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.ACZHxzt7_lfXi8KeUbSzXOgjz0spT5QFz54bA6YYN_c&_LANG=enus)

- Renewal notice dates
- Expiration dates

Note: If a notification already exists for an agreement, the Manage notifications (bell) icon is displayed. Select this icon to update the existing notifications for the selected agreement.

See the [Set Document-Level Notifications](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tsk62370c10-0d3b-4218-a325-4383b9d5f7cd.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to set and manage notifications to track upcoming milestones for an individual document.") topic to learn more about setting and managing document-level notifications.

title: The Agreement List Table
text:
The document-level More actions (three dots) menu gives you access to:

![A snippet of the agreement list table that highlights the document-level More actions (three dots) menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-list-table_Actions-menu_options-displayed.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY0NTIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.ACZHxzt7_lfXi8KeUbSzXOgjz0spT5QFz54bA6YYN_c&_LANG=enus)

- Download the agreement.
- Share the agreement with other users or groups.
- Remove the agreement from the Agreement Manager and the account.
- View the activity log for the agreement.

The availability of these options is dependent on your document-level access. For example:

- Users with Can View access can read and download the agreement.
- Users with Can Edit access can edit attribute values and download the
  agreement.
- Users with Can Manage access can edit attribute values, remove access to
  the agreement, and delete and share the agreement.

Note: The Agreement Manager permissions also control which options are available in the document-level More actions menu. For example:

- Users who are assigned the View all agreements permission can view and download all the agreements in their account.
- Users who are assigned the Organize and manage all agreements permission can view, edit, download, and delete all agreements in their account.

See the [Agreement Manager Permissions, Profiles, and Roles](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=eut1712157553977.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the available Agreement Manager permissions, including how users and groups are granted access to Agreement Manager agreements and example roles for each permission.") topic to learn more about these Agreement Manager-level permissions.

See these support topics to learn more about these actions:

- [Download Documents from the Agreement List Table](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=ach1724363496037.html&_LANG=enus "This topic answers the question: How do I download agreements from Agreement Manager?")
- [Share Agreements with Others](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=vyz1722979030156.html&_LANG=enus "This topic answers the question: How do I share agreements with others? Learn how to share an agreement with other users or groups, including assigning access levels for each recipient.")
- [Manage Agreement-Level Access](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=zal1712248843872.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to manage access to an agreement, including revoking access and updating access permissions.")
- [Remove Documents from the Agreement List Table](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=tskbbe43778-7b32-4bf9-ba95-187ed5140ffd.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to remove documents from the agreement list table on the Completed Documents page. This topic answers the question: How do I delete agreements from Agreement Manager?")
- [View the Activity Log for an Agreement](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=ciq1738201081422.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to access the Activity Log to view agreement-level events for a selected agreement.")

title: The Agreement List Table
text:
As new or updated AI models are released, the previously ingested agreements can be processed again to surface these data points. Depending on the type of new insights that are available, users can choose to:

- Opt in for reprocessing from the Track obligations badge (obligation-related attributes and insights)
- Reprocess all the outdated agreements for the account (new or updated AI model)
- Reprocess selected agreements (custom agreement types and fields)

Note: See the [Agreement Manager Reprocessing Experience](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=con44590246-0f0e-43b4-9692-caadb57335ac.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about the Agreement Manager reprocessing experience, including its purpose, trigger points, and job size considerations.") article to learn more about these reprocessing features.

Processing happens in the background. While it does not impact your current experience, processing may take some time depending on the number and size of agreements. Once started, processing cannot be canceled.

title: The Agreement List Table
text:
When new obligation-related attributes are available, the Track obligations badge is displayed on the Completed Documents page. For example, when an account is enabled for obligations. This badge indicates that you are not using the latest AI model.

Select the Track obligations badge to get started:

![A screenshot of the expanded Track obligations badge that shows information related to processing agreements for added insights.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reprocessing_New-Insights-pill_expanded.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY0NTIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.ACZHxzt7_lfXi8KeUbSzXOgjz0spT5QFz54bA6YYN_c&_LANG=enus)

Select Start Now to opt in for additional processing. Another dialog box opens:

![A screenshot of the dialog box that contains the opt-in toggle for processing your agreements for new insights.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reprocessing_Turn-on-processing-toggle.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY0NTIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.ACZHxzt7_lfXi8KeUbSzXOgjz0spT5QFz54bA6YYN_c&_LANG=enus)

Turn on the Process for additional insights toggle (blue position) and select the Save Changes option. The dialog box closes and a message states that your agreements are being processed for additional AI insights.

Note: Once the toggle is activated, it cannot be turned off. Also, you can no longer deactivate processing. Agreements will be processed based on the user's account access. For example, if the user has obligation management as part of their plan, opting in for additional processing includes obligation-related insights.

Review the [Extracted Agreement Insights and Definitions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=uwk1702945852405.html&_LANG=enus "Look up the names and definitions for the attributes and values that are surfaced in Agreement Manager agreements with AI. This topic answers the question: What data is being extracted from my Agreement Manager agreements?") article to learn more about the insights being surfaced in your agreements with AI.

title: Update Custom Agreement Type Details
text:
Learn how to update the details for your custom agreement types.

Administrators and contract managers can create and maintain the custom agreement types for their account. For example, if your organization changes how a category of agreements is defined, you can edit the details accordingly.

Note: Administrators automatically have access to configure agreement data. For non-administrator users, this feature requires Organize and manage all agreements access to Agreement Manager. These tasks are primarily completed by administrators and contract managers.

Custom agreement types are managed from the *Agreement Types Manager*. Access this feature from the Settings (gear) menu on the Agreement Manager homepage.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed. A list of documents to which the logged-in user has access is displayed in the table.
4. Expand the Settings (gear) dropdown from the top right and select the Agreement Types option:

   ![A screenshot of the Agreement Manager homepage that highlights the Agreement Types option in the Settings (gear) menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-types-manager_Settings-menu_Agreement-Types_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc3MzAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.QFJzUktwa1ImFzkFMC122GRu20hJX56ZPm5zMLcfheI&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Agreement Manager homepage that highlights the Agreement Types option in the Settings (gear) menu.")

   The Manage Agreement Types page opens. The account's standard and custom agreement types are displayed in the Agreement Types table.
5. Locate the custom agreement type that you want to update.

   Important: Only the user-created, custom agreement types can be updated.

   ![A snippet of the Manage Agreement Types page that shows the Created By filter.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Manage-Agreement-Types_Created-By-filter_User-selected.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc3MzAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.QFJzUktwa1ImFzkFMC122GRu20hJX56ZPm5zMLcfheI&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Manage Agreement Types page that shows the Created By filter.")

   1. Expand (click) the Created By filter and select the User option.
   2. Select the Apply option.

      The Agreement Types table updates and all the account's custom agreement types are displayed.
   3. Refine the results list as needed. For example, apply the Category and AI Status filters or perform a keyword search.
6. Expand the More actions (three dots) menu for the relevant custom agreement type and select the Edit Details option:

   ![A snippet of the Agreement Types view that highlights the Edit Details option in the More actions (three dots) menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-type-management_Edit-details.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc3MzAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.QFJzUktwa1ImFzkFMC122GRu20hJX56ZPm5zMLcfheI&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Agreement Types view that highlights the Edit Details option in the More actions (three dots) menu.")

   The Edit agreement type details dialog box opens.
7. Update the details for the custom agreement type as needed.

title: Update Custom Agreement Type Details
text:
ew that highlights the Edit Details option in the More actions (three dots) menu.")

   The Edit agreement type details dialog box opens.
7. Update the details for the custom agreement type as needed.

   ![A screenshot of the Edit agreement type details dialog box that shows editable fields.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-type-management_Edit-agreement-type-details-dialog-box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc3MzAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.QFJzUktwa1ImFzkFMC122GRu20hJX56ZPm5zMLcfheI&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Edit agreement type details dialog box that shows editable fields.")

   1. Enter a new name in the Name field or edit the existing name.
   2. Update the Description for the type. For example, if the agreement type is being used for a different purpose, update the description to reflect the changes.
   3. Expand the Category dropdown and update the
      classification for the agreement type. For example, if the agreement
      type is classified incorrectly, select the relevant category from the
      dropdown.
   4. Select the Save option.

   The dialog box closes and the Agreement type details view is displayed for the selected agreement type. The associated standard and custom fields are displayed in the Fields table.

   ![A screenshot of the Agreement type details view that shows the standard and custom fields that are associated with the agreement type.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Manage-Agreement-types_New-agreement-type_standard-contract-fields.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTc3MzAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.QFJzUktwa1ImFzkFMC122GRu20hJX56ZPm5zMLcfheI&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Agreement type details view that shows the standard and custom fields that are associated with the agreement type.")

   From this view, you can:

   - Update custom field details
   - Add custom and standard fields to the type
   - Remove fields from the agreement type
8. Return to the Agreement Types view. Select the Agreement Types option from the breadcrumb navigation in the top left.

   You are returned to the Agreement Types view.
9. Return to the Agreement Manager homepage. Select the Close (x) icon from the top right.

   You are returned to the Agreement Manager homepage. The Completed Documents view is displayed.

You have successfully edited the details for a custom agreement type.

title: Validate Extracted Field Values (Complete a Data Review)
heading_path: Validate Extracted Field Values (Complete a Data Review)
text:
This topic answers the question: How can I make sure that the suggested attribute (field) values are accurate? Learn how to complete a data review to verify suggested field values.

Data reviews are necessary to validate the data that is surfaced during processing and to provide values for empty attributes (fields). This data review is an important part of using Agreement Manager. It ensures accurate agreement data for reporting, business planning, sales forecasting, and negotiations.

Tip: Use the *Bulk Metadata Editing* feature to overwrite the extracted attribute values with user-provided agreement data. With this feature, you can edit the metadata for up to 1,000 selected documents in a single processing job. See the [Bulk Metadata Editing](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=concb493b18-adf8-4fe5-8b99-dd90cbc28399.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the Bulk Metadata Editing feature, including use cases, typical roles and permissions, and a process overview.") topic to learn about more about this feature.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed.
4. Use the Filters option to locate the agreements that you want to review:
   1. Select the Filters option.

      The Filters menu expands.
   2. Select the AI Suggestions filter.

      The AI Suggestions filter opens.

      ![A screenshot of the expanded AI Suggestions dialog box that shows the two filter options, Needs Review and No Suggestions.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-documents-table_AI-Suggestions-dialog-box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY2MjAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.ji2WZDNjTRDHA91rzWygwnn04pAXgBV3hgCUmEYUKFU&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the expanded AI Suggestions dialog box that shows the two filter options, Needs Review and No Suggestions.")
   3. Choose the Needs Review option and select Apply.

   The filter closes and the agreements that match the applied filter are displayed in the agreement list table.

   Tip: ![An image of the spark icon used to denote the use of AI.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/AI-Spark-icon.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY2MjAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.ji2WZDNjTRDHA91rzWygwnn04pAXgBV3hgCUmEYUKFU&_LANG=enus "An image of the spark icon used to denote the use of AI.") The spark icon indicates that the agreement contains suggested values. Review, edit, and approve these values to ensure accurate agreement data for reporting, forecasting, and other business purposes.
5. Select (click) the agreement that you want to review.

   The Agreement preview page opens for the selected agreement. The Details pane is displayed by default and shows a list of attributes and suggested values. These attributes and values are displayed based on the identified agreement type.

   A summary displays at the top of the Details pane. The summary includes the identified agreement type and the number of fields and suggestions to review:

title: Validate Extracted Field Values (Complete a Data Review)
heading_path: Validate Extracted Field Values (Complete a Data Review)
text:
ed based on the identified agreement type.

   A summary displays at the top of the Details pane. The summary includes the identified agreement type and the number of fields and suggestions to review:

   ![A snippet of the Details pane that shows the identified agreement type and the number of suggestions that require review.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-Preview-page_Details-pane_summary-statement-snippet.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY2MjAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.ji2WZDNjTRDHA91rzWygwnn04pAXgBV3hgCUmEYUKFU&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Details pane that shows the identified agreement type and the number of suggestions that require review.")

   Tip: Use the Find details search field to locate specific attributes or values.

   Validate the suggested values for each attribute.
6. Place the Details pane into *edit* mode.
   - Select the Edit (pencil) icon.
   - Select the Review All option.

   Note: You must have Organize and manage all agreements access to perform data reviews. For all other users, the Edit (pencil) icon and the Review All option are grayed out.

   The Review & edit details pane opens for updates.

   ![A screenshot of the Agreement preview page that shows the Review & edit details pane with editable attribute value fields.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Review-and-edit-details-pane_Agreement-details-open-for-edits.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY2MjAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.ji2WZDNjTRDHA91rzWygwnn04pAXgBV3hgCUmEYUKFU&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Agreement preview page that shows the Review & edit details pane with editable attribute value fields.")

   Important: When you accept or edit the suggested attribute values, the content in the agreement does not change.
7. Accept or edit the suggested values until each attribute is validated. If the attribute value does not exist within the agreement, leave the field empty.

   ![A snippet of the Review & edit details pane that shows an extracted value highlighted in the agreement.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Review-and-edit-details-pane_verify-extracted-value_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY2MjAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.ji2WZDNjTRDHA91rzWygwnn04pAXgBV3hgCUmEYUKFU&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Review & edit details pane that shows an extracted value highlighted in the agreement.")

   1. Compare the suggested attribute values against the data in the agreement.

      - Select (click) an attribute to view the suggested value within the agreement.

        The value is highlighted within the agreement.
      - Select the Accept AI Suggestion (checkmark) icon if the value is *correct*.
      - Delete the existing value and enter the correct value if the suggestion is *incorrect*.
   2. Continue reviewing and updating attribute values until all the fields have been verified against the agreement.

      Attributes are grouped based on usage and purpose. A progress bar is displayed at the top of each group and shows the number of suggestions that require review.
8. Select the Save option.

   The agreement details are saved. The Details pane is reverted to read-only mode.
9. Select the Close (x) icon from the top left.

   You are returned to the Completed Documents view. Your search criteria and filters are still applied and agreements requiring review are displayed in the table.

You have successfully completed a data review for an agreement with unverified AI suggestions.

title: Custom Extractions
text:
Read about custom extractions, including how these data points bring value to our organization.

Custom extractions unlock organization and industry-specific agreement data and insights beyond the standard Docusign-provided fields. This includes custom agreement types and custom fields. Once the AI detection is enabled for your custom agreement types and fields, these values are surfaced in your agreements.

Note: See the [Custom Agreement Type Extractions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=con6f74f3c5-e52b-4611-9d7b-8455c97725b4.html&_LANG=enus "Read about custom agreement type extractions, including their use, purpose, and entry points in Agreement Manager.") and [Custom Field Extractions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=condece8d7e-2da8-4e5f-8ba4-3f0175c2a625.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the custom field extractions, including entry points for creating new fields and a review of the AI detection setup wizard.") topics to learn more about these custom extractions.

Custom extractions bring value to your organization:

- Value of increased productivity (hours saved)
- Value of unlocking data and insights
  - Boost productivity by tracking the data points in terms that are unique to your organization or industry
  - Simplify compliance monitoring with more consistent and precise agreement records
  - Enhance strategic decision-making with greater visibility into your agreement data

Important:

Customers do not need to consent to AI training to use this feature. When customers create custom
agreement types and fields, their data is used as **input** to the AI model to help generate
results for their use. This process does not involve AI training.

If a customer has given consent to sharing data for AI
training, anonymized data may be used for training Docusign AI models. This may
include anonymized data from custom extractions.

See the [Docusign FAQs for AI](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fzd1707173174972&topicId=uss1707173279973.html) topic for more information about how
Docusign uses data for AI training.

title: Custom Extractions
text:
Standard, Docusign-provided agreement types, and fields are surfaced in
your agreements automatically during processing. Custom extractions let you extend
your agreement data and insights to include your own organization-specific data
points.

Through the use of custom extractions, your agreement data is transformed into actionable strategic insights:

![An infographic of the custom extraction process for mastering agreement intelligence with Agreement Manager.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Custom-extractions_process-orverview_inforgraphic.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTgwOTksInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.c5ZKUh5ikeyOTNFDADltqZAuLBZN31UF3sndHL1KlIw&_LANG=enus)

1. **Define unique agreement types:** Categorize your document portfolio with custom types that reflect your specific business needs.
2. **Add targeted custom fields:** Capture unique data points by creating and adding custom fields to your agreement types. For example, create custom text, number, date, or dropdown fields to surface these attributes in your agreements.
3. **Enable AI detection:** Activate automated extractions for your custom types and fields. The
   AI model identifies data without manual entry. The definitions and
   examples provided during the setup are used to teach the AI detection to
   recognize your data points.
4. **Process documents to unlock data:** Reprocess historical documents or upload new ones to surface your custom extractions. Once the AI detection is activated, your custom extractions are surfaced in new documents automatically. However, reprocessing for historical documents is manually triggered when you activate the extraction.
5. **Drive strategy with strategic insights:** Use the expanded data points to drive strategic insights, including tailored reporting. For example, use the extracted data points to:

   - Identify cost saving opportunities
   - Simplify compliance monitoring
   - Make informed contract renewal decisions

title: Custom Extractions
text:
Organizations can use custom agreement types and fields to meet their specific and unique use cases. For example:

- A financial services organization can monitor Loyalty Program Agreements more closely. The data surfaced in these agreements can be used to personalize customer outreach and boost retention.
- A medical supplies organization can track agreements with specific volume discounts and industry-specific compliance requirements.
- A human resource (HR) team can manage specific types of HR-related agreements. For example, independent contractor or gig worker agreements. The agreement data can be used to enhance workforce planning and ensure compliance with local labor laws.

title: Custom Extractions
text:
Before your custom agreement types and fields can be surfaced in your new and historical agreements, they must be activated for AI detection. Enable the AI detection for your custom extractions from the *Agreement Types Manager* or the *Fields Manager*. Both features are accessed from the Agreement Manager homepage under the Settings (gear) menu.

Use the Set Up option to configure the AI detection for your custom extractions:

![A snippet of the Fields Manager that highlights the Set Up option for a custom field.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Custom-extractions_AI-detection_Set-Up_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTgwOTksInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.c5ZKUh5ikeyOTNFDADltqZAuLBZN31UF3sndHL1KlIw&_LANG=enus)

The AI detection setup wizard launches. Follow the prompts to find and categorize your custom extraction in three easy steps: 1. Define your custom extraction, 2. Provide examples, and 3. Test for accuracy (or active immediately).

![A screenshot of the setup wizard for enabling the AI detection for your custom extractions.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Custom-extractions_AI-detection_Setup-wizard.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTgwOTksInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.c5ZKUh5ikeyOTNFDADltqZAuLBZN31UF3sndHL1KlIw&_LANG=enus)

title: Custom Extractions
heading_path: Why do we ask for examples?
text:
When you set up custom extractions, you are prompted to provide examples, in addition to a text
definition. The definition tells the AI model what the data means and the examples
show how the data displays in your agreements.

![A screenshot of the AI detection setup wizard that prompts you to choose a method for providing examples.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Custom-extractions_AI-detection_Setup-Provide-examples.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTgwOTksInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.c5ZKUh5ikeyOTNFDADltqZAuLBZN31UF3sndHL1KlIw&_LANG=enus)

Examples are important to the AI detection setup process:

- **Teach the AI model real patterns, not just the labels and field names.** Your examples should include the actual value plus the surrounding text for context. When you include the context, the AI model learns to detect a pattern, not just the field or attribute name.
