- **Account for variation in language and layout.** The same custom extraction can appear in different sentences, clauses, tables, or forms. When you provide multiple examples, the AI model learns to generalize across different formats, document types, and writing styles.
- **Distinguish between similar concepts.** Examples make it easier for the AI model to detect the differences between closely related fields or attributes. For example, different dates or fee types may look similar but have different meanings.
- **Improve your accuracy score.** It is equally important to include example documents that do not contain the custom data. Including incorrect examples teaches the AI model to recognize when the data is not present.

title: Custom Extractions
heading_path: Why do we test for accuracy?
text:
Before you activate the AI detection for custom extractions, the setup wizard prompts you to test for accuracy. This step is optional, however, it is highly recommended. Testing for accuracy helps you confirm that the AI model is finding the right data in your agreements.

![A screenshot of the setup wizard that prompts you to test the accuracy before activating the AI detection.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Custom-extractions_AI-detection_Setup_Test-for-accuracy.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTgwOTksInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.c5ZKUh5ikeyOTNFDADltqZAuLBZN31UF3sndHL1KlIw&_LANG=enus)

Testing is important to the AI detection setup process:

- **Validates how the AI detection behaves on real agreements.** You can see where the custom data was correctly identified and where it was not. This gives you the chance to refine the AI detection and improve its accuracy before activating it.
- **Catches setup issues early.** Many accuracy issues are caused by vague or overloaded definitions or from poor examples. For example, the examples don't fully represent the data. Testing for accuracy provides feedback so you can refine your definitions and examples before activation.
- **Builds trust in your results.** Accuracy and guidance are key to achieving success with your custom extractions. Accurate agreement insights and reporting depend on being able to trust your data. The testing experience is designed to give you more confidence in your agreement insights and data.
- **Protect reporting and compliance workflows.** Validating the extraction quality reduces the risk of incorrect values flowing into your filters, dashboards, or compliance monitoring.

Once you are satisfied with the accuracy score and sample results, you can activate the AI detection for your custom extraction. Your new and selected historical agreements are processed for the custom data point.

title: Custom Extractions
heading_path: Custom extraction and AI detection limits
text:
Custom extractions and AI detection are evolving based on feedback and usage patterns. Be sure to review these limitations before using custom extractions:

| **Limitation** | **Description** |
| --- | --- |
| **Limited AI detection** | AI detection is available for custom agreement types and fields. AI detection is not available for obligations or clause extractions. |
| **Custom field dependency** | Custom fields do not exist outside of an agreement types. Link these fields to a standard or custom agreement type. |
| **Limited custom field options** | Custom fields are limited to text, number, date, and dropdown options. |
| **Reporting limitations** | Custom extractions cannot be used to build custom reports. However, data from custom extractions shows in existing reports where relevant. |
| **Custom types (no AI detection)** | The limit for custom agreement types *without AI detection* is 200 types per account. When AI detection is not enabled, users can manually tag agreements with a custom agreement type. |
| **Custom types (with AI detection)** | The limit for custom agreement types *with AI detection* is 15 per account. |
| **Custom fields (no AI detection)** | The limit for custom fields *without AI detection* is 100 fields per account. When AI detection is not enabled, users can manually enter values for custom fields. |
| **Extraction limits** | Customers with IAM Professional, IAM Enterprise, and IAM for Sales plans can create up to 50 custom extractions per account:  - The limit represents the combined number of custom agreement types and fields with AI detection. - The limit can represent any combination of custom agreement types and fields that equal 50.  Consider these scenarios:  - Customers can create a maximum of 50 custom field extractions across standard agreement types. - Customers can create a maximum of 49 custom field extractions across one custom agreement type (with AI detection). The custom fields are also surfaced in other standard agreement types. - Customers can create a maximum of 35 custom field extractions across 15 custom agreement types. The custom fields are also surfaced in other standard agreement types. - Customers **cannot** create 34 custom field extractions across 16 custom agreement types   (with AI detection). **Reminder:** The limit for   custom agreement types with AI detection is 15 per   account.   Note: Without AI detection, customers can create up to 100 custom fields in total. Field values for custom fields without AI detection are populated manually during data review. |
| **Filters for bulk processing** | When locating agreements to refresh for custom data, do not use search queries to locate agreements. On-demand or bulk processing supports the use of filters to identify agreements to process for custom data. |
| **Numbers support** | There is no explicit support for number fields, such as currency, percentage, and unit of time. |
| **AI detection samples** | You can provide up to 10 example documents when setting up the AI detection. |
| **Document processing limit** | There is a 1,500 document limit when processing documents. This limit applies to both newly ingested documents and documents that require reprocessing for custom data. Tip: The 1,500 document processing limit is the "allowance per seat, per year" and is pooled at the account-level. For example, if an account has 100 users, the document processing limit for the account is 150,000 per year. |
| **No service level agreement (SLA) for processing jobs** | There is no SLA for processing times. You can view the status and details for processing jobs from the job monitoring hub. |
| **View processing usage** | Currently, there is no way to monitor the volume of processing at the aggregate level. |
| **Unable to cancel jobs** | Processing jobs cannot be canceled once started. |

title: Custom Extractions
heading_path: Custom extraction and AI detection limits
text:
| **View processing usage** | Currently, there is no way to monitor the volume of processing at the aggregate level. |
| **Unable to cancel jobs** | Processing jobs cannot be canceled once started. |

Note: See the [Understanding Your IAM and eSignature Plan Allowances](https://www.docusign.com/iam/plan-allowances) page to learn more about processing limits by plan type.

title: Custom Extractions
text:
Once custom agreement types and fields are enabled for AI detection, you can choose specific agreements to reprocess for new data. For example, if you have a number of *Miscellaneous* agreements that fall under a newly created custom type.

Filter the agreement list table to locate the agreements that you want to process for new data points. When you select one or more agreements, additional actions are displayed, including the Update option:

![A screenshot of the Completed Documents view that highlights the selected agreements that you want to refresh for custom data.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Custom-extractions_Refresh-Custom-Data-highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTgwOTksInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.c5ZKUh5ikeyOTNFDADltqZAuLBZN31UF3sndHL1KlIw&_LANG=enus)

Expand the Update dropdown and choose to reprocess for Standard Data or Custom Data.

title: View and Update Your Custom Reports
heading_path: View and Update Your Custom Reports
text:
This topic answers the question: How can I review and update my custom reports?

After you customize and save a report, it is available from the Custom tab on the Reports view.

![A snippet of the Reports view that shows the custom reports found on the Custom tab.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Reports-view_Custom-tab-snippet.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwMDAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.90XSO6yZMBgZQpW1P1Kx_u55qXvRFP2J2hiPgNBqmTE&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Reports view that shows the custom reports found on the Custom tab.")

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Insights from the global header.

   The Insights hub opens and the Overview is displayed.
3. Select Reports from the navigation pane on the left.

   The Reports view opens. The Recents tab is displayed by default.
4. Select the Custom tab.

   The Custom tab opens.

   ![A snippet of the Custom view that highlights the View option for the custom reports.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_View-saved-reports.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwMDAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.90XSO6yZMBgZQpW1P1Kx_u55qXvRFP2J2hiPgNBqmTE&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Custom view that highlights the View option for the custom reports.")
5. Select the View option for the relevant report.

   The detailed report view is displayed.

   Note: See the [View and Download an Agreement Report](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=izo1702945825083.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to view an agreement chart, drill down to the detailed report view, apply filters, and download agreement data. This topic answers the question: What are my reporting options for Agreement Manager agreements, including applying filters and downloading results?") topic to learn about the detailed report view.

   ![A screenshot of the detailed report view for the selected custom report.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Reports-view_View-custom-report.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwMDAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.90XSO6yZMBgZQpW1P1Kx_u55qXvRFP2J2hiPgNBqmTE&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the detailed report view for the selected custom report.")

   Tip: Add the report to Your Favorites list. Select the Favorite (star) icon located in the top-right corner:![A snippet of a detailed report view that highlights the Favorite (star) icon.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreements-dashboard_Report-details_Favorite-icon-selected_generic.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwMDAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.90XSO6yZMBgZQpW1P1Kx_u55qXvRFP2J2hiPgNBqmTE&_LANG=enus "A snippet of a detailed report view that highlights the Favorite (star) icon.")Your starred dashboards and reports are displayed in the Your Favorites section on the Overview dashboard.

   Custom reports can be updated or deleted at any time from the report details page.

**Edit the report name**

6. Update the name of the report, as needed:

title: View and Update Your Custom Reports
heading_path: View and Update Your Custom Reports
text:
orites section on the Overview dashboard.

   Custom reports can be updated or deleted at any time from the report details page.

**Edit the report name**

6. Update the name of the report, as needed:

   ![A snippet of a detailed report view for a custom report that highlights the Edit report (pencil) icon.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-dashboard_Edit-custom-report_Edit-icon_highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwMDAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.90XSO6yZMBgZQpW1P1Kx_u55qXvRFP2J2hiPgNBqmTE&_LANG=enus "A snippet of a detailed report view for a custom report that highlights the Edit report (pencil) icon.")

   1. Select the Edit (pencil) icon located next to the report name.

      The Save Report dialog box opens.
   2. Update the Report name.
   3. Select the Confirm option.

   The dialog box closes and the report name is updated.

**Update the report filters and table data**

Create custom reports by applying filters to existing dashboard charts and choosing the Save As option.

7. Update report filters and customize table data:
   1. Remove a filter. Select the Clear (x) icon associated with the filter that you want to remove.
   2. Apply new filters or update the existing filters for the report. Expand the relevant filter, choose a value or enter a date, and select the Apply option.
   3. Update the columns in the table:

      - Select the  (toggle) icon. The Customize columns dialog box opens.
      - Select the toggles to turn the associated field on (blue) or off (white).
      - Re-order the columns as needed. Hover the cursor over the Reorder (dots) icon for the column that you want to move. Drag it to the appropriate position.
      - Select the Save option.The dialog box closes and the table is updated based on your column customization.

      Tip: If the Status column is not displayed by default, use the Show or Hide Fields (toggle) option to add it to the table. From the Show or Hide Field dialog box, turn on the Status toggle and drag the field to the appropriate location. Be sure to Save your changes.
   4. Save the changes to your custom report:

      - Expand the More actions (three dots) menu in the top right and select the Save  option.

        The Save Report dialog box opens.
      - Select the Confirm option to save the changes.The dialog box closes and changes to the custom report are saved.

**Update the report configuration**

Custom reports can be created from scratch by using the *Create Report* function, located under the Create dropdown:

![A snippet of the Insights hub that shows the Create Report option in the Create dropdown.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reporting-hub_Create-menu_Create-Report_highlighted_generic.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwMDAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.90XSO6yZMBgZQpW1P1Kx_u55qXvRFP2J2hiPgNBqmTE&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Insights hub that shows the Create Report option in the Create dropdown.")

Tip: See the [Custom Dashboards and Reports](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=conac48cc6c-d92c-4187-a733-08968c218a74.html&_LANG=enus "Read about custom dashboards and reports, including use cases and limitations.") topic to learn more about configuring custom dashboards and reports.

8. Update the report configuration, including the data source, metrics, and visualization:
   1. Select the Edit report (pencil) icon from the top right:

title: View and Update Your Custom Reports
heading_path: View and Update Your Custom Reports
text:
ut configuring custom dashboards and reports.

8. Update the report configuration, including the data source, metrics, and visualization:
   1. Select the Edit report (pencil) icon from the top right:

      ![A snippet of the detailed report view that highlights the Edit report (pencil) icon.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Custom-report_Edit-report_highlighted_generic.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcwMDAsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.90XSO6yZMBgZQpW1P1Kx_u55qXvRFP2J2hiPgNBqmTE&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the detailed report view that highlights the Edit report (pencil) icon.")The report configuration page opens.
   2. Update the Data source for the report, including applying filters as needed.
   3. Update the report's metrics, including the measure, aggregation, and
      currency.
   4. Change the Group by field as needed. Select the field by which you want to group or break down the metrics.
   5. Update the Visualization field to change how the report is displayed.

      As you make changes to the report, the pane on the right updates to match the new configuration.
   6. Select the Save option from the top right.

      The Save report dialog box opens.
   7. Update the Report name and Description fields as needed and select the Save option.

   You are returned to the detailed report view for the custom report. The report reflects the changes that you made to the configuration.

title: Manage Obligations at the Agreement Level
heading_path: Manage Obligations at the Agreement Level
text:
Learn how to view and manage the obligations associated with an agreement.

Obligations, such as payments, renewal notices, and termination notices are surfaced in your
agreements. These AI-assisted insights are displayed in the
Obligations pane on the Agreement
preview page. Users can also manually capture and define obligations
from the Agreement preview page.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign
   Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the
   AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed. Search for the agreements
   containing the obligations that you want to manage.
4. Perform a search and apply filters to locate the relevant agreements:
   1. Enter a keyword or search string in the search field and select
      Enter.

      Agreements containing the keyword or search string that you entered
      are displayed in the table.
   2. Use the available filters to refine the list of search results. Expand the Filters option, choose the relevant filter, specify a filter value, and select
      Apply.

      The table is updated to reflect the filters that you applied.
5. Select an agreement from the list.

   The Agreement preview page opens for the selected agreement. The
   Details pane is displayed by default and shows a list
   of attributes and suggested values. Attributes, or fields, are displayed based
   on the identified agreement type.
6. Select the Obligations (flag) icon from the left-side of
   the Details pane.

   The Obligations pane opens. A list of obligations that were surfaced
   with AI or manually created is displayed in the pane.

   ![A screenshot of the Agreement preview page that highlights the Obligations pane, including surfaced obligations.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Data-review_agreement-preview_obligations-pane.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcxNDYsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.I6MrrCDmGResnmMUClYDQawZYUSryystssFboHxfNt8&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Agreement preview page that highlights the Obligations pane, including surfaced obligations.")

   The name, owner, due date, and status are displayed for each
   obligation.
7. Select (click) an obligation from the list.

   The obligation details view opens in the pane. Additional obligation fields
   are displayed. These fields were surfaced in your agreements during
   processing.

   ![A screenshot of the Agreement preview page that shows details for a selected obligation.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Navigator_Agreement-Preview_Obligation-details.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcxNDYsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.I6MrrCDmGResnmMUClYDQawZYUSryystssFboHxfNt8&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Agreement preview page that shows details for a selected obligation.")

**Complete a data review:**

8. Review and validate the obligation details:
   1. Place the pane into *edit* mode. Select either the
      Review All option or the
      Edit (pencil) icon.

      The Edit Obligation view displays and the
      attribute fields are opened for edits.
   2. Review the attribute (field) values that were surfaced in the
      agreement.

title: Manage Obligations at the Agreement Level
heading_path: Manage Obligations at the Agreement Level
text:
Edit (pencil) icon.

      The Edit Obligation view displays and the
      attribute fields are opened for edits.
   2. Review the attribute (field) values that were surfaced in the
      agreement.

      ![An image of the spark icon used to denote the use of AI.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/AI-Spark-icon.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcxNDYsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.I6MrrCDmGResnmMUClYDQawZYUSryystssFboHxfNt8&_LANG=enus "An image of the spark icon used to denote the use of AI.") A spark icon is displayed next to the fields that require
      review and approval.

      Compare the suggested values against the data in the agreement. Select
      (click) an attribute to view ("jump-to") the suggested value within the
      agreement.

      Tip: The "jump-to" feature is only
      available for fields with a spark icon.
   3. Select the Accept AI Suggestion (checkmark) icon
      if the value is *correct*.
   4. Enter the correct value if the suggestion is *incorrect* or
      *missing*.
   5. Repeat this step until all the attribute values are reviewed and
      approved.
   6. Select Save.

      The obligation details are saved. The pane is reverted to read-only
      mode.
9. Select the < Back option from the top of the
   pane.

   The Obligations list is displayed. Use the More actions (three dots) menu to manage obligations in the list, such
   as editing details or deleting the obligation.

**Edit an obligation**

10. Expand the More actions (three dots) menu
    for the obligation that you want to update and select
    Edit:

    ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Obligation_Actions_EditDelete-options.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcxNDYsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.I6MrrCDmGResnmMUClYDQawZYUSryystssFboHxfNt8&_LANG=enus)

    The Edit Obligation pane is displayed. The obligation
    fields are open for updates.

    ![A screenshot of the Agreement preview page highlighting the Edit Obligation pane.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Navigator_Agreement-Preview_Edit-Obligation-pane.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcxNDYsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.I6MrrCDmGResnmMUClYDQawZYUSryystssFboHxfNt8&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Agreement preview page highlighting the Edit Obligation pane.")
11. Update the obligation's data fields, such as the name, type, status, due date,
    amount, frequency, repercussions, and description.
12. After making the necessary updates, select Save.

    The obligation details are updated, and the fields are closed to edits.
13. Select < Back from the top of the pane.

    The Obligations list is displayed.

**Delete an obligation**

14. Expand the More actions (vertical ellipsis) menu
    for the obligation that you want to update and select
    Delete:

    ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Obligation_Actions_EditDelete-options.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcxNDYsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.I6MrrCDmGResnmMUClYDQawZYUSryystssFboHxfNt8&_LANG=enus)

    The Delete obligation dialog box opens.
15. Select Delete to confirm that you want to delete the
    obligation.

    The obligation is removed from the Obligations
    pane.
16. Return to the Agreement Manager homepage. Select the Close (x) icon from the top-left
    corner.

    You are returned to the Completed Documents view. Your
    search criteria and filters are still applied.

title: Access Renewing Agreements from the " Completed Documents " Page
heading_path: Access Renewing Agreements from the "Completed Documents" Page
text:
This topic answers the question: How can I access my renewing agreements from Agreement Manager?

A banner message displays when you have agreements with upcoming renewal notice dates in the next 30 days. This message displays at the top of the Completed Documents view and contains a link to the Renewals dashboard.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed.

   The insights bar displays a message about your renewing agreements. You have agreements with renewal notice dates within the next 30 days.
4. Access your upcoming renewals:

   ![A snippet of the Completed Documents view that highlights the hover state for the Upcoming renewals card in the insights bar.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-Documents_My-Insights_Upcoming-renewals-card_View-Renewals-Dashboard.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcyMzksInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.37HkdlZ0iR-gt2-7C1IY5uQ4Y3IDzb7d5e-7XoGl5hk&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Completed Documents view that highlights the hover state for the Upcoming renewals card in the insights bar.")

   1. Select the Show Insights option from the right side of the insights bar.

      The My Insights bar expands and various agreement insight cards are displayed.
   2. Hover your cursor over the Upcoming renewals card.
   3. Select the View Renewals Dashboard option. This option is displayed when you hover over the Upcoming renewals card.

   The Renewals dashboard opens in a new tab. The dashboard charts are filtered to reflect the agreements with renewal notice dates within the next 30 days.

   ![A screenshot of the Renewals dashboard that shows the dashboard charts are pre-filtered to show agreements with upcoming renewals.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Renewals_Renewals-Dashboard_Accessed-from-Completed-docs-view.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcyMzksInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.37HkdlZ0iR-gt2-7C1IY5uQ4Y3IDzb7d5e-7XoGl5hk&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Renewals dashboard that shows the dashboard charts are pre-filtered to show agreements with upcoming renewals.")
5. Access the detailed report view for the Upcoming renewals chart:
   - Select the chart title.
   - Hover the cursor over the chart, expand the More actions (three dots) menu, and select the View Report option.

   The detailed report view opens for the selected chart. The agreements represented in the chart are listed in the table.

   ![A screenshot of the Upcoming renewals report that is pre-filtered to show the agreements with upcoming renewals.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Renewals-dashboard_Upcoming-renewals-prefiltered_from-banner-message.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcyMzksInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.37HkdlZ0iR-gt2-7C1IY5uQ4Y3IDzb7d5e-7XoGl5hk&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Upcoming renewals report that is pre-filtered to show the agreements with upcoming renewals.")

   Tip: If necessary, apply additional filters or customize the table data before downloading the report.
6. Download the report. Expand the More actions (three dots) menu in the top right and select the Download option:

title: Access Renewing Agreements from the " Completed Documents " Page
heading_path: Access Renewing Agreements from the "Completed Documents" Page
text:
, apply additional filters or customize the table data before downloading the report.
6. Download the report. Expand the More actions (three dots) menu in the top right and select the Download option:

   ![A snippet of a detailed report view that shows the Download option in the expanded dropdown.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Reports_Renewals_Detailed-report_Download.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTcyMzksInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.37HkdlZ0iR-gt2-7C1IY5uQ4Y3IDzb7d5e-7XoGl5hk&_LANG=enus "A snippet of a detailed report view that shows the Download option in the expanded dropdown.")

   The associated report is downloaded to your local Downloads folder.

You have successfully accessed your renewing agreements from the banner message on the Completed Documents page. See the [Monitor and Report on Renewals](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=iug1734742317393.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to monitor and report on renewals from the Renewals dashboard, including subscribing to receive notifications. This topic answers the question: How do I report on my renewals?") topic to learn more about tracking upcoming renewals.

title: Manage Agreement-Level Access
text:
Learn how to manage access to an agreement, including revoking access and updating access permissions.

Users can share an agreement with individuals and groups within their account if they have the appropriate permissions. When you have Can Manage access to an agreement, you can:

- Share the agreement with additional users or groups
- Assign Can Manage, Can Edit, or Can View access to an agreement for users and groups
- Update the agreement access for users or groups
- Remove agreement access for users or groups

Important: The Can Manage permission relates only to agreement access. It is not the same as having Organize and manage all agreements access to Agreement Manager.

You can have Can Manage access to an agreement while only having View all agreements in this account access to Agreement Manager.

1. Log in to your Docusign account.

   The Docusign Home page opens.
2. Select Agreements from the global header.

   The Docusign Agreements page opens.
3. Select Completed from the AGREEMENTS section in the navigation pane.

   The Agreement Manager homepage opens and the Completed Documents view is displayed.
4. Perform a search or use the available filters to locate the agreement you want to share.
5. Expand the More actions (three dots) menu to the right of the agreement and select the Share option:

   ![A snippet of the Completed Documents view that highlights the Share option in the More actions (three dots) menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Completed-documents-table_vertical-ellipses_Share.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY2ODgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.K6-tIkmVZutb9BPAsQ5WGUXNU0201u6BhkHFf9Dy6f4&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Completed Documents view that highlights the Share option in the More actions (three dots) menu.")

   The Share with others dialog box opens. Complete the necessary access management tasks for the selected agreement.

   ![A screenshot of the Share with others dialog box that shows the people with access to the agreement.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Share-with-others-dialog-box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY2ODgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.K6-tIkmVZutb9BPAsQ5WGUXNU0201u6BhkHFf9Dy6f4&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Share with others dialog box that shows the people with access to the agreement.")
6. Share the agreement with additional users or groups:
   1. Enter the name of the user or group in the Add user or group search field. A type-ahead dropdown displays names matching the criteria that you entered.
   2. Select a user or group from the dropdown list.

      The Add users view is displayed.

      Note: The Notify users by email option is selected by default. Leave this checkbox selected so that users are notified of shared agreements.
   3. Expand the Access dropdown and select the appropriate access level for the agreement. Choices are: Can Manage, Can Edit, and Can View.
   4. Select the Share option.

      The dialog box closes. The agreement is shared with the selected user or group and displays in their agreement list table.
7. Update the access level for users and groups with whom the agreement was previously shared.

   Open the Share with others dialog box. Expand the More actions (three dots) menu to the right of the agreement and select the Share option.

   The Share with others dialog box opens.

title: Manage Agreement-Level Access
text:
usly shared.

   Open the Share with others dialog box. Expand the More actions (three dots) menu to the right of the agreement and select the Share option.

   The Share with others dialog box opens.

   ![A screenshot of the Share with others dialog box that shows pending access changes.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Share-with-others-dialog-box_Manage-user-or-group-access.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTY2ODgsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.K6-tIkmVZutb9BPAsQ5WGUXNU0201u6BhkHFf9Dy6f4&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Share with others dialog box that shows pending access changes.")
8. Update the agreement access for users or groups listed in the People with access section.
   1. Locate the user or group that you want to update.
   2. Expand the Access dropdown and select the new access level for the agreement.

      Note: Select the Remove option to revoke access to the agreement for the corresponding user or group.

   Pending changes display in the People with access section.
9. Select the Save option.

   The dialog box closes. A message briefly stating the assignment was saved.

You have successfully managed user and group access for a selected agreement.

title: Agreement Set Management
text: Read about agreement set management including how to use sets to control access to agreements. This topic answers the question: What are agreement sets and how can I use them?

title: Agreement Set Management
text:
Agreement sets are named groups of agreements that can be shared with users and groups. These sets are task- and action-oriented. Contract managers use agreement sets to organize agreements for productivity and data privacy, including managing access at scale.

Note: Contract managers, or any users that are assigned the Organize and manage all agreements permission, have access to agreement set management. See the [Agreement Manager Permissions, Profiles, and Roles](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=eut1712157553977.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the available Agreement Manager permissions, including how users and groups are granted access to Agreement Manager agreements and example roles for each permission.") article to learn more about Agreement Manager permissions and using agreement sets for access control.

Consider this use case:

- Contract managers oversee thousands of sales agreements and need an efficient way to grant access to subsets of these agreements. For example:
  - The Renewals team needs access to all master service agreements (MSAs) expiring in a quarter without auto-renewal.
  - The U.S. Sales Team needs access to all MSAs under U.S. jurisdiction.
- With agreement sets, contract managers have a way to grant access to both teams to the appropriate agreements.

Important: There is a 1,000 set limit per account. You can create up to 1,000 agreement sets per account. If you have multiple accounts, each account can have up to 1,000 sets.

title: Agreement Set Management
text:
You can set up smart filters to add agreements to your sets automatically. This feature streamlines the agreement management process by updating sets based on agreement attribute values.

You can apply Smart filters to sets during set creation or at any point afterwards:

![A snippet of the New agreement set view that shows the Smart filters step used to add and remove agreements based on attribute values.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-set-management_New-agreement-set_step-2-Smart-filters-snippet.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTczMDcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.4fBhIdQwi60mtUu7kLw09mDcz15TqJNIMsKkOGsT9Cw&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the New agreement set view that shows the Smart filters step used to add and remove agreements based on attribute values.")

Note: The Smart filters feature supports the use of custom fields (attributes).
Use the All Filters option to search for, select, and
apply filters based on your custom fields: ![A snippet of the All Filters dialog box that highlights a user-created, custom field filter.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Smart-filters_All-Filters-dialog-box_custom-field.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTczMDcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.4fBhIdQwi60mtUu7kLw09mDcz15TqJNIMsKkOGsT9Cw&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the All Filters dialog box that highlights a user-created, custom field filter.")Enable your custom fields for AI detection to have them show up in the
All Filters dialog box. See the [Custom Extractions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=yrx1747083411531.html&_LANG=enus "Read about custom extractions, including how these data points bring value to our organization.") article to learn more about custom extractions
and AI detection.

After applying the smart filters and saving the set, a recurring rule dynamically adds and removes agreements based on the fields specified in your filters. Agreements are selected if they match conditions across filters. Consider these examples:

- You applied the Agreement Type and Parties filters to the set. The agreements must match the selected agreement type *AND* parties to be added to the set.
- You applied the Agreement Type filter to the set and selected multiple values. The agreements that match *ANY* of the selected values are added to the set.

  Tip: Some filters, such as Agreement Type and Renewal Type, support multiple values. When multiple values are selected, the agreements must match one of the values to be added to the set.

Important: The smart filters feature does *not* support these types of filters:

- **Rolling date filters:** the filter value is based on a relative time period that updates as time passes, such as "last 7 days".
- **Duration-based filters:** the filter value is based on a length of time, such as: "within 30 days".

You can update the smart filters for an agreement set at any time. Expand the More actions (three dots) menu for the set that you want to update and select Edit smart filters:

![A snippet of the All agreement sets view that shows the Edit smart filters option in the More actions menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-set-management_Actions_Edit-smart-filters-highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTczMDcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.4fBhIdQwi60mtUu7kLw09mDcz15TqJNIMsKkOGsT9Cw&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the All agreement sets view that shows the Edit smart filters option in the More actions menu.")

title: Agreement Set Management
text:
NoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.4fBhIdQwi60mtUu7kLw09mDcz15TqJNIMsKkOGsT9Cw&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the All agreement sets view that shows the Edit smart filters option in the More actions menu.")

Tip: Smart filters can also be created from the filters that you apply to the agreement list table on the Completed Documents page. See the [Create Agreement Sets from the Agreement List Table](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=dmx1750998922002.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to create a new agreement set and apply smart filters from the agreement list table on the Completed Documents page.") article to learn more about this feature.

title: Agreement Set Management
text:
Agreement set management is primarily used by contract managers, not administrators. Consider this example:

- *Administrators* manage users and groups in their organization, including granting access to software and features in the Admin portal.
- *Contract managers* create and manage sets to make sure the right people have access to relevant agreements. They also add agreements into sets and share sets with the appropriate users and groups.
- *Users and groups* view agreements in a set to focus on agreements that need their immediate attention.

Note: The separation between 'user and group management' and 'agreement set management' is intentional to address access control concerns. For example, IT administrators should not be exposed to sensitive agreement data. Contract managers use agreement sets to ensure that only the appropriate users have access to sensitive agreement data.

title: Agreement Set Management
heading_path: Agreement set access points and visibility
text:
Access to the agreement set functionality is dependent on your Agreement Manager permissions. Contract managers, or any users that are assigned the Organize and manage all agreements permission, have access to agreement set management. See the [Agreement Manager Permissions, Profiles, and Roles](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=eut1712157553977.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the available Agreement Manager permissions, including how users and groups are granted access to Agreement Manager agreements and example roles for each permission.") article to learn more about Agreement Manager permissions and using agreement sets for access control.

Review these examples of where this functionality is accessed and used in Agreement Manager:

- Sets option (located in the Settings (gear) dropdown): used by contract managers to create and manage agreement sets:![A screenshot of the Settings (gear) menu on the Agreement Manager homepage that highlights the Sets option.menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-sets_entry-point_Manage-sets-action-menu.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTczMDcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.4fBhIdQwi60mtUu7kLw09mDcz15TqJNIMsKkOGsT9Cw&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Settings (gear) menu on the Agreement Manager homepage that highlights the Sets option.menu.")
- Sets filter (located in the agreement list table): used to filter the agreement list table to show the agreements contained in a selected set:![A snippet of the Completed Documents page that displays the expanded Sets filter.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-sets_entry-point_Sets-filter.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTczMDcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.4fBhIdQwi60mtUu7kLw09mDcz15TqJNIMsKkOGsT9Cw&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Completed Documents page that displays the expanded Sets filter.")

  The dropdown contains sets that were either shared with a user or group, or created by the logged-in user.
- Sets toggle (located in the Show or Hide Fields dialog box): turn on the Sets toggle (blue position) to display this column in the table:![A screenshot of the Show or Hide Fields dialog box that shows the Sets toggle.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement-set-management_Customize-columns_Sets-toggle.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTczMDcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.4fBhIdQwi60mtUu7kLw09mDcz15TqJNIMsKkOGsT9Cw&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Show or Hide Fields dialog box that shows the Sets toggle.")

  This column displays the set information for the agreements in the table. See the [Display the "Sets" Column in the Agreement List Table](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=bti1750999198357.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to display the Sets column in the agreement list table on the Completed Documents page.") article to learn more about this feature.
- Sets  filter (located on the Agreements Dashboard, including related reports): used to focus the Agreements Dashboard on a specific set:![A snippet of an Agreements Dashboard report that highlights the Sets filter.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Agreement_sets_entry_point_Agreements_Dashboard_Reports_filter.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTczMDcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.4fBhIdQwi60mtUu7kLw09mDcz15TqJNIMsKkOGsT9Cw&_LANG=enus "A snippet of an Agreements Dashboard report that highlights the Sets filter.")

title: Agreement Set Management
text:
Before using the Share function, or sharing agreements in general, it's important to recognize the different access levels. They are: Can Manage, Can Edit, and Can View.

![A screenshot of the agreement access permissions for the direct sharing function.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/Share-an-agreement_available-access-permissions.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTczMDcsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.4fBhIdQwi60mtUu7kLw09mDcz15TqJNIMsKkOGsT9Cw&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the agreement access permissions for the direct sharing function.")

These permissions provide varying access to the agreements being shared:

- Can Manage:: Users can edit attribute values, and delete, share, and remove access to the agreement. Users with this access can also add the agreement to agreement sets.
- Can Edit:: Users can edit attribute values and share and download the agreement.
- Can View:: Users can read and download the agreement.

Important: These document permissions are used when setting access controls for
document uploads and shared agreement sets. See these topics to read more about access controls:

- [Agreement Uploads and Ingestion](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=rrs1712255668350.html&_LANG=enus "This topic answers the question: What are my upload options for Agreement Manager, including limitations and required permissions?")
- Agreement Set Management
- [Share an Agreement Set](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=pvb1736186431453.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to share an agreement set with individual users and groups. This topic answers the question: How do I share groups of agreements with the appropriate people?")

By default, all users have Can Manage access to their uploaded and completed agreements. This includes the documents that are ingested from eSignature. Both senders and recipients are given Can Manage access to their completed documents that are synced from eSignature.

Note: Users can only share an agreement with individuals and groups within their account. Users must have Can Edit or Can Manage access to an agreement to share the agreement with others:

- Users with *manage* access can share an agreement and assign *edit*, *manage*, or *view* access.
- Users with *edit* access can share an agreement and assign *edit* or *view* access.
- Users with *manage* access can revoke user and group access to an agreement.

title: Create Docusign App Connections
text:
Learn how to create Docusign apps connections for different user
credentials in Docusign agreement workflows. You can create connections
that are shared by all account users or
used
privately by the user who created
the
app connection.

You can create Docusign
app
connections for use in Docusign agreement workflows. See the [Manage Docusign App
Connections](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=xyw1716318604389.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to create and manage Docusign app connections. Use connections to access different accounts and environments.") article for information about user permissions and connection
types.

Connection types

Depending on your user credentials, you can create
these
app connection types:

- Shared: Created by an Admin. Any account user can view
  and use the connection.
- Private: Created by an account user or an Admin. Only the
  account user who created the connection can view and use the connection.

Before you begin

Install and configure the app as described in the [Install Apps in the Docusign App Center](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=ctg1698170340729.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to install Docusign extension apps in the Docusign App Center.") article.

title: Create Docusign App Connections
text:
As a user with Administrator privileges, you can create shared
app
connections. Any account user can view and use in Docusign platform services, such as Workflow Builder and Agreement Manager.

1. From the Docusign App Center Manage page, select an app
   to display the app's detail page as shown in this example.

   ![a screenshot of a detail page with the Manage menu to access app connections.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/ac-new-det-w-man-con-menu.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTgxNjYsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.ul7qnG2SakMtv5K0-4N5vsCvGDDVfVOxSinwznHJhRM&_LANG=enus "a screenshot of a detail page with the Manage menu to access app connections.")
2. Select Manage Connections to display the app's Connections page.

   Note: If you have more than one account, select the
   account for which you want to add a connection.
3. Select New Connection.
4. Salesforce only select a Salesforce account environment.

   Choose from:
   - Salesforce Production
   - Salesforce Sandbox
5. At the Select Account Type page, Select the Shared connection type and select
   Next.
6. At the Name Connection page, select a name for your connection.

   Tip: Change the default name to something you can easily identify.
7. If prompted, select Login to
   connect
   your app.

   You have created a shared app connection to use with platform services.

   See the [Configure Docusign Apps in an Agreement Workflow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=hno1706034267152.html&_LANG=enus "You can integrate Docusign apps to automate your agreement workflows and boost productivity.")
   article for more information.

title: Create Docusign App Connections
text:
As a user without Administrator privileges, you can only create
private
app connections. Only you can view and use
these
app connections in agreement platform processes.

1. From the Docusign App Center Manage page, select an app
   to display the app's Detail Listing page.
2. Select Log in and enter your credentials.
3. If prompted, select Connect Account to enable access
   to the app account.

   Note: If you have more than one account, select the
   account where you want to add a connection.
4. At the Select Account Type page, select the Private
   connection type and select Next.
5. At the Name Connection page, select a name for your connection.

   Tip: Change the default name to something
   that you can easily identify.
6. If prompted, select Login to connect to your
   app.

   - Select Allow
     to confirm access to the account.
   - Enter configuration or authorization information specific to your
     app and select Next.

   You have created a private app connection to use with platform services.

   See the [Configure Docusign Apps in an Agreement Workflow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=hno1706034267152.html&_LANG=enus "You can integrate Docusign apps to automate your agreement workflows and boost productivity.")
   article for more information.

title: Manage Apps in the Docusign App Center
heading_path: Manage Apps in the Docusign App Center
text:
Learn how to view, install, or uninstall Docusign App Center apps.

In Docusign App Center, you can view,
install, and uninstall apps from the Managed Apps page.

![A screenshot of a sample Docusign Manage Apps Page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/ac-newest-conn-dash-inst-apps.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTgzMDIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.4Nn6LLfElrJjolojGPYM7q4VIqqleRCnN_RFLv9520c&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of a sample Docusign Manage Apps Page.")

The Manage Apps page provides a left-pane menu that provides these
actions:

- Installed Apps: Select this option to display installed
  apps for accounts under your organization.

  The display includes details, such
  as activity status and version number.
- Approved Apps: Select this option to display apps that
  are approved for all users in your account.

  As an administrator, use the
  access toggle switch to permit non-administrator users in the account to
  install the apps.
- View all Admin settings: Select this option to access
  your account's Admin dashboard, as described in the [What is Docusign
  Admin?](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=rrf1583359212854&topicId=jte1583359129941.html&_LANG=enus)
  article.

  Use the Docusign Admin page to perform tasks such
  as setting user permissions for your apps.

To perform management activities from the Manage Apps page:

1. Access  [Docusign App Center](https://apps-d.docusign.com/app-center).
2. Select Log in.
3. Select Manage Apps to display your apps.
4. Select an app to display its app detail page.

   You can:

   - View a detailed app description.
   - Display app details, as shown in this example.

     ![A screenshot showing sample App Details page information.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/ac-newest-details-works-with.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTgzMDIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.4Nn6LLfElrJjolojGPYM7q4VIqqleRCnN_RFLv9520c&_LANG=enus "A screenshot showing sample App Details page information.")
5. Optionally, you can uninstall an app by selecting the app and selecting Manage App  > Uninstall App.

   You are prompted to confirm the action.

   ![A screenshot of the Docusign App Center uninstall app dialog box.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pqz1702943441912/page/images/prompt-to-uninstall-an-app.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE3OTgzMDIsInNoZWFmIjoicHF6MTcwMjk0MzQ0MTkxMiJ9.4Nn6LLfElrJjolojGPYM7q4VIqqleRCnN_RFLv9520c&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Docusign App Center uninstall app dialog box.")

   Important: If you uninstall an app, or remove the app's authorization, the operation fails. For example, apps you use in a Docusign Workflow Builder workflow step, or Data Verification fields.
6. Enter UNINSTALL and select
   Uninstall to remove the app from Docusign App Center.

   Note: Use all uppercase letters to activate the
   Uninstall button.
